The exams are really hard, but it is this way for all of MA165, and if you do the practice exams  you should be fine. This was a really great class!
Overall, Toms is a great professor. He is also hilarious, which makes his lectures worth going to. Go to class and take practice exams and you will do just fine in his class.
Toms is a good professor, and tangents in class are hilarious. Do the HW and take practice exams to do well. He will also drop exam hints in lecture the days before an exam.

Exams are a bit difficult to do well on, being only multiple choice.  Averages are in the 60s.  Toms doesn't spend too much time teaching.  He just goes over the examples in the book.  However, he is pretty funny.  Most of class is him telling stories.  Only reason I went to class.
Among the funniest people I have ever met; the dude is definitely a comedian in disguise as a mathematician.
he doesn't teach much but most people in this class have taken calc before, I don't go to lecture because only 15 min will be about calc. Recitation has quizzes every week
class in class (adjective class)

I don't know, everyone seems to love Toms. Sure, he is hilarious and makes lectures entertaining but there were two times I remember he came to lecture with a bit too much to drink and had to cancel class. We're paying to learn? He's great for math when he's not inebriated. Granted, he's probably the best teacher for Calc 1 at the moment.
Amazing teacher, knows his material, knows how to teach, knows when to draw the line. Made me look forward to math lectures.

Easily the best Calc 3 teacher when I took the class. Funny, and clearly explained examples that were pertinent to the exams and homework. Normally not good in math, but got an A strictly because of his teaching.
Every lecture was great. Many students from other sections tended to go to his class instead because he teaches so well.

Amazing hilarious professor. He uses humor to give you a break between hard-to-grasp concepts so that your mind can take a breather from math. He's also very clear in his explanations and doesn't make classes longer than they have to be. If the concept at hand is fully viewed, he cuts class short and let's you go home early. 10/10 would take again.

He is a very funny professor but a lot of the times he just gives definitions on how to do things and examples that are extremely easy compared to the homework and exams. It is important to know the importance of just learning yourself unless you have already taken Calculus before.

Amazing, hilarious teacher. He knows the material very well, explains it phenomenally and takes these breaks where he talks about random things in the most hilarious way to give your mind a break and then go on with the class. 10/10 would take again. If Toms is an option, always pick him.

The man is hilarious. He knows that lectures are boring, and that they can be long, so he will tell us the most random stuff to keep us entertained, and it works. He's not perfect, but he get's the point across well.

Highly recommended. Entertaining and understands the material. Keeps class engaged with humor usually has a break halfway through a long spiel with a funny or interesting video. Lectures are mercifully short as well, especially on Friday afternoon. His pace can be a tad fast for those with no background in the unit so you should go to every lecture
Wore a pickachu hat to lecture once you could say he's a pretty cool guy. Most of the time his lectures on last about 20 minutes.

He is a funny guy. He does not use the whole class time to explain concepts and many times finishes the lecture in 20 minutes. He does really easy examples in the class, which hardly help while doing the homework or studying for tests.
Young guy, doesn't have an accent which is hard to find in the purdue math department

I loved this class. Some of the material got a little hazy and confusing. But this professor definitely helped me understand way more than any other prof could here at Purdue. Hilarious guy, and never wasted time. When he was done teaching, we all went home. Great class.
Great professor, really makes the easy calc 3 easier. Pretty young guy and makes lectures interesting
He's an awesome guy

Great Professor, my favorite here at Purdue so far. I would take his class again in a heartbeat. Always telling funny stories, lots of in-class example problems step-by-step. Let us out early when he had finished teaching what he planned to teach for that day. Not a time waster.

Toms is a pretty funny/entertaining guy.  He always throws in some jokes or goes on a funny rant during lectures.  He also writes the exams to be very easy.  His teaching skills are just average in my opinion.  He uses the same examples in lecture that are used in the textbook and tends not to fully explain the key concepts.
ALWAYS CHOOSE TOMS

Professor Toms jokes around sarcastically with the class and is actually hilarious. He's also a great teacher; he normally pre-writes his notes, but he explains them clearly and I can actually follow what's going on. He does real examples and doesn't really waste time going through proofs. By far the best math professor I've had at Purdue.
Great Math teacher. Very clear, goes over examples a lot. Likes to tell jokes and show videos in class. Best math teacher ever.

Really fun, awesome guy. He will crack awesome jokes, in the middle of the lecture. I went to all of lectures. I audited his Math 362 class, and he explains everything clearly. He taught some more real mathematics of Vector Calculus, around the end of the course, because the Chapter 8 material was just a rehash of Math 261, but I didn't care.
He did a really good job teaching the class. Very clear. Keeps your interest. If you have a choice between Toms and someone else choose Toms/
Best Math Teacher Ever!!!!
Great math teacher ever!!!!
Fun, witty, and makes the math fun when he is teaching.
I wasn't even in his section.  I still attended every lecture he did.
Great professor, Makes the class a lot of fun (at least as much fun as a math class can be...)

Best Math professor/teacher I have ever had. I don't have to use the book because the class notes are good. I have not missed a class so far because he makes the class interesting, even though it's not required (it is not a morning class either though). He also had the highest exam 1 scores of the professors. Try to take his class if you can.

Very hard to follow or understand lectures. Answers to questions were not in depth. Very hard to verbally understand, and the written notes often had jargon that was not explained. Goes too quick through lectures too. Very smart and probably a good guy too, but not good at teaching.

He is by far the most famous and qualified real mathematician, teaching intro Calculus. His lectures are absolutely impeccable. He does not give undeserved grades, that is why the previous negative remarks obviously from one person.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Its honestly so hard to understand him. He definitely knows what he is doing but his lecture structure is impossible to follow. (Most notes are already written before class starts). Just watch ChenFlix

I took Calc 2 freshman year, his lectures were awful, you can't understand anything he's saying, and then even his writing, while legible, was hardly over content relevant to the homework or exams. I got saved by chenflix. He seems like a nice guy, but just shouldn't be teaching

His lectures are very difficult to understand. I didn't attempt to understand him because Chenflix was much easier to use. He just doesn't teach; he works through examples like we should already understand the material. Overall the class wasn't that difficult so it is doable.

Very difficult to understand, I ended up attending another lecture or using Chenflix. Better off with another professor, but ultimately attendance isn't mandatory, so your assigned section doesn't really matter.

Professor Gabrielov is too smart to teach this class. He knows the content but doesn't know how to teach it. He frequently goes silent during lecture just writing on the projector and then stating “Okay” at the end. Hopefully you are blessed with a good TA if you have him.

Look, he's a brilliant mathematician, especially in calc 3, and his expertise is clear. But my biggest issue (a critical one) is his inability to teach effectively. His lectures are dull, almost a like a sleep-fest. Save yourself the hassle,use Chenflix or online resources. Calc 3 is manageable for self-study, so don't waste your time in his class.

It's not even worth it to show up to the first day of his class, just immediately start watching Chenflix you'll be less confused. The only reason to go to class is to watch the notes be written live, because he barely speaks during lectures. Ask questions in recitation and watch Chenflix because this professor won't help you.

His lectures are slow to the point of painful, he skips over most of the logic behind why something is true (ie: A is true - ok - so B is true - ok - so R is true - how in the world did you get here?), and when you ask about these issues, there's a pretty low chance that you'll get a good answer to your question.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Gabrielov is a pretty nice person, but my god his lectures are horrible if you are trying to actually understand anything. If you have his class, you are much better off watching Chenflix, as you will be able to understand the material much better that way. He has a very thick accent and also kind of mutters his words, which does not help at all.

He's a nice guy, and truly wants to help answer questions that people have. But his lectures are pointless to go to. Watch Chenflix instead. 

At the end of the semester, there were maybe 10 people left in the entire lecture hall which started with 200 people.
He was very difficult to understand. He wrote down every word he said on paper, making the lectures boring and hard to follow.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This is absolutely the worst math professor I've had in ANY math class. He just copied notes from a scratch pad and doesn't explain anything of substance in detail. He spends so much time talking about material that won't be on the test it's awful. Super bad lectures!!!! It's very clear he is just here to get money from Purdue for research.

Actually one of the worst professors I've ever had. WATCH CHENFLIX if you have this prof. Yeah he's a nice guy, but he's awful and I mean awful at teaching, and I respect all instructors since my mother's a teacher. BEWARE OF THIS MAN! He is in at Purdue to get money for research

This professor was decent. I definitely found it worth it to attend his lectures, as he taught things differently than Chen, but I did supplement with Chenflix on occasion. The few times I went to office hours, he was helpful and patient with my questions, though it took some trial and error to make sure we were on the same page.

Honestly, just use chenflix. The class itself is really difficult so be prepared to do a lot of extra work outside of expected class time and homework if math isn't your strongsuit. He wasn't the problem as much as just the class was, but Chen's lectures were much more accessible and oftentimes made much more sense to me.
Awful, just watch Chenflix if you're forced to be in his class.

The professor tries to cram way too much material in each lecture, often meaning that he does not finish his planned content and just advises students to read the textbook or look at notes from previous years. I found this professor to be condescending during office hours and seemed dismissive of questions. Worst course I've ever taken.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This man made the midterm and the final exam multiple choice in a graduate level class. Who does that?????? Guess he has tenure
Very professional and successful

Avoid this professor if at all possible. He does not teach, but just copies prepared notes. There is little value in class; you will get no more than exactly what is in his notes. He tested material not yet covered for both midterms. Up to 30 hw problems/week, only 4 graded. Gives very few examples. Be prepared to teach the material to yourself.

Highly recommend avoiding his classes. Lectures are mostly silent; most of the time spent talking is reading what he is writing without further explanation. Some examples are clever. But, he expects you to use tricks on exams which are long/hard, and he could not get 100% in the allotted time. Accent is fine. Class curve was ~10% and needed by all.

This was by far one of my worst classes at Purdue, which is saying something since I have had a lot of worthless classes at this university. Exams are graded without partial credit, without a calculator, and involve many numerical calculations. Practice exams are essentially a trap that he will use to catch you on the real test.

This guy sucks! He's mean and grouchy. And no one understands what he's saying. The first day of class, he starts teaching (from slides btw). He then asks the lecture hall if he was teaching too fast. Everyone unanimously groaned, "yes." He then paused, and said one word... "Good." And moved on! He makes your life difficult for no reason.

Glubokov focuses on material that is irrelevant to the rest of the class, and is hard to understand when he talks. Beyond this he has little respect for students. On one of the first lectures when we stated he was going too fast after he asked if he was doing so, he just said "boo hoo, write faster"
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Gabrielov is definitely a great mathematician but is not great at conveying information to students. Most of the lectures were spent with him quietly at the front of the room writing, and it was not always legible. When he did speak, it was not very clear what was being said. Overall not a great experience.
Over explains the most useless stuff, while not explaining the important things. Very few examples in lecture, Never gives enough time for ppl to copy down because "the ppt is online"

He was pretty hard to understand and basically read to you directly from what he was writing in the notes as an explanation which wasn't very helpful. Only reason I got a good grade was going to another professors lectures as well as his lectures. Grades are based mainly off of exams, so do the study guide, know it well, and you'll do alright.

Professor Gabrielov has a strong accent, but he basically says everything he writes. He also posts his notes at the end of each lecture, so it is not too bad. This year, Professor Matsuki did videos of his notes. If you combine Professor Gabrielov's written notes and Professor Matsuki's notes, I feel like you get a better range of information.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Many have said that his accent made him hard to understand. At some points this was true, otherwise he was easy to understand. He clearly knows how to do the materials, and even proofreads the other professor. He was very funny, and always mentioned about his office hours.
-Try to get another professor -Very hard to understand -Lectures are not very clear -Not easy to reason with
It is super hard to understand him. His lectures are so boring and he skips way to many steps in the examples.

Others have said his accent is too difficult to understand, but it really isn't after the first class. He shows proofs for why mathematical concepts and methods work, then shows some examples of said method in practice. His examples are simple, yet complex enough that you'll have to look over it twice. Overall, great teacher in my opinion.

Gabrielov is not a bad dude, he is just super hard to understand. Because of that, I and the majority of students in his lecture have stopped coming to his lectures either going to Matsuki's or simply learning the material alone.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: You will barely understand this guy as he has the strongest accent ever. He is also not understanding at all and can't keep an argument even though he knows he is wrong. Probably the worst professor in the whole math department.

Professor Gabrielov (by no fault of his own) has an extremely thick accent that makes it quite hard to understand what he is talking about. Furthermore he never explains material, but just recites whats in the textbook, which makes everything super confusing, and youre always super lost in his lectures.

Professor Gabrielov isn't a very good professor. He's brilliant and definitely knows his stuff, but he has a thick accent and most of his lectures are almost completely silent since he just writes instead of speaking. If you've had calc 1 before you might be okay, but I didn't find his lectures very beneficial. MA165 is weedout for FYE and hard.

Gabrielov is not necessarily a bad professor, it's just that the other professors for MA165 at Purdue are better. For me, Gabrielov was difficult to understand (which isn't his fault) and his lectures seemed to drag on.
Stopped going to his lectures in September. Just be sure to get extra help from TAs and peers in other lecture sections
He was a little hard to understand; however, if you communicate with him, then you learn your material quite well. I thought he was friendly.

From outside Professor Gabrielov looks like a nice old guy. But as a math professor, he can not do his job professionally. Professor Gabrielov can not speak properly in class due to his heavy accent that makes lectures useless for students. A high knowledge in math is useless as a professor, when you can't teach properly at a reputable university.

He is a very knowledgeable guy, but he does not convey what he is teaching well to an audience. He is not a good lecturer. I would recommend finding either another section with a different lecturer or save your time and just read the book and take notes over that.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: He has the most monotone voice, and also has a voice that sounds like Gru from Despicable Me. Very boring and doesn't care at all about us as students.
He has a thick Russian accent and talks super fast which makes it impossible to understand. I took calc before his class and all he did was confuse me on stuff I had already learned.

I took the MA52800 for ME Masters. This was the last class I needed to graduate and I had chosen poorly. The material itself is not as difficult, but I decided to drop it after the first test as he gives no partial credit for work. He will give you a zero if the final answer is incorrect. Class average was 60%. He does not care if students learn.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Professor Gabrielov is a kind, jolly and intelligent man whose reputation precedes him. He knows many secrets about the art of mathematics, however he struggles to clearly express them to his students during lecture. Often times he can be found skipping steps or making something seem more complicated than it actually is. Reading the book will help.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: While he is a sweet and funny old guy (and I wish he were my grandpa), he is not the greatest lecturer. Certain days you may leave class quite confused. I HIGHLY recommend using the textbook (for calc 1-3) as there are great lessons to follow along with. I ultimately ended up doing this instead of going to class.

This class was difficult. Understanding the more complex parts of the course was made more difficult because of his accent. The exams were difficult. He is a nice guy, but he doesn't make the class easy.

All of the exams are fair. The exam question are all easier than homework questions. When you go into the exam you know what to expect. For some people he was hard to understand, but I've never had any trouble. He uses a lot of examples in lectures, which was personally very helpful.
If you an help it, do not take this professor. He was hard to understand and I ended up going to a different lecturer for class.
Russian. Understanding what he is saying is hard enough let alone learning math on top of it. I had to rely on friends and other professor's lectures to pass the class.
scanned notes are really helpful.
He got an accent that is kinda hard to understand. He writes notes in class and send notes to students through e-mail after class, which is truelly helpful and amazing.

I didn't take Calc 2 in high school, so I started my freshman year taking Calc 2 with Gabrielov.  I read the book and took notes BEFORE coming to lecture, and I left his lecture more confused about the material. I did all of my studying outside of class. Somehow I got a B when I got a 45% on the final. Good luck to any courses with him.

Absolutely terrible. So hard to understand, skips steps all the time, and writes really hard tests. Going to class with Gabrielov isn't even worth it. I survived through using the internet, but it was not a good experience. Avoid taking at all costs.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: Gabrielov is difficult to understand at first but you get used to it. He is a very smart man with lots of accolades but that makes it hard for him to stoop to our level so to speak. He writes extremely difficult tests, much harder than the homework.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: This was my first time taking Calculus 2 and I never understood what was happening in lecture. His accent made it hard to understand him and he didn't teach the material well. Going to his office hours was helpful, but his multiple choice tests were very difficult. I had to go to the math help room all the time and teach myself the material.

As lecturer, Gabrielov does not excel. He has a moderately thick russian accent, and when combined with a small amount of background noise and a low quality microphone he can be hard to understand in lecture halls.  His strengths lie in face to face interactions. He is extremely intelligent and resolve any issues you have with course material.
An intelligent but friendly, nice old man. He's a pleasure to talk to face-to-face. It's a real shame it's so hard to understand what he's saying in lecture.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Caring

reviews.comment: He is almost impossible to understand. He is the reason I decided to change majors when I ended up unable to understand any of the material.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Gabrielov's like that sweet old grandad you have. The kind tries to teach you things but in the end you don't really know what he's saying and end up smiling and nodding at everything. Now imagine that grandad being Russian. Needless to say, I didn't understand much of what he said and just like at family dinners, I excused myself and left.

He is impossible to understand and makes so many mistakes in lectures that by then end you have no correct example to go off of for the homework. His English isn't great and the pauses in his sentences would make you think that he is using Google Translate between each statement. Defiantly would never take a class with him again.

His handwriting is very bad, he reuses paper so you can see work on the other side, his accent is difficult at times, his lectures have NO structure whatsoever and he blames students for his own mistakes and says 'you need to watch what I'm doing and tell me if I make a mistake'. If you get him, watch other lectures on BoilerCast. Good luck...
Terrible teacher. Whenever he is trying to say a sentence, he will stop several times and think for a few sec and than precede. I hate the class first time he start to speak

Accent was amusing.  Gabrielov makes literally everything harder than it needs to be, and he (tries) to explain things in the most roundabout way possible. Also, he makes countless mistakes during lecture. Very annoying and confusing, especially if you're learning it for the 1st time.
He's a great guy, and incredibly smart. He is just very hard to follow in lectures, as his english isn't the best, combined with the way he explains concepts is hard to grasp.

The lectures are usually absolutely useless apart from trying to get the different equations you'll need for homework. The example problems he does in class are either impossible to follow or wrong in the end. He often says "make sure you don't mess up like that on the tests!" several times a lecture.   You'd be better off learning from the book.
He is difficult to understand because of his accent and when you get past this you still can't understand because of his teaching style.  Do not recommend.
It's hard to understand him most of the time.  If you ask for help he will try but it still might not make sense.

Basically the wacky uncle of the Math department. He seems like a very friendly person, but he skips steps in example problems, is very difficult to understand (thick accent), and doesn't explain new material very well. He may also be a Soviet sleeper cell, but that's based more on suspicion than evidence.
He's smart but cannot teach  at all. If you are thinking of taking math class, evade this professor!
He had very poor English, and a terrible way of interacting with the class. I had to resort to going to another teacher's lectures when my schedule didn't get in the way.

Gabrielov is a very nice professor who has humility.  However, it is hard to follow his lessons sometimes because it seems like he is translating his words into english as he speaks so it is very choppy.
I found him very hard to understand, but when i could, he explained the material fairly well. Open to questions and very nice.
No Comments

Uses too simple of examples most of the time.  The first exam was incredibly easy.  He seems fairly helpful if you're having problems.  His English is OK; if you're in engineering you'll have no problem with it.

I really enjoyed MA261 (Multivariate Calculus), and everything up until Flux/Circulation Gabrielov did a really good job of explaining. He answers questions very well, even if he's hard to understand. First exam was a joke, second a little harder.

He seems like a nice guy, I want to say happy, not angry at the world.  He has let us out early on several occasions after finishing the day's lesson.  Not the best, but pretty good.  I believe he's Russian, but with that name, how could one not be.

Can't lie, I think he's slightly overrated but is pretty good. His homeworks are long and tedious but the exams are super easy and there is 5% extra credit between attendance and 2/3 of the class doing course evals. His lectures are sooo boring and intuitive though, especially if you took Micro first.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: This was an ok professor, but his lectures are basically him reading presentations. He doesn't make content easy, and I would learn the same amount of things through the textbook or youtube. He says attendance isn't mandatory, but uses iClicker for points. Not the best if you want to be genuinely engaged during lectures.

Dr Vargas is one of the greatest economics professors and lectures I've had! he is super kind and very helpful. His lectures are well organized with lots of examples and always willing to answer questions before, during, and after class. I've had him for macro too and although the topics can be quiet hard he can help you through understanding well!

Dr Vargas is a ok professor. I personally found Macro a little bit more interesting compared to Micro. His exams are not bad as long as you do some studying. His lectures can be a bit boring but attendance is not mandatory and is counted as extra credit via iClicker. Homeworks can take some time since they are done on Pearson. Way better than Fox.

I love Vargas, I am actually planning to take as many economics classes as possible with him. He cares about his students and their success, and he is so accessible. He gives out extra credit for attending lecture, and he is kind and honestly hilarious. While I earned an A-, let that not reflect him in any way, just study more than me :) goat!!
Breaks down difficult statistics topics and does a great job at explaining them.

Vargas is clearly very knowledgeable and passionate about teaching. The class material doesn't always line up perfectly with the exams, but the exams and homework aren't too difficult. Overall good professor, and especially way better than the alternatives (Fox) for intro Econ classes.
honestly a super kind professor, but his material doesn't match up with exam. Lots of homework and material is difficult to understand.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: The GOAT ECON Professor!

Professor Vargas was by far my best instructor this semester. He takes time to help his students and his lectures are very efficient, covering everything on exams. He offers optional in-class help sessions for the harder homework assignments, which makes a huge difference. He is always willing to answer questions. Easy A if you do the assignments.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Dr. Vargas is awesome. The material can be a bit tricky, but he breaks it down well. Tests aren't too hard.

Vargas is an amazing econ teacher. His lectures are very clear, and he is willing to talk with students who don't understand the material after class. The homework assignments are due every week are manageable but they may take time. He provides a practice exam with material similar to the real exams, and they helped us focus on what to study.

Vargas is a smart lecturer who knows what he's talking about and has taught this class for many years. Nonetheless, his lecture pacing can get very boring. Although it's likely because he's not native to the U.S., his voice lacks enthusiasm and isn't great at making the topic interesting. Overall, the class is moderate, slightly harder than 251

Vargas is good! Personally I thought the class was boring. Final and second exam are much harder than first so do well on the first one. Study and know concepts. He does care and explains questions well.
Good class, lectures dragged out a bit but in a good way since it helped us understand the concepts.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: lectures were boring but not terrible. homeworks r on pearson, never read the textbook. he seems like a nice guy but i never went to class because it was boring, still got an ok grade. if u alr took 251 this will be pretty easy. second midterm is randomly hard.

The most incomprehensible exam writing ever. Someone needs to proof read him. For exams being 80% of your grade, its kind of unacceptable. Also be prepared for every question to not be a topic thats important at all to Macroeconomics but rather about random theories he didnt even mention in lectures.
He uses lots of visual aides while teaching like videos and real world data that is extremely helpful.

Vargas is a great professor for ECON 252, and he offers extra credit for showing up to class and answering an iClicker question or two correct. Gives weekly homework and tests are challenging but he does post practice exams which help. He does have an accent but you can still easily understand him. Would definitely take another one of his classes

Pretty good class with lots of extra credit. Attendance is extra credit. Homework could be time consuming and annoying but you can redo questions you get wrong. You don't really have to pay attention/do anything in class, but it does make the exams a bit difficult. The lectures are interesting and the professor is nice.
Dr. Vargas is a good professor, and I enjoyed his class. He was able to break down complicated topics, making them easier to understand.
Econ 252 with Profe Vargas is really easy. Exams are worth 80% of the grade but you can get up to 15% extra credit just by showing up to class.

Vargas is one of the better lower level Econ professors you'll find at this school. His lectures are entertaining and his exams aren't horrible. He explains economic topics using real world examples, which I found really helpful. Loves participation points so definitely show up to class.

I didn't like Macro as much as Micro, but it was still an interesting class. He is a fun guy and lectures were engaging. The content is all on slides. The homework was easy as long as you understand the lectures. Tests weren't bad at all.

I took this during the summer 4-week course and just to let you know NEVER take a 4-week course as this blows. Homework every day with 1.5 hours of lecture and midterms that are draining. I got an 87.5/100 for the first midterm but the average dropped considerably on the 2nd midterm. He's not a bad professor but 240/300 points are Exam related. :(

Manageable class if you pay attention. The lectures were mediocre. Slides and graphs explain concepts well enough for a basic understanding. There is a lot of extra credit opportunities to help you pad your grade.
exams are nothing like the practice ones he provides. would never take it again

This class is great! The homework can be time consuming but those are the only assignments. The midterms will be decently easy if you make sure to study for them but otherwise this class is mostly based on the lectures. I will warn you that the class gets harder as it goes on but if you pay attention you will be alright!

Professor Vargas is an amazing lecturer. For a beginner class, he explains economic topics in an understanding way, and provides many examples to real-life scenarios in class. Also, extra credit is offered for every class attended. If you can get over his accent (which you'll get used to by lecture 2-3), he's a great lecturer.

His class was good even though he has some kind of accent, but as long as you attend the class this will not influence anything. There was a lot of extra credit through the hotspot and economic panels which could boost your grades. If you have already taken Econ in high school, then this class should be relatively easy for you.

Vargas seems to care about what he teaches, which is always a plus! The material can get tricky, but he was open to answering questions during lecture. Macro is tough, but he is a nice guy, so if you run into troubles understanding the material, don't be afraid to reach out to him.

Professor is good and helpful, but the class is not recommended. Without enough examples in class and explanations in HW, it's not easy to get a good grade. The professor may help during the office hour but it's still too hard. Exams worth 50 each about 70% grades and 11 assignments worth 6 points each with unclear explanations were frustrating.

Professor Vargas gives clear and interesting delivery of class content, which is fun to learn. Attendance not mandatory but he gives extra credits every class, even telling you the correct answer every time. Helpful reviewing sessions provided before all exams. Easy to communicate as well. Would definitely recommend.
He is a great professor who wants his students to succeed in the course. His lectures are also very beneficial and he gives extra credit in those lectures. overall would definitely take his class.

Vargas is a good lecturer who explains concepts well. His class is exam heavy but show up to class and answer the hotseat questions to get a small grade boost for those on the edge of a grade. Homework is easy as they are once a week due on Sundays. He has an accent but still is very understandable.

Vargas is fine. The practice exams felt SO similar to the real exams so do them a couple of times. His lectures can be really boring at points and were a downgrade from Fox's 251 lectures. That being said, he is always willing to help and gives a bunch of EC.

Exams are worth 80% of grade so If one exam goes poorly there are not many opportunities available to truly make a difference in your final grade without doing perfect on the other 2 exams homework does nothing for final grade at the end of semester
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This professor taught very well and even though he has an accent, you were able to understand exactly what he was saying. He was able to help answer your questions and was accessible outside of class. If you go to class, you are able to receive extra credit on hotseat. I really recommend this class and professor.

Professor Vargas makes this class very manageable. Your grade is heavily based on exams, but he offers practice exams that are very similar to the real exam. He also offers extra credit for every lecture you attend, for a total of 5% of your total grade. He has a thick Columbian accent, but it is understandable. I would definitely recommend Vargas!

The first thing you'll notice is Prof Vargas' thick Colombian accent. But it's very understandable, and I like how he says the word "fundamental" as well as "econometrics". But anyway he lectures very clear, makes occasional comedic remarks, I found his lectures interesting. He picks very good videos to show the class. Exams+content pretty easy.

Professor Vargas is the sweetest professor I've had. He always answers questions and his lectures are actually interesting. I didn't do well in Econ 251 but he helped me understand and I did better in Econ 252. He offers extra credit by coming to class. His lecture slides are also very helpful. The practice exams are VERY similar to the exam.

Professor Vargas is so sweet and kind. The class isn't the easiest ever but he makes it very accessible. He gives plenty of extra credit and not much homework so if you just show up to class, it's very easy to pass and with not much effort, get a good grade.

Dr. Vargas is great. I have never met anyone who dislikes his classes. I have personally taken two courses with him, and he teaches the exact same way in both. Lots of example problems. Plenty of out of class resources to succeed. His homework can be tedious and very long, but it shows you anything that might be on the exam. Take his courses.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Professor Vargas is a great professor and person overall. Going to class is important because of the hotseat extra credit which can help you a lot at the end of the semester. The exams were never too difficult as long as you paid attention during class, do the homework, and go over the practice exams. One of my favorite professors at Purdue.

Andreas is an amazing professor. His lectures are concise and clear, he provides tons of information and specifies what is important to remember. Every chapter has a homework assignment, but if you keep up with lectures they are pretty easy.

Professor Vargas is probably one of the best econ professors I've had. He's very straightforward in his material, makes sure no one is behind, and offers outside help to students who need it. Everything is clear cut and seems to be an experienced teacher. He makes everything make sense. He GOATed.

Coming from ECON 511 to ECON 512 you will realize how much a good lecturer matters. His lectures are well organized, the textbook describes the concepts in details (I highly recommend you read the textbook). Tests are pretty straightforward. Practice homework questions as well as the textbook questions and you will do good.
He is an amazing professor who knows his subject and is always ready to help out his students.

Pastor Vargas is a blessing to all his disciples in 252. The homeworks can sometimes be tedious but they're easy work, and the tests are very easy if you just pay attention in the lectures. Recommend attending all lectures to get the Hotseat points, getting all of them adds 4.7% to your final grade.
Andres Vargas is a savior from heaven who has come down to teach econ. He teaches the info effectively and has fair exams.

Vargas has a thick accent, but after a while, it gets easier to understand him. Lectures always last the whole class and I highly recommend going to every lecture. Each lecture we have Hotseats that go towards extra credit, which can bring you from a C to a high B easy. When it comes to exams you really have to know the material, they can be hard.

Professor Vargas really knows his stuff in the world of Economics. He does have a strong accent, so at times it can be a little difficult to understand him. Pay attention to the lecture videos, do the homework assignments thoroughly (will be needed for exam understanding), and do the practice exams. Class revolves solely on understanding of content

Prof. Vargas wasnt bad by any means. He clearly was knowledgeable about the subject matter and that made for some really informative lectures. One thing, and this may not bother many people, is that he is from Colombia I believe, and has a somewhat strong accent. It can be a little hard to understand him at times. Overall, not bad at all!
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Test heavy--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Great professor and fairly easy class. 1 HW and 1 Extra credit assignment each week. He goes over the material in clear videos, reserves class time for any questions, and provides all his powerpoints. Responsive to emails and DB posts. Tests were open note and all over content that was covered. Would definitely recommend

Econ 512 was a quintessential "slides, homework, test, repeat" sort of class. Prof. Vargas does try to spice up lectures with videos, but I found it hard to focus much of the time. Beware of the weekly homeworks, they may seem easy at first but towards the end of the semester they tend to get very long. Otherwise, nothing else really stands out.

This class requires tons of work, which is not always bad! The materials are mainly appliances, so you need to grasp the topic proficiently. I think taking his class online affects my rating significantly because my friends who took the class in-person said that he was such a good professor.

I had taken Macro before, and Vargas' lectures helped me reinforce a lot of concepts. He is a great lecturer and a fair grader. Would take more classes with him in the future if given the opportunity.

Professor Vargas was passionate about the topics of every lecture and would explain things very clearly. He would then apply what we learned to the real world so that we can see how it acts in real time. This was effective and he was always willing to answer questions. Great professor!
Very organized. Explains well. Exams can be a bit tricky. But plenty of opportunity to get a good grade.

I took Macroeconomics with Professor Vargas and it was a very straight-forward class. As long as you put in some time every week, it is not too difficult. He offers a small amount of extra credit. He is very fair and logical and he does a good job of connecting course content to current events.

The class was asynchronous this semester and was honestly a free A if you put in the time to do the readings and the homework. Dr. Vargas does a great job with making himself accessible and if you email him a question he usually responds within a day. By far one of the smoothest classes I've taken and I would love to take another class with him

Lectures clarify concepts. Tests fairly represent class material. Answers questions clearly and quickly. Im an engin. student, but Vargass ECON 252 class was my best class the semester I took it. Extra credit opportunities optional. CONS: Doesnt release course material all at once, so you can only get 1 week ahead max (instead of 1 mo ahead).
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Graded by few things--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Lectures were clear and concise, and easy to contact if you're having problems with the material, but the grade cutoffs were rather harsh. I would appreciate it if the online learners were given a chance to gain more extra credit opportunities as was with the students who attended the lectures physically.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Vargas was a nice guy that would go through example problems and answer any questions that you might've had about the homework. I personally didn't attend the optional lectures because as long as you read the textbook and do the homework, this class is a breeze.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Professor Vargas overall does what is needed to push material onto students. Grading is very tough for Honors Assignments, but the class is manageable. You will need to dedicate A LOT of time to understand the concepts even if you have previous experience. All practice tests he gives are NOTHING like actual exams, so study on your own.

I don't think I went to a single lecture after this first for this class. You're better off reading the book, this class is not difficult if you have taken ECON251 or have taken any econ beforehand. The homework is easy, exams are cake, and there is extra credit if that's your thing. This class is exceptionally boring so only take if you need it.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: He makes tough economic concepts easy to understand. Every class that was held in person, pre-COVID-19, would have a Hotseat question that was worth extra credit and he would tell you the answer so you always got it right. Reviews helped prepare for tests and the notes were organized and very helpful, would definitely recommend.
Great professor. Always reply to my email whenever I don't understand how to answer hw problems

Vargas isn't a bad professor but coming from Blanchard for econ 251 he was a bit of a letdown. The lectures are just boring and the notes are on slides so they are difficult to keep up with. I just took notes outside of class and then sat in lecture and zoned out until the hot seat questions.

His accent is not hard to comprehend at all, i'm not sure what some of these other reviews are talking about. If you listen to the lectures (they're all posted online too) and do the homework, you should get a good grade.

Professor Vargas is such a kind person and really sets us up for success on the tests. You don't even need the textbook as long as you pay attention in class and review the sample tests he posts before each exam.
Large lecture and sometimes hard to pay attention in class
Prof Vargas is a gem.

I had Andres Vargas for ECON 252, and he was pretty good. Showing up to lectures definitely helps because you are obviously there listening to the professor + he gives out 0.5 points of extra credit for each class one attends. This ultimately boosts your grade by 5%. Furthermore, homeworks are given A's, and there are no quizzes to worry about.

He cares about teaching and is accessible if you have questions. Straight forward lectures and exams, but the homework that makes you think a little bit. The class could get a little boring as it consisted solely of lecture, homework and exams, but it was organized and fair.

Took this class Fall 2018, do not underestimate it. Vargas does a good job of explaining topics and has really good notes, not engaging enough to keep me awake during a 7:30AM lecture three times a week. Inserts humor occasionally and good example videos. Exams are hard.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Investment goes up
252 is a breeze and Prof. Vargas teaches it well.

Vargas is an incredible professor. In addition to teaching very well, he uses third-party elements (e.g., news clips) to enhance his lectures. The class is not difficult; he gives extra credit for merely showing up to class (very generous), and the hw/practices exams are exactly like the exams  very approachable guy & knowledgeable.

Lectures are SO BORING. He drones on without checking in with students for comprehension. Watching grass grow is more exciting than one of his lectures. He also talks too quickly to take notes, so either listen or write, you can't do both.

At first it's a bit difficult to understand his accent, but you get used to it. He gives max 15 points extra credit on Hotseat for going to lectures. He is very accessible outside of class and very personal. He posts videos on BoilerCast and the tests are very similar to the practice exams we get. I enjoyed this class and he was a great professor.
reviews.rating_tags: ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Really interesting teacher and his lectures were exactly like the homework assignments. The exams also covered what we'd do in class, as long as you attended each lecture you're good to go. Didn't buy the textbook and did fine. The hoteseat extra credit questions really help boost your grade too.

The class is okay I guess, chances are you would need to take this class due to a requirement. Only went to 5 classes throughout the semester and still got an A, but I know of a few friends who went to all the class but still got a B/C, so I think it really depends on the individual. He is an okay professor.

He's rather boring, and you're gonna have to buy the textbook because it's paired with an online homework application. He doesn't take participation and the lectures are all online, so that's a plus. Tests aren't too hard, but you're gonna need to study quite a bit.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Clear grading criteria--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: Amazing Economics Professor! Would definitely take a class taught by him again. The first thing you will probably notice at the beginning of the semester is that he has an accent, but you will DEFINITELY get used to it after going to a few lectures. Sit closer to the front for the best in class experience. Offers extra credit and exams are Easy A!
reviews.rating_tags: GRADED BY FEW THINGS--EXTRA CREDIT--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Prof. Vargas is a real gem. Though his accent may take a few classes to get used to, you'll realize his lectures are invaluable. He makes sure to go through every topic in the text and explain it very well. Make sure to go through his sample paper well, and you can be sure you'll pass.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Pretty good. Lectures can be boring, but getting an A isn't too hard
Great teacher, he really puts in the effort to get the message across.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: The lectures are clear and straight forward. He knows what he is talking about and does a good job connecting the material to actual situations in the present and past. If you understand the material, this makes it much more interesting. The class is not hard and attendance is optional except for extra credits.
if you go to class, you don't even need a textbook, all the exam covered is what he covered in class and HW
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: He puts up all the lecture slides in advance. There is normally one homework assignment a week. Tests are multiple choice and follow closely with the material covered in class and the review session. Plenty of study material for exams. He is passionate about what he teaches and willing to help any student who needs it.
He's a good teacher who knows exactly what he's talking about. Just kinda hard to understand at times. If you listen to the lectures and do the homework you'll be fine
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Great professor, some people found his accent a little difficult to understand but you get used to it. Class is mostly powerpoints and homework. Come to class so you can get participation points for extra credit
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--EXTRA CREDIT--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: He is a good professor, the problem is his class is incredible boring. If you go to class he gives extra credit points (15 at Max). If you read the textbook and understand how everything connects with each other this class will be very easy for you. Your grade is determined by 3 tests but it is easy if you read the book and understand the material.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Make sure you read the chapter before lecture. His accent is quite confusing for me. You guys should sit in front so that you can hear him better. Attend class cuz there's lots of hotseat opportunities.

Be sure to get all the hotseat points as they can boost your overall grade by 5%. Homework is easy and tests are easy, pretty much everything comes straight out of the powerpoints that he provides. Easy to follow whether you go to class or not.

I really enjoyed Dr. Vargas's class. He is a solid prof and knows his stuff. There is a lot of homework, but you have a week to do it. He doesn't take attendance, but you get extra credit by answering questions in class. This class is easy to do well in if you do the work, study, and go to class.

Only showed up to lecture once after the first exam and still got an A-. WIll be easy if you've already taken microeconomics. Vargas is one of the most unintentionally entertaining lecturers I've had at the university. HOWEVER, he speaks very quietly and has a significant accent, and his voice modulation is off (he speaks really loud on some words)
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: He is really friendly and helpful. Regarding to materials he covered in class, he explained every detail clearly and logically, which really helped me to understand the content. He also led us to do many sample questions during the class, which was also helpful. I would definitely take other classes he teaches in the following semester :)

His lectures may not be the most exciting, but if you go to his office hours, he will work with you until you understand it, and do everything to help. Watch out for his exams, they're all multiple choice but the all the answers are the same except for one or two word differences - make sure to know your conceptual stuff! Hot Seat Points!
Could not understand him with his accent that i honestly just gave up trying. i actually enjoy economics so i was sad that i didn't get much out of it.

His accent was difficult to understand at first, but don't let that scare you because after a few classes I knew exactly what he was saying. Go to class for the hot seat points!!! Don't waste money buying the text book. Just use practice exams and the power point slides to study for exams. Homework was harder than the exams in my opinion.
Thick accent. Homework is unnecessarily long, repetitive, and often does not work.  Take 251 if you only need one ECON class. Avoid this prof classes if you can

Lectures are slow but it makes the material easier to understand. He shows examples in lecture that are similar to the exam questions, so there are no surprises. Make sure you show up and pay attention because he takes attendance using Hotseat. Should be an easy A.

He's a good professor and enjoys teaching economics. His lecture notes are very useful, posted online, and the textbook is barely needed. Hotseat extra credit points during lecture is also nice. He has a bit of an accent, but it's not too bad. He frequently checks and asks if people have questions and is very good at answering those questions.

He 's very helpful in class and at office hours. You do not need a textbook if you attend lectures and use slides. You should probably practice his problem sets a lot, I didn't realize this till the final, so don't be like me. Hotseat points boost grades ideally by a letter grade and his exams are pretty difficult since there are no sample exams.
Dr. Vargas's lectures can be a little boring sometimes. Other than that, he's a good professor. If you show up to lectures and do the homework, it won't be hard to get a good grade in his class.

Prof. Vargas is a really good and helpful professor that taught me Macro-economics really well. If you attend his lectures and listen to him, you will certainly excel in this class. He is a really nice guy and gives cool homework sometimes. Also there's extra credit.

Tests are somewhat difficult. He has an accent that is very thick and hard to understand. He seems like he enjoys teaching and what he does, but very difficult to learn because he has such an accent. Not mandatory to go to lecture but the extra credit from lecture is helpful
His accent is wired. Lecture is long. Too hard to focus on his lecture

He covers the material well. I had him for a 7:30 so it was a bit boring and long. If you read the chapters in the textbook and somewhat have an idea of what is going on you will do fine. He gave us extra credit for hotseat questions that he would randomly give in class (if you skip just keep the hottest website open and you can still answer).

His lectures were not interesting in my opinion and his accent didn't help, but it may just have been because it was a 7:30 class. He covers the content well and posts slides so you won't need the textbook. Exams are not difficult if you have a feel for economics in general. BEWARE: Final exam is not cumulative.
He is so great with his materials. And he has office hours every day of the week except for friday. He is a excellent teacher as long as you go to class and talks to him. Very patient.

Take his class, seriously! A good professor, very helpful !!!!!! His lectures and slides are easy to understand and helpful enough. No need to buy the textbook. And also, he took participation points. Exams are moderate, and getting A's for exams is not that hard. It's all multiple choice questions. If you do all the homework, it's A in your hand!

My GOAT. Professor Wolanski was amazing. I actually did better here than in MGMT 30301 (which is basically AP Stats). He's very available in office hours, quick with emails, funny, and genuinely cares about Purdue (he's from Indiana). His engaging style encourages collaboration and discussion, so you're basically studying while learning.

Got an A in this class and a C in 30301. The content in this class was harder, but Wolanski makes it easier to understand. He is very caring and really wants you to do good. Go to his office hours and get help on homework it makes the class a lot easier. Do not wait to do the homework last minute it takes a lot of time. He is an amazing professor!
Inconsistent policy enforcement. If you're having a tough semester and end up with him, good luck.
Go to class and you will understand the material. He allows 1 regular sized note sheet each exam. The exams are very similar to the practice exams. He is a great professor.

I hate stats so I dreaded this class but Prof Wolanski made up for it. He is very into what he teaches and wants to see his students succeed. He knows that this class is a pathway course for a lot of classes and thats the only reason students take it so he tries to make everything as painless as possible.

Professor Wolanski is one of the best professors I have had at Krannert. I struggle a ton with Stats and he really helped break down the material and made it a lot easier when studying. He really takes feedback to heart and is really great. Overall, one of the best professors and deserves an award.

If you go to lectures and office hours, there is no reason you should do bad in this class. Professor Wolanski is great, he really cares about how you do in the class and that shows in office hours. Lecture material is exactly like the homeworks, quizzes, and exams. Goodluck.
If you like yourself, then you ask for this guy. Cares about the material and each student in his class.

He knows what he is doing, and he tries his best to make it as easy as it can be while not simplifying too much. Homework takes a while, but they're just once weekly. Exams are OK, 15 multiple choice questions don't sound bad, but you have to know the material, just like every other class. Not a class to stress about, which I liked.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: He is such an amazing prof. He really cares about every single student he has. He makes sure that he know everyone's name and gets to know them. I would say go to office hours because homework can be challenging. Another piece of advice I would give is to make sure you to to lecture because he goes over everything that you need to know.

He really cares about his students and is very helpful when you have questions. Attendance is taken at random, but I recommend showing up every class because the lectures really help you with assignments and exams.
Really good prof. 305 is a lot easier than 30301. There was no surprised on exams and he provides enough practice material. Listens to what students give feedback on
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Great teacher, good response time, good lecturer, nice guy, no complaints

Overall very good professor. Tough grader on the homeworks, but provides lots of help in office hours. Exam review is so helpful as he asks the class what THEY want help on. There is random attendance, but go to class anyway as the in-class examples are super helpful

Wolanski clearly cares about his students and the material. I struggled in the stats class required before this class but Wolanski makes the material very understandable and he is patient when explaining. On top of that, he just seems like an all around sweet and caring guy. Hoping he continues to teach this class for other students!

Professor Wolanski is great! One of the better professors I've had in my time at Purdue. Very organized, and communicates his expectations very well which is much appreciated. Fantastic lectures allow him to break down complicated concepts in a very effective way. He also has consistent office hours which are very helpful.

He is a great professor I took him his first semester @ Purdue and he paid attention to the students wants/needs very well. Anything that you asked him to change he would. The only problem I had was that he gave us a practice exam that was nothing like the exam meaning that everyone studied the practice exam failed, but he listened and changed.

Just make sure to get 100% on the homework by going to his office hours and asking questions. Study for the exams and quizzes and you'll have no problem getting an A. If you don't understand something, don't dwell on it and go straight to office hours to understand. He always responds to emails and is helping students.

Professor Wolanski is a bright professor who clearly has a passion for the content and his job. He is very accessible via office hours and email. He is willing to be flexible on homework grades (a large portion of the final grade) within reason. Get ahead on homeworks and use office hours to help and you will do fine. Very approachable and caring.

Professor Adams is passionate, but it lacks in the class structure. The exams are trivia-style questions rather than testing your understanding of computer architecture. Expect to self-study for this class and spend time reading the textbook, as lectures aren't very helpful. You'll learn a lot this way, but don't expect a good grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: He just reads everything from the textbook during lectures. His lab setup is pretty bad, as it absolutely does not prepare you well for the courses later down the road (CS252). The avg for the final exam was a 55%. He will ask you the stuff you'd find in the corner of a textbook during midterms and finals. Bro also argues on Ed...

By far the most disorganized of the core CS classes. GBA is clearly passionate about the topic and his lectures are serviceable, but he runs the course horribly. Homeworks don't help you learn the material, labs don't have you meaningfully applying what you learn, and exams are terribly worded and vague. Snarky and unhelpful on ED. Generous Curve.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: GBA is by far the worst professor I've had at Purdue. The labs are poorly worded, badly designed, and fail to encourage meaningful application of the material. The exams are similarly flawed, using convoluted wording that tests English grammar more than Computer Architecture. He is also condescending toward students on Ed ("unask the question").

Professor Adams's class was without structure. To do good you had to memorize the entirety of his ridiculously long online textbook. He is clearly passionate, but gets off track without clear goals. Everything was through "dint of student effort"; practice exam released the day before, no solutions, refused to answer anything on EdDiscussion posts.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: He's the kind of professor everyone should have at least once.

The exam questions were ambiguous and unclear. Even if you know the material well, the wording makes it difficult to succeed. There were issues in midterm grading and labs being unfair across sections.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: By far the worst professor I've had at Purdue. The labs for the majority of the class were answering questions on Word docs. Instructions were confusingly worded and unclear. We never once did any coding whatsoever. Exams were basically a test of how well you can interpret his questions. I don't know anyone that got an A in the class.

Sometimes rude/unhelpful on Ed Discussion. Several questions worded poorly, don't properly test understanding. Accepts question appeals once in a blue moon. Doesn't communicate with lab TAs, they give contradicting advice. Lectures help but not much better than textbook. Near impossible A. Very frustrating experience. 1/5 because he deserves it.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Labs and exams were mistake prone. Test questions were often ambiguous or on subtle nuances, you had to guess how Adams interprets the wording of the question. Lectures help but just don't prepare you well enough. Office hours and appointments were relatively good. Not 1/5 but I'm counterbalancing the guy who gave 5/5 to balance other 1/5s.

Avoid him when possible. He makes it nearly impossible to get an A. Exam questions are poorly written and test on his "interpretation" of the question, a majority of which are vague. Not a very kind person either as he is unwilling to help students besides only answering questions about lecture content. Doesn't seem like he enjoys teaching anymore.

- you have to self learn everything on your own, his lectures are extremely boring
- extremely opposed to answering ed discussion posts when you ask him for general help
- writes exam questions 2am the night before the exam
- answers on exam are wrong and he still tries to argue that he's right
- nearly impossible to get an A, < 5 students did

Exam questions are more reading comprehension and most questions attempt to trick the test taker, not actually test any knowledge on computer architecture. Professor thinks he's smarter than everyone else, and makes this known on Edstem by being demeaning and passive aggressive, and not actually answering any questions. Avoid, even if required.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Tests feel like you're on a gameshow trying to win a million dollars, except there are 5 answer choices instead of 4. Options like "cannot be determined" or "insufficient evidence" often show up on super ambiguous questions where you have to guess what he's actually asking. Dude tanked everyone's GPA this semester.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Lectures felt boring, and he goes on random side quests by overly explaining content. The lectures don't feel like a review of the actual content covered in the textbook, which made me not go to this lectures ever.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Adams 250 felt more like a guessing game than a class. Textbook answers were misleading and marked wrong on exams; exams did not cover material gone over in class; and lectures were mostly Adams rambling about random computer history that was not relevant to the class.
Labs are completely disorganized with no clear direction from TAs or the professor, tests are incomprehensible, and resources are insufficient.

Worst professor at Purdue. Argues with students even when they are right and he is wrong. Refuses to reward any credit back on tests even if the key is wrong or the question is clearly poorly written. Mocks students when they ask questions. Final was incredibly difficult, barely covered the new content, questions were essentially riddles. Avoid.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: While Prof. Adams is clearly passionate about what he teaches and is a generally good lecturer, he has by far some of the WORST tests I have ever seen. Focuses on testing whether you remember obscure trivia rather than if you truly understand the material. Furthermore, the tests are structured to be ambiguous and an english comprehension test.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Instead of being tested on our mastery of computer architecture concepts, exam questions were merely poorly-worded trivia. I do not find it fair for my answer to depend on what I THINK the professor meant when writing the question! Because of that, exam preparation was ineffective, and the course was completely unenjoyable.

For a class I was looking forward to taking, Adams did a great job at ruining that experience. The class is a disorganized mess entirely based on reading a textbook with very little to no opportunity to apply the topics we learn in class. The labs started off as enjoyable, but then devolved into filling out Word documents with major errors.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: People complain about Adams/CS 250 a lot yet the lecture hall is only 20% filled every time and their idea of studying is "reading the textbook" and wondering why they don't perform well. Adam's tests are admittedly hard to understand due to wording, but you can do well if you actually apply yourself. GO TO LECTURE. IT HELPS.

The worst Professor I've had the displeasure of enduring. No amount of study, knowledge, or preparation helps. I thought I'd already taken SCLA, but clearly this class is another req, since it is based on your understanding of his English comprehension. This course was so poorly run that he got more questions wrong on his midterm than I did.
Does not care about the success of students, stubborn.

Professor George B. Adams writes the tests for the course based on trivia and trick questions, rather than testing on the material taught in lecture. All study content provided for the exams is completely unrelated to the form the exams take. He seems to lack a comprehensive vision for the way the course is ran, and the students are left to suffer.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Easily the worst professor I've had at Purdue. Read the textbook.
Good prof, lots of experience. But for example make sure you read the book he provides! Greate lecture but topic is broad
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Inspirational--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Lectures are primarily him putting the textbook on screen and reading directly from it, while labs are filled with errors. Exams are written in a way that does not test comprehension of course material; instead, they seem designed to confuse. Dr. Adams consistently replies to Ed posts in a patronizing tone, as though talking down to students.

It was a pretty chill class, and Prof Adams was an interesting lecturer. He gave lots of extra info to contextualize things better. While he would sometimes go on tangents, they didn't take away too much from the lectures. Overall, I enjoyed the class content, and homeworks and exams were pretty straightforward.

If you did the labs and HWs you basically got a free 40% in the class.
Meaning your grade was entirely based on how you did on the 60% that were exams.
Exams were hard but overall fairly graded. Adams was open to regrades if justified.
His lectures were some of the best I have had at Purdue.
The course itself is just hard.
I now have PTSD whenever I see anyone wearing a black mask because of Adams.

The worst professor I've ever had. Horrible at lecturing & goes off about random topics, super vague about what is going to be tested until the last minute. Always behind on releasing stuff. The worst is that he answers questions (specifically on Ed) with backhanded & rude comments, ignoring the actual question and refusing to tell the answer.
George Adams was a fabulous lecturer. There were times when he'd go off on a tangent talking about hardware, but overall he was a well equipped professor.

Professor Adams has a passion for the material, but his lectures can be a bit dry. Still, if you stay engaged and use resources like the textbook and unstuck AI, you can do well. The course itself isn't too difficult, especially if you have prior coding experience.

Smart, friendly, and very knowledgeable too, even about CS182 concepts. 
Unsure why some people say he doesn't teach. The slides were there, he happily answered any questions, and people comfortably responded to his questions in class. If you have to stoop that low to leave a 1 rating and say he doesn't teach, sorry but I don't think CS is for you.

bro this man does not do anything. Everything is based off of TAs. His lectures teach you nothing that will be on the test. If you take him do NOT fall behind or you'll be cooked. No notes were ever posted, and studying for exams is ACTUALLY IMPOSSIBLE
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: A very passionate and friendly person. He does tend to go into hardware due to his ECE background, but is goodly theoretical. This allows us to learn the theory behind seemingly easy concepts, so that when newer changes come, we can adapt easily.
Mysterious in a good way. Great explanations into hardware and the homeworks felt like I was building stuff on my own, making me a better programmer. He's also very helpful and understands jokes.

Very knowledgeable and passionate although his lectures for 180 were kind of dry since he would go on tangents about hardware. It was interesting but it's not helpful for those who didn't understand that class material. He is very approachable and was a pretty good professor for CS180 who answered questions very well.
Had him for 182. Just switch to someone else if you do too. 182 is an extremely difficult class on its own, but his lecture style made it that much harder.

Attendance was mandatory but the lectures were difficult to follow because he often went on tangents about material unrelated to what was actually supposed to be taught in the course. Other than that, he seemed like a nice guy and the homework was manageable. Very easy especially if you took AP CSA.

His lectures were fine. Heavily text based but he does drawings to help sometimes. Homeworks have readmes that give clear instructions. Exam materials are very much covered in class. People rating this professor poorly probably have a skill issue!
No Bueno.

His lectures could be worded better, as they were not easy to follow even as someone who took AP CS. He does talk about hardware a bit and goes on tangents, which makes it difficult to pay attention in class consistently. If you have coding experience with java, then taking cs180 with him isn't too bad.
Severus Snape reincarnated. Dry lectures, poorly worded questions and unclear grading criteria.

Adam's is quite monotone and likes to talk about hardware a lot (which is mostly unnecessary in object oriented programming), but CS180 itself isn't bad, especially if you have prior coding experience. Just start the homework and projects early and be aware of your group for projects 4 & 5, as they're worth a lot of your grade and a lot of work.

While he does tend to go on tangents and talk about hardware and memory in an introductory class like CS180, he is overall a fine lecturer and managed to stay on pace. For the course in general, keep on top of the weekly homeworks and start your group project early.
He tends to digress and talk about hardware and his time at NASA, but overall a good lecturer.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: Honestly, I'm not really sure why he gets so much hate. I honestly thought that he was fine. CS 25000 is lecture heavy, and as long as if you go to class and pay attention and take good notes, you should be fine. He also gave us more than a 10 percent curve for the Spring 2023 semester, so that's even better.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor often went on tangents unlike the other CS180 professors.

Dude hasn't matured since high school. Doesn't have an ounce of respect in his body, talks to students like pests. I don't understand why he doesn't quit his job, he clearly hates what he does. VILE attitude in discussion boards. He's the type to say "uhhh technically, " when you ask a question, with extra disrespect, instead of just answering you.

Extremely passive aggressive in class discussions. Is clearly unleashing some internal issues on students. So far the rudest professor I've had, seriously needs to spend some time learning how to treat people with respect. Loves reminding everyone that he knows the topics and they don't. I learned more from youtube videos than from his lectures.

Rude. In a regrade request (i verified my answer with an IDE so I know I was correct), I said "i believe my answer is correct," and this snarky, rude man replies: "Algorithms are not evaluated on anyone's belief. The are evaluated based on analysis." What a childish response by George, a full grown man, btw. Oh, and he didn't give me my points back

Amazing lectures, extremely passionate about the subject. Class itself is very content-heavy and hard to master, but he does a great job delivering information in an interesting way. Some questions on exams and homeworks are difficult to comprehend, and can sometimes be a bit ambiguous. Generous curve given at the end of the class made up for it.
