reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Test heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Adams teaches a difficult course that is difficult to fully master. He understands that topics are difficult and unfamiliar and while he does not decrease the difficulty of his exams, he does give a fair grade that reflects the effort you put in.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Test heavy

reviews.comment: I think everyone else here has done him about justice. Tests are brutal and lectures aren't always approachable or easy to follow. He's a fun, passionate guy but his passion just wears off after the first few weeks.

I'm in Prof. Adams's GenEd class, CS101. He's clearly upset that he teaches an easy class, and tries so hard to make it difficult. His questions on tests are like riddles: he does not care if you know the material, he just likes to trick students in his questions and take points off. I have an A, and he refused my homework that was 15 minutes late.
Wrote tests in riddles and lectures were very dry and boring.

Adams definitely knows the subject well and is passionate about it. His tests are pretty much just memorization but the homeworks aren't that bad. Reading the book helps a lot. The wording of questions is pretty bad though: They are worded so confusingly for no reason. His lectures are pretty good though they can sometimes be boring.

Great at lecturing and brings a lot of energy to the lessons, and overall is very clear in his explanations of the topics. However, he cannot write good exam questions to save his life. Often, the questions are just unclear or poorly written and he does not respond well to criticism on that front (Reluctant to change his mind, but not impossible).

Questions that read more like riddles than actual questions, awful homeworks, extremely poorly designed tests are all components of this course. While I think that the professor is genuinely passionate about the material, he ruins it entirely by how he assesses knowledge. Condescending and unhelpful replies do not help either.

Prof. Adams is clearly passionate about CompA! But, be sure to DEFINITELY attend lectures since his slides aren't easy to understand without lecture context. Also, his exams are memorization-heavy, so study ALOT and know the content extremely well (use office hours!). Only 2 midterms + 1 final so do really well. Don't rely too much on a curve. GL

Adams is very passionate about his subject and you can definitely see it in his lectures. But be prepared to put in hours towards his class.  His labs get hard fast, and doing them helps you understand certain aspects of the course in depth, but still be wary of his exams. You just need to memorize everything to do well on his exams.

Prof. Adams has an often vague way of asking questions on homeworks and exams which cause unnecessary confusion. He also does a little trolling. However, he is a passionate and available professor and has a sense of humor. I'd advise anyone taking 250 with him to read the textbook often: that ended up helping me keep up / prepare for exams.

Enthusiastic lecturer and knows a lot of about comp arch. However, his homework are written in the very confusing terms, and when we ask on discussion forum, he replies with passive aggressive answers. MC/FR exam requires you to memorize terms mentioned orally in the lecture. Decided on no curve with 57% exam avg then changed mind last minute.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: bad

People complaining are crybabies in CS who cannot survive themselves and need a pack.
Enrolling in a course does not guarantee you won't fail. Instead of barking on discord and being radical, try attending townhalls arranged by USB.  Mere high school graduates pumped to rally and write a letter to the HOD and not following a complaint hierarchy.

This guy is an actual nutcase. You cannot understand his lectures the first go and have to decipher his homework and test problems before actually understanding and solving them. Also, a huge dick about grading, didn't round my grade by .07% to give me a higher letter grade. Try to avoid if you can
I liked his course. No curve but you get what you deserve.
This guy is horrible. Just terrible. 57% final average like too many ppl failed the course, 0 curve, around 4 ppl with an A. I dont know what he is doing but he's bad but only professor for 250
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Our final had a 57 average. The highest was an 85. We had no curve.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The homework and exams are confusing to the point where the TAs aren't even able to assist without studying the question and answer key. This class desperately needs a curve but none is given. Though Adams is frequently on the discussion board, his answers are arrogant and do not address the question that is passed by the student. Terrible class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The wording on his tests are not very clear, and he gives out no study guides in order to help prepare for his brutal tests.

A nice guy and generally, like his lectures although they could be boring at times. The big is that the way he writes questions is just so confusing. He will make the simplest question feel like you don't know how to read. And then the way he tests does not reflect how he emphasizes things in class. asks about the most random little things.

Just very confusing in the way he answers questions, explains topics, and writes assignments. Also, the way he answers students' questions often feels very rude. He either talks down to us like answers should be obvious or give a convoluted response making it difficult to have questions answered. 

TLDR: Smart dude but very difficult.
He goes by his lectures slides extremely quickly. It's hard to know what to take notes of. On homeworks, labs, and tests, the instructions given are sometimes ambiguous and confusing.

He's quite proud of his ability to rewrite his exams from scratch each year, regardless of how badly he words each question or doesn't provide his students a way to study for his exams. In addition, too many of the exam questions are so badly written that there are often > two interpretations available, which are too easy to confuse.

Let me preface this by saying that he's an enthusiastic prof. who's passionate about what he's teaching. However, his lectures are incredibly confusing with too many points crammed into one slide. His questions are horribly worded and are designed to trick you instead of aiding your comprehension. Otherwise interesting class ruined by a bad prof.

By far the worst cs professor I have had, the most boring dry lectures to go along with poorly designed slides and scattered non-linear discussions. The questions on hw, labs, and exams is worded horrendously, like the he is trying to mess with your head instead of teaching. Sadly he was (and normally is) the only CS250 professor, 1/5

My least favorite professor in the CS department. Boring, dry lectures, precariously worded questions on assignments, and tedious labs. Unfortunately he is the gatekeeper of CS 250; when I took this course, he was the only professor you could choose.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment:
Run as soon as possible.
He will use any of confusing word expressions in homework, quiz, and exam.

He is indeed a good lecturer and enthusiastic to teach you. I appreciate this side of him. However, the grading for 101 class is insane. A starts from 94 for no reason
He never truly admits his mistake. Exams are just insanely tricky; even though you have studied, he will find a way to let you down if you are looking for a grade.

Adams gives great lectures with energy and examples, and I found that I learned much, much more in his class than the other CS250 sections. However, he is not very good at answering questions, he is stubborn about regrading, and his homework and exam questions are worded with the intent to confuse. The curve was about a full letter grade.

Professor Adams seems like a cool guy with lots of knowledge and enthusiasm about his subject! But his style of writing quiz questions often leads confusion about the intent of the problem. Probably one of the hardest CS classes I've taken so far (more so than 381) because of that. I only would take a course with him again due to his expertise.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Our average for this semester was lower than previous semesters and he did not give us any curve even though previous semesters had such a good curve. Also, he does not know how to teach for his life, in his lectures he will go off on random tangents and talk about INTEL or DELL or some random stuff that's not related to the lecture at all.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Did not teach well and only about 12 people out 300 would show up for his lectures. Don't expect any curves because he only curves if the grade average of the class is low.

One of the best people in the CS dept hands-down. Very fun to talk to, incredibly active either on Campuswire or in his numerous office hours, and very willing to help. However, has some of the toughest, most cryptic, and easily misinterpreted questions I ever saw. It mostly feels like RNG when I am solving quizzes. Not to mention a ton of reading.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Get ready to read--Tough grader

reviews.comment: His grade is exetremly tough. Less than three percent got A thus semester cut off for A is ninety-four, while no one got more than ninety five percent. There was less than one percent curve if you are not in C range. He is good lecturer, but I highly recommend to take with other professor since you will get better grade with less workload.

Avoid at all costs. He never answers questions from students in a clear way, always answering with something cryptic, and you can never negotiate with him about anything. If he makes a mistake on a quiz/exam, he will just get defensive and try his best to gaslight you into thinking you were the one who made a mistake. Please avoid I'm begging you.

First I would like to say that this professor knows his information like the back of his hand. However, if you have a question for this class don't bother asking unless you want to decode the answer. Every answer to a question is either another question or a form of the answer that is worded in a way that makes you somehow more confused.

Terrible professor, avoid at all costs. He refuses to help students out when they have questions, and when he does answer them, Adams will make you more confused or even guide you in the wrong direction. Also, if you go to office hours for help, he will turn you away, saying he is too busy.

Makes understandable concepts more confusing, doesn't answer questions with an answer (basically leaving students more confused than they already were), makes quizzes extremely hard, and has unrealistic expectations from students coding in assembly for the first time in lab. Not to mention the mass amounts of reading along with 3 lectures a week.

He's sarcastic and makes you more confused when you ask him questions. He's not a bad lecturer but the way he conducts class is simply arrogant and does not inspire passion in what is being taught. Furthermore, his language when asking questions in homework is simply bizarre, so much so that native English speakers are confused.

He sucks. Doesn't teach anything for labs and never answers questions clearly. He will always just answer your question with a question, I am convinced he has no clue what he is talking about. Hope you don't get him.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: The content of this class isn't actually that hard, but they way he explains everything is terrible. He speaks in an overcomplicated way during lectures, gives useless non-answers to even the simplest of questions, and accepts no feedback on anything. Homework problems end up being simple after taking hours to decipher the confusing language.
Professor Adams is a great lecturer and a fascinating professor to talk to. He's always accessible for questions and gives great feedback as well.

His lectures are very good, he explains things very clearly and is very engaging to listen to. However, everything else in the course is anything but. Many homework questions make no sense. If you ask him a question he will always reply sarcastically and never give an actual answer. Quizzes and exams are intentionally worded confusingly.

This class was a bit of a mess. I will admit, I think Professor Adams is a great lecturer. It's just the way he writes his questions that makes the course crazy hard. The material itself is not that bad, but rather trying to figure out how he would expect us to answer a question. I didn't get an A from studying, but rather luck.

This is frustrating. Prof Adams is a stellar lecturer, however, his exams have a penchant for bizarre, counter-intuitive readings used which often result in a low exam average. This is often (partially) offset by a generous curve at the end of the semester, but it's frustrating enough to merit the score given.

This class was definitely one of the hardest out of the core CS classes. As others have said, his lectures are pretty good for the most part, but they can still be boring at times. The homeworks/labs/mini-exams weren't easy and part of the reason for that was a lack of clear concise wording. The final exam was terribly hard, but he did curve well.

One of the most organized and caring professors in the CS department. Spends a lot of time responding to student questions and making quality lecture videos. His style each semester is making brutally hard exams, but making up for it with a thick curve at the very end.

Worst professor ever. If you think Turkstra is bad, wait till you get George. Extremely unfair with grading. He can never admit that he's wrong. He literally changed the grading distribution 3/4's into the semester, making us do and long and tedious assignment that ended up not counting for our grade.

Lectures fine, but the wordings on his assignments and tests are bizarre that he even admitted his class is a low-key English class. He always asserts himself to be right and is unwilling to compromise whenever he makes mistakes, such as when people get something wrong on the homework because of an error in the lecture slides. Avoid if possible.

Don't get me wrong he's an amazing lecturer and seems very passionate about the class in general. However, his exams are worded horrendously. There are a consistent amount of students who simply get the question wrong because of the wording in the question. This class should fulfill our English requirement for the college of science.

Amazing lecturer. Labs were okay, the TA's for my lab were really bad so that kind of ruined my experience. Exams are a crapshoot. In office hours, he sometimes he gets his own past exam questions wrong because they are worded so vaguely.
The worst course I have taken. It's too difficult.

For CS182, Adams explains things concisely and in an easy to understand manner. I do not think he does the same for CS250. He is obviously passionate about hardware, but it is difficult to understand his lectures where he seems to skim over everything and provide little detail. His exams and HW are also not representatives of lectures.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Prof. Adams was the best professor I had in Purdue CS, and his class was the most difficult I took. If you actually want to learn, his lectures are incredible and he goes out of his way to help students succeed. Really knowledgeable and had fair exams.

I dropped out of CS because I was bad at it and unmotivated. The lecturers felt dead and the material was hard and I wasn't interested at all. Adams was the exception. He loved teaching and inspired me to learn more about the subject. I'll remember him as an amazing person in CS.

Took CS250 in s18 and worked for him as a TA for the next three semesters. Adams is a fantastic lecturer, and a genuinely nice guy. Shows a lot of passion for the topics. He expects a lot out of hist students. Exams are difficult, homeworks are difficult, a couple labs are hard. Homeworks are taken straight from the textbook.

His lectures are entertaining though I don't like hardware. However, his exams are very difficult. I mean, it is hard to understand what he is really trying to ask. Anyway he gave a big curve so I ended up with an A although I got 66% in the final.

Professor Adams has an almost tangible enthusiasm for computer hardware and is up to date with all hardware happenings. He once explained the difference in cpu speed and # of cycles to retrieve memory from disk. CPU being 10^6 times as fast is like a jet travelling 1k m/sec waiting on a snail at 1m/hour. Will have to read the textbook to pass well.

By far the most overrated professor at Purdue. Don't understand why so many people like him. Only his delivery is good, his content and exams suck. He doesn't slow down at all and his lectures are quite frankly hard to understand. He assumes that everyone is as passionate as him and thinks everyone understands hardware the same way he does.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: Adams is one of the best lecturers I have had at Purdue. However, the test are truly insane. Be prepared to study like crazy for weeks for the faintest chance of getting a 60. You just gotta do your best and pray for a curve.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: His lectures are entertaining. He's very good at casting doubt on what you think you know on the test. The homeworks/labs make you work for your grade. He has lots to share on computer architecture so if you get the chance to ask him about his work, do so!

He's a great professor, and very willing to have conversations and explain material to you. He's very, very interested in the material, even if its a little dry. He's very enthusiastic. Class material is still hard.
Just amazing.

Prof. Adams is a fantastic, passionate lecturer and devotes a lot of time outside of class to holding office hours and helping students. He's an expert in the material and explains it very well at an introductory level, and he's very approachable. His tests and homeworks are quite tough, but he curves fairly.
reviews.rating_tags: ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Amazing lectures--Tests are tough

reviews.comment: Adams is a great lecturer and very passionate about Computer Architecture. His tests cover more in depth than the book or his lectures. Overall very solid course and professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures--Tests are tough

reviews.comment: The best.
Prof. Adams is great. His lectures are almost always interesting and enjoyable. He holds lots of office hours to answer questions, and he does give great answers.

This guy is awesome. He is very enthusiastic. Cs250 is hard. If you pay attention and read the textbook, you would not have any problem. He posts slides. You do not have to take notes. Exams are not straight forward. He is always available to answer your question.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: He's way too distracted while talking.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: Great professor. He's great at explaining things clearly and entertaining while doing so. Holds a ton of office hours and is very accessible outside of class. The class is difficult, but it's graded fairly and if you work hard you'll do fine. All in all, easily the best professor I've had at Purdue.
His slides is full of information and exams are not related to what was covered in class.

Adams is probably the best professor I've ever had. I've never met someone so willing to help--he holds a crazy number of office hours and answers questions on Piazza almost instantaneously. His lectures are really lively and he's a really funny guy--def worth going to office hours to get to know him. Not an easy class, but super worth it with him.

Great Professor! Enthusiastic and Knowledgeable. Go to his office hours. I wish I would have. He is someone you want to know. Take his class. The class is hard but if you try your best you will do well.

Phenomenal, often humorous lectures. In-depth, easy to understand explanation of content. Labs and homework are challenging, but force you to actually learn.  Thorough feedback is returned. Listens to the students and is available 24/7. Exams are reasonably difficult. Adams is a superhero among men. Only con is that his questions can be very vague.

Very enthusiastic, great professor overall. Ignoring the class, he is a great guy. You'll find that in class he is a nerd, during office hours he is overall interesting. The class itself is tough, it isn't the professor. He's always there and I've found that even at 11 p.m., he still is helping. If you need to take this class, GBA is the way to go.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: Lecture, homework and exam are not related at all.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: Adams is a great guy. He had an insane number of office hours, and is willing to go off syllabus to help you succeed if you ask nicely. He makes things clear. He's really proud of that book, you're going to have to read every assigned page twice and study the diagrams. He speaks slowly. If you decide to watch lectures online, make sure to keep up.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Caring

reviews.comment: His lectures are phenomenal and he explains topics very well. His questions are often very specific and tricky, to the point where the are unhelpful and do not accurately test the student's knowledge. He is fun-loving and will drop dry humor throughout lecture. If you argue for points using logic and the textbook, he will reward you.
CS250 was a hard class. Adams is very enthusiastic and expects you to know a lot. On exams, he tested on things he only mentioned once. Labs and homeworks do take a good amount of time as well.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Gives good feedback--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: He's a decent professor. I really like his passion for teaching and he is very helpful and enthusiastic. However, the lectures do get a little boring sometimes and his lecture slides are crammed with lots and lots of text.

Amazing person and burningly passionate for the subject he teaches, but unfortunately just does not teach the material very well. I know he tries so hard to make the lectures interesting as he floods with enthusiasm, but he doesn't do a good job of hinting what exactly you'll need to study and not which sucks cause there's so much material thrown
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Caring

reviews.comment: Professor Admas is a good lecturer, he is always willing to help and teach you new things, but he always leaves tons of formidable slides and readings for you to read and study. Exams are not that easy.

Really passionate about what he teaches, but because of his EE background, he goes way too in-depth on topics and rambles a lot during class. Exams are extremely hard even if you study and class has over 1000 slides for the final exam.  Too much material crammed into one course imo which makes retaining knowledge on what was taught difficult.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: He's not a good teacher at all. Lecture is boring. Lab is absurd. Too much homework. Exam material is not on HW or lab or textbook. You literally need be able to connect with God to know where to study. There also $40 textbook that looks like crap.
Amazing professor, amazing lecturer. Class is quite hard, but I'd go so far as to say it's worth it, because he's that good.

Professor Adam's is one of the best professor's I've had at Purdue. He comes from a ECE background and is a hardware guy so expect more detail in that regard than necessary. But his lectures are interesting and you can tell he cares about what he teaches. It's not a easy class at all but keep up and it will be a class where you learn a ton.
His test's do not test you over what you have learned or studied for. The material is fairly easy, so he likes to word the questions so they are all impossible to read or comprehend.
To me the exams felt quite hard and the class seemed to go into more depth than probably necessary. Mosfets and such especially in the beginning.

Pros: Makes every lecture interesting, really easy to understand and explains concepts really well, helpful if you don't understand something, actually cares about the material he's teaching.  Cons: (Mostly) weekly homework assignments

Professor Boersma is awesome. She makes an effort to foster a community in the class. You will become a much better speaker by the end of the class. As long as you participate and put effort into the assignments, you will do well. One of the most fun classes I've taken at this school.
She gave good feedback on my assignments and understood us as students. Also was very welcoming and everyone in my class liked her.

She made this class so fun by fostering a very nice community within the class. It was my favorite one this semester! Very presentation based, and you become way more comfortable presenting in front of the class by the end with feedback from her and your classmates. Just follow her instructions, and you'll be all set.

She is a very good COM teacher. She makes everything that we learn very relatable. The class focuses more on presentations with feedback from your peers. The class is pretty laid-back as long as you do what is required of you. She makes the class very fun!

Cari runs an easy yet fair class. as long as you do the quizzes and prepare a little for presentation you can get a A. The class atmosphere is lively, and I looked forward to going to class every day.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Funny, talkative, relatable. Cari inspired a culture of community within the class and made it one I looked forward to each week! Highly recommend.

(Actually took COM217) She's a lively woman. She keeps the class engaging and pretty simple. She makes the course more presentation-oriented than other classes but that's a strength for me instead of having to write more papers. As long as you participate and play nice with her games then she's great!
An awesome teacher!

She was a great teacher.  She really cares about all her students and wants to see them succeed.  She makes the class fun and engaging.  If you need anything, just reach out to her, and I am sure she would be very accommodating.  It should be an easy A if you put effort into presentations and do decent on the quizzes (online).
Grading is kinda random honestly.

Her class is really laid back and a lot of fun if you get a good group of classmates.  She doesn't grade too hard and really encourages all the students, letting everyone know what they've improved on each presentation.  As long as you put in a little bit of effort and do the extra credit, it's an easy A.

Com 114 is already a pretty easy class. I had Boersma for the honors section of Com 114 and it was definitely my easiest class. I've heard other professors grade more easily, but I still did well on all my presentations (A average). For context, I usually started the presentations the day before.

Very much a chill and easy professor as long as you do what you are supposed to and take her feedback on your speeches. Our class often got distracted by outside conversation, which made it very fun and laidback. So crack a joke or two with your classmates and you'll have fun in this class.

I was struggling very much my freshman year of class and Cari was absolutely the best help and was the most understanding teacher I have met. Not only did she help me with all my class needs but also with my outside mental needs when I felt like I had no one else.

She is an awesome professor. She cares about her students outside of the classroom and doesn't grade too hard on the speeches. If you do your work you will get an A in the class. She is also very funny and has good stories.

An amazing professor! she really seems to care about her students. Always gave good feedback on speeches and told me what i needed to do in order to improve. As long as you put in effort and show improvement you most likely will get a good grade. Each week you have a couple of assignments and then a lecture quiz and a chapter reading quiz.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Participation matters

reviews.comment: I originally did not have my speech schedule included from my course requests, so I just signed up for Boersma's class out of the blue. I'm glad that I did. She is a wonderful professor who was understanding and her made expectations very clear. If you do the assignments and use her feedback for your speeches, you will get the grade you want.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: lectures are very easy to understand. you will leave lecture feeling like you understand what is going on instead of studying for hours trying to figure out what the bullet points are saying. very concise and engaging

yes you have to put in a lot of work in and study a lot, but the more work you put in, the better results you'll get with his lectures! i learned so much from hudmon and he made me excited about biochem. he cares so much about our learning and explains very well by breaking topics down into digestible materials.

He made this class bearable, his lectures had structure and had enough to keep you focused while not overwhelmed. His tests come directly out of class material and makes efforts to help students out on his free time.

Would take again, wish he taught the majority of class as well.

Dr. Hudmon is the one redeemable factor in this mess of a class. He works hard to make sure we are understanding his material, which can be difficult at times. However, his exams are extremely fair in comparison to Krusemark's and he pulls directly from the slides. Exam review sessions are helpful, given he actually answers questions unlike others.

Dr. Hudmon is amazing and picked up the slack for his co-lecturer Dr. Krusemark. Extremely resourceful and caring about students understanding material. His content is tough, but thats just Biochem, he did all he could to ensure we understood and were successful.

Hudmon seems to really care about his students and explains lecture in way for us to understand. His exam questions come straight from the slides or iClicker questions. He emphasized important information that we needed to know (like for homework or exams). He rarely fell behind in slides and if he ever did, he made extra videos to compensate.

Dr. Hudmon is the only reason this class is bearable. His lectures are much easier to follow and he takes time to ensure we are understanding the material. Exam content is pulled straight from the slides and he will even recycle past exam questions. If he was the course coordinator rather than Krusemark, student performance would be way better.

Dr. Hudmon is SO much better than Dr. Krusemark. Don't be discouraged by the horror that is exam one, once you reach Hudmon's content things become a lot more clear. Dr. Hudmon is extremely knowledgeable and seems to care a lot more about his impact on students, he will tell you the key takeaways you need to know for the exams and future courses.

Was WAY better than Krusemark when teaching MCMP 208. His slides were definitely hard to follow, but if you went to lecture and paid attention/took notes, they made sense. His exam questions were a lot more straightforward and everything was pulled from the slides (as it should be)!

I am the type of student who studies so hard and maintains a 4.0 til my junior year. Don't take this class plz. The professor doesn't know what he is talking about and speed-reads all his slides. There are around 400 slides you need to memorize per exam and a total of 1200 slides for your cumulative final. VERY DETAILED exams too.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Test heavy

reviews.comment:
It was disheartening to see Hudmon misjudging students' talents. 
What concerned me even more was his lack of support when students faced an abusive environment under other PIs in different labs. His failure to stand up for his students during such distressing situations demonstrates a lack of integrity.

This class is nothing but a train wreck and a professor who refuses to upload answer keys for study guides does nothing but make it worse. I can't express my distain for this class and professor in words.
He is a funny man and cares about students. If you communicate with him, he will always help you. If you have questions, don't hesitate to ask him.

Biochem is one of the hardest classes I have taken. Dr. Hudmon is very nice for students who want to know more and ask questions, go to his office hour and he will explain things clearly to you. The exam is hard and needs a lot of time to study.

Most of the class is spent expressing his opinion. I thought he would get better in a few weeks, but as the class progresses, he is expressing his opinion more and more. The test was also based on his opinion. Mentoring students is the worst part, and he, ignoring the facts, is ending g graduates students' lives again with his opinions.

He was a great professor even though it was his first time teaching the class. His lectures are engaging and he will ask the class questions throughout lecture. He is very accessible at his office hours and he was always very helpful at answering questions.

Prof. Ameranis is goated! I found value in his lectures, and his style of teaching is very open and flexible. The accent was a bit hard to understand at times, but otherwise no complaints. Exams were tricky, but nicely curved. Do get started early on the projects tho. Also, his lectures on graphs & hashing were some of the best ive ever seen

Professor Ameranis speaks clearly. Even though he is "new" at teaching, he gets to the point in lectures, he is not monotone or repetitive which makes the 50 minute lectures a breeze. If you have never programmed in your life, well lets just say you will need to put in a lot more effort than those kids who have been programming since 6th grade.

He's a new professor, but by no means unexperienced. I really enjoyed the setup of the lectures, as the concepts explained in class clicked very quickly apposed to if I had to miss a class and learn the information outside of class. 

Main thing: If you show up to classes, put in effort in class, and think critically you will do well.
aced everything and still hate her
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Incredibly difficult to understand and grades unfairly. Uncooperative with students and refuses to help push students in the right direction.
professor is not good at explaining things in the lecture and isnt very engaging

Project instructions were consistently vague, and the final project was graded unfairly. Feedback was returned too late to incorporate, yet most of the class received unnecessarily low grades. Points were deducted for not being “creative” despite using queries taught in class. TA guidance also conflicted with grading.

Poorly organized, hard to follow. She had 1 assginment in Excel and 2 in Tableau. The course is a TABLEAU class, there shouldn't be Excel assignments! Spacing between due dates was bad. GRILLED project presentation groups in front of all students. Her grading was inconsistent, there was hidden grading criteria which many people got points off for.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Doesn't know how to speak properly, cannot understand
Hard to follow during lectures. Class was poorly structured.
She does not know how to teach, consistent issues and wrong queries/data taught.

Dr. Bokrand was a great professor and a lecturer who wasn't boring to listen to for the 50-minute class time. There were reviews and homework for each chapter learned, and there are 3 total exams (including the final). The exams are difficult if you don't understand the material.
Not a ton of homework. Very helpful slideshow and materials on Brightspace. Prof was very nice.

I took Dr. Bokrand's accounting class and thought she was a good professor. Some material was confusing at first, but her explanations and review days helped a lot. Homework was challenging but useful, and exams reflected what she reviewed.

Great professor, the notes style is not the most useful. Provided is fill in the blank notes. I believe handwriting them is better. Be sure to use the book and the practice provided for exam prep. Otherwise, a memorization heavy class.
Although the lectures can get boring, she gives lots of extra credit

I personally didnt find the class very interesting, but Dr. Bokrand was very passionate and knowledgeable about the topic. Additionally, she was very nice about extra credit, which really helps your grade as the exams are not easy. This class is straight forward, but you need to put in the effort to see the results.
Dr. Bokrand is kind and compassionate. She loves accounting. The class, however, is a little hard to sit thought sometimes. It gets boring, and you HAVE to pay attention at all times to succeed.
Kinda boring lectures but that might just be bc its accounting. but its an easy enough course that it doesn't matter and you can learn off the hw

She gives amazing bullet points on what to study for exams. The lectures are super boring, but that is more so accounting than her. Dr. Bokrand is super kind and passionate about what she does. She gives great resources to succeed, and extra credit here and there. The class is an easy GPA booster, even if you have trouble staying awake.
Well-spoken and passionate about accounting. Offers lots of extra credit and days off throughout the semester. If you get stuck on the homework, look at Piazza. Exams are all multiple choice.

This class was so boring. But, Dr. Bokrand clearly knows what she's talking about and truly has an interest in what she teaches. The class isn't too hard if you do the homeworks. Plus you get extra credit if you go to class enough.
She is a good professor, but she just talks really fast.
Her voice is magical! Can put anyone to sleep. Avoid
avoid if you value sanity

COM 204 is a challenging course, but really, if you go to class, follow the rubric and his grading scale closely, and consult Dr. Boyd and his TA's for help, you will succeed. Dr. Boyd is honest in his grading and his words, and wants his students to grow; however, a level of commitment and consistency is expected for this class.
Incredibly tough grader. He uses a 5-point scale for grading and if you meet all requirements on the rubric, you get a 3. Good luck, it's rough.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: While the class was okay I found myself losing some sanity over it. Every assignment is graded out of five. While the rubric is clear, it is very objective and opinion based. Major papers are graded on the five-point scale and then multiplied by 6 to get your grade out of 30. Same for the exams. DO NOT use Grammarly; he considers it AI.
I did NOT like his class, it made me question my sanity, and I felt stupid. GO TO CLASS he doesn't put the slides online
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Clear grading criteria--So many papers

reviews.comment: Really bad, my mental health went down the toilet during my time in his class. Super nitpicky
grading scale sucks and too hard of a grader made me lk cry every day
Class is rlly hard and grading is REALLY hard
Very friendly professor, you can tell he respects his students. He is a very tough grader though. It is hard to get an A on the speeches but he gives awesome feedback.

This class was difficult, but if you put in genuine effort you can get a good grade! Go to office hours on both major papers! I got a poor grade on the first paper, but still managed to get an A in the class, so don't give up. Don't ever skip lectures, no excuses. He does not post anything online! This being said, his class will teach you a lot.

This class is ridiculously nitpicky. I understand the need to teach students the importance of clarity when it comes to writing, but the way each assignment is graded makes it nearly impossible to succeed. Every assignment is graded out of 5 and, oftentimes, the grading is subjective under the guise of an objective, "clear rubric".

I recommend taking thorough notes during lectures--though attendance isn't mandatory you won't pass if you don't go. There are two major papers, and I suggest starting early to have your TA review them before the final submission. The class is interesting, but the grading is tough, and you must put in the effort to earn a good grade.

Prof Boyd does a great job of explaining material in lectures and is very clear about expectations. Help was made readily available for major papers, you were able to submit a draft for feedback, and exam review sessions were offered. If you attend lectures, take half-decent notes, and follow instructions you should have no issues getting a B
GO TO LECTURE, go to office hours when doing papers. exams online.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Amazing lectures --Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: While his class made me question my intelligence and wonder if he even wants his students to succeed in his class, he does give somewhat entertaining lectures. I would recommend letting him give you feedback on papers, and ALWAYS going to lecture as he doesn't upload his slideshows. Every assignment is graded on a 5 pt scale, so put in effort!
reviews.rating_tags: Beware of pop quizzes--Respected--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Oh boy, this class is HARD. You absolutely have to show up to every lecture. He will throw in pop quizzes or in class activities. He is understanding, but he will not listen to excuses. Read every word on the syllabus and assignments. Talk to him about every assignment and go to office hours. You will succeed in this class, but start strong!

Dr Boyd was a great professor, however I really disliked the course, as someone in fye, it was difficult for me to write at the high level he expected but through the course of the semester I learned a lot. Attending lectures is the most important part of this class because he does not record or post notes.

It was very clear that he wanted you to succeed. At the start of the class, there are a ton of reading assignments paired with a quiz and a lot of them are loooong, so make sure to start them as soon as possible and keep up to date with them. In class, you have to participate in small impromptu speaking prompts but they're very low pressure.
literally awful grading system. hated this class made me want to cry every day.

Attendance isn't required but if you miss a class, you WILL be behind. This class was challenging and demanding. If you are a pre-comm major and this is one of your only comm classes, you will likely have more time to work on the assignments. If you're taking it in another major with heavy work, it's hard to manage. Be prepared!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Boyd and his TAs have very high expectations. You will be expected to do a lot of work, and really challenge your brain. At the same time, he is an amazing guy, very smart, engaging, tells funny stories, and is always willing to help. Dont skip class, and PLEASE go to office hours. It is a necessity.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Boyd is a very nice professor and knows a lot about what he talks about. My advice to you if you need to pass is to ask him about every homework or paper assignment. He has a very clear grading criteria and it is easy to miss one little thing and end up with a 2/5. Listen carefully to lectures and ASK QUESTIONS! Trust me, you need to.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Dr. Boyd is an excellent instructor who truly cares about each of his students. This class can be challenging at times, but he is always willing to give extra credit to help students. Each assignment is directly correlated with that weeks material and he works with each student individually to raise their grade. Overall, great course!

Nothing is good enough. Dont take this class unless you want to be degraded as a student. I have gotten As in all of my other writing classes and would consider myself a good writer. But in this class... nothing is adequate. The fact that it is a 200 level class is absurd. requirements and expectations are set up as if we are PhD students... dont
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Easily one of the most engaging professors and lecturers I have ever had. Boyd has taught this class for years and has perfected his craft. If you attend the classes and take the appropriate time to research and plan your papers, this will be one of the most enjoyable classes you will take. He is very gracious, but don't mistake that for indulgence

Before taking this class, I heard mixed results about it. I think the prof is super informative and gets into some of the theoretical concepts of communication. My advice: do not skip the lectures and go to as many office hours for your TA as possible. A lot of the content in the lectures are on the tests. Going to office hours helped me a ton.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: I was nervous about this class going into it, but Dr. Boyd is an amazing teacher. The class is a little tough, but as long as you attend classes and complete the assignments you will be fine!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: I LOVED Professor Boyd. I had heard bad things about Com 204, but once I went to the first class, he made me genuinely excited to get started. He gives plenty of time to do assignments and the advice he gives in office hours is amazing. Go. To. Office. Hours. He genuinely cares about his class and kept my ADHD brain engaged all semester!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Boyd is extremely intelligent and makes class enjoyable. This class is hard and very paper driven. With only 200 points, its important to go to class and ask questions if you need help.

Com 204 with Josh has been very challenging because most of your grade is determined by 2 papers that can only be 1,000 words. Overall, though, his lectures are so entertaining. Also, go to office hours to get your papers reviewed. He and the TAs give really valuable feedback!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: If you're a COM major, you have to pass this class. Josh is a really great teacher and so are the TA's. Go to every single class and recitation and go to office hours if you want to be sure you'll get a good grade. He's a tough grader but this is a really good class. Weekly quizzes and assignments & 2 major papers. Go into it with a good mindset.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: On the first day of class, Josh will make it clear that this course is difficult. Embrace it! He cares about his students and wants them to succeed. Get feedback on the major papers as early as possible, study the practice exams, and put at least some effort into the daily assignments. Do this, and you'll get a B or better no problem.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures--Tough grader

reviews.comment: I had him for COM 114 and he made a gen ed class seem really fun and enjoyable to go to. I looked forward to going to the class every other day and it was a great class to end my day with! Definitely recommend the Welcome to COM LC for incoming freshmen as it is the only way you will get him as a professor for COM 114!

For some strange reason 204 became the easiest class of my entire semester. But some people found it difficult. Dr. Boyd is an amazing professor and he is super knowledgeable of the course. GO TO THE REVEIW SESSION AND OFFICE HOURS. It helps a lot. Start the class strong and you will be fine

He is an amazing lecturer, it's so easy and interesting to follow his lectures. He makes them very engaging and funny! I would definitely recommend taking his classes, but don't skip class. You'll definitely get stressed out in this class but worth taking to prepare you for the future. GO TO OFFICE HOURS  BETTER GRADES
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Professor Boyd is an incredible lecturer and is very engaging. He makes you work for the grade, but he has been my favorite professor at Purdue by far! I definitely recommend taking one of his classes, but don't try to blow them off.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Everyone told me that COM 204 was the hardest communication class at Purdue and I should be afraid, but this class was manageable as long as you do your work. Dr. Boyd is an amazing professor and lectures were always interesting, I found myself actually paying attention! To get a good grade, all you have to do is go to class and follow directions.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Every point counts in this class. I slacked, and I found it difficult to get decent grades on the papers and weekly assignments. This is also a weed-out course for COM majors though, so it makes sense. The only reason why I wouldn't take this class again is because I just wasn't cut out for it, but his lectures are actually really interesting!
A great professor and guy overall, makes you work for your grade. Is tough when grading speeches.
Makes learning fun again. One of those professors that makes you interested in a subject you thought you'd never care about, but you gotta put in the effort to get the reward.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--EXTRA CREDIT--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: His class is incredibly well organized, his assignments and exams are reasonable, he offers lots of in-class extra credit opportunities. He is a genuinely nice guy and he taught our class of around 80 student and seemed to know the names of every single one. I took this class 4:30-5:45pm MW and I was excited to go every time

Totally unreasonable guy. Be extremely careful when drafting your papers, especially when it comes to APA citations. When I cited articles that I had read to prepare for a speech analysis paper, I was given a 0 and accused of being academically dishonest and "padding my bib". When I tried to refute it he was extremely disrespectful. Avoid him.
Amazing professor. You have to put in a ton of work, but it is such a rewarding course. He is always willing to help, and is a super interesting guy. If you put in the effort, you will do well!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Great guy. Respect the class, the exams are pretty easy, but the papers are B. R. U. T. A. L. if you dont go to your TA frequently for revisions.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--SO MANY PAPERS

reviews.comment: He is an incredibly kind and understanding teacher. But, that being said, he does not put any lectures online so you definitely have to go to class and actually pay attention. The grading is tough so be prepared to put in the work.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Hilarious

reviews.comment: An easy class as long as you apply yourself! It was a Learning Community, so we had a chance to get to know everyone a little bit better. I enjoyed this class very much!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Very strict with grading. Super detail oriented. Impossible to get an A
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Boyd is a great teacher, but he's an ideologue who shoves postmodernism down the throat of his students. Critical thinking is highly recommended. The class is not challenging, but the workload definitely is.

Attendance for this class is not mandatory but you need to go. Slides are not posted on blackboard and if you don't have the notes you will not pass. Along with the TAs, Prof. Boyd is always very willing to look at your papers and give feedback before you turn it in. Lectures held my attention and if you do all the assignments you should pass.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Boyd's lectures are enjoyable and easy to pay attention to. Very very hard grading. Prepare to write your best work and only get a 3 out of 5. Extremely hard to get an A

Prof Boyd is an AMAZING professor. Hes so much fun in class and it's obvious that he wants every one of his students to succeed. He is easily my favorite professor at Purdue. Attendance isn't mandatory, but the powerpoints and notes are only provided in class. Assignments are also assigned in class. He gives ample opportunity for extra credit.

Boyd is the most incredible professor. He doesn't make any class easy, but he give you the information and opportunity to learn. COM253 is NOTHING like COM204, but he still does hold you to high standards.

Joshua Boyd is an awesome prof! EXPECT. TO. WORK. FOR. THE. GRADE. This is a writing intensive class. Everything is on a 5-point scale however it is possible to get back on track if your train becomes derailed. He was one of the most interesting profs I have ever had. He knows his stuff and is absolutely hilarious. I got 35 on both papers .
Lectures are fun and entertaining, however the class is challenging

Currently taking the first online maymester version of COM 204. I'm telling you, TAKE THIS VERSION OF THE CLASS. Easily the best, most organized online class I've ever taken. It's not necessarily easier than the semester long class but getting it all done in one month makes it fly by and you can easily stay focused. Definitely would recommend.

He's very engaged in lecture and clearly enjoys what he's doing. I had an easy time staying focused in class and understood material. The major papers are a huge part of your grade, so be ready to devote time and even meet with him or a TA during office hours. The midterm was harder than the final, for me. Also, no textbook is nice. $$

Professor Boyd is a wonderful teacher. I respect that he takes his work very seriously and it makes you want to do well in the class. You can always expect great feedback and him to stick to a routine and the syllabus. Offers office hours and lots of help.
The absolute best professor on campus. Very interesting lectures and I am a much better student because of this class. TA's were also great!

Professor Boyd is one of few professors at Purdue University who will make you try your hardest in this class.  If you take this class you will find it in yourself to do anything you can to get the grade you need.  It takes a great amount of effort and time to do well in this course.
Very tough class.  Don't take for granted.  Grading is tough and everything is only on a 5 point scale.  Tough to actually be interested in what he is talking about
He was a very engaging professor. You must go to class since there isn't a text and there is homework assigned through the class and recitation.

Professor Boyd has proven to be an incredible professor and mentor. Although his class is considered difficult by many, he requires that you exert your best effort and expand your thinking in order to receive the grade you want. He is a professor that truly cares for his students and promotes success in all aspects of his teaching.

Easily the best class I have taken at Purdue with the best professor ever! The class was diverse and interesting. It was hard work, don't get me wrong, but it seemed worth it in the end. My writing improved 10x by the end of the class. Josh Boyd is also a total cutie with his awesome bow ties. He made it worth coming to class everyday.
Teaches PR but never worked in the industry has little idea about the real world.

Advice for anyone taking his class: MAYMESTER IS EASIER. Don't be afraid that is it a lot at one time, it really does help to keep you on track. For the papers: Focus on each paragraph having a topic sentence, once you are done with your paper go back and place each topic sentence with these to connect. Best professor at PURDUE!

I might not have received the best grade in this course but it was truly a great experience. He really teaches you to think critically on your own. Just be sure to go to your TA multiple times about the papers.

Boyd is the best professor I've ever had.  If you have a chance to take com253 DO IT! Don't be discouraged if you thought he was a tough grader in com204, he goes much easier on his 253 class.  As long as you attend class and take good notes it's hard not to get an A.

Boyd is a great teacher, but don't slack off. Come to class and make a friend to get notes from in case you missed something. Get your major paper checked by him or the other TA, not yours at least twice. Always go to recitation and class, and always do the weekly assignments because they help out in the end with the few extra points.

Prof. Boyd is an AMAZING professor. I had him for COM 253, a Public Relations course and I was EXCITED to come to class. Attendance is not mandatory but if you don't come you miss out on the notes and homework assignments. There is no textbook so taking good notes is a must, but i guarantee a fun and interesting class every class period.

It was tough but I learned a lot. All the lectures are interesting and easy to follow but assignments are killer. DON'T GET LAZY. If you are not clear about the lecture or any material email him. He's really helpful. Though it was hard i'd retake it if I have time. AWESOME CLASS.

What's weird about Boyd's class is how the material is about grammar, metaphors and watching youtube clips of The Princess Bride and The Matrix...but the papers are set up for students to write poorly and it's nearly impossible to earn a decent grade

He is one of the best professors I have ever been taught by. As long as you go at least 3 times to the TAs to get your paper checked out before you turn it in, you should be fine.  He's so interesting and helpful, you have no reason not to enjoy his classes, unless of course, you enjoy being lazy.

One of the hardest grades I have ever worked for but go see Boyd in person about your papers. Do your daily assignments and take good notes. 5 points scale is very irritating at times but he is a fair professor when you get to know him. One of my hardest classes and one of the hardest B+ I have ever worked for

I have graduated and have found that most of my classes i took at Purdue are useless, however i have been able to apply the things i learned in Prof Boyd's course to my job and daily thinking.  He wasn't the easiest professor, but if you actually put some work into his class, you will get a huge return on your investment.
While this class is difficult Boyd is by  far one of my favorite Professors at Purdue.

I've never had a more beneficial class and professor as Boyd. I learned more about real life situations than I could have ever imagined. If you are interested in PR you should take this course. It makes everything a bit more real.

josh boyd in probably the best professor i've had at purdue. he teaches you stuff that you are actually gonna use in the real world. com 204 and 253 are very different though. if you have taken 204 and are taking 253, the classes are very different. you fee like you hate your life in 204 but it all pays back in 253. i hope he is never replaced!

he is pretty tough i admit - but he is an excellent professor. I rather work my butt off in an interesting class than be bored out of my mind, and he always has your attention. probably the best com professor i've had at purdue.

Boyd's class isn't easy by any stretch of the imagination, but you do learn a lot. His grading policy is harsh, but he's structured the scale so it kind of works itself out in the end. You spend much of the semester with no real idea how you are doing. Attend EVERY lecture (and recitation), and do EVERY homework assignment asap!

I think Prof. Boyd is hard, but fair. I feel he is the most helpful at Purdue. He is a true professor. At least he didn't rely on TA's heavily. He is passionate with the material. I personally loved his classes.

Class was difficult but I finally felt like I got my moneys worth for attending a world-class university. Like others have said, Boyd can seem  arrogant.  In some communications with him over the semester via email he did offend me by making negative assumptions about me and my performance in the class.  Just take him with a grain of salt.

I felt that his office hours were a waste of time. I followed his advice after waiting in his line for hours and still recieved the crappiest grade possible. My advice is study as hard as you can for the midterm and final and complete all daily assignments. Don't miss class because you may miss an assignment and trust me, you need them!
Hard class.  Relies heavily on teaching assistants.  However, he's a nice guy and has a great personality.  Classes were fun.
He is informative but he is an harsh grader

People need to realize josh boyd is extremely arrogant. I did well in his class, but am smart enough to realize that he is an idiot and has not even worked in pr,  but acts like he knws everything....
He is clear, but arrogant. typical prof on tenure.
The bow tie he wears is funny... but it gets old (just like his class). Grade scale is messed up 1-5, not A,B,C,D,F. Overly difficult grading on papers. Curves class at the end

He's funny to listen to and I didn't mind lectures at all, especially because he has such an extensive collection of bow ties.  Go to EVERY lecture, and take notes!  Make sure you talk to your TA's about EVERY paper, because as everyone before me said, the 5 point grading system is KILLER!
No Comments
Wonderful speaker. I actully enjoyed going to lectures.  He is a really nice and understanding guy and I am glad I had the oppurtunity to have him as a professor!
The best professor I've had at Purdue. One of the only ones I've had that actually care about his students. He rocks
TAs were not helpful and overly critical on grading papers. size of class diminishes opportunity for students to learn.
very pompous and arrogant. teaches about communication and never worked in the field
He is a hard teacher and a smart aleck that likes to publicly humiliate students.
He is a very interesting teacher and I do not mind going to his class at all.  He loves what he does and he is very knowledgeable.
Boyd us an amazing professor.. I wish I could have him again for another class

You will work your butt off in his courses, but it's worth it. The 5 point grading scale was horrible. I worked my a$$ off for a B...and I am pretty proud of that grade because most people re-take that class.

Josh Boyd is a very smart professor and probably one of the best I've had in the Comm dept at Purdue. I actually had to take the class twice because it is difficult, but the second time around I did so much better. The grading system is confusing but it was a pleasure being in his class (twice). And his bowties are awesome!!

excellent speaker, however, i sometimes think his public speaking skills hide the fact that he's not really saying a whole lot in lecture. he is definitely always friendly and easy to ask questions of. seems like the T/A's do most of the grading.  for what it's worth, this class is probably the most difficult non-math course i've taken in my 9 year college career.
No Comments
Quality professor that was challenging. More professors need to be like Josh Boyd.
What a great guy!!  Yeah right!!
run away
He tought me a lot, but his grading policy was ridiculous.  You can write a perfect paper but mess up a minor thing on your references page and get a D or F.
Boyd is the best professor I've had at this school. Yes, the class is hard. But Boyd does a great job of teaching and assisting students.
This class grading system blows.  The material is interesting, but damn you 5 point grading system!
Josh Boyd is one of the knowledgable professors in his field and a joy to learn from. His class might be tough, but its definately worth taking
No Comments
This class was one of my faves because he made the material actually interesting. However, they are the toughest graders I've ever had!

The class is difficult, and the 5-point grading scale SUCKS... but there's usually a generous overall curve at the end. Best recommendation: go to every class you can, TALK TO THE TAs or Josh ABOUT YOUR PAPERS!!! ESP THE FIRST ONE!!!!!! very picky about w
Love the bowties!!
He's a hard grader, but you need it to write professionally!
Grading policy is no good.

This professor isn't just a "tough grader". ECON 340 is essentially a math class, but you can complete all of your math perfectly and receive a C if your work can't "teach another student how to do the work as well". You're supposed to write a textbook for every assignment, and feedback is vague or nonexistent. TAs were bad, too. Avoid if you can.
so specific, super unfair grading, lectures make no sense and she confuses left from right stuff like that, taught myself better on my own than going to class.

Dr. Klose is lecture-heavy. Her entire class is composed of going over material in a pretty bland way. She's unclear on how much work to show during problem sets and exams. Exams are pretty tight on time.

I had Dr.Klose for Game Theory (ECON 451) and she was fine. I was worried with the bad reviews, but most of the content was not hard to follow. Guessing that most of the reviews are from an earlier period. The exams were difficult, but we were told what to study. This course was more difficult than most but not the hardest I have ever taken.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: She doesn't know how to teach She's a great person but she doesn't know how to teach and she just skims through the slides that's all
She needs to take a step back and really consider that teaching is maybe not something she is capable of. Also none of us believe those positive reviews those do not seem genuine.

Poorly designed course. She lets the curve do all the work. You will pass the class as long as you aren't the very bottom. Her grading is the most unreasonable and inconsistent I've ever seen out of all my years of undergrad, graduate and even high school. This is the first and only time I've cried because of class. Avoid at all costs if possible.

Bad lectures, I would ask for help with something, and she seemingly wouldn't read the full question before responding with a nonhelpful answer. Prepare to teach yourself. I repeatedly had to go and protest against her grading mistakes she made in grading to give me my correct score. I should not have to review the professors grading ever.

Don't believe what people say here. She does give lengthy assignments, but she is extremely helpful and will guide you. The hw is necessary and working through the problem sets will help you learn and retain the material. In the end you really feel like you learned something. If you take accountability you will do great.
Super tough grader. She will deduct full points for the tiniest of errors. Also the lectures are awful. Pretty much have to teach yourself

Buckle up for loads of hw. Tests are pretty similar to the problem sets but they're only 45 minutes long. Not a lot of guidance but she does offer help sessions. She cancels them if specific questions aren't sent to her in advance.
Bettina was terrible at explaining things. Without a doubt, the worst economics professor I have ever had.
Lectures and textbook will not prepare you for the homework and tests. Awful class, made me regret my masters at Purdue.

Unless absolutely necessary do not take any course with Dr. Klose. The lectures are very brief, you are expected a lot more details on the tests and homework. The textbook is also not helpful, but she refuses to use a better textbook.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Exams were very similar to homework and sample papers, so if you could do the assignments, the exams would be a breeze. Responds to questions on the Brightspace discussions boards very promptly. Grading was very lenient as well, as some assignments are graded for completion instead of correctness.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: There is a lot of homework that is difficult and takes a lot of time, however, the exams are very similar to the homework's, as well as the old exam provided. She also gives extra credit assignments that are worth a lot of points to help your grade.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Gives good feedback--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Dr. Klose is great. Yes, her class is extremely homework heavy and the homework is quite difficult but if you work hard enough on them, preparing for the exams is a piece of cake. Dr. Klose is also extremely approachable and helpful and responds very quickly on the Discussion board. Also, her exams are very very similar to the hw and sample exams.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Gives good feedback--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: I am a good student. I have a 4.0 GPA. I have taken calc 3 and linear algebra... I am not afraid of hard classes. I remember looking at the first problem set in Dr. Klose's class and having absolutely no idea what to do at all. She simply does not teach the content. Take this class with someone else if you want to succeed or learn.

One of the worst professors in Microeconomics. Homework is nothing like the lecture, test is nothing like the homework. The textbook is extremely brief, you will get more content from the lecture than the text so dont even bother. She does curve the grades though (no one will make it if she didnt)

The homework is never like the lectures, there is always a curveball that she never shows the class how to do. You have to participate online with other students to collaborate and find answers, and this just makes the process even worse, it is like a Facebook of questions... Her tests are about as hard as her homework, and you'll always fail.

The issue with Professor Klose's class is not even the content itself, but rather the way she structures the assignments. She expects us to complete the assignments in ways that she never covered during class. Only once the solutions come out do you understand what steps she expected, but by then it's too late. Very annoying!

Assumes students have the same level of knowledge she does, criticizes anything that she didn't personally teach, even though she's not good at teaching herself, and essentially requires students to learn everything on their own.

Dr. Klose is a very nice and caring professor. But that does not change the fact that her lectures are so high level compared to the homework assignments. This class was like someone blindfolded me and covered my ears and asked me to describe the colors of a painting in front of me using only my sense of hearing. Plan to spend 15+hours a week
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Not sure what's the point of the final exam. To me, the final is used to test how quickly you can write and use a calculator.

This course is a complete disaster and nightmare, and this is 100% because of the way the course has been developed and is being delivered by the professor. Essentially this professor has taken fairly straightforward material and made it unnecessarily convoluted and extremely time-consuming. Horrible experience all-around.
She cares about students but she doesn't know how to teach.
Her voice just makes people sleepy.

She is nice, but the course is strangely taught. I think the justification is that she teaches advance leveled courses, but her approach left me confused and frustrated. I often found the concepts to be far simpler than she taught. Overall I do not think she did an acceptable job as a professor in my semester.
Worst I've ever had.

Dr. Klose's homework is math heavy and requires A LOT of time and thought, same for tests. She cares about students though and answers questions promptly, which is a huge help. As long as you work hard and reach out when you are struggling, you'll do well. Her class is definitely not easy but very rewarding and a good intro to grad-level classes.

Lectures are not comparable to the difficulty of homework or exams. Is responsive to questions, but is also incredibly vague and will intentionally mislead students and/or leave out crucial information. Absolutely the worst professor I have taken.

I've never had a class that felt more arbitrarily graded in my life. You spend the entire semester in the dark worrying about the low raw scores. After receiving my final grade, I couldn't reconcile my relative performance with the grade distributions she'd provided on numerous occasions. Lectures were insufficient and tests way too long.

I don't believe I have ever had a worse professor. I do think she cares and she is responsive, but it is impossible to complete the homework or exams using the lectures or Powerpoints she provides. I finally gave up and read the book and Google everything. The homework takes 20 hrs a week if you want to get a passing grade. Don't take this class.

I liked her. A lot of students seem upset because they have received low test scores and doesn't curve anything until the end of the class. She is very responsive both for course and administrative questions. But she tries her best to guide you to the correct answer instead of spoon feeding you. It is not an easy class, but it isn't supposed to be.

This professor is NOT interested in student success. Test averages are below 50% and she considers that "high". Homework requires copious amounts of googling because class lectures and text are not sufficient. Klose refuses to address concerns from entire class about work load and inconsistencies. Grading is arbitrary and in some cases retaliatory.
If you ever met a teacher in your lifetime is worse than Bettina Klose, please do tell. Because that would be a miracle.
Craig is literally a king. Exams are def hard though. Zoom class on Fridays which was a plus. Can see why people may find him intimidating but he's actually super nice.
He does not go over the topics thoroughly as he expects you to know the material without ever teaching it. Not approachable and his lessons are not engaging.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The rest of the reviews are very telling for Professor Brown. I am suprised Purdue admin hasnt seen this and looked into his teaching style. Basically, he teaches by going at 2x speed through things he expects that you know to teach complex topics. He does not blend the theory and math very well together and makes things very difficult. dont. take.

Professor Brown is extremely disorganized. He puts material on exams that we never learned, and the study guides are useless. No answer keys are posted, and lectures aren't tied to textbook chapters. Homework isn't reopened, so there's no way to review or improve. You'll end up teaching yourself everything just to keep up. Would not recommend.

lectures are very confusing and hard to follow and do not aid you properly for the exams that he writes.  I genuinely feel like I understood the content when studying for an exam but would give questions on the exam that are nothing to do with what we learned. Brightspace page has no information. Cold calls sometimes and is rude and unapproachable

One of the less enjoyable courses I've taken at Purdue. He does not seem to be trying to help when asked, as previous review mentioned, refuses to show clear grading standard, refuses to give homework and test answers, practice paper is pretty much irrelevant from actual test. Allows cheat sheet, has a take home final that is in the form of essay.

Tough grader, exams are based solely on his lectures, exams does not reflect the practice exam he gives out. Participation is 20%, but when asked how he keeps track of that, he stated "I have a good memory". Monotone lecturer but definetly knows his stuff.

Professor Brown does very little to actually help you succeed in his class. I am a student who loves a challenging course when it is because the material is difficult; however, Professor Brown refuses to upload homework answers or exam question answers and does not leave notes from his PPTX. Would NOT recommend to anyone.

As long as you have the attention span of a 10 year old, you'll do fine. Most of what was on his exam he reiterated in class multiple times, you just have to pay attention. He's also quite funny, and isn't mean when he cold calls and you're wrong.

He does not know how to explain topics and expects you to know everything he is talking about. His exams have nothing to do with the study questions he gives you and overall does not prepare you for the exams.

Do not take this professor if you can help it at all. By far the worst professor I have had in my academic career. He makes the exams near impossible to finish in time and does not even show up for the exams on time. He gets mad when people don't understand a topic he just introduced and is not helpful when trying to get more help after class.

He is knowledgable in the subject but cant teach it at all.  He makes class very unpleasant to attend, for the first test review session he kept saying "oh man since everyone says the same answer I guess its too easy and I should make the exam harder." nobody knew what was happening and we were all struggling.  He is just very difficult to approach
lectures were very hard to follow and the exams were impossible. Homework is on Chegg though
