reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Lectures are very confusing with the content being confusing and then becoming more confusing the more he talks. 2 midterms 15% each and a final case 30%. Very tough grader and very unhelpful when going to him for help. You will not know your grades until after the class is completed. Overall very terrible class with a very terrible professor.

Without a doubt the worst professor at Purdue. He does not explain topics clearly and assumes that you know everything already. He gives two exams which are only a few questions that will be marked wrong even if they are right just because you didn't write it how he wanted it. Participation grade is 'his opinion' on what you should get. Avoid this.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The lectures do not prepare you whatsoever for the exams and he is very unreliable in explaining good detailed explanations to your questions. Final is take-home and worth 30% of your grade. Expect 8 pages of work to earn you a D.

The wrong textbook is in the book store, so you either have to order the McGraw Hill textbook or use the online version. Not a huge deal but kind of annoying. He is not very clear. His participation grade is ambiguous. He jumps right in assuming you will know where you are at. Overall, not a good experience.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Do not take his class, even you think you are the top students. Just don't try!! Do Not Try!!!!!! I got full credit on the first exam. After that, he covered all the grade and I received a B at the end. I didn't even know how was my second exam and final report. Terrible lecture and rarely replies email.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Would not recommend at all. The grading criteria is unclear, and questions are also not worded clearly. Exam reviews and study guides are not useful, as the exam prep sessions & material will be completely different from the actual exams.
DO NOT.... UNDER ANY CIRCUMSTANCE.... TAKE HIS CLASS. Lectures are too complicates and exams are next to impossible.

DO NOT TAKE A CLASS WITH THIS PROFESSOR. This was definitely the worst class I have taken while at Purdue. His exams are very difficult and he will give you a zero on essay questions if you do not set them up how he likes (even if your answer is completely correct). The lectures also make no sense. Overall, the worst professor I have ever had.

Craig is by far one of the wort Krannert Professor ever: he is rude and unhelpful when contacting him. Does not explain the material, makes lecture incredibly difficult and to follow. His course is basically 2 exams n a final case all poorly covered. And if you have an accommodation forget about that, he will ignore it! Save yourself, don't take!!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: DO NOT TAKE IF YOU CAN HELP IT. -cannot read a room and makes concepts more confusing when "explaining" them -created 2 exams (online& in-person) and took 1.5 pts away from 1/2 of class because he wasn't happy with the mean between the two (the exam was out of 14 pts) -class has 0 info on final proj (30% of grade) due in 2.5 wks, other than due d8
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Craig is a smart guy. However, that is actually what hurts him the most. He doesn't explain things well and expects that everyone already knows what's going on. His tests usually expand on a topic that is not heavily talked about on the exam and the exams are normally out of six questions.
Might as well just buy yourself a textbook and read it on your own

He's very passionate and encourages questions from students in lecture. The assignments take a while to do and there is not a lot of time to do them. Extra credit at the end was nice and the exams are reasonable. Taking notes during lectures is a must because the slides are complicated and he explains the important parts in lecture.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback--Lots of homework

reviews.comment:
Very convoluted, confusing lectures. Completing readings beforehand is sometimes helpful (if you can understand them), but certainly not exhaustively so. 
The HWs are very lengthy & time-consuming and don't necessarily align w/ lectures.

If you're not sure, I'd suggest taking a different prof/class unless you're already proficient in the subject.
He keeps talking about GNN research all the time. Not everyone cares. Class is too hard. No time left for research.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Seems like a very arrogant person who doesn't like being questioned. The HWs are very time-consuming, but even if you get full grades it doesn't feel like you learned anything really meaningful. The syllabus is very niche and biased towards his research. Symmetry feels like an esoteric concept whose practical utility in DL is taken for granted.

I dont think Bruno deserves 1/5. He couldve spent more time explaining the intuitions behind the maths instead of just reading them off the slides. But in the end it wasnt too bad, if you did the readings beforehand. Course logistics were messy, but also wasnt that bad. He also did his best to cover as many modern topics in DL as possible.

Overall good and well-designed course, although course logistics could have been improved. Lectures were great, but are often tough to understand if you don't pay attention or do the appropriate readings. HWs and exams were decently hard, but lectures were enough to keep up with them. Would suggest if you want to challenge yourself with DL topics.

The course definitely tough and it is heavily mathematic. However, I don't think he was rude or stop student to ask questions. He constantly ask us to ask question.
One thing I would hope him to do is he first explains the concept before going into the mathematical proof. If you want easy course, then he is not your go to option.

The content says its deep learning but its rather just the professor's research in a very niche area being taught to a 100 student batch where hardly anyone is interested in it. Moreover, whatever he tries to teach has so much mathematical background required that he just assumes that everyone knows.

Tough professor to say the least. English is very rough and lectures are hard to understand. A lot of the homework problems are completely unrelated to the lectures, but are doable. Would try to get someone else if there is another option

The course was tough with the lectures being pretty theory intensive. The homeworks were time consuming, but there was a lot of extra credit. Midterm was a shitshow, but the final was one of the easiest cs exam I have given at purdue. As long as you understand what's going on in the class, it's not a bad course. Bruno def made it easier.

This is a pretty difficult class taken with this professor - very time consuming and somewhat difficult homeworks, and decently difficult exams. However, he really cares about students learning and tries his best to teach pretty theoretical and math heavy content. Can be occasionaly tough to understand at times as he reads formulas from slides.
worst class ive ever taken. terrible prof
I have never taken a class this terrible. Horrible lectures and very harsh.
Horrible Lecturer
Worst course I have ever taken. very harsh.

The professor's English is not great and is often an obstacle to understanding the material. It doesn't help that he isn't great at explaining concepts either, nor are the slides. The topics aren't explained in much detail and it's almost impossible to implement them without help after the topic is covered.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment:
Prof. Ribeiro is great. He's clearly passionate about his research, is very knowledgeable, is patient with explanations and very approachable.

However, the one issue I find is that he tends to overwhelm us with math. The slides really need improvement. They do a poor job at explaining the material, and it's very easy to get lost because of it.

Riberio is what happens when a circus clown is asked to teach a 300-level CS course. Horrendous slides have no takeaway material to help you with the homework or exams. No communication with the TAs. Will humbly brag how he asked android to change its facial recognition code several times in the semester. Maybe I should write that on the final.
Teaching was very unhelpful and homeworks were extremely time consuming.

This is the worst class I've ever taken at purdue. His course material is very hard to understand and he will not explain it clearly during classes. Sometimes you could understand what the lecture slides is talking about but you may still find you don't know how to solve questions in the tests. There were huge amout of formulas too.
He is very knowledgeable and great at conveying difficult concepts. This was one of my favorite classes for the semester.
WORST LECTURER IN CS
He is an freaking early bird, for the exam, he arranges 8:00-10:00 on winters, who does that. If he is so harsh on himself, he is not going easy on you
The course is not hard in the subject of statistics, but the professor is able to make it harder than you thought.
I don't think anything could improve this recitation. It's BAD as it is
skipping steps makes it hard to understand how they arrived to an answer. Show some more work
If a student doesn't understand how you are doing a problem, explain it in a different way. Also, do the work in a more organized manner.

He cares, don't get me wrong, but it was so hard to understand what he was teaching because he didn't know what was going on in lecture. His work and explanations were difficult to follow and understand.

I don't know what to say. He is passionate and his English is fluent. But his logic is sooo bad that it could make you feel like: what is he doing for the whole class and why am I here. Also, he made too many political jokes and lauged at the joke made by himself, which would be really awkward for international students.
He is trying to persuade us that he is a good teacher and beg us to connect with him at office hour. But, you are unable to convey us the information that you can solve the issue at class.
He is not a good teacher. English is bad and nonlogical
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: Worst ever. The slides are not well organized and have many mistakes. The homeworks are peculiar and just wasting of time.

Professor clearly passionate about the subject and teaching. Willing to listen to student feedback and act upon them. Happy to explain and clarify new concepts. Gave up to 10% extra credit in the class (Kaggle performance depending). Homework is challenging and time consuming but not impossible and provides great opportunity for learning.

One of the most caring profs I've had at Purdue. I remember near the end of the semester she told me that my smile genuinely brightened her day. Said at the beginning of the semester that she would always take our side no matter what. Genuine unconditional kindness that you don't see often elsewhere (but should).

ENGR 131 isn't a hard class. It's not too exciting either, but Prof. Galarza did her best to make classes kinda fun or have us practice an engineering soft skill (teamwork). 
Prof. Galarza is one of the nicest, most caring professors at Purdue. She also bumped my A- up to an A because I asked (the FYE 3.2 GPA, iykyk), because I got to know her.

Professor Galarza is super caring and always makes sure to asks students how they are doing. She is also very helpful when answering students' questions. The class curriculum itself is slow and tedious though so I would recommend taking ENGR 133 over ENGR 131 if you're taking a lighter course load.

Probably the nicest professor I have ever had. She cares so much about all of her students and is willing to help everyone. She has no problem giving points back to students if they ask and she is always willing to give good feedback and listen to problems you may have in this class.

I am very grateful to have gotten Professor Galarza as my ENGR 131 professor. She very obviously cares about her students education and their future. The classes consist mainly of review of pre-lecture videos with group work after.

She might be one of the most caring professors at this university. She's always available for her students and she truly wants you to succeed. ENGR 131 wasn't a hard class but I had a 7:30, and I'm really thankful that I had her class because I didn't often dread getting up to go to it. She also gives you a lot of studio time to work on projects.

ENGR 13100 I think is a pretty tedious class because the course fits a lot into a few months. Dr. Galarza made the class so much more tolerable. She is a sweet and supportive teacher and is the first person at Purdue who made me feel like I belonged. She makes effort to know all of her students personally and really cares about their well-being.
Lovely person, so supportive and kind. Will happily explain anything you're struggling with, gives a lot of extra credit opportunities
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: ENGR131 itself is tedious but she helped by giving extra credit opportunities and solving any doubt from the assignments or projects, exam criteria is unclear but she is fair when reviewing exams and if you explain why you deserve the points for an unclear question, she gives at least some or all of them. I'm taking her ENGR132 class next semester.

She is very helpful if you have any questions you wanna ask directly in person. Her lectures are boring but I don't know who could possibly make this class any less boring. She is bad at answering emails though. I still recommend!
Boring, rambling lectures reading straight from slides. Accent not bad at all. Recommend someone else for 131.

It's wild to think he's the course coordinator when he is the reason students don't pass this class. For a course coordinator, he says a lot of "I think" and "maybe"s like he doesn't know what he's talking about (which is true, he doesn't). He's also the type to start the semester with a speech about how everyone can get As and why he won't curve
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: All I will say is that his score on here is an understatement, and he has one of the worst RateMyProfessors I've ever seen. Just read through the other reviews, and you'll get a pretty good idea of this man and the WONDERFUL time you'll have in his class

There is way too much information crammed into the slides. The textbook seems more concise compared to Krusemark's own lecture slides which is absurd. The TA help sessions are better to learn from in comparison to the actual lectures. The horrendous exam scores should be a wake up call to change something up. He's not mean, just hard to learn from.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Krusemark's ability to teach is a little difficult; he also fails in answering students' questions properly in lecture. His exams are worse; he picks out some of the most random topics from lecture slides and puts them into his exams. He doesn't even release his exam average and I'm convinced it's because it's so bad. He's not concise.
He can not teach and the exam averages are like a 40%
he is not very concise which makes things difficult to understand, lectures are based on the smallest detail in a slide and there are many many slides you have to memorize
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: tests are unfairly difficult and does not align well with lectures
you have to memorize 11 lectures, and he'll ask a question about a minor detail in one of them. the average for the exams are so low. he seems confused in lecture
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: lectures do not align with exam, seems confused all the time in lecture, exam averages are very low, does not provide review sessions or any helpful tips, won't tell you average of the exam, goes on about unnecessary topics, rambles, the curve doesn't account for how low the test grades are
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: beginning of the semester, my heart rate would go up by how mad i was to be in the same room as him. at the end, i started to hysterically laugh in lecture cause i couldn't believe he was real. this class with krusemark drove me to insanity and it will for you too.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment:
Krusemark makes tests out of so much cursory material that is not connected to each other that I came out of this class feeling like I was trying to prepare for fun facts on tests instead of actually learning key concepts of biochemistry.

Cool person, just not someone that I would want to ever write my tests or lectures again.

This is the kind of class where you celebrate getting anything above 50%, purely because his exams are impossible to prepare for. Krusemark throws so much material at you in a 30+ slideshow presentation and will choose the smallest details to write exam questions over. The only time I knew peace in this class was when Hudmon was the main lecturer.

krusemark pulls questions out of his butt. no matter how much you study, you will not pass any exam of his. this class has made me question my sanity and whether im fit for pharmacy. do not recommend taking a class of his

If you value your sanity, please take this godforsaken class anywhere else. "Unlike most sports games, you can all win this class!" (quoted directly from his first day analogies), and then proceeds to have exam averages in the low fifties. I am a victim of this man's poor ability to teach and will have nightmares about his exams.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: It was so bad, I want to give you a zero, but that's not possible, so I give you a one.

if assembling a wall of text and blurting it in the form of a "powerpoint presentation" is considered teaching then yeah Krusemark is an amazing teacher. Add to that the fact the man can't do public speaking about his own topic for his life and his exams ask about the smallest sub bullet point on exactly one PPT slide and it's a recipe for failure
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Way too much information on lecture slides, exam questions are very specific and tedious. Very bad.

Dr. Krusemark should not be teaching this class. Copying and pasting textbook material onto lecture slides is not helpful at all. In fact, all of his lecture slides have confusing material, abbreviations, and images. He always question himself about what could be the "right" answer and never conveys what he wants us to learn in lecture.

Dr. Krusemark has to be the worst professor out the pre-pharmacy curriculum. He could never give straightforward answers during exam review sessions and wouldn't know the answers to his own questions. Slideshows are an absolute eye sore and information dump, and somehow everything is important given how hyperspecific and hard his exams are.

biochem is supposed to be a challenging course, but  not like this. his section of the course makes no sense, and he agrees bc he confuses himself and cant even answer questions from students. he seems like a nice guy, but as a professor... he is terrible. his counterpart in this course may be the only reason why students pass.

Lecture slides jump around a lot which make it impossible to follow along and also includes abbreviations without explaining what they mean. Also, no answer key to practice exams and he can't even answer questions over them (exams he wrote himself) which keeps you guessing in the dark from day one. Good luck, you are in for a ride!

I'm not joking every time I sit it or watch his lectures back it just makes the content more confusing. I wish he would take his job to help students learn the difficult content and also test us fairly on them. We have an exam tomorrow and I'm predicting the average will be around 30%, not even joking.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: might as well try to memorize the whole textbook because Dr. Krusemark does not know that a PowerPoint presentation lecture should be bullet points rather than a copy-pasted essay on what he's talking about (and even then he struggles to get his point across which is insane)
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Doesn't provide answer keys to exam practice so there goes trying to see what you need to work on. On top of that, he can't even answer questions about the exam practice so they're basically useless. Don't even bother going to lecture either, just read the textbook- no idea how to lecture, completely disorganized, slides are all over the place.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Doesn't even know how to solve his own practice exam questions. There isn't really any advice I can give on how to be successful in this course because its so unbelievably hard you are guaranteed to struggle no matter how good of a student you are! If you are not in pharmacy, do NOT take this class.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This prof cannot teach for his life, often not being able to explain his own slides or exams. Exams are based off the textbooks rather than lectures, so get ready to read the book. Good luck, you'll need it
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: I understand that biochemistry can be a difficult course. However, Dr. Krusemark is an absolute mess during lectures. The slides he uses are filled with unnecessarily complicated words which he himself seems to be unable to explain. He also very often just stops blankly for minutes during lectures, as if he doesn't know what he was talking about.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Dude was a LITERAL hot mess. He would host review sessions before exams, students would ask questions and he would say: "Great question! I actually don't know the answer to that." Like HELLO? Would not take a class with him AGAIN. Best of luck!

Professor Krusemark did not know how to help students when questions were asked during exam review days and would stall and waste time, yet on exams he writes questions asking for the smallest detail out of the long wordy lecture slides. Krusemark does not seem to care about students success in the class.

This class is literally the epitome of absolute lunacy. Never have I gone through a class with a professor who barely knows his own content. Took him 10 mintues to answer a practice exam question with his own slides. Just a hot mess honestly. Exams are really heavy and Dr. Krusemark asks very minute questions from lecture slides. Good luck...
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: He is a very hard and confusing professor. He doesn't really care about his students. Seems like he only teaches so he can do research. When asked a question he can't fully answer it and will answer it in a confusing way. Exams are HARD.

This class is a joke. Most of your grade will come from how you score on the exams, but there are very few resources to help you succeed, even if you go to all of the lectures and pay attention. Dr. Krusemark is extremely vague about the content breakdown on the exams and refuses to provide answer keys for the practice exams.

I made a ratemyprofessor account just to say how much this class sucks. class is insanely difficult. each exam is like 12 lectures and each lecture has about 30 slides worth of content and 50 pages worth of reading and the exam questions are extremely specific. he doesnt give us study guides either.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: terrible class, everything was poorly explained... average on the final was <60% and not curved.. professors did not explain things well at all. take biol 307 instead of this mess of a class
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Krusemark is not a good lecturer. He is obviously very smart and knows what he's talking about but he can NOT teach the material. I didn't learn anything from his lectures but I still managed a 70 average on the tests so this class is really learn on your own.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Graded by few things--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: The multiple times I have asked to discuss course work he has been very rude. He has cool in-class demonstrations and knows his course material but I do not appreciate the minimal respect he has given to me as a student.

Krusemark is a terrible professor. He is very disorganized and is a really bad lecturer. He is a smart dude that does not know how to teach. Overall, would not take this course again because it was hell.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Dr. Krusemark is a really nice guy but seems to be all over the place. He and his lectures are messy, and a lot of students struggle to follow. He is really passionate about the material and isn't hard to look at, so thats a plus.

Classes are monotonous and poorly prepared. The slides are often confusing and overtly abstract for rather simple topics. And, at times, it's very difficult to understand the professor's explanations. Grading is generous, but her teaching style is not. Would not take this class with this professor again.

Congfang genuinely cares about her students, especially when you are willing to discuss with her. However, I DO think Congfang needs a better preparation of the course, especially for teaching students materials in a logical and mutually understandable way. You might have to rely mostly on yourself for the course, although the grading is generous.

Grading Scale is pretty lenient, with an 84 being the minimum grade for an A. Not a lot of material, but it can be difficult to understand. This was her first time teaching, and it was at times difficult to understand. She has good exam reviews. Her review guide is very similar to the exam. She does genuinely care about her students/
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The course material itself was fairly easy, but understanding the professor is a bit of a challenge. This was also her first time teaching the course, so maybe it was just a general nervousness or getting used to teaching the material. Prepare to teach yourself the material. Don't let this discourage you from taking this course.

Congfang's IE 230 class is made up almost entirely of exam credit. There is barely any material to actively practice the topics covered in lecture. She is very hard to understand, which I know is not a fair criticism but it should still be taken into account. Her slides are just mindless info dumps with little to no analysis of the topics.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This professor is honestly so bad at teaching and the lectures are completely different from the recitations. The TAs don't know anything about what they're teaching and we're blindly expected to know what to do on the labs. The midterm was bad as well as the professor didn't even talk about 1/3 of the contents on it n now my GPA is suffering.

Total disconnect between lecture and lab. They feel like two different courses. The professor is detached from the lab, and TAs lack any passion for teaching. The lecture content is disorganized and has zero practical use for the lab. As a business major, this is the biggest waste of tuition at Purdue.
Kahoots and group projects are your only grades, and she grades the group projects pretty strictly, with not great instruction on how to complete them.

The lectures have almost nothing to do with the recitations. Lab grades didn't come out for 3 weeks, while we had already submitted another one. Doing iClicker in lecture, 80% of people were getting the questions wrong because they didn't know what she was talking about. I dropped bc I'm CODO'ing to HK & this class was a complete waste of my time.

Dr. Ying is easily one of the nicest people I have ever met, however, I did find that her teaching style wasn't the most effective in terms of learning the material since we never really applied it in recitation. However, if you participate, she will reward you and help you out through the class.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: this class is just.
She is such a nice person.
honestly just fries bag

such a bs class. projects have no real world application. lecture are fluff. teacher is nice you can tell she is like sweet and what ever but what is this class. dont take it. i'm stuck in it but i wish i could get out

No idea how she was hired by Purdue. Every other Professor I've had in Daniels is miles better. While the content in class itself was fairly easy, the grading system made no since. 40% of our grade was based of 4 random in class kahoots made from unspecified material. Other than the lab projects there was no hw. Purdue please hire someone else.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She is nice, but not a good professor. Our grade is based on 4 Kahoots, and 3 projects, all of which are not explained. The lectures have no connection to any of the assignments, and there is no direction for the assignments. She said it should be an easy A, but it definitely is not. It has nothing to do with business either.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She is literally the most chillest professor I had in this semester, the group projects are the main grade for the class, so if you have a good sense of working in a group you are fine.

Class has a lot of potential. It's first year of the class(fall sem 2025)but there are flaws. The projects are overall interesting; you make your own chatbot agent for a local biz, good for resume and overall skills. The only coding we did was prompt engineering- we put into Gemini and it generates for us but we interpret. It's alright tbh take it.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Graded by few things--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: Idk what she expects. Too many topics, and it's impossible to tell what's important for exams, which are graded super specifically. Kahoots count for participation but everyone cheats. The course isn't structured, yet we're expected to memorize everything. Exams come back very late.

Kahoots for accuracy, that's enough. Kahoots have fewer than 10 total questions, so if you miss a question, you should pray for extra credit. There are many chances by doing AI headlines or getting a podium on Kahoot. Make sure to use the lecture slides that are posted. Taking notes doesn't really help, She reads from the slides.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Graded on three tests and three projects which I thought wasn't enough. Projects were very vague and confusing. Lectures were not helpful at all and the class absolutely shouldn't be a required course for finance majors, it's an IT class. She was nice though.
It is her first year and she is kind and passionate, but just don't.

Worst professor I have ever met. Said class is easy A, but not. Grade is 4 Kahoots (each 10%) and they are only 8 questions. This means you have 30 seconds to answer a question, and if you get it wrong that's around 2% off of your final grade. 3 projects (20% each), but super strict grading. Class has nothing to do with business, mostly coding.

Genuinely the worst class I have ever taken. Grade is determined by random Kahoots that were not started until the second half of the semester. She provides no way to study for them and the lectures lack academic value. She doesn't check her email, provides poor explanations and has caused outbursts over the poor planning and instruction in 187.
She says so many words but nothing at the same time.

Chenflix saved my life during math courses at purdue (162, 261, 266), and I've heard his in person lectures are even better. Very good at breaking topics down, explaining how to do them, and why we do them. Very efficient teaching style with not a lot of time being wasted too. Very easy to understand, one of the best professors at purdue.

Since I had the ASYNC lecture format, I decided to just ChenFlix instead and it's been amazing. Lots of flexibility to watch the lectures whenever you want, and Chen's explanations are great at providing a conceptual understanding of Calc 3. Ended up acing both midterms and doing alright on the final.

Professor Chen is a great lecturer, not only for his ability to explain mathematical concepts in a clear way, but also his commitment to making his lectures accessible to other students. He does a great job at giving his students a strong intuition about how we model/solve differential equations. This intuition is easily applied to other courses.

unlike many Chenflix users here, I had the fortune of taking Dr. Chen's class in-person. he does explain and simplify complex concepts really really well, focusing only on the topics we would actually want to know for an exam. and although the exams were a bit harder than i expected, the grading was pretty lenient.
The goat. If you haven't heard of him, you been living under a rock. Chenflix for life.
GREATEST OF ALL TIME

Through Chenflix, I was able to self-study the entire calculus two curriculum. His lectures were clear and easy to follow. His way of teaching the intricacies of calculus so simply made the nightmare class seem somewhat easy.

Chenflix is a great resource/crutch for almost every nightmare math course at Purdue. Professor Chen is a natural teacher, he cares about his students and their learning and works hard to make his lectures engaging and informative.
This man got me through Calc 2 and is therefore the GOAT.
Best math Professor of all time.
I never went to an in person lecture of my actual professor. Chenflix is goated and he is pretty funny. He brought up a mushroom hat from Mario to describe a shape. I love him.
Best purdue math professor. Chenflix is goated

I do not even go to my professors lectures. I use that free time to watch Chenflix since the way that he explains concepts is very clear. Also since this most math courses til differential equations are standardized if you watch chenflix most lectures are aligned with my professors. His lectures are amazing and I am glad I had him for CALC2.
lectures+boilerexams=wiin

Professor Chen's MA162 class is challenging but engaging. His lectures are incredibly helpful, and I found unstuck ai to be a useful supplement for study prep. Attend class, do the work, and you'll learn a lot.
Great professor.
Prof. Chen is the goat. Love that man.

The best of the best when it comes to math professors. Will do everything he can to simplify complex concepts so that anyone can understand. Is always very available for extra help and overall just a great dude.
W Professor

I started using ChenFlix after a few weeks of going to class in Fall 2023. Chen did a great job of explaining everything and making it clear, in addition to the homework help on MyLab. Amazing professor.
Chen usually has early lectures, however, if you can't make that just watch his recordings at chenflix. Go to his office hours as well, he is very patient and helpful with students
I used chenflix for this class(originally had professor Hood) and the lectures were extremely organized and helpful.
Chen is the undisputed goat.
I had Glubekov (idk how to spell) and I only attended two of his lectures. The rest of 162 was from Chenflix and I got a 100 on the three midterms. Phenomenal.

I think that professor chen is an amazing teacher. His lectures are easy to follow in person and on the online Chenflix website. He makes taking Calculus 2, one of the hardest math courses at Purdue, a little easier. He's easy to follow and understand.
Dr. Chen is an amazing professor and he makes one of Purdue's hardest classes seem easy. I would gladly retake this class just to have Dr. Chen as a professor again.

Calc II itself can be challenging but Chen is a great lecturer who explains things well. He provides great examples in lecture and answers some questions. He has office hours which are helpful and cares a lot about his students even in a difficult course.

(MA 26100 DIST, Summer)

I've been watching Dr. Chen's Calculus lectures through the entirety of calc 2 and 3, even when he technically was not my professor for calc 2 last spring.  As a lecturer, he is very gifted at engaging students with relevant examples, and has a clear passion for seeing his students succeed.
People overrate him a little bit but he is still one of the best calc professors at purdue. Really clear lectures but VERY tough exams. Grind boilerexams before each exam and you'll be ok though.

Dr. Chen is the best professor I have had the pleasure of taking class with. He communicates clearly and concisely in language easy to understand. All of his lectures are online through Chenflix and can be accessed through a simple Google search.
Professor Chen is an amazing teacher. He presented ideas very clearly and even saved time for questions during lectures.

I love Chen. I did not do amazing in the class but that was more on the content then his teaching. Calc 2 is hard but he makes it understandable. After my semester with him I chose to take a 7:30 am calc 3 class just to have him as my lecturer. Hard tests but again that may be the content. Has great office hours and great lectures. Hard but amazing
If you take calc at Purdue just watch chenflix or go to his lecture

Lectures were amazing - I found them very helpful and interesting! The course is very exam heavy though, and the exams are quite difficult sometimes, but his lectures really helped me pass the course.

MA 162 is likely one of the harder classes you will take at Purdue, but Chen has the best lectures available for the class. The course is super heavy on exams (3 midterms) and the final, around 2 homework assignments and quizzes per week. This course is a lot of work with HW, quizzes, exams, lectures. Exams are usually at 7pm.

Professor Chen's lectures are very easy to follow and straight to the point. Everything covered in his lectures can be used to solve problems on the homework and on the exams. Definitely recommended to ATTEND his lectures but his recordings work well too.
Professor Chen has amazing calc lectures that help you understand the material in detail. I would highly recommend him.
this man got me through calc 2, lectures are very easy to follow and easily accessible
Loved Chen
Dr. Chen met with me even though he wasn't teaching 261 at the time.  Tremendously helpful and unselfish.  I wouldn't have gotten my good grade without his help.

I had hoped that he would be easy to work with, but always made me feel like I was wasting his time. 
Don't listen to the other reviews like I did, they aren't always true!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: I LOVE THIS MAN! Chen is so amazing at what he does! His lectures are so straightforward, and he actually tests what he teaches. The man is a godsend and gave me back my confidence in my ability to do math (I've had several negative experiences with professors that made me question my abilities in the past). Easily the best teacher I've had so far!

Best mathematics professor I have had. I retook Calc 161 and had no trouble understanding since the first lecture I had with him. Also, Chenflix is excellent; use it to study, clarify, or do whatever you need. Even use it for other courses. HE is just a fantastic professor. He made me enjoy math again.

He is a good professor but not a good examiner. He explained the concepts pretty well and had his lectures and notes shared for everyone. Cares for our understanding and answers questions clearly. His exams though were on another level compared to what he gave in lecture. Thanks to the curve everyone passed. Very respected professor though.
Great teacher and makes the hard class easier to understand. Definitely take him for all your math classes if you can.

Chen is a very good lecturer, but he also writes some tough exams so pay attention to his lectures as he is often the course coordinator. Very good professor, I also watch his old 265 lectures for linear instead of going to my lecture.
Explained all calc clases better than any other teacher.
The notes Prof. Chen writes in lecture are very clean and easy to understand. He covers all topics clearly and thoroughly. He is patient and wants to ensure the students all understand his material.

This man is the GOAT!! He will get you through any course you have him on. And that's a guarantee! Everyone watches his lectures instead of whoever's professor they're supposed to be assigned to. Every year he teaches a slightly different course in math, but whatever he chooses, he's instantly in high demand!

I personally had Glubokov for MA 162 but stopped attending his lectures as I got confused.  I then found Chen's 2019 MA 162 lectures and have since been watching those.  Thanks to Chen, I have gotten a 100% on all three midterm exams as he does a fantastic job at explaining the content and providing relevant examples.

Prof. Chen is a fantastic lecturer. He explains complex calculus topics in an easy to understand way. He is far and away the best math lecturer at Purdue, so much so that most students in MA261 use his Chenflix even though he isn't their assigned professor. He writes tough exams, but assures students that they will be successful in the end.

Dr. Chen has a true gift and passion for mathematics as well as teaching. He makes a very difficult concept very easy to understand. He is a very kind and genuine person and if you have questions he will make sure you understand the concept you are stuck on. Amazing man and professor.

Dr. Chen is awesome. Doesn't matter who your "professor" is, watch chenflix. Explains the material well, but just because he explains things well and is a good lecturer does NOT mean this is an easy class. Textbook is sorta required because you need to buy it to do the homework. Overall great professor, hard class.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Dr Chen is a good lecturer, not as GOATed as some claim he is, but still very solid. However, I found myself struggling in the class due to hard tests that weren't as simple as the lectures made it seem. It also doesn't help when the tests are worth 72% of your grade. Overall, he is a good prof and I'm very thankful for the large curve.

Dr. Chen is a great lecturer. His notes are easy to read and he gives clear explanations of topics. Unlike many of the other math profs here, he has legible handwriting. However, his exams are extremely difficult and stressful. At least he lowered the final grade cutoffs by 15%, or I wouldn't have passed.
THE GOAT
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Hearing a lot of good things about the professor before getting into the course were simply very wrong! He makes the hardest exams compared to past papers and his lectures doesn't help at all.
The exams were very difficult, I literally studied for them for weeks and still did horribly bad. The professor classes were not that helpful. Simply overrated
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Chen is the best calc professor at Purdue.
Chenflix. Just go watch Chenflix. For class, for fun, alone, with friends. It doesn't matter. Just watch it.

While I only watched his videos on Chenflix, Dr. Chen performs extremely well in terms of explaining concepts and making analogies. He understands that most students have trouble visualizing problems, so he goes through many practice problems thoroughly. Overall a pleasure to watch.

Textbook not required at all. You can get it if you want to do textbook practice problems, but I wouldn't advise this. You can find plenty online and on the course website. All you need to purchase is the Mylab and mastering, which is the platform that the homework is done on. This can be a resource for studying practice problems as well.
JUST AMAZING YOU DON'T EVEN NEED HIM AS A DESIGNATED PROFESSOR. JUST WATCH CHENFLIX FOR CALC3
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Dr. Chen is a life saver. If you hate your math professor, you can always rely on chenflix.

Chen teaches like you've never heard of Calculus before. It's really helpful and straightforward, and he makes class super accessible. Not to say it will make Calc 3 easier--it's notoriously hard--but Chen gives a really good base of understanding and is accessible outside of class. Overall, fantastic lecturer. Good pace and keeps class engaged.

Chen is the best math professor I've ever had; his lectures are clear and easy to understand and all his notes & recordings are posted online which makes the information super accessible. I would definitely recommend watching his lectures for 261 even if you are scheduled for a different professor.
I love him. best professor ever
Great!
Best ever

Dr. Chen has hands down been my favorite professor I have had in my college career. I watched his videos to get me through all the math in my curricula and he has taught me from Calc 2 all the way up to differential equations. He is not only a great lecturer with neat notes, but he is very kind and cares about the well-being of his students.
This class is exam heavy but Chen is a super nice guy. Definitely recommend to take his course.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: By and large one of the best professors I've ever encountered. Lectures are simple, concise, yet manage to convey complex material with relative ease. Rarely gets off-topic. Tests are weighted for a majority of your grade but are relatively simple (given you do homework, study, etc). Overall, I am extremely happy with Chen being my professor.
Dr. Chen explains the material really well. He gives you what you need to pass the class.

Chen is literally the most genuine person ever. He understands what it is like to be a student and he really strives to work with you. You don't have to go to the 7:30am lecture. He posts all his videos to ChenFlix about an hour after the lecture. Wonderful experience. The class is hard, don't get me wrong, but Chen has got your back.

The best professor I have encountered in all of my academic career. I used his lecture videos for all of the math courses I took at Purdue. I have not found any person or source on all of the internet that can teach better than this man. Even if you don't have him I recommend using his lectures for any Purdue math course.

Had him freshman year for calc 2, havnt had him since but I have used his boiler cast videos since for 2 other math classes, he is very good at explaining things and the way he writes down notes is very clear and easy to follow.

I used Chen's lectures to clear up some confusion after my first lecture with my assigned professor and since then I stuck to watching Chen. He creates great review problems and makes the homework manageable by covering lots of practice problems.
The best professor, just watch Chenflix. He is outstanding.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Test heavy

reviews.comment: I had a different professor but used Chenflix instead. His lectures are super helpful as he explains everything very clearly.

Chen is a great professor who makes all of his lectures open to all students. He wasn't my original professor but I soon just started watching his videos. If you don't know ChenFlix, then you don't know Purdue.

Dr. Chen is one of the best math professors I've ever had. I took the class online and was able to understand everything from his videos. You definitely need to put in the time to understand the content but if you paid attention to his lectures and did the homework the exams were very fair. 11/10 professor in my book

What else can I say? Chen is a Purdue legend. In the midst of COVID, his online lectures were exceedingly clear and well-done, and not a single mistake was ever left uncorrected. The grading isn't the best, as I had an A+ before the final alone sunk my grade to a B-, but otherwise I would def take this class again to watch some more Chen lectures.

Professor Chen is a miracle to the Purdue Math department. Purdue Math is ruthless, but Professor Chen is a guardian angel. His lectures are simply beautiful and incredibly informational. The professor himself is very forgiving.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: The online course was essentially watching Chen-flix and doing homework/tests on mylab. I rarely used the actual textbook, the lectures are cover the material. Chen is great at explaining concepts. This was one of the best handled asynchronous class I took; it was well organized and executed.
i want to have his kids
Amazing lectures. Purdue legend.
explains sooo clearly

Didn't technically have professor chen when I took the class, but basically stopped going to lectures in favor of watching his prerecorded past lectures. He is simply the best, the absolute gold standard in teaching. This guy understands what he's teaching and how to teach it.

Professor Chen is amazing! I took MA261 online this summer. Although it was an online course, he still records new lectures every week, instead of using old recordings. He makes this class very well organized and well explained, and he always encourages us to ask questions.

Not sure what the last review was complaining about. I am in the same class right now (summer 2020). And yes, the exams are challenging, but not outrageously hard or unreasonable (all the exams have averages in the high-70's), and all the problems are very similar to homework problems. Quizzes are written by TAs and can differ by a bit.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Took MA 216 MultiV online and there was no partial credit. Quizzes were like 2 points each with only 2 questions so getting F's on quizzes wasn't uncommon. Tests were also extremely difficult with no partial credit, all multiple choice. I've never gotten below an A on a math exam before. Got C's on all his exams.

This professor is absolutely amazing. He explains everything really well with organized and helpful notes! You can tell he really cares about his students and does everything he can to help them succeed.

Dr. Chen was a great professor! I had him in the spring of 2020 (Covid) and he was my only professor that still gave lectures. He was always open to questions and in person lectures were great while we had them! I had friends that would skip class and they didn't do very well so make sure you go to lectures and Si sessions for extra help!

Finished all my required math classes, and still go to his lectures often, just to hear him talk. I think that says enough. If you have him, GO TO CLASS. If you don't have him, and your professor is terrible, skip class and watch his lecture. This man is a Legend.
Had him for Calc II and it was a very positive experience.

Had him for calc I, II, III and linear algebra. Technically he was my real professor only for calc I, but for the other ones, I watched his lectures online instead of going to class (like most people). An amazing professor that explains difficult concepts clearly.
One of the most respected professor at Purdue.

Never actually had him as a professor unfortunately, but his online lecture uploads saved my grade in both MA261 and MA303. He really knows what hes teaching, and also is very good at explaining if you don't know what is going on. Would also recommend his office hours, he sees people that aren't even in his class!
Awesome lectures that have great examples and his note style is extremely easy to refer back to when working on homework/studying

Prof. Chen is a Calc god. I am retaking MA16100 and he is my lecturer. My lectures are at 7:30am, and he is the reason I drag myself out of bed and walk the mile from my dorm to the lecture every day. He explains the material in ways that my last professor did not, making it so much easier to understand. Do yourself a favor and take his class.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures--Clear grading criteria--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: Professor Chen is such a clear and concise lecturer. Calculus is just personally a tough course for me, but he has made it as easy a possible for me to succeed. Do your work, pay attention, go to class, and you'll do great.
I watched all of Professor Chen's online lectures for Calc. 3. He explained everything very clearly and gave good examples. This man is the GOAT.
This man is a god among mortals! If you watch his Boilercast lectures for Calculus 1-3, you'll be all set.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: The best professor Ive had so far at Purdue. He is clear and concise with his explanations. His handwriting is very legible. He comes prepared and is able to teach the material very well. All of his lectures are uploaded online a couple hours after. The tests were pretty difficult.

Hard to know what you're getting with purdue math teachers but dr chen is easily the best math teacher i've had. His classes are normally at 730 which sucks, but I've watched his lectures online for 3 semesters and have done really well.
He is nothing but awesome. i had a 7:30 class in morning and all the entire world would have not made me go to that class. The only reason i went was joseph chen.
I love his class so much that I'm gonna take it again next semester! Even though I'm failing 261, he is a great lecturer and his 7:30's are worth getting up early for.
Had him for 161, 162 and now 261. Best professor ever. I recommend watching chen lectures for 162 the most
Great Prof!
Clearest math lectures ever, fairest math exams. Bonus that all his lectures are recorded. Even gives you notes
reviews.rating_tags: ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Chen is an excellent and helpful lecturer. I stopped attending my scheduled lectures with Buzzard and instead watched Chen's lectures on BoilerCast. MA 162 is a challenging course and the difficult exams sucked all of the fun out of the course for me. I recommend watching Chen's lectures, practicing as much as possible, and getting help. Good luck.

I never went to lecture because he posts all of his lectures online. His lectures are very good because of his great handwriting and great explanations. After watching other math professors' lectures, I feel like I've been spoiled by Chen.
I stopped going to my lecture pretty fast and started watching his lectures online... Highly recommend.

He's the best! Honestly, the best Maths notes I have ever seen; clarity and perfect knowledge of the subject. He uploads all his notes and LECTURE VIDEOS ONLINE so you don't even need to go to class. But tbh he explains everything the best way possible.
Loved his lectures! Gives great explanations of the material he is covering and genuinely tries to help us understand the material thoroughly.
amazing professor. very clear and explains things great
reviews.rating_tags: ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Amazing lectures--Would take again

reviews.comment: His notes are really great and go along with the homework. The exams are tricky but he's very willing to help students and is very accessible! I'm not one to usually go and talk with professors, but I went to him after exams I didn't do well on for advice and he was very helpful!
I am a junior at Purdue, and in 3 years I have not had a better math professor.

Dr. Chen's online lectures for MA 261 were extremely helpful. He unfortunately didn't teach a section this year, but his lectures from 2016 covered the same material. Always works many examples and explains formulas and examples well.
Really just a quality dude- actually cares about the course and always uses great examples

Professor Chen is always welcoming. He has a lot of office hours so it is always easy to find him outside of class and if you have any quick questions he is very good at responding to e-mail in a timely manner. Totally recommend his lectures!
reviews.rating_tags: ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Amazing lectures--Would take again

reviews.comment: He is amazing!!
Very good professor, teaches utilizing a lot of examples that are similar to what is on exams.

My best math "teacher" to date. He explains concepts very well, and what used to take me 10 classes to learn in high school, he can teach it in 50 minutes. His notes are easily understood and clear. He has boilercast uploads which make it great to review. He is funny at times, very friendly, and is always willing to help during office hours!

He is an extremely eloquent professor. He simplifies all the material he teaches so that we are embedded with the concepts, rather than just over-complicating things- something our professors here at Purdue love doing. He teaches as if he wants everyone to do well, and doesn't care as coming off as an easy Professor. He is a real gem!

Simply a wonderful and very methodical, lovely Math professor. Very very neat and clean steps in his notes and his presentation of lecture material and application is very good if you watch his lectures. Also very open and very willing to share any materials or lectures that can benefit all students. He has taught all Math classes at Purdue.

Simply the best math professor I have ever had in my life. Most of his lectures are at 7:30, but it is WELL worth it if you can get him as your lecturer. He is clear, concise, and actually knows how to teach math. I would LOVE it if he could teach all of my other math classes.

Holy cow Dr. Chen is great. Actually had a different professor for Calc 3 and I skipped all of his lectures so I could watch Chen's lectures online. Great handwriting, explains things at a reasonable level and pace. Gave me an unreasonable standard to hold the rest of my professors to.
Greatest math lectures I've ever seen at Purdue, and I am a junior, so I've been through a lot.

Great Professor. Had him for MA 261 and MA 303. I took 303 online, but he posts his lectures online for all his classes, so even if you don't have him you can use his lectures. Super helpful during lecture and during his office hours. Always responds to e-mails. Wish I had him for every math class at Purdue.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: The lectures are recorded and put online-- helpful for when those 7:30s are not your thing. 2 quizes a week; difficulty varies on TA.
His notes are always on point and he always keeps communication between us and himself whenever there are changes. He always uploads notes and lecture recordings. Very clear and easy to follow.
His lectures are full of information and easy to read. Better than Khan Academy.
Professor Chen is the best math professor I have had at Purdue. He cares about his students and is willing to put his extra minute to make sure that you understand the material.
Prof. Chen is wonderful. If you have the choice pick this guy. I've had him twice for MA 261 and now MA 303. I'm getting through this class just because of him. His notes are a good review.
Love this dude. Great handwriting and clear and concise explanations and examples.

Best math professor at Purdue. He records all his lectures and posts them online. Lectures are at 7:30, but attendance is not mandatory as lectures are online. Lectures are extremely helpful for this very difficult as he runs through lots of homework and exam type problems. Very lenient on make up exams. He let me retake the final that I missed.

One of the best math professors at Purdue. Many of my friends stopped going to their own lectures and watched Chen's video lectures on Boilercast instead. He speaks very clearly and goes at a pace that you can follow along. Also go to his office hours if you can, he actually wants to make you succeed.

Probably the best professor I will ever have in terms of raw ability to teach. Makes what is considered by many to be the hardest lower level undergraduate math course actually quite fun and I'd go as far as to say easy imo. He talks slowly, but that's a good thing because it gives you ample time to process everything and he doesn't fall behind.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Hilarious--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Great at explaining concepts. Cares about his class. One thing that could be fixed is the arbitrary grading system that the Purdue Math Department uses..

Professor Chen is really good at what he does. Even in large lectures, he is easy to understand and explains all of the concepts really well. He may make a rare mistake but he acknowledges the mistake and corrects it instantly.   The best part is that you do not even have to show up to lecture. You could watch his online videos and still do well.

Outstanding professor! Would say that the only problem is that the class starts at 7.30 in the f-ing morning. Then again, he has BoilerCast lecture videos posted on BlackBoard after every class. Getting textbook is not mandatory if you attend his class. Alos very helpful in answering questions, via email or through office hours.
Awesome professor for MA 261, best lecturer out there for large group of students. Clear and concise examples.
Chen is an amazing lecturer. He is always prepared for class and he presents difficult material in an incredibly clear manner. If he taught all of the math courses, I would major in math.
He is 100% amazing at explaining higher dimensional mathematics.

Love Chen! Such a great professor. He does have 7:30s but you can always skip class and watch the lectures he posts online! Has the occasional jokes that are hilarious. Overall, really genuine guy who cares about his students. His lectures are also usually very clear.

Students admire Professor Chen and would like to take him for every math course they take.  He explains everything so well and is worth waking up at 7 to attend his lectures.  best of all, his lectures are on boilercast so that way students can review his lectures after lecture.
The best professor at Purdue by far!
Professor Chen is an amazing teacher.  I had him for Calc 3, which is a hard class, but he's by far the best professor for it.  Kids who don't have him ask kids with him for their online lectures!
Really good professor for a difficult class.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Solid math professor, goes over topics very clearly and works through lots of examples.  You have to pull your weight a little bit and 7:30 lectures aren't ideal but they're worth it.
BEST CALCULUS PROFESSOR AT PURDUE!

Professor Chen gives very good-quality lectures with many examples on top of concepts. The examples he gives are always relevant to homework/quiz/test questions. I have him for MA 261 and it is definitely obvious that he knows his stuff. He's pretty funny too.

The best math teacher I have ever had, he does a phenomenal job explaining concepts and makes 7:30's enjoyable. Even if you're not an early-riser it's not a problem, he sends out recorded lectures and notes. He'll actually make you enjoy math unlike the other math professors at Purdue. He values learning and wants to see you succeed. DO HOMEWORK.

This man is an amazing teacher! He comes to every 7:30 lecture excited to teach and does a perfect job explaining the material. All of his examples are similar to the homework which really helps. He is very approachable for help. He is worth waking up at 6am three days a week. I'm taking his MA 261 class next semester because I enjoyed him so much!

Amazing professor. You can easily notice that he hard work that he puts into the class because he genuinely wants to see his students excel. He doesn't make things harder than they have to be. He explains everything thoroughly, clearly and makes even 7:30 classes worth going to.
Great professor! Very clear and answers all questions clearly. Highly recommend any of his courses.

I switched to Chen after having Catlin for four weeks and this probably saved my calc 2 career. Chen is funny, super into what he's doing, and explains everything wonderfully. Calc 2 is quite hard, but now that I have Chen's lectures, I'm totally with it.

Good instructor, very clearly explain the class. Another hard class, but with his help it's gonna be fine. He sends notes and videos after every class, so even though that's 7:30 class you still will be fine.  But the exam is still not easy either, and you still must be hard working

Good instructor, very clearly explain the class. Another hard class, but with his help it's gonna be fine. He sends notes and videos after every class, so even though that's 7:30 class you still will be fine.
Really a great professor compared to others! I think I can get a A+ if my MA 261 teacher is as good as him.

Awesome guy! Loved him. Clear notes. Nice sense of humor. You can even skip the class, b/c he would send recorded notes (video including his voice and notes) every time after the lecture. I wish i could have him for calc III. Once more - amazing guy!
Best of the two lecturers in Fall of 2015.
Great professor. Lectures are clear and have a good structure every time. Also records lectures and emails them to the class which is a life saver considering the lectures were at 7:30.
Great professor. Clearly goes through material while adding humor. Furthermore, posts all lectures online, so I never had to actually go as it was a 7:30 class.

Attendance isn't mandatory, but you really need to make sure you don't miss lecture. Chen takes time to go through a plethora of examples to make sure his students completely understand the material, and if you don't quite get something during lecture, he's always in his office hours to offer more help. For MA162, you can't get better than Chen.
Awesome class. Very tough tests and material.

If you're gonna take MA 162, you're not gonna get better than Chen. Really amazing. Very easy to understand  and he has a genuine interest for what he's teaching. You must study for tests, otherwise you will definitely fail the tests. If you have trouble understanding something, he's always at his office hours.

Chen really cares about wether or not his students understand his lectures. He goes through many practice problems and sends you the lecture notes and lecture through email after class. The material can be hard but he does his best making sure you are well prepared and know exactly what kind of problems will be on the test. Amazing professor.

He is a very good professor. His lectures  are clear and cover nearly everything you need to know for the homework, quizzes, and tests. He has a genuine love for Calculus and it shows. The class can get rough at times, but Chen is there to help when you need it. He also has all the lectures recorded and emails the link to us every day of class.
He's a solid professor! For calc II, he's about as good as it gets.
So awesome. Examples done in class are directly on the homework and tests. The subject material is tough but if you study, you should be good.
I love the class even though I'm not doing too hot in it. He is very helpful. Tests are tough but will certainly prepare you for any other math you take beyond Calc 2. Highly recommend.
The lectures are very helpful, and easy to understand. All lectures are provided online and emailed to us.
He is a great lecturer, it is worth waking up for the 730's instead of going to the 430 lecture. He gives fair tests, and he does his best to prepare you for the tests.
excellent professor. clear and precise ! If you study ... You will learn !!!!
Everything he covered in lecture was on the homework and tests. If you go to lecture, pay attention and do your homework, then you should have no problem with the course.
He's a great professor and his biggest strength is his clarity.

Cui is a great prof., he is very passionate and explains things thoroughly. However, he goes fast in lecture and has a thick accent so it is hard to understand him at first. The exams and material of 301 is very hard. However, prof. Cui is very inspirational, and works with students to help them understand the material.

Cui's lectures were very fast, but he did a good job explaining things and gave useful examples. Homework and exams had a few more difficult problems but were fair. My main issue was that we didn't have practice exams.

This class was difficult. I always found that I learned nothing during the lectures, so I'd recommend watching the recorded lectures posted on brightspace which are very helpful. Cui is not a bad lecturer, and he seems very kind.
Very tough class
He'll get the key points across but if you actually want to learn and succeed in the class you'll need the textbook and other resources. Paying attention wasn't worth it most of the time.

Cui is a decent lecturer and I would consider him a safe choice if you don't have any good options. He has an accent but its not bad. His lectures are nothing special but he does a lot of practice problems which is nice, and responds via email decently quick, but wont care about any issues or conflicts you have, so expect no leniency.

He's not it. The lectures are the biggest waste of time. I have played an hour of clash royale in every single lecture this year. That being said, I know more about the material than anyone else I've met just because I only learn by myself. This means you only learn how not to do it by listening to professor Meng teach.
Do not take this professor's class

Amazing professor. He both taught the material extremely well and taught to the exams extremely well. Answered questions very well and was overall a good person. Advocated for the students after the average on the first exam was like a 45% instead of blaming them like some other professors.

Dr. Cui can be a little hard to understand sometimes (especially this semester with his gas mask) but I really enjoyed his lectures and he gave a lot of great tips before exams. He did practice problems, discussed a few hidden variations on formulas, and is always willing to help students. The course is tough but Dr. Cui made it bearable
Purdue ECE department is a embarrassment. Im not gonna write an essay but just know they dont care about you at all.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Group projects

reviews.comment: Lectures were mostly examples and problem solving. The professor used different techniques to solve circuits than the online course videos. He would give motivational speeches while we worked out problems in class.
Lectures were not too bad considering I had him at 7:30. Didn't have the same grading scale as other professors in the same course but it worked out in the end
AMAZING professor. Explains everything super super well and makes himself very available with office hours. Gives hints for exams and they too difficult or tricky. He posts lecture notes online.

Very good professor for ECE 201. Has Chinese accent but is perfectly understandable. Tells funny stories and always gives you the easiest ways to solve the problems. Had only one "quiz" that was worth 3 points. Gives extra credit points for volunteering to solve problems in front of the class.

Dr. Cui is exceptionally caring about student performance. ECE 201 is a hard class and often there aren't many good choices for professors. He doesn't have the best handwriting as others note and his accent can be hard to understand, but he is very dedicated to making sure students succeed. Lectures on boilercast and lots of worked exam problems.
Goes through material very quickly, often skipping steps when he is explaining.

Very good professor. Will answer questions well and explain things in lecture. Minor accent. Homework for class occasionally uses problems from textbook. Somewhat short homeworks online are assigned due before each lecture.

He's an amazing professor for this class. He may have a thick accent and pretty bad handwriting, but he is so caring and really goes above and beyond to make sure every student understands. He goes at a good pace, goes over homework solutions and lecture reviews everyday, and he hand writes all of his notes (which I love). Great guy, I recommend!
Very good performance in the class room. Very interesting and shows how things connect in the class. Love this professor.

He likes to write things down instead of using ppt(worst handwriting). Hard to follow his mind. You have to do homework prior to 2 days before next class if you want help with it.( HW due just before the class, classes held on MWF). And his office hour is right after the classes, most of the students couldn't get to it because we have other classes

I love this professor with my whole heart and wish she taught all my classes. She made chemistry make sense and I'm SO mad they swapped professors halfway through. She's awesome. Throwback to that time she sawed a cooler in half to explain chirality.

Prof Jean has been the best chem professor I have ever had. She teaches at a good speed and provides not packets that are very helpful. Her exams are not too hard or long. The lectures and Psos don't have attendance because she respects students' ability to choose how to spend their time. You don't need to buy the model kit that she asks you to.

Excellent lecturer. Teaches OChem in a very understandable way with lots of opportunities to practice. Prepares you very well for exams and for 256. One of the most passionate science lecturers out there

She melted away my deep-seated fear of organic chemistry and helped me to discover that I'm actually good at it! She makes intimidating concepts look doable and has many ways to help us visualize concepts, such as demonstrating chirality with a saw! I hope future CHM255ers can have her as she is one of the most amazing professors I've ever had
I love her so much. She's such a great teacher that genuinely wants you to learn the content. One of the best professors I have had!

She is a great professor who seems to care about what she is teaching and how her students are doing. Lecture is not required, but if you go, the content makes much more sense. Most of your grade is based on exams, but she does offer some extra credit opportunities.

She made CHM256 enjoyable even though its a tough class! Her lectures were really thorough, and she always made time to answer all of our questions. Most of the grade points come from the midterms and final exam, so make sure that you understand all of the lecture material for those and do the problems in the back of the textbook. Overall, great!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Had a really good experience in CHM256 with her. Her lectures were always really helpful and she has a really good way of explaining things. She was always willing to answer questions and overall just make a tough class very manageable.
Great professor that cares for her students. Definitely need to watch her lectures to succeed in the class.
Shes a good person, she cares. Give her chance and shes got a pretty good joke or two during lecture. Also, she has a few technical difficulties with her IPad but she figures it out.
Chmielewski is awesome. Excellent lecturer who really cares about her students. You cant go wrong with either her or Uyeda, 2 of the best professors in the chem department.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Accessible outside class--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I had her for both orgo 1 and 2. She doesnt do hw but does give problems to do at the back of the book those are very helpful. She knows how to teach Orgo very well. Its A LOT of work but she provides you with all the resources to do a good job. She answers question in lecture too. Notes packets are huge but she tells how to summarize for final

She gives really clear explanations of lecture material and is always open to answer questions. 255 and 256 are graded mostly by exams, but the grading scale is pretty generous and she gives a lot of extra credit so the class isn't too hard.

Professor Chmielewski is an amazing lecturer. I had her for Orgo 1 (the first Organic Chemistry course: CHM 255) and she decoded organic chemistry into something I could understand and follow throughout the course. Orgo became my favorite class because she made it fun and easy to understand. You have to go to lecture because she doesn't record them
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Absolutely love Dr Chmielewski! She made organic chemistry fun, relevant and not too hard. Offers multiple extra credit opportunities, some of them very easy. Explains concepts well. Do the back-of-the-chapter problems for extra practice. Get the model kit because sometimes you just need to see the molecule. Plus, you can use it on exams.
She is an amazing professor who cares and wants everyone to do well. She gives you many resources to help. She does not do boiler cast but does hand out note packets every week that you just fill in.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: She's a great professor, it's obvious she wants to make sure students understand the material. If you go to every lecture and recitation it is very possible to get a B or A with the extra credits she also gives. My tip: mechanisms seems to give people the most issues draw it again and again with simple and then more complex structures!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: As if CHM 255 wasn't hard enough, Dr. Chmielewski was ok at teaching it. Her lectures were interesting, but the subject itself is just difficult. It didn't help that the class was only out of 500 points (4 homeworks & 3 exams) with no curve!
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Dr. Chmielewski is awesome. She makes organic chemistry feel simple, and she has so much experience teaching the class that her lectures are well-understood and super organized.
