There aren't a lot of grades outside of exams, but a lot of extra credit is offered, especially if you go to weekly recitation.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: I LOVE THIS PROFESSOR. She makes something as daunting as O Chem seem like a fun challenge. Well structured lectures keep your attention and the note packets she gives out at the beginning of each week are EXTREMELY helpful. Use the packets to study for each test and you will pass without a problem. 255 is very test heavy, but with plenty of EC.
You MUST go to class or you won't pass! She is a decent lecturer and wants to help you understand it. Exams are decent as long as you know the material from the practice exams well.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: There is no one else you should take CHM 255/256 with. Dr. Chiemelewski is the real deal. Do the back of the book problems and actively participate in recitation and lecture. Tests are fair and should be doable if you study. Hands out extra credit like candy (attend lecture) and wants you to succeed. Examples in notes are medically related.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: She's very thorough in her lectures but not so much that she's hurrying. The exams in this class are a bit tougher than CHM 255 since the material is learnt more by practice rather than rote memorization but she generally makes them pretty fair. My advice: try your best to attend all lectures.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: An absolutely amazing professor. Notes are concise and clear, going to lecture is important. Very FAIR grader; if you work hard, you can get an A+, if not an A. Extra credit is offered, both in exams and during lecture (surprise!). TA office hours are the key to this class. Didn't read textbook but DO the BACK of CHAPTER PROBLEMS before the exams!
reviews.rating_tags: GRADED BY FEW THINGS--Amazing lectures--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Dr. Chmielewski is a fantastic professor. Taking organic chemistry was such a daunting task. She made it so easy! The way she lectures and explains the material is fantastic! She is very efficient,  and she makes sure to put a lot of extra study material in the notes! I recommend doing the back of the book questions! She is absolutely superb!!!!!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: While I don't enjoy organic chemistry, this professor managed to make it bearable. She is incredibly fair in assessment and grading. She is very invested in her students' success. If you need to take organic chemistry, you can't go wrong with this professor.

Overall, she was a superb teacher that made learning organic chemistry as interesting as it can be.  I would 100% take another course offered by this professor because she is usually full of energy despite class being at 9:30AM.  However, she rarely posts her lectures online, so while attending lecture is not mandatory, skipping is not recommended.

Great professor! Although you don't get points for class or iClicker questions 10/10 recommend going to every lecture! (Even review sessions) Notes are not posted online so need to get the notes from going to lecture. Weekly handouts are given out to fill out during lecture.  Exams are very reasonable and practice exams are extremely helpful.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Had the opportunity of having her for CHM 255 and 256. She's a great professor that wants you to do well. Offers a lot of extra credit. Material isn't too difficult either. She really wants to make Organic chemistry as fun as it can be. She also made us cake on the last day :)
There are tons of TA's and office hours that you can go to for help.

Amazing professor. She gives out handout at the start of every week. At the end of each chpt she gives you a bullet point list of things she want you to know. She also makes reaction maps and gives a great study packet for finals. Give out old exams as practice. All exams were fair, and she gives extra credit.

Jean is absolutely amazing. The lectures have packets that you just pick up at the beginning of class and fill out. I don't see why you would want to skip her class, but in case you do--- don't. Organic Chem isn't a walk in the park, and just as a heads up, the first exam is the easiest one, but props. the one you'll do the worst in
Great professor. Don't bring electronics to class... she will call you out.
She is excellent.

Chmeilewski is easily the best professor for taking organic chemistry. She made Orgo not just clear and straightforward, but interesting and even FUN. It's crazy how great she is. If you get her as a prof, you're in for a great semester. (Only complaint - she talks kind of quietly, even with a mic, so definitely sit in the front rows.)
Best chemistry professor I have ever had. I couldn't imagine anyone else teaching this course.
The best organic professor at Purdue! Extremely knowledgable and easy to follow! You are really lucky if you get her!
Made organic chemistry so easy to understand! I aced every exam! It's truly unfortunate I couldn't have her for chm 256...
Best teacher at Purdue hands down. Teaches organic very well. Class is structured very well, and the teacher is very helpful. Easy enough to get an A in the class.

CHM255 She is the best teacher, very cute and funny. Easy course as long as you study the note. I am still trying to get over the fact that she is leaving Purdue and will not be able to teach CHM256 ;(

This class is definitely challenging but I thought she explained things really well. She was also very willing to help any students with questions and tried to make sure everyone understood things. I would definitely recommend doing the assigned readings and practice problems and don't fall behind and you'll be fine in this class

Gives out handouts of all the lecture notes, and goes over them in class. Sometimes isn't as clear in explanations as she could be, but the handouts do make studying for exams easier. Go to recitation for clearer explanations, and do your memorization of mechanisms and you'll do fine in the class.

Generally crabby and unapproachable. Did not show up to her own office hours after I made an appointment with her. Chem help room was no help for this class. Only really has one teaching style for this info so if you don't get it, you're screwed.
she's a great professor for those who understood her teaching style with tons of handouts.  It didn't work for me at all..

She did not know what she was talking about. All she cared about was doing her own research and that is what she would talk about during class. She also could not tell us which way the electrons would go in organic reactions. Seriously, that is job!
Jean gives out a lot of handouts that are very helpful when studying for exams and learning the material.
Easily the best professor of the Sophomore Biology Requirements.  For every ounce of effort you put in, she'll be willing to match it.  Gave out free tickets to Duke game.

She is really clear in her lectures and uses relevant examples. Her exams are not bad at all for Organic Chem. The only issue I had was that the textbook was not extremely helpful to supplement what she said in lecture.

Only complaint is that I got an 89.5 and she wouldn't bump me to an A! I had gone to her office hours, attended every class (records kept with CPS) and had completed all homeworks! So while she's clear and a good teacher, don't expect her to help you out at the end of the year! (I'm still bitter)

CHM 255/256  Her style of teaching didn't really help me learn, especially with such a large class. She can be very mean, but on a rare day she's happy.  Hated the class though.  That's why I'm a bio major.
Very good teacher, with very clear explanations of everything and tons of extra credit.  The only reason someone would do poorly in her class is because they didn't study.
She is amazing at getting her idea across to you. Her classes are fun and the information I got from her is amazing!
I second that motion.
Chili pepper!! ... best TA's ever
let me add that the cake had little frosted benzenes on it! oh she's too cool- you'll be lucky to have her for organic

I hate organic chemistry. I've taken 256 with other professors at PU and dropped the class both times. But this semester I took 256 with Dr. Chmielewski I've got a B (before the final), so I would say she is a pretty good teacher. Plus she brought in a cake on the last day of class that said "Goodbye to Organic Chemistry." So I'd say shes awesome.
She is very clear about what she wants you to learn, and everything makes sense.  The class is very easy.

I love this woman, and love listening to her. Such a good instructor- so clear. Most always in a good mood- ADORABLE son :) Love most of the grad students too. SOO glad I got to have her both semesters-Study, read, and put some work into it and you'll do just fine. Multiply this rating times about 20 cause all my friends in there feel the same way!

She reminds me of Martha Stewart.  The way she talks, the way she thinks she's better than you, and the way she isn't.  Her class is boring, and she makes mistakes in the lecture all the time.  If I didn't take O-Chem in high school, I would have failed the first semester.  She bites.

Dr. Jean is amazing! Great teacher, great person. We love her and her team of grad students!! and the way she pronounces her words, the way she gets into the lecture, and just loves being an organic chemist!! Most of us don't even have an affinity for organic, but we sure do have an affinity for Chmielewski! :)
She's a good teacher, but the tests are still very difficult.  It's also like being taught organic chemistry by your grandma

Exams aren't the easiest things, but she is so great. Very clear, knows how to make you understand difficult concepts. This course is sooo much better than Purdue gen chem courses and I think that can be credited to Dr. C.
Great teaching method. Makes organic chem less cryptic. In
Awsome
I still hate Organic Chemistry but she is an excellent teacher.

I liked this class as well. She ended the stereotype of organic being hard.  She was very clear and I learned a lot. Maybe you messed up because you didn't read the book. It's a two step process. Read and go to class and you will get an A. 256 is the same
No Comments
You guys must have been the few that did good. I remember she explained it well, then the tests totally bombed. Half the class dropped after the cut off date
It actually made organic fun and not evil!
I thought I would hate organic chemistry, but she makes it interesting and easy to understand.  Her lectures are very organized and simple to follow. One of the best profs I've had so far.
Very hard class with hard professor to deal with.

Ronda simply does not know how to engage or instruct a class. She uses slides and information that alone would not prepare a student for their commercial written or practical. I have never had such a sad experience in a class as I have had with Ronda.
She does not teach what needs to be known. She didn't prepare me well for my exam at all. I had to use other sources to prepare. Attendance is mandatory by law.
Her lectures are the most boring thing ever. Makes jokes that don't make sense. Adds comments that don't need said.
Ronda is a fantastic person and a good instructor, but the course outline doesn't exactly prepare the students very well for the written let alone a checkride
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Ronda is a great person but when it comes down to it she simply cannot teach. We spent about 3 classes learning how to draw holds and hold entries and on the third day after her not teaching us properly for the last 2 classes, she shows us a 7 minute video which explained it perfectly.

Absolutely terrible professor. Ronda no idea how to teach. If you get her for a professor you should seriously consider finding tutoring or online help as she can't explain anything. Worst experience I've had at Purdue so far.
Ronda is great as a person, but teaching is a little more difficult for her. Incredible human being but simply doesn't know how to effectively teach.

Nothing could pull me into another class led by Professor Munt. I would change majors before sitting through another one of these classes. The whole class felt performative… it felt like he wanted us to be his adoring fans, and it never sat right with me. I got through it, but at the cost of some of my mental health. Received almost no feedback.

There was so much reading due each week and it often didn't relate that much to the class. Also there were a lot of long papers throughout the semester and I never received any real feedback on them. He seems really nice and caring but the way he structured the class made me dread going to it.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--So many papers

reviews.comment: He was kind and caring but he assigned a lot of useless reading and unnecessarily long papers just to give zero useful feedback on them. The class content got boring and repetitive, and it became difficult to bring myself to go to this class towards the end of the semester.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--So many papers

reviews.comment: He is super caring and friendly! Took his class for my minor and I loved it. He clearly cares about his students and is very understanding.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Get ready to read--Caring

reviews.comment: The class wasn't hard, and the content was okay. There definitely wasn't enough substance to make me love it. Professor Munt was kind, but gives off a level of arrogance/self-importance that definitely dragged down his score for me. Fair grader, but definitely could have behaved less like royalty. Teach a class vs. basking the spotlight/attention.

Christopher is a really nice and easy to talk to professor. He seems genuinely interested in his students' voices, thoughts, and perspectives on issues revolving around the LGBTQ community. My only issue is that he takes forever to grade assignments so it's tough to predict your final grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: very fair, write a couple papers and do some weekly assignments for a fair grade

Christopher is an amazing person. He is such an advocate for the LGBTQ+ community, and he is extremely understanding of conflicts in other classes. I would love to take another class from him, as class was very interesting and he is very open to feedback. I recommend WGSS 282 to everyone.

Yes, BIOL 439 is VERY challenging especially compared to any other labs. But the reasoning is important. He treats us like adults who can think for ourselves, he wants us to learn and succeed and there are more than enough resources to do so. He devotes a huge amount of time and energy into making sure we have fun and learn a lot.
he is super funny in lecture and really knows his content. The exams are based on memorizing test questions, which aren't that hard.
he is so funny and good at lecturing, it is so easy to pay attention in class, he is very generous with mcq's on exams and is very knowledgable when people ask questions

The lecture portion of this course was actually quite easy. Focus on memorizing the bank of test questions he has for each lecture and explaining the learning objectives for the exams. He explains the lecture content quite well. I found the lab portion of the course to be much more difficult.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: It's hard to respect a professor who creates his exams from a series of 400+ MC questions you need to memorize for each exam that probably hasn't been changed since 2022. He comes off kind but does not show effort in preparation of the course.

Dr. Pappas is an ineffective instructor. Success in this course requires extensive memorization of lecture quizzes rather than learning from lecture, which is often confusing and unhelpful. Exams consistently contained errors, and concerns were met with delayed or dismissive responses instead of meaningful support. Don't take BIOL221 w/ Dr. Pappas.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The dude loves smoking meats and loves cheese. Class is hard.

A fine professor, nothing special, but information is clear, though sometimes lecture can be info-dense. But, micro is already very difficult so not all him. Make sure to study like crazy; everything is extremely easy to study for if you give yourself time to do so. Lab is honestly more difficult to do well in than lecture, lab exams are impossible

Grading isn't harsh. Did not appreciate the occasional political comments. Do not take the BIOL439 lab with Dr. Pappas unless you are very passionate about micro. Otherwise, you'll probably finish the semester with a DISinterest in micro. It may fulfill 3 lab requirements, but it's not worth it without heaps of passion. C: I crashed out mid sem.

Dr. Pappas is a hilarious and passionate professor, but be warned, this class is very content heavy. The exam questions are pulled from a pool of 400 multiple choice questions, with additional short answers. To perform well, memorization of these multiple choice questions is key. No exam drops or curves, so you have to be on top of every exam.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Class doesn't promote meaningful retainment of information, as exams are created from a pool of ~350 questions, excluding the cumulative final. Instead, memorization of the right letter answer is the best way to complete the class as there is simply too much material. Exams worth most of the points. Avoid if you actually want to learn microbiology.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Pappas outside of class seems chill, but the class itself (Eukaryotic Genetics) was a mess. We didn't actually learn anything in lecture and just went over homework questions every lecture. There were no provided old exams to study as well. You just go to lecture to go over homework and you can't skip since there's attendance.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Caring

reviews.comment: 10/10 awful experience. I have never witnessed a class more disorganized and purposeful in being a weed out. The syllabus states that 1/3 of students will not pass with a C! If you like memorizing over 500 MCQ for Exams worth almost all of your grade and participating in chaotic labs with no structure then this is the class for you. Avoid!

This is the WORST class you will EVER take at Purdue. He even says on the first day that it is a weed-out class. This class is insanely difficult. He is trying to weed everyone out of their majors. I would HIGHLY recommend taking this class at a Purdue extension OR Ivy Tech. Don't waste your time and energy taking this class here.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I took the 8-week summer version of this course. 2 lecture exams and 2 lab exams, each of which covered a decent amount of content without feeling overwhelming. Plenty of extra credit built in. By studying question bank and lab manual, it was easy to learn and feel well-prepared. Pappas is an engaging lecturer and a funny guy; he clearly cares!

Overall, nice guy and cares about his students. However, this class is pretty difficult in regards to both lecture and lab. Make sure you pay attention to the MCQs he posts to review for the lecture exams. His lectures can be entertaining, but lecture quizzes are a thing and can be challenging. As long as you're on top of it all, you'll be fine.

He is an amazing professor. He is kind, understanding and extremely helpful. As long as you explain what you are facing, he is very open to help you, give extensions. You will do great in class as long as you reach out for help. Probably one of the most understanding profs i've ever met at Purdue.

The course's lectures were exceptionally well-delivered andpacked with info and that made complex topics easily understandable. However the sheer amount of homework was quite a lot requiring a lot of time investment to keep up with. Overall, it's a highly educational experience for those willing to dedicate effort to reap the benefit.

The class covers a lot of topics and it is hard to connect them as they are wildly different. The course is meant to introduce different architectures and is mostly manageable. The learning experience under his instruction proves to be quite demanding. Some labs are easy but after the first few you need to know some linux to work on Pi projects.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: The class overall wasn't too difficult. The labs took a bit of time but could easily be completed with a bit of effort. The lectures were extremely boring. I've taken a class that is similar to this one that is taught by professor Adams who does a much better job of sounding like he is interested in the material he is teaching.

I believe this class is the issue, not the professor. I'm sure he knows his stuff well but is not a good lecturer at all. Some of the labs make no sense and sometimes don't relate to the lectures at all. You have to get lucky with your TA, mine was terrible at grading and explaining. Extremely disorganized class if you don't know what you're doing.

Prof. Nadig is a nice person. He is incredibly intelligent and has done a lot of research in his field. That being said, he is a poor lecturer. His lectures are incredibly boring and it very hard to learn from him as he doesn't do a good job of explaining the concepts. The class itself is manageable but learning from him is difficult.

The class is easy but I have had two friends kicked out of her class for getting sick. she is not understanding about these things at all and does not accept doctors notes. you better be in the best health of your life while taking her class. she will make you take it again if you get the flu and are out for a week
A fantastic instructor. Grades you according to your existing skill level, so if you put effort in you'll be fine.

Ms. Dubikovsky is a very kind and passionate professor. She is always willing to help students understand material and will help with any questions you may have. She is particular about attendance though. I don't believe you can learn the material for her class properly within being in class so I understand why she's so particular about it.

The class is easy but just know that excused, sick absences with doctors notes are still counted to your absences limit of 4. Don't waste your time getting a doctor's note if you are sick. The professor will not level with you if you have 4 absences even if you have doctors notes for them. Also, if you are 10 minutes late, you're marked absent.
This class was actually not bad. As long as you attend class everyday and participate you will be fine. She sometimes plays favorites but overall she is very sweet and a decent professor.

Nadya is so sweet and wholesome, even though the class can seem a bit weird at times. Just leave behind your shame and dance, sing, and play the recorder with her and you'll have a good time. This class has helped me to feel more prepared to be an elementary teacher even though it can feel awkward sometimes. Have fun!

Nadya comes off strong and the class can seem cringey at times, but she is the sweetest woman and grows on you quickly. The class has been the one that I feel has prepared me the most for being a teacher in the future. Don't take yourself too seriously and just let yourself have fun dancing and singing with everyone.

She is absolutely lovely. She's very passionate about music in the elementary classroom and it shows with how interactive and unique her lessons are. She was very understanding when COVID first began, and continued to offer excellent feedback and teaching despite the challenges it brought on.

take anyone but him for this class his requirements are not clear whatsoever and most of the assignments that we do in class has nothing to do with marketing management. he does not lecture properly and the exam is built on very niche things where he says that there maybe multiple good answers but we have to pick the best one AVOID HIM AT ALL COSTS

I created a RateMyProfessor account because I'm shocked at these low scores. I agree that attendance was highly graded and that he took a long time to grade things.  However, he was usually clear that with grades we would all be okay after the curve(even with the class doing poorly on the pop quiz). Overall, he was a nice guy and a good professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: Pretty harsh with grading, and there's lots of assignments that he never gets around to finishing so they just linger on Brightspace ungraded. You have to purchase access to this business simulation website for 60 bomboclat dollars and run trials with a team. He's chill most of the time but also incredibly unenthusiastic at presenting/lecturing.

Very disorganized and difficult to learn from. Lectures are mostly random case studies with sloppy explanations and no slides or structured material to follow. It feels like you're teaching yourself most of the time. For a 7:30am class especially, the lack of organization makes it a waste of time. Would not recommend
Dry and boring is all I have to say. Ended with a 91.8 and got curved down to a B+ and he just says grades don't matter in the real world. Avoid if possible

Disorganized, unclear, and poorly run. Grades posted so late you never know where you stand. Midterm was 13 MCQ with 8 answers each in 20 min and felt designed to trick, not test. Teaching is outsourced to student debates and the syllabus totally ignored. Final project timing is unrealistic. Professor lacks basic course management skills. Avoid.
Sad Sad Sad, the worst professor at Purdue.

Every class feels more like a waste of my time. Very off script and has us doing random assignments that are not applicable to the class outline from Purdue. His grading is subjective and just based on ranking your group against others, and there is very little guidance besides trying to figure out what he may be interested in. Avoid.
I too would like to state that the one star reviews keep getting deleted. sus...

Dr. Nowak is an awful professor. Do not take this class. He grades unfairly if you disagree with him, and missing more than two 7:30 AM classes drops you a letter grade. Most students scored under 40% on the midterm, and you must write a paper to get the curve. I would also like to say that 1-star reviews keep getting deleted- avoid at all costs.

His grading system is inconsistent and highly opinionated. Quizzes are completely subjective and there is nothing you can do to effectively prepare for them. Class averages are almost always below a 50%, and he continually blames students for his historically low-class performance.

Dr. Nowak is disorganized and doesn't know how to prepare or educate students. He also goes off-script, ignoring the course outline in the Purdue catalog for what this course is described as. He makes snide remarks to people who don't agree with his viewpoints, which are opinionated and HIGHLY bleed into his grading style (which is never linear).

No syllabus, no lectures. You're intended to grasp random concepts from different Harvard business cases that groups present on but that he never lectures on. Very unclear what concepts we're supposed to be learning. Class average on our 13-question midterm was below a 50%. Grading is slow. Said he was a "type-B" professor, meaning disorganized.
Awful. No practise questions for the midterm exam.

Dan is a good prof. Extremely strict about AI and attendance (miss more than 3 and you drop a letter grade for every absence). There are a LOT of group projects and discussion posts in this class, but if you just put effort into it he grades nicely. Also, one surprise pop quiz that the entire class failed lol
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Group projects--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Professor Nowak says that he has experience teaching but honestly, I'm not sure that he does. His course is disorganized and he doesn't have a clear grading criteria or a clear schedule for the course. It felt like he was making everything up on the fly and nothing was planned ahead. Lots of group work and some projects were not relevant.

I liked Prof. Novak, he had a very large turn around from the beginning to the end. In the beginning you could tell he was a bit overwhelmed by being a new professor at a new school, but by the end he was comfortable. He's a nice guy, fair grader, and gives everyone equal opportunities. If you don't like group work, you won't like the class

Everything about him is great, except his teaching style. He goes way too fast, sometimes to the extend that you find it hard to catch up with the material and end up being confused. I have taken two classes with him and this has happened every time. Lectures very content heavy.

During lecture he appears to be a fun and relaxed professor. Attend his office hours and you will begin to see his true colors. He is condescending, conceited, and almost holds palpable contempt for you if you are visibly struggling to understand a topic.

Although he curves grades I would not recommend taking him. Had to teach myself.

Prof. Milisavljevic is an amazing professor with super engaging and funny lectures. He has a very clear understanding of the subject matter and conveys the information to students very well. 

If you attend all the lectures the homework and tests aren't bad - he tells you exactly what to study and where to find resources.
Great prof, funny but good lectures

Dr. Milisavljevic is one of the best educators I have ever come across. He is clearly passionate about the subjects and in learning with the students. The extra credit is worth it. Homework should be done ahead of time and be sure to study for the exams with a group. It would be beneficial to take Calc III beforehand.

He's interested in seeing students succeed and he's really nice. Definitely do the extra credit if you can. The tests and homeworks are really hard, and even if you study a lot there's always going to be something unexpected. It might be easier if you take calc 3 and are in a STEM major, which I wish I had known before

Clearly passionate & always trying to find new ways to help students learn (some classes done in virtual reality!). He also has a great personality & sense of humor, making his lectures super fun! The material is difficult, but he does his best to help you learn. One of my favorite teachers for sure!

Fantastic professor that very obviously cares about both his subject and his students, one of my favorite lecturers at Purdue, and taking more astro classes to learn from him again. Graded on 4 homeworks, a midterm, and an exam, all of which took some time and effort, but definetly worth it.

This is my second course with Prof Milisavljevic & he has been terrific for both! He has such energy, loves to crack dad jokes, & includes unique teaching methods like supplemental VR sessions! He encourages class participation & teamwork. His passion for Astronomy is evident!

Loved this dude's energy. Very enthusiastic about what he was teaching, always smiling, very interactive with his class. I didn't find it too difficult, the clicker questions are super important for learning concepts so I'd recommend going back and reviewing those before exams. Definitely recommend taking with Milisavljevic!

It's a physics class, so it's generally going to be difficult but Prof Milisavljevic is amazing and is very engaging. All lectures use iClickers for questions on topics in class, but he pretty much gives the answer in hints. Weekly homeworks that take some time but they boost your grade if you do well.
Great energy in lecture, explains difficult content clearly and with examples. Grade is heavily dependent on exams but there is usually a curve.

Had him for a class I really struggled with and I definatly think if I had it with any other teacher I would have failed. Would watch many of his lectures online and they were actully intresting. 10/10 would recommend

His lectures always woke me up in the morning with wild and wacky demonstrations. Attendance is mandatory and judged by iClickers, but at least the lecture is fun. He accepts questions and gets students involved in lecture demos and halts lecture by hiding until a brave soul answers his question. He is cute and spunky in a young dad kind of way.

Dr. Milisavljevic is ADORABLE and INCREDIBLE! He makes physics so much easier (but it's still hard, FYI), and will actually make you laugh in class. iClicker questions are attendance points, but he is flexible if you need to miss a day for a good reason! Study the past exams and the iClicker questions (LIKE REALLY DO THIS) and you'll be fine!

Prof Milisavljevic is a great professor, he teaches the material very well, and answers all questions thrown at him in lecture. He also gives about 2-3 iClicker questions each lecture, and normally reveals the answer before closing the poll so most people get it right. He also explains that iClicker for those that don't understand.
This professor is young and gives very lively lectures. Lots of iClickers in lecture and recitation--which are worth a ton of points. Past exams were basically useless to study for his exams.

Milisavljevic was not just a fun lecturer, but good at teaching the material. He also writes his own exams that are heavily based on what he said in lecture and in homework. Very understanding of our other workload too. Overall great, and if you see he's teaching the class and you know you need it, go ahead and sign up, you won't regret it

Honestly, he's gotta be the best physics professor I've had yet. He does his best to help out as much as he can, keeps the mood light, and teaches the material well. He's a pretty funny guy and cares about his students. No complaints at all here, only praise.
He's a really funny, smart professor that you can tell cares for his students. The subject is not incredibly difficult, and he makes it as interesting as he can and explains it as best he can.

Very energetic and funny guy, who understands the material extremely well, yet articulates it in a manner that a first-year can comprehend. Lots of analogies and demonstrations to aid understanding. Brought his nephews in for a demonstration of an idea. I became jealous and now wish he was my uncle.

Prof. Milisavljevic is a funny professor, and often tries to lighten up the mood in our morning lectures with jokes and sharing his research in astronomy. Though the topics covered aren't easy, he does explain concepts well and often invites students to participate in demonstrations with him. Overall most students in the class agree he's great.

Physics 220 is a difficult course if you haven't taken AP physics in high school, but Milisavljevic does an amazing job of demonstrating what he is teaching and if you talk to him at office hours it is obvious that he cares and wants you to understand the material. So many professors just talk, Danny teaches.

Prof. Milisav is a genuinely fun person to be around. He understands how difficult the material is and uses many demonstrations during lecture to try to best explain the content. Plenty of resources outside of class as well. Labs are a bit ridiculous, but that is out of his control. The final exam curve brought me up to an A from a B.

Here's a professor who WILL ROOT FOR YOU if you genuinely put in the effort. Very accessible and friendly. HW and lab assignment every week though neither have anything to do with exams. Exams very lecture based! No extra credit. If you're struggling, the book is your bff; not mandatory tho. There are help rooms open 2X/ week for extra support!
I was lost from the first till the last day. I ended with a 61% and still got a D+ tho. He rounds quite a bit
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: great professor though phys220 is tough.

Given the inherent difficulty of the course material, he does a fantastic job of relaying the information in a way that is easy understand and apply. He does a good job of keeping the class engaged, and he does a great job of making lecture worth your time. If you attend and participate lecture and recitation you will do well in the course.

I took 410 and 410 with him. Uses his own book which is exactly what he teach in the class, and both are not very helpful. Doesn't really answers the questions students asks (doesn't like being questioned repeatedly). Few questions on homework but they take forever. Grading is pretty easy though. He is basically the typical old physics professor.

(PHYS410 H Mech I is now defunct) For intermediate mechanics, Nolte teaches from his own textbook, which is adequate but not ideal (the good part is beyond the scope of a non-honors class). When answering questions, he tends to talk in circles until the questioner gives up. Class is difficult and time consuming, but the grading is generous.
Not a big fan of his teaching style.
Hes a great professor but my god the homework and exams are long. Dont expect any plans over the weekend if you have them. Most do fine just the typical Purdue physics course
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Lots of homework--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: He is great at teaching the material and is very clear about the things you should expect on the exams and how to prepare for them. The class isn't easy but its very fair. If you do the work, go to lecture, and study, you'll do fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: He knows the material however seems to forget that his students are taking this course for the first time. Class average was consistently a D the entire semester. Very test heavy and likes to try to trip up students with his questions. Be ready to have to teach yourself and pay attention in the lectures. Avoid at all costs.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: He gives good explanations of the material, and he is interesting and lively when he lectures.  A very good professor overall.
Nolte takes the act of teaching the material more seriously than any other professer I've had here.  If you have him, you'll get to enjoy the process of learning again.

The course felt disorganized, with lectures lacking clear progression and connections between topics, making it hard to build a strong foundation. Slides were dense and often just read aloud. Exams emphasized unclear details, and limited feedback made it difficult to improve.

He didn't know the material he was teaching and only ever lectured with PowerPoints from other teachers. When asked a question he almost never knew the answer. Also would take up class time with stories and barely go over the stuff on the tests.

He has a very distinct personality, in the school i feel as though you either love him or you hate him. I got in many arguments with him and almost always left my lab feeling more drained, frustrated, and upset then when i arrived. I know a lot of the teachers feel the same way about him and know how students feel.

Gouwens hates Siri. Be prepared to not be allowed to use your phone because Gouwens has a hatred for technology. Also be prepared to bring up ChatGPT in the presence of Gouwens, as he hates creativity of such tools. Gouwens does love sitting around in lab, so be prepared to get no knowledge from him. Wouldn't recommend if you want to learn material
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Gouwens wishes he has the presence similar to David Goggins. Unfortunately he will only be David Gouwens as he lacks the experience and ability to teach while imposing his "standard" of expecting everyone to know everything for a freshman course. Expect a lot of bite with nothing to back it up.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Terrible professor. Homework is repetitive and pointless. Boring in lecture. Very abusive communication style. Would not recommend.

This professor ran the lab section of the class and didn't know the material at all. The TAs ran the lab while he just sat there in the front. To make it worse, the TAs had differing instructions that made the class confusing. I had TAs ruining my gain and breaking machines, worksheets badly explained, and forced to use ancient equipment. Terrible!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Teaching is not the word to describe the work Professor Gouwens does. Instead, he sits around and watches you fail, then yells at you for not knowing material. Unfortunately, will not each the material, so it becomes a never-ending loop of failure and yelling. Fortunately got a slightly better grade after talking with the head of the program.

One of the most boring classes to take but he makes it fun with stories. Not too terrible class if you understand the content by studying. Don't think too hard over the quiz and exam questions as he formats them to start making you familiar with FAA written questions. Would take this class again for the professor and how to start taking exams.

Took this class semester and actually really enjoyed it. Honestly the content is really boring, but Gouwens made the class enjoyable and I still remember a lot from it. There aren't many homework assignments and your grade depends on the effort you put in. Pay attention, take notes, and study a bit and you will do well. Tells entertaining stories.

AMAZING professor. Tough on the outside, warm and squishy on the inside—basically a sarcasm-wrapped teddy bear. LOVES ducks as he has a jeep. Legend has it he once calmly graded 50 terrible lab procedures with a smile on his face and a duck video playing in the background.  Most well tempered person you will EVER meet. MAKES LAB BEARABLE.

Genuinely one of my favorite professors I have had. His classes are definitely a little challenging, but they actually prepare you for future classes and a career after college. He doesn't assign busy work without a purpose or make you do homework just to do it. A good professor who makes hard topics bearable and actually cares about his students.

Got Diarrhea in lecture at one point and cancelled class the rest of the week, passed with flying colors because Gouwens didn't realize I kept putting laxatives in his coffee so he kept cancelling class. Besides that every test there were like 3 questions that never made sense, he could be suffering from dementia but the jury is out on that one. -Q

The professor was at first a terrible instructor. Class attendance was mandatory and very test-heavy. At one point, I was on the border of failing the course. But then the professor pulled me aside and helped me figure out what grades I needed to pass. After that point, he and I always went over a topic so I could understand it better.

Professor Gouwens is a character of unparalleled variety, from stealing one of my pets, to doing backflips across the lecture hall to try and wake up a student. There was one time that he yelled so loud at a sleeping student that he set off the fire alarms in the lecture hall and we had to evacuate. Then there was another time he waterboarded me.

Professor Gouwens was a great professor. He was a tough grader on the homework assignments, but explained why I got the points off when I asked. He was willing to sit down with me and help with test prep. I would love to have him again as my professor
Taught the material very clearly and had a sense of humor. Not a difficult grader. Not super high workload.

I think he was a pretty god prof, he made a really boring and annoying topic more bearable by making jokes and having fun with it, he posts his slides and makes the class very easy to pass if you just do the work and follow the instructions. chill guy, can be a little annoying but i would take him again.

This was a lecture class so of course he did some lecturing but really chill. He brought his dog into class and if we made a paper airplane that made it across the lecture hall, we did not have to take the final exam. Wish i could have him for every class.

Gouwens has showed some improvement from comments left by students from previous semesters. His lectures can be a cluttered mess at times and confusing to follow. On top of that, he may also go on long tangents about his own personal life. The tests are simple if you look over the slides beforehand.

Not great. Makes attendance mandatory, then spends half the class either telling his same 4 life stories about when he "worked in the industry" every class, or talks about sports. He will also make fun of students for longer than the joke is funny, making the whole room uncomfortable. His exams are multiple choice, but the wording can throw you.

Professor Gouwens does not teach the slides, but talks about himself the entire class. He ends class 45 minutes early almost every time. He doesn't let us see the review slides before exams. He never replies emails. He accuses students of cheating without actual proof and punishes the entire class. He makes it clear he doesn't respect students.
Funny professor but, terrible at teaching, AT127 is also just difficult.

Professor gouwens is an awful lecturer who goes through slides at light speed while he then refuses to post his powerpoints after the fact. He will tell students they should take notes while making fun of them for writing information down.
He punishes the entire class because some students claimed to see others cheating on an exam.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He really couldve been a great teacher if he wasnt as egotistical and hot-and-cold so often. His emotions and the football season really determine how good of a day youll have with him. He needs to learn to take constructive criticism from students with Grace in order to grow. Good luck to future students

Unprofessional lecturer. First week of class, he asked if we read the rate my professor and mocked the students who wrote about him. Communal punishment. Student mockery. Poor environment. Grading criteria and point deduction is harsh. He has a power authority complex in the grade book, because he cannot control the authority in the class room.

Professor Gouwens is the only instructor I've ever had that makes me feel it's not ok to ask for help. The lectures and labs are incredibly disorganized. He is rude to the students and the other professors. He will treat you like high school freshmen. He does not seem to care about your success. It's like he's only here to get a power trip over us.

Very inflexible professor. Lectures don't seem to be taken seriously. He spends most of the time just talking or telling stories. Doesn't spend enough time actually teaching. Quizzes always extremely difficult and only memorization. Won't adjust anything to make it better either. Also did collective punishment when some people cheated on the exam.

AT 372
Too much tests and quizzes. Only professor who will give you in-person final in final week. Lectures are literally him reading off PowerPoint slides, but that's okay. I heard that this is his first year teaching. I am sure he will improve. He always want to make students suffer. There will be many cases you won't understand this person.

Doesn't tech, reads off of slides that were copy and pasted from an old book so you have to translate them in to understandable English first. Quiz' take up most of your grade but they are impossible to get good grade. he told the class prior to taking a the first quiz that last semester the entire class failed. he does not curve .
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Horrid. Had her for both 255 and 272. If you miss class and an ICE assignment, that's 1% of your total grade gone (ICE accounts for 20% of your final grade). Insanely rude and inconsiderate, never helps, and constantly assumes the worst in you. Exams 1&2 are impossible. The labs are free tho.
whoever gave a 5 has been paid
didn't learn anything and just waste of time

Said in class that quizzes are made with GenAI. Had am impossible requirement on the final (Lockdown browser and another tab open). There were 2 assignments per class period, plus 3 for every lab period. We weren't taught to code, just to copy examples.
not a great professor but easy class, exams 1 and 2 were hard but generous curve, exam 3 and final are free A's and she gives extra credit, good TAs and easy labs.

Make sure you come to class every single time because she hands out assignments that are due at the end of the class and you're grade will be deducted 30%. Labs are easy. Exams not even what she covered lol, her lectures are disorganized and expects everyone to know everything. you'll be succeeding in class then f up on exams and you get B.

Overall, Smith's 272 class wasn't too bad. She's a nice person but her lectures are a little disorganized combined with all the ICE's and practice's. As long as you study the slides and do a little bit of outside studying, you will do well in her class. Labs were not too difficult, and the group project was pretty easy as well.
much better at teaching 272. lectures were easy to understand and content wasnt that hard if you show up. attendance is absolutely mandatory if you dont wanna lose 30% of your grade.

not really fit to be teaching 2 important courses all CIT majors have to take. if your class average on midterms is usually in the 30s and 50s, i think that shows genuine cause for concern. only highlight is that TAs were good for labs and some extra credit.
Horrible at teaching, difficult to understand, makes students hand-write code

Prof smith was not that bad guys, some of these reviews are very dramatic. I had both 255 and 272 this semester and they were two of my best classes. Yes, the exams are hard in 255, but they were also curved. 272 tests were almost harder than the 255 tests. She isn't that great of a lecturer tho and the 255 7:30 class kinda sucked.
She is not great professor. But she teaches 272 better than 255. Her labs are not bad. 272 exams are not bad too.

She is really sweet person, but her lectures are hard to keep up. The wording on the exams are just really make you confused. Especially 255 exams are hard. I would recommend taking during summer time.

Smith is a nice lady but lowkey she does not know how to teach. I would go to her lectures and learn nothing, you have to teach yourself. Class is definitely disorganized, you have to know some Java before going into it. Overall class wasn't too bad, lab, in class assignments, quiz on the reading, and lecture reflections. 7:30 lecture sucked tho.
you guaranteed will fail the exams lol
I never felt the urge to either drop the school or drop the major this bad
why just WHY
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Smith as a professor is a nice person. If you can't make class for a valid reason she's pretty understanding. That being said the lecture's weren't done well. If you review the slides and use online materials you can still do well in the class. If you know APCSA shouldn't be too bad. Class assignments and lectures were trash.

Professor Smith is nice, but clearly not fit for teaching. Averages on the first few exams were incredibly low, and lectures did not make any sense, requiring students to revise the material on their own. Quizzes (online) were due before the content was covered in lecture. The final exam required a lot of writing, but all the answers were given.

I dreaded this class after reading negative reviews, but my experience was the opposite. Professor Smith genuinely cares, provides detailed study guides (some with exam answers), curves based on improvement, and even gave us final exam questions with a rubric. Professor Smith is organized, clear about her expectations, and always willing to help.

Doesn't give clear lectures, she bounces from one thing to the next regarding topics she is speaking about. Study guides are unhelpful and doesn't provide good study resources. Overall, bad at properly communicating content, creating a difficult learning environment. Lots of the "learning" I've done has been outside of class on my own without her.

I feel like although she was not a great professor, I still got something out of the class. If you read the book and study hard for the exams you will be fine. Reading these reviews had me terrified for her class when in all seriousness it's not that bad.

Probably one of the worst professors I've had at Purdue so far. Her exams were very obviously made using ChatGPT and also encouraged us to use ChatGPT to get our answers. She basically gave up for finals and is giving the questions that will be on the finals so that we can basically get a free 100% because the class average is so low.

Most of class was just being told to Google answers or ask ChatGPT—there was little actual teaching. Many students were confused and unsure what to do. She also posted surprise quizzes with no notice, like one on a Friday night due Sunday. It was disorganized, frustrating, and felt unfair. (This is a way trimmed down version of my long course eval)

This is undoubtedly the worst professor ive had at purdue. While this class isnt hard material wise, having this professor will make it extremley difficult to do well, especially if you have no prior knowldge of java or object oriented programming. Do everything possible to avoid this professor for now as she is ver new to teaching.

Worst professor I've ever had. Beware. She makes you write code.

Additionally she doesn't teach very well and tends to skip slides. Also don't even think about asking for a review guide because outright refuses to provide one.

Also tests are IMPOSSIBLE!!! Averages went from A to F :(

P.S: I heard she's teaching CNIT 272 now…. good luck 💀

I'm not as passionate as the others, but it was a pretty terrible experience overall. Historically this class has been an easy A for anyone willing to put in the work, but a 52 class average on exam one and an equally low avg on exam 2 killed my chances for an A, despite only losing a couple points all semester  on just the final project.
DO NOT TAKE THIS PROFESSOR! You will suffer, exam averages are an F, DO THE TEST OUT
Horrible Professor STAY AWAY

CNIT 255 with Professor Smith was an absolute disaster. Lectures were incoherent and chaotic, relying on irrelevant code with no clear explanations. Study aids were refused, and exams were brutal, with abysmal averages. It's shocking Purdue allows such a mess of a course—urgent changes are desperately needed to fix this nightmare.

Most unprofessional class I have ever taken. Her English is nearly indecipherable.  The request for a study guide was straight up denied. The professor insists that canceling class and not providing an exam review session is the best course of action as it is "our responsibility" to know what to study. Exam averages were in the F range.

Most unprofessional class I have ever taken. Her English is nearly indecipherable.  The request for a study guide was straight up denied. The professor insists that canceling class and not providing an exam review session is the best course of action as it is "our responsibility" to know what to study.

She is probably the worst professor I've ever had—and trust me, I've had some terrible professors. She seems completely unaware of what she's doing. Her ability to speak and understand English is poor, and she lacks the skills necessary to teach effectively. The average grade on the exams dropped from an A to a D.

Try to not get this professor. Worst professor from my time at Purdue. Surprised that Purdue is even allowing her to teach not just this class, but other classes as well. She yells at her students when everyone does bad on the exam, she changes her mind a lot, she is basically all over the place, and she is unfair in how exams are formatted.

Embarrassing that someone like her can teach at a school like Purdue. Lectures are impossible to understand and all over the place. After each class, there is an in-class activity that consists of entirely irrelevant code that you need to handwrite. The tests are impossible (Avg. 52%). I feel bad for her future students.

Absolutely in the running for the worst professor at this university. Had no idea what she was saying every single lecture. She requires an in-class assignment every lecture, can't be made up. Also has a very condescending attitude toward the students, and will not take responsibilities for her own shortcomings regarding the class's performance.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Professor does a poor job of teaching during the lectures yet makes attending them worth a large chunk of your grade.  One lab assignment is worth the equivalent of one days worth of attendance. had a horrible class average on both exam one and two and blamed the student for poor work ethic.

Prof. Smith is probably the worst professor I've ever had. Her tests are incredibly difficult for what was supposed to be an easy class, and she refuses to give comprehensive study guides. Her lectures are confusing and the lecture slides she posts are even worse. In-class assignments pretty much every day. Labs for this class are easy, good TAs.
Exam 1 average was a 52% and instead of curving it she yells at the entire class about how we didn't study enough.
The tests impossible the averages were in the F range.

Truly abysmal instructor, it is mind-boggling that this course is in an accredited program at a well-regarded university. Lectures are disorganized and disseminate minimal knowledge. In-class activities are practically meaningless. Exams are formatted atrociously and read poorly. All issues exasperated by the instructor's petty attitude.
Cannot understand this woman, and she changes the expectations for the class constantly. Her exams are formatted and worded terribly. Most definitely the worst teacher I have ever experienced.

Professor Smith is the worst professor I've had. Her lectures are very unclear and all over the place, with slides and code snippets being swapped to back and forth without a clear link. She notably took away the exam review day and blamed it on the students for not understanding her. Without prior Java experience, outside resources are needed.

This professor jumped straight into stack and heap memory before understanding the basics of java. Very difficult to understand what the professor is trying to convey during lecture. Her "in class activities" are pointless and waste time with busy work (made me feel like I was back in middle school). Highly recommend acquiring the recommended book.
Literally the worst professor I've had during my time at Purdue.

Teacher needs to restructure the programming class better and needs to remove the inclass assignment to make time to better explain the code/build it in class. Be prepare for sudden changes in the course due to emotionally fueled decisions.  Tread lightly and find outside resources.

This professor is by far the worst professor I have had period. Their idea of teaching is showing the slides for a maximum of 5 minutes before showing us code for the rest of class. While a hands on learning approach might be useful in the hands of another teacher, it doesn't work when she ends up describing complex concepts without explanation.

Horrible lectures that are all over the place with very little structure whatsoever. Rather than going over slides or working through a program, she poorly explains pre-written code for 90% of the class and nothing is retained. There are in-class activities but they are just busy work like "Copy this program with paper and pencil and then submit"
Continuously ends class 20-30 minutes early lacking content to teach, cancels class after student complaints, and refuses to answer some students' questions, and overall doesn't teach the class well.

The professor does not care about self improvement and self pity after knowing well enough she can't teach. 
It's never her fault she can't teach but the students fault for not understanding her!
Exams are not only horrible, lecture slides are messy and lack detail. And now we are expected to be philosophers to decifer her lectures notes?!
She is not clear in her communication and rushes through her slides. Does not keep it on time and is strict about attendance but teaches horribly.

Professor Smith is one of the worst professors I have had. Her English is unclear, and gets upset when students don't understand her; notably, she took away our exam review day because we "did not understand [her]." Her lectures feel like going in a circle and learning nothing. Without previous Java experience, you *will* need outside resources.

Smith is the worst professor I have ever had at my time at Purdue. She cannot teach this simple Java class and refuses to post previous professors' teaching material to supplement her atrocious instruction style. She needs to get fired.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Super knowledgable! Gets through lectures quickly and cancels class a lot. Does a good job warning you about the tough parts of the exam. He is also very laid back with logistical stuff; and this class is not bad if you took AP stats. They made it less work compared to previous years. I think his rate my professor is super harsh.
Professor was good. Grading on exams were fair, but lecture slides were a bit so,so and unclear. The textbook was better at explaining and problems on exams were easier than homework.

I feel the other reviews here are harsh; he did his best with the course material that he had to teach. He's a very caring and approachable professor. It's not his fault that STAT350 is designed very poorly.

professor was difficult to understand but the slides were somewhat useful. i felt he was good at answering questions. the course has a lot of overly difficult homework that wasn't very related to exams. exams were easier than homework.
The worst professor at Purdue

He used another lecturer's slides and recordings for "quality-control purposes", but I believe he should have given lectures himself. For exams, we were told to type our work and answers into lockdown browser, but we were told that we could upload pictures after I had already submitted. There was a discussion forum that he almost never answered.

Pros
- Insane curve, you won't fail this class
- Book is easy to read
Cons
- Required purchase of online homework service
- Horrible test logistics (typing your math into a non-LateX text window)
- Took someone else's notes and lectures (which were completely not worth watching)
- Doesn't reply to discussion forms

imo the class itself was kinda strange with all the R assignments. tests were graded very nicely (extremely generous with points), but lectures were honestly a waste of time to attend. i don't like the tophat assignments that were due weekly as the content he taught generally deviated/was more specific so that entire platform was a waste.
Why I'm still alive?

prob worst teacher ive had here. hes rly sweet so i hate saying that but i had to teach myself every single concept on my own. he reads off slides and doesnt do example problems at all. coding homework and 3 other homeworks /week that are irrelevant to the exam topics. dont go to class. its a waste of time. pretty close to hs AP stats but worse

He was a very nice and passionate teacher. The lectures involved him mostly reading off of slides without many example problems, so you would have to learn on your own too. He's always open to help if you reach out to him though.

Does not teach, just reads out complicated math out of slides. You will have to learn to code in R by yourself, and its HARD. Additionally, no curve at all and exams are tough (cheat sheet allowed tho). Don't recmend.

He worked under a course coordinator, so a lot of criticism cannot be attributed to him. He had a lot of material to cover each class. Lectures were very boring, but he is an incredibly kind and helpful man who you can access outside of class if you need. Class itself is doable if you do the assignments and study.

Professor Matangi is incredibly kind and understanding. The workload in this class is very manageable as long as you keep up with the lectures and stay organized. One of the great aspects of his class is the practice exams he provides before each test (very similar structure to actual exam). Highly recommend!

I would argue that Dr. Matangi did the best he could under the conditions of the course. There is a lot of content, the course powerpoints are confusing, and there's little to no overlap between hw, computer assignments, and exams. The content isn't too difficult to teach to yourself and he was extremely helpful and kind outside of class.

Probably the worst professor I have had. It is amazing how someone is able to make a class so disengaging. It wasn't even worth it to show up as I learned much more on my own than I did ever coming to class. He just reads off PowerPoints, often skipping over steps when it comes to giving examples. His tests are also so poorly structured. Avoid!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Matangi seems to genuinely care for his students and is a nice guy. However, this instruction for STAT 511 was quite terrible. He would just read from a PowerPoint and not show any process. I stopped watching lectures after the first week and learned on my own after seeing the lack of effort given on his part. Exams were fair.

He made this class way harder than it needed to be because he doesn't know how to write exams. He expected you to show your work, but the exams were online with only a text entry box. So you had to type everything out while trying to also solve the problems. It took forever and made them way harder. He doesn't know how to run a class like this.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: He just read out the PowerPoint from 2001, and his course design is awful. Maybe he's good at statistics but he knows nothing about teaching.

I have done awful with math classes at Purdue but I consider STAT 511 relatively easy compared to other math classes. It was not easier because this prof was good. He did not go through a single practice problem step by step which is obviously important. He just reads off a powerpoint. I am astounded how he never could understand student questions.
He is not a good teacher.

Martinez is undoubtedly the worst CHE professor at Purdue. He messed up every student's final grade, and we cannot get them fixed until the end of next semester, so our GPAs are lower because of his mistake. His lectures are incoherent and he makes constant mistakes. No one learned a single thing from his lectures. Avoid him at all costs.

This course was a disorganized mess. The lecture schedule was largely ignored. Error-ridden announcements were frequently sent out causing mass confusion. Grading standards were unclear and inconsistent between TAs. Released laughably incorrect final grades for half of the class and missed the final grade submission deadline for the second half.

Martinez shouldn't be teaching. Lectures were hard to follow and constantly filled with wrong information. Homework was a huge time sink, mostly due to poor explanation of topics or topics not being covered altogether. Grading errors between TAs were known and ignored. Failed to input accurate final grades for the majority of the class.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Lectures were a mess and exam contained content not covered in class. Unclear grading standards and homework was long due to lack of instruction. Bro should retire asap. Also entered grades horribly wrong when they were due.

Martinez needs to retire ASAP. His lectures were unintelligible and on multiple occasions he taught office hours in Spanish. His grading was all over the place. He had TAs grade a certain number of whole exams with subjective grading rubrics. There were grade discrepancies that he knew about and disregarded. This class was a headache bc of him.
senile and dont recommend

Simply put Martinez is too old to still be teaching. His lectures absolutely blew no matter when in the semester it was. Third exam was impossible, don't know anyone who scored above a 70. Gave us a week and a half to do a project that we were supposed to have available for at least 4 weeks. Do not take a class with him if you want happiness.

(CHE 435) Came to senior engineering lab 30 minutes late every session (even though students are expected to arrive on time). Does not explain assigned projects at all and expects students to be able to conduct an entire 9 week experiment on their own. Released scores for all reports on the final lab session. Solid human but needs to retire ASAP

He's kind and helpful if you go to his office hours but his lectures aren't reliable. He'll write formulas wrong (wrote a formula wrong on exam review day) and doesn't always give you the information you need to do the homework.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Nice guy
His lectures are clear and he's really generous when giving us 50 points for the extra credit option

I think Martinez is getting a little old to be teaching. His lectures are absolutely awful, he basically just reads the material from a powerpoint, and the textbook he uses isn't much help either. Multiple times he assigned homework problems over material that hadn't been covered, and his exams are over concepts that haven't been in the homework.

Lectures are honestly not the best, but he pulls all of his material from the book so it doesn't really matter. Posts all of his lectures online so it is an extra resource outside of the book. Very application based professor, rather than theoretical. Extremely caring and sweet professor. I would definitely take a class with him again.

I'm not sure if Martinez's teaching has gotten worse from the previous reviews but this guy absolutely blows. He has given us 9 wrong equations in lecture (yes I'm counting) which causes frustration on the homework. He seems to love the material but is the worst professor I've had at Purdue. Simply put he is a horrible lecturer.

Tells lots of stories in class and isn't focused on material. Is VERY KNOWLEDGEABLE of the material, but not the best at teaching it to a group. GO TO HIM if you have questions about the material, a really nice guy. Tests are NOT NUMERICAL, you must write out a PROCEDURE for solving the question.
Great guy.
Good humble guy. Always there to help, grading is easy. Would definetly take his class.
Professor Easley was a great professor. She provides a lot of helpful tips, and she makes the class easy. The few assignments are meant to help you for your future career.

She is a regular instructor if you have this class and you don't need any help. Its an extremely easy course. However, if you need help with a serious situation that relates to you needing flexibility- she is empty headed who will smile and be fake to you, and make things extremely difficult even though you have documents.

She is super nice, and the class is pretty easy. Attendance is required, but lectures are fine for a one-credit hour. Barely any assignments, and the few there are were easy. She enjoys when students participate, but will never really force you to.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Caring--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Emily was good for MGMT 295. There is a lot of work for her class and she WILL NOT accept late work. Attendance is mandatory, points are taken off even if you have a doctors note with one drop. Points are also taken if she sees you on your phone. I learned a good amount from the course. She's chill, just do the work and come to class and your good.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This is fairly new class but she does not know what she is doing. The class is not very useful information but its an easy A mostly. Its mandatory attendance and she is very strict on phones and stuff while she is "teaching", which is literally just her reading off slides of things she got from somewhere else. Do group work every single day also.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: she disorganized, unhelpful and made fun of presentations at the end of the semester. i think she really tried but she was new and i was just confused the whole time none of the information made sense and was usually wrong.

When I finished presenting with my group, she told me my information was wrong and gave me a hard time about it. We looked it up after the presentation and I was in fact right. Even after all of this, she still said I was wrong. Take someone else she is a headache
She is all over the place. Very disorganized, doesn't respond to emails. Always late to class. Her notes were nothing like the exams.

Statics is challenging for me but he is helpful in labs and gives lots of extra credit and gives lots of credit for work shown on exams. Office hours weren't helpful because he just asked me to work with another student to figure out my question. Still recommend him and would take again.

Heard this guy changed the way he teaches and I believe it if so many people struggled before. He did an amazing job getting me to understand statics where future professors just rush through everything. If you go to lecture you will understand it but if you don't then you will struggle. Great professor
He really just wants to do what is best for his students, 10/10 would take a class with him again!

Eddy Efendy is the GOAT of MET 111. He was always in tune with what level the class was at and would adjust the exams and homework accordingly. The labs were very helpful, he would would walk students through questions individually.

Lectures are brutally boring but he is VERY helpful in lab and is willing to work with the class and is open to curving things if people ask. Will remove topics off exams if he feels he did not cover them good enough. He will check your lab work before you turn it in so you get a good grade. One of the better profs I've had, pretty hard to do bad.

Eddy is the best professor I've ever had at Purdue! He genuinely cares about all his students and how they're doing in the class. If you didn't understand a topic or assignment, he encourages you go to his office hours and he will walk you through how to do it and make sure you understand. Very opening, welcoming, and caring professor!

Eddy has been one of my favorite teachers at Purdue. He may not have the best handwriting, but it's never bad enough that you can't understand it if you've actually been learning the concepts. He cares about you and will help you in lab, and will crack a joke and try to empathize with you. Overall, good teacher, great guy.

This class can be very simple as long as you put in the effort. Efendy is very gracious and caters to the students and listens to them well. He adjusts due dates and exam questions based on the feedback of students, he regularly surveys the students to see how he is doing as a professor and how the students are doing in the class.

This class is one of the easiest ones I've taken. The homework problems are litterly taken from the book and has an easy to find solution. Same for some exams. If you just work a few example problems you are over prepared for the test. Idk how so many other students are this bad at the class. Purdue needs to increase the requirements to get in.

I was having a hard time with the concepts of the class. I went to open office hours and help sessions held by Prof Efendy. In my opinion, Prof Efendy cares about his students and their success. I'm hoping he will teach other coarses in the future.

Professor Efendy is a good teacher, but is very bad about communication and help outside of lectures. I treid to get help on a grade I received for one exam, and he was not communicative and did not help me at all once I was finally able to get in an email conversation with him. I would not take one of his classes again because of this.

This man was the single least useful professor of all time. I had him for lab and he never even bothered to show up. I'm honestly glad since the TA's were much more helpful. The only time I asked him for help in person he told me to restart the entire lab three separate times. I asked a TA and they figured out what was going on immediately.

Had him for ENGT 182 as the lab professor. He is not good at answering questions in a way that explains what you are asking. He also will often respond in a very condescending way to questions asked and make you feel stupid for not already knowing the answer.

Prof. Efendy teaches MET 111 as a typical math course so if you come in expecting math, you'll do fine. It's not the most engaging topic nor does Efendy do a great job engaging you but he did great at teaching you the content. Overall not bad.
Hard to learn new topics and projects I never learned before, never offered very much help.
Took student feedback into account. Class itself wasn't super interesting but Efendy made it bearable for us.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Efendy had no idea what he was doing the entire year. His whole lesson plan, homework, solutions and tests were directly copied from the textbook that everyone can access online. Labs were never hands on, but just more redundant homework problems. He tried to make things easier at the end because everyone was failing but it was too late.

I took ENGT 181 with Eddy, and I didn't like it, but he has been a lot better in MET 111. He cares a lot more, and wants to get to know the students better. I think the class is paced very well, and I am very happy with the lectures this semesters.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment:
If you come in expecting a math-heavy physics class, you'll be fine. Most of the content is doing FBDs for various situations. Around ~10 decently long questions each week.
Other than that, prof. efendy does a decent job explaining concepts. You would probably want to look up videos explaining it but otherwise not bad.
