This man is just a drag to be around. I didn't do all that bad in his class, but I watched him countless times shoot down questions and give absolutely terrible answers. I have him again in MET111 and he is uninspiring.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: He makes this hard class harder than it has to be. Bad communication, unclear expectations, and too much work for the lab time, 2 hrs a week for 3-4+ hrs of work. DON'T do the extra credit project if you don't need to. This class was constantly frustrating on several levels. Seems like a nice guy but the class was terrible.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: He's lazy, that's pretty much how it is. He cancelled lab help sessions for one week because people apparently were not wearing masks in class. I find this unconvincing though because he was in my class once with his mask off, so if he really cared about masks all that much why would he break the rules and take his off. I think he wanted time off.

If you are sick once drop the class. He is such a pain to talk to and doesn't consider being covid positive as an acceptable absence. Also the class is taught by the TA's and I've never actually seen him in person, only a photo on the intro slides from the TA. The work isn't hard, but you are given an hour and a half for 5 hours worth of work.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The Lab's need updated for sure. It is difficult to get the labs done in the 2 hours given but if you ask the TA's enough questions they help you very quickly and if you don't finish in time you still get a 90% for the lab if you finish in open lab. Lab 3 was rough but the TA's were super helpful and made it so I could get done fast.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: This man hates life and makes you hate it too. He gives an UNGODLY amount of work each lab and is "surprised" when no one person finishes. Great bonding experience going to open lab every week but you can't worry about understanding what's happening when it takes SEVEN HOURS IN OPEN LAB TO FINISH LAB THREE BECAUSE THE DIRECTIONS LEFT OUT ONE NUMBER
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: He doesn't care. Actively tries to make students have a worse experience. I don't think he's an educator by choice.
Worst Professor I have ever met.
The class aint even hard the guy just grades like satan himself. Forgot a few measurements? Thats a 60% for you. The dude didn't even make the course material its all recycled from another professor.

He doesn't even teach the class you do everything yourself and then a TA grades it. If you have previous knowledge about 3D modeling software, easy A. He's an okay guy but he's literally never there, the TA did more work than him. I did enjoy the class but that way because I knew how to do CAD / CAM before I took it, also you get to use a mill!

RUN AWAY FROM THIS GUY especially if you are taking his course online. He and prof Jones would not teach you anything--one assignment pdf then off you go! Both of them avoids questions and don't want to solve problems. Efendy would like to give you extremely low marks although you followed every step he told you. NO SINGLE HELP--even final project.
Avoid this man as your course I instructor.

I'd rather get ran over by the boilermaker special than go to his class. He's soooooooo boring. There's a reason his name is E"DD"Y and that's because everyone gets a "D" in his MET 111 and MET 211 courses. Best of luck Y'all.

I'll start out by saying the material he teaches is not hard. If you did well on AP Physics C: Mechanics then it should be pretty easy for you. That being said, Eddy does not put any work into teaching. He doesn't upload any of his work, doesn't mind if people talk in his small-ish lecture, he just talks through his irrelevant examples and leave.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: I'm sorry, but if you have Eddy Efendy as your professor, you're in tough luck. Once you fail one exam, you're basically guaranteed at least a C. Exams are worth 40% of your grade which is a lot for a Statics class. What he does during lecture does not match what the textbook says and to be really honest, you learn from the solutions of problems

Third time in the class, and still don't know what I'm doing. Does two examples in class that aren't related to the homework in any way. He also sits at the play place at the Tippecanoe Mall. I have photo evidence of that
Only goes over few examples in class and doesn't upload class examples online
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Absolutely the worst course cause of the professor

The class really is not all that difficult if you can keep up with the work. I think most people struggle later on in the semester because they struggle in the beginning and dont ask for help and since everything builds to some extent on previous concepts, it can be difficult to bounce back. he has si sessions before all exams which can help alot
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: The concepts of his class are difficult, but he gives tons of examples. What you put into the class is what you get out of it. Make sure you pay attention, these classes are the foundation of this major, they come up in everything!!!
reviews.rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: You know its bad when exam averages are so low half the class fails and is sitting at borderline grades. The labs are hard and are graded there is homework and lab reports that are graded harshly. There is a test every 2-3 weeks and he is really bad at explaining things. Lecture is confusing and be prepared to teach yourself everything!
This man is why I hate college. He is one of the worst teachers i know. I am sitting in his lecture right now writing this review and you can't even hear him over other people talking.
The lectures are the worst. I actually knew most of the material in the class from high school but he made everything harder.

Eddy did a great job explaining things during lectures. The class is not an easy one to take though. Tests are very heavy and he cancelled a lot of classes that made it hard to understand with limited time in lecture. Overall he was great when we actually had class.

Eddy is a professor of a class where you either understand the material or you don't. There's almost no grey area in that comment. He does go out of his way to help those who need help with help sessions and helpful office hours. Good guy. Tough accent at first, you grow used to it.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: Eddy is a great professor. Goes out of his way with his help sessions and easy reachability. Has an accent, but very understandable. Explains topics well. Tests are very fair. He gives out reviews that basically mirror the exam. Highly recommend
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Eddys good bruh!
Very helpful and open to student's suggestions . Really wants the student to understand the materials. Very great class! I learnt a lot from it!

Pros: He really wants you to know the material, he did optional review sessions every sunday and is very easy to receive help. he's a good guy that cares about this students  Cons: Sometimes doesnt fully understand the problem he is trying to demonstrate. Handwritingdiagrams were hard to follow.  overall, not a bad for a new professor.
Prof. Hess has great examples and is very engaging for the students. She clearly cares about the students.
Professor Hess did great job with this course. Lectures were pretty engaging and useful. The course is well organized and deadlines are clear. No complaints.
Hess was super awesome. Most of the time in lectures there was either a guest speaker or a personal story, and all of the assignments were super easy.

Course taken Fall 2024. Overall, it was the course that I didn't like overall. She was a pretty average professor, seemed monotonous and ok at lecturing, but it was an ok class, just sat there every week to get my attendance points, but it was fine.
Professor Hess genuinely cares about her students and is very engaged with her students. ME 290 is almost all dependent on attendance.

290 is a really informative class. It's an easy A, but you learn a lot from the alumni who return to present at the seminar. The speakers are often inspiring and cover all types of industry within ME so its great for all students of all interests. Hess is an amazing professor who genuinely cares about her students and wants the best for them!

Professor Hess is a great lecturer and very easy to talk to. She made machine design very interesting and had very fair exams. The quizzes were very easy, but numerous, so don't get in the habit of skipping class. I see why she is considered the best professor in the department.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Beware of pop quizzes--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Prof. Hess was a fantastic professor and mentor for me. I struggled with the topic of machine design and my grade is more reflective of my poor test-taking skills than Hess' ability to pass information on. She gave relevant examples, was easy to get to outside of lecture, and is always looking to improve her course. Highly recommend taking with her

Professor Hess makes it seem like the material you're learning is easy. She's straightforward, down to earth, funny, and understands what students like and dislike so the class is reasonable. In-class quizzes usually on Fridays so show up, but you'll enjoy coming regardless.

There's not much to say about Prof. Hess. She is known as a top tier MechE professor for a reason. She is a fantastic lecturer and clearly cares about the students she teaches. If she is teaching a course that you are taking, you better do everything you can do get into her section. Favorite MechE professor by a mile.

Great coordinator for the course. She invited inspirational speakers but were sometimes boring to attend to. She is really caring about her students and how they engaged with the mentors. Activities were fun and only 2 "homeowrks" were needed to be submitted overall. Very engaging with the ME department news and opportunities.
Great lecturer, fantastic person. Lectures were available online for all sections, so even though I had another prof, I watched her lectures. You will need to buy the textbook for open book exams.

My favorite assignment of the semester was when Dr. Hess assigned us a "doodle" (i.e. a little drawing of whatever) for extra credit. It's creative professors like that that I prefer to learn from. Her lectures are engaging and often funny and she really shows that she prefers to help students succeed with the content. : )

A very chill but thorough teacher with a good sense of humor. Provides anecdotes and short videos throughout class to help split the lecture slides. Genuinely cares that you learn but won't give you any busy work. Everything learned is necessary for some type of problem. Quite possibly my favorite professor yet.
My favorite professor by far my 3 years here at Purdue. Very approachable and honest with students, easy to learn from, and always makes the class laugh.

Dr. Hess is literally a queen. She makes lectures easy to understand and doesn't want to make students stress more than what is necessary. She is extremely involved with student organizations too, so she is very approachable and fun to have class with. Lectures are full of puns and side-stories to wake everyone up when they're tired mid-semester.

A really good professor for machine design. Most students ended up going to her section instead of the one they were signed up for. Her office hours were also very helpful and she answered emails quickly.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Best Engineering Teacher I've had
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Group projects

reviews.comment: Professor Hess had great lecture notes and made extremely reasonable open note exams. When everything shifted to online due to Coronavirus her class was by far the most accommodating and had the best transition.

Best Professor in ME. This is the first Professor Ive had at Purdue that I genuinely felt that she cared about her students (both learning the material and outside the classroom). She does such a phenomenal job teaching the course material. Her sense of humor is great too lol.

Prof. Hess is a great professor that really cares about her students. I had a 7:30 AM lab with her and she felt so bad for making us come in that early that she brought donuts for the whole class one day. She teaches the material really well and does a good job of explaining hard topics
If ME professors were Purdue football games, she would be the win over Ohio State.
Great teacher. Actually cares about her students. First professor at Purdue that knew me by name.
Great professor
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Hilarious--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Doctor Hess has been one of the best instructors I have had at Purdue. She really truly cares if you understand the material and always goes above and beyond with the explanations as well as alternative approaches. She is very approachable outside of class if you need clarification. Also she cracks a couple good jokes throughout the lectures.
The best professor at Purdue, hands down. I have now had her for three separate classes, and she has been fantastic each time. I would highly recommend.
Excellent Professor, makes fairly complicated material easy to learn and enjoyable.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Hilarious--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Probably the best professor I've had at Purdue so far. Explains material well ,grades fairly, writes reasonable exams and just wants your full attention in class. Probably one of the best trade deals in the history of America, ever.

An excellent instructor for ME200. Thermo 1 is a tough due to the nature of the content as well as the overall organization of the course but Dr. Hess made it very manageable. Very accessible for office hours and gives you clear feedback. Has an enjoyable teaching style, cracking the occasional joke during lecture. Would highly recommend.

Dr. Hess is a very blunt professor and will actively call out students for not paying attention or being late to class. Regardless, she is a fantastic lecturer, is always willing to spend more time in class going over topics, and is readily available outside of class.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: I had her for PHSC 204, and she is the sweetest person ever. I loved having her as my professor and would definitely want to have her again!
She went thru the lecture slides nicely and gave pointers on how to remember certain things. It was very relaxing. Probably the best instructor out of the 3 mcmp204 instructors.

She doesn't seem to have it all together, which only sometimes affected lectures. Most quiz and homework content from last year's were consistent with what we had this year (could become a bad crutch if you don't make an effort to understand the content). Easiest professor to understand from PHSC 204/MCMP 204.

Dr. Dykhuizen was knowledgable on the content but did move too slowly throughout her portion of the course. She was available outside of class for office hours, even during the parts of the semester when she wasn't the main professor. Definitely use the old quizzes/tests on brightspace as she follows the exact format.

Whoever wrote Dr Dykhuizen's last review must have never pay attention in class. I loved her! She did an amazing job at showing examples for the material we were learning as well as breaking it down in simple turns. I did a great job on the exam when she was teaching. I also appreciated how she did a recap of the last lecture before we started.

This professor has no idea how to convey Organic Chemistry to her students, and is all over the place in her lectures. Information is rarely given in a clear way and you must do so much work outside of class to actually understand what is going on. Very bad at teaching and relaying information to students.

One of the worst teachers I have ever had in my career as a student. I understand organic chemistry is challenging, but the lack of preparedness and inability to explain the topics clearly is unacceptable at an institution like Purdue. Her condescending emails and remarks to students are also not appreciated when it's obvious we work very hard.
this woman doesn't know organic chemistry and also doesn't know how to teach and also looks like a couch cover at times!
Dr. Dykhuizen gives you good information but doesn't do the best job explaining. I don't recommend going to her personal office hours unless you want to be criticized.

Overall, this class is taught by the sweetest kindest most joyful professor in this god forsaken, freezing, featureless, hell hole. I have read some of the other reviews and yes, it is hard to get an A in a college course. But overall, she's pretty generous when it comes to grading especially compared to any class with math in it.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Very cool and passionate about what she teaches.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: Nice person. She bases so much of work on group work when it should be more individual, and is extremely unwilling to change on certain things. poor feedback, doesn't really help you get a good grade. I could teach this class a LOT better than her.

The class itself is not bad and many of my friends have taken the same class with different professors. One thing that I didn't like was that she was very unwavering in her grading. Many friends who deserved A's ended up with A-'s and B+'s. One assignment can majorly screw up your overall grade. My advice is doing the extra credit and participating
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: she gives a horrible final that no one i know is able to finish
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I don't appreciate that like so much of your grade is group work, and she tends to be kind of unmoving on certain things. Would not recommend, if it was just individual assignments would have an A, not an A- in the class.
Grading seems random and accompanying comments are not clear.

Professor Frketich shows genuine interest in her students' lives and well-being, and by the second week she had learned everyone's names. She leaves great feedback on assignments, will help you in-class, and wants to see you succeed! So grateful I took this course with her!
She's a harsh grader for some and an easy grader for others. These other ratings are capping.

Do not take a class with her. She is extremely unfair when it comes to assignment grading and your grade for each class is determined if she decides to call on you or not in a room full of students all trying to get their participation points for the day. Overall awful teacher would not recommend.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Dr. Frketich is very kind, understanding, and takes joy in what she teaches. Her assignments are minimal work (if you're moderately okay with essays) and usually pretty interactive if they're not essays (although I had to do a group essay). Attendance is mandatory, but she makes an effort to get to know her students and is generous with grading.
love her. great person to discuss with, very empathetic and kind

I took her class as a requirement for FYE and I've got to say she made her classes one of the most enjoyable parts of my semester. Almost everything is participation, we had philosophical readings and just had to read one book. Most projects were in teams and she gave us plenty of time and resources to prepare. Take her class, you won't regret it.

Great prof and super easy to reach outside of class. A couple group papers+project but they're very easy if you have a good group. She doesn't like tech in her class and you have to print things out for class (I spent most of my printing credits on her class alone) but overall very chill and easy

Professor Frketich is very kind and dedicated to her students! She makes the effort to learn everyone's name and is ready to offer help to anyone who asks. As an engineering student who often tends to gravitate away from the humanities, her class was by far the best English course I've ever taken.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: Professor Frketich is an amazing professor! All readings bar one whole book you read are provided to you through PDFs. If you show up, do the readings, and talk once or twice a class you'll get an A. 3 papers througout the semester, 2 group, 1 solo. She gives great feedback and grades fairly.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: She is very easy to communicate with and the work is not difficult.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Professor Frketich is a chill teacher, she helps and is cool
Great teacher
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Highly motivated professor, really fun classes!
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: I think that she is one of the nicest people at Purdue. She cares for her classes and is super creative in her teaching style. She is a very fair and understanding professor who will be open to communication on anything related to the course. Get ready for field trips and maybe a play performance.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: Frketich is a unique teacher, she seems creative. 20% of your grade is attendance AND participation. I feel she's a pretty good teacher because she likes to include the students when making rubrics, the class does not feel like a 50 minute lecture. Only downside is that she may call on you to read aloud or answer a question. A couple field trips.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Professor Frketich is incredibly accommodating and loves to answer questions. Talk a couple times a week and you get above 90% on participation points. She is very good at weaving her own interpretations of the text into class discussion time, makes for a great experience. She also provides the class with all the text, so you do not have to buy it.
Very nice and caring teacher. Interesting class discussions where participation matters. Relatively easy grading.

As someone who hates English/reading, Prof. Frketich made it bearable. Classes were entertaining, especially with the fun game at the beginning (which really just wasted time but I'm not complaining), and she was very flexible with her grading and granting of extensions. Very much recommend getting on her good side or at least introducing yourself.

Best English teacher I have ever had, her classes are engaging and actually quite fun. Even if attendance wasn't mandatory I think I'd still show up for class. One thing I really liked about her is any feedback you give she actually uses to improve her course. My only complaint is that she doesn't teach SCLA 102.

(SCLA 101) She's very kind. When finished the course a few months ago, she remembers my name and we've caught up. So, evidently friendly. She's a very joyful person and it subs off on her students. SCLA doesn't have many points available, so I'd recommend attendance. Actually about to drop by her office to chat

She is so nice and understanding. She really cares about you and your learning. Participating in class is important but there is extra credit or make up assignments you can do to make up the points. Best English professor I've ever had.

Dr. Frketich is such a sweet person. This class was discussion based and had little work aside from readings. There were 3 essays total as well as plenty of extra credit opportunities to make up missed participation points. She's a very fair grader. I would definitely recommend this professor! She's fun and really cares about her students.

Low-key, take these reviews with a heavy grain of salt. I personally thought she was a great professor who wanted us to do well. She taught well & homework weren't bad, but class was boring at times. It was well structured & the exams are ok. It does cover spoken topics, but it takes extra outside work to understand concepts to prepare 4 exams;
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: The worst professor I have ever taken at Purdue. The questions she puts on her exams do not correspond with her lectures about 80% of the time. Most of the time, she has no idea what she is doing.  I took a different professor for this class, and they helped me understand Microeconomics so much better. Not sure how she is still employed.
Maxistan deported her for a reason

In the words of One republic "Boy, run, run, run, run, run, yeah, run run run." If the fox catches up to you. Prepare yourself for a semester of blood, sweat, and tears. If your not religious pick one and start praying. But if your fast I repeat Run away, Run fast, and never ever look back!
Just so bad!!! I got a 5 on AP macro and failed this course!!! This class is designed to teach but rather to make you fail. If your seeing this during open registration chose a different prof!!!

The lectures are very important but not very difficult of concepts. There is a no technology policy for the most part, though you are allowed to use iPads. The exams are pretty difficult, reading the textbook is kind of important.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Rigor is one thing; arbitrary difficulty is another. I recommend avoiding this professor; she's on some power trip where she believes that making tests insanely difficult for her ENTRY level class is valid, and it's not. You can understand everything important in macroeconomics and still do poorly on exams since they test random niche concepts.
One of my harder classes I've taken. The exams were short answer and fill in the blank.

When one first goes into college they expect that the whole point is to prove you have learned your skills. One class with Dr. Fox will make you realize that is simply not the case. Exams are designed to make your fail and lectures are simply not helpful. If you practice hard you'll learn but not any info that is useful come test time.

Avoid this professor at all costs!!! If this is a required class and she's the only one available, take it next semester. If that doesn't work switch majors. This Econ class was my 13th 14th 15th 16th… 67th reason why. Hours upon hours of studying. Staying up 36 hours in a row graced me with a D+. I'll be taking my talents to a diffrent prof.

imo: great lecturer, very approachable, always answered questions and explained concepts throughly if something's not clicking in class or office hrs. 

however long you think you'll need to study for a regular exam, double it for this class. exams are insanely difficult relative to other intro classes and there's always grading errors

Lectures are barely relevant and boring since she talks fast. Exams are tough with vague questions and few multiple-choice options, so you need to thoroughly understand your knowledge. She has taught for a while, but her teaching style still weirds me out. HWs and activities are worth little, while exams are worth so so so so much.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Very difficult cumulative final that is like really intense and on so many modules about so many topics doesn't clearly tell us to focus on for anything. SO DIFFICULT TO FIND WHAT ASSIGNMENTS ARE DUE WHEN SHE DOESN'T SAY WHEN HALF THE THINGS ARE DUE.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Don't bother trying to take notes she talks so fast and then will call you out you if you try and use your laptop to take notes. 2nd exam was nothing like the practice exams, a girl sitting next to me during the exam asked the TA if there was a typo because there was concepts that she never mentioned in class
She sucks mucho
Speaks a lot inn lectures goes too fast i think and most of her class are like exam heavy most of the grades are based on exams not that much heavy oriented for participation or hws
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She treats you like a child. She's nice in person and I didn't feel that the homework or exams were unnecessarily difficult, but I didn't enjoy her acting like a helicopter parent. She has a strong no laptop policy and has zero issue with yelling at students. Acted like she was the best teacher, but openly admitted to incorrectly teaching content.

I love Dr. Fox as a professor. She is super kind and interesting to talk to. She is one of the best teachers that I've ever had. With that, the grade is 80% exams which is super annoying. The exams are also super hard, but she gives a generous curve. It is hard, but she made me fall in love with econ. If you like econ, take her!

Prof. Fox is ok. She is clearly knowledgeable, but runs her lectures a bit like high school. No laptop, no phone (except iClicker), annoying assignments. The exams are NOT multiple choice, and they are very frustrating. Poor exam grades are common, even with hours of studying. She also basically refuses to give partial credit. Avoid if possible.

This class will have you wanting to take a one way trip to Maxistan and never come back (ifykyk) The course content itself is not hard theres just 1000 hops you have to jump in order to get through it... Hours upon hours of studying graces you with TERRIBLE test scores. She does curve so ill give her that. For your own sake chose a diffrent prof!

Dr. Fox was an amazing lecturer and made the content understandable. A lot of the grade is based on exams and a combined class participation/attendance/homework grade. Overall, the work was not that difficult, and the class was easy, but she is a little blunt and not super approachable.

The class is just so poorly set up. The course is over 75% graded on exam performance and Mrs Fox (yes that was said on purpose) is nowhere to be found during them. TA's had to correct 5 questions mid exam and 2 weeks of vigorous studying graced me with a 62%. Expect for exam grading to take 3 weeks at the minimum.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The lectures were kind of confusing sometimes, and you have to pay attention the whole time or you'll fall behind, but if you pay attention and take notes its not super hard of a class to get an A in.
Fox did a great job elaborating on content while still keeping the class interested. She runs a bit of a strict lecture, but it is worth it if you are going in there to learn about economics.

I took this class over the summer, and it was condensed into 4 weeks. While the class wasn't necessarily hard, the homework, practice tests, and even actual exams were riddled with errors. The assignment dates weren't adjusted from last session and the lectures still made references to the pandemic. Why did she not take time to fix the errors?
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Just pay attention and you'll be fine.
good but also bad at the same time. hard to learn because she makes everything seem harder than it actually is. but overall, a kind soul who is passionate about economics. class is VERY exam heavy.

Had Prof. Fox for ECON 252 & took her again for 251—she's amazing! Lectures are clear, recorded, and she truly cares (even canceled class when it was freezing). Tests are tough but fair with lots of practice exams. Attendance with iClickers keeps you engaged. You actually learn—bad ratings don't reflect her teaching at all!

This course is out of 400 points. 300 points are taken up by the three exams throughout the semester. You do bad in one exam and your grade is already dropped. She gives you homework consisting of an insane amount of questions but it ends up only being out of a few points. Lectures are extremely boring. Avoid if you can.

Fox's class isn't easy, but getting an A is realistic. The hate she gets isn't warranted—she's nice, you just can't loaf. Attendance is 15%, but it made me show up more than I would've. Office hours are underrated—she tutored me 1-on-1 bc no one came. I got 80-90 on exams, 70 attendance. Did provided practice to study & understood each mistake made

I've had her for macro and micro and for some reason micro was taught so much worse than her macro class. You need a PHD to understand what your grade is throughout the semester as the grading is the most confusing thing ever. Lectures are okay just very very boring not the absolute worst though. Exams are 80% of your grade which also sucks

We use achieve for homework and when I had missed a couple assignments, she emailed me asking me what was wrong. I told her that I was late on paying the fee so that's why I had missed them, and she immediately opened them again. She uses very easy to understand language when teaching and offers a lot of extra credit but make sure you go to class.
I have never wanted to sleep in a lecture more in my life. If she says Maxistan 1 more time, I will crash out and book her a first-class flight to Maxistan. Avoid at all costs!!!!
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I honestly like her lectures. I understand everything she says and her "language" is very clear. It is exam heavy so u have to work for a good grade but nice professor overall. She helps me out with anything and everything. I do not undertsand the hate in other ratings

I am not great at Econ but needed to complete in order to fulfill a requirement. Dr. Fox isn't the worst professor in the world, but she's tough. The course is test heavy, so study hard for those. Attendance is mandatory through iClicker but you don't really have to pay attention-the questions are usually easy. As long as you study, you'll be fine
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: she is worse than anything you can imagine x10. lectures are so boring i was fighting sleep, practice exams are so wrong its not even funny. she made my semester insufferable.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: To begin, i am currently studying fir the final in my microeconomics course, and i want to die. I was reviewing the practice tests and on one of the questions there were 2 correct answers. I was speechless. This is not the first time this has happened as well. The practice exams were nothing like the normal exams whatsoever. Find a different class

I did not like this professor at all. Sure, she knows her content, but that's the bare minimum of a professor. She has no empathy for her students, is extremely harsh about anything, doesn't let you have a single device out during class so you can't take notes on your iPad or laptop & even clicker on ur phone u cant leave the tab.

Not as bad as it seems. Everyone made it seem like she was terrible, but I found the course relatively easy. Just go to the lectures and you will be fine. Exams are 75% of your grade, but that is how it is for all Micro classes.

I took this during her first semester coming to Purdue, which was two years ago. It's hilarious that the same problems I had with her my freshman year are still prevalent today. 1/3 of the practice exams had wrong answers, lectures were way too fast, and office hours were less useful than asking GPT.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: She makes it so iclickers are not only used for attendance, but you have to get the questions right as well. She likes to draw out a lot of notes which can make it more confusing. Also, if you do more than one attempt on the homework she will deduct points.
her notes are awful

Terrible Professor! Goes way too fast in lectures, and nonstop repeats herself. Half her practice exam answer keys are wrong. She does not care about any of her students. Do not take her class. You will want to drop it.
I swear I ain't trippin but there's questions with incorrect answers on all of her practice exams.
She's is the worst person ever.

She literally teaches concepts wrong, acknowledging that they're wrong and claiming she likes them better, setting her students up for failure in higher-level micro classes. Her husband wrote the textbook which is the only reason she uses it, and the exams are riddled with mistakes, she will not take any feedback or accountability.

Very exam heavy grader, and there's the occasional tough question. But honestly?  If you show up to lecture and do the practice exams, you'll be fine. 
The exams are not unfair - just be sure you're adequately prepared by studying. 
Always tries to explain things 3 ways (graphically, mathematically, story-wise). She's a perfectly fine professor.

The exam is hard if you haven't taken any economics classes before. About 75% of the final grade is made up of the exams. Lectures rarely help, and you need to learn by yourself. Professor Fox is caring, but it is a large lecture, so it's impossible to know each student well.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Fox is a very smart professor and I felt that she explained topics very well. Some reading outside of class might have to be done to fully understand topics, but you could definitely get an A with only the lectures. Fox speaks well and has an understanding of the topics, and makes the lectures more interesting than boring.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: I took Fox for micro and compared to that this class was a breeze. Tests were significantly easier and one was dropped. I do not like her teaching style however because a lot of what she teaches isn't on the exam which isn't very useful but overall easy and didn't have to pay too much attention in lectures.

Very clear what needs to be done in order to be successful. For this class, i attended the lectures, took notes, did the homework, and utilized the textbook readings as supplemental if i needed help on a concept.  That seemed to be work well enough. Uses Iclicker for participation
I felt that sometimes she went through the slides too quickly. I really like taking notes in class so this really annoyed me. Other than that she does explain topics clearly.

very test heavy, but doing practice exams, the module practices, and boiler exams were super helpful and all I needed to prepare. there is in class iclicker so you have to go to class. class was pretty easy, but third exam material is tough so make sure to do really well on the first two as one exam grade gets dropped.

I had fox for micro and she was horrible, I took her for macro and she was amazing. You can tell she really tried to change things up this time around and it shows. She is lovely, accomodating (I missed two exams and I still got to take them) and also the course is really easy. Don't listen to the people who are giving her 1 to 2s, she is amazing!

She is the worst professor I have ever had. If I hear her say Maxistan one more time I am going to lose it. The class is 90% exams so if you don't do good on those goodluck the rest of the semester. She is also not a nice person. She is extremely passive aggressive, and it's almost like she wants her students to fail. It gives her joy.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: we getting out of Maxistan with this one

She is a good professor if you just pay attention. Everyone that doesn't like her most likely messes around during class time. If you take the multiple practice opportunities she offers you will do great on the exam. Overall I think that everyone doesn't like her because it is a class you have to put at least a little effort into.

Errors with practice exams, module practice questions, actual exams too. Consistent errors such as typos (grammar mistakes, wrong numbers) or marking the wrong question as right. Extremely frustrating when trying to study for finals. Not a bad professor, just careless. Responds to emails and answers questions though, so that's good.

Consistently gets answers wrong on practice exams, making it extremely difficult to predict test scores. Very rough grader, impossible to get an A on an assignment. I was one of the lucky ones. Would not recommend.
Makes everything harder than it needs to be

she sucks, practice exams have problems constantly, lectures are filled with so much extra info it's hard to weed through and find the stuff you actually need. very passive aggressive, overall she grades 80% of the course on exams so good luck!! I would avoid her like the plague
she says Maxistan a lot

There are consistent errors with Fox's exams and she lacks of accountability for apologizing for them. Examples include grammar, typos, and wrong numbers on practice and actual exams. These errors aren't just here and there, they are consistent and extremely frustrating.

If you pay attention in lecture you'll be fine with minimal studying. 

Sit next to someone for the iClicker as they can be tricky. 

Dr. Fox's in-person macro class is easier than online micro over the summer.

I took this class during a 4-week summer course, and it was generally set up pretty poorly. The lectures missed some of the content, so you had to read the book to get all the information. Overall, the class is pretty easy if you do your work, but the lectures weren't too great, and there were a lot of online bugs in the class.

I took this class in the summer so it was condensed into 4 weeks, with 4 exams. The first exam was simple and easy but as the topics get harder the lecture videos don't explain and teach well at all. In my opinion the subject of Econ isn't hard to understand, Fox takes easy concepts and makes them as hard as possible.

This course was the most disorganized 4-week course I've taken, none of the exams are actually what the practice exams are like. The content she provides is not useful at all and the Achieve work is God awful. AVOID
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Personally, I thought this class was pretty easy so the lecturer didn't really matter. She had us do this group project the whole semester and it was pretty easy, and she graded it super easy. She was REALLY boring in lecture, but it's fine the content is the same as any econ class. Exams are okay, curve is generous. Easy A :)

Melanie Fox's content is not very helpful as the topics get hard. Her lectures are hard to follow and during the summer it is much worse. The exams, assignments, and quizzes are in no way related to each other. For a 4-week course, this is by far the most stressful, disorganized course.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: I took this course over the summer so the class was condensed into 4 weeks, the class is set up really bad, the summer class is still very exam heavy and you barely have enough time to grasp any of the concepts. Her videos online do NOT help whatsoever so you are stuck reading the horrible text book you are assigned. DON'T TAKE IT IN THE SUMMER
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I had to take this class twice. Difficult class, very difficult professor. Course is 90% exams no curve, 2 midterms 1 final. Homework is 6%, attendance is 4%. No drops. Extremely long, time-wasting homework that is recommended by professor NOT to be used as study material. Difficult exams will cherry-pick niche topics hardly explained in lecture.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Tests are 75% of your grade so make sure you study hard for them, although they represent class content very well. She seems harsh at first but then she warms up and if you attend class, pay attention, and put effort in you will do well.

Went into this class with no prior exposure to any economics. Long lectures, but very relevant. Exams mostly reflected lecture content and module practice questions. Good amount of extra credit options. Advice: pay attention in lecture, do Dr. Fox's practice module questions and past exams a few days before each exam and you will be fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Does not care about her students, test heavy, worth 90% of grade, setting you up for failure if you do bad on one. Her responses to emails are passive aggressive, provide little to no help.Cancels office hours more often then not. Lectures will have you asleep. She makes it nearly impossible to pass the class,practice exams are filled with mistakes
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: avoid at all costs. way too test heavy and although homework assignments take hours, they are not factored into your grade as much as they should be. no laptop policy limits access to well taken notes. exam study guides are full of mistakes yet are never changed. avoid avoid avoid this professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She wrote the textbook used in her class, but it doesn't cover any mathematical portion of economics, only the theoretical possibilities of modern economics. This forces you to attend class(and attendance is 10% of your grade) or learn things yourself, but the pace of lectures is immensely slow and tedious.
Hard exams, Exams are each 20%-25% of overall grade
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Exams were curved fairly (you could get well above 100%) and more than ample extra credit was available.

In my experience, Professer Fox was extremely unorganized, made an incredible amount of mistakes, and taught the class like the students were 13 years old. She often gets off topic during lectures and does not emphisize/explain topics that are actually going on the test. Her practice tests/study guides are horrible. They often are just wrong.

Does not care about her students. She takes pride in the fact that Supplemental Instruction is needed rather than working with students to make the class more manageable. Avoid her at all costs, and if you have her, I hope you already know economics.

She was pretty good. I think she is overall complained about too much but the exams are all multiple choice and fairly easy. Lectures are boring which is the only downside, but she often puts back homework assignments and the workload is very manageable. Exams are curved and the average sits around a B. I didn't study much for exams and did fine.

Good lectures, made the material easy to understand, and was willing to answer questions both inside and outside of class. Occasionally makes errors on homework, but I don't think there were any on the tests. Very clearly knows and likes the material she is teaching.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: She is very understanding and helpful when you need it. As long as you go to lecture you should be good

The class was tough, especially if you hadn't taken economics before. While knowledgeable, her strict iClicker attendance and laptop policies make the class less enjoyable. She moves quickly and there are errors on every practice test/quiz, so it's hard to study. She also sends passive-aggressive emails after poor test grades.

By far the most boring and worst professor I've had. Always called off due to being "sick" posted some online video bs then the class after her absence she would teach the exact same thing from the video. If you had entered her class about some knowledge about economics, you will come out not knowing a single thing.
Exams and study guides are riddled with errors.

I think Fox is a person who genuinely cares about econ, her lectures are interesting but she also makes a decent amount of mistakes. Honestly just don't be like me, a stupid freshman who didn't study until it mattered. If you grasp very little econ knowledge then you'll do well. She is also very nice and office hours are very easy to schedule.

I'm actually disappointed in Purdue. Do they not quality check these classes? For an introductory course, with students who have never taken an Econ class before, this is the worst possible experience to have. What an absolute robbery. Like i get a couple mistakes here and there but this professor's consistency just astonishes me. Unbelievable

The amount of time I have wasted going on wild goose chases trying to figure out how my answer is wrong just to find out that this incompetent professor has screwed up the answer key, not once, not twice, not even three times, but FOUR times one just ONE OF THE STUDY GUIDES. No way I am being burned by student loan interest rates to waste my time

I am writing a second review (and maybe a third) because 350 characters is nowhere near enough to describe this professor's utter incompetence. She makes SO MANY mistakes mixing up units and values and subtracting a price from a quantity it's just shocking. I feel like I am viewing my 3rd grade brother's homework when I look at her review guides.

CONSTANT typos that make for the MOST confusing assignments and quizzes. The number of questions that are objectively incorrect on the study guide is actually laughable. She shows her work so you can see the silly mistakes she makes. Honestly it's disappointing and gut-wrenching that we pay thousands of dollars for this quality of education.
She makes mistakes on exams and homework repeatedly, which makes it harder to learn and prepare for exams.

I found her to be extremely rude throughout the entire year and have zero connection with any of her students and when I emailed her after exam two about the study guide errors she had on every single exam and how it messed with a lot of students during the exam, her best response was to remove all of the study guides altogether for all exams....

Although the content itself wasn't horribly difficult. The way she explains most concepts are overcomplicated and confusing. The most frustrating part is that every single review material and exam has errors in the answer keys. Therefore, it's impossible to know if you don't understand the concept or if the answer key is just wrong once again.

The amount of homework that this person gives is insane. Additionally, this professor can't PROPERLY adjust and publish prep quizzes. This person always finds a way to make a mistake about anything regarding either the due date or something else. The course isn't even hard, it's just the content is just so badly written and it's so boring too.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: The amount of homework that this person gives is insane. Additionally, this professor can't PROPERLY adjust and publish prep quizzes. This person always finds a way to make a mistake about anything regarding either the due date or something else. The course isn't even hard, it's just the content is just so badly written and it's so boring too.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: The hardest professor I've had yet, makes easy concepts overly difficult.
Doesn't teach properly, teaches international economics in micro, makes the entire syllabus confusing and complicated

when doing the homework there were multiple questions that were objectively incorrect. this was also the case on quizes and exams. when it comes to teaching she definitely covers the topics but for each topic she'll do up to 3 or 4 examples making her lectures really boring.

Attendance is mandatory, she uses the phone iClicker that checks location

She doesn't allow computers in the class room and makes you get approval to do so as she (accurately) believes that hand notes (especially for Econ) are better.

She is also REALLY nice. She just pushes assignments back all the time, and is always answering questions.
Stay Away
Exams are worth majority of your grade and are written very poorly. Countless typos and other errors that make the exams frustrating. Overall unprofessional teacher inside the classroom and out.
Stay away if you value your sanity!!!

The worst part were the exams. She has four total, but the lowest is dropped at the end of the semester. They're worth like 90%. Some questions sound like they were written by a twelve year old and then revised by a thirteen year old. Practice exams are nothing like the actual exams. I took notes on literally everything and still somehow got a B.

Dr. Fox is the main reason I genuinely struggled in Econ. She offers practice test, and I did one before an exam, and got an A on it, took the real exam and failed. Later Dr. Fox left a note in the grade book saying, "I saw you did the practice exam, don't do them, they are not an accurate portrayal of the exam" WHY ARE THEY THERE THEN!!!!

The course was generally well taught and concepts were explained in digestible chunks that made it easy to understand. Professor Fox herself was helpful and responded quickly and in detail to anything of concern. That being said, the amount of work was very large and a bit confusing, and there were often mistakes in the exams that she would write.

Pr. Fox's lectures are boring and it's as if she designed them for middle school. The exams are poorly written; they have unclear questions, hand sketched figures, and sometimes even mistakes. There are no good study guides for the exam, so preparing for them is hard.
Makes easy material look hard.
As bad as Fox news.
One of the worst professors I've had at Purdue

Mediocre lectures, uncaring, hard to deal with, unpleasant person.

There are not many options for ECON 251, so if you have to take Dr. Fox, get ready for some boredom. Try to do everything without dealing with her on a personal level. She is difficult and a good slang term for her would be "Karen".

I can tell that Dr. Fox knows her material well. However, tests being 90% of my grade was difficult to adjust to. Also, the exams were fraught with errors, resulting in disorganization. I tried to get an A, but it was hard given my first exam score, so make sure to study lots! There are opportunities for EC, but it requires semester long effort.
Melanie Fox doesn't care about students. She knows things but does know how to teach and she assumes that we are all supposed to know things. try to avoid her.

Most noticeable thing in this course is that exams are 90% of your grade. The lowest exam is also dropped. Make sure to do well on exams and you'll be more than fine, though they can be tough. Professor talks fast and assumes too much for a beginner course. It's a steep learning curve if you never took a microeconomics course before.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I thought she was a clear lecturer, and I appreciated the ample extra credit. I honestly thought a lot of her lecturing was helpful, even if I found the slides difficult to understand. However, the class was disorganized, especially the exams, which had many mistakes. This is especially bad due to the fact that exams are 90% of your grade.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Her lecture is boring, but that's understandable; it's a big class. However, the frequent errors in the exams and review materials are confusing, and the errors are not updated on Brightspace or explained. The exams are not so difficult. It's okay to get an average grade. But I will not recommend taking her class.

Lectures are often mono-tone and slides are very confusing. Exams are utterly disorganized with multiple spelling errors, and print errors across ALL exams. Homework reflects perhaps 50% of the material on the exam, so it's best you supplement it by attending SI sessions and Khan Academy. Find another professor if possible (if offered).
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr Fox is excellent. This class is a breeze as long as you keep up with the assignments. She makes a recommended pace and publishes it on Brightspace, if you follow that you should be fine. Exams are exactly like practice questions she posts and there is so much extra credit (go to lecture for the points!). Thought this class was a lot of fun

This class has one homework assignment per week and allows you up to 15% in extra credit alone. Melanie Fox is very knowledgeable and designed the questions on Khan Academy if you want to know how her exams are like. She moves quickly in her lectures but that's understandable since this is a large course. Attendance is only needed for extra credit.

Dr. Fox puts on Brightspace a preview of what we'd learn that week. She shows that she cares about students' learning, as well as her passion for teaching the subject. As someone who particularly doesn't enjoy or understand economics and government topics, Dr. Fox does a great job of teaching in a way I understand and find interesting.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Professor Fox was not fantastic, not horrible, but a really middle of the road professor. She is kind and helpful, willing to help students as long as you seek help. Unfortunately, her lectures are incredibly boring and if you already hate economics like me, you will struggle. Exams are collectively worth 90% of your grade. 5/10 average professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I think Fox is a okay teacher but her class is extremely disorganized. We never finish covering content when we are supposed to. She posts all review material like 2 weeks after she says she will. Doesn't respond to piazza. Can be passive aggressive. Her exams are chaotic. She has passed out whole correction sheets, with 8-10 problems wrong.

She explains the lecture content in a very cut and dry manner. Teaches at a relatively slow pace, but is expected for a gen-ed/basic ECON class. The exams are incredibly fair; there's really no reason to do poorly in the course. And there are other opportunities for extra credit and to do well in the course.

Prefix: ECE student taking class for gen-ed req. Overall, easy class. Exams concept heavy (can cram day before, get ~90%), only basic math, 90% of your grade, though can drop one of 4 exams. Free bonus credit ~15 points, iclicker & watching few videos. Classes sometimes slow, though prof. is clear in explaining (you'll get the stuff if you listen).

All around ideal professor. She obviously loves the subject and teaching about it, and she is really good at explaining it. Very good at explaining things with examples. I can't imagine a better professor

Melanie Fox knows a lot about economics but does an extremely poor job teaching it.She acts as if it is all "common sense" and while this might be for someone who has been studying this topic for years, it is not for a student who may be learning about these terms for the first time.Even in her office hours she is extremely unhelpful. try to avoid!
Hardest class this school year. Tests are difficult and large.

Professor Fox is well-versed in economics and although the lectures are a bit boring, the content isn't overwhelming and the course is fairly straight forward. There are also lots of opportunities for extra credit if you do the homework and go to class. All exams are multiple choice.

Be prepared for a lot of homework!!!! BUT the homework is genuinely so so so helpful and super easy. She's a good professor and she genuinely cares about making sure her students are learning. Don't be afraid to reach out for help.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Gives good feedback--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: The lectures I felt were vague and didn't sufficiently cover the material that would appear in the homework and tests. She posts video recordings, so those are great to use. There are many opportunities for extra credit. Dr. Fox obviously knows the material well, but I don't believe it was conveyed in the best way.

Professor Fox was an overall great professor. Her explanations are clear and give great+abundant practice exams, which is very helpful when the course is exam-heavy. She really cares about students' grades because there are so many extra credit opportunities and lots of help sessions.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Test heavy

reviews.comment: she explained micro so it actually made sense. very open to take questions and actually wants the students to understand what she's talking about.
Dr. Fox is tough on you but her lectures are clear and if you pay attention and take notes then you shouldn't have a problem.
Keep track of hotseat for extra credit. Homework can be a bit long but not at all difficult. Exams aren't too bad. Likable personality.

Do all of the "optional" assignments (PCTs and LCs). Use them to study for the exams (LCs especially). Go to class and make sure you understand the HW. Fox is very smart and will be a good professor if you do everything she asks you to do per week.

Professor Fox is great and definitely knows the material they are teaching, but the notes and lectures are not posted anywhere online after the lectures, and this makes the course slightly more difficult due to a few resources not accessible to any students who have to miss the class for any reason. Class is not too difficult, but expect the work.

Homework/CY's are easy. Lectures are optional, but can help with the extra credit she offers. The class doesn't appear hard at the surface, but the exams weigh heavily on your grade, 25%per, and the tests are pretty difficult compared to the rest of the work and concepts taught.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Test heavy

reviews.comment: She's always willing to answer questions and help students.

Dr. Fox was very helpful when you went directly to her for office hours. However, her lectures left me very confused. The textbook is a lot to read but more useful and clearer than her lectures. There is a lot of homework, but if you take the time to try to understand the exams are pretty straightforward. Follow her pacing schedule for homework.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Dr Fox is a good professor and I thought she taught well with good examples.  Sometimes there would be stuff on the homework that wasn't covered in the lecture but you get multiple attempts.  You can always find this information in the reading (which isn't mandatory) but it really isn't a problem.  I barely touched the textbook and exams were easy.

This class should not be difficult, however, the material presented in the course makes it difficult. Melanie Fox knows what she is talking about however, her explanation is not good. The homework that is assigned does not equate to the actual lectures. Her lectures are very vague and do not cover what is presented on the homework often.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I love professor Fox! She organizes the course very well, giving you a "recommended pace" every week. She provides lots of resources to help prepare for exams (which are not bad if you do all of the homework and look at the resources she provides). There is a decent amount of homework, but if you follow her pacing schedule it is very manageable.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: She is very active on piazza, use that to your advantage. Unfortunately there were lecture where I walked out more confused about the content than if I just read the textbook. The textbook is the primary resource; it is honestly easy to read and has everything she bases the CYs and Exams around.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: She's definitely a really caring and kind professor who is very passionate about economics, but the class itself is confusing in terms of questions and her lectures don't necessarily allow you to excel on their own. It takes lots of practice problems and asking TAs to re-explain topics since her lectures can be incredibly confusing.

I really love her! The homework isn't super hard and it really helps me keep up even if i miss a class every now and then. She's super helpful and reaches out personally after exams and stuff. And she's always active on piazza to answer questions!! I'd fully recommend tbh.The expensive textbook is a bummer though
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Good lecturer, not a bad class, would still recommend against it. My reason for recommending against it is that you're forced into buying Macmillan's Achieve for >$100 (can't remember exactly). This would be reasonable if Achieve was a functioning website, which it isn't in the slightest. You'll have to redo an assignment at least once, I promise.
Honestly, a real cool guy. Went to his office hours and he really cares about his students.

The only grades are the short-answer tests done every 2 weeks, but they have a generous curve and are not super difficult if you show up to lecture and pay attention. Ford has a lot of knowledge on the material and is willing to answer any questions asked. Tests have questions that are sometimes very niche, despite the material being super general.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He's a nice person and all, but lecture slides provide 0 information about the course content. No recorded lectures. Talks super fast making it hard to take notes on everything. Grade is entirely on exams but with an extremely heavy curve. A 7:30 section didn't help. Overall if the course was better organized, this would've been a great class.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Little tough but overall good expierence

Best history Professor I ever had. I honestly love his class. Pretty heavy tests though but he does put a 32% curve on them so it's worth it. Go to the review sessions because that can make a big change for your grade. BTW his initials are O.T.

He helps with the history club and puts on a lot of fun events for the club.

Probably my facorite proffessor ever. He's easy to understand and his HIS 123 is a blast. He's extremely kind to everyone and is an amazing person. Grades fairly and actually helps you learn. I genuinely enjoy his classes and hope to have more of him.
not at all good, route learning
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: lectures were a nightmare

Overall the professor is good, but you cannot miss any lectures. The slides are messy and do not contain any helpful information. You will be quizzed on the information he mentions super quickly only once. The quizzes are super random. It doesn't seem that he has any structure during his lectures. Discussed politics for the whole class once.

The course is hard because there is so much content (Survey of global history) and his slides could be better, but he is a good professor; very knowledgeable, enjoyable to listen to, and wants you to succeed.  Go to the review sessions and study before the tests.

Hard to take notes from as his powerpoints tend to not have any bullets or notes on it and lectures are not recorded online so it's an inconvenience if you miss class, especially considering that all the tests are based solely on the smallest things he says in the lecture. However, there's a heavy curve making it easier to get a good grade.

The tests are like trivia games that pick random small things he said from lectures that you probably missed or couldn't write down because he was rambling so fast. Messy lectures. No study guide. However, the test is curved very heavily. Not too hard to get a decent grade but incredibly stressful as every test is a big guessing game.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The course is graded on tests which cover lecture material, which is purely verbal with no slideshows, no transcripts. Hard to hear at points, talks fast. The lectures content is hard to follow and jumps frequently. Incorrectly presented some history (Opium Wars). The tests were strange in topics picked, trivia like. Professor quite rude at times.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He speaks very fast in class and the lectures are not recorded. If you miss anything he says, you are done for because he doesnt upload any notes or lecture content. If you want to get good grades, you will have to type like a maniac and listen to each and every word he says. The saving grace is that all tests are curved well.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: His lectures are just him rambling with no clear emphasis on what is important. Slides show no information outside of pronunciations and maps. He also provides no study material, so it is impossible to predict what material will be tested.

Hes a pretty chil guy, but crams a lot of info in each lecture. attendance is not mandatory, but you're cooked if you dont pay attention to every second. every test and exam is curved, which is amazing.

This class has a bad rep, mostly because it was a new class with a lot of moving parts. Now, it seems to be smoother and more polished, and I have hardly run into any issues. If you want to be a mechanical engineer and get into CAD, this class is a necessity and will provide you with the skills needed.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: The content isn't too bad but the class is very, very tedious. He assigns a lot of busy work, making a 2 credit hour class feel like 4.
Makes you buy his textbook, which he wrote. Got most assignments wrong and had to change them 1-2 days before the due date

Lectures were interesting and the new Teamcenter site was good to learn. Going to office hours and making sure to follow the rubric really helped me do well. Most important thing is getting on top of assignments and not being against asking for help, as the software can mess up at times.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Not a great lecturer, TAs carry the course. He gives extra credit assignments but downcurves at the end of the year based on distribution. So even if you get an A according to the syllabus you could end up with a B. Those extra credit assignments are really just normal assignments. He's the worst and is not transparent about it.

Honestly one of my favorite classes so far. The lectures gave a solid look at how engineering design works in industry, and the labs made the CAD and PDM stuff click. Fuerst is super approachable and helpful with both class questions and career advice. Not sure why people hate on it.

0 lenience & mercy, doesn't care about his students.Not structured to be a 2-credit course,rather heavier than 4-credit.Didn't give extension for a legit technical fault,& asked me to drop the entire course for the 2nd time.I have no motivation to take it for the 3rd time now. He's doing whatever he wants as he's the only professor for this course.

Initially, I was unsure about Fuerst considering the less-than-stellar reviews, but he seemed completely fine. The course itself is super easy if you have any previous CAD experience although there is a lot of homework. Other than the cost of the textbook, I have no complaints.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: This class isn't hard as long as you do all the work and get every extra credit. By the “final” practical exam, I had so many points I could have skipped and still gotten an A-. Take every assignment seriously and don't be afraid of going to office hours (the lab office hours run by TAs) and and the class will be way easier than you think.
Useful lectures and homework. The content in mfet163 helped me achieve proficiency with CAD and plm in a few months.

I took MFET 10301.

Mandatory $200 textbook, extremely buggy software that barely worked on the school computers, useless information about PDM, and average CAD training made this class really more difficult than it should have been.

If you can avoid taking this class, do so. The TA's made it tolerable and they did the best they could.
85% of the class gets an A so even though its a lot of work can't complain, 200$ textbook is a bit annoying but apart from that pretty chill class
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Participating in labs helped me get some extra credit, have to say, got good feedback each time I lost points. He is strict on grading but definitely follows Industry standards. Overall, a tough one but a good one

You can expect a lot of homework, but I gotta say it's worth it. It was frustrating to buy that $200 textbook, but at the end of the day, helped me learn things on my own. BTW, I landed an internship!! Yay!!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Gives good feedback--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: I have to say, I was a bit unsure about this course at first because I'd heard mixed reviews. I had some wrong assumptions going in. The course is good. The only downside is the cost of the textbook, but still, it's worth it. It is mostly for PDM. Even though I usually work with SolidWorks, I got to try a different version of NX.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: At first, I thought this class would be tough, but after a few weeks, I realized how helpful it actually is. It gave me a solid foundation in engineering topics,. Yes, there's quite a bit of homework, but you learn a lot. Eventually, that knowledge helped me land an internship, and I saw how useful PLM systems are, especially in large companies.

The course is a good opportunity to learn CAD, PDM and PLM. There are industry wide used softwares like NX, Teamcenter, Anark that are really helpful in Industry. The learning curve is steep at first but things settle down after a mid-term. Course is designed to make students experience work in Industry. Really good for students new to engineering
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: People complain a lot, but it is a very useful class with lots of practical wisdom given by him and others that he brings in.

Professor Fuerst's course isn't great but it's not nearly as bad as people say. The textbook's $200 which sucks but other than that it's a pretty straight forward course that's not hard to get an A in. Just know that this course is like two seperate courses: in labs you'll learn NX and then in lecture it's more about industry and technical terms.
He's definitely a bit of an attitude shock straight from highschool, but Professor Fuerst manages his class remarkably well.

I dont know why he has a bad rating. This class helped prepare me so much for a future career and if you're going into Engineering you NEED this class. Lecture's are a little pointless but that's how the course is built. The professor makes tutorials which really do help a lot. You have to try and it takes time but it is worth it.
Lectures are terrible, labs are a waste of time. Prepare to do a ton of work outside class.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Worst class at Purdue. $200 required txtbook w integrated quizzes, happens to be written by the prof. Takes half the class to open 6+ apps before working, currently spend 3+ hrs outside for 2 credits. Syllabus is years long + you gotta jump through a hundred hoops to email the professor about anything. I have a strong background in 3d modeling too

He uses NX and Teamcenter, which are some of the worst apps I've used. You can't rename files to match the professor's requirements, nor can you remove or edit them. Instead of repeating instructions to add a part, he links to another document, making task progression tedious and confusing for students trying to follow along.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment:
Don't be scared by Fuerst's score!
If you are a freshman and you know how to CAD take MFET 162 now!
This class should be more credit hours, the textbook is $200, and lecture is mandatory.
But the class is kinda easy, Quizzes/Tests are a joke I didn't even have to do the last practical cause of exam drops. Honestly gets too much hate.

Class is fairly easy and has plenty of extra credit. The lectures are the biggest waste of time I have ever attended. The worst part is that they're mandatory attendance, but you never actually learn the topics in class during them, so they're essentially just hour-long rambles. The labs are alright.

Professor Fuerst was great. I think the overall course itself, especially the exams, could use some improvement but I think he did a good job at introducing fundamental engineering concepts and showing the importance of them. The class isn't too bad, it can just be a lot of work some weeks. Overall, I would recommend taking this course.

It appears that many disagree/dislike Professor Fuerst, but I believe the issue stems less from him and more from the nature of the class. From what I can tell having taken the class, it appears Fuerst was tasked with digging MFET163 out of generation debt and had to experiment some (thus the hate) to make the class better. Overall, easy class.

Professor Fuerst was one of the best professors I had this semester. His lectures where well thought put and throughly enjoyed the guest speakers he brought in from industry.
