While the grading is tough the feed back was excellent on what I got wrong and if I didn't understand my TA was easily able to explain.

The class has some negatives (like a required $200 book, unintuitive software and annoying quizzes), but I think that he does well providing useful tutorials and material (I think many dislike lecture material because it is often not necessary to the class, but it seems relevant nonetheless). Overall, I think the class prepared me well.

Lectures are mandatory but useless. $200 textbook just for some weekly quizzes. This class is a time sink; only 2 credit hours, but heavy workload & they love shaving off points any opportunity they get, so attend office hours. That being said, work can be interesting at times despite old/laggy interface.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: Travis fuerst is an easygoing and generally kind man. With that said, this is a terrible class. Nearly all of the information learned is buzz-words and memorization game, and quizzes require reading the incredibly dense $200 textbook to understand. Lectures are middling at best in terms of usefulness, and most lab work has to be done at home.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Cool dude. He seems knowledgeable about industry and can form an educated opinion. I don't know why I go to lecture other than it's free points. It has little to nothing to do with the lengthy assignments from the lab. The TA's are good in the labs, but lowkey I could watch the tutorials he posts for the labs and get 99% of the class content.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: He is an amazing professor, his TAs put in a lot of work to ensure that students understand the topics covered and how to use the software.

Since Prof Fuerst decided to only have 1 lecture a week, this class isn't as intense as last semester. It's an easy A, but the lectures provide no information needed for the class. Your lab TA's will be much more important in teaching the content of how to CAD.

Fuerst is a nice guy who is always trying to improve MFET 163, and his lectures might not be useful but they are pretty interesting and he brings a few cool guest speakers. He also cares about his students and makes time for them.
Useless lectures but easy class because of many opportunities for extra credit and many drops

The lectures are pretty much useless, but attendance is mandatory. There is a 200 dollar textbook that I only used for the quizzes, which could have easily been available on Brightspace. A lot of the assignments will take a while but they usually have tutorials from the professor so that's nice. Grading scale is also weird, but an A is easy to get.

Going to his lectures is a waste of time because he rambles about anything unimportant (attendance is still mandatory). Requires you to buy a $200 textbook just to complete a couple quizzes. However, the class isn't that hard as long as you follow the rubric and use the search function in the textbook.
Grading scale is terrible. 92.99 is a B. 93.00 is an A.

Prof Fuerst does a great job running the class, especially for those with no prior experience. Had no CAD experience going in and had no problem learning using his textbook and video tutorials. Exams were all online and open note. Plenty of extra credit opportunities and drops. He and TA's were very accessible outside of class for help.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Assignment's were very straight forward about what needed to be done, but the grading scale was kind of horrendous.
Spends lecture rambling on boosting his own ego. Homework takes forever and you have to cad "his way" so any prior cad experience will actually hurt you in this awful class.

A month into the class assignment documents were changed after released, at times forcing students to redo work that was done to the initially released assignment docs. Also, during the lectures Fuerst talks about whatever is on his mind and does not actually help you learn for the class.

Siemens NX is not meant to be used in an educational setting, and most of the teaching assistants are just as confused as you are.

How to survive one of Fuerst's classes:

1) Go to as many TA office hours as it takes (it will take multiple)
2) GO TO FUERST'S OFFICE HOURS - in person, he is actually super nice and he is really really helpful!

This class is going to suck no matter what. I hated it and it was one of my worst classes here, but I really don't think it's Fuerst's fault. He was fine in lecture, I stopped going, but the exam things were really easy, the class is really easy but it can be frustrating and take a really long time. And you need to buy the $200 textbook >:(

There is a huge learning curve in the class. I didn't find labs helpful since there was a 40:1 TA ratio. I would rec going to diff. lab/103 labs to find which TAs are helpful. Prof. Fuerst is a great guy; he helped guide me through the course when I was at an F and ended up with a B. Use manual uploads in Aras since the connector can be buggy.

This class has greatly improved from previous semesters as I can tell. Aras is a lot less buggy, and the class generally ran very smoothly. Fuerst is a pretty good lecturer, and the information is actually useful. PDM and CAD skills are very important, and Fuerst communicates that very well. I would have given 5 stars, but the textbook is $200.
He doesn't know what he is doing and often relies on TA's to demonstrate what we need to do. The website used to turn in most homework often doesn't work, so good luck turning in assignments.

Listen, all these reviews that are negative are exaggerated. I read them and was horrified, but I had no other choice but take the class. It really isn't that bad. Work is a little tedious, and lectures are a little unrelated, but interesting. 

Stay on top of your work 
Use the TA's 
Find a buddy to work with 

I learned a lot. 

You will be ok.

This class is not very well organized and the required $200 textbook is a huge pain to use. Most of the class materials come from it and the interface is very old/buggy despite being a brand new edition. The directions for the labs and modeling assignments are often confusing and the mandatory attendance at 8:30 or 9:30 in the morning isn't great.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Im not sure why his reviews are so low, I took MFET 163 and the course was well structured and the TAs were extremely helpful. Only bad part is that lectures are not really useful but you have to go since it counts for your grade.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Ungodly amounts of work for a 2 credit class, but I can definitely say I learned how to CAD. Lectures were overall pretty useless, and your lab section is where you really do everything. TA's are willing to help you through your assignments during lab or office hours.

- Lectures don't really teach you anything you need to know, just talks about industry
- You learn how to do everything in your lab, where you're expected to already know everything
- TA's are nice
- Homework isn't actually that hard, just takes forever and there is a lot of it
- Should get an A, just will make you want to die
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Textbook was useless and ridiculously expensive ($200 and he co-wrote it), Weekly work is tedious, really time consuming, but not too difficult. The real hard part is submitting it through Aras Innovator which has not had a functioning streak of 1 week since its inception and takes forever. Lectures do not teach at all. The TAs will save you.

Horrible class. The class itself is not even that hard, but it is the most disorganized class, despite how much they tell you they have restructured and fixed this class. The TAs don't know what they are doing.  Grading for this class sucks, and they are constantly giving out 0s and 50% with no feedback. This class is time-consuming for no reason.

He's really not as bad as people make him out to be, besides having to buy a $200 textbook you never use. The course had a stupid amount of issues and is really terribly run. ARAS had so many issues it was comical, and I felt bad for Fuerst and the TAs. I thought he was a pretty nice guy and I never really had a bad interaction with him.

Professor Fuerst and his TA's grade harsly. This is very much so a "busy work" class, on av. 4 assignments a week, and half of the semester is a project that you will constantly have to work on. The lectures are not mandatory and seem worthless. Exams are online and a breeze just read the mandatory purchased textbook. I do not recommend Pr. Fuerst
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: In general he is a nice guy, and though the technology (both Aras and NX) are sometimes a major pain, they are tech issues not Prof issues. The TAs are knowledgeable and you are honestly good to go if you do your work in lab and don't wait till the last minute. Textbook is expensive though; no way around it. Grade scale is weird (86-93 B, 70-86 C).

Most disorganized class I have ever taken. You need to download like a million things to actually do any work. Submission was incredibly difficult for every assignment and the $200 textbook was required. Awful class.

This was genuinely the most disorganized class I have ever taken. The required textbook for this class costs $200 dollars and is a mess of poorly written directions. You are left on your own as it is impossible to get help on any issue without waiting days. There were constant issues with the software and apps used. Do not take this class.

The way that he grades horrible. Schedule is messy and even when due dates are changed they really aren't. The TA that i got was no help and made everything more confusing. Only saving grace was open software, so you can work on assignments outside of lab.

This class is taught by the TAs. To receive assistance you have to submit a help form, there is no in person teaching. This makes problems that would take 5 mins to solve in person, take days through emails sent back and forth. It created uneeded congestion and resulted in due dates being pushed back for weeks. This is just the tip of the iceberg.

Spring semester was very enjoyable, he made it clear he is dedicated to improving the course. VERY manageable workload, you are given 3 weeks/assignment with 2 hours a week of optional TA lab time for help. Lectures are pretty common sense, but still enjoyable if you really want to get into the inner workings of CAD.

This course was a train wreck. The TA's and the professor don't know how to transfer grades from Aras to Brightspace. In Aras, I have full points on a project and have feedback that I did a great job. In Brightspace, I have a ZERO (and it's worth 4% of my grade!) They also put grades in the Friday of Finals week and are not answering emails

Likely the worst course I've had to take at Purdue. The course load is totally insane given that this is a 2 credit hour class. He makes you buy 2 $90 text books that you will probably forget about after the weekly quiz assignments go away half way throught he semester. The TAs end up teaching most of the class too. Avoid if you can manage it

This course has been the worst experience I have had with a professor. It is two credit hours, but the workload per week for the back half of the semester often exceeded six hours. Much of this time was spent deciphering poorly dimensioned CAD models and dealing with cloud server issues. Responses do not come in a timely manner.

You have to buy both of his textbooks for the class, both because there are quizzes through the textbook software and because the quizzes are filled with terms and abbreviations which you'd only know if you read the textbook. The lectures are bad and useless, they just cover the reading again and don't add anything. Go to lab prep lectures though.

The purchase of 2 textbooks, totaling $190, is required for completing the weekly homework. These texts are so riddled with spelling and grammatical errors that this class could double for a writing credit for the errors I've had to correct to make my own study guides. Also, he manages to talk all lecture, and yet, say nothing. Avoid at all costs.

One of the most infuriating classes I have ever taken. There are $200 in textbooks that are required to do the quizzes, but they only help slightly because the wording on them is awful. Your TA almost entirely decides your grade, as they grade all assignments in the class. Fuerst also doesn't round at all, so if you aren't at a cut-off, that's it.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Just awful. About 30 people come to lecture in a 300 person class, and for good reason. Lectures are just long rambles while he stumbles to demonstrate just about everything. Very little material to study with for the final exam and the textbook has to be the worst part about the class. He makes numerous mistakes on about every assignment.

The weekly quizzes are rough but they don't hold much weight at all when compared to the other assignments. I stopped going to lectures after the first few weeks and did just fine. Just make sure you get started on the assignments early and don't leave them until right before your lab. It is really the TA who dictates your grade, not Fuerst.

Requires you to buy two textbooks which he wrote and are riddled with spelling errors, and you can't even keep them, they are online and you buy a 6 month access window. Encourages using unique solutions to be efficient, but then punishes solutions on grounds which make sense after the fact but were never explained. Prepare to jump through hoops

I have never had a professor that doesn't know the layout of a class over a month in. Fuerst somehow took 4 weeks to decide if we would have a lecture over Thanksgiving break and still hasn't figured out if there will be a midterm assessment, a main portion of students' grades. He is clearly out of his depth and is not capable of running a course!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Lectures are useless in this class so students must rely on the textbook to gain any sort of knowledge!! Weekly quizzes given are absurdly difficult and require memorizing every textbook page from the reading!! The workload is extreme when considering the class is only 2 credit hours!! Fuerst is totally unaware of the type of class he's teaching!!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Hands down best professor so far! Definitely recommend taking her classes. She will regularly attend lab sessions she isn't required to just to help students in between her own work. She is extremely kind and deserves respect.

The nicest Professor (and one of the nicest people) I've met at Purdue. The course is planned very well. Labs are straightforward and educational with ta's always available to help. The course content isn't the easiest, yet she presents and teaches in a very accessible way. Couldn't give higher accolades, great job

She made the material very accessible and was eager to help in lab and walk through how things work. Always patient and respectful in the process. While the course was easy, it wasn't completely handed to us either. Absolutely no cons, this should be the benchmark for how a professor and course should be in the ECET department.

One of, if not the best Professor in Electrical Engineering Technology. I took ECET 229 with her and she is the type of professor that deeply cares about her students. Gentle and caring professor.  is always available for help. She also tries to come to every lab section and help students in need. She is a role model for other Purdue professors
An overall good professor and was pretty chill. We only had 3 assignments per week- 2 being due Wednesday night, and 1 on Thursday night.

Lecture slides/emails often have errors and don't cover what is on the exam at all. He and the TA were unhelpful or unresponsive when I emailed them. 3 assignments a week that were tedious but easy. Would send out emails criticizing us for making up multiple-choice questions that weren't good enough for him to use on his test.

Professor Feld organized SOC383 very clearly. He provided both PowerPoints and video lectures which,made the content easy to understand! At the beginning of each week, he provides thorough instructions on what is expected of us. He is a kind professor that cares about our learning!
Great professor! He is very kind and his class is not very hard. Also took SOC 383 and it was good as well.
Great course to take as an elective. Weekly discussion posts and some quizzes. All the material is in the PowerPoints and easily referenced.

Took this class asynchronously and had a good experience. Dr. Feld had clear expectations and the workload was very manageable. There are weekly discussion boards and assignments as well as quizzes every two weeks.

Tookk SOC100 online this semster; very easy class. One thing I will say is that it is a bit monotonous, but that is normal with any online course. There were exams every 2 weeks, and the questions were generated by students, which was sometimes a bit weird. Overall an easy class and easy professor.

Please don't take SOC 38300 with him, especially if its online.  You have an exam basically every other week and exams are the biggest chunk of the grade.  This lectures and study guides don't even remotely cover what is on the exam, and he did not set any of us up to be prepared for the exams.  The hw during the week is manageable, but not exams
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I took SOC 100 online with him this semester. He is very caring and assigns easy discussion questions. The tests are open note and he gives out study guides before each one. He records his lectures, but you don't have to watch them if you don't want to. He posts exactly what he says via PowerPoint as well.
Enjoyed his class!
This professor is so hard to understand . I dont understand his lectures . Doesnt seem to care about students and pretty weird lectures.
Horrible. Worst Professor I have ever had. He rambles on and his lectures are unorganized and make no sense at all. He is boring and his class is hard to go to and be at.

Avoid this prof at all costs. Extremely bad at teaching. Late to class fairly often. Unclear on what he wants from exam questions. His exams are terrible. He gives 20 extra credit points at minimum on them, and the average was still a C-. Seriously, avoid this guy at all costs.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Get off from this class, he talks incessantly without clarifying a single thing. Social research method is supposed to be informative instead of tedious.
it wasn't bad at all.
Absoulute worst teacher I have ever had. I failed miserably when I retook this class with another teacher I got an A. Drop this class immediatly and wait for another teacher.

One of THE worst professors I have EVER had@Purdue. I went to class and struggled all semester only to get a 59% F at the end of the semster. His TA routinely did NOT show up for office hrs & he did not seem to care when told about TA. The text was impossible to understand, he was worse to understand. Most of us failed.GET OUT WHILE U CAN!

Horrible at lectures. Cannot get his point acrossed and does alot of stuff in his head so you don't know where they are coming from. Inconsistent grading and unwilling to talk about how things are graded afterwards. Made review sessions at 3pm on thurs. when most had classes and wouldn't look at changing it.

he makes simple topics extremely confusing.  the TA was the reason I got through the class.  there are a ton of bonus points though, its an easy A if you use the book and go to the TA's office hours when you need help.
useless as a prof.

He is the foghorn leghorn of college professors.  All of the material is made to be as complex and unusable for a real-life purpose as possible.  The class is about collecting and analyzing data and he somehow cannot communicate his thoughts to people at all.  Simply horrible.

Lectures are only worth going in order to get possible assignments, but his lectures are extemely inadequate and ultimately confusing.  Read the textbook and attend lab meetings.  Make friends with the TA because they will be the only one able to help you.  Do everything you can to not get his section.
The worst, most incompetent teacher I've ever had.
No Comments

The class was easy and but I found the material boring. Professor Flory is so kind and funny and I did enjoy the lectures and talking to her. She's very understanding about any issues you may have and works with you. Two essays, a midterm, and a final, so pretty light work too.

Professor Flory is a brilliant professor who possesses so much knowledge. She encourages but doesn't demand participation, is understanding with absences/extensions, and is available for feedback during office hours. Just make sure you listen to discussions (Especially for inspiration for poems in essays) and complete the assignments.

Professor Flory is a passionate lecturer with a very entertaining teaching style. While the class is graded on only 3 papers, she gives great feedback and truly cares about the success of her student's.

Dr Flory is clearly passionate about English and wants students to become better writers, and succeed in the class. The only gripe is that your grade is based only on three papers, but her feedback is constructive and well thought out. Take her class if you want to engage with the material, and be prepared to write and revise.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Gives good feedback--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She really likes to go on tangents during lectures and if you don't agree with her opinions, she makes it clear that you're wrong. She won't give trigger warnings for upsetting topics either. VERY strict grader and very nitpicky when it comes to the wording of papers.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: very discussion heavy class. prof flory is the nicest woman i've ever met in my whole life she is super understanding if you need more time or extensions she's a really nice grader gives great feedback. has a tendency to go on tangents about other works during class discussion but it is very engaging and fun and she also said she liked my poem :)
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: While at Purdue, Flory was a great professor, and even though she was a very tough grader and made me rewrite a ton of papers, she cared about my writing and always wanted me to improve. She loves to get off topic in class though.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Gives good feedback--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Flory is not a bad professor. She based the final grade off of 3 papers ONLY (you can rewrite them as much as you want though). She did not include a TW to specific readings (SA, Suicide). until a student was upset. Doesn't like differing opinions/interpretations HOWEVER, she is very kind/understanding and will work with you on the papers
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. Flory is a kind individual and I appreciated her enthusiasm for her work. The only problem I had was that she was a very harsh grader at times, though she was always willing to discuss your work with you. I didn't enjoy how specific the essay topics were, as it didn't allow for creative freedom when writing. Otherwise, she did a great job.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Just avoid her if you can. There are so many way better lit teachers at Purdue. Her classes are extremely boring but at least they're easy to pass.

She is not open to alternative interpretations. If you don't see what she sees in the works, you're wrong and she'll tell you that. The whole semester was graded around 3 papers, and if it isn't eye-to-eye with her views, you'll get low grades. She's smart but too self-absorbed

Professor Flory is great. She is blunt and harsh and brutally honest, but that is honestly what you want in a teacher. She told me at the beginning of the semester that I had a "problematic" style of writing but I learned so much from that comment and her helpful feedback. She is a brilliant woman with a ton of information to offer. Loved her!

Her class was really enjoyable. She did a great job at making the interpretation of older poetry relatable and fun. Her papers had tough expectations, but if you made a poor grade, she would work with you to redo and resubmit it. By far the best English professor I've had.

Her class is easy and she isn't bad, but if you actually want to learn anything about poetry, then don't take 237 with her. Class discussions are awful. No one wants to talk because she disagrees with anyone's insights that don't completely agree with hers. She only asks questions to fish for specific answers, not to spark open conversation.
Very fun to work with.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Participation matters

reviews.comment: If you don't agree with her interpretation of the poetry, don't even bother turning in a paper. You will get a bad grade. She doesn't post to blackboard and doesn't keep you up to date on your grades so you don't know what your grade is throughout the course. However, she is very friendly and will answer questions very willingly.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Very old fashioned. If you write a paper and she does not agree with your argument she will give you a low score because it doesn't match her personal opinion. Very understanding if you need to miss class or need an extension. Lecture is not discussion based just talking at you. This class is straight poetry. She will let you redo papers.

Just took my final, and I can safely say I didn't learn a thing all semester. Interesting woman and interesting-ish class, if you like to sit and listen to someone talk a lot. No grading criteria, syllabus, or real information given out at any point. I have literally no idea what my grade is. All you can really do is show up and hope for the best.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: She is a wonderful person, professor, and very intelligent. All students learn about life from her lectures- she'll surprise you will her wisdom and antedotes. Recommended highly for all students, not just English or English Education majors.
intelligent, fun lectures. Knows how to run a survey course the right way.

Great teacher! She truely cares about her students, and she is always willing to help you out. If you go to her office hours, you won't be disappointed. Very knowledgeable and has actually met many of the writers. Sometimes gets off track, but you still learn a lot.
She was amazing!  Everyone should take her class just so they can see how great she is.  She really knows her stuff!  She has met so many of the people that she teaches about...she is just great!
She is so enthusiastic and fun.  Listens to everyone, and doesn't make you feel dumb for saying the wrong thing.

She is one of the sweetest people I've met at Purdue.  She will always work with you and she'll always tell random stories about her Bristish or Texan life that are funny because they're quirky or just so lost.  I love her!

She definitely knows her poets.  Course requirements were clear.  Not a lot of papers to write.  Class discussion was like pulling teeth, though not through any fault of hers.  Loved her Harry Potter tangents!  I would definitely recommend her.

She knows what she's talking about and is willing to go above & beyond in helping you understand material. She is very approachable and friendly. One of my favorite profs. ever! And as for the tangents, they link the poetry with other seemingly unrelated factors - and make class fun! So there! :)

She tells you in class what she wants... just go and listen to lecture. The tangents are fun, and useful if you're having an off-day and need to space out for a bit. Very knowledgeable about her subject, and an incredibly nice teacher!

I love Professor Flory! I took many classes with her. She is always excellent, and challenges you to think. She does go on tangents, but I find them amusing. If you show up, do the reading, and meet with her about papers, you'll always get an A.

If she doesn't agree with your interpretation of poetry, you might as well not submit a paper. She will find biographical information that you know nothing about and sugges that you add it to your paper. Not helpful!!
Nice woman who clearly knows her stuff, but she goes off on random tangents that have nothing to do with anything and are a huge waste of time.  SO annoying sometimes.

His lectures are extremely disorganized and hard to follow. Never explains definitions or basic concepts, just randomly starts solving problems or showing MATLAB code, and doesn't explain why or what they mean. Moves really fast, skips sections without warning, makes quizzes much harder than other sections, and office hours are not helpful.

Frazho is very eccentric, energetic, and funny. Our section was ahead of others for the entire semester, which allowed him to give us an abundance of examples that helped us understand the material. His quizzes were very easy if you attended lectures. The homework was, at times, too difficult/long and included things we didn't learn in lecture.

Professor Frazho is an energetic professor and cares for his students in AAE 590 (Probability and Estimation Thoery).  He is a bit disorganized when he teaches but you still learn a lot from his well-written course notes.

Professor Frazho lectured 301 really well this semester. His lectures were clear and he made sure you were able to 'see' the math in action with MATLAB to help your understanding. He also gave great hints during the Homework Help Sessions.

Frazho may be a genius, but he isn't good enough to teach. From disorganized lectures to a complete lack of interest in teaching us something (he would show up to class and start doing math without explaining what he was doing nor how to do it), you will feel completely lost in his lectures and will be on your own, as office hours are not helpful.

Prof. Frazho is a very unique character. He's scatter-brained, foul-mouthed, and brilliant; his lectures feel kind of like you're a muggle trying to take a class at Hogwarts. His tests are extremely tough, but he curves most people to a B at the end of the semester. If you sit in the front and pay attention you'll probably do fine.
During prelabs will give you some hints for the labs and lab reports
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: You MUST pay attention in class otherwise that will be a pain.

Frazho is very unorganized.  You will never really know what your grades are in the class because he "doesn't know where to draw the cutoff." Generally gives you B's if you're within the standard deviation of exams. Exams are graded inconsistently. You don't really learn FROM him and there is no clear purpose of the class until 3/4 in the semester.

Professor himself seems to be a nice guy but a course itself seems unprepared. This is my first class without course syllabus. Exam was scheduled one week in advance. HW just pop up. Course note is quite unorganize and weird (graphs need to be in color but it's in black and white rofl) and really hard to read. Expect self-taught.

The best course I took in my grad school till now; Prof. Frazho pushes the course forward in a really great way. Most of the projection theorem, Hilbert spaces make sense in the beginning but in the final few weeks he uses them to derive the whole Kalman filter from scratch. Greatly knowledgeable instructor!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Its sometimes hard to understand what Frazho is talking about or how what you are doing relates to anything, but if you review the notes he gives after class while you are working on the assignment, it makes scene. Also, prepare to be blown away on exams.
Tough professor to learn from. He knows his material but does not present it well. Be ready to self-teach.

I took 301 with Garrison and dropped it half way through b/c he's incompetent.  I learned more in a day with Frazho than half a semester with Garrison.  I felt very prepared for AAE 364.  If you go to class you'll get at least a B.

I took this class as an undergrad, as did a few others. He said "I admire anyone who is an undergrad and taking this class, so I will not give you less than a B."  I got an A, but he was a genius and tried his hardest to teach.
Very funny guy.  Makes me always want to go to class.  He teaches by example which is great.  Best Purdue Aero professor I've had yet.

A great overall prof.  Can honestly say that I never came close to falling asleep in his class.  He is the only professor that I have ever had that will cuss at math.  Very entertaining and presents the material in a way that I found very helpful.
Has his quirks, but he is a genious.  He doesn't like teaching so he makes his class very easy, which is cool.  However, he is amazing with Math and his knowledge of the subject
No Comments

Good professor.  He has a lot of weird quirks about him, but he is definately the right professor for the class.  He takes a normally hard class with difficult material, and most of the time makes it interesting and easy to learn.

Lower level classes probably aren't the high point of his day (or semester) which is why he practically gives the answers away for his homework.  Good prof if you want to pass, not good prof if you *really* want to learn material.
Very knowledgable and exciting teacher. One of the challenging yet interesting courses I have taken. Final exam was oral and I thoroughly enjoyed it. I am glad I took his course.

He is hands on the best professor I have ever had. Very caring and super supportive of students, he is defnitely passionate about teaching and care about your progress. Personally, I think Purdue should increase his pay rate. The readings he gave us were all extremely interesting. 20/10

Great professor, he really cares for students and makes the class really easy to understand. You don't need any previous knowledge to take his class. Midterm and final are exactly what he taught during the semester, no weird stuff or trick questions. He loves what he teaches and that makes the class so much better. I would take it again 100%
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Prof. Girard is a great person and makes his classes interesting! Would love to take another class from him again. Homework isn't overwhelming and I strongly recommend his class. Has a very accepting classroom that facilitates conversation.

The best professor at Purdue. Genuinely engaging and understandably teaches course material without diluting complex topics. Lecture based with project-based assessments. Would take any of his courses.

Found on reddit that this is the easiest gen ed and was hoping to get an easy A. Little did I know... The textbook has hundreds of pages and is still useless cuz the lecture slides have the same content. Slides are not posted online, so you have to be in class and take notes every lecture. Prof Girard is really cool and helpful tho.
He is the greatest he's one of my favorite professors I've ever had
hes gooooddd

Class is very easy. Labs are great (40% of grade). Low stress, interactive, structured well, actually kind of fun. Lectures are next to useless, mostly him talking about how great his class is and how valuable we are to employers. Group projects for some reason with busy work that really tanks this classes enjoyablity. 7/10 def not the worst prof.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Group projects--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: If 100 people like Professor Richards I am one of them. If 10 people like Professor Richards I am one of them. If 1 person like Professor Richards I am him. If 0 people like Professor Richards I am dead.

Prof Richards is a great professor and is willing to help every student succeed! You genuinely just have to ask him (or any of the TAs) for help, and they are more than willing to aid you in the best way they can. His lectures use online resources which might seem a little odd, but we are learning about technology which continuously changes.
He is a very understanding professor, and he is very attentive to any problems his students have.
While the class primarily uses Wikipedia as a resource the professor is good and very willing to help students if needed.

Easiest A of my life, at the beginning fo the semester you choose what is ghted what and there is a good amount of extra credit, and all homeworks and labs are due the last day of class so it is very easy to get an A, however he doesn't really lecture.

Prof. Richards cares about his class but was clearly sidetracked by other stuff (such as setting up the new Gateway Building and creating new curriculum for MET284), so we (Fall 2022) were given a lot of lee-way with HWs, Quizs, etc. Labs were the only redeeming quality which consisted of programming PLCs which I considered fun.

Ok where do I start? Let's just say he gets heavily funded for his research at the university and that's too much for someone who cannot teach industrial controls to undergrad students. Lectures are literally a joke where he will tell his students to read a ton of WIKIPEDIA articles on certain topics. Exams aren't fair game. Wish I could say mo

You could probably get the same grade in this class by vaguely googling what the hw is and then choosing the first link. Thats basically how lecture feels for a majority of the semester, the class isnt the absolute worst as could be improved but currently its kinda cringe.

Richard does not care about teaching, and his module based learning leaves everything up to you. He grabs seemingly the first result from googling the topic of the week, and force students to learn from those materials. (wikipedia, articles, 600+ page manuals). Then Richard will quiz you on said materials, using questions unrelated to the reading.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Group projects

reviews.comment: Lectures are a joke. Nothing of substance given, basically a list of what to learn and then never gone through in depth. Almost never prepared for the lectures, let alone properly posting assignments on Blackboard. It appears that he cares more about his other jobs/contracts than this course. Better off with another prof. Class was worthless.

He literally doesn't teach you anything. The course is completely self taught. Class is also unorganized and there is no course topics taught in class. Richards just talks about random things that won't help you with any of the assignments.
He did not care of the students. Didnt even show up to class, leaves the TA with everything. Worst teacher in my life
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--So many papers--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Learnt more in lab with TA than in lecture with Prof. Richards. Richards is a bad lecturer.

Richards is a great person outside of lecture, however as a lecture professor he is absolute trash. He spends a small amount of time going over vague topics, then sends everyone home to teach themselves the rest. Cant say I retained much from this class.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: doesn't care a whole lot, just put in some effort and you will pass. deceivingly simple
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: Grant Richards is not very good of a teacher for this course. Class is made up of ECET and MET( MET 284/ECET 30201). He goes way to fast and does not lecture.  Exams were challenging compared to the fact that it was nothing like the book or teachings. Knowledgable but needs to improve.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: This was a newly added course but he did a nice job at teaching. He isn't available that much outside of lecture. He seems to have a really nice grasp on the basics (which is what this course is) and can teach it well.

The two main purposes of this class is to expose you to the information that will carry you on through the ECET major and help you get to know the people in your major as they will be your best hope for help in the future.  Because of this, he often made us beg for help as he was trying to make us use others instead of him.

He was great at teaching you the basics for the course and he was really easy with grading. I think he gave a total of 5% extra credit which really helped since this was most people's first course at Purdue. You have to show up for class every day if you miss more than twice you fail the course but that is the how every ECET class is.
Nice guy.Answers any questions you hae and gives extensive examples.

Attendance is basically mandatory since he "doesn't believe in Brightspace" or share any materials, so if you don't show up you miss everything. Exams(60% lecture, 30% reading) are insanely specific to lectures, and he never once discussed the readings. At least there's a decent curve.

Honestly, I was annoyed he was using his own textbook that was not even published yet. His brightspace uploads have minimal to no relation to class material. This was during the pandemic where class material should be uploaded for students who cannot be present in person. He made life really miserable for students trying to study who did not attend
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Lovely Stories. Exams are strictly based on his notes. I suggest recording them on your phone filling them out later if you missed something the first time. I took both COM 374 and COM 212 so I speak from experience
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: i have mixed feelings about him. He's a good guy with lots of info and hilarious stories to relate to the lecture material. However, his exams are damn near impossible to pass, the only reason why half the people do well is because he has to curve everything, including the quizzes. if you don't go to class, you won't pass.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: More than willing to meet with students for extra help at any point. A lot of the tests were based on lecture notes so don't plan on skipping many lectures. He's a funny guy with a lot of good stories that connect to the material which makes him easy to listen to. Bottom line, you won't regret the class.

Such a wonderful professor! He is challenging, But extremely helpful with everything. If a student doesn't understand a topic, he goes into great depth to figure out a way to help them understand it. He gives great feedback too!
Helpful in anyway possible. Class is very challenging but would do anything to help students out. Personable and also funny. Always talks about his many wives.
Really friendly and helpful.
Challenging but interesting and very easy to take notes since he makes clear what is important.
John Greene is hot in a super confident kind of way. He's also hilarious and the best teacher I've ever had.
No Comments
No Comments

606 with Klimeck was the most difficult class I ever took, but also the most rewarding. Lectures are recorded online, there are two recitations a week to ask questions (one with TA, one with prof), and there is a good textbook. There are two big projects (very hard), an exam (hard), and homeworks (hard but completion) that you're graded on.

Attendance is strictly mandatory. He will not count your attendance if you are on your phone. Expect to significantly improve your resume while ranking yourself against your classmates. However, he does prepare a list of good speakers, about 40% were really good, 15% really boring.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Great professor with a solid intensity to the course. He challenges you but also helps you succeed through extra credit and clear expectations. Really appreciate how invested he is in students learning.

Very challenging yet super interesting course as it gives good concrete exposure to the fascinating world of semiconductor devices. Different topics covered under the vast syllabus of this course find application in 2 well-defined projects. Recitation sessions are helpful and instructor is very responsive, supportive & caring for the students.

Prof. Klimeck strives for excellence, making this a demanding but enjoyable course. It is perfect for budding device engineers due to its heavy focus on the intricate details of physics. However, for those seeking a broad overview, the depth can lead to losing interest. I highly recommend having a solid exposure to quantum physics before enrolling
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Gives good feedback--So many papers

reviews.comment: Prof. Klimeck is a great teacher/mentor. His structure for ECE 694 is quite thoughtful especially the module on Financial Literacy for grad students. I enjoy his presentations and the way he organizes the course. I just feel that most of the presentations on Nanohub are quite old and could make for renewed Presentations from later years.

I am not on for attending non-technical seminars, but this course has a great range of talks. I can count on one hand the amount of talks I disliked or didn't find helpful. The resume exercises were great. The only issue was that the professor could be really direct - which is not a bad thing- but I am not a fan of the way he conveys the message.
He does good job creating some interest in an otherwise uninteresting course
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Prof Klimeck might seem strict but that is because he is serious about the course and wants the students to take something out of it. The lectures were interesting and mostly focused towards professional development. He did a very good job of analyzing the data from the resume voting activity.
Professor Gerhard's aura and the amazing lectures from other faculty truly made this course very interesting and definitely worth taking.
Prof. Klimeck clearly put a lot of effort into making it more than just your generic seminar course.

Prof. Klimeck talks a lot of content that is helpful to grad students, and his speaking is clear and not fast so that you can catch up easily as a non-native speaker. Prof. Klimeck also devotes a lot of time to preparing the courses even though he has taught in other semesters. I appreciate all the efforts

Took the graduate seminar with Prof. Klimeck. I thought I was going to hate the course (like I had every other seminar before this one) but it's clear Prof. Klimeck puts lots of effort into this class. I am surprised at how much I actually enjoyed the class.

he is a detailed person, I learned a lot from him and his course, but it was time-consuming 30+ hours a week of lectures, solving quizzes, 9 homework, recitations (very useful),  2 projects, and 4 peer reviews (Very active learning experience) but cons: 3.5 hours per review. Also, 4 additional reviews, believe me, my friend, you want this bonus.
Professor is experienced and looks to help out all the students grow fairly.
Enjoyed his talks. Inspirational and clearly outlines guidelines for a successful PhD and after should-do tips.

The course both expanded and deepened my technical/physical knowledge and skills.  Professor is eager to explain concepts, one can address him with a research issue and get a pointer in the right scientific direction. Some homework solutions are more connected with mandatory readings. Group projects were an enriching and insightful experience.
The professor is great. It's me who's dying. Not good for mental health. I feel like we don't deserve this course.

VERY challenging course requiring ~25 hours per week for lectures, readings, quizzes, and HW.  HW is challenging requiring a lot of time.  Put in the work and you will succeed.  Group projects are great for fully grasping the material, assuming you don't have dead weights in your group forcing you to do the work on your own. Lots of extra credit!

I had Prof Klimeck for 606 and it has been my favorite at Purdue thus far! I'm in the military so I have weird, long work hours but I found the lectures/readings very interesting (and relevant) and I used that knowledge on the group projects--which were HIGHLY valuable, and are why the class has been so meaningful to me, 10/10 would recommend!

Really good professor! Enjoyed learning the material. One suggestion would be to have individual office hours? Asking question in class can be overwhelming for me. Other than that, enjoyed the class and really appreciate the projects instead of exams. Learnt so much more that way instead of trying to memorize formulas and so.

Excellent professor.  He makes time to really make sure his students know what's going on in class, and is very fair with providing students a lot of ways to succeed.

The class is a lot of work, but that's because the subject matter is difficult- it is very well balanced by the professor's zoom sessions, and quick grading and feedback.

The professor gives unreasonable amounts of work. He will reply that the course has remain unchanged for years, but my issue is not with the content, but the amount of time it took to complete the first few HWs and the first project. The project was a 15 page document with a massive 20+ page rubric. I spent about 20hrs a week on this class.

Prof Klimeck provides (at least in ECE606) a variety of teaching avenues for you to succeed when taking his course. He records his lectures and has live classes his homeworks are moderately difficult and his group projects allow you to really learn and understand the material but they're challenging. He is one of the best profs I've had a 10/10!
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: This informative seminar series managed/organized by Prof. Gerhard greatly contributes to the holistic development of graduate students and should be made compulsory for all during their first year of study. The professor's dedication to thoroughly analyzing resume data was commendable. Seminar presenters came from diverse professional backgrounds.

Prof. Klimeck has a unique teaching style and has the ability to explain any concept or fact using real-life stories and examples. His guidance in building an effective resume and in financial management was quite helpful and informative. I hope to take some other classes under Prof. Klimeck again in the future.
He was chill and the only constraint in passing was to have the attendnace.
I really learnt a lot from his lectures. He is always accessible even though his incredibly busy schedule. An honor to know him

The course is hard and requires you to do a lot of readings. You need to work hard with you teammates on group projects to do well. However you will learn a lot eventually. This is not an easy course.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Group projects

reviews.comment: Undeniably cares about the professional development of grad students. I think a wider representation of diverse people may have made the speakers more enjoyable, but you can always get credit outside of class and therefore near the end of the semester I did.
He is really a great professor who cares. He is hard but has so much knowledge to give. Honestly the class is a grind but if you stick with it you will learn alot.

1)He is cooler than his contemporaries
2)He treats students with a lot of kindness, sincerity, empathy, respect, sincerity
3)Very down-to-earth, approachable, funny, humble
4)Champion of the underdogs

He has introduced projects which are very beneficial for understanding the concept and scoring a good grade. The course is rigorous as it takes time to complete homework and watch lectures. Bonus question helps to boost your score. Try to be up to date with course as it might get difficult to cover everything a week or two before finals.

My only complaint is the amount of work that the course implies, especially for full-time employees taking the course online. It takes excessive time to complete the weekly homework, work on projects and study for the final exam at the same time.

Be sure to get started on homework early and don't be afraid to look through the suggested textbooks for solutions. He does a great job giving you a vast overview of each topic, but in order to be successful with the homeworks, use the book. With respect to the projects, find a group early and pay attention to extra credit criteria, it's beneficial

He may initially come across as a strict, by-the-rules German, but he's actually a softy. 
Banter he has with the different speakers is enjoyable and also gives good presentations himself.
This is a great class. You wouldn't quite easily get such a class. The review assignment is quite differently placed and would allow you to learn things that cannot be taught in a class.

Pros:
- Excellent speaking skills. Contents are clearly delivered in lectures.
- Well organized seminar course, very inspirational.

Cons:
- Workload was more than expected.
- Strict on course participation if you only come for easy credit.

I audited the course out of interest. As a retiree, I am intrigued by emerging technologies which combine math, physics and chemistry. Avogadro's # is ancient. I left enlightened and more able to relate to current technology. I also intend to invest in quantum computing companies armed with information. Thank you Professor.

This is a review for Prof. Klimeck leading ECE694, a mandatory lecture course on career skills. Pros: He really wants to make the course more interesting, genuinely cares about feedback, tries to get interesting lecturers. Cons: Very strict about punctuality and technology out during the lecture.

He prefers projects over tests and he is very clear about the grading. Doesn't teach much by the textbook but many homework questions come from the textbooks so they're still important to read. It's a difficult class but there are lots of chances for bonus points and Prof. Klimeck really cares that you learn the material.
The class particularly might be very challenging. This class genuienly demands a solid work every week, but will kinda actually understand how semiconductors work.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Group projects

reviews.comment: The group projects took some time but the bonus point offers a great boost. Lots of content.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Group projects

reviews.comment: Dr. Klimeck has changed the structure of the course from 2021 Fall: 2 projects instead of midterms. 20 out of 22 students got B+ and above. He provided online and in-class recitations. He is so knowledgeable and willing to answer questions.

Dr. Klimecks simulations helped me understand the material in a more visual manner. I feel like I have a better conceptual understanding of devices after 606. The quizzes could have been designed better. Some choices appear to be the same answer but with one choice being more verbose, neither of which could be eliminated as incorrect choices.
He crammed so much material into 1 damn course. It was back-breaking. His explanation of several topics was very shallow.

One of the most inspirational professors at purdue, really helps you feel like you belong as an engineer. His teaching style is good, and he is amazing at answering questions. Lots of stories in class, and relating what you learn in ENGR 130 to industry experiences. However, Engr 130 is very homework heavy and can feel like busywork at times.

Professor McFall did a pretty good job introducing material but he loves to talk. He spends too much time going over material which usually not needed for the tasks that we have in class. The homework assignments are graded very hard with no rubric to go off of. The group projects come down to how good your group is and if they want to work.

The lectures can be long, but he makes them entertaining with his personal anecdotes. The course, however, is organized in such a way where homeworks and exams are graded with intense scrutiny, yet projects are not. Be aware of this and make sure to double check every detail of information you put on homeworks especially.

He's really not that bad. Sometimes the lectures he gives can drag, but little of what he says is needed to do well (and you can just play on ur phone). Plus he seems to care a lot about making sure women/minorities in the class don't feel excluded. All of my gripes about this class come from the TAs and the curriculum.

He isn't a bad professor by any means. He does have a habit of going on tangents which are only related to the topic half the time. The rubrics on gradescope are quite vague and a lot of people have lost points for seemingly no reason on assignments or tests. Your experience in the class will probably be more dependent on the TA's for the semester.

McFall was an alright professor in ENGR131. The assignments are graded on a rubric inaccessible to students and on an extremely nitpicky basis, meaning you often lose points for things that weren't very clear. His lectures at the start of class are often too long and frequently go off topic, but he is very kind and helpful when answering questions.

He likes to talk way too much at the start of class. You end up missing a lot of "studio time". His lectures aren't very engaging. He is, however, a kind guy and will help you out if there is a problem.

Decent enough person, but terrible professor. Every single lecture was covering material that was already required to learn through the pre-class videos. The first hour of each class was just restating what was in these videos, then expecting students to complete large group assignments in the remaining hour or less.

I'm honestly surprised to see so many negative reviews. To be fair he does talk, a LOT, but there are way worse things for a professor to be than talkative. He seems to care about his students. You're allotted 6 unexcused absences per semester, which is honestly a lot, and there's a ton of extra credit opportunities at the end of the semester.

I think the professor is pretty helpful and funny but his lectures tend to drone on a lot. However, the class itself just isn't super engaging and a lot of slides for lectures. The coursework itself isn't super hard but the grading can take getting used to at first as it is strict for the home works but it prepares you for the exams.

He talks so much. He doesn't care for his students and is very strict at times. The class itself I feel like doesn't depend so much on the professor, but I would take it with a different professor if possible.

He's a great guy, he's funny and genuinely cares about his students. His lectures drone on and on and are incredibly boring, but it's the same set of slides for every teacher. The class itself is basically entirely group work and you're with the same group the whole semester. The grading can be pretty harsh but the guidelines are clear.
grades unfairly, he made fun of a disabled person in my section. Horrendus class take it with literally anyone else
I don't even know what to say just take it and u will understand it.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I had McFall for 132, and he was a very nice person, but the class was really poorly organized. We received ru

130 is a horribly organized course. This might be a course a student would take freshman year of high school. It is all busy work, team building activities, and projects that are entirely useless. Nothing learned in this class will make you a better engineer, besides how to avoid AI detection on coding homeworks. 
The instructor isn't much better.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Group projects

reviews.comment: It is a good class to learn Excel and he does care about his students. Make sure you complete all work and assignments on time and he is very understanding. Gives out extra credit towards the end of the semester. Gave a bad vibe right at the start yelling at us to be quiet but improved throughout the semester.

Mac was a very caring professor and was a very fair grader, as long as you name your files correctly before submitting. He can be hard to listen to, as he talks a lot in class. Good luck if you have him at 7:30. Also ENGR 131 will test you on the amount of effort you are willing to put in. For a 2 credit class, it will push your limits some weeks.

The grading criteria was extremely unclear and resulted in unnecessarily harsh grading despite the content not being particularly difficult. Professor McFall was very unhelpful when it came to clarification of the grading and nobody seemed to know who was in charge of the grading criteria.

The criteria for grading is completely unclear. The TA's are completely clueless about what's going on. We are tasked to do things that we haven't learned. There is no clear guide that we can follow on our assignments or our exams or our group projects.

Really cares about his students! Lectures always run long and are a bit boring but a super nice prof who makes you feel like you can succeed in ENGR 131. A bit condescending when he is trying to get the attention of the entire class, but overall super great!

My experience with ENGR 130 is heavily boosted by the superb experience with my groupmates. We became good friends and honestly this class might be the happiest class of all time. But other than that the lectures are quite a bit boring and there's just so much stuff to do. The exams were also not very easy. So Meh I guess.

Feels extremely condescending and doesn't seem to care for his students. Assignment requirements are convoluted and he's not very accessible outside of class, particularly with extenuating circumstances or any sort of leeway.
He talks most the class, and is somewhat boring yet he is a great teacher. He gets through the material and cares about his students.

Took Prof McFalls ENGR 131 class, the class itself was very structured but due dates and grading criteria could be very vague at times. The lectures were not very interesting but the class is not super difficult if you are familiar with excel and technology in general. The group projects were way too long and the grading was very ambiguous.
He seems to contradict himself often but he is at least interesting.
Class is very structured, and if you hit all of the learning objectives Prof. Mac is a great teacher. Gives interesting answers to questions.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Gary was a fun simple guy, but whenever he lectured, it got very boring especially if it was longer than an hour. Gary responds quickly and is always eager to help, but at times he didn't even know the answer to your question so he just relied heavily on his TA. As long as you do your work and ask for help when needed, then you'll be fine.

Others are just upset about not getting an easy A. The assignments are slightly disorganized, but Gary is a great teacher, and easy exams + HW if you put the effort in, it just takes time. CS takes a lot of self research and practice along with help from others. Do this, and you'll leave this class with a very good base in python and programming.

Took this class over the summer with zero prior python experience. He's not a terrible lecturer, but the "help" he gives is pretty vague most of the time and TAs are far more valuable in this class. Also some assignments require pretty big time investments. Overall, not a terrible guy, there are better professors out there though I'm sure.

The first half of the semester the course goes pretty well, after it's a mess. The projects are hard and there is a lot of work. Homeworks are almost never related to the weekly material. He is kind of rude and a tough grader (89.9% is a B) Avoid at all costs
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: He is a decent lecturer but you are going to have a very hard time if you come to the class with zero coding experience. Also, be ready to put a huge amount of time working on labs and projects.

His lectures were just him reading off a PowerPoint and the projects were always so much harder than what he taught. The grading seems fair though, even if your code does not work you still can get credit for the parts that do. I would recommend picking up a few python skills before as I often spent the most time on this class.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: He is so RUDE. He is NOT nice. This class is one of the easiest classes. However, Gary was the worst professor ever in my life.

The worst professor I have ever had at Purdue. He was not helpful and accuses people of cheating daily. He does not want you to get help outside of the class but the TAs don't have enough time to help everyone. Very cynical and rude in his responses. Only way to pass is get a tutor/beg the TAs to help you. Piazza is useless.

Lectures have nothing to do with homework, programming exercises, labs, or exams. Only thing he teaches you in lectures is what to do in the milestone projects. Its an intro python class and the dude acts like everybody had been coding for 10+ years. Exams are hard as hell. TAs are useless. DONT TAKE THIS CLASS

I took CS 177 over the Corona summer, and it was ok at best. I would HIGHLY recommend taking this course with some Python background, especially if you are taking the course with the online format. It was hard for beginners. It was rough online because of the lack of help from TAs. Emailing about code can only go so far.

If you have any programming experience, this course will be so easy. If you don't, I recommend participating in office hours and TA sessions. CS is not a course where prof can teach you everything, so self-teaching is basically half of the course.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: I think all of those who rated him a 5 or 4 were just saying rubbish. He is by far the worst besides Corey Thompson in this uni. He is rude and does not give a f on the students. He teaches you 1+12 in lectures and then expects you to do some calculus stuff in lab. Many ppl said that they got yelled by him, such barbarism is utterly objectionable.

Absolutely no cohesion in this class. The homework and labs have nothing to do with the lectures and projects, and vice-versa. It is so frustrating to have to constantly look up how to do everything in the homework because we never discuss the homework topics in lecture or recitation and there is nothing in the textbook either. Awful experience.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: He is very to-the-point. Great lecturer with a lot of energy. Least monotone prof I've had at Purdue. Very clear, lets you out early, not afraid to be corrected, and makes actually funny jokes. Slideshow is very good, with lots of sample code.
terrible help during office hours, DId not get back to an email for a whole week, not understanding, makes terrible jokes

Prof. McFall is a real gem; he explains slides clearly in class and will quickly correct any mistakes he makes. Very personable and easy to ask questions to, after lecture or during office hour. You will get out of this class what you put into it, as far as lectures and labs. Exams are extremely difficult.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Hilarious--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: This class was overall way too hard for a intro to python where most students havent had coding experience. The lectures are engaging but that was the only notably good thing about the class. There are office hours, but so many people need help that you dont get the sufficient amount of help you need if you are struggling.

Not as bad as these other reviews say!! Is engaging is lectures even though it might not be over the most relevant stuff, but he and his TAs are available all the time and they give pretty good help on projects and labs. Would only recommend that the projects were a little easier. Recitation was completely useless though
reviews.rating_tags: ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: Before taking this class, I was scared it would be really difficult and that the professor was a monster. Instead, I was met with a very respectful caring teacher who did his job to teach well in my opinion. If you've never learned programming before the class is more difficult but if you study you will get an A.
He is a really bad professor. He didn't answer questions any mroe.

People really hate on Gary for being a tough teacher. However I can promise that if you get through this class, you will have a solid foundation in Python. Many people simply were not willing to put in the time and effort into getting a good grade. If you put in the time, you will do very well.
Whole lot of work!!! It was challenging and frustrating at times but very rewarding at the end. Gary only handles lectures which are extremely useless. You need to go though to get participation.
reviews.rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Clear grading criteria--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: The professor is the only bad part of the class. He made one of the labs extremely difficult just because too many people were getting 100% on them. He is kind of a hypocrite because he says that you can work with other people to share ideas but then penalizes you when you do because the codes become similar. The codes are similar - same ideas.

The class itself is very useful, you get to learn about excel and access which is very relevant in the work place. However, the lectures are awful they have nothing to do with the homework it's just something you need to get a better grade. It's pretty easy to get an A. DO NOT BUY THE BOOK!!! It is the biggest waste of money

There's a TON of homework. It honestly felt like there was constantly homework or lab preps to get done. DO NOT COPY SOMEONE ELSE'S ASSIGNMENT. He WILL fail you, and he's very serious about it. The lectures have nothing to do with what you're learning, just have to deal with it once a week. It's an easy A, just go to lecture and do your own work.
reviews.rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Clear grading criteria--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: Worst of the worst. Nothing more to say.

Heres what to know if you are thinking about taking it: This is not a class that the teacher will tell you how to write every program. He teaches you the concepts and its up to YOU to go home and practice them over and over. Everybody has unrealistic expectations out of him. Every program is different but the concepts are the same. Its up to YOU.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Participation matters--Tests are tough

reviews.comment: I put more time into this class than any of my chem classes (chem major). Most of my learning took place outside of class in UTA office hours and reading on my own. I've gone to office hours 2-3 times every single week all semester. Be prepared to learn on your own. Extensive projects and labs with very little time to complete them.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: Very disorganized class, lectures teach you nothing, the book kinda teaches you what you need to know but not the the extent that he expects you to know for labs and projects, even with online tutorials and help from CS majors still could not figure out how to do most projects and labs.

HE ISN'T THAT BAD. He makes it clear that in his emails he isn't being rude with his responses. He was more than willing to work with me when I had to miss a week of school. If you visit his office hours he is more than willing to walk you through EVERY single step. The organization of the class sucks, but it isn't HIS fault. His lectures arent bad
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Caring

reviews.comment: God help you if you take CS 177 online. Easily one of the most poorly thought out and unorganized classes I've ever had to endure.

He pretty much doesn't teach you anything in his lecture. And everything is not well organised. Lab grade is 20% of the overall grade, but there is no way to finish the lab with the knowledge that you have. Just don't take his class!!!

Easily and honestly the worst professor. He's  unapproachable, unorganized, and unwilling to give you sufficient answers/help to questions while acting annoyed in the process, he even completely blew me off one meeting. DO NOT take the online version. Nothing is organized, you can't get a hold of anyone, no boiler cast, and on-campus exams only.

I don't know where to start with this professor. He showed up late to every lecture and seemed not to care about the class. When students ask questions, he usually quotes the assignment and acts very rude and unprofessional. The class is ridiculous with the textbook being useless. Avoid this professor at at costs

Gary McFall is easily the worst professors I have ever had. He does a poor job explaining class topics and does not give good feedback or answers when you ask him a question. Our homework assignments were due BEFORE the lecture over them. The textbook he had us read was a waste of money and any resources he gave were absolutely useless.

Every time I reached out to him, he always responded as if irritated and unfriendly. He always said he wanted to help but never really did. Acted as if even some simple questions  I had were just overly difficult and would never help me, instead pointing to a book that I had already reviewed and that did not have examples useful for doing homework.

The lectures are useless and unrelated to the assignments, but attendance is mandatory so you have to get through it once a week. There is quite a bit of homework but it isn't difficult and as long as you understand how to do it the exams will be easy. Should be an easy A.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Greg is a funny laid back teacher. Labs matter for this course and there is a lot of homework, but Greg does a good job during lecture and even will hand out food sometimes. You will need to know your stuff, but overall good teacher and class.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: I've had nothing but problems with this professor. Aside from the buggy Pearson software he uses, he's very unapproachable. Whenever I approached him for help on homework or lab preps, I was told was how I should've spent more time on my homework. He spent 20+ minutes of my appt in office hours telling me to work harder on my hw, not teaching me.
Very knowledgable and helpful. Easy going guy.

Good teacher. Interesting, appropriately challenging (not to easy, not to hard) assignments. Always graded things quickly and posted scores with comments.  I think he's hilarious and he made me want to do really well. Extra credit isn't easy, but it is plentiful. Exams are open book, open note, and you can even use past homework.
Do homework by yourself! It really helps in the exams. You can use your previous homework and google during the exam lab. McFall doesn't curve the grade. 89.99 is a B.

It kind of sucks that he is the only professor who teaches this course, but for this course, it really does not matter who your professor is, but more on who your TA is. His lectures can get a little boring, and attendance is mandatory. Expect a lot of homework. Make sure not to use anyone else's work, as he is very serious , nd will give you an F.
The lectures are a complete waste of time. You will basically teach yourself all of the topics in this class, but if you do the lab preps and homework you should be fine.

Class is laid out like a road map. Lots of homework, but if you keep up with assignments you can earn an A. Exams are practicals (not multiple choice) and are due in class, so you gotta go. Several lazy students caught cheating this past semester now want to smear the class... it's not the most exciting course but it's certainly VERY useful
Strict grader. had an 89.4 and wouldn't round up. there are no B+ or B- so my 89.4 was a B. also when I was emailing him about it he was very rude.

His Syllabi states he has a tutor, I  looked for him and never find him. teacher toll me to talk to him only but this arrogant and prideful teacher was never able to help you anyways. I failed this "easy class", lots of computer errors and he did not took it as such. horrible grader. Unfortunate teacher for a Great University.

The lectures and labs have no relation with each other and I honestly never learned anything in this class even though I'm getting an A. It is a very ambiguous class and if you have to take it, pray for helpful TAs for the labs.

Seems nice i lecture, but when you talk to him one on one he is very sassy and rude. We emailed trying to find a time to meet outside of office hours and he replied with an extremely snarky message saying there is no possible time to meet. Lectures are boring but don't miss because they're easy points. Bare thru the excel formulas. The rest is easy
Waste of time, lectures are pointless and the homework is just there to waste your time.
Meh, just not the most entertaining class I've taken in the world. Gary is a nice enough guy though.

The class was useful but there were tons of assignments. It was manageable but the software was annoying. The lecture Prof was awesome though and helped motivate the class. He was funny and goofy and reminded me of Doug from the TV show King of Queens.

MyITLab is so glitchy, and you can't even complete some assignments with a macbook. He thinks he's helping during lectures but it really does nothing. Only reason to go to lecture is for the attendance quizzes. The class isn't hard, so the prof not being helpful isn't THAT big of an issue.

Gary is great and enthusiastic in lectures, but the material is just too boring to fix. Also, he says the lab handbook is helpful, but it really isn't. The multiple choice tests were ridiculous and there is absolutely no way to study for them in my opinion. If you do well on the labs though, an A is not hard to get.
