McFall sets up the guidelines for the class and makes assignment due dates clear. The software used for lab assignments isn't perfect, but if you work through the assignments a couple of times, you'll get an A. Advice:  When you have a problem, don't just send him an email -- go to office hours. He *will* help you.

Incredibly unhelpful and not understanding. The program we used to turn in assignments glitched and caused many students to not be able to submit assignments but he was unwilling to not only correct our grades but not correct the system. I would not recommend taking his class if you can avoid it.

During class he seems like a nice guy, but when you try to meet with him or recieve help he's actually pretty rude and very unhelpful. If you're struggling or need help he is very unwillingly to work with you and suggests just "trying harder". This is a 100 level class which should be easy, but he made it a bad experience. I wouldn't recommend him.

Not a very difficult class just kind of tedious.  The quizzes and exams are a joke because the only way to complete them is to Google the answers.  I wasn't impressed with McFall.  The lectures weren't helpful whatsoever. He tried to switch the last exam of the semester to comprehensive despite the fact that he maintained all year it wouldn't be.

Super nice guy. Always helpful and replies to emails quickly.  All test questions are available through the practice test, easy to get a great grade if you study. My TA was helpful for lab assignments and I never had to take anything home and do it.  Only negative of the class is the handbook that costs $30. Only used it for test sign in worksheets

Course is relatively easy, just make sure you show up to the labs if you need help with the assignments and take the practice test 5-8 times before taking the multiple-choice exam in lab.  The lectures were pretty boring but I don't think there's anyway to make them interesting (teaching Office).

Not a bad teacher.  The material from labs and homework aren't really reflected in the exams.  The labs are the majority of the grade weight for the class, so always make sure to get the labs done.  There's no reason not to get a perfect score on every lab. You just have to put in the time and effort/
He tries to force an exam during dead week against school policy. It is an easy class but useless class. You will get an A but you will get very annoyed along the way.

Class is not easy but McFall makes it clear how to get an A. Show up for class, do the homework and run the practice tests like 20 times each. Beware: his "no cheating" policies are strictly enforced!
He is a nice enough guy but has no business being called a teacher.

The course is well organized. If you go to class and study for the exams it's an easy A. If you're lazy and wait until the last minute to do the assignments, you'll have problems. There's a lot of information but if you pay attention and keep up you can ace this one!

Grading software will screw you out of points on almost every assignment. TAs will not be able to help as they don't really know anything about the software either. Poorly designed and poorly run course in general.

Tests seem to have absolutely NOTHING to do with anything else. Other than that it is a very easy class. Review on your own for tests because what happens in lecture and lab is completely irrelevant on the test.
Just go, pay attention in class and it's an easy A

Easy class, go to every lecture as there is an attendance quiz nearly every lecture. Study hard for the exams via the Blackboard study guides, and you should get an A. The reason I got a B was because I screwed up a few labs practicals.

Very easy class... Just go for class and do the quizzes he will tell u the answer most of the times or else someone will just shout it out.... The exams are really easy if you practice using the question bank on blackboard do the question bank about 6-7 times homeworks are easy labs are easier if you do the homeworks and practicals are a bit tough but manageable  u shud get an A VERY easily if you just attend lectures and labs...
If you don't get an A in this class you have done something wrong. He can't make things any clearer than they are in this class. Good teacher and entertaining.
An easy A if you go to class and take the exams seriously.

He is a good professor, very clear in his teaching style. However, it's not very pertinent to the class, you can get by without going to class. Labs can be easy if you get a good TA. Gives surprise quizzes(esp right before/after holidays) and usually at the end of class, so I used to have a friend text me if there was a quiz. Overall, an easy class
Teacher sets you up for success, but students are lazy. His lectures are boring, but you have to work for an A

The class is only hard cuz he spends little time going over material. Labs are pointless unless you have a nice TA (mine was ok) and tests are hard. He gives you practice exams but there are so many questions so it is still hard. You dont learn anything useful. I got away with a B and I'm just happy to be done.
Useless prof, useless class.

good professor, personable. Very clear about what were going to cover. Goes over schedule of the semester every class. Test are not easy but he gives sample exams that have the EXACT questions so you just have to put in work and ull do good in the class. Easy quizs are given randomly in lecture! Overall very useful class and good professor!

Class was difficult. Exams were challenging, but we were given practice tests with the EXACT questions. Anyone who spends a minimum amount of time studying should do well. Warning: Don't miss lab and be sure to submit assignments on time even if they're only 1/2 done.
Gary is a jerk! His attendance quizzes are basically impossible and his tests are really hard. Good luck!
No Comments

Such a fun professor, makes the topics on ANSC relate to real life. His labs are really hand on which is great to learn what we saw during the lectures that week in a different way. Very knowledgable about what he is teaching, honest, and tests are relevant to what we see in lecture. Workload is perfect.

Professor Nicholls is a very solid professor, and I recommend going to lectures as recordings don't always work. There are no study guides, does hand out all the lab quizzes and has a discussion post where he occasionally answers questions. Assignments are straightforward, and you're given a week to complete.

I highly recommend Dr. Nicholls to freshman. He's very funny, nice ,and personable. I would recommend going to class because the recording don't always work. The tests are always straight forward. The labs are interesting. Highly recommend him!
Amazing professor cares about student success and is very funny in class

Dr.Nicholls is by far the best professor Ive ever had. He cares so much about his class and makes it so fun. The labs were amazing opportunities. He is also very down to earth and personable. As long as you pay attention and actually try in this class, you will learn A LOT.
Dr. Nicholls is a great professor! He's very passionate about what he teaches and that made the class so fun. Labs were amazing and I learned a lot. His lectures are also entertaining.
I loved this class. He wasn't even supposed to teach the class, but he did an amazing job, had a ton of fun in the labs, and I learned a lot.
He is a good professor.  Fun guy, class isn't difficult.  He teaches off of slideshows and gave fill in notes.  The slides don't get posted so you do need to go to class.
Nicholls was an amazing instructor this semester. He is super interactive and passionate about what he teaches, which makes the class a lot more enjoyable. He gives very good feedback on questions.

Nicholls yaps a LOT, his lectures are always super long because he talks about every bulletpoint for a hot minute. One thing I didn't like about him was that he refused to be flexible when I was stuck at home after break due to a huge snow storm in my hometown, and did not allow me to join my group mates via zoom/facetime for a presentation.

Professor Nicholls is absolutely amazing. I would recommend him to anyone. He is a beef guy and loves cows. He loves teaching about all animals and is knowledgeable. He is willing to help any student who needs it as long as you reach out to him. He is a hilarious person and so down to earth.  You can get Aan  as long as you pay attention.

He's a reasonable instructor who asks questions in class instead of assigning homework, giving students until the next day to submit responses. He also reviews answers in class to reinforce learning. Only thing he could improve is adding multiple-choice questions and also provide more exam review material.

Dr. Nanda is a smart instructor, but I feel like there is no passion for teaching in his classes and I get the sense he hates his job. He is very monotone and mainly just reads off slides with a heavy accent. However, he is a very fair grader and he makes content understandable, although exams can be tough since all questions are free written.

He teaches a number of IET classes at Purdue, mainly just reads off of the lecture slides but tries to keep the class engaged with questions throughout. Grading is consistent, fair, and well defended or lecture assignments and project work. Opposite of Stevens, like a fire and ice scenario within Purdue IET. You will do fine in any of his classes!

For IET 235, which is super easy homework, the exams were a bit tougher since they were open-ended and had to be argued. I would say I don't like Prof. Nanda's teaching style since he can be monotone most of the time. Yet, he is super nice and an easy grader. I got late to an exam and he gave me an extension which is unheard of.
He gives out a lot of homework (3 per week) but they are usually easy and he solves all the homework in class to show how to do it. Great lectures and you learn a lot. My top 5 professors at Purdue.
He is a kind soul

Prof. Nanda is one of the best professors at Purdue Polytechnic! He clearly explains the material and walks you through the topics. I took several classes with him and every time the course quality is highly exceptional. He is caring, always makes sure that everyone stays on the same page and helps outside the classroom.

Very unorganized. Was late on posting the exams and assignments. She is a nice person, not a great teacher. She talks like she is on 2x speed and does not have the best lecture slides. There was a lot of info on the exams that were never talked about in class. The exams are extremely wording and give you no time to process the question.

Dr. Hart was my favorite professor this semester. While her lectures were not mandatory, they were always fun and interesting. Lectures were mostly helpful for case study sessions, as Dr. Hart and the TAs were available to help. She is often difficult to reach over email, but if you ask a question in person or follow-up, she is very willing to help

Definitely a good professor, but I think the class is challenging. The advice I would give is to read the textbook and know the content before coming to class, and be engaged. Incrementally studying will ensure that studying for the exams is not a chore. Exams are not a joke and are synthesis-based with some route memorization.

She often asks random questions in quizzes that are not covered in class or her lecture slides. you can entirely understand biochem at a 1st semester level and still lose sevral points. cannot switch between questions on her quizzes/exams either, so you can't manage your time(why??). also unclear/ incorrectly graded quiz questions. nice ladyatleast

Dr. Hart is a very understanding professor. She gives plenty of exam prep and extra credit opportunities. The only issue I had was that her lectures relied heavily on examples, and it was hard to figure out the general trends that we were expected to know for exams and quizzes.

Dr. Hart breezes through lectures, so do readings before class!! The exams had recommended time limits on the questions and did not let you go back which made them tricky. Even if you knew the content the real-world applications made it difficult to prepare for. Overall OK class, just wish she slowed down and elaborated on important things.

Professor Hart is a wonderful woman and very passionate about biochemistry. Unfortunately, her exams were extremely difficult. I did not fail this class only because she gave a good amount of extra credit towards the end of the semester. The exams were long and in paragraphs and you are given less than a minute for each question to read and answer.

Dr. Hart gives amazing and interesting lectures and she really cares about her students. Some of the exam questions can be tricky but imo very doable. Mostly they require you to think analytically about biochemistry and sometimes require consulting outside sources. Nothing compared to engineering exams but you cannot get by on pure memorization

As a person, phenomenal. interesting lectures and a great speaker. Prepare to be assessed over things never discussed and have NO TIME to do so. Very long, wordy questions that cant even hardly be read in the amount of time given. its an unfair assessment of knowledge with such little time given. "goal is to learn while taking the exam"

Dr. Hart provided plenty of extra help on the brightspace page. However, she was extremely hard to get ahold of. I took this class over the summer and I emailed her more than 3 times and she never responded. I had to send the email to the TA to get an answer.

Nice prof. Generous grading scale with a functionally optional final. There's a decent amount of work each week, but nothing excessive. Quiz and homework questions sometimes feel nitpicky or poorly worded. Case studies took months to be graded, despite numerous promises from the TAs. If you've done cell bio/genetics, this class is 85% review.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Dr. Hart seems like a down to earth person, but her class is unorganized and had a numerous amount of tech issues which caused confusions with due dates. The material that she would go over in class is completely useless for the actual exams that you take and tend to be fairly difficult.

Solid professor, and passionate about Biochemistry. Dr. Hart is super down to earth and easy to access outside of class. This class can be a lot of work!  2 homeworks and quizzes each week. Exams are online/open note, but can be hard! You will still need to study  in advanced for them! Definitely learned a lot, great MCAT and other classes prep!

Dr. Hart is one of the coolest professors I've ever had. However, this course has lots of workload with up to 2 study guides, pre-quiz, and post-quiz due each week. Exams are open-note but somewhat difficult. It's very easy to let the work pile-up in so make sure you stay on top of it.

I took the online summer course.  Expect to cover 2-3 chapters per week.  Each chapter has a pre-quiz, a study guide, and a post-quiz.  I highly recommend watching the lecture videos (on 1.25 or 1.5 speed) and filling out the study guide as you go.  Exams are tough, but open note.  You may be asked to discuss your exam afterwards, so be prepared!

Will never take this course again even I got a C. My 3.95 GPA was totally ruined by this course. Can't understand lecture cause professor spoke too fast. A lot of homeworks and a lot of materials for a week. Homework and post quizzes are outside range of the knowledge talked about in class. Homework was graded very strictly.  Extremely hard exam.

I thought that this class was super easy and a great introduction to biochem for the MCAT. The lectures are all online, the office hours were always on a new interesting topic (optional). The exams are take home with an emphasis on applying knowledge to new concepts. The only bad thing is that exams and hw take long to grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment:
Extremely unorganized class (no information on when homework, quizzes, exams and final exam were to be released/due until hours before). Professor did not have office hours, and some TA's wouldn't show up to their office hours.
Completely inaccessible via email. 
Grading occurred very slowly.
Tested on material that was never discussed or taught
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: TBH I thought this class was cake. Exams are open notes and resources and she encourages you to work with others, as the problems require understanding and application of concepts, not regurgitation. Was lecture heavy over summer w/ 3 50+ slide powerpoints a week. No A+ given either, which is annoying.

Genuinely speaking, you will be hard pressed to find a professor who is more down to earth and understanding than Dr. Hart! Not only does she treat students like adults who have real world problems, but she also has such a truthful passion for teaching students about biochemistry. She will help with problems and doesn't judge you for getting lost.

The class was a hybrid lab class, and pretty standard. The graders were easy. She has online videos you can watch and refer to make sure you're doing the right calculations. Just do the work, turn in on time and you'll do fine. NGL the end of semester was wonky with deadlines.

At best, the class is sloppy. The course page is awfully difficult to navigate, due dates are unclear, what will be on quizzes is also unclear, and feedback from TAs takes forever. Dr. Hart's lectures are decent and she is definitely passionate and knowledgeable, but the lack of care for the course and clarity of expectations makes this unenjoyable
Liked this class way more than I thought I would. Great start to my MCAT prep, and she is really available to discuss stuff outside of class time.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Participation matters--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: Lab reports are demanding. You'll put a lot of effort into this class.

Tons of work required, but Dr. Hart is really helpful and is really dedicated to making sure we learn and understand material fully. One of the best profs I've had at PU. I did well, but you have to keep on top of the work; you could see some people just didn't spend the time prepping for class, and they struggled.

Do not recommend taking her class if you know absolutely no German. I had issues with her that led me to drop the class (against guidelines to explain) despite getting good grades. Speaks only in German in class, most learning is through homework, does not explain things well for new speakers.
It is important to stay on track of homewroks and participate in class.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: She means well and is a sweet person, however she is not the best at teaching material. You only get two attempts on her homework, and homework is where you will do a lot of your learning.

Very helpful when it came to learning German from scratch. Did a really good job explaining grammar tenses and how to use them. Homework and Quizzes are very fair and review sessions in class really help to study. My only complaint is that she'll tell us we have a test or a project the day before it happens (syllabus isn't updated). I'd take again.
I learned a lot of German language in class. Students are equally respected. There is a lot of individual work with students when they need help.

She has good intentions for her students to learn and do well but is a little shy when speaking and has a pretty harsh accent. She also forgets things quite a lot and students need to help remind her of things. Overall not bad, I learned German, she was just a little inconsistent.
Kind and good intentions. As long as youre participating in the class it should be an easy A. Willing to work with students and not terribly hard on grading in my opinion.

The class is easy enough but she does a very poor job in delivering the material. She repeatedly interrupts people when they volunteer to answer questions. She's pretty easy on grading but if you get her, good luck actually learning from her.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: It is a great class go have.

The class itself is not that hard because it's just an introductory course. Most of the time she would not understand or misinterpret students' questions, since she does not speak very good English. I do not recommend taking her classes. Most of time she spends the class time going over textbook questions that you already did for homework.

He wasn't very good at explaining the concepts of the class. I found myself having to go home and review what we learned in class using Youtube or a tutor, as he was not very clear in class about things
Keeps lectures engaging and teaches lots of information at a manageable pace
This class was incredible. I absolutely loved having Hojun Seo as my professor. He cared deeply about his students and made the lectures very interesting. If you have the choice please take Hojun.

Not a great professor - he rambles in class and it's difficult to follow. Exams are way too hard, not representative of our learning, and all the averages so far are Fs. He is late to post slideshows, answers, and practice on Brightspace. Even though the exams are terrible, we are allowed cheat sheets, the final is open note, and he does curve.

It's a difficult class, but if you stay off your laptop and engage, it's easy to relatively succeed. Exam averages were around 50%, and he curved them 20-25% each time. He cares about your learning and provides a lot of examples and opportunities to learn. HW is completion. The material is dry, but he can't do too much about that...it's accounting

I feel like I learned nothing in this class.
If the HW was graded for accuracy and not completion it would incentivize students to actually learn and ask questions. 
Crappy answers to all 13 questions on the HW is a 100% but accurate answers to 12 is a 66%.
The exam is identical to past exams/ HW, just have everything on your cheat sheets.

Professor Seo is a good professor. His class had no attendance, however being present for lectures is a good idea. The only grades are from exams. Each exam is curved by 20-40 points. Not hard to do well, do the homework even though it is optional. Exams are open notes (2 Pages).
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Seo is a very nice guy, but the class can be a bit dry at times. The class really consists of 3 exams that make up about 90% of your exam. The exams are tough and long, but they are curved at a pretty good clip.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This is a class about a subject that many find boring, but professor Seo makes it pretty interesting. The material is hard, but after the first exam the next three were made open note. At the end there was a large curve so don't worry if you grade seems low because you can still finish with an A if you are beating the average.

I had him when it was his first semester teaching here. No homework, three exams worth 90% and 10% of professionalism in class. His exams were similar to the practice problems he gives and they were never too tricky. His lectures are basically straight from the textbook.

I think she has good intentions and is easy to reach for questions. But, giving very little guidance on a project worth 75% of our grade, lengthy and unclear requirements being added to it at the last minute before an academic break, and having us write 20+ paragraphs of reflection, all for her to obtain a grant, has been incredibly frustrating.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Course was unnecessarily difficult. Class was online and was disorganized - assignments being added near the end of the semester after having a clear schedule. Expectations were not always clear or were changing. She seemed like a nice person and interested in the students, but she liked to highlight her career/accomplishments.

IDK why she keeps getting selected for integral EEE courses like 350 and 380, she's just not a great professor. She is on sabbatical while teaching so is almost never present. The one good thing is that her courses are almost all graded on homework, plus one generally easy final project and attendance; no exams! ...which for thermo was a lifesaver.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Doesn't teach homework material in class. Spends class time doing things unrelated to homework, then expects us to read the entire textbook and learn homework material for ourselves. Also, homework is worth half your grade.

I thought that Professor Hua did a great job at explaining the course content. Any criticisms I would have would be with the course itself and not the professor. The class just had a ton of group projects that could become quite overwhelming fast, especially if your team members weren't great.

The course material itself was not difficult, but the workload was large but definitely not unmanageable. She was very clear about course and assignment expectations. However, I found the lectures to be too long, especially when the material discussed was always reviewed before class.

One of my favorite teachers here yet. Grading is a little tough as you need to put a ton of time into the homework assignments and there is only one exam, but the lectures are super interesting and the team aspect of the class is super fun. I would recommend reading the book because many of her lectures skip over stuff covered in the book.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: No midterms, little problem solving, lots of class time wasted on group work or skits or activities, then offers you no guidance on a cumulative final (will not even reveal layout or topics covered), is strangely secretive about it, and asks you to make an outline of the entire course as your study guide and study everything. Impossible.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Group projects--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Her lectures are not very clear, as her voice is low. And there are a lot of group work in class, which are time wasting. Scores of the assignments really depend on the TAs , and there will be no solutions afterwards. She also don't post practice exams, which make us have no idea about how to prepare for the exam.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: There aren't exams which, as good as that sounds, makes the final very scary. She doesn't really tell you what is on the final except that its cumulative but we cover so much material that you have no idea what to expect for it. The homeworks take a long time and, other than the book problems, you never know what they want as far as answers.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Group projects

reviews.comment: bad professor, no solution for hw/quiz or everything, and have very unclear grading policy.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: A lot of the times, her homework or class assignments are not from the textbook. How are we supposed to know things that are not even on the textbook? Never again
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: She is smart and easy to talk to, but absolutely terrible at teaching. Everyone I spoke to in class, have no clue on what is going on. HW's are random questions, unrelated to the class material and does not really help you learn anything. Answers are not given out either, so you have to look for a needle in the haystack while reviewing for finals.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: She is good teacher but the class is hard. attendance is mandatory, she has participation questions to check . Had a group project. Has mini in class test which are worth a lot of points and are relatively hard.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Meh. Lectures were kind of interesting and she used them to address the practical implications of what we were learning. Only problem was she never actually teaches the material, and there's almost no homework. So by the time you get to the exams you'll have had almost no practice with the material and you'll just be expected to know everything.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Lectures were unique. This was one of the easiest classes I've taken at Purdue. if you do the readings and use her office hour she's really clear. the exams were three questions. two easy from the book and one conceptual. lectures were straight from the text. engaging but not too helpful (required as well)
Very boring and helpless professor
If you do the assignments that she assigns, you'll be fine. If you pay attention in class, you'll be fine. Great professor! Helpful in class and out of class!

Teaches in an effective manner including lots of examples and lots of class participation. She even has some game show formatted classes. I strongly recommend this professor. She's probably the best I've had at Purdue and is extremely helpful with any questions you have.

One of the worst professors I've had.  Just goes through the lecture without any class participation.  Requires attendance even though nothing is really taught.  Although the course material did not help to make the course interesting, her teaching style made it even worse.

Attendance is required even though she teaches directly from the book and adds little insight to the material.  2/6th of lectures are either spent on worthless review sessions or quizzes.  Equations are provided for the quizzes but not random constants.
Very Boring Class! She basically just teach straight out of the textbook, and have quizzes every other week, the quizzes include a lot of useless material that you have memorize.

Currently taking this class. Half of the lab reports are graded on things that are barely mentioned in the lab or by the TA. The instructions on lecture or lab assignments will state one thing, but they're actually graded on very specific hidden criteria that is not mentioned at all, so it's impossible to somehow know what they want.

Dr. Hans is a truly incredible professor who loves the material she covers and cares about the people she's teaching it to. However, the TA's for this course really bog down the class, they grade harshly on things things they did not inform you on in lab prep. The lectures are absolutely fantastic, but the labs are a complete nightmare.

Wonderful class experience, she truly cares about the material (especially if it has to do with entomology). The class is very easy and if you pay attention in lecture you should have no problem with assignments.
Lectures were find but the labs and analysis report papers take long to complete and have very vague instructions. Terrible ta

She loves what she teaches and it shows through her lectures. There are not a lot of assignments and attendance was only mandatory during guest lectures. However her labs were difficult due to instructions being vague. My TA for her class would tell us to do one thing then grade completely differently resulting me getting points taken off on labs.

Directions and rubric for analysis reports and labs widely unclear and they grade super harshly. Experienced glitches with iClicker, talked to her about it and still refused to give me credit for showing up to guest lectures. Does not care as much for lectures unless they are about entomology.

Very knowledgeable professor and covers a variety of areas in forensics. Labs are pretty hard, and grading is pretty tough. I found the rubric's were not very clear and they aren't lenient with grading. Make sure you ask all your questions in lab/over email before completing assignments. She's nice, but I found grading tough.
my fav class at purdue. absolutely loved it. considered changing my major to forensics. super interesting, would love to take more classes with her if i have time

I absolutely loved having Dr. Hans as a professor!! I have never had a professor who truly cares about what they are teaching. It makes the class so much more enjoyable. I am taking another class with her and I'm so excited to see how this one will turn out.

Dr. Hans was a good lecturer and she clearly cared about the class subject. She is also very understanding that this intro class was a filler for a lot of people's schedules, so she made sure to have a light coursework. The class is mostly completion other than the quizzes, which you will have to read articles for. Do the work get the grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Prof Hans is a great lecturer and very nice who is easy to communicate with. However, I am critical on the cold case project itself. It feels like an unfair project to work on if you're given a victim that barely has any information about themselves. If you don't have any info, you get deducted a lot of points. Other than that, the class was fun.

Labs are somewhat interesting but the lecture material was so boring. I walked out multiple times because I felt she didn't seem interested in her lectures (unless it was about bugs).  She claims that you can TA for her but then as soon as you reach out she says "Spots are full". I wish there were other professors besides her.

This was a great introductory course to forensic science. There are quizzes and/or assignments almost every week and two projects, but if you watch the lecture videos you'll be fine. No late work is accepted but just keep track of the syllabus. Overall a pretty easy and interesting class!

Her lectures were always so interesting and it was cool how she has experience in the field. She has a textbook to use but she gives it to the students for free which was really awesome! I highly recommend her as a professor!

Dr. Hans is an absolutely amazing professor. She is super engaging and extremely passionate about her field. I took three courses taught by her and was a TA for two of her classes; I never had a dull moment! I would highly recommend taking her classes :)

Dr. Hans is hands down the best professor I have had. Her lectures are always interesting and she loves to tell us about cases she has worked on. Having a teacher currently working in the field gives great perspective and you can tell she genuinely cares about her students and the subject she is teaching.

Very easy class with minimum work (max 1 hour per week) and has interesting contents. Although keep in mind that there are very few criteria in homework/few questions in quizzes, and it's easy to get low grade (50 - 70) if you mess up a few questions. No EC opportunities, but it's easy to get an A if you put some care.

Dr. Hans is amazing and this was the most unique class I've ever taken. Dr. Hans is extremely qualified and has some amazing stories/experiences that she shares in lecture. The labs were pretty easy, especially if you put in the proper amount of effort. The analysis reports are kind of annoying while you are writing them, but the grading is fair.

This class was entirely online and asynchronous for me. You have a quiz or an assignment every week but if you watch the lectures, you will be completely fine. She does not accept late work no matter what but everything is due at midnight on Sunday. You'll get an A on every assignment as long as it's obvious you watched the lectures.

BEST PROFESSOR EVER!!!!  She cares so much about her students, gives you so many good stories of cases she's worked on, and overall is super personable. She doesn't require textbooks or has exams only online quizzes. If this is an area you are even slightly interested in, definitely take her classes. The labs are also really fun!!!

I have had two classes with Dr. Hans and will have more to complete my minor. I am so thankful that she will teach my future classes. She makes the course so fun and interesting yet you learn so much at the same time. You can tell she is passionate and loves what she does. She is hands down my favorite professor I've had at Purdue.

Professor Hans is by far my favorite professor at Purdue. Not only did she give amazing lectures (even early in the morning when I would usually rather be sleeping), but she really ignited my passion for Forensics. I am so glad she is basically teaching all the classes for my Forensics minor because I feel like I can really thrive in her classes.

By far the best professor I've ever had. Not only did I love the class, but I just loved going to class (even at 9:30 AM on a Monday) and getting to hear her lectures. She loves what she teaches and is always available to answer any questions.

Amazing professor! She really loves her job and her lectures are always very interesting. She also made sure the homework we did was directly related to work in the field of forensics, rather than a bunch of busy work. Labs are very clear and fun as well. Love that she's teaching all the classes in the forensics minor!
good prof, good class. very passionate about her field. She gave some very interesting cases that kept me engaged.
shes a literal queen. she makes the course fun and quite easy to pass.

I LOVE DR.STARR!! She's one of the sweetest professors that I've ever met and she truly cares about her students understanding the content! Her and her TA's do a phenomenal job of helping you as long as you reach out to them (especially in office hours). She gives tricky exams, but she gives many resources to prepare for them.

Dr. Starr is a great professor! I had her for both CHM 125 & CHM 126! She makes her class challenging but doable. Her tests are reasonable and are always related to class material with very few curve balls if any at all. Moreover, she gives plenty of extra credit for students to earn! Going to class isn't required, but counts as extra credit!

Dr Starr is amazing! The class is challenging but very doable. She explains the concepts well and is very helpful during office hours. Labs and homework were fairly easy, and exams reflected the material. There are a lot of opportunities for extra credit, go to class and read the instructions on your exam!! Overall, a very fun and engaging class.

Dr. Starr is great. Go to lectures, it will improve your grade. There is no curve for any of her classes but the extra credit helps. If you study you can probably get over 100 on every exam. Study for the final unlike I did!

I love Dr. Starr. She genuinely loves what she does and teaches with passion. Use her office hours whenever you can because they are really helpful. She will help you with any confusion one-on-one. She gives extra credit just for participating in class. I loved 125.

Dr. Starr is one of the kindest professors ever. Her office hours are really helpful, and she offers to meet with students individually after exams. Her expectations are reasonable and fair. She makes class at 8:30 fun.
Dr. Starr is one

This professor's grading criteria is my biggest issue. The amount of homework was definitely manageable, but I did not like that the homework was due the lecture after finishing the homework material, I would much rather prefer the homework have constant due dates (Ex. Every Friday). I did not like that the lab reports were due 3 days after lab.

Dr. Starr is a great professor who is very passionate about chemistry and made lectures engaging. I looked forward to attending every class even at 8:30am. I strongly recommend this class for first year chemistry majors since it is a great class to build foundational knowledge.

Dr. Starr is such a kind person who truly wants her students to succeed. I had her for the whole year and she made freshman chemistry fun and helped make it less intimidating. She has very high expectations, but she gives plenty of resources to help and she offers to meet with students individually. You should definitely go to her office hours.
She was absolutely amazing, so intelligent and so kind. Tests were fair and she provided a lot of study materials and was always so helpful and approachable!
Actually an amazing professor. Class is easy besides for exams but office hours are so welcoming and a great resource!!

Dr. starr is a super caring and kind person, she really cares about her students, and she prioritizes  making meaningful relationships with her students. She have a very strong grasp of the material and has never not been able to answer a question. she gives a good amount of partial credit on the exams and all the exams are very fair. 10/10!

She is accessible outside class, offers helpful advice, and uses a clear grading rubric. Tricky exam questions are flagged in lectures. Attendance, though at 8:30 AM, earns bonus points. Exams aren't curved; most content is covered in lectures, with key lab and recitation topics revisited in the three midterms or final exam.

Class is pretty easy if you have taken AP chem if not, it might be a little harder, but there are so many opportunities to have lectures explained in smaller settings. Overall, she is amazing and absolutely love as a professor.

I had Dr. Starr over the summer and she was great. Even though the class was online, she still had regular times to meet with us and was very clear about what would be on exams. She was kind and supportive and I'm a little sad I couldn't take a course with her in person. She's probably the best professor I've had at Purdue.

CHM 126 has a good amount of assignments, but it also has many opportunities for extra credits. The exams this year were also exceptionally easy, being mostly short answer questions with points for work. Dr. Starr was a good lecturer but I did not go to class much because she went a little too slow. Her notes are great, very concise and neat.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Dr. Starr was a great professor. She really cares about her students. Her lecture example problems reflect what she puts on her exam. Her lectures can be fast paced, but she is easy to follow.

I loved Dr. Starr! She has high expectations of her students. The exams were very fair since most of the questions followed her in-class examples. I really like how Dr. Starr is passionate, down-to-earth, and very energetic; even at 8:30 am. She is super helpful and friendly in lectures and office hours. By far my favorite prof at Purdue!

Dr. Starr is one of my favorite instructors I have ever had. It is very clear that she cares a lot about her students and she makes an effort to connect with us. She's a very good lecturer and while she does go quickly through her slides, she is not difficult to follow. If you show up to class and put effort in, you should have no issue succeeding.

Dr. Starr is super accessible and incredibly kind and energetic. It is not hard to do well as the exam questions are almost always formatted exactly as the examples during class. She effectively prepares you for exams and assignments and is very fair with giving partial credit on exams. Great professor for chem majors to build base knowledge with.
She does a good job at keeping up the pace in lectures because this is a very fast-paced course. She's fairly new, but it isn't that obvious!

Fast talker with dense lectures in order to get through all of the CHM 129 content. Get ready to ask for help if you don't have a strong chemistry background - it's easier if you sit near the front of lectures or go to her problem solving sessions - but she's very approachable and very friendly.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Dr Starr talks VERY fast and it is sometimes hard to keep up with notes. It taught me how to take notes efficiently. The class is really homework heavy, but that forces you to study. Dr Starr was really helpful and friendly when I had questions. It's obvious she enjoys what she does. She provides a lot of in class practice problems that help a LOT.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: One of the nicest professors. She covered a lot of info very quickly but that was due to the course and not her. Lecture was kind of pointless as long as you knew how to do the problems but she made lecture interesting and it helped some. Def recommend her

Very fast paced lectures, but taught everything we needed. I recommend going to problem solving sessions as you get limited practice problems in lecture. This class isn't bad especially if you have previous chemistry experience as most of the course was review of topics I have already seen.

Chem 129 is a challenging course, but Dr. Starr made it very manageable. Despite the course progressing at an extremely rapid pace and the tremendous amount of homework, Dr. Starr explained the content in an understandable way and was very engaging with students. Take the course with her, instead of Conklin, as Starr is far superior in my opinion.

Her lectures are very fast. They have a lot of content, if you miss one lecture, you're screwed. Overall, fun chem labs and recitations, but lectures are too hard to comprehend. I recommend READING THE TEXTBOOK to do well in the class. Exam 1 and 2 were easy A's, while Exam 3 was awful. Study up for the final y'all!!.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: CHM129 is such a horrible class and genuinely sucks up at least 10 hours of time every week just to pass the class, however, Starr genuinely cares about her students and tries her best to make lecture understandable. If you have to take CHM129, try to be in her class or just at least attend her lectures instead of Conklin's.
Dr. Chen of Chemistry. Took CHM 116 over summer, but had a copious amount of experience with her. Genuinely cares about every one of her students.

Prof. Starr was a breath of fresh air when she took over the course following exam 2. She included a copious amount of practice problems in lecture which allowed me to understand the content. She has a problem solving session for additional practice and supports you in your learning if you so require. A great professor with an awesome attitude.

She is so sweet and it was a relief when she took over the course. It is clear that she wants her students to succeed and she gave so much practice to help us. She's also very nice outside of class and I always felt like I could reach out to her for help. I hope I can take more classes with her.

Very caring. Makes lectures like SI sessions rather than mindlessly copying notes. Be sure to make acquaintance with a few people around you to go through lecture problems. I was already very burnt out by the time she took over, so my opinion will be a little negatively biased towards 115 overall.

Professor Starr was amazing!! In general, she spent about half of the lecture going over the new content we were learning, and the other half of the class was spent doing example problems (very similar to exam questions). Also, since we only spent half of the class going over content, prof. Starr only covers what you truly need to know.

I absolutely loved Dr. Starr's class! Even if you're not the best with chemistry, Dr. Starr goes over so many examples in class and tries to explain difficult concepts in multiple ways. She's very approachable and even offers problem solving sessions outside of her office hours. I highly recommend this professor!

Starr was AMAZING. She would have warmup problems and would always do practice problems during the lectures. She was super caring and would ask us what we needed. I always felt prepared for labs and exams after her lectures.

She was an amazing professor during lectures and made sure to ask for improvements from the beginning. There were always practice problems to work on throughout the lecture and she made sure to go over the solutions in lecture, unlike some other professors. She also had a problem solving session on top of her normal office hours for extra help.
approachable

Professor Starr is amazing. She is very kind, and I felt like she really cared about our learning. Use the resources offered in chm 115 and the class really is not that bad. Make sure to study for the exams because they can be a little difficult if you do n out study, but one exam is dropped.

Professor Starr is awesome! The "Pet of the Day" started our lectures on the right path. She worked several practice problems and I felt very prepared to take the exams. She's very enthusiastic about the content and chemistry was the highlight of my day. Take Professor Starr if you can!
I enjoy Dr. Starr's lecture style and practice problems. She explains things in depth and writes notes on the slide with her apple pencil.
W
She is an amazing lecturer. Go to her office hours she is very helpful.

She is great the switch from Nash to her in the middle of the semester was monumental. You can tell she really wants her students to succeed and really wants to help. She even implemented extra help session during the week. Very sweet and easy to understand.

Dr. Starr came in halfway through the semester, and I can already see the difference in my understanding of the material. She is so sweet and does a great job lecturing. She really puts an emphasis on doing examples during lectures and in her weekly help sessions, which is so helpful.

I wasn't even assgined to Dr. Starr's lectures but I started going to them and she is awesome! Super kind and makes sure everyone understands everything. Her office hours are also awesome as she is really good at explaining things one-on-one. She also has problem solving sessions once a week that are great for those extra practice problems.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Dr. Starr is amazing!! She was my lecturer for the second half of CHM115 and it's so evident that she's dedicated to her students learning the material! She gives lots of practice problems in class that mimic exam questions, answers questions really clearly, and is so down to earth and passionate! Definitely would recommend!!
Worst class I've ever taken
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: I have no idea how Purdue is still letting this woman teach. The textbook she wrote (and made us buy) is lacking essential information, and it will glitch on you. She can not create a coherent thought for anything, and her shoes look like they came from the discount bin at Ross.

I would rather relive my parent's divorce than deal with Lisa ever again. You have to pay for a textbook (that she wrote) that glitches and contains no information. Ever been to JCPenny's and seen the mannequins, that's her outfit inspo.
Absolutely disrespectful and Purdue should find another professor.

while this class really was not as hard as others make it out to be, it is a lot of work and you cannot just get by through memorizing. i've had lisa before and was pretty nervous for a second time, but honestly she was not terrible. she just forces you to go to completely useless active/in-person sessions that are at 7:30 every other friday.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: This class was frustrating, but not as bad as I had heard. Be prepared to lose points because you chose the wrong synonym for a word or gave an answer that was too specific. Pay attention to the exact wording of questions on tests. Keep up with studying throughout the week and make sure to get as many homework points as possible.

Not sure why everyone was complaining so much about this class tbh. I only had to go to 3 in person classes, never got above an 82 on a test, didn't put that much effort in and I'm pretty sure I'm ending with a B+. Just do your flashcards + if u have anatomy background ur good. Its easy to get 100% on MLL hw assignments, and 85-90% on LBM hw.
you need to study outside of textbook materials. Memorizing all terms in textbooks + knowing which root words for single term matches the specific suffix suck so bad!!!!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Took HSCI 131 at IvyTech to escape Hilliard, needed this anyways, 2 long lectures a week & your grade could be staked on any word. Disagrees with the textbook, labs are done on paper with no tech on things she said once. Takes off pts for "too specific" and questions are indecipherable. Constantly talks about her medical issues. People cry in lab.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This class is organized SO poorly. You only meet for lecture 3 times the entire semester. The MANDATORY "lectures" are basically Lisa repeating herself and rambling off-topic to fill time. You teach yourself the entire class because she's "hands-off" (doesn't do anything). Exams are extremely difficult for no reason. DON'T TAKE THIS AWFUL CLASS

She literally wants her students to not get an A. She says all the time that it doesn't matter the grade you get and that you don't need a 4.0 for med school. This class, which should be an easy/good class, is very much the opposite. Having us memorize over 500 terms and word parts for every exam is crazy.

If you listen to anything on rate my professor, PLEASE let it be this: DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS. For context, I am an all A student with a 3.95 GPA. Trust me. This course was horrible. I studied ~500 terms before each exam, with 3 exams total. This led to having to study ~1500 terms. No word banks: "you are the word bank". Take at community college!!
I never wore a seatbelt while driving to school because I wanted to die before making it to Lisa's class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Thought this class would be an easy A but Lisa makes the class harder then it needs to be. For a one credit intro class, she loves to grade extremely hard and will not specify why. With most assignments never getting 100% despite doing it exactly how she asks. I pray for med lab students who have to take multiple classes with her.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment:
A really really tough class, and not because immunology is a hard subject…
This course is extremely disorganized. The lecture slides are useless and she has a tendency to ramble on about adjacent or completely unrelated topics. She doesn't allow photos of her slides, so write quickly. Hard grader. Nearly impossible to get an A.
thinks she is Christ's gift to mankind.

Lisa Hilliard does not care about her students. She does not foster an environment to let you learn and is very specific in what she is looking for in terms of grading which makes it impossible to get points. Attendance is graded for CORRECTNESS as it covers the last lecture or even content from the lecture learned that day. DO NOT TAKE!
she probably thinks she is a doctor

Was not very willing to explain how improvement can be made. Very memorization-heavy, exams are extremely difficult (50/50 multiple choice and free response), and homework from her website is rambling on and does not get to the point she is attempting to make. I would suggest taking this class somewhere else, if possible.

Course super unorganized and harks that she is getting her students ready for the real world when none of it will be applicable. Grades so hard if not exactly what she wants. Have no idea how she grades, and schedule is very hard to follow and slides and brightspace is a mess.
made the class way more harder than it needed to be.

Class is very memorization-heavy. Not as hard as others may say as long as you review every day. However, she is terrible at answering questions and the "tips" she gives out are often inconsistent, which is made worse by harsh grading. Tends to ramble when writing when few words would do, which makes it very hard to understand her textbook.
Rambles on and will not shut up. Class could be done in 20 minutes if she kept her mouth shut.
Not a fan of the class style.
Made an easy class way harder than it needed to be.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: I brownnosed her so I got an A. That's not how a course should be run. It shouldn't be run on ego and as an excuse to make money off students (based on the new textbook she wrote that I hear about). Take it elsewhere, not just to save you the headache, but to deprive her of any monetary gain she doesn't deserve. Make TAs do something if you have 7.

WORST class/professor ever. Attended every class, put so much effort into assignments & got lowest grade so far at Purdue (perspective, got an A in Calc).  Submitted 9 page reflection for final grade, spent 8 HOURS on it because of all the F's my smart group was given on projects & got a 50- all for a 1 credit class exploring lab science careers.

For a 100 level class, Lisa makes the class very difficult with her "unique" style of testing where she makes up her own stories to incorporate medical terminology. Making students pay for her own website where she publishes assignments is also crazy, I would not have taken this class if my major did not require it. I HATE YOU LISA
Any good reviews on this page were made by Lisa, kinda sad on her part :(
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment:
If you make flashcards for medical terminology, you will be fine! Also, make sure to understand how words are put together to save you from memorizing hundreds of terms!

I am glad I took this class before anatomy.

Took 2 classes with her I accidentally called her Lisa at the start of my 1st class so that meant I couldn't get above a C. Getting a good grade in her class requires stroking her already MASSIVE ego, which I refused to do. She was going to be the program director for my senior year, but I changed majors to avoid dealing with Lisa, she is that bad.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Lisa Hilliard believes she is never wrong. Now that she has published her own med-term workbook she swears it is God's gift to education. She preaches to study, study, study, but it is almost impossible to understand her lecture tactics or follow her workbook. The MLL textbook is nothing like the LBM workbook. Never take this class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Group projects

reviews.comment: Had her for HSCI 131, the class wasn't bad at all. I was super excited to take HSCI 333, until I noticed how disorganized the class was. She can't understand how to use technology, and the assignments are always graded harshly. If it's not exactly what she wants, it's not 100% correct. Lectures aren't recorded and goes off on tangents A LOT!

BEWARE! This course is designed to make you fail, she doesn't have word banks for hundreds of terms. Last exam she made a question about her super-specific autoimmune condition because it always has to be about Lisa. She loves the power she holds over grades and isn't forgiving at all.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: We did not have any exams or quizzes, but labs were graded hard. Dr. Hilliard is very knowledgeable of anatomy and passionate about teaching clinical applications.
Very unorganized, grades things very hard without a rubric or good directions. Yells at you every time you have a question. Does not understand brightspace.

Her TA told our class we couldn't get a 100 on assignments. She literally has no idea how to use bright space. Assignments were worth the lowest amount of points possible. We compared her to Miss Frizzle because she looks like her. Good luck.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: whenever you ask a question she doesnt answer and just says look at the syllabus. She is super loud too, outside of class she is a nice person but I do not like her as a teacher. Very bad at answering questions and she always says she is open to emailing but never answers!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Drop any class you have with her while you still can. Awful at instructions, and doesn't tell you when things are due. She is very hard to talk to, selfish, and extremely condescending. Overall, she's one of those teachers who loves to rub it in that she has power over your grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She will grade your assignments one day before they are due before the semester ends so you will get no chance to go over with her. Will not round up even 0.5%. Never really responds to emails and will expect you to visit her during office hours (usually time waste). Is very strict and will expect a lot from an undergrad.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Honestly the worst professor by far! all over the place. Unclear instructions about the assignments. also took med term and anatomy with her, hated it because she doesnt give material outside of her lectures. Her slides have a few sentences on it and do not go in detail, will not record lectures. The assignments are graded SUPER hard!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Awful professor take any and all classes you may have with her at a community college seriously.

Professor Hilliard is the worst. She refuses to record lectures so if you miss one good luck. She also isn't clear on assignments and overall just seems like she tries to be vague when explaining assignments so people do bad. I think she gets a feeling of power by giving students bad grades since she has to work at Walmart to make ends meet.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Hilliard grades hard for no reason whatsoever. I received many bad grades for assignments that weren't clear and the final reflection project was a joke. Many students in 130 including myself received a zero for the final project because we were missing 1 paragraph, my paper was 10 pages long and I got a zero for missing 1 paragraph.

While Professor Hilliard is an intelligent woman, her approach to making students successful in this course is by making it "hands off." Each week, we were expected to memorize over 100 terms leading up to 400-500 terms before each exam. The exams were always difficult. No word banks were provided and the class average was always below 75%.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: The self study med terms class is great. We only met 3 times the whole semester, and while I was worried originally, it ended up working out perfectly. If you create a quizlet for each chapter, the test questions will be easy to recall.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Online Savvy--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: Professor Hilliard expects students to cumulatively memorize terms, up to around two thousand of them. She does not teach and she expects students to learn all the terms on their own. No curve, no word bank, no resources for us to be able to pass. She does not provide additional resources besides the textbook and she it not good with technology.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: HSCI 131 was probably one of my favorite classes, and I was able to learn so much from it. Most people who struggle with this class don't understand how to study for this class, and lash out since they didn't do well on the tests. Study every bold term and root word in the textbook, make flashcards, and MAKE SURE YOU CAN SPELL THE TERMS!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Test heavy

reviews.comment: I was excited for this class, but the lecturer made it impossible to enjoy. She was very unclear regarding grading criteria, didn't provide rubrics or relevant feedback, and changed the syllabus multiple times (including during dead week). She also did not hand back assignments at all, and didn't communicate well. Avoid this professor if you can.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Group projects

reviews.comment: The content isn't difficult, but the professor should be avoided if at all possible. She's really bad at communication and the assignments she writes are difficult to understand, even if you understand the content well. She runs this like a middle school class but wants to be respected like a college professor - it's pretty difficult to deal with.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Lisa is a difficult professor, in that she seems to be 'winging it' at all times, in terms of her own knowledge of the material and her grading policy. You won't have assignments returned to you, and you won't receive feedback. Lectures are barely comprehensible, and not recorded. Getting an A is purely an exercise in pleasing her ego. Good luck.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Group projects

reviews.comment: She rambles a lot, and the lectures are not recorded at all. The notes provide very little information, so you need to rely on her speaking. She often says "have you guys already learned this?" referring to things I doubt anybody has ever learned before. The quizzes aren't horrible. Office hours do not help much. She is very approachable though.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Group projects

reviews.comment: This professor has an odd teaching style and is a SUPER TOUGH grader. The quizzes are all over the place and so are the format of the notes. I wish the class was more organized its just all over the place. Good luck with it. The group projects and homeworks take a long time to complete and are not fun, but are kind of helpful way better than class.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Group projects

reviews.comment: Did not respond to my email- Would avoid this course and take at IVY Tech if you are not going into Nursing, takes more time to study/retain information compared to my 3-4 credit courses that meet +3 a week. Not always a fair grader.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: She's an awesome professor.

This class is not as hard as most people say it is. There is definitely a lot of memorization needed but if you review a few words each day, it is very manageable. I recommended making quizlets for each chapter. Lisa is very helpful and energetic. She's passionate and genuinely cares about her students!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: This is an independent study class, meet only for exams. By far one of my most favorite classes and have never learned more in a class! I went in for office hours every week which was really helpful! Read the chapters thoroughly and you have to study at least a week in advance. Quizlet is helpful and its great to study a little each every night.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: Lisa is wonderful! She is VERY talkative & gets distracted easily, but if you can get past that, she is a pretty good professor! She responds to emails quickly, and is always more than willing to help out as much as she can. In HSCI130, treat her "quizzes" as exams if you want to feel confident & do well.

No interaction w prof; a hybrid (self-taught) class. Only met during exams. Can't live w/o textbook. A rules stickler, doesn't make exceptions very often. Homework every week; work @ own pace. Quizlet is your BFF. Learn first 3 chapters VERY WELL, as it will eliminate 1/2 the work in later chapters. Straightforward grading, study HARD.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: Prof. Hilliard is great!  I really enjoyed her class.  She clearly states everything you need to do, and as long as you THOROUGHLY and CONSISTENTLY read the textbook and keep up with assignments, you will do fine.  The tests are doable and interesting (she writes them really creatively!) as long as you study well.  You will learn a lot!  :)

Prof. Hilliard is kind, helpful, and funny.  She emails back with thorough answers to any questions.  If you study the material in advance and daily, the tests are definitely doable.  Make flash cards and take them with you wherever you go; study bold terms.  I really liked the textbook that was used--very clear and easy to understand.
Not as bad as described but flash cards are not much use.  Tests are different but interesting.
Good class for medical terms.  I feel I learned a lot but it was not easy.  Tests are tough.

New instructor but pretty good.  Lost some classes to the weather and got behind but not really her fault. Tried to work with us with exams and quizzes. Might be harder if you don't have some Bio first. Case study project was interesting.

Learned a lot from this class. Now I feel more confident using some of the terms we learned. Tests are not easy but they were pretty fair. We had to be able to apply some of the terms, not just memorize lists.  Heard it used to be super easy but not so much now.  Be prepared. No lecture so communication was through email, but it worked out.

Friends told me this class was an easy A. I would not say so anynore. I did OK but really had to read the book and study. Exams are tough but I can definitley say I learned a lot. This class would be better with a lecture.
I liked this class. Case study project was cool. Having some Bio background helped.  No multiple choice but was tough but OK.  She was helpful if you went to office hours.

Self Study Med Term. This course covered a lot of material. Reading some of the comments on here, I wonder what people are actually expecting from college? The homeworks were pretty easy but the exams were challenging. I learned a lot from this class, since I had to study quite a bit to do well on exams. Good class if you really want to learn terms

Online class but you have to meet in person to take exams. Worst 100 level class I have ever taken. Professor does not help and always claims to be too busy. The exams are impossible. Even after doing the hour worth of online exercises, all the exercises in the book, and reading the chapters you still feel very unprepared.

The worst class I have ever had, the syllabus was not followed at all. This professor also is ill a lot(not her fault at all but it does become frustrating)and will spend more class time explaining why we don't have a lot of class time rather than teaching. She will not post completed slides so if you miss something during class you're out of luck

Hardest 100 level course I've ever taken. HSCI 131 should be a manageable intro course involving memorization of terms, but this class focused on physiology as well (aka systems). The only reason I did ok was because I have taken those upper level courses. She doesn't make many concessions for people who are struggling, hard to work with.
he's the goat
Take his classes if you get the chance

Absolutely loved having Steve as a professor! This was my favorite class all semester and despite it being early, I never wanted to skip. He deeply cares about his students, is passionate about what he teaches, & is hilarious! Loved the tangents, loved the goofiness, but still loved the serious parts. 10/10, I hope I have more classes with him.

By far the best professor I have had in my college career. He has much life experience to learn from, and always had a solid answer to any random question that came about in class. Lectures are thought provoking and he keeps the class engaged. He cares for each of his students, and gets to know everyone. I wish I could take this class again.

I LOVE Steve. He is one of the best professors I have ever had. He truly cares about the subjects he teaches and his students. He makes classes fun while ensuring that you learn. I wish he could be my professor for all of my classes and cannot wait to have him again next semester.

I love love love love love this professor he is the best! so caring about all of his students! Will make funny snarky remarks at you in a very kind way. super passionate about his subject and a genuinely natural teacher. Steve is smart as hell, brilliant, really, but one of the most humble people i've ever met.

This is one of the best classes I have taken at Purdue. The class is discussion based, so participating in the conversation is important. I think that I got the most out of this class because it was set up like that. Also, it is one of the easiest classes with no exams and one paper. It was the most enjoyable classes I have had at Purdue.
Steve is one of those guys that continues to surprise you with how smart he is. The fact that he also happens to be personable and entertaining makes the class totally worth it.

Dr. Hallett is an absolutely hilarious professor! He will swear and kid around and make you dance but do not take that for granted. The class is difficult and takes studying go in and talk to them after every exam though! I didn't and I regret it!

Very energetic, and even though his class was early, he is very engaging. The random attendance quizzes meant that you always had to be in class, but they weren't hard as long as you attend lecture. He frequently gets off topic, but is very entertaining and overall effective at getting the point across. Very British, and a great sense of humor.

He was a great prof. However, during his lectures, he liked to go off on tangents that really didn't involve the material we were covering. GO TO CLASS!! He has numerous pop quizzes that are extremely easy. Plus he has a gorgey accent ;-)
Awesome prof. I found his classes to be very interesting, he tried to find a real world application for all that we learned.
You will have weekly quizzes. That's fine but they will be super ambiguous, that's also fine. The problem is she won't give marks to other interpretations of the question and give 0 straight away.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Prof Pomeranz does not care whether or not you know the material. Her quizzes are ambiguous, impossible to prepare for, and unforgiving in the way she grades. If you do not answer the quiz questions the way that she wants, she will mark it wrong, regardless of if you are actually wrong or not.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: This class is not at all recommended. Every week I have the stress of exams, grades are tough, and nothing is helpful. Every ECE core class is bad except Prof. Hu's Lump System class. This is really bad class.

The professor is knowledgeable but grades heavily. Quizzes are like exams, with one per week covering multiple chapters and no partial credit. It's tough to succeed without fully dedicating yourself. Not recommended for those working full-time while pursuing a master's part-time due to the intense workload.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: No patience. Doesn't know how to teach. 40 students in the beginning. 30 something dropped by the end.

An amazing and intelligent person with numerous amounts of research,
but horrible in the classroom. We had 14 quizzes for the whole semester. most of the quizzes were 1 question most of the time very different from the lecture notes and sample questions.
I made a horrible mistake taking this class, don't make my mistake even if it's a 5 am class
reviews.rating_tags: Test heavy--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Unreasonable grading criteria.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The lectures are structured where the "tough" concepts are glazed over too quickly and the "easy" concepts are the focus. The quizzes regularly presented new, untaught concepts and were graded less on correctness and more on similarity to the professor's answer. I sincerely hope she reconsiders her teaching style so students can learn better.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This course only need three things: TA holding office hour, brightspace, Chatbot telling you to read brightspace again. Course is about reading notes taking quiz.
Quiz gradings are very binary!
Tough and tricky quizzes. Need to look at previous question papers to get good marks.

She screwed my overall GPA and is very strict about the deadlines. Her explanation is an endless inconclusive statement if you come up with doubt. Seems like she isn't confident about the subject and dodges the question with a more complex and vague explanation.
Professor Pomerania seemed to have made the course on the fly. It was so disorganized and her lectures unclear. Any questions you had she deflected/gave an insufficient explanation.

There were no assignments, and most parts of the quizzes are theoretical. You must hit the key to get 10/10 -- otherwise most likely the grade would be under 5/10. Have to listen carefully to the derivations, examples of the lectures and pseudocode, and make sure that you can reproduce them without the textbook and notes.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Graded by few things--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: Ridiculous exam style that resulted in most of the class failing the exams, because there was no preparation, and the exams were only 5 open ended questions. Anytime somebody had a genuine concern about the class should would either make excuses about there not being enough time, or would guilt trip us. Lectures and notes were also super dry.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Lectures - very long, teach material not covered by HW, labs, tests; has flat voice and makes lectures chore to listen to. HW covers material not in lecture and can be difficult, but much of it is google-able and not strictly graded. Tests - worst part, no practice material given. Overall, not too difficult but more than should be. Lab is more fun.
ECE 270 was the worse class ever. Pomeranz sucks at teaching, zero material is given for exam preparation, and the exams are impossible. She is the reason I hate engineering now.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Pomeranz seemed to know the material, but was awful at teaching it. Her lecture slides seemed to be made by a 6th grader and she often would not answer questions about the exams or homeworks in lectures. She often was unhelpful and borderline unprofessional on piazza when questions were asked. Harsh exam grades but fun labs.
