Her lecture was super boring. I barely fell asleep every time I watch her lecture. During her lecture, she ignores questions from students. She went through her lecture notes pretty fast. She does not care whether her students can catch up or not.

Professor Pomeranz tends to be very unresponsive and unhelpful inside and outside of class. You're better off getting a mermaid to teach you how to make fire than you are to get any kind of professionalism, communication or help.  Her lectures are read from the slides which are in themselves entirely unhelpful in working through homework.

Lectures are boring and monotonous. Notes are inconsistent and vary from organized and informative to vague. Doesn't seem interested in helping students. Doubts asked during lectures are not met with explanations, rather with answers like "Just read the example again". Exams do not have review sessions and no effort is made to give extra examples.

She was terrible and went through lecture super fast, even the TA are saying she went through lecture too fast. I understood the lecture but don't know how to do any homework questions, it's super tough. I don't understand why she went through material so fast and the homework is that hard.
She has well-organized lectures and she introduced some useful data structures and algorithms. We have 14 quizzes and her grading is a bit tough.

Everything was based on tiny closed-book quizzes every week that made up your entire grade, and the quizzes were difficult too. I believe I learned algorithms just fine on my own but apparently this class doesn't think I did.

Her grading is a bit harsh, she either gives full or near zero in the quizzes. Her lectures may not be the best, but if you don't understand something, you can ask her any doubts that you have and she will make sure that you get the attention you need. If you reach out to her, she will help.

A very friendly and caring professor. Her knowledge in the domain of Data Structures is unparalleled. She will spend time with you if you don't understand the concepts. The tests/quizzes are graded a bit harshly. Overall, solid choice.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: It is a very organized professor, I did not get an A but I learned and it was worth it. The best professor I have had in ECE.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Beware of pop quizzes--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Professor Pomeranz gives great notes with lots of examples that she does in class. Attendance is not mandatory at all. Homeworks are handwritten and from textbook questions. Some answers are on chegg but she mainly grades the hw for completeness and effort. Exam questions are from past papers. There is a possibility to skip the final exam as well.

I took 201 last semester and had to drop after the first exam. This semester with Pomeranz has been a completely different experience. She gives out note packets and is very good at explaining concepts fully and in a way that is easy to follow. Now I'm getting A's on my exams because they're straightforward and easy if you know the material.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Terrible.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Surprise (unpredictable) quizzes every week. Grading - 75% on (14+) quizzes, 25% on programming assignment (topic: dynamic programming but subject to change every semester. Some quizzes on proofs were the hardest with class average 3 on 10. Only previous 2 to 3 class lectures to be reviewed for the quizzes every week. NO EXAMS/HW/PROJECTS.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES

reviews.comment: 75% of grade was from pop-quizzes. Was a Tu./Thu. class, and quiz days were predictable given the material covered and analysis of previous year's quiz content. Quizzes require firm understanding of the material from last 2 classes.  25th-75th percentile get B's. Hardest quizzes were the proofs, but never designed to trick. Just have to think.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES

reviews.comment: Professor Pomeranz is clear about the marking scheme, and gives out printed notes. Weekly quizzes require you to be on top of the material covered in last two weeks. If you attend all the classes, the course is quite easy as there are no exams. There could have been more programming assignments though.

Her policy of not partial credit does not make much sense. Other than that she's pretty decent. Assigns HW once a week, unlike other professors of 201. Her exams are the most straightforward, easy exams, that you can finish in one third of the alloted time, and have much time left to go over and correct math mistakes. I recommend her.

She is a bit on the rude side in class and definitely doesn't like repeating herself when it comes to rules, like getting up during class. But in all honesty her tests are some of the most straight forward and easy ive ever taken. Her class may seem intimidating right off the bat, but put in the effort and go talk to her and you will be fine.
No partical credit.Only four questions in the exam. If you make any error in the calculation,you will not get an A.
luckly  she don't take attendance

I found her class to be quite interesting (granted i had taken a class that helped me understand pointers prior to this). The exams were quite easy/basic, and if you put the time into the homeworks, it gives you a very good understanding of the class. She gave out all programming examples some of which were hard to understand, but the T

She tries to teach C programming from an overhead.  She doesn't explain or actually teach.  It's just the same stuff, droning on and on every class.  You're better off taking somebody else.  The homework is terrible, she comments nothing, there are no standards, the exams...a joke.  A hard joke at that.  DO NOT TAKE HER.
Hard to understand.  Tried to teach programming on an overhead.  Worst ECE professor I've had.
terrible
THE WORST!

This class is currently making me want to change my major. It is SO confusing what they want us to know and for the homeworks, there are 10 different TAs grading so some people fail and other people get an 100 for doing the same thing. Its basically a flipped classroom because you have to learn everything yourself and do "activities" in class.

The slowest and most boring professor you will ever take. He talks so much and yet nothing he says makes any sense. It's a waste of time coming to class, but you still have to because he now grades based on attendance.
Lecture Slides and Exams were identical. Every detail on slide were on exams; study the slides to great depth, using his lectures. His lectures are verbatim on the exams.

Ended the class with a 99.2%. As long as you are a dedicated student, this class is extremely straightforward and an easy A. While Camarillo isn't a great lecturer, I'd say it's still important to watch his lectures to gauge how well you need to know the material. Take detailed notes, write a LOT for the homework FRQs, and start studying early!

Don't bother showing up to lectures. They are extremely boring. The best way to do well in this class is to watch the lectures they post, put them on 2x speed, and take notes. The TAs are horrible and write horrible tests, with some questions having no correct answer. There are about 15 TAs, but it will take a month to get your scantron test back.
Worst professor I've had so far by a long shot.

Actually so bad can't believe he has a job... Get ready to RUMBLE your way to a failing grade with no fault of your own. Worked hours upon hours to receive a B in this class and was forced to take a dip in others. I went to every lecture even when I came down with the flu in order to pass this class only to find myself teaching him basic technology

This professor might have been the absolute worst teacher I've ever had in my entire career as a student. He was extremely monotone and had 0 passion for this class. He showed complex diagrams and failed to explain them, and would provide a summary slide but would instantly click away. I've also never had to wait so long for a test grade to return
Genuinely so bad. One of the worst professors. Super nice guy but actually cannot with this class.

Camarillo is funny and a sweet guy but the lectures feel like a waste of time. Also:

- Exam grading takes eons (scantron??)
- Unprepared TAs
- Weekly homework graded harshly
- Entire class just feels disorganized

On the bright side, the exams and quizzes aren't too hard if you watch the lectures online or read. The study guide is very clear.

He teaches very slowly and the concepts are not explained thoroughly. The TAs are very underprepared. The lectures are a waste of time, the homework is graded harshly and the homework can tank your grade. There was extra credit but it was harshly graded and not worth a lot. The study guides just have wrong answers and there is no curve to the class

Dr. Camarillo is a nice guy, but his lectures aren't very helpful. I really disliked the grading system as I ended up with an 89.5, which counts as a B (not even a B+). He gave us extra credit which was nice, but I don't think it's fair to assign the same grade to people who averaged 90s on exams to people who averaged 75s.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment:
This guy...... 
He mumbles, stumbles, and fumbles through every lecture. 
His study guides. 
Are full of deceit and lies. 
His explanations.
Deserve damnation.
We miss Minchella.
We don't like this fella.
He's not funny. 
He thinks he's punny. 
He talks about breasts. 
I just want to rest.

No plus grades or curves, but some extra credit they begrudgingly offered. TAs are unfriendly and create bad practice problems with ChatGPT that didn't have any correct answer choices. My least favorite course I have ever taken purely because of the bland lectures, TAs, and instructor. Nice enough guy but awful as a lecturer.

He tells you to just study the content on study guides and you will do great on the exams, but then half of the exam isn't from the guide. One exam took a month to get graded (a scantron exam), and you have to request a regrade on homework often because the TA's can't grade. Exam seating comes out way too late and got messed up during the final. :(

I didn't go to lectures and instead taught myself everything. His lectures are incredibly boring and while the content itself could be interesting, the way he teaches it is not. Before every exam, he spent time trying to get us to chant "let's get ready to rumble." I have never wanted to rumble just give me the exam. The TA's are useless as well.

Terrible lecturer, slides were awful and barely explained the topics. Difficult exams, and way too picky on the HW grading. Fight for your points. Unfortunately this class is required, and I don't think anyone else teaches it.

If you ever need to learn why good public speaking is so important, take this class. Lectures are unclear, and he goes on tangents so you don't know what is important. TAs seem lost in review sessions, and might give wrong information. Beware practice exam errors. Every bio student needs to take this class, so buckle up and GET READY TO RUMBLE!

The most disorganized class I have ever taken. Camarillo has no passion and just reads off slides. There is no point in going to lecture because he rambles about useless stuff for most of it and glosses over what the exams actually ask about for maybe 5 minutes. There are too many TAs for this class to post the seating chart minutes before an exam.

He delivers information/explains processes during lecture. The course is difficult, it is hard to understand the content purely from lectures. It takes lots of personal study time. Exams are challenging, but fairly sourced from the lecture slides. At times directions can be confusing, however he is quick to correct. Creates fun class environment.

I went to almost every lecture and stayed for the whole time. The material would have been better if he would have been more enthusiastic about what's he's teaching instead of hump day. The TAs never came to class but made the exams and a lot of the questions were just flat wrong. Do not take this class unless you have to. Horrible teacher!!

This guy is the worst professor I have ever had or will probably ever have. He takes like 4 seconds to explain things that are important and "will be on the exam", but takes 30 minutes to explain things that are irrelevant and just filler information. It sucks that he's the only professor for this class so you're stuck with him no matter what.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Dr. Camarillo is a very nice person, but his class is unorganized and it often takes a long time to get grades back. He truly does care, but his lectures are not very well put together. There are too many TAs for this class to be so disorganized. His lecture videos he posts online are great! lectures are just hard to sit through.

Somehow manages to take easy concepts and makes them impossible. Worst experience I've ever had being a student by a landslide. Doesn't annouce when he's absent 30 minutes after class has started. Lectures are a waste of time and you're way better off learning it on your own time. This is what shows up when you search worthless in a dictionary.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Some people simply do not have the spark for teaching. Lectures are monotone as he reads and rambles off the slides. The class was also extremely disorganized. Seating charts went out the morning of the exam (in some cases 15 minutes before). Luckily the concepts taught are simple so it isn't hard to teach yourself.

I cannot with this guy.don't go to class if you don't have to bc this guy is a snoozefest and can't teach to save his life.everyone leaves 2 seconds after turning in the weekly quiz.i don't even know how they take so long to score exams and quizzes?they're on scantrons its not that hard? he reads off the slides which are terrible actual insanity

Most of the people on here giving him bad ratings are the people that leave in the middle of his lecture, talk over him and, and scroll on their phones. If you go to class and actually pay attention you will pass this class so easily, and learn really cool concepts.

The lectures are boring because he rambles for ages, the slides are awful, and the tests are riddled with typos. The textbook will be your best friend in this class, because it is the only thing that makes the material make any sense. Also, make sure to save up for a new computer just in case you send your cranium through the first one.

Course is incredibly disorganized. Tests, practice exams, and quizzes are riddled with mistakes. Often reads off of his slides, does not give the impression he knows the content well enough. Slides get progressively worse. Exam seating has come out as late as 10 minutes before the exam.
I was NOT ready to rumble.

This course made me change my major from bio to engineering because I hated it so bad. This was the first B I received since elementary school cuz of my sheer lack of motivation to listen to dr C/TAs, but the work is manageable. 
Memorize all the terms/outcomes of processes w/o understanding the meaning behind any of it, you will do just fine.

I had to remind myself that Dr. C has earned a PhD. Does not explain concepts well, wastes 5 mins on his computer each class looking for the ppt, exam questions are sometimes incorrect and removed from the exam after we take it. TAs post scores late and are always confused. 
At least he tries hard to be funny in class, but it never made me laugh.

I think the majority of the disorganization from this class stems from the TA's. Camarillo clearly enjoys the content he teaches, and the slides he provides always have the information needed for the exams. There are weekly quizzes and homework, and the homework is generally great prep for the quiz. This course requires real effort, so be prepared!

Dr. Camarillo loves Hump Day and that's just about the only good thing about his class. The course claims to be dependent on the study guides but that is not true for exams. The course is so sloppily organized that you better have a group of friends to help each other through the class with the poorly written slides. Friday has the masses leaving.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: Terrible class, and terrible prof. Lies about what's on he exams, gives us study guides that literally aren't accurate, and the most boring class of all time. Bad lecturer, unaccommodating, all in all arguably worst professor I've had this year.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Lectures were terrible and frankly embarrasing. The slides were worse after each exam. I essentially memorized everything since he didn't teach a single thing. Makes a biology degree from Purdue worthless. If I were an employer, I would never hire a bio student from Purdue.

A lot of people dislike his lectures, and while they can be repetitive and jumbled at times, the information is very valuable. You can tell he truly enjoys what he teaches. He also gives study guides that give the exact information that will be on exams. The main issue, I found, with the class is the way homework is graded.

Honestly an alright class if you took AP bio.  Yes, he only reads off the slides and it is as if he's learning it with the class, but it's a bio course. Just memorize all the buzz words and you'll be fine.  The exams are all multiple choice and none of them are cumulative, even the final.  A lot of memorization, but pretty straightforward class.
Decent seeming guy, but awful at conveying the information in the course. Reads everything off the slides and comes off as not knowledgeable about the material.

wow...just wow. camarillo's lectures are incoherent mumble-fests if he's even there. slides are filled with so much information and a lotta diagrams, and you never know what you'll need to know for the exams. he throws in so much extra/unneeded info that note taking is really annoying.

Worst professor and course at Purdue. He didn't even know what he was saying during lecture (which btw don't bother to show up, you'll end up failing either way). TA's tho the worst part of this class since they created the quizzes and exams which never related back to lecture and were unreasonably difficult. AVOID THIS CLASS IF AT ALL POSSIBLE
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: A lot of people dislike Dr. Camarillo because of the disorganization of the course or the exam difficulty, but he is a good professor. He tries his best and enjoys what he teaches, the exams are not written by him. If you attend/watch lectures and make flashcards you will do good. He is also very approachable and will answer any questions you have.

AWFUL. He's so unorganized and reads off of slides, scantron midterms are graded slower than handwritten ones for CHM. Midterm seating charts are sent out from 12-30 minutes before the exams, and he moved a quiz up to Wednesday so we could take Friday off for spring break, then rescheduled the lecture on that Friday an hour before it started.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: He mumbles during lectures and reads off the slides. Going to his lectures is so useless because you will genuinely fall asleep. Better off reading the slides on your own. Grades the tests super slow. The Chemistry exam that is HANDWRITTEN is graded faster than the scantron BIO131 exam... WHAT COULD HE POSSIBLY BE DOING?????
Do not go to class. Lectures are disorganized and you are better off memorizing the slides at home.

Dr. Camarillo is an awful lecturer. He reads directly off the slides adds big words to make it sound like he knows the material, but he really doesn't. He is extremely unorganized. We went from Chapter 7 to 6 to 34 to 35 and now are on 42. His lectures are all over the place and he does not help if you are struggling.

Doesn't know his content before class and just reads off of the lecture slides. He emphasizes that the study guide is going to have everything on the exam, and then expects you to know things he never went over. Every practice exam has at least one question that is wrong on them and he doesn't bother fixing it.

only had a few classes with him and whilst he's super sweet, HE RAMBLES SO MUCH and goes off topic very easily. He'll start reading something off the slide then just go on a tangent and no one has any clue what he's saying. avoid him if possible
Dhdhcjcjjcjcnc

The class was very disorganized in general, his grading strategies were not clear, TAs weren't ever prepared for questions regarding homeworks or quizzes. You're better off not going to lecture and watching the videos at home. I will say the material itself is not difficult if you memorize the presentations and practice with past exams.

Bring a pillow to his class so when you inevitably lose consciousness your head won't slam into your desk. And bring a pillow for your pillow because your pillow will fall asleep too. 



Nice guy tho but his exams have too many ambiguous questions that are worded in the most trick question way possible.

The class was completely disorganized, the worst part of the class was the TAs themselves. They weren't always prepared for content questions or syllabus questions apparently. Also whoever wrote the exams/hws ALWAYS made typos, it was infuriating. I did think the content was pretty straight forward if you studied all details of the lecture slides.
taking this class made me genuinely question if I should switch majors. if you have the choice DO NOT TAKE!!! you're better off not going to lectures in person and watching recordings in 2.5x speed.

Everything that Minchella did great in BIOL 121, Camarillo does horribly in BIOL 131. Lectures are boring, he doesn't explain things well, and the exams have very poorly worded questions. The only thing that made me rate him a 2 and not a 1 was that he gave a 20pt extra credit assignment at the end of the semester to save everyone's grades.
A very very disorganized class and TA's could not answer simple questions about the course and they weren't even sure about the total grade in class
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes
Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Dr. Camarillo seems very sweet, but his lectures are easier to watch online in 2x speed than in person. Quizzes and exams aren't bad, just be prepared to memorize the slides. Lots of mistakes in assignments/exams/quizzes, but he throws out the bad questions. Make sure to also take notes on what he says, not just what is on the slides.

Dr. Camarillo is nice, but the class is very unorganized with slow lectures where he mostly reads off the slides. Syllabus is often inaccurate and course policy is vague. 

Homework + quizzes are given every week with unnecessarily picky grading on homework. Tests are fairly straightforward using the study guide. 

Avoid this class if you can
SO BAD!!!!! He rambles and jokes through lectures then tests on the most minor of details. Not accessible outside of class. Do not take this professor if you don't have to.

Very nice, but the worst professor I've had in my time in undergrad. His slides have little to no words and lecture consists of him stumbling over his words trying to explain them. He literally has to supply supplemental resources to go over the basic topics covered in class because he can't properly explain them himself. Avoid if you can.

There are quizzes every week, and the quizzes and midterms are all based on obscure information found in the lecture. He tends to ramble a lot during class. Grades are very slow to be put in, one HW assignment was put into the grade book the day of the final exam.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: The worst teacher I have ever had in my life. Doesn't care about the student, shows up late, reads off the slides with no extra information provided. There is no extra instruction from the professor and he is very hard to reach outside of class. IF YOU CAN AVOID IT, DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS.

reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: A very very unorganized class
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Camarillo is a funny little guy. Unfortunately, bad dad jokes only get you so far in life. He will not answer your emails; TA's are unknowledgeable. Why take melatonin when you could listen to his lectures. Even in 2x speed this man somehow talks in .5x. Homework's are worth too much of your grade and even then, he refuses to give 100% credit.

This course will make you rethink your decision of majoring in the life sciences. This course is required for Biology and many other majors. Camarillo has a unique way of lecturing where he can talk in .5x speed so it actually is more time efficient to watch his lectures on 2x speed online instead of going to class. Tough it out
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: He is such a sweet and funny guy but he's not the greatest lecturer. He tends to ramble on a lot and I found it hard to focus in the class. They were very picky about some of the open ended homework questions because  they were always looking for a super specific answer. There is a quiz just about every Friday, but those tend to be somewhat easy.

Camarillo is so sweet, but he rambles a lot and it is really hard to follow what is important material and what is useless. The quizzes and homework are extremely easy if you take notes, but the exams just kinda take the seemingly irrelevant parts of the class to test. If you put in time to the course you'll be fine.

Dr Camarillo is a very sweet professor and I feel bad giving him a 2. He has a tendency to ramble during lectures and it makes them very hard to follow. For every exam they really stressed to use the study guide, but a lot of the exam questions were details that you wouldn't be likely to put down when you fill out the study guide.

I'm not going to give him 1 star because he is super kind and understanding. But it feels like the class is in slow motion and you end up falling sleep within 5 minutes no matter how much caffeine you drink. His exams/quizzes are oddly specific and often unorganized. Study VERY SPECIFIC DETAILS about EVERYTHING he says or you will not do well.

There is a reason he is already in hot water with the university. While he has an amicable and docile personality, he is extremely poor in communicating, is known to ignore emails, only has zoom office hours which inhibits the flow of discussion at any other professor's office hours and highlighting how little he cares about students' success.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I cannot believe this professor is still teaching at this university. Many answers on his past exams and even on slides is blatantly incorrect information and the professor is incompetent in effectively communicating ideas, what information is the most important, and in answering questions.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Caring professor, but very disorganized. Lots of errors on exams and quizzes, and TA team is not very knowledgeable when it comes to answering questions.  He is not very accessible outside of class. Material itself is not innately difficult to learn as long as you watch the posted online lectures. Extra credit and a class curve were given.

The pop quizzes arent pop, but they are every Friday. Camarillo is a friendly and funny person, but the tests are harder than the lectures. A lot of the lecture slides don't have words so it's hard to catch everything.

Horrid class. The TAs could not answer questions, answers on the practice exams were wrong, and homework was graded harshly. There was an instance where I got a homework question right, but I got 2 points taken off because it was not in a format the grader liked. Don't take this class if you don't have to.

Quiz and test questions are not based on the lectures, and almost feel like trick questions with the goal of you failing. The course is organized extremely poorly and they are constantly making grading mistakes. They even messed up the printing on one of the exams  and we only got half the questions. They did not give a makeup exam or a curve.

This class was a mess. Prof. Camarillo is a nice guy, but he is the most inarticulate person I've ever listened to speak. The TAs were worthless, most of the class didn't even go to lectures they were so bad. The exams are relatively easy, its not hard to get an A in this class, but I wish it was taught better.

He literally told us they changed the class because too many people thought it was easy. The content wasn't harder, but they made it harder to get an A because of the complete lack of organization. I started doing better in class when I stopped attending lectures. They completely ignored our concerns and made us feel stupid for having questions.

The most unorganized class I've ever taken. When I had a question about an exam question and asked a TA they didn't even know what was going on. Quizzes are weirdly specific and homework is graded harshly with no helpful feedback. Class would be randomly cancelled at times and they completely ignored the concerns of everyone taking the class.

By far the worst class I have taken in my entire college career. The content could be incredibly interesting and well-understood if a competent professor was teaching the course, but unfortunately that wasn't the case here. Exams and quizzes lacked organization, extremely strict grade thresholds didn't help either. Students were always stressed.

Professor is not organized at all and doesn't care what his students have to say. Graded so harshly it doesn't make sense. Exams have errors on their end which is not fixed or given points back. Attendance is mandatory for Friday quizzes, even if youre sick or bedridden. I reached out with concerns and was told to suck it up or drop the class.
I liked this class overall but the majority of the grade is based on exams.

If you are not required to take this class, don't. The content that is taught isn't all that will be on the exams. Exams and quizzes tend to have numerous errors each time. Many people in the class have brought their concerns just to not be listened to. There is no curve despite majority of the class failing/being close to failing.

Class is ridiculously difficult for no reason. I got A's on exams, but I barely managed to keep a B- average for his quizzes. Quizzes are weirdly specific and lecture heavy. Everyone doesn't like the class and aren't doing well. Anytime students express concern the professor brushes it off because "some people are doing better than they predicted."

only take if required!! attendance not required, but YOU NEED to watch the lectures at home. Friday's are quiz days, the quizzes will either kill you or be a breeze. textbook is unnecessary, later in the semester the slides go from filled with info to just diagrams. lectures are jam packed with info, it takes 3 hours to write notes for one topic.

This professor does is to some degree able to explain the material. However, the way he chose to structure his course was with the intentions of lowering the class average. He nor the TAs listened to anything the students expressed concerns about throughout the entire semester. Class was often such a disaster to where we were literally sent home.

This class was an absolute nightmare. You are told not to use the quizzes or homework to help you study. There is such an overflow of material and a lack of organization making you feel like you have to teach yourself everything. Along with this, you never know how tough the quizzes will be. The slides also change from being useful to just diagrams

This is the most unorganized class I have ever taken. The material itself isn't hard, but this class definitely needs to be run better. Bad exams with many errors (some of which aren't corrected) and homework that really overreaches the scope of what's taught in class make it incredibly frustrating. Not to mention the random lecture cancellations.

This is truly the worst class I have ever taken. The class is insanely disorganized and ridiculously unfair. The same complains have been made since 2019 and yet there has been no change in the way the professor teaches. The only good rating are from when the class was online due to COVID. Attendance is mandatory for in person online quizzes.

This man's weekly quizzes and his exams both had averages in the 60%. Which he decided was a 'good grade to have'. Every head injury I got from high school sports can't even begin to compare to the brain damage I got from slamming my forehead through my desk trying to understand this class.

really disorganized. undergraduate TA,s do all thr work and he has no idea what's going on. No one stays in class past the first 10 minutes of quiz. Exams have the type of typos that a single proof read could fix. Nightmare class
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: This class was pretty disorganized at first, but they got it together towards the end of the semester. I found homework and quizzes pretty easy, and his exams are also not bad if you are good with memorization. He is nice, but he doesn't write any of the assignments, quizzes, exams, or runs the review sessions so there is a big disconnect.

I personally do not feel as though Ignacio is a terrible person, however his teaching style and the way he formats his lectures are not helpful for exams or homework. Every exam has had typos, the low grade point average in the course is not a cause of concern, and he seems to refuse to respond to emails in a timely fashion.

This class unfortunately is much more poorly set up than that of bio121. The lectures are hard to follow, very boring, and almost useless. The textbook is not regularly encouraged, but can be a cheat code to the course. Camarillo cares about his students well-being, but not necessarily about their grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This class is super interesting and Dr. Camarillo works very hard to encourage student success. Honestly one of the most sincere profs I've had at Purdue. Strongly recommend this class it is an easy A since he and the TAs are extremely invested in your success.
Professor Ignacio has one of the most organized class I have ever seen. He is extremely professional and his class is a must take.
just don't take this class and live a happy life. lectures cure insomnia, and recorded lectures average to well over an hour long per video

horrible class. worst i've every taken at purdue. Most premed students in this class are rethinking their futures. Will look terrible on your transcript. Not because its difficult- it is the technical issues (poor grading, cancelled class, typos)  that have made the class average a C-
They also couldn't care less.

Worst class I have ever taken in my life. so badly organized. Every single exam had multiple typos. questions and MCQ's wouldn't match, question repeats, bad grammar.

Its evident nothing was proofread. lecture slides were only pictures. class got cancelled 70% of the semester.

The class average was a low C and they didn't curve the class. awful

This entire course has me completely reconsider whether I should be continuing bio at Purdue. Completely useless lectures, cannot answer students questions, cancels classes while students are in the room, unable to review past quizzes and tests, unorganized class, worst class I've ever taken, hw not graded correctly and cannot find answers.
Worst professor I have had so far, shows up late to class and lecture slides are poorly put together. The quizzes and exams are also often full of errors on grammar and content.

This was probably the most difficult class I have taken so far. This class is an absolute mess and the teaching staff was unwilling to work with students in order to help make it more bearable. The homework is graded on correctness for some reason and did not help boost most people's grade (as homework is normally intended). Pray for a curve.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Where to start..  this class was a mess. Camarillo does not write the homework's or the tests and he seems pretty oblivious to what is going on in the class. His lectures are sub par and honestly painful to watch. the TA's control everything and they are undergraduates. Be prepared for tests filled with errors and no curves. Good luck :).

This course was rough. The BIOL131 team told us they are trying to make the course "adaptive" since last semester was online, but it was just hard. If you can't go beyond what they teach you in lecture and expand upon it yourself, you will struggle. HWs have a different grading criteria every time, it is impossible to get 100% on every homework.

Dr. Camarillo I'm sure is a great guy. His class is the most disorganized class I have ever taken and a very high majority of my peers have and continue to have issues. We collectively have reached out to him and the TA's and nobody is helpful or willing to accommodate students in order to help them succeed. The tests and quizzes are ridiculous.
He doesn't care. He's just going with the flow

Dr. Camarillo seems to be a great person who cares about his students. However, his class was so painstakingly disorganized and boring that it was very difficult to learn. This class was plagued by technical difficulties, lectures posted late, and 5 hours a week of lectures. Great guy, bad class.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Camarillo is a great guy. His lectures are incredibly boring, however. I watched them on 2x speed and still it was too slow. Brightspace and the TAs in this class were horrid and so bad at helping him run the class. Homeworks and quizzes were nearly straight from the lectures, and exams were a repeat of quiz/hw questions and open note. Easy class

If you want an A: go to every class, take good notes if you can, and go the weekly TA review hours. Getting a B is possible by watching lectures on boiler cast before exams and attending classes even without taking notes, but it's stressful. He's a nice guy, very accomodating and doing his best with boring material.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Graded by few things--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Unlike other Bio prof's Camarillo genuinely wants you to succeed. He adapts the course each year. Content for Exam 1 is a lot easier than the content for the rest of the semester, don't let yourself get complacent. While his lectures can be a bit boring all the info you need is there. Go to the lecture. Review for EVERY QUIZ!! Do practice exams.

I feel others reviews do a good job at explaining this class. Definitely get the book. I found the book clear up a lot of stuff from class. A lot is from the powerpoint word for word. He does talk slow though. He a really nice person to talk to do. There were often mistakes on quizzes and exams. Do the practice exams! He does not curve.

The quizzes and tests is what makes or breaks your grade. They are worded to be tricky so make sure to read carefully. Lectures were important for the exams. Lots of things were word for word from the lectures. The book was nice to study with the first half of semester but not super important if you take good notes.

Nice guy, but the class can be frustrating. Quizzes and tests range from general and easy to overly specific and difficult, making it hard to prepare. There were frequent grading mistakes on simple multiple choice questions due to vague wording or just a lack of proofreading. Second half of the class was just him projecting pictures and talking.

This professor is a true gem! Most biology classes are unreasonably hard, but Camarillo makes his class both informative and manageable. He provides you everything you need to pass and to learn the concepts well.

It's obvious he likes to teach, but his class is extremely boring. He speaks slowly, so it's hard to stay engaged in the material, and the lecture slides usually just have diagrams and a lot of repeated information. Tests are pretty easy if you've watched/gone to the lectures. Frequent homework assignments, but don't take tool long to complete.
I think he explains things very well. The people who arent doing well are the people who arent paying attention/studying.

He's overall very unorganized. We didn't cover about 15-20% of the material we were supposed to cover in the class. He did not stick to the syllabus in the slightest. His lectures were extremely boring bc you couldn't really takes notes. He would often just put a diagram on the screen and barely explain it. Dont take this class unless its required.

Worst professor Ive ever had. His lectures are monotonous and impossible to focus on. Attendance is not mandatory but there are weekly quizzes on Friday. Class is super disorganized and poorly structured. The lecture slides are boring and next to useless. If you dont have to, dont take the class, if you do, buy the book and read everything.

Dr Camarillo is the worst professor I've ever had at Purdue. Despite his passion and knowledge for the subject (and friendliness) his lectures were dry and included an overwhelming amount of material that was not in the exam. Our final exam seating was posted 10 minutes before the exam was meant to start. Very disorganized

Dr. Camarillo is a way better prof than Dr. Lanman. His topics are more interesting and are easier to understand. Although he does get a bit off track and confused, he knows how to get his point across (even if it takes a few lectures to do so). His exams are usually repeats of past years. Go to class for his funny jokes and for the iClicker points

Camarillo is super passionate about biology and it shows. He explains things in a manner that first year students will understand. He can be scattered at times, but he does make an effort to make sure everyone understands the material. The only negative is that he has no clue how the grading scale works, so point distribution got confusing.

He is not the best professor, but he isn't horrible.  He has very little understanding of the grading technology we use and anything with technology, so keep all your assignments and a log of points earned so you can fight for your grade online if needed. If you go to class, he actually explains everything that will be tested on.  Not a bad class.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Camarillo is a very sweet man. Unfortunately, he is an incredibly boring lecturer. He simply fails in engaging his students. The material on exams seems random and you can never predict what it's going to be. I would avoid this professor if you can.
Professor is boring in lecture. Exams are very lengthy and require attention to many details on the material in lecture. Professor has some great dad jokes though.

Great professor who is funny and genuinely excited about teaching phys. I don't really understand the negative reviews because his class is straightforward and lowkey so easy. Being mad that a professor goes off topic for the excitement of physics is wild, he still covers all of the content with time to spare. Class is so easy its almost offensive

Dr. Arnold is the most caring and hilarious professor, but his course is difficult (as expected with physics). There is quite a bit of online homework, written homework, quizzes, and lab reports, but exams make up most of the grade. Lectures are not super helpful, as he tends to get off track easily.

I enjoyed lectures as he was very friendly and he answers questions thoroughly. However the lectures felt like they were a bit all over the place. He was nice and knowledgeable but the class was hard to study for
Gives great lectures, answers all my questions.

I'm not sure why Dr. Arnold gets a bad rep. He is one of the smartest professors I've had so far. Physics is naturally difficult. But if you go to lecture, do the mini readings, practice exams and go to office hours you'll do good. The tests are not weighted heavily. And he is super helpful if you go directly to him with questions.

Some of those exams were definitely unfair. Labs never lined up with what we were covering in lecture and there was unclear expectations. I emailed him a question once and then he insulted the question in lecture the next day and berated me for 15 or so minutes in front of the class. That was unredeemable to me. Don't take unless you have to.

Passionate and good and speaking, but waits until the last 10-15 minutes of each lecture to go over what we actually need to learn for exams. Labs are unorganized and often have nothing to do with lecture content. Grading and exams are generous and straightforward which is the only saving grace.

Overall, this class needs a lot of work. It is super unorganized, and although exams are relatively straightforward, attending lectures and doing homework do not help at all. Attendance is only required in labs and recitation, but they were such a waste of my time since they were completely disconnected from both each other and from the lectures.

For what this class is, Professor Arnold would be the person to take this class with. He's funny and he seems to genuinely care about the content of this class. Whomever you take this class under, the class is lecture heavy, and you'll have to parse through which material you should know for the exam and which material is more there for context.

Class wasn't hard, but it was disorganized. This was not a great experience for my first ever physics class. He went off topic a bunch, and honestly you were better off skipping lecture and teaching yourself. The class wasn't super exam heavy, and there was either a small curve at the end, or they rounded the grade. Either way, I'm not complaining.

Professor Arnold is very engaging, but the lectures tended to run a little long and supplemental videos were a must. The class in general wasn't easy and covered a lot of content, but Professor Arnold did a good job of explaining the concepts at a level that could be applied.

Labs and recitations are easy—just show up for full credit. Lectures often ran late, never finished covering the content, yet still had time for iClicker questions. Homework was disorganized and took forever. The professor rarely did examples in class and overall, the course felt unstructured and very frustrating.

He was fun to listen to in lecture, but I do have a few small complaints. He acts like everyone has had a basic physics course before and doesn't really teach us the equations (I'd recommend YouTube tbh). He can be a little squirrel-brained. Which I found funny, but some may not like it. He was good overall, but struggled to stay on task often
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Hilarious

reviews.comment: Rarely finished a lecture, so you're forced to watch his videos online. Gets off topic quickly and rambles about nonsense. NEVER does examples. Overall I felt like the didn't really get much from lecture and had to do a TON of work outside class to compensate.

Can get very off topic very fast in lectures. Talks super fast. Lots of homework, however it makes the exams worth less. Exams are not super difficult, however if you do bad on the exams your grade won't suffer much.

I loved him a lot more in 234 than 233 but I never didn't like him. He cares about his students and would hold long office hours before exams to study. He even explained a concept right before our final to a student in front of the whole class. Great guy for a class no one wants to take as a bio major. If you have to take it- he's your guy! 10/10

I really enjoyed his lectures and most concepts are explained really well. He also knows a lot about random science which is nice.

The only issue would be the fact that he often takes too long on lectures, but this makes them much easier to follow. However, this meant we sometimes had to watch an extra lecture at home to make up for it.

Ian is a great person, making him a great lecturer. He loves the subject and he loves students. He will often go off on goofy tangents during class but this eases the tension from learning the difficult subject. This was my 1st physics class, use orgo chm tutor on youtube to suplement you learning, study lecture content, youll be good.

Very good guy, very real. Physics for life sciences is never something us bio majors look forward to, but Dr. Arnold recognizes that and tries to make it a bit more digestible for those of us who don't like that physics is a university requirement. Exams are OK, lots of conceptualization, less memorization that I wasn't used to. Overall good class!
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: ended up just watching lectures online because he talks SO fast. tons of points available so there's some wiggle room with exam grades (which is good because they are worded very weirdly). nice guy, but not the best lecturer if this is your first time taking physics.

Wouldn't have taken a class with him again if I had a choice. Exams are all multiple choice because he can't figure out how to grade free response questions efficiently. Because the exams are so multiple choice heavy, each question is worth like 5% of the exam, making it hard to do well. Lab is useless but lab TAs are chill and grade easily.

Dr. Arnold made physics a very pleasant experience, I had him for both 233 and 234. I highly recommend this professor, he is very lenient with exam grading and truly cares that the students are learning. The class itself is not as much work as I was expecting it to be and an A is a really reasonable goal if you attend lectures and recitations.

Dr. Arnold is a good guy but his class was just bad. Lecture material was poorly explained. Recitations and labs were almost never related to lecture content. Grading criteria was terribly unclear. Course administration, including assignment instructions, TA responsibilities, and exam grading, was grossly mismanaged. Overall, avoid if you can.

Dr. Arnolds lectures are amazing and he presents the info in a fun and clear way. His exams are tough, however, attending recitation and his review lectures help enormously. He does demos and sometimes talks about pop culture which makes learning physics enjoyable. Some of the labs are difficult, and one of them doesnt relate to the lectures.

He really does try his best I think, but with that being said try not to take physics with him. His explanation of fundamental physics is poor at best. Goes on theoretical tangents and lectures are not helpful in succeeding in course assignments or exams.

Dr. Arnold is a good professor who explains the material pretty clearly. He often starts lectures early and goes over, which was a little frustrating. TAs run the recitations and labs, and it can be annoying when we are not given solutions or feedback, as exams are based on recitations. Seemed to be accessible outside of class for extra help.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: He's humorous and covered everything that would in the test. But you need to spend a lot of time outside class to understand the stuff and do past exams to help you.
The only acceptable TA choice for phys450 is Ian Arnold.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Participation matters--SO MANY PAPERS

reviews.comment: He is a kind and engaging teacher. Very fluent in english and encourages discussion. I did not have any problems with his class. Would 100% recommend.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: The class was all lecture. I was bored but if you like the topic you might not be. At times he was difficult to understand because of his accent but it wasn't that bad. You wont pass if you don't do the assignments, because the tests are not common knowledge. I didn't do any of the readings though. There are two projects, start them early.

I really like Professor Ju, I do agree with some other points here. The class is simple, exams are open note and it's Connect assignments. A few other assignments like a video presentation but nothing complicated.
He did very little teaching. His hour and a half lectures only existed to talk about the simple stuff and over explain ads

This was the most pointless class. Everything was done online yet attendance was still required and taken through Hotseat. I don't remember a single thing we learned. I don't think the TA's graded particularly hard because I got A's on both papers even though I didn't learn anything. Dr. Ju was nice and tried to be funny, but I don't recommend.

Like earlier ratings, he is pretty forgettable. I finished one point from an A and asked several times for an opportunity to earn that point and he wouldn't allow it. A pointless I took for a minor. Lectures are boring and he doesn't post slides to reference from. Overall a bad professor.

I just finished a Maymester class with Dr. Ju and he was great. There are maybe 3-4 Connect Study Books a week along with 2 exams, but since they are open book, if you use the textbook search tool the tests were an easy A. Only thing that was a little challenging was writing a report, but as long as you know it's coming then it's not a big deal.

Super fun/easy class to take esp during the Maymester! It was pretty easy with 3-4 concept quizzes a week, a discussion, and an exam. Exams are fairly easy if you have the online textbook. Dr. Ju is really nice and very responsive! If you have any questions/concerns, emailing him is your best bet. He's very willing to help as long as you ask!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Test heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I am currently a student in COM 256 and I'm really liking professor Ju. He does weekly check ins for us and makes sure we all understand the content for the week. The only downside to this class is there are six exams but if you read the textbook, watch lectures, and do the homework they are very easy.

Dr. Ju was a fairly forgettable lecturer. He uses Hotseat once or twice during class for attendance, so you have to show up, but you really don't need to pay attention. If you get the textbook, the online exams are a breeze.

This is a large lecture class and he takes attendance through HotSeat every lecture. The online book is helpful when taking open note exams so you can easily search. Overall, the topic is interesting and the class is an easy pass.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Easy A if you just show up to class for attendance reasons and turn in the open book exams online but Professor Ju is not the best. He is hard to follow and slightly confusing. A little full of himself. He would assume we were all advanced communication/advertising majors when this was an into class and students were of all majors.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Group projects

reviews.comment: Class itself is fine and concepts are easy to understand but he assigns SO MUCH and doesn't upload assignments early or have a schedule.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: If you are looking for an easy class this would be the one to take. Kaplan is very chill and allows a healthy amount of absences. The only thing that truly stinks about this class is the paper evaluations. You have to do 5 throughout the semester and they are just very difficult to read through.

ENTM 311 was one of my only in-person classes this semester and I really enjoyed it. There's a short paper due every class period, but you can probably read and do it in around an hour or less. The class is based around discussion and overall very fun!
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I had to retake this class because I didn't pass the first time but Kaplan really cares about his students. If something isn't going well, talk to him. He may give harder assignments but it's just because he wants you to learn the content and the reasons behind it.
Pay attention and you'll do fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: 12/10 prof.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Great prof, ME539 is graded solely based on homeworks and is very interesting. I really appreciate that he used real data in assignments. ME697 is a different beast - if you are thinking of taking it after ME539 just consider the workload  nad difficulty of content.
great prof overall

Grade was based entirely on homework. Grading was very lenient. Lots of partial credit for effort. And he provides a lot of code to help you. This class is much easier compared to Numerical Methods. He really loves proofs…which weren't very useful for homework, and so lectures weren't too helpful.

By far the best online class I have taken. His book is free through GitHub and the course is very well designed around the book. As long as you do all of the homework, you will get an A. His online office hours are also very helpful.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: One of the best professors ever. He was very caring and kind to his students. He simplified concepts in a very understandable way and made the lectures fun and engaging. Doing homework was easy thanks to his well-explained examples. Exams were harder than expected but doable thanks to his methods. Grateful for taking this course with him.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Respects and cares about his students. Some of the class material was well explained some wasnt.

Prof. Bilionis is clearly a master of his subject and the material reflects that. The lectures were very mathematically dense and he lectures quickly. No exams in this class but 10 lengthy homework assignments - all in Python. You will rely heavily on the TA's and your classmates to get them done. Lots of outside study required for understanding.
Great guy, pretty entertaining and good at teaching the material. It's not overly complicated material, but I enjoyed coming to his class nonetheless.

Great class. Not overly hard, but you have to put in a lot of time to complete the homework. I'd recommend you have a decent handle on basic python before taking this course. He is a good professor, and a great course designer. I really liked the guy!

Ilias is Funny. So funny. Totally hilarious. His hilarity matters way more than you would expect. I didn't want to miss a single class just because he was so gosh darn entertaining. He once drew a potato that very much resembled male genitalia completely on accident. By far the most enjoyable class I have taken was ME 270 with Ilias. What a guy.

Great lecturer that goes over all of the concepts well. Very easy class which is comparable to basic physics. Just make sure you do all of the homework, which is assigned 3 times per week and typically consists of 3 problems. Ilias is very chill and does his job well.

Prof. Bilionis is by far one of the best professors I have had while at Purdue. He gets that the homework might make you solve it the long/hard/stupid way, but he always (eventually) takes the time to show you the shortcuts so that during the exam you can quickly answer problems. Prepares us well for the exams (2nd highest section average).
reviews.rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--Hilarious

reviews.comment: Oe of the best professors I have had!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: He's funny and entertaining, and his accent is objectively superior to all others. As for the class itself, it's like Unit 2 in 11th grade physics, so I'm not really sure why it exists.

Bilionis is one of my favorite professors I've had at Purdue. He really understands the concepts and taught them in a way that was really beneficial for me. Lectures were interesting and never really bored me. Lots of homework but that's true throughout the whole course-- not professor specific.
Good professor

My best professor this semester. Very engaging, excellent knowledge of material and even better at communicating it to the class. He finds a way to make the class fun, while still keeping control over the students. ME 270 is a class which will require more homework than you might expect, but, upon completion, exams will be a breeze.

He is a good lecturer, funny, and keeps the class interested. Loved his attitude. Would recommend him for this course. Text is never used to read but has all the homework in it. Prepare to have homework every class.
Explains stuff really well. Easy class.   Textbook has the homework that you need to do, but you don't need to read anything on it other than to do the homework.
Does not give clear feedback on mistakes. Not easily accessible when asking questions.

Prof. IB's lectures are good. She explains the materials well. For our semester PHYS17200 just sucks because of the problem quizzes IMO. But there is a lot of bonus at the end of the course, but I am not sure this will be the case for the upcoming semesters since this course tends to change quite a bit from semester to semester.

This course (PHYS 172) is quite frustrating due to the homework, recitation, lab, pre-lectures, and problem quiz every week. However, for the lectures themselves, Professor IB described the content well and helped people understand the content with demos. Her lectures were well organized.

Prof Iyer-Biswas does a good job at getting through material in a timely manner, clearly cares about her students, and makes time to answer questions after lecture. The grading for the course is very fair and is not too heavy in any one category. She does a good job at redirecting off topic questions as to not get behind in lectures.
Easily the best physics professor I have ever had. She made quantum finally make sense. If you can get her for a class do it.

She's an amazing professor, hands down. She really genuinely cares about her students succeeding. It's clear that she puts a lot of time into her lectures and thinks of the best ways to present difficult topics. No attendance, she has participation as a grade but honestly I didn't want to skip a single lecture since she explains the topics well.

Professor Gibelato had an extremely upbeat teaching style, no matter the time of the class, which I heavily appreciated. However, at times the class was disorganized, and the professor rarely responds to emails. Once Professor Gibelato adjusts further to the Purdue curriculum, I believe she'll do just fine.

You could do all the work and still receive a bad grade. She was very difficult to work with and did not fairly grade the labs. She's bad with responding to emails, so you will need to go to office hours.

Dr. G has a bubbly personality and makes class very engaging. However, she is slow to respond to emails, and instructions for assignments were unclear. She cleared up a lot of confusion during office hours, but she isn't a clear communicator and is slow with grading. I think this is because she is adjusting to being a professor at Purdue.

Dr. Gibelato's OBHR 330 class was one of my favorite classes this semester. She makes the class really interactive and fun. Sometimes the OB Labs can be confusing, and the class did feel a little unorganized at times, but it was her first semester teaching at Purdue, so I'm sure it will be structured better in the future. Take this class!

I enjoyed having Dr. G for OBHR330. You can tell she genuinely cares about her students and the content she is teaching. She is very upbeat and tries to make class as fun as possible. She is also available for questions outside of class if you are ever cofused. As long as you show up and do the work, you will get an A.

She is an amazing professor all of the semester and says she cares till the end of the class. She made the grade offs to be absolutely BS where you can get a 94% in the class and still get a B. She doesn't make this clear from the start of the class either. Her syllabus was very vague and not even put up till more than halfway through the sem

This class was the easiest class ever. Although the course was the most disorganized class ever, I'm not complaining since it was so easy. Take OBHR330 with her, I've heard other professors are difficult. Exams are easy, labs are easy, and she'll basically give you an A if you ask her nicely.

Professor Gibelato is easily one of my favorite professors I've had. She's so caring and wants all her students to succeed. If you feel you're not understanding certain content, she's so accessible outside of class and is so welcoming. If you show up and do the work, you'll get a good grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Really unnecessarily difficult. Take literally any other eaps 100-level course. There's hours of lecture and readings every week and the homework is unnecessarily time consuming for what the class is. It's really not bad, but compared to the other intro eaps classes it is ridiculous.
I hate this class, way too much work for a 100-level science class. Multiple lectures to watch every week, and each lecture is around 40 to 60 minutes.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Professor Nelson is a great teacher who is very passionate about her class. The exams for this class are very intensive for a 100-level course. Studying the weekly wrap-up quizzes is not enough to do well on the exam, but the positive is that there is one exam drop.

A harsh person. Class is easy but she doesn't really want to give the tough student an A. She just try to find some small error then she can curve your grade down. Make you feel confused and disappointed, you only have a little step behind A, but finally you can't touch it.

She is definitely a good teacher, the grading criteria is so clear and keeps tracking your progress.
The lectures are mandatory for credits. The labs are long but easy to get a high grade.
The exams cover too much material sometimes, but she keeps the average above 80. And she is open to drop the lowest score, which is definitely good news.

She was an excellent teacher, and the class itself was laid out in a way that made everything pretty easy to follow. She also gave many opportunities to boost your test grades if one didn't go well, which I appreciated. The only downside was the labs, which were super long and honestly pretty boring.

It feels unreasonable to have in-person exams covering so much material, especially the third midterm. It's surreal that professors expect this level of commitment for a 100-level Gen Ed course. My friend who took it last semester had a much easier experience
Completely terrible
Took this class online it was very easy. There were a couple times where I got points off that I thought were unfair, but that's all I can say about a professor for an asynchronous class.

Overall this class is very easy and you should get an A in it. If you do your work and pay attention to the lectures it is very easy. I will say there is a lot of busywork that got annoying, but other than that it is easy. Take advantage of the extra credit.

Do not take the EAPS 104 online class with her, it is a RIDICULOUS amount of busy work for an 100 level class. One of the most annoying classes I've taken and it is all unnecessary assignments that don't actually teach you anything.

I've had Prof. Nelson for multiple classes and she always makes it clear that she cares about her students and values everyone's time. She's a good lecturer too - learning new lecture material was my favorite part of the class. The labs were tedious and sometimes boring, but graded leniently. HWs and exams weren't hard - overall good class.
i was a bit scared at first for her being known for assigning lots of reading, but honestly, as long as you have good lab mates and work ethic, this course should not be difficult.

I enjoyed the content and she's very helpful. The class itself is very heavy on homework, likely because it's an online course. I probably wouldn't take a class like this again because of that, but I've enjoyed what I've learned so far.

I took EAPS 104 as an elective so that i could get one of my mandatory credits, but the work load is very heavy. there's a quiz every thursday and on every sunday there is either an exploration that takes 1-2 hours or an exam. i spent 2+ hours everyday taking notes/reading. I had to drop it because I had no time for other classes.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Test heavy

reviews.comment: TAKE THIS CLASS! This was one of the easiest classes I've taken, but I still managed to learn a lot. The workload was very manageable and there was only 1 midterm. Lots of readings and videos to watch, but they're all relevant and helpful to the assignments.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: I very much appreciate that Jennifer is laid back. I think she understands that most people are only taking EAPS 327 to fulfill a requirement outside of their own major's department. Homeworks and projects are straightforward but can be time-consuming.
She was awesome in lectures, and made me feel like going to lectures was actually engaging

Many people think this will be "minimum effort A" classes like many other EAPS10X classes, but you need to put more work than that. You have to read every week (with the online textbook costing $40) and take weekly quizzes, every 2-3 weeks you have an extra assignment or essay due. A lot of EC opportunities though, and she communicates well.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Do not take this as an elective, so much homework. Professor was OK though.
If you follow the guidelines and rubrics for each assignment you should be golden.

Professor Nelson is a great professor and the class is extremely manageable. There is one one hour quiz per week and two essays in the class and that is pretty much it. The quizzes are easy questions straight from the book. It is an asynchronous class, but the amount of time you need to put in to do well is not high.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: I took EAPS 104 as an elective class but never thought I would have to spend this much time on it . There are 30 question quizzes every week. Plus a quiz extra every three weeks. Not to mention you have to write 1000 character long essays and reply to a classmate's essay with 500. She assigns way too much work and avoid taking her course.

You can tell that Jenny really enjoys her field. It shows through her effort put into making the class. However, the class is SO heavy. A LOT of assignments, which are very long. Quizzes are every week and those are also dense. It's a good class, but there's lost opportunity since the material is engaging, she just lacks making the most of that.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Caring

reviews.comment: If you can think of any traits you don't want in a professor this guy probably has them. I have never had / seen anyone be THIS bad. Turkstra makes a joke out of the CS department. I have not learned a single thing from his mandatory attendance lectures. Going to his lectures is a waste of 50 minutes. Prepare to teach yourself everything.

I have had half a dozen cs teachers, and my experience with Turkstra has been the worst. Lectures are disorganized and he throws in random facts that are irrelevant to the material. He is also dismissive towards students more often than not, and his criteria for grades is inconsistent and vague. I recommend taking another instructor, if possible.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Homeworks are challenging and can take up to 20 hours a week, but they prepare you well for the exams and build problem solving ability. As long as you do them yourself, you will learn a lot and do well in the class. Additionally, the exams are very fair and test your understanding of the material in depth. The class is not as bad as they say.

Goes off on tangents about every possible way to get a zero in the class for multiples minutes at the start of each lecture. Prefers to talk about "trivia" and how he's an actual weirdo rather than actually teaching the class. Policy makes it incredibly easy to receive zeros with no negotiation. Disproportionate amount of homework too.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: 85% in class -> C+. Didn't know curving down was a thing until Turkstra.

Turkstra is not an amazing lecturer. He is ok when explaining the concepts but the pace is slow and frequently goes on tangents about unrelated topics. He also is not very kind. He often would completely stop and call out people in our lecture (of >200 people) if people were whispering/entering late. He is kind of hostile towards students.

Though Jeffrey A. Turkstra is a man of expectation - geolocation quizzes inserted at the beginning of class to punish any tardiness, homework directions that, if not read carefully, will certainly force you to restart (yes, this happened to me) - you MUST overcome your fear of approaching him. Emails, TA office hours, even his own will serve you.

Professor Turkstra is clearly passionate. He always answers students' questions and provides good, live demonstrations of concepts shown in lecture. In my case, he was open-minded and helpful during office hours. Exams are fair, but may contain content shown only briefly during lecture. Homework assignments are manageable.
reviews.rating_tags: Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Extremely smart and knowledgeable about the topics, but his condescending nature definitely rubs people the wrong way. Not a huge fan of his lectures - would mumble and speed through slides and live coding. I felt like I learned more from HW than lectures. Despite that, 180BLK>180GLD because team project members are more likely to be competent.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: I think Turkstra's negative reviews are more centered around CS240. For 180, he was pretty good. Lectures are very organized and he is willing to explain some content more clearly. Generally nice during office hours. Almost a quiz every class and homework every week. The group project is manageable if your team is competent. Overall he is fine.

Turkstra was not as bad as people claimed he was. His lectures were somewhat insightful, although you will have to review a lot in your spare time. The workload was heavy during the group project. Exams felt quite fair, although the course not having a curve and only having 1 practice exam available per test did not help in this. Would take again.

he's very passionate about cs - if you like the subject, you'll enjoy his class. lectures are informative, he coded live and didn't excessively rely on his slides - many of his demonstrations in class were really nice! homework is tedious but doable. he did end up going quite quickly through some topics and was hard to follow along with.

Man, had him spring semester last sem and looking back and comparing to some of the professors now, he really wasn't too bad. The exams were difficult, but they were at least straight forward with what the question was asking.
reviews.rating_tags: Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Turkstra wants his students to suffer.
Really good professor and class structure, but the man himself has no sympathy and is kind of a really big asshole. You mess up once and you are cooked. But really good teacher and lecturer.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: I had a lot of prior experience from CS A AP and Data Structures in hs, but if you don't have any experience, Turkstra will be quite difficult as all he does is read from lecture slides, and the code snippets in class are rarely published online if ever. Projects/labs are most of the grade and also there is a pop quiz in like 70% of lectures.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Overcomplicates everything that should be very easy about a class by insisting on mandalore. Attendance is weighted pretty high, 10%. Can be unreasonable and uncompromising very often. Also often tests over things that can be kinda niche and hidden within his lectures, so make sure to memorize the small things.

Very passionate but manages to over-complicate everything: tests, explanations, lectures. Feels like he tries to make the class more about him than the content itself. Pretty outdated as well in terms of programming knowledge considering he didn't know about tools that have been historically used with Java for more than a decade in the industry.

Hard time explaining certain concepts in lecture but his passion for the material is obvious and inspirational. Fun person to talk to outside of class. The grading system is absolutely HORRIBLE though as the grade boundaries require x% in homework AND exams AND overall. just use a standard curve PLEASE. Other than that, he's a good professor.
Turkstra is there just to make your life hard. Any other professor clears.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Dropped the class. Turkstra is not the best at teaching as he uses slides but he did have some very good advice: you should not take the class if you do not have a passion for coding.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Honestly, he wasn't even that bad; people took him out of proportion. I'll even say he is an easier professor than Adams and his grades are much nicer in comparison to the latter, but he sacrifices the lecture quality. But yeah, he doesn't seem that bad... he gets a 5.
He was very passionate about the material, but was awful at actually teaching, we often learned more from the recitations with the TA than we did from lectures from the professor.
