The grading is restricted, as there is no + or—, just letter grades. I was 1% from an A, but I still ended up with a B. Make sure to understand the fundamentals of C and other miscellaneous stuff deeply.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Test heavy

reviews.comment: For someone so clearly passionate about what he does, Turkstra does an unusually good job stripping the passion of anyone with the misfortune of taking this class with him. His lectures are perfect for anyone who already knows the content, the homeworks are more tedious than rigorous, exams are brutal, and the grading scheme will only hurt you.

knows content very well but doesn't teach it in the best way. mumbles through his lectures a lot and speeds through examples. exams are fair if you simply show up to class and actually do the HWs properly. decent professor who can explain things well though, just not a super nice person. would take again if he had a better grading scale :^)

During lectures, he talks with a condescending attitude and goes through examples so fast you can't understand. He doesn't make the homework, but you will spend absurd amounts of time (15-25 hours a week), especially on later ones. His exams are lengthy and contain trivia, and it seems like he takes pride in failing students, as it fuels his ego.
Absolutely horrendous grading scale. There is a lack of explanation when your grade comes out. Once sat on a chair and broke it. Clearly the reason the program is failing.

Lectures were extremely mediocre, has a nasty attitude but acts high and mighty, the most annoying grading scheme ever (grades limited by specific test/hw scores rather than directly weighing the tests more so your cumulative simply does not matter 99% of the time). Midterms were okay, final was all trivia, homeworks were more annoying than hard.

Turkstra is smart, knows the material well, and likes his job. However, he is condescending and egotistical. He is honestly not a bad lecturer, but do not talk to him on a 1-1 setting unless you want to feel like an idiot. If you just go to lectures and do the homework you will survive
King von of purdue
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Hardest class I've ever taken. Avoid at all costs. I spent 15-20 hours a week handwriting the homework before coding and it is mentally draining.
Great professor if you actually want to learn!
Unusual guy. Brutal class. Probably the class I learned the most in.
Absolutely horrible...just no words for the PTSD I am gonna have from this class. Avoid Turkstra at all costs.

This professor, makes learning C diabolical. He gives too much homework, and he makes grading impossible. I would try to utilize office hours/lab sections as much as you can, and don't ruin your mental and physical wellbeing over this one class.
The course design is extremely unreasonable, and the professor does not care about students. He is unwilling to offer any help. but TAs are fine.
Turkstra could be a good teacher if he tried, but unfortunately he made the class a lot harder than it needed to be and was often an obstacle instead of a resource.
Just don't take him for any class, if you need to wait a semester do it. For you health physical and mental, skip him.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: This guy has attitude issues, he is very condescending. I really don't understand why he is the only 240 professor. For all 240 students, no other professor will be this bad, and have such a twisted grading scheme. I plead for Purdue to free future students from having to suffer the way I and many other did under his dictatorial reign.

If Turkstra has a bad day, you are also now having a bad day. Overall, this class was a very good learning experience, but by the end I deeply disliked Turkstra as a professor. Make sure to work hard on the assignments and definitely practice/study for midterms. Also highly recommend going to SI sessions, they really helped me out.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment:
Dr. Turkstra is smart, knows the material well, and clearly likes his job, but can come across as standoffish and egotistical. He makes things the exact way he wants, and is not the most understanding.
His grading scheme was unconventional, but not unreasonable, and he dropped some projects or extended deadlines. Fairly interesting lectures.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I had taken 3 classes with Turkstra all of them were terrible experiences because his grading system was garbage. He basically tries tricking you into losing as many points as possible. I know some of his higher up TAs who talk shit about him behind his back. He has a big ego and thinks he is smarter than he is so I highly recommend cheating.

Some don't like his tone, but policies and expectations were reasonable. Many had 0s on every project. He said they would not pass no matter the number of students (fair; many dropped). The tests had a lot of hyper specific info but were doable if you studied the slides. Be prepared to spend time on projects, but it's not as impossible as seems.

He was fine, not much different from 180 and 240. The best I can describe him is tough love, because he cares about his students but isn't afraid to take away points unnecessarily (for us, it was a simple submission error). I can see why some people don't like him but you will be fine in his class.. maybe not in 252 from what I've heard though.

really hard midterms but fairly easy to score on homeworks, quizzes and lectures are pretty good. The final was really easy though and made up for the midterm. However, the grading cutoffs were brutal.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Amazing lectures --Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: This professor is a terrible person with no regard for people's lives. The syllabus stated 15 hours of work per week, but when a group of 6 became 2 due to dropouts and slackers, he said we should've done 45–60 hours per week to “make up for it.” Despite meeting the syllabus requirements, he gave a terrible grade. Avoid him at all costs.

Worst prof at Purdue. Would not change a seating chart even though I could not walk. Forced me to go up and down stairs without crutches during an exam.  His lack of empathy and garbage teaching make him unfit to teach. If you care about your education or being treated with respect as a human being, avoid him.

Jeffery Turkstra is the least pleasant professor I have ever had. He has a superiority complex and makes it known that he thinks he is better than us. He Made the first midterm way too hard, and when the second midterm was too easy, he told the TA's to go back and take points off because the average was too high. Squirrels are not funny.

Turkstra made CS240 extremely unenjoyable. You can tell he's a smart guy but his superiority complex outshines anything and everything this man tries to teach. Called a group of thirty students "A-holes" for being late and blamed it on the way they were raised (when the bus was >15 minutes late) without letting anyone explain why. Avoid if you can

Turkstra's lectures can be fast-paced, but the homeworks are valuable learning experiences. Unstuck study and the free textbook helped me prepare for the challenging tests. Overall, a rewarding but demanding course.

Seems like a nice dude ig. His grading system is absolute garbage. I had a 90+ course average but was limited to a B because of my midterm averages. The homeworks are ambiguously worded and you will usually spend hours in office hours figuring out what actually should be done and debugging because of the insistence on using Vim instead of an IDE.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Turkstra fills CS307 with as many assignments as he possibly can. A class with a lot of work is okay, but his assignments actively get in the way of the progress of our semester-long project. Whether it's 3 page reports about SWE ethics or some Linux book snippet, there is no end to the menial busy work thrown onto us repeatedly.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: Amazing man and even better lecturer. He makes the class difficult to prepare you for the real world which makes you work to actually understand the concepts.
Worst prof I've had at Purdue by far. He doesn't really help students or pay attention to their needs outside of class.

Homeworks were easily the best part of the class, despite the fact that I hated them. Instructions were often unclear and they were time intensive, but they helped me learn the content and prepare for exams. I cannot say the same about lectures. Slides had little (sometimes wrong) info and Turkstra rushed through them.
A flawed person and a flawed professor. Has his epic moments, but in the end is just cruel towards the students.

Not accessible outside of class. Does not care about his students at the least. He does not help you when it's required. I was fortunate to have good lab TAs who helped me through the course. His lectures are ok at best, he goes off on tangents a lot.

Professor Turkstra tends to run through slides extremely fast and you are forced to spend hours outside of class reading the slides. Homework is pretty difficult but you learn a lot from it. Tests were horrible as you had to learn anything and everything he had ever said. He also has an awful grading policy and "curved" my grade down by 13 percent.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Turkstra is certainly very knowledgeable about what he teaches. However, he has a very "linux user" personality and vibe. Not very flexible, and isn't the most empathetic person in the world. His CS240 class also has big homeworks that can take multiple hours over multiple days to finish, although it gets easier in the second half of the semester.

Had him for 240 and this was the first year he used test averages to limit your grade. Overall, not a bad lecturer but loved to make tests extremely difficult. Always expected you to set time aside just to play around with coding in C (as if people have time). Homework often took days to complete and overall was not very sympathetic.

The content and learning outcomes was clear on syllabus, however the way he teaches it sounds as if he's reviewing it for people who already know the content. He is not approachable despite his claims, grading scheme is the worst I've encountered, test was C code on paper (bad idea) and code standard was overly strict. At least the slides were good
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I'd avoid Turkstra if you can, but chances are you'll probably have to take at least one of his classes since he takes mandatory classes.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Beware of the grading scale for CS 240.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: This man is pretty smart and funny, but unfortunately he is terrible at education. Which is unfortunate given his choice of career path. His course design is god awful and exam cutoffs are the worst idea I have ever seen in my life. He also has a superiority complex and forces a quirky personality. Avoid him at all costs.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--EXTRA CREDIT--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The homework were hard, often had elements that were not mentioned in the readme's which should have been there. Overall, the homeworks were fine and taught me a lot. In lectures he used terminology without explaining it at all which could be unclear to students with less experience. Horrible grading, grades were pretty much curved down.

These replies are real except that review rating a 5 that claimed to have A+ which is obv Turksta. "Clear grading" + "charming" are dead giveaways. Hopefully he looks at these reviews with self reflection but no, he  blames the 400+ instead of himself. Is fine with ~50% getting A/B but never reveals A %. I wonder why. Because it is probably sub 10%
turkstra is
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: Bad
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Test heavy

reviews.comment:
Slides could have been more detailed since it's hard to take notes with the fast pace of the lectures. Code standard often has some silly rule that costs a few points but it makes you a better programmer. After you hit a certain average your homework scores don't matter much so your finals grade will depends only on your exams.
Learned a lot though
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Has exam cutoffs that lock out anyone at a B. Very weird lecturing style and exams that tests contents that aren't practiced during homework. Avoid if you care about your gpa.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Best professor I ever had! He is funny and charming and I would love to have him as my professor again!
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Do not take. New grading scheme this semester in fear of AI as if stack overflow/begging to TA didn't exist. Requirement for A: Get >85? on HW, >90 course average, >85 on test. If you get 2/3 but on test you get below 85 automatic B/B+. Also nobody cares about his squirrels even if that is part of his quirky strange personality he made up.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment:
Created a new grading scheme for the spring 2024 semester: Grade cutoffs! 

TLDR the entire class is decided by 3 exams. If you ask him any question online, he will simply respond with "This was discussed in lecture." and will not elaborate. A friend of mine ended the course with a cumulative average of 96%. His final grade in the course? B+.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Don't take any class with Turkstra. His grading system is really unfair, so many people I know got between 90-95 as their course grade and still got a B in the gradebook because of his grade cutoffs that no other professor has. His exams also make you handwrite code and are graded strictly.

His tests are extremely hard and require you to write extremely long and complex code functions on paper. I spent so much time on HW just for it to not matter because he views HW and test averages separately to calculate your grade. So despite ending with a cumulative average of 87%, I received a final grade of B- because my test average was 75%.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: While I learnt a lot from this class, the pop quizzes where you had to write code and upload it, and the coding standards which were completely useless made this class terrible.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Professor Turkstra's reputation precedes him as a tough instructor, but within his demanding lectures lies a depth of knowledge that students deeply appreciate. Despite the challenging coursework, his passion for the subject shines through, captivating students and fostering a genuine love for learning.
Spare yourself some trouble and don't take this class with him
Terrible. He never gives the help you need. Especially when you go to his office hours, he is extremely unenthusiastic and unresponsive. Gets annoyed when you ask him questions rather than help.

One of the easiest professors I've ever had. I would recommend taking his class as not only does it give you a foundational knowledge of c, but will allow you to gain a deeper appreciation for programming in general.

(Fall 2022 term, Black section). I had significant prior Java experience (5 on AP CSA). I found the lectures pretty well explained and easy to understand overall. The homeworks and projects were manageable, but the all-MCQ final (shared in CS 180 classes) was kind of a doozy out of nowhere.

(Spring 2023 term) Lectures were generally pretty easy to understand but I skipped a good number of them. Homeworks were manageable and tests were a bit harder. I did really well on the HWs and not so well on the tests.

If you take this class, please throw your mental health down the drain. You could literally spend over 20 hours every single week on the long time-consuming homework assignments, stay up late every night to study for the exams, go to all the lectures, and still end up doing bad in the course because of Professor Turkstra's unfair grade cutoffs.

Please don't take any class that he teaches. He has a huge ego and often makes side remarks in class to emphasize his own perceived superiority. He also talks REALLY fast at times throughout lecture and goes off track to say things like "back in my day...", but also has attendance quizzes that force us to show up.

Sets one exam too tough and his response is to mostly change the grade distribution for B and C according to what he said in class. No change in A. Heading towards a B with an 89% on the course. Is also partly my fault for not attending class and missing out on attendance.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: sadness. beware of your mental health. + from previous years he doesn't curve huge so not expecting the same here it is so over.

His class almost made me change majors. Exams are awful, he made a question too long that no one could finish. His apology consisted of "sorry, but" and blamed us. Hw given week before & week of are on exam, giving no time to prepare. He says something isn't on exam, it is. He will extend hw but makes it sound like a chore. Avoid if possible.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: I would generally recommend avoiding this class if possible. Honestly, the lectures don't feel useful, and he jumps around a lot, making it hard to take proper notes. I really LOVE the code standard. He can be kind of funny at times, but then there's also those times when he lectures students for being like 5 mins late (he was 10 min late one time)

Extremely and unnecessarily long time taking homeworks (and you can NOT get help that is actually helpful unless you go to office hours or labs -which is hard to do for me as I live off campus-), fun lectures but extremely fast pacing, randomly taken attendance quizzes, and the materials covered in class != materials asked in midterms.
Much more mellow than in previous years I've heard.

This man has no regard for human life at all. 

Me before this class :)

Me after this class :(
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Personally, I do not feel Turkstra is fit to teach the class. He zooms over the material even though the students come in with little to no knowledge. He is extremely unenthusiastic to help students in his office hours even though he keeps saying you should get help. I would not recommend this professor.
Please don't take this class. Or any class he teaches. I can't stress how much you will regret it.
nah

This is one of the hardest classes you will have taken in your academic career by the time you need to take it. The exam averages are in the low 40s with no curve to help fix the curve, and ontop of that the topics he mentions won't be on the exam end up being on the exam. The class teaches you alot but it is extremely difficult.

Possibly, one of the worst professors the CS department of Purdue University has to offer. The despair one feels after taking his exams is second to none, as the materials are unreliable. During his lectures, the slides are bad at explaining concepts, as some of the examples he places are either incomplete, incorrect, or downright deceptive. AVOID.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: You have to be OCD to not miss anything in this class.

Mr. Turkstra tries hard to come across as chill, and relatable when he is not. He rushes during class and is unnecessarily rude, always comparing our work to work in a professional setting, where clearly comparisons cannot be made. His constant, know-it-all attitude, results in hesitation to ask questions. Homeworks are very diffcult.

Dr. Turkstra is a good lecturer, but there's nothing to be gained by taking 252 with him compared with Gustavo. This class is far more time consuming than a 4 credit class should be, yet Turkstra still blames the students for procrastinating. The additional project at the end of the semester was rushed and ultimately felt unproductive.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Test heavy

reviews.comment:
Extended every project due to how hard they were
Harsh code standard which docks more points than actual failing code
Exams averages = failing
Berated students for "procrastination" half the semester
Class did worse for my mental health than any other
Class is 4 credits but felt like 10
Takes attendance inconsistently, so good luck making up points
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: If you're the type of person who cares deeply about their GPA and doesn't like spending tens of hours per week writing C code, then CS 252 with Turkstra is definitely not for you. However, if you don't relate you might actually learn a lot from him. It's very hard to do well in Turkstra's class, but it's worthwhile if you manage to pass it.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Overestimates the abilities of his students and makes things more difficult than they need to be. Code standards should not be weighed so heavily into grades. Just takes time away from focusing on the actual project. There is no need to add this much extra work on top of the rigor and difficulty already involved in the course.

Awful class structure. Each project demanded more hours out of each week than I had available, and everything seemed to be geared for someone with 0 outside responsibilities. The exams were equally tedious, just being a trivia on random slide bulletpoints instead of the main content, with thr final being 105 questions. Dont take CS252 w/ Turk.

This man suits CS 240's narrow syllabus. His teaching style is at odds with 252's broad coverage. 6 poor projects, each > 40 hrs a week, and terrible exams made this class horrible. Average is around 50-60%, which he is proud of for some reason. Also would randomly start making inside jokes during lectures with 3 or 4 girls who sat in front of him.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Terrible professor:  Failing 25% of students, overloads with projects.  Fails 25% of students; demands unreasonably strict coding styles with misleading grading standards. Even his code fails the tests. Misguides students about exam focus(saying later half but, in fact, first half), leading to poor performance. Avoid at all costs.

Very unfair grading policies with an insane workload. Somehow made a web server project harder than shell, with an average of 55%. Exams were unfairly challenging and the projects were opposite to what he would say in class. Didn't care if you had other courses. Sent several people I know to CAPS for breakdowns because of the course.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: His CS interest != his teaching ability, which is distracted and unprepared. Poorly structured assignments that penalizes you for HIS mistakes. Would do the opposite in exams/assignments of his class teachings. Unapproachable at all times. Spent all his time flirting with a regular group of 4 to 5 students sitting in front of him.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He's insightful and passionate about what he teaches. However, he tends to overestimate what his students can do and can be condescending to pretty much anyone that talks with him. His policies and projects are harsh - unreasonably so at times. Make sure you're prepared and don't expect any sympathy if things go south.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Turkstra is a smart man, but he clearly lacks a) empathy, the only empathy he shows is empathy thru university policies b) interest in the course, he clearly is bored with teaching the material and often sidetracks to topics that are of interest to him. Very bad, uninformative slides, made harder course even harder. Expect no life but 252
Best computer science professor at Purdue. Loved his humor and positivity in classes.
Your grade is heavily dependent on the team you choose at the beginning of the semester. The rest of it depends on the mood of the TA grading your sprint demo.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Group projects--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: If you go on boiler grades You can clearly see the average for CS 252 is drastically lower when taught by him. His lectures made me fall asleep. And the exams were awfully hard. FALL 2023
Very difficult course with very strict grade distribution cutoffs. Got a B with 88.4% in course.

Lectures were basically useless as Turkstra just threw walls of text at us and expected us to know them. Luckily, the majority of the course grade came from a semester-long group project. The course was disorganized, with communications for trivial things like posting grades/assignments not occurring until the day of the deadline.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: A lot of people complained about his strict code style and other things related to it, but I found them quite reasonable. Companies in real life do have their own kind of code style and this is a good way to get used to them. Homework and quizzes take up 50% of this class so start early and you would be successful in this class.

Most of the content was basic programming stuff but CS180 is meant to ease you into college. The intensity did go up later in the semester and I learned a few new things. Great lectures and content and the group projects were graded ridiculously leniently (atleast for my group). Took his CS240 and am writing this hours before the final haha
Not enough practices for the exam, trick questions would appear

I had Prof. Turkstra for the BLK section, and overall, he was decent. Most lectures were spent live coding. Lectures were not required, but there were short online quizzes after. The homework site wasn't great, but labs were available if needed. Group projects were the worst, as teams were random, and little was done if your team didn't contribute.

Embrace the challenge of penning code on paper. Face relentless debugging marathons, followed by painstaking hours refining style, only to earn a meager 50% for aesthetics. Beware, a single crash guarantees a zero. This is not the weed-out class. This is not the end of your suffering. This is Purdue Computer Science.
Homeworks an tests are hard.  Takes days to do one homework. TAs are not very helpful.

He's a great teacher and is passionate about the material. If you're willing to put in the work this can be a very rewarding class. The last 2 projects are team projects, but they were graded very leniently for us, probably because they were trying not to scare us or something. Great class, great professor, would recommend 100%.

Attending lectures is important. He is a fun professor, yet the material is difficult. It is crucial to pay attention to the material in order to enjoy the class. He will answer questions in class, as long as it's not a stupid question because you weren't paying attention.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Lecture heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Professor Turkstra is a professor. Not much to say more than that. He explains concepts well, is very passionate about CS, and you can probably learn a fair bit from him because he is clearly knowledgable and has a lot of experience with computers. When the going gets tough, he gets more unclear and hard to understand though. Attend lectures!

Professor Turkstra is an alright professor. His lectures are kind of monotone, however the bigger issue is that when he is answering questions, he often becomes much quieter and mumbly. This may be just for CS180 though as he clearly doesn't care much for Java. I do really like his live coding examples, though they can be hard to follow.

Professor Turkstra is a solid lecturer, though it can be sometimes be difficult to follow his live coding demonstrations after just being introduced to the concepts. Would recommend briefly skimming through the textbook before lectures. Workload is pretty easy with the exception of Projects 4 and 5, which depend on the quality of your group.

The lectures feel like they could've contained much better explanations and details than given and overall could've been better. However from what I have heard he is a helpful teacher that tries to find ways to interact with students.

From what I've heard Turkstra was a lot worse a couple years ago, but he's fine now. He seems to care about what he's teaching, with cool examples to go with it. He does make some jokes but can be a little stern sometimes and tries to scare students a little bit. He does break up lectures with trivia breaks and invites students to come to lunches.
Professor Turkstra is a fine lecturer and is readily available through appointments or office hours. His negative point is that he has a tendency to be a bit passive aggressive towards students.

Turkstra is an average lecturer, nothing special, but not bad. He is very condescending towards students and will treat you like you're an idiot. He uses scare tactics on students, such as the first day of class saying: "if you don't send this email, I will assume you're dropping this course". Also, his course logistics are an ambiguous nightmare.

Turkstra is an alright computer science professor. He's a nice guy but you can tell that he's not the biggest Java fan in the world and I don't believe he enjoys teaching it. If I had to take this class again I would probably take another section with a different professor. I think that C is more of his expertise and he's better at teaching 240.

A lot of people hate on him because he goes over lectures fast. He definitely can get people lost while browsing files around quickly in vi in the terminal. However, he is extremely nice and is passionate about teaching and helping students learn. One time after class I asked him some questions, and it was a great talk.

I found that if you go to class, engage, start the homework early and use available resources, you will pass the class. His lectures are pretty fast-paced (except for the trivia, which I appreciated) and he has a lot of examples in vim that are often hard to understand but just use google you can do it i believe in you :)

This is a difficult course, and Turkstra is just an OK professor . He does go through very complex code examples fast in lecture and then tells you to "try it on your own". I don't think I ever understood any of his examples. He explains the information fairly clearly. I wish they would get rid of group projects, because they made me hate CS.
Professor Turkstra is extremely rude and condescending.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Yeah I barely passed the class, but that was my fault. Just do all your homework guys. He is very funny and his passion for C makes the class so much easier. He is incredibly caring as well. If you guys are having trouble with work, do not be intimidated and go talk to him. He is understanding of mental health and gives great advice.

I failed this class, but it's completely my own fault. Bad mental health and didn't attend lectures. You need to go in order to pass! And get a head start on homework each week, it's not doable at the last minute. Turkstra is a bit quirky, but very kind. I met with him during office hours to discuss my grade and he was very understanding.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Not sure why some people dislike Turkstra. I think he's a great professor and cares about his students and is clearly passionate about what he teaches. The class is hard but if you keep up with the lectures I found it miles better than CS 182.

Great professor that actually cares about what he teaches. His lectures are quickly paced, so it's hard to understand them at the time of the lecture, but if you spend time out of class you'll be fine. His lecture slides are understandable and readable, plus he has a recorded video you can watch if some slides are confusing
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Beware of pop quizzes--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Turkstra was a great professor who is very passionate about the subject. Learning C from his was very straightforward and fun. Homeworks are easy to get 100s on and worth 50% of the grade, but midterms are fairly difficult with some concepts that weren't covered a ton in class. Code standard can seem daunting, but that is also easy to get 100 on.

He gets more hate than he should. Getting 100 on every homework is very achievable (TA's, office hours, or just doing it yourself), and getting a B average on midterms makes the final (mandatory my year) irrelevant. Midterms had a few, small point value pedantic questions, but still very doable to get an A on both. (I didn't but still ended with A)

Prof. Turkstra made learning C a very enjoyable experience. You can be intimidated by his coding standard and harder quizzes, but as long as you utilize lab, office hours, and other resources, it's easy to get a good grade in this class. He also actively communicates with the students and tries to make the class better. Very very good professor.
He is an amazing professor that enjoys teaching the subject. However, as others have said his tests tend to be on the harder side compared to review guides that he gives us.

Professor Turkstra really enjoys teaching C and has made CS 240 a much more maneagable class compared to previous semesters. The homeworks at first can seem like a lot but become fairly straightforward as the semester goes on. The exams do have in-depth questions on topics he barely addresses in class so make sure to really study his lecture notes!
Turkstra's teaching style has changed over the past few years, he is much better than some of the reviews say.

Turkstra is an amazing lecturer for CS240. He makes his lectures interesting and you can tell he really loves what he does for a living. His tests and homework are absolute trash and very hard. Homework is long, especially if you don't know what you're doing and he always makes exams harder than they need to be.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Professor Turkstra is a very nice man. His lectures are for the most part engaging and interesting. Should you choose to pay attention you'll learn a lot. But good god are his exams the worst. He gives you practice problems and whatnot and then throws the most insane curveball questions at you. He made us rewrite strlen() twice on our midterm!
CS240 is absolute trash and torture, and I'm so sorry if you had to take it
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: He is a passionate professor overall and is cared about his students. The lab attendance is not graded, but TAs will help with homework and other questions during lab hours, which is an excellent resource outside the class. Although exams are somewhat hard, I think he has tried his best to make this less stressful.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Beware of pop quizzes--Caring

reviews.comment: Personally, I enjoyed learning C, so that's probably I don't hate him like the others. He really tried to teach students the material. Homeworks are a lot of work but as long as you work for it, they're pretty easy. Coding standard is a bit tough but not unbearable. Quizzes are not that bad. Great professor and human overall.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Turkstra is personable and seems like a nice person overall. Unfortunately, he structures his classes to make his students hate it as much as possible. Pop quizzes every other lecture, poor lectures in general that are slide-heavy, and extensive homework assignments with nonsensical coding standards and expectations.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Beware of pop quizzes--Caring

reviews.comment: The worst professor that I have met. Turk got mysterious standard for plagiarism, I have seen people copy homework in lab but they don't actually give a sh*t bout it, instead, use some "plagiarism detect app" to check the code, when all people follows the documentation, the functions looks the same but they recognize it as plagiarism.

Turkstra is a very organized professor with interesting lectures. He will often give live demonstrations in class (though these can be sometimes daunting because you don't always know what is going on). He does call students out in lecture for talking, and I think that has earned him most of his negative reviews. His exams and labs are very fair.

As of Fall 2021, Turkstra is a pretty decent professor. A friend of mine who previously had and hated Turkstra agrees that he's changed for the better. This semester we could skip the final if we were satisfied with our midterm grades. He wrote two final exams so we could go home early instead of staying till Saturday. Had some extra credit too.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: He may seem really nice and approachable and while that may be true sometimes it doesn't make his class any better. Each week the homework has horribly vague descriptions, the TA's rarely give helpful feedback and his exams are tough, like really tough. I think he forgets we've never programmed in C before. Overall a horrible experience.
Be more specific on the exam.

I know a lot of students hate him. BUT JUST GO TO HIS OFFICE HOUR ONCE WITHOUT ASKING ANYTHING ABOUT GRADE! and then you will see how nice he is. He is somehow too idealistic. Thats why sometimes he is considered a harsh professor. but he will be super helpful if you come to his office hour with some bug u tried a lot but still don't get it.

A very caring and endearingly eccentric professor who kept online lectures interesting in inventive ways, from trivia breaks to an interview with a squirrel. Nevertheless, I disliked his example-based lecture style. The final project was a significant amount of work, and my grade was only good because he reduced the content and offered remediation.

no just no. If you can avoid him, please do so. There are so many awful memories with him. Just came into lecture when he was passing his quiz, I got 0 cuz I was late by a few seconds. Gave me 12 on homework, so I ask him to re-grade my homework. His reaction was like he can't be wrong, but he was. (got 100 after his 1500 loops of check)
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Graded by few things--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: The first half of CS307 involves lectures about different aspects of software engineering. Professor Turkstra gave excellent information and advice about the field. Uses his personal experience to help reinforce the topics he is teaching.

Very good lectures. Obviously cares a lot about his teaching. Quizzes were annoying and his coding style requirements are too. The occasional badly thought out assignments (second due-in-a-week homework was 1000 lines of code, and an 80 hour+ project to replace a 2 hour exam) are a bit more than annoying. But very good class otherwise.
reviews.rating_tags: Beware of pop quizzes--Amazing lectures--So many papers

reviews.comment: He is a great professor who made me like the field even more. He helps a lot and really explains things. Always ready to help and he knows his field extremely well. Yes, he is a tough grader, but that is what helps you. There will be no handholding in your future jobs either. His class made me a better programmer overall.

A very hard teacher that shows little care for his students, and has ridiculous expectations. Always finds a way to throw a curveball on every assignment. The lectures are very important if you skip them it will hurt you a lot.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: The grade distribution doesn't make sense. Why does a project count towards 40%? I tired to get TAs to help me debug, butthey couldn't, and me either. The crashes didn't get me many points, even though I wrote thousands of lines of code. The project was also on something we didn't learn in class. I wish it was broken down into smaller assignments.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Beware of pop quizzes--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Homeworks are not hard, but the project is super hard!!!!!!! Please start the project as soon as possible. GTAs are very helpful in this class, they help me get full points in the project. Some UTAs are useless, they don't know how to fix my problem and waste my time.

Turkstra is an amazing professor. One of the best I've had so far. His lectures are engaging and helped my learn a language I did not have any experience in before this. The assignments in this class are difficult, but it is definitely manageable if you watch his lectures. Most of the TAs and GTAs are helpful too when you get stuck.

I'm not sure why he is rated so low, I though professor Turkstra was a really great teacher. The homeworks aren't too bad and neither are the quizzes. Make sure you watch the lectures/go to lectures. I think everyone mostly complains about the project which is pretty difficult (I had an A average and then the final project dropped me to a B).

This class is extremely challenging by nature. People bash this professor unnecessarily. He cares for how the students are doing in the class, and does offer extra credit, and drops part of the project if necessary. If you do all the quizzes, and do the homework, you should be able to get an A in this class. Some of the GTAs are helpful(Yubo Shao)
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: He is a great professor, If you are a person that has bad time management, be careful this class will be suffering. But overall you will learn a lot from this class. He is super passionate about computers and a funny man, and his project is super super hard!

I had him the second half of spring 2020, and from my understanding he created the first exam. I feel like he genuinely cares about the students he's teaching, but he does make the material challenging. I think he's made out to be more of a monster than he really is. Just don't slack off and make sure you know you're stuff prior to an exam/quiz.
Dont take the class if covid is happening still just switch majors, Turkstra says he is willing to help but he nor the Ta's do. They always look at my campuswire post and leave it without a response.

Turkstra has a lot of bad reviews on here but after taking CS240 with him I completely disagree. I struggled in CS180 and was not looking forward to CS240 with Turkstra after reading the reviews, but he's super chill, explains things really well, and gives fair assignments. One of my favorite professors at Purdue so far.

Has no respect for his students. He has good lectures and is passionate about computer science. He is pretty inspiring when it comes to his love for open source development. There are pop quizzes at the end of many lectures often covering the things taught 5 minutes prior - so there's no time to practice or study them. Handwritten coding exams.

I will say that Turkstra is very knowledgable and very passionate about C and CS in general. However, the project he gave Spring of '20 was so hard and unnecessary I really have a hard time believing he cares about his students. He refused to listen to our feedback about the project and treated us very poorly during a very difficult time.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes--Tough grader

reviews.comment: I really enjoyed the first part of the class, and it is true that Professor Turkstra is really passionate about the topics he teaches. He likes talking to students and is a great teacher overall. He is very strict, and the online transition was particularly rough for this class. The final project still keeps me up at night, unnecessarily hard
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: CS240 is a hard class, but if you actually watch the video and take notes and sign up for office hour you'll be fine. Turkstra is very enthusiastic about the things he teaches and it's always better than someone who has no passion teaching it. I wouldn't learn as much if I had this class with someone else.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--Caring

reviews.comment: Although there are some tough moments, good instructor after all. Enjoyed the class and learned a lot.

You definitely work towards your grade. I can say that this is the only class that has taught me something at Purdue. The Professor makes sure that you learn the stuff. If you want a good grade you will have to study and listen to him. For me the pop quizzes were annoying but I think that was the only way to ensure attendance.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Beware of pop quizzes--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Overall a pretty good prof. Definitely makes you work to get a good grade, which is - in all honestly - probably a good thing, despite how many kids might not like it. The pop quizzes can be rough, but they make sure you're keeping up with the material like you already should be. Made some great changes to the class due to COVID. Overall, solid guy
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Clear grading criteria--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Really undeserved amount of hate. He's a very passionate teacher, but doesn't give away free A's. Expect to put some time into the class in terms of homework and review the material to prep for pop quizzes. He was also very understanding when it came to COVID, basically only making us do half of the project that was assigned. Very underrated.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Respected--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: He is a very passionate professor. He wants all of his students to try their best and succeed. However, he comes off as very rude and condescending. He is not easily approachable because he often does not give straight answers to questions. Coding standard is a waste of time, and the pop quizzes were unnecessarily difficult.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Beware of pop quizzes--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Sure there are some things to not like about Turkstra as a student  could be his code standard, difficult pop quizzes, or his occasional strict and demanding personality. But ultimately, the class organization, clear assignment instructions, and his honest love for teaching shines above the rest. Worth taking if you have the time in your schedule.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Professor Turkstra expects you to really keep up with his lectures and gives pop quizzes covering material from the most recent lecture. However, he grades fairly and listens to feedback from his students. He has high expectations, but he really does care that you learn something from his class. Very respectable guy.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Lots of people don't like him and make the mistake of taking another professor. Trust me, go with this guy. Sure he gives pop quizzes, but its not all bad if you pay attention in lecture. He is so knowledgable and explains everything well. TRUST ME - TAKE ANY CLASS WITH TURKSTRA. He really cares about his students, and its overall a reasonable guy!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Beware of pop quizzes--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: I am in my freshman year and I am currently taking the class in my second semester. I came to Purdue with 0 coding experience, and honestly no love for CS, but he really changed that. He is so passionate, witty, and he really cares about his students. I'll admit the class is work-intensive, but he really does make it worth it.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Beware of pop quizzes--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Everyone has their own opinion, but Turkstra explains that this project is a replacement for an entire midterm as well as a final, and he will also be curving the class. He genuinely cares about teaching and is undoubtedly the best professor I have had. There are pop quizzes, but if you stay with the material, (as you should), you will be fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Beware of pop quizzes--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: This has possibly be the worst class I have taken yet, Turkstra is a literal psychopath. If you have heard about the project, this class really was that unnecessarily intense. Highly recommend avoiding Turkstra, please take CS240 with any other professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Coding standard is checked with no leniency and can get you from a 100 to an 80 without even running your code once. Any late submissions results in severe penalties. Bad morale: "I have a list of you", "If you cheat, give me your ID". Silences and deletes posts on discussions (not complaints) on work severity. It's bad: and I've worked with C.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: He's an extremely passionate professor who makes me enjoy watching lectures. Always excited about the material on hand and will never shoot down a question.
Just made our lives harder now that classes are online. Gave an undoable homework along with a month long project that seems to actually require 2 months. Better to not take 240 than do it with him.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Very good lecturer. Pop quizzes aren't hard, just means of enforcing attendance. While there is a textbook, it's easily acquired and really only supplements the lecture content. Assignments - it's C. It's hard. Does a good job of being available outside class (he even invited the class to lunch regularly when I took 240)
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Many reviews strong opinions about Turkstra, each side has their points. He can be very strict with his course policies (like pop-quizzes worth a pretty large portion of your grade and daunting coding style guidelines) and very intimidating to ask help from. That being said, He is a knowledgeable lecturer and I am a stronger programmer bc of him.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Beware of pop quizzes--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Unusually strict, makes course artificially challenging by enforcing random policies, tends to be rude to students and sometimes uses intimidating language and scare tactics to have students abide by certain course policies (i.e. "Respond to this email by the end of the day or I will assume you are dropping the course."). Is sometimes pleasant.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Inspirational--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: A very intense person and a very tough grader. The strict coding style and other nonsense course policies can take a lot of points off of your hard work. Although he is passionate about his work, I still recommend that you stay away from his courses.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: God I've only had Turkstra for 2 weeks, but he's so bad. Not so much in terms of his teaching, but he's so obnoxious. He's expects us to know as much as he does which is not fair considering most of us are first year students. And his coding style is so specific, I can't stand him.

Teaching style is okay. He is so passionate about coding that he can't simplify the material to students' level sometimes. Can be scary to ask questions to. Unnecessarily strict course policies that cause you to lose a lot of points in an already hard/weed-out class. Rarely gives late days even if there is a technical problem like data being down.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Beware of pop quizzes--Tough grader

reviews.comment: I am kind of disappointed in all the reviews of Professor Turkstra on this site. Yes, taking a class with Professor Turkstra may not be a cakewalk but why should it be? I have taken both CS 240 and CS 252 with him and can say that his classes are very organized and thought out, he's a great lecturer, and expectations are laid out at the beginning.

I would definitely not take the professor again. He has pop quizzes which are worth 25 percent of your grade. I lost most of my points there. The exams are all FRQs and you have to write really long lines of code in the exams which is pretty bad. And the coding standards are a huge pain. His teaching is alright. But really bad course policies.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Awesome Professor. The best course policies at Purdue. Will take again any number of times.

I also had him for CS 180. Hes strict, so beware. I was not a fan of pop quizzes to enforce attendance. I did, however, like that tests werent just programming, and rather made you genuinely understand how the language worked (unlike assignments where you can just fumble around until it works). Code standard sucks though. Long final too.

Avoid at all costs. Below average lectures, ridiculous course policies which accomplish nothing but lower grades, pop up quizzes that make students lose confidence and many more. This course is a total nightmare if taught by Turkstra.

Jeff Turkstra is a decent lecturer but has tough course policies such as code standards and in class quizzes. If you enjoy C and linux you will like him. But to the students the in class quizzes were not fair since not everyone was up to date with material. Not a prof who accommodates those who struggle to grasp concepts quickly.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Ruined a perfectly good year for me. Please avoid him if at all possible. Makes class adhere to his unnecessary standards that doesn't add anything to the learning experience.
Please avoid taking Turkstra at all costs if you want to graduate college.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Beware of pop quizzes--Tough grader

reviews.comment: AVOID HIM BY ANY CHANCE. HE IS STRICT, and NOT LENIENT.
reviews.rating_tags: Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: He strictly enforces a very arbitrary and ridiculous coding standard. Written exams are extremely long and unless you know what you're doing the moment you finish reading the question, which you won't unless you memorize all homeworks and slides by heart, you will never finish everything in time. Overall a very inflexible and unreasonable person.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes--Tough grader

reviews.comment: A lot of people complaining are upset because Turkstra actually has standards and expectations. I walked out of his class knowing I was better because of it.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Turkstra can be really funny in a quirky kind of way. Lectures are well thought out. Takes a little getting used to, but learned a lot.
Lot's of people complain about Turkstra being difficult, but I'm learning a lot and am definitely a better programmer as a result.

Pop quizzes, a strict code standard, and written exams were all introduced by Turkstra. These are great examples of him as professor because they don't enhance the students' learning in any form, but rather work to wear down and punish students with no justification.
avoid him at all cost
A little particular about lectures, and has firm (odd, and maybe a bit elitist) beliefs. Overall, though, no complaints. Cares about his students and wants to make sure they actually learn.
No curve
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: It seems like he is not helping student, but being against with them

Easy class for me tho. If you love C programming, then his voice sounds better and you will start to find he is OK. He has strict rules and I found these rules help me in various ways (e.g. coding standards.)

Turkstra is one of the most unusually strict professors I have ever taken. Lectures are boring due to his monotonous voice and him basically just reading off the slides. Although the course isn't too difficult, it isn't enjoyable at all and the professor isn't doing anything to help it make it more interesting.
Purdue Computer Science Department, you guys have lost your minds.

Prof Turkstra is definitely not the best professor I've had, but I've certainly had worse. The class is definitely difficult but he's willing to help and debug code with you for every step to ensure that you understand what went wrong. He tangents during his lectures which is annoying, but he generally gets through all of the material for the day

His lectures are fairly dry, and very hard to pay attention to. Had some extenuating circumstances, and he made it hard to deal with a change in schedule. Your grade is ridiculously dependent on the moods of your TAs, and there is a wide, wide variety in difficulty and grading levels.

He just reads someone else's slides over the entire semester and gives homework unrelated to the course material. His voice makes everyone sleepy after 30 mins of the class every time. Try to avoid him at all cost unless you have good teammates to carry you.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--SO MANY PAPERS--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: Prof Turkstra showed no respect for his students by telling them that they are one of the worst team. His comments about the project were superficial and not as elaborate as we expected.
Just terrible.

I enjoyed the class because most of the course revolves around the project of your choice. However, it is extremely unreasonable. If you do well on the project, then your grade pretty much depends on the homework and midterm. Cutoffs: 80-88 was a B, 88-89 was a B+, and 89-90 was an A-, and 90+ was an A. Cutoffs were unfair for those expecting a +/-
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--TEST HEAVY--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Professor Turkstra is the sole reason that this class, which should have been very fun, was an absolute nightmare. His curves make no sense, his grading is unfair, and I would never ever take him again.

Professor Turkstra is a terrible professor. Avoid him at all costs. He is extremely unreasonable. We had one homework for our class, and it was graded according to an algorithm. I wrote 2 pages and got 4/15. He did a regrade and I got 4.5/15. I did well in his class, but he's the worst professor I've ever had. AVOID HIM AT ALL COSTS.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: One of the worst professor in CS department. Nothing more than reading slides in lecture time. The lecture, besides, really rarely helps the project. We have to figure most of the things by ourselves. The only homework this semester, I got 7 out of 15, and none of my friends in this course got better score than me. NEVER take his course.
Boring and monotonous, reads slides that are not from him.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Although He does rely On The slides, he does a great job of elaborating on them. He explains things really well and gives good demos too. This course was easy for me since I have prior programming experience. Without that I might have struggled more in the beginning because he went through the basics pretty quickly (since he taught BLK).
I really enjoyed playing Facebook Tetris. His voice was monotonous and he relies heavily on the slides. His beard was on point tho.
He's amazing!!!!!!

Like all other comments. He read slides that is not from him. Perhaps because it is not from him, he don't know the answers for the quiz. It feels like student don't have the right to know the answers. Be prepare not to TALK during ICLICKER!!! We are not allowed to discuss with other students and with him. Watch out for mandatory meetings.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: He read presentations in a very small voice and monotonously. He gave emails to students which only say we need to meet. Prepare to do a lot of homework and lab without gain anything in the lecture. Basicly you just learn by yourself through the lecture and the book.
He likes to rush through the slides and leave students with tons of confusion. Then, he throws super hard labs and homework out and expect students to figure out on their own.
He has low energy when teaching in class. He teaches like he hasn't fully wake up yet. The coursework are heavy and he doesn't give enough clarification on assignments.
his lecture is boring as hell but exams are not too bad
very boring lectures. very tough on grading and exams are aweful
He just read through slides that are not even his. He didn't even know certain parts of the slides when student ask him questions.
LOTS, LOTs of work and does not know answers for quizzes and is very very boring. Does not explain well and is very rude and strict.
reviews.rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: He refuse to tell students the answer for iclicker quiz. It would be beneficial if he can solve the iclicker question just like any other professor
Update...i don't know why but i start to like him...
He just read through PPT most of the time. However he is still a rookie professor. Hope he will do better

Very monotonous and boring lectures with quizzes at the end of most lectures. However, the midterm is easy as long as you read all the slides a day or two before the exam. It was primarily about vocab. The group project is what you make of it. Pick something hard? You're going to struggle. Do pick something that sounds hard, but is actually easy.
reviews.rating_tags: BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: Nice guy but boring lectures. Nonetheless, class is rather easy granted you complete the assignments on team and do somewhat well on the midterm.
reviews.rating_tags: BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--Clear grading criteria--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: Professor Turkstra is a great guy who just hasn't learned how to give a good lecture. He is often monotonous and simply reads slides. The 1 exam given in the class was just 60ish questions taken straight from around 400 slides. Boring, but he grades fairly, and if your group works well together this class should be no problem.

Professor McKinney's teaching style is poor. Fast paced, reads off the slides, all work is done before class and briefly shown. Not much reasoning or conceptual conversations within class. Questions are are answered with no explanation and jargon heavy. Exams are arbitrarily over the top difficult, and don't reflect anything.

McKinney explains course content and practice problems clearly during lecture. His exams were more difficult than Al Othman's but they were reasonable (similar in difficulty to textbook problems). However, the final had a few mistakes (there was no answer on two MCQ questions), but he gave full points back for both questions.

His midterm 3 was designed to deceive you and induce mid-test anxiety so you ended up performing worse and no longer able to solve questions you once could with a clear mind. Glossing over small details when studying lead to failing entire questions. The final was better but still had several tricks. Cutoffs were only lowered by 1%.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: His exams were actually insane. Run. Had Al-Othman and halfway thru McKinney replaced her and he singlehandedly brought everybody's averages down. A 45% average on exam three is DIABOLICAL after a Bish average on the first two exams. Idk how he decides what to put on these tests but they're not indicative of any of the material we get. Hated him!!!

Feel he was put in a tough spot based on the current 2k1 situation , but he teaches like its his part-time job. Barely responds ,or answers questions extremely vaguely and his exam average of when he came in was a 40 compared to high 80s in the first two- maybe thats a course related complaint but not sure.

Always just reads through his powerpoints in class without providing much examples. Doesn't feel like he cares about our grade, when I go talk to him during office hours, it simply feels like he does not want to help.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: In complete honesty, he is very neglectful, has low effort, and has no compassion for teaching. I struggle to find a worse example. Said to me in person that there are too many people in the class for him to give a damn about a particular student. Like me.

He doesn't give two shits about people's struggles. I asked him to fix my exam grade since I had the right answers written down squarely on the paper with work to support, but the bubbled choice didn't reflect that. Thus, he refused. His reply was that 1000 people are taking this course, and he doesn't care if you fail or not. His exam avg: 40
Bro has curved his classes down before
Very nice guy, willing to help if you need help. He cares that you know the material and makes him self available.

I feel like Professor McKinney had a lot of potential in his lectures and he knew the content well, but honestly he would click through the content too fast. His best lecture was when he finally did the lecture without a slideshow and had audience interaction. Overall, pretty decent choice and would be great if he lectured like his last lecture.

2k1 is a hard class, but doable. Exams are difficult, but if you go to lecture and pay attention the class is passable. Make sure to put in extra time outside of just doing the homework to make sure you understand. McKinney's lectures are mostly helpful, but sometimes he answers too many questions and breezes through explanations but otherwise good

Somewhat unengaging lectures; though if you ask questions, answers are good and helpful. Responds to emails quickly and is a fairly chill professor. Lecture attendance in Fall 2024 wasn't mandatory; lecture slides were posted on Brightspace as well (other professors lectures were there too).

He definitely knew what he was talking about and was knowledgeable in what he was teaching. I do think some of the exam material was more so meant to trick us rather than testing us on what we did in homework and in lecture. Lecture notes are definitely a bit messy with some inconsistency in notations. I gave him a 2 because he really was ok.

One of my favorite profs so far. Knows a lot and is very open to questions. He makes everything super clear, be prepared to work hard though to understand the material (those who don't end up complaining the most).

Professor McKinney cares a lot about his students during lecture. He will stop and answer every question anyone has even if it makes the lecture go over time. Sometimes, this leads to us having to cover content outside of class. The 2K1 exams for Fall '24 were really easy, difficult, and mostly difficult, in that order.

Knows his stuff but isn't too great at presenting it on slides which makes it hard to follow. Sure you can say the slides are fine or alright at best but I would never say they were ever good or great. He's good with answering questions. Exams seemed to have tricks in them rather than testing what we actually did in lecture and homework.

Good lecturer, content and slides are well organized and well structured, explains concepts well and answers questions. My rating stems from the exams not being created in good faith, 8 multiple choice options, every question felt like a trick question. Exam 2 have an average in the 50s AFTER giving partial credit and removing bad questions.

Prof. McKinney is a good instructor for 2k1. Slides are well-organized and easy to follow. He does take many questions during class which sometimes disrupts the flow of the lecture, but not a big issue overall. Very helpful in office hours. Exams were difficult (typical for 2k1), but they are the same for all sections.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: He may seem like he would be a good professor to take 2k1 with based on his reviews. DO NOT BELIEVE THEM!! He had failed over half of the class the semester I took 2k1. It was so bad that he was taken off of the course the following semester. His lectures are confusing to connect to the homework problems and the exams he wrote were excessively hard
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: almost everyone FAILED

I was easily able to schedule a meeting with him when I was worried about my exam performance and I felt like he gave me good feedback that drastically improved my next exam score. My final grade is more of a testament to 2k1's difficulty rather than his ability to teach, as I feel like he was very good at explaining all of the concepts.

Good lecturer with deep understanding of the topic, kind, and thoughtfully answers questions. Semester was curved up mostly because he made the second exam too hard, but told us he would account for it. In my opinion, answers too many questions in lecture which doesn't always let him get through all of the material.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Jason McKinney loves what he does and wants his students to be successful. His experience makes him an amazing guide into the world of the music industry and his personality is great too. He's funny, charismatic, and great at what he does. 10/10 would recommend.
Very friendly, down to earth, helpful.  Genuinely tries to help students.  HOT!   Exams were a bit difficult.
Genuinely cares about his students more than any other professor I've encountered.  The subject material sucks, but you couldn't ask for a better guy to teach it to you.
He is not the greatest professor I have had but he is easy to understand and very helpful during office hours.
great guy. he never taught me,but i attended his class jus to see him smile n made my great.
Great guy, took the time to learn all our names.
Great teaching enthusiasm.  Took extra care to ensure all students learned and understood material.  Use his office hours, he's more than willing to reteach the basics to make sure you understand.
just a nice guy who seems truly concerned about his students.
Doogie Howser, Ph.D.

I hope he gets all the sleep he wants over the summer.  As for teaching, he's the man.  I wish I had him for other classes, like 302!  Oh and know what to ask before you get to his office hours.  He'll stare at you like you're an idiot if you don't.
cool guy , prof that looks normal, for now, good material presentation, if you dont understand ask , he'll spend time to explain.
was really nice and funny, was clear about what he wanted when gradin, gave a lot of feed back quickly, overall great
Made class fun. Grades easy. Try to get him if you can.

Jon was a great professor to have! I took the honors section with him, and he did a really good job on making class engaging. If you do all of the work and actually try, it shouldn't be difficult to do well. Speeches are recorded and he is willing to send the video afterwards so you can learn from your own speech, which helped me a lot.

Jon was really nice and definitely cared about all of his students. Classes were a bit dull sometimes, but overall there was usually one activity per class that would keep you engaged and help you prepare for upcoming speeches.

Great guy. Class is easy, and he gives good feedback on all major projects. Creates a fun environment that succeeds in reducing speaking stress as much as possible. Lots of extra credit available just in case you screw up a big assignment
COM isn't fun but Jon Wright is a great instructor. He makes the class as enjoyable as it can be and gives extra credit opportunities.

Just finished up COM 217 in a horrible semester, but Jon made everything as seamless as possible. Very quick on grading and understanding. Lectures are extremely succinct and helpful, grading is very clear and fair. Would highly recommend.

Jon is a good professor for taking COM217. He offers extra credit during the Summer of 2020 so it was extremely helpful. The online class was structured so for each week there were recorded lectures to watch. Also, he responds very quickly to emails! Yeah... there's reading. The textbook and is lengthy but it helps with the assignments.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Best professor, Supper easy class. There are 3 papers, 3 individual presentations, and one group presentation in this class, but it was so easy, and he gave us a lot of extra credits. I am an international student, but I still got 878/875 in this class, so don't worry about any English problem.

He is very helpful and clear on what you need to do to get an A. Lectures are short and to the point, and there are interesting activities to help improve your public speaking skills. Pretty much exactly what a COM class should be.

I definitely recommend Jon for communication courses. He's friendly and cares about students, not to mention the (usually) pretty good speaking activities. 4 speeches/presentations, but they weren't terrible. Persuasive essay was a little rough. Missing days  lower grade, must attend 85% of lectures or you fail.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Very reasonable instructor that gives great feedback and tries to be very friendly. Sometimes brings food for his class and has no issues letting classes out early. Class has some busy work but was totally manageable. Lectures are not very exciting and the book isn't really worth it, but the focus is on presentations anyways so its excusable.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Jon has a nonchalant teaching style that translates to a relaxing class. Often dismisses class early, brings food for the class, and is not too hard on grading. The coursework and readings are beneficial and not difficult to complete or understand. I appreciated the variety of assignments and learned a lot about science writing and communication.

Prof Morgan asks/makes very vague and incomplete questions for most iClickers and exam questions. All I can say is thank God he does not make the homework questions up on his own; they are from the textbook. However, the very best homework grades will still not be able to do much to offset the extremely test-heavy structure of CHE205.

Professor Morgan writes purposefully vague and almost incomplete practice and exam questions that require assumptions, guesswork, and clarifications. He taught with Prof. Liu and while Liu will make sure to re-explain a difficult topic when a student asks a question, Morgan seems to give up. He is condescending when you ask him questions.

Just be aware that about 82% of your grade in this class in exams, which are at most 4 problems (first and third were 2 questions only). Okay lecturer Helpful office hours. The questions he writes on tests are fairly confusing and you usually have to make multiple assumptions to make them work. If you know the content the exams are fair but hard.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Morgan gets a lot of slack, but I believe it is just because 205 is a difficult course. He is a caring professor, who is great at explaining difficult concepts. He is always helpful in office hours when you ask good questions. And you can always email him for additional help. I appreciate his effort and care he puts into it all.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: bro has the worst handwriting

Professor Morgan is very poor at lecturing and tends to not go into very much detail when doing example problems, skipping over key steps assuming that his students already understand how to do the problem. The homework in this class is the only way to truly learn the material, make sure you do all the problems and read the textbook to pass.

Morgan is approachable during the times I have talked to him outside of class. I understand how he would be an amazing professor for upper level ChemE classes, but if you have him for 205, be prepared to work. His exams are unreasonable and his lectures at best mildly helpful.

Dr Morgan is very very condescending both during lectures and office hours. He has refused to post answer key for the last midterm which he himself admitted had the lowest average. During the review recitation, he vented to students about his busy schedule instead of doing THE REVIEW. Hi attitude genuinely made me anxious to ask any questions

Had him for lecture with Prof Beaudoin as well. Both are very good lecturers and are leaps and bounds above some other professors. Very approachable in office hours and helpful. Homework and exams are both graded very fairly. Exams cover topics covered in lectures with no surprises.

Dr. Tanoos is an abundance of knowledge and energy which could translate very well to his course if he knew how to. The class is completely DIY and should be straight forward, but lectures provide little to no guidance. The workload is actually absurd, typically 2-3 assignments a DB and in class quiz weekly. Shy away if possible. Hybrid Course too.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: This class was taken online as a graduate course. Tanoos is an excellent professor and I was able to gain a lot of progress towards completion of my dissertation.
