He is clear with assignments and helpful with feedback. He took extra time to provide extra feedback on the nuances of academic research writing and even made himself available via zoom.

Dr. Tanoos is a passionate and goofy instructor and I really enjoyed taking his classes. He focuses more building people/workplace skills rather than just teaching straight knowledge, which I can appreciate. He is strict about attendance and participation in his class, but as long as you stay on top of those, his class should be a pretty easy A.

Excellent Professor.  Dr. Tanoos is very well organized and efficient with classtime.  He's also willing and available outside of class to help students with their careers.  I gained several unique resume boosters through the semester that will be impressive to possible employers.  Definitely one of the better classes across campus.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: A good instructor overall, the only problem with him is that he is too particular about coming on time and also very strict regarding participation

Disappointed that Professor Tanoos had representatives from z!/0n!/st companies come to class to talk about job opportunities at their establishments. One of the companies that came to speak supplies heavy machinery to I$rae/! in order to demolish Pa!estin!an homes and land. This company also specifically designed the D9 bulldozer for the /1!D!F/.
I studied abroad with Jim in Germany, and he is a great leader. He made the trip a wonderful experience.
Great Professor.

This course was fairly straightforward and manageable. All quizzes and exams are online, and the slides are posted in advance. There are occasional attendance quizzes, so regular attendance matters. The main assignment is a group presentation that makes up a significant portion of the grade. Lectures follow the slides closely.
The class is well organized and always stays on schedule, she's really friendly and approachable, i hope she could teaching other classes as well
She reads off the slides mostly, otherwise the class is very easy so no need to sweat. If you do have this professor though its one of the easiest A's you can get

The class covers basic aviation history and information about airlines. She reads from the slides and they are posted online and available to use during quizzes and exams. Very easy class that you don't need to ever go to aside from a short presentation that you do in a group. Overall boring but an easy A and nothing much to complain about.
bad experience
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: His lectures are insanely confusing! He writes random and irrelevant notes down only to realize that they're all wrong. Good luck!

Dr. Thomson started off very poorly. His lecture notes were impossible to follow(go to lecture, ignore him and take notes off of the brightspace notes). The initial material is very hard to follow and understand if this is your first ever stats class. However, he was very helpful with the group project at the end.

Some things to note about this professor: He is a very good lecturer and has worked in the field, very clear and follows the textbook in lecture, sparse amounts of office hours, difficult to email (make sure to include the course name in the subject line). Homework is difficult because it follows the textbook's difficult questions. Very good!
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Very selective availability and chose to cancel office hours multiple times. Is not helpful over email. Generally seemed to not care about helping students learn.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Solid professor. Is knowledgeable on the course. Lectures tend to be a little unorganized at times but overall are useful. He provides his own notes which make much more sense and are more condensed than the textbook. Slow at responding to emails, but is helpful in answering questions in his office hours.

Lectures were alright but his notes are disorganized. He didn't answer his email until the group project at the end of the semester. The homework is very long, and some requires the use of JMP, which labs are supposed to help you learn--but they don't. Exams aren't too bad if you do the practice exam and review formulas.
Tough Prof who knows the field. Good real life examples. Hard to get a hold of but you can do it with adding course name in subject email.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: This professor is too bad at teaching online. No replies! No respect to students!
The man chiefs

The material is very difficult, but out of the professors that taught CHE320, he was probably the strongest. He was helpful with our end of semester project and provided clear guidance and advice for it. His lectures outside of the project were not very helpful though, but they were alright.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: this dude straight up vibin tho
Co-taught with Professor Kim, Thomson's a good lecturer but doesn't respond to emails and gives hard exams. His office hours are extremely helpful.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: Lecture is half beneficial - follows textbook, Posts practice exams that are easier than exam, Class before exam is so helpful (MAKES EXAM SO MUCH EASIER), very helpful with final group project, Homework every week ( USE COMPUTER - this will save so much of your time), they grade 1-2 problems of the homework (that takes you hours each week)
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--SO MANY PAPERS

reviews.comment: Although the course material is complex, Professor Thomson makes it so much easier in his lectures. Honestly, I didn't really read my textbook, but just attending his lectures got me an A-. There is a group project at the end of the semester, and you can easily do it if you just pay attention to his project explanations.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: My least favorite professor so far and this is my junior year. His lectures are typically the theory behind the math (and not how to use it -.-), very unorganized in lecture (my notes are worthless), and rarely shows examples. Never responds to emails either. His exams are fair but studying for them is a pain.

super casual (to a fault) about everything.  his lectures feel heavily improvised and include tons of derivations that do not help with hw or exams, almost like he is trying to show off how excellent he is at math.  pray I do not end up with him for any classes going forward.

He is a good speaker. His lectures are organized. His exams do not reflect the material in the book nor do they reflect what is taught in lecture. I have gone to him for help outside of class and he either doesn't show up or smells like whiskey.  I wish I was kidding. He is the reason my GPA is lower than I want it to be.

In order to get what he is saying in lecture you have to had read the book. He just goes straight to it at light speed. Hardly ever helps besides some help in office hours. All his exams are extremely long, is like he does not want you to finish it.

Thomson is probably the best 377 professor. His lectures are typically very beneficial, but there is the occasional class where all you take from it is that he's good at math. Class certainly isn't easy.

Gives clear lectures, but they are repetitive and ultimately unrelated to homework or exam material. Changed the semester project three times during dead week. Probably the most inaccessible professor in cheme
Decent lecturer. Exams are not very representative of lectures or homework. Will not respond to email or help in any way.  Seems to not care about students.
greeat

He will NOT REPLY TO E-MAILS, so don't try. He is a good prof who wants to teach and not research. Only reason he is still at Purdue, is because he likes to teach the students. He is also BRILLIANT, but is also laid-back. He tests on the ESSENTIALS for ALL his classes.
Very helpful and clear in lectures. Exams are generally fair.
Tough class with tough concepts. Thomson is a great teacher though and easy to understand and follow. The good: two exams, no final. The bad: no curve (at least for my class there wasn't)
Good professor. Impossible to get hold of.
One of the best profs I have had. Very clear and great at explaining concepts.

Great. Clear and concise. Tests are perfect. made to make you realize a little trick and solve it easily and quickly but if you dont get the trick you lose alot of points. always has time for students. go to office hours.

Great. Clear and concise. Tests are perfect. made to make you realize a little trick and solve it easily and quickly but if you dont get the trick you lose alot of points. always has time for students. go to office hours.
Impossible to get a hold of, doesn't help students AT ALL
Awesome professor! Enough said!

Horrible. Why? 1. Barely follows the text book. 2. Doesn't release study material for exams until the day before. 3. It takes ridiculous amounts of time to receive grades. 4. Assumes that everyone goes to the office hours, and actually releases important information ONLY in the office hour (ridiculous).

Awesome professor. Unlike the typical CHE professor at Purdue, Thomson will sit down with a student for personal time if the student is struggling. He is a great lecturer and his lectures are very helpful. His homeworks are relevant and help you learn the material. Best CHE professor at Purdue, easily.

Proffesor Khan is knowledgable about python but his quizzes and exams are beyond the scope of what he teaches, and he gives you no resources to practice for quizzes. He also assigned a midterm the Thursday before spring break AND a lab the following Sunday. He didn't care that half the class had travel plans and made it an extremely difficult lab.

Dr. Khan is a good teacher; he explains everything pretty clearly and helps when you ask questions. Grading can be tough, but is mostly fair. The quizzes covered some material that wasn't covered in class, but for the most part weren't too bad. Take this class if you want to learn a lot about Python, but be prepared to work for your grade.
I really love the class. I personally learned python exactly the way I wanted to. All the assignments and quizzes reflected what is taught in class and what is needed for Industry.

I appreciate how Professor Khan comes to lecture with positive energy each time. His powerpoint slides are clear and helpful, and made it easy to follow along and complete assignments with. It's
challenging to teach such a large amount of students at one time, so I have a lot of respect for him for maintaining a high quality of lecture.

For a difficult class the professor makes sure to teach everything step-by- step and in very great detail. Professor Khan most definatley cares about his students and it is apparent he wishes nothing
but success for his students. It really helped that we worked together on assignments and projects with partners - helped my team skills.

This course helped me land my dream job in data analytics. The content is super relevant to the industry (realized this more while interviewing) and the way the professor balances theory (in lectures) and practice (through live demos in class) is awesome. Had tons of learning and am grateful to Dr. Khan for teaching this course the way he did.

Professor Khan is very knowledgeable in the course content and usually checks in with students during lectures to make sure we are following along or have any questions since the content can be
a bit technical sometimes. The assignments aid in understanding the content
as well since we are able to apply the skills talked about and demoed in class.

This professor is not at all active with homework, projects and assignments. Till the very last moment, you will be clueless as to what needs to be done. He considers that all people are python experts-whatever he teaches is so simple. You will be swamped with exams, quiz, assignments-all at once and there is no learning cos then AI to the rescue!
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Hilarious--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: This class was very well organized and I appreciated all the live coding the professor did in class! I learned so much better in this course than I did in my first Python course, and I'm so thankful!

I believe the professor worked hard to teach us about Python. He took the time to ensure we understood the subjects he was talking about, and why the code we previewed did what it did. This
class has taught me a lot about python that I have saved and can implement later in life. The professor is a great guy who wants to see us succeed.

Dr Khan is the typical bad professor. He shows little care for his students and it is shown through his stale lectures. He assigns assignments inconsistently. Some assignments were given 2 weeks before they were due, others were 3 days. Everyone bombs the exams and quizzes and relies on the curve. The scores in his course reflect that no one learns

Professor Khan was very knowledgeable yet equally disorganized. Quizzes and assignment dates are not outlined in the syllabus and are seemingly assigned at random. Lectures are very informative but extremely boring. I sent him three emails over the semester and received zero replies. Classmates have had similar issues through email and Piazza.

Dr. Khan is a really nice guy and a great professor. 388 isn't super test heavy compared to other classes such as math, but it is a pretty difficult course. He teaches it well and if you pay attention you will learn!!!
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: The most disorganized and unprofessional professor I have had at Purdue. He was always late to class, no class plan, changed grading policies, quiz formats, and exam structures halfway through the semester. Communication was poor, completely unreachable by email and Piazza. Didn't have a course schedule and was rude/hostile to students. Avoid Him!
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He refuses to make his office hours work for the 50% of his students that all have the same course from 3-4:15pm (3-4pm office hours). It's almost dead week and this has not been resolved. Inaccurate and vague tentative schedule, good luck getting an email response, and coding questions on quizzes and exams are done in brightspace on lockdown.

Disrespectful and condescending to students asking questions. Locked students out of class for being late yet shows up late to his own class very frequently. Incredibly un-engaging lectures so get ready to teach yourself to code. Assigned both a final project and a final exam during the designated quiet week. Poor, disorganized professor.

Very generic lectures that do not keep students engaged. Makes it very hard to be involved in a power hour class. Homework is much more in-depth than lectures, leaving students to wonder how to complete their tasks. Gives little to no time on quizzes and exams where you are expected to write code. Poorly planned class structure as well.

Dr. Khan is an outstanding professor who made learning Python for data analytics both fun and practical. His clear explanations and real-world examples kept me engaged and motivated throughout the course. He's incredibly supportive and always willing to help - his passion for teaching really shines through.

Professor Khan is the worst professor I've ever had. He is late to his own class. He is harsh/belittling when people ask questions. He doesn't respond to emails. He responds to questions with annoyance. He grades incredibly late considering 70% of the exams are multiple choice. He has boring lectures where he makes mistakes. He needs a new career.
This prof is Not at all reachable, doesnt have clear path in class. Doesnt even bother replying to emails. Not fit to be a professor

Not very helpful or respectful to students. When I asked for suggestions regarding the midterm, I was roasted in class and accused of not studying well. He refused to provide a sample solution for the sample questions. Meanwhile, his own slides were not helpful as the questions on the exams were much more difficult than the lectures.

I really like the way he teaches, I think he is very good at what he does, but you have to go to his class everyday, yes he doesn't provide a schedule but he answers questions in class and his office hours are super helpful.

Probably the most disorganized and terrible professor I have had at Purdue. He does not have a course schedule and seems to make it up as he goes. The HW is incredibly vague but will have unknown grading criteria. He also seems to hate helping students or clarifying his assignments outside of class. His lectures are boring and difficult to follow.

This is unfortunately my 3rd time having Prof Khan, each time gets worse. He is very unclear in how many and when assignments are due, all instructions in assignments are disorganized. He fails to communicate with students outside class, doesn't grade in a timely manner, and is rude to students when they ask questions. + He's a bad teacher!!!!!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Never again
best of best

I don't understand how people are giving Dr. Kanakri a negative review. He is without a doubt one of the best professors in the Indy campus. An inspiration for anyone planning to go into embedded systems and taking ECE 36200. Get ready to work hard in his class (since it is a difficult course) but the results will also be rewarding!

The course is challenging so allotting plenty of time to complete the homework is necessary. He cares a lot about his students. I have had him for another class and he was great there too. 

The frustrations from other students could be from the course material itself and less on the professor.
can't teach. Avoid even if it delays your graduation
Even sydney sweeney can't convince me to take a class with this guy
Very accessible outside of class. Genuinely cares about his students and wants the best for each one of them.

I saw two ppl saying he is a great professor, either they are lying to themselves or got the class confused cause we didn't have any peer reviews. This class was horrible and the professor succeeded to make it the worst possible version. Not available at all to students
I saw two ppl saying that he is a great
EVEN IF SOMEONE PAYS ME TO TAKE A COURSE WITH HIM, I WILL RETURN THE MONEY. WORST PROFESSOR I HAVE EVER HAD IN PURDUE.

I appreciate the peer-review system and how we got the opportunity to do multiple revisions before submitting our final essay. The grading for the assignments was very well communicated. We knew what to expect and how to earn full marks. Professor Haitham responded to all of our comments and emails, that was very useful. He is also extremely kind
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Haitham is a great educator. I like his classes very much. He is always available for us.

If you want to learn or want a professor who cares about you, drop this course with him. I have never seen a professor wishing for his students to be unsuccessful as him. No communication, multiple wrong answers. Doesn't teach well. 
(Don't tell me I didn't warn you)
AVOID, his lectures are bad. Tests are wrong and he won't reply to his emails. Basically no communication with him. AVOID AVOID AVOID

The professor doesn't communicate outside of class. Even during class he will ask you to mute yourself. Only TAs would answer us and the exams had multiple wrong questions. Worst curve ever. Doesn't teach well and doesn't care about students. Avoid even if he is your last option

I orginally passed 2k2 last semester but wanted to take 2k2 again to better my understanding. I don't mind if a profesor's teaching is bad or not, because you can learn the material yourself. The main issue was the incorrect test questions that were unfair, along with poor communication, especially the professor leaving us on read end of sem 😭
bad, VERY bad.
Does not communicate well about homework or after grading exams.
The only thing I can say about this class is just how unfair it was. Hard tests, barely any curve, doesn't respond to emails nor students concerns.
Not very in touch with students and mostly communicates through TAs. Lectures are very dense and confusing which would be fine if not for the harshness of the course.

ECE 20002 is hard enough without Dr. Kanakri making it worse. Lectures are flat and unengaged, with terrible communication, he changed the schedule without notice, taught final exam content the day before, and refused to justify grading on disputed questions on the final. Avoid if you want clarity or fairness, or an instructor who teaches at all.

I never felt strongly enough about a professor to resort to RateMyProfessor, yet here I am. Dr. Bellisario gives unclear grading criteria for recitation and group assignments. She acts like you can read her mind about what she wants out of your group and then proceeds to be extremely condescending and critical of your work. Switch professors!

She gives way too much homework for a one-credit class. The weekly nature journals are a waste of time. Interrupts you in the middle of presentations to give critiques. Regardless, this class is still easy AF but try to take it with a different prof if possible.
She was very clearly passionate about naturalism, and was super nice, but incredibly unorganized. Be careful you wont know when stuff is due sometimes. Incredibly nice and awesome professor though.

The grading criteria for group assignments tended to be unclear, but it didn't really matter in the grand scheme of things because of the lenient grading in 19902. Beyond the assignments, the topic was interesting and she taught it passionately. She's kind, and the readings aren't long at all.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Gives good feedback--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I took her naturalism class and although she was very passionate about the topic, I don't think the class was taught well. She is extremely disorganized and never told us when assignments were due. She was very critical about group presentations, but didn't provide clear criteria for us to go off of. The class was kind of a joke honestly.

She was clearly very passionate about the subject matter (naturalism) but the class itself was so disorganized it was hard to be engaged and know what was going on/when stuff was due. Pretty consistently would contradict herself about certain things and then blame us for making a mistake based on what she said intially.

She is very passionate about the topic, but very disorganized. Its impossible to know when anything is due or what you need to do for next class. She grades everything last minute. She is not a good representation of the honors college.
Very disorganized and confusing on assignment information.

Dr. Bellisario is very passionate about the topic (silence/extinction). Grading criteria were unclear for many assignments and the final project, but this could be due to HONR19902's new format this year. She offered several extra credit and revision opportunities as a result of the unclear criteria. Overall, a good professor for 19902.

This professor is disorganized, doesn't have set standards, and will make getting your honors seminar requirement completed very difficult. This professor was constantly changing her expectations and always talked condescendingly when people had questions.
For the sake of future students, she needs a new job.

Teacher has a remarkably difficult time speaking english for having lived in the united states for 20 some years. She moves through content very quickly and often struggles to finish all of the slides before class ends.

The class itself wasn't too bad, but the lectures are a little hard to understand, especially when there's a lot of information crammed into one slide. Attendance is also required for every lecture, so you can only miss two lectures.
Must watch videos outside of lectures to stay afloat. Hard to understand her. Final was pretty easy.

The lectures are boring, but the best way to learn the topics are through the homework anyway. My only critique is that the labs are unnecessarily complicated. Most of the time the homework is just that weeks lab with a few extra steps.

I have a lots of programming experience and am a CS major so CS 176 was pretty manageable. If you do not have programming experience, this class is pretty hard and its good to learn some python before hand. The lectures are pretty useful and she pretty much tells you everything you need to know for the exam.

Worst professor I have ever had. Her slides contain several typos and misinformation. I brought her slides to the TA because they weren't making sense and the TA agreed that they were completely wrong. She shows up late for lectures and will yell the whole time. She will scold students for leaving for any reason including using the bathroom.

If you cannot understand a standard Chinese accent (for whatever reason), don't take this class. If you are normal, she is not a bad lecturer remotely. If you choose to pay attention, you will learn everything that you need to. Stop blaming your failures on a lecturer.

This professor is absolutely terrible. Reads directly off the lecture slides, and if anyone asks a question, her response is to simply re-read the material louder. I learned more from watching python tutorials on YouTube than I did from her. She commits academic terrorism...do not take!!!

This 100-level Python class was supposed to be "beginner-friendly," but Xiaojin Liu will personally escort you to the ninth circle of coding hell. I have never been so utterly confused by a class in my academic career. Lectures are mandatory. Xiaojin Liu performs academic terrorism. It's 50 minutes of her reading slide answers aloud. Avoid Liu.

This is easily the worst class I have ever had the misfortune of taking. The "teacher", if you can call her that, genuinely seems to hate her own students. How you could yell at the mic every single day at 9:30 am for no good reason is simply beyond me. The lectures and labs tend to be more gibberish useless busywork than the slightest useful task.

The only problem that I had with her is that i dont always understand her language. However, I think her slides explain stuff well. In general, this course exams are not typical coding questions. You NEED to study past exams and study the textbook because a lot of material comes from there.

The professor teaches at a nice pace and uses lots of examples. Attendance is mandatory no matter the instructor you have. She barely has an accent (especially compared to other professors), and you can easily get a good grade if you review slides and participate in labs.
Gave me a migraine each lecture, is obnoxious and reads off script and asks unintelligent questions that they seem to believe helps students learn but only serve to confuse the lecture hall

Honestly shes not as shi d as people say but her lectures really are not very great. Shes not understandable, she has an accent but its pretty clear honestly its just she only reads off the slides and it can be pretty boring.

Ok this is my first prof review because I have to put some respect on Xiaojin's name. I have literally 0 c experience and I found her lectures very understandable. Her accent is really not bad at all and I have a really hard time with accents usually. Her lectures are actually engaging and I find myself not even looking at the clock. Great Prof

The worst professor I've ever had no competition. She yells at the mic and gives me a headache. I dread going to her lecture every time I do. Also has a very thick Chinese accent and really hard to understand.
Is there a way to program her so her teaching isn't bot mode
Inflexible and imposes assigned seating for lectures which are mandatory.

Terrible prof, attendance is mandatory even though it doesn't help reinforce material. Simply reads off slides and her way of trying to answer questions is simply reading the material louder. Very unclear lab and HW prompts and the autograder doesn't make it any easier. Don't take her class if you don't have to.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The worst professor I've had. Her lectures are mandatory, which i can understand because if they weren't no one would show up. You would get better results by spending the semester watching python coding tutorials on YouTube.

Easily the worst professor I've ever had the misfortune of having. Lectures slides, quizzes, homeworks, and even exams are always riddled with typos and mistakes, the TA's are all useless, and the professor is barely coherent, yells into the mic at 8 in the morning, shows up late, and poorly communicates important information. Avoid if possible.

I had no coding experience before this class and it was so hard to go through can't believe i clutched an A but my god presentations, practice exam and even the final exam are riddled with mistakes. Attendance is mandatory and having her in the morning screaming into the mic is just a recipe for a headache

Do not take from her if you can. She has you going to every lecture and uses iclicker, which would not be a problem if she actually taught the material. The powerpoints that she has are full of typos and do not actually explain how the code works. The exams were difficult but I would say she was very transparent with what is going to be on the exam

I felt this course is not freindly for beginners.
+) I asked TA to fix my quiz grade (bug), but they said I must email professor.
But she is not responding to my email for 2 weeks, so I got 0 for a lecture quiz even I attended lecture.
+) They only have one previous exam to prepare final; even some answer was wrong
DO NOT TAKE IF YOU VALUE YOUR MENTAL HEALTH AND SANITY AVOID AT ALL COSTS!!!!!!!

1 of the most horrible classes coming from a person with no coding experience. She's pretty unprofessional (late to class sometimes and has slides with incorrect information that are INCREDIBLY frustrating to deal with) and hw and labs sometimes use concepts not covered which is time consuming to deal with and just so annoying

For a person who has barely coded in Python and wanted this class to help lay the foundations, the professor didn't help with anything. She always screams at 8:30 in the morning and doesn't show how the code works, only reads the materials of the slides. On top of that, the midterm has an average of just over 50% showing how she teaches.

She posted maybe 1 due date for the homework on bright space. Her lectures are terrible and it just feels like she is yelling at the class. Most weeks she doesn't even get through all the of material that is needed to do the homework and lab.
Awful, avoid at all costs

i would not recommend this class to anyone, especially if you do not know how to code at all. the professor is loud at 8:30 am in the morning, screaming into the microphone and requiring everybody to attend. class is heavily based on her powerpoint slides that do not really do much. ive had to learn on my own, hoping for the best. midterm was crazy

lectures were mandatory and subpar at best. spent most of the classes just yelling, student body is heavily skewed as some people have a lot of coding experience whereas others have none so our midterm results were all over the place. wouldn't recommend, avoid if u can. prof is very nice and understanding just not very great at being a teacher.
umm she's smth

She has provided us with NO guidelines for our midterms, the content is not helpful at all. Even the TA's don't know what is going on more than half of the time. She does NOT reply to ANY emails, and neither do the TA's, she doesn't talk during lectures, she SCREAMS.

Honestly, this is not a beginner friendly class.
Just Awful
Worst class I've taken at Purdue.

She has a heavy accent that is very hard to understand. The class is at 8:30 in the morning, and the mic is too close to her face. I can't hear anything she says in class. The teacher rages when students leave, repeats unnecessary things, and stutters like she's trying to sing rap god. Vote her out.
TERRIBLE DON'T TAKE CS CHANGE MAJORS RIGHT AWAY PLEASE GOD DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS PLEASE IF YOU WANT TO SAVE UR SANITY AND UR MENTAL HEALTH AND UR EARS.

Heavy accent aside, she makes no sense in class. She reads off the slides, and never answers questions in office hours. I watched One Piece in the lectures and had a blast. You could look up everything in the textbook and do better than rely on her lectures. Her lectures are required because otherwise, no one would go.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I enjoyed her class and I leaned a lot. Will definitely take another class with her again.

CS176 is taken by non-CS majors & AI majors. I had programming experience before so many concepts were familiar to me. This class is ran in a similar way to other CS classes here (required attendance for lectures/labs and expected to use a lot of submissions/test cases for the hws). Noticeable increase in difficulty in the 2nd half. She's friendly

This professor is very hard to understand and does not elaborate on the course material in a meaningful way. There are also very few clear instructions on assignments and starting the course generates an abysmal feeling of unpreparedness which does not change much with time. If you need to take CS 159 at Purdue pray you get a different lecturer.
Professor Liu is great in explaining concepts. The class is very well organized. Grading is fair.

She's not horrible, but the pacing of this class is bad.  I have never coded in my life, so the pacing in this class was rough, I do not feel she really understood what it's like to never see have seen code, then immediately have to write a program of nums from -100k to 100k.  Very nice, decent amount of extra credit, and the class isn't too hard.

Lectures are utterly useless yet still required. Actually going to labs is utterly useless yet attendance is required or else you will get a 0% on the lab. If you have a question, you have to use this garbage forum website that the professor doesn't even check, so it's a lot of TA's saying they'll ask the professor for you. Take a diff CS course
Can't teach
The only good thing I can say is that it was a nice lecture to take a nap in because attendance is mandatory and you don't miss anything by sleeping.
Terrible. What more can I say

I ended up having to utilize my academic accommodations and this professor fought me about them. Additionally, I needed to take an incomplete for the class and the professor was terrible with communication and timeliness. The homework takes ages to do and the grading software is very strict and doesn't make sense if you've coded before.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: The class itself is a waste but easy. Attendance is required but reading the unnecessarily long slides is equally as helpful as her lectures. I paid attention maybe 30% of the time. There are "discussions" which are just clunky group assignments. Sometimes hw or exam questions were worded in a confusing way which was annoying but generally easy.

The class was quite interesting, and the homework and tests were easy, however, I feel like they could sometimes be worded a little better. Attendance is very important, as she does Hotseat every class, and the points add up at the end.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--EXTRA CREDIT--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: If you can, pick Crum as a professor because he writes the exams and the course book. Liu makes her own slides that do not follow along with the course notes book you have to buy for the class. Lecture attendance is required, and I found the lectures unhelfpul. Exams are a heavy part of grade. Use Boiler Exams to practice and you will do well.

I actually really liked the way that this professor taught; however, I took this class online so my experience may be different than others. This class was kind of difficult because I have never coded before, and I was put into a group that barely put in any effort. Boilerexams is definitely a life saver for this class!
Cannot understand her lectures at all
She is incomprehensible during the required lectures; spend them doing homework instead and use Boilerexams to practice for tests as she just reads out of the textbook during class.

I didn't think much of her as a lecturer until we had Crum substitute for us and he was infinitely better. Upon reflection I realized that her slides are hard to follow and don't really follow the notes packet you are supposed to buy at the beginning of the semester. She's not terrible, but do what you can to get Crum or another professor.

Gotta be one of the worse professors here. While all the other professors teaching CS159 use a packet to go over all the information, she creates her own slide shows that exclude crucial pieces of information that will appear on the exams. Her presentation slides are filled with giant paragraphs of information, making them useless to follow.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Beware of pop quizzes--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Explains curriculum very poorly and lectures are not interesting and hard to follow. Very boring overall but CS159 isn't too hard in general
Does not know what she is saying in lectures. Repeats unnecessary things. Would not have her again.

She's not as bad as some of the reviews are saying, but definitely not good either. If you can switch, I'd recommend it. She reads of the slides and sometimes doesn't finish. She also doesn't allow people to leave early either.

This class genuinely feels like trying to retake 1st grade math but you have to use the 27-step process they teach kids now. "IF" statements? Never heard of her. Leaving during class? She will call you out in front of everyone. Definition of a level 100 professor on a power trip.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: The professor read straight off the lectures which would be 100 slides per week. It was extremely hard to pay attention because her teaching is pretty boring. You have to go to class, which isn't worth it because you could just read off of the slides. You have one assignment per week along with a group project every 2 weeks.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.--Group projects

reviews.comment: DONT ever take this class if you think it is an easy GPA booster, while you have zero knowledge of how the Internet/computer works like you do not have a desktop built by yourself or you don't even use Discord lmao.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Lectures don't teach what is on the labs and the exams. Labs/Homework are unnecessarily difficult for an introductory course. Exams don't really access your coding skills but your ability to read poorly written code that is designed to trick you. Liu is a sweet lady, but she doesn't teach the material and instead just reads off the slides.
Read off slides that can be read within 29 minutes
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--So many papers

reviews.comment: Lecture Hotseats at the beginning of almost every class. She is very unhelpful when it comes to asking questions and she verbally argued with a student when they gave sound reasoning behind their answer. She is almost always late or barely on time to her own lectures. She has several errors in her many slides and homework assignments.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Group projects--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Liu feels like a nice person but does not qualify to be a professor. Half of the time, she discovers mistakes in her slides during the lectures and starts fixing them right in front of student during class time. She seems lost at times and just reads the slides. She had to make attendance mandatory for students to show up to her class. Do not take
Read off slides that could be read within 30 minutes

Lectures are cryptic, and difficult to follow, moreover, the homework is exceedingly difficult for the content taught in class, which is in itself barebones. Students are forced to rely on their parents/eternal sources to teach code. Midterms will be a major pain if you are a beginner, prior experience is a must. She's nice, but a bad instructor.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Lectures are poorly connected to assignments, and lab coding sessions. Code is rarely, if ever, taught in the class, and if taught, is quickly shoved under the rug. It is highly difficult to succeed without prior knowledge in the class. Moreover, the professor is somewhat difficult to understand. She is nice enough, but her teaching falls short.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Group projects--Caring

reviews.comment: Mandatory lectures that make no sense and have no correlation to assignments. Nearly every lecture she's late and her powerpoint is full of typos and errors in her code. When asked a question she gives a blanket statement about an entirely different CS topic or says to see her after class but leaves asap. Worst lecturer I've had.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Homework and Labs have no relation to what you learn in the lectures. The lectures are beyond boring and I think 90% of the class sleeps through them. I haven't learnt a single thing so far from this class other than from my classmates.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Group projects--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Lectures focused heavily on review content and never got to the important/ complex topics. Lectures didn't help prepare you for the exams, just stick with watching Crum's lectures and supplemental videos for this class. She's really sweet but came to lectures unorganized and had errors in her slides.

Professor Jin is such a nice person & is very helpful. Her lectures are informational & most of the info is in them, with little information needed for exams from the book. She cares about her students & always encouraged students to contact her if they need help & stayed after class. The points were distributed evenly amongst assignments & exams.
Professor Liu is a very easygoing professor, and always makes class interesting :)
Very smart professor, great lectures

Calling the final project a "mini project" despite it including a 15-page paper, presentation, and video walk-through, is a slap in the face to students who spent days and weeks on this project. Final exam also covered many things that weren't mentioned in class and was extremely difficult. Prof also laughs at students for getting answers wrong.
Great guy. He really cares about the students and the subject matter. Easily one of the most applicable courses (and projects) that I've had at Purdue. He does take attendance though.
terrible professor. DO NOT TAKE HIS CLASS.

She teaches using mostly slides, walking us through equations and derivations of other equations from those. She uses some demonstrations and interacts with the class well. She is not overly serious and can be fun.

Maybe I'm slow but the lectures were kind of hard to grasp, especially the derivation heavy ones, so I ended up not paying attention off during the derivation parts since you don't need them for exam (I know, bad mentality). I ended up self studying the entire course myself which is not fun. On the bright side she's energetic and passionate!

Took the professor for PHYS221, not a bad class but understanding the formula sheet goes a long way on the exams. Urba is a very good professor who you can feel cares heavily about the subject and teaches it very well. However, some of the slides are a bit confusing but overall not bad. Uses iClicker for attendance.

Good prof. Sometimes lectures get really heavy into derivations that you may not need but it's still good to see how the equations you will use are derived. Looking at the slides before lectures and writing down some key points is very helpful. Try to as well as you can on the midterms since they are weighted more than the final.

Professor Urba did a great job at teaching this course. While the slides do include derivations, they are very important to build intuition to the real physics at play. She stresses key ideas over memorizing formulae, which is the best way to truly learn the material. Exams were very fair and the course is very well structured. Totally recommend.

Prof. Urba is such a kind lecturer, but at times her lectures are boring and hard to engage with. The exam were extremely difficult, but the content is also just hard to learn through a lecture format. There was about a 2-3% curve, but our exam averages were also pretty low. Lectures are mandatory attendance except for a few absences.

Urba is a great lecturer. She has an accent but it is bearable and understandable. However, her slides are very derivation heavy which can confuse many. The class itself is fairly difficult compared to 172.

Great 241 lecturer. Had a decent number of derivations, but they weren't crucial for the exams. Covered all contents at a good pace with excellent slides posted to BSpace. Know the equations on the sheets and know the homeworks well to succeed on exams

Urba is an excellent lecturer. She has derivation-heavy lectures that often go beyond the scope of the tests. The homework and review packets are better representations of the questions that are in the tests. Tests were often miserable as nearly every single (multiple choice) question would have both the correct answer and the same answer negated.

Urba is very clear in lecture with good explanations. There is iClicker for points every lecture, but it's a small percent of your grade so it's not the end of the world if you miss some. Mid to low exam averages, but fair otherwise.
For phys 221. Switch out of Xie's section and into this one. I was in Xie's section but watched all of Urbas videos. She is the best. Very caring, good at explaining

Prof Urba is a great professor who knows the content very well. Sometimes it can be a bit confusing in lecture, but as long as you do the homeworks and don't cheat yourself it shouldn't be too bad of a class. Make sure to practice homework problems along with the test review guides as the review guides don't always reflect what will be on the test.
Enjoyed her lectures and she was a reasonable professor. Answered questions and gave help to those who asked.
Professor Urba's class was really relaxing to be in. She explained the concepts well and made sure we understoof everything. The class was a breeze with her teaching the concepts.

Professor Urba is a caring professor, yet has a lot to improve in terms of teaching. The overall lecture is very hard to grasp in class, as she goes very fast. The questions in the class are nothing like the questions in the exam. The exams are very hard, and the exam average was only 60% even though attendance is mandatory.
reviews.rating_tags: Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: She would often move too quickly and read directly off slides while lecturing. Concepts were hard to understand as a result. I found myself having to study a lot outside of class. In addition, time management was an issue. She would start the (graded) lecture quizzes after the class had already ended- I was lucky my next class was close.

Urba is a great professor who actually cares about students. She explains the material really well, always replies to emails, helps students outside of class, and shows the derivation process. She talks very fast and covers a lot of material, so sometimes I had to go back and watch recordings of lectures.
Prof. Urba is definitely a good choice for PHYS 272. Her lectures are intuitive and easy to understand. She is also very humorous. She is caring to her students.

Professor Urba does a great job at explaining the content in PHYS241, but the lectures are extremely fast. You'll definitely have to spend time outside of class to digest the material. If you need help, Professor Urba is extremely helpful, and she really cares about your understanding of the content.

Urba is a good lecturer, she gauges the class, does a good job conveying content, is very nice and very helpful outside of class. Her main downside is for some slides she just reads formulas off slides for a large chunk of the lecture which puts a lot of people to sleep, but its physics so its fair.
Very helpful outside of class. Lectures were very theory and derivation heavy, but out of lecture resources help understanding. Good at answering questions and even following up outside of class.

Lecture attendance was no longer required through iClicker which was nice. I found lectures helpful, but you could understand most of the material by looking at the slides and doing the homework. Professor Urba was great at answering questions quickly and accurately, as well as being available outside of class.

Learned the content through her despite not being in her section. Solid pace & consistency in highlighting essential equations/concepts from slides. HW is ANNOYING, but she will share, to all sections, solutions for tricky problems. Communicative in emails & for questions, she was a straightforward & solid lecturer that I'm glad to have pivoted to.

iClicker attendance and participation mandatory. She's a decent lecturer. It's not too hard to understand her through her accent, and she successfully executes the demos unlike most physics lecturers. Always looking at the students to answer questions so you'll never have your hand up for long. I see her stay after every time for questions also.
Professor Urba is a great lecturer and makes a boring topic interesting. The class itself is a bore, but she tries her best to engage everyone.
Easy-to-follow lectures, slides are informative, answers questions about HW and will send an email explaining the answer to any questions that are particularly hard
I really liked her lectures
A very nice teacher who helps her students to do well on exams with the study sessions during class.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Stat 301 is by no means an easy class and it will take alot of work to get a good grade. This is one of those classes you need to watch every lecture, take notes, understand the material throughout the semester and stay on top of your work. I thought Urba taught the class well, it's just difficult material to teach but I thought she did well.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Urba embodies what Purdue education is. As someone who has never taken a STATS course, Urba did a tremendous job at teaching this material to students. She would kindly answer anyone's question slowly and in depth. Her lectures are all written by her and are an excellent resource for exams. Labs are tedious at times but are doable.

She was one of the best professors I have had. I never took a statistics course, even in high school so I was skeptical. With her lectures, I was able to understand the material rather easily and did not have to put much time outside of class studying the material. If you pay attention in lecture, you will do just fine. She is a sweetheart.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures--Caring

reviews.comment: Prof. Urba is very genuine with her students and clearly does her best to help them succeed. She gives "quizzes" that help you assess your own knowledge and count for completion/attendance. Her lectures flow nicely from one to another and she does well explaining SPSS software to non-tech people. She is always smiling and will reexplain hard topics

Dr. Urba is an amazing professor. She really cares about her students and gives helpful advice at the beginning of each class. She teaches the material very well and makes it easier for students to understand.

Laura Urba is a great professor. I had never taken a stat class before, but she made it easy. She clearly explains the topics. She gives "quizzes" which you get credit for just by coming to class, but they help review for the exams. She does her best to prepare you, and she is very approachable if you have any questions.

Dr. Urba is amazing, she honestly cares about her students understanding and being able to apply the information she teaches in class. Her exam averages are usually higher than that of other sections.

She's such a nice professor! Before class, she always made sure to talk to students and give us all advice. Lectures are amazing. Exams allow a cheat sheet (front and back) and her lecture slides are impeccable to study from. Glad she was my professor because she inspired me to take on a minor in statistics.

I love this woman! She is so funny and super nice! She is a great instructor who really knows what she is talking about. She always tried to make time to meet with me outside of class and outside of her office hours.

I really like Prof. Urba. She does have a bit of an accent but it's not very distracting after a bit. She explains things pretty well and is very open to questions. Attendance is "mandatory", but you only have to be there half of the time she takes attendance and she tells you ahead of time when those are. My lab TAs were also very helpful.
reviews.rating_tags: ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: most boring lectures ever, genuinely want to kms everytime im forced to attend, not even necessary u can learn everything on ur own

The exams were a bit hard, but there was no final. Had to get a Raspberry Pi for the lab but the project with it was easy if you researched a little bit. He does talk a lot, and most of what he does explain isn't as thorough on his slides so its hard to go back to after lecture. The extra credit is pretty easy to do tho.

He is not bad but he is a YAPPER...you will not get anything from the lecture. He knows what he is talking about but it hard to catch on so just review on your own. As for labs, quite confusing and you will have to buy a raspberry pi. the TA are also quite harsh on grading so my best advice is to go to office hours for help.

Prof. Hiday is an amazing lecturer and most people who rate him poorly are just mad they actually had to try. Be aware exams are relatively tough but won't decide your grade completely. There is a buffer window of around 3% extra credit which is extremely obtainable.
While I do believe the prof is very knowledgeable, he has got to be the most boring lecturer ever. Literally reads of slides with way too many bullet points and tests on the most niche topics ever

Overall enjoyed the content taught in this class. While lectures went a bit fast, they were manageable as long as you looked over notes after class. I enjoyed how he would explain each concept in depth if you had any questions. The labs were very interesting and included fundamental computer topics that were touched on in lectures.
Worst course ever

Great professor for CNIT176, though he does move through a lot of material very fast in lectures, so taking notes is recommended. Good sense of humor and very accommodating, though the TAs for the lab sections grade according to their own scales, and some grade much harder than others.

The professor himself isn't too bad. The lectures can be boring but he answers every question and doesn't hesitate to clarify anything students are confused about. The course can feel overwhelming and confusing, mostly because it covers a wide range of topics only at a surface level. Labs are graded harshly, but it wasnt something I struggled with.

He's clearly passionate and knowledgeable about IT, but he's really hard to follow during lectures. Labs, in my opinion, were pretty easy but if you're not careful, you can mess up one small thing and fail them. How well you do in this class is heavily dependent on your TA.

This class is so boring and the labs can be super difficult if you don't have any experience with Linux. Labs are basically learn it yourself and the exams are pretty difficult but not worth much. However, you can easily get a passing grade if you do all of the assignments but the grading is pretty strict.

I NO prior experience going in and I was shell shocked. That said, I learned a lot (I still am getting an A) but NOT from his lectures. Prof is smart but hard to follow. Exams are not worth much, but be prepared to do a lot of extra work to learn the content if not familiar, especially for lab when working with Linux. TA's were a hit or miss

Larry seems honestly like a nice guy and he knows what he's talking about but he talks... and talks...and talks. It's hard to grasp the concepts and you're better off just teaching yourself. Exams are insanely lecture heavy but you can legit fail them and still do well in this class. TA's are straight up awful and unhelpful.

The problem is not the course itself, the content is quite interesting. But the way it is set up makes me want to drop out of his class. Beware of tough TA grading in labs and a very messy course. On the other hand, the professor knows what he is talking about but rambles on. You'd think an intro cnit course can be properly structured.
Great professor. Great course. Great reintroduction to a major I switched out of then back into.
bad professor

Lectures are extremely hard to grasp especially for an introductory class. TAs are inconsistent and can grade harshly on labs with little to no guidance. Exams are worth barely anything which makes it easier to get a good grade in the class

Class is easy. There are multiple opportunities for extra credit, the assignments overall don't require a lot of work and are quick to complete, and the information is pretty basic in terms of computer architecture.
prof hiday is my spirit animal. hes great. don't listen to all the freshmen salty and ungrateful that they had to try in 176. i would've killed to have hiday as my 176 prof

Hiday just reads off the slides and it is very hard to grasp material. Labs have little to no correlation between what is being taught in class and exams. TAs are harsh and inconsistent with grading. Would not take again.

I attended all the lectures but still felt lost most of the time. The class is an introductory class, but it didn't feel like one to someone with no previous experience. There's 2 midterms, and the timing of the 2nd one was so poor. Exams are very lecture-heavy. During lecture he reads off the slides with no extra information added on.

Professor Hiday is nice but he just talks and talks. It was hard to pay attention or even understand the lectures, and he speaks as if everyone already has a basic understanding of how a computer works. The labs don't entirely line up with what is being taught in lecture. Overall, the class is pretty easy, but I taught myself the material.

-Hard to understand in lecture. 
-Labs and lectures dont really go together like in other classes. 
-An 80 and a 89 are apparently both B so you dont need to try that hard.

The class overall wasn't that bad. Ended with an A and I didn't know anything about computer architecture coming into the class. For the lecture material I strongly strongly strongly recommend getting the optional textbook. I realized that's where he gets all his content and images from so it's a good guide. Lecture's boring but he's nice.

CNIT 176 was a pretty average class; lectures tended to be boring, and the labs were pretty fun. Hiday was a nice professor, but I felt it was hard to understand his lectures because he didn't explain his lecture slides well. The exams were hard since there was not any review material online. Be sure to follow the formats the TAs want.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Hiday is a solid professor, but your opinion on the class will likely depend on your TAs and experience working with computer architecture. Hiday mostly just reads off the slides, but is always open to talk to students and answer questions. 176 is pretty unorganized, and your final grade will likely depend on if your TA is chill (Shoutout Nathan)

Hard class, definitely not an intro. Second Exam was extremely difficult and did not have anything to do with the notes. Takes a lot of outside work, you need google for this class. Would not take again

When I took it was his first time teaching this course. Read off the slides and occasionally went on tangents that were incredibly difficult to follow. Lots of busy work. Coursework and tests are easy enough though.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: He was a pretty good lecturer, even though he just read the slides sometimes. Some exam and lab information weren't covered in class, Google is your friend. He released grades for materials very late. The TAs took forever to grade labs and could grade harshly sometimes. Some extra credit was available on labs, attendance, and homework which helped.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Hiday is a cool dude, but his TAs ruined the course. The TAs graded unnecessarily harshly and were never on time with their grading. Over 70% of the course's labs & assignments were graded the week before final grades were due. Had to go to his office hours to appeal a wildly unfair grade, but he was very understanding and fixed it promptly.

He just read off the slides in class it felt like, there was minimal expansion on the material. There was stuff on the exams that wasn't in the notes or anything he said in class. The labs weren't too bad.

I liked Hide's class, but this was the first time he has taught 176. Sometimes the lectures didn't match the labs, so take good notes and ask TA's for assistance! Lots of in-class participation, but if you skip class you can still submit it afterwards. Don't recommend skipping though, a lot of info is shared verbally instead of on the slides.

this class wasn't bad at all until the final exam which is 50% of your total grade, 60% if you missed an online exam throughout the semester. Minimal homework assignments and the exams prior to the final aren't worth many points towards the final grade. I felt like this is structured for you to fail and lectures are as boring as they come.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He was an okay lecturer but his course was pretty crypto-focused. I didn't feel like he covered the overall subject of investment management very thoroughly. The way he graded the course by giving extremely easy homework and assigning grades based on rank meant that your final grade basically depended only on the final.

Professor Liu is a decent teacher, the content of this course is pretty hard, and it is up to you to keep up with him and read class materials. He leaves the classroom super quick after lectures, so you'll have to be fast if you want to ask anything. We had 3 exams and 3 homework, all are online except the cumulative final.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Liu is the best professor I have ever had at Purdue! The content could be challenging but he did an outstanding job explaining the materials! The exams were straightforward so never give up!

The most confusing Management class ever. He only reads his slides and make the lectures very boring. The final practice test has no help at all. If you really want to learn the course, just avoid him.

I dont even know what I learned in this class. I have no idea how it relates to investments. He made his lectures way too involved and I never knew what was relevant. His exams are all free response and only 10 questions. The only thing good about the class was that he was an easy grader.
Good guy, but I don't appreciate him not being able to come to class on time. So far has been late to every lecture. If the professor is not expected to show up in time, neither should I.

Dr. Ledbetter goes on tangents that take away from his slides. The slides already skips lots of exam material. You'll end up reading the textbook anyways, so the slides feel a bit redundant (though they can help).

He cares about his students. Beyond that the lack of attention span makes the lectures highly uninformative, therefore the tests were very hard because we didn't learn 80% of what was on the tests. Read the book, don't listen to the lecture. Who knows where it will lead anyways.

He yaps about very random things during lecture (one time spent a class talking about an ostrich fable with an AI generated image of it on the screen). I didn't feel like I learned anything when he was talking. His slides that he goes through leave so much stuff out, but the textbook is helpful. Mostly I personally didn't like the material tho lol

He goes on soo many tangents about unrelated topics, the entire room couldn't care less to pay attention in his classes. He doesn't seem to actually prepare the lectures or he's just really bad at teaching, because he overcomplicates a 5 minute concept. Passive aggressive emails are a bonus. Bring earbuds to class.
No words needed. Very likeable professor, you can tell he cares about everyone and loves his job.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: (Cnit322) He shows up 10 minutes late to class and then expects people to show up and take attendance. He goes off topic very easily so you're gonna have to self-study. He plays AI-generated lectures and say he's “introducing new technology”. It feels like a waste of tuition and time, and Polytech shouldn't give him the next contract.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: There's nothing to say

Seems like a nice guy but is not good at explaining topics at all. Gets side tracked very easily and skips many slides. You have to rely almost fully on the book for info. Lab difficulty depends on TA. Tests are all online and are not too bad. Big group project at the end, not very hard content but it is difficult to split up work as a group.

Very kind and evidently caring professor. Class can be a little disorganized/confusing at times, however, as you're basically on your own for the labs. TA's didn't cover or explain any of the material and released lab grades only just before lab exams so you had little chance to see anything of concern. Also, an A is a 94+ only so 93.9 is still A-.

Look, he's not a bad guy, but his lectures can be really confusing even if you are paying attention. The lab is taught with many materials from the previous professor and if you get a bad TA for lab, you are on your own to figure it all out for the most part with the lab manual and a video. Quiz on each chapter of the book. Big group project too.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: This was the worst class I've ever taken in Purdue. If possible, please cancel the summer vacation courses. The experience was extremely poor. There was no effective assistance and no real-time courses available. Before I signed up, a friend said he was better suited for conducting research rather than teaching, I completely agreed with her.

The professor graded a month late, used another professor's recordings instead of teaching, and even reused last year's Lab 5 as this year's Lab 6 without renaming it. Group projects were harshly graded—teammates' mistakes hurt your grade, and explanations were dismissed. Late work got a zero.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Honestly this guy is a super nice individual. Goes on rambles sometimes but I never seemed to mind. Great person overall very nice and understanding.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Great guy. Lectures aren't the most productive, but 180 lab is much more important than lec. Weekly quizzes online. Honestly not the hardest class, use quizlet for your quiz and exam studying.

This instructor should refrain from teaching/offering this course in the summer. From ignored questions in emails, to practically your-on-your-own convoluted labs, to a situation where I submitted a lab "incorrectly" and it instantly earned me a F with no feedback left whatsoever, it seems those who are taught in person have it better.

Pretty chill class. Most people don't go to the lectures, but I found the content somewhat interesting. He is an okay lecturer, does go off on tangents at times but it's not like the material is super hard to understand so it's fine. Assignments and exams are pretty straightforward, with a group project at the end.
Very chill guy. Lectures can be boring but very nice and helpful. No complaints really.

The professor just read off the slides, you can get a good grade by just going through the information yourself. Labs were easy, I enjoyed them. There was a final group project, it was okay just get a good group. It is based on the labs and is pretty simple. Was relatively understanding when issues came up, especially with groups.

Very caring and engaging lecturer. Grading standards for the research paper draft that you submit every few weeks then piecemeal together into a research paper at the end of the semester are very inconsistent even after referring to the rubric. Exams on the other hand rely on rote memorization, but the questions are straightforward nonetheless.

Will is one of the nicest and most caring profs at this whole university. That being said, it doesn't seem like he plans his lectures. He's so knowledgable but the lectures are wild and all over the place. Assignments are actually pretty fun but beware of the grader who takes off points for literally anything. Exams are online and open for a week,

Ledbetter taught about 1/3 of my CNIT18200 class and the other professor taught the other 2/3. Ledbetter is a nice person and is willing to help when you ask for it (especially on project deliverables). However, his lectures can be boring and he can be scatter-brained at times; he seems to know his stuff, he just has to work on conveying it better.
Great Instructor!!

William Ledbetter is a graduate student, but he is a better instructor than most professors I have. He genuinely cares if the student learns the material and will try to find different ways to explain the material so that the student understands in the best way possible. Genuine guy; knowledgable from his experience in the field.
runrunrunrunrunrunrun
Dr. Li is super caring, delivers clear instruction, and supports students genuinely. I learned how to make the best use of ChatGPT in research paper writing from her class. Quite helpful.

She was my International English professor during Spring 2024, her class was easy, but some assignments took a lot of time. She gave extra credits and easy to receive a good grade if you attended all her classes on time. Be ready to actively participate in her discussions, and don't use your phone during the class unless she allows it.

Dr. Tucker is an amazing lecturer. He takes time to meet with all of his students, and genuinely cares about his students. His classes are very useful, and you learn great skills that will help you in your future career no matter what that is.

One of the best professors I have had. Truly cares about his students and the material in the class. Dr. Tucker is a very intelligent and genuine person, providing many thought-provoking opportunities in class.

I've had Dr. Tucker as an instructor and advisor all throughout my college career. He is a compassionate, caring professional. He's always willing to work around special circumstances, and understands that sometimes life happens. He shows expertise in his material and is a phenomenal communicator. You won't regret taking any of his classes!

I love Dr. Tucker. not only is he passionate about what he teaches, but he also cares about the students and makes an effort to get to know them. 152 was a fun class, with some reading and some grammar exercises. He makes the material relevant to the students.

Dr. Tucker is a great professor and advisor! The lectures were interesting and Dr. Tucker encouraged participation and questions. He was always happy to help and give advice, and he truly wants everyone to succeed in class.
Dr. Tucker is pretty cool. He lectures pretty well. Any quizzes we had were pretty easy. He also really wants to help you do well.

Melloch really studies how to learn, and it shows during his lectures. Expect lots of handouts, demonstrations, and learning to build fundamentals and intuition. Content is hard, and the class is very Exam heavy, and can feel all or nothing at times. Melloch is one of the best in the business at teaching concepts and intuition, invaluable skills.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Gives good feedback--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Very nice professor and easy to talk to. Class was a easy A+ if you just showed up.

Prof. Melloch only wants for you to take your learning from this class to excel academically/personally and doesn't want this class to be something you stress over. Attend weekly lectures for attendance points and submit reflections for all your points. Learned useful stuff in his course and recommend for freshman getting adjusted to college.

I really enjoyed this class its pretty interesting and there is no work involved. Attendance is graded but the professor is very understanding and is enthusiastic about the material. It teaches you a lot of important skills.

Melloch is a very good lecturer and is able to make all of his content very interesting. His 10301 class is very easy and actually gives good information, too. He knows his content well, is interested in what he is teaching, and doesn't just read off of slides.

While this class did teach me that I am horrible at taking exams, Melloch was always fantastic and I learned a lot. The homework is just a weekly reflection, with physics problems being for extra credit. He is incredible at teaching Maxwell's equations conceptually and I feel like he is one of the few ECE profs that actually care about students.
Overall a great and interesting class that isn't much work. Professor does a fantastic job lecturing, highly recommend.
Really interesting class! great professor

Melloch is a really great professor. Exams and HW are fair and his pre-recorded videos on Brightspace + practice exams are the script when it comes to doing good in this class. Lecture is a good way to get familiar with the topics and watching the videos is the way to master them. Wish he taught Emag 2.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Extremely caring and genuine professor. Teachers extremely well and answers absolutely any question asked. Exams are very lecture heavy and homework is used as an extra credit opportunity. Overall, grading is very fair for this "difficult" of a class and Melloch made this one of my favorite classes at Purdue.

Professor Melloch is easily the best professor I've had at Purdue. He genuinely cares about his students and makes an effort to help everyone understand the material. His lectures are clear, and the handouts and demos he uses in class really help bring the concepts to life.

I would take a bullet for this professor if needed. he is so kind and genuinely cares. I sucked at the class but he does a great job teaching. he knows everyones names and plays music at the beginning of some classes. he also has an entire online course and demos. I showed the demos to my family at thanksgiving because he is so awesome

Professor Melloch cares a lot about students' success and wellbeing, and it shines through in this class. He told us that he designed Keys to Learning just so that students can learn techniques to do better in school. A decent amount of the material was common sense, but I still recommend this class just because of how well it is taught.
Show up for class, do the reflections, and you will do well on the quizzes.  Easy course, but great information in it.

Imo, the best professor in Purdue's ECE dept. I haven't had many professors that genuinely care about their students and their learning. Lectures were good and they are recorded, but you can win points back on exams simply by going to lecture and office hours.

Keys To Learning is a very new course Professor Melloch just started teaching, its a course he genuinely cares about and is extremely passionate about. Lectures contain so much concentrated interesting and inspiring content, and attendance is the vast majority of the grade making it a very easy course.

Mike is honestly very overrated, i took his section and got a somewhat good grade too. You have to put in extra studying to do well unlike other classes. Homework is extra credit but he makes up for it by absolutely destroying you in the exams crossing questions left and right just because you didn't do the question his way.

Definitely the best professor for 30411. You need to show up to class, though; he doesn't really teach from the textbook, and the notes he posts aren't enough to learn from. The homework is all extra credit, which is nice. Show up, take good notes, and do the practice exams, and it won't be hard to get an A.
This professor goes through material on presentation slides based off on the research book he wrote. It is all very informative on how to study and think when learning how to adjust to college life.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I took Prof. Melloch's ENGR10301 class. Content was engaging and very useful for my academic career. His teaching was clear and memorable, focusing on student success. Exams matched lectures and texts, making an A very achievable. Highly recommend, will be trying to get into his ECE classes next year.

Normally he teaches ECE courses, but he started teaching ENGR 103 which is a course for FYE students about study habits and learning strategies. I found it interesting because he looked at the studies behind learning strategies, but I didn't find this class that useful since most of the strategies are fairly generic but definitely effective.

This class is physics heavy, but professor's explanations are pretty good. 2 cool things about the class: 1) all homework was extra credit, so you might as well do it. 2) if you do well enough on an exam, there's an oppurtunity to get some credit back for a question you missed; just schedule a meeting with him and work out the problem from scratch!
