Best ECE professor I've ever had at Purdue. The content was relatively difficult but easy to understand if you attend all his lectures. He makes it worth going to all lectures with extra credit incentives.

One of the best ECE profs. Great lecturer (i.e. go to class and you'll get the stuff). Lots of extra credit. Straightforward exams. As long as you don't slack off (completely ignore class etc.), you'll probably get at least a B. Really cares about students. Very helpful in Q&A. Highly recommended. Only con is wish he taught more ECE classes.
Best professor at Purdue by far. He cares a lot about his students, and his lectures are very interesting. If you do the past exams, you'll do great !

An absolute gem of a professor, completely changed the way I approached the subject. His lectures are incredibly well organized with lots of practical demonstrations, worked out examples, and thorough explanations of concepts. Exams and homeworks are straightforward and not very hard if you put in the effort. One of the best teachers I've ever had.
Easy A I feel. He gives you chance to get some points back if attendance is good. The content is not quite easy but it's easy exams.
Easily the best professor I have had. Offers a lot of extra credit and is very available to help students.
Dr. Melloch is the professor I've had at Purdue. I didn't have that many interactions with him, but I know that I can talk to him anytime!

The best professor I had at Purdue. He is able to take a subject frequently hidden by calculus and derivation, and make it demonstrably intuitive to students. Worked with me and helped me to understand a lot about myself as a student. Dr. Melloch is an outstanding person, a devoted educator, and was a real mentor to me during my time at Purdue ECE.
One of the best professors I've ever had. Truly a hidden gem of Purdue ECE dept.

This was my favorite class and Melloch is my favorite professor. Hands down- he is accomodating of outside situations, willing to meet with students whenever, will explain a problem multiple times and ways to help you understand, bring in real life demos to make sense of some of the weird formulas, and keeps everything fun. Best prof for this class

Professor Melloch is a very extraordinary professor in the Electrical and Computer Engineering department. He can introduce electromagnetic topics very clearly and always do some physical experiments in class. I think that is a valuable experience to observe current and magnetic field effects. His exams are fair and test what you have learned.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Best professor I have ever had in ECE. Gives great lectures with experiments. He gives points back on exams and extra credit. In office hours it is obvious he truly wants his students to succeed.
One of the best instructors I have ever come across. Very helpful and inspiring and motivates you to give your best. Always ready to help, if you are genuinely interested to learn.

This is the only professor in Purdue that will give you his 100% to learn. If you dont take a class with him, you are losing big time. Never had a better Professor, I dont think i will (I really hope I do).
One of the best professors at Purdue

Prof. Melloch was the first professor I had in ECE that I could tell really cared about his students.  He would always come in with demos prepared to explain further the topics from the lecture.  In addition, he had a point recovery system on tests based on a short presentation, which really helped me learn the stuff I wasn't as comfortable about

Easily the best professor I've had at Purdue so far, which, in turn made ECE311 the most interesting course I've taken. Truly cares about his students and wants them to succeed. He's always available to help, and is very friendly. I could write a paper about how excellent he's been this semester. Keep it up, Dr. Melloch.

Prof. Melloch truly cares about his student's success. He makes himself and his TA more than accessible. Additionally, he uses numerous visuals and experiments to really create a good understanding of the concepts.  The only problem was going too slow which might be attributed to the students and the Prof being taken advantage of slightly.

Prof. Melloch is the best professor I have yet to have in the ECE department.  He is dedicated to the success and learning of his students.  He is very personal with his students as well which makes it easy to ask for help.  If you show up to class, do the work, and get any needed help, it will not be difficult to do well.
Cares for his students, I'd take him for any classes he's teaching. One of the best proffesors I've ever had
He's one of the best professors I've had at Purdue! He gives out lots of helpful handouts and homework is extra credit. He really cares that his students do well.

He puts forth an incredible amount of effort to teach, and it really shows! From his illustrative demonstrations, to his thoughtfully-prepared paper handouts and video recordings, he provides many alternate resources to explain and expound upon the phenomena covered in this course. He emphasizes and cultivates a strong conceptual understanding.

Prof. Melloch is a professor who is not as interested in making you work as much as helping you truly understand the concepts of the course. He therefore gives a reasonable workload, has enjoyable lectures that are engaging, and has plenty of opportunity for effort to shine over genius. The tests are very fair and the resources help immensely.
Hands down the best professor in ECE! I would take any class that prof. Melloch teaches.
Definitely a great professor. Awesome demonstrations in class. Very clear and detailed notes. Really nice as well.
Best professor ever

Prof. Melloch is a really nice person, helpful and patient. His course emphasizes the most basic concepts so that you can get a solid foundation with this threshold level electromagnetism course, but not get lost in the tons of weird exam questions without a straightforward sense of what is going on.

Very good professor. Will go out of his way to answer questions for students. If he doesn't know the answer, he will research and come back next class with a good explanation. Attendance isn't mandatory. However extra credit is contingent on attendance, so most people go. Concepts can be difficult, but Melloch makes it manageable for students.

Awesome professor, one of the best in Purdue's ECE department. Really cares about students success, holds extra office hours nearing exam time. If you go to every class, bonus points available by making up exam questions. Exams are reasonable, studying previous exams will prepare you. Teaches very well, lots of cool demos.

Professor Melloch is clearly the best professor in ECE. He makes his class go so smoothly that you never feel the pressure for the vast syllabus it covers. Prof. Melloch's policies and lunches are the coolest thing ever. I wish he teaches more courses and I will happily take them.

Professor Melloch might be the best professor I have ever encountered in college. He was  very good at delivering ideas and the lecture contents. I loved the course and i wish to have him for future courses that I might take.

Professor is truly concerned with students understanding of the material and sincerely puts time into making sure that students get a good grade if they are willing to pay attention and put forth effort
He is really nice
nice person and helpful course

Prof. Melloch is an insanely good prof. He is clear to understand, his assignments and exams are clear-cut and straightforward, and he makes every attempt to learn every student's name (in a class of 60) by the second (of 3) midterms. I could get an A in any class if he was the professor. GET HIM FOR ECE 311!

The definition of a good professor. Actually, I can't think of a professor better than him. Professor Melloch actually cares about how his students understands the material and he puts all his time to help students.

If this professor is available, TAKE HIM!! you will regret if you dont.Also, if your major doesnt want you taking this course, just switch majors! Professor Melloch is the most amazing professor I have ever had.

Attendance is not required, but he gives the opportunity to redo an exam problem if you attended 90% of the lectures before an exam.  Easily the best ECE professor I've had at Purdue.  Its clear that Prof. Melloch cares about the students taking his class.
Melloch is a super cool dude. He tries his best to help you and gives the opportunity to give points back on exams given class attendance is 80%. Highly recommend.

Professor Melloch suggested we do this eval which is already an awesome statement of the care he puts in his teaching. He takes care to make sure he knows each of his students on a first name basis. He does this by planning lunches with tthe which h is great! All hw is real hard, but it's extra credit and preps you real good for the exams! A+ prof!
I would take any class with this professor regardless of the subject!
Helpful
Nice person
Great Teacher. Easy to talk to

This is the only professor I've written a review for on this website, because honestly he deserves to have people informed on how good of a professor he is. No one else cares for his student as much as Prof. Melloch. If you're struggling, just talk to him.
Very open to talk to anyone that needs help and just want to talk.
Attend class an pay attention.  He's great at what he does, but it's a difficult class.  However, he's willing to help out wherever you have problems and is very helpful.  GO TO OFFICE HOURS
The professor is amazing and really nice. He gives a lot of time to his students.
A great professor overall. He's very understanding and makes sure his students learn the material clearly.

Sometimes the material covered in the lecture is different from the homework and the book is not that useful so I don't know what i can do if I cannot understand the material in the lecture.By the way, most of the time, the lecture is understandable
Good professor and easy to talk to outside of class. Helpful when answering questions.
Great professor, one of the best I've had at Purdue
Wish more professors were like him.
Very helpful and interested in getting to know his students.
Presents every single topic clearly and uses helpful demonstrations often.

Puts forward more effort than any other prof I’ve had.  Brings handouts to class, performs in-class demonstrations, gives historical context of material, posts supplementary explanations and real-world applications of material on blackboard, takes class time to answer questions, has ample office hours, arranges lunch with students.

Professor Melloch is the best teacher I have had at Purdue. He is very good at explaining and demonstrating difficult concepts. His policy of allowing students to make up lost exam points by demonstrating mastery of a topic that you did poorly on is very helpfu, turning weak points into strong points.  I highly recommend him as a teacher.

Possibly the greatest professor in the entire ECE department. He wants his students to learn the material and structures his lectures in a way that promotes learning. Exams can be tough, but he gives his students plenty of old exams to review from. I rarely had to use the textbook thanks to the clarity of Professor Melloch and supplemental text.
great guy
Very nice professor
Brilliant Professor. Really Cares about his students.Knows the material. The Bar to which all other professors should be like.
Really great professor, material is hard to understand in general but it becomes clearer when he teaches it. Also go to Office hours they help a lot!

Dr Melloch places great importance on actual understanding of the material and goes to great lengths to make sure it happens, he posts several videos on experiments to better illustrate concepts which are fun and help you learn. One can also get back points on every midterm exam , which is a great confidence booster.Verdict: Take it !

Mike Melloch is one of the best professors I've had at Purdue. He tries his best to make sure you can understand the material. In my opinion, the only problem is that the material in EE 311 is really hard to understand especially if you don't love physics
Fantastic Teacher, Always helpful and really wants you to learn the material, He is very laid back and likes to get to know the students, overall great experience
He genuinely seems to care about his students learning the material. He wants everyone to succeed in his class.
GOOD KIND HELPFUL
Very Helpful. Tries as much as possible to enable full understanding of concepts.

Professor Melloch puts more effort into teaching his class than I've seen from any professor in my 3 years here. He posts helpful videos, and offers sessions allowing us to earn points back on our exam by "teaching" a concept from a missed problem. He has made me enjoy electromagnetics when I've only ever heard bad things about the course.
Great guy! He really has a good way of explaining complex concepts and making them simple enough to understand. Provides a lot of opportunity to learn the material.
Professor Melloch teaches everything in a clear, easy to follow manner. He really wants his students to understand the material and offers a lot of office hours and extra help.

Professor Melloch is great, he shows excellent demonstrations in class, he tries his hardest to keep us students in the loop with information and to keep us entertained.  My one gripeis that hishomeworks don'tmatch up so well with hislectures or the book.  I look at thehomework and compare the problems with my notesand the book and I'm leftconfused

I like the fact that he explains topics that are not clearly covered in the textbook, and gives derivations for formulas.  I also really like the fact that he has demos for many of the important topics, and posts videos of the demos, along with other videos, on blackboard, so I can go back and watch them again to clarify topics.
A very approachable and nice professor.  It's always possible to get an A in his classes if you put in the effort.
This professor works very hard to make the material understandable and clear, and go to great lengths to help out the student.
Easy to understand, very helpful. Puts a lot of effort into actually teaching the concepts
Well organized and very approachable. Offers demonstrations and videos to supplement lectures. Always willing to help. Take a class with this professor if you get the chance.

Prof. Melloch is probably one of the best professors in ECE. He does a great job at explaining the concepts in class. Very helpful if you meet him during office hours and extremely friendly. ( He treats everyone in class to a pizza lunch ).
Good teacher. Ok class. Like that he showed physical demos of the forces in action.
Excellent professor who actually cares about his students and puts forth the effort to have them succeed.  Easily one of the best professors in the ECE department here at Purdue.

The course is difficult simply because of the difficult subject material that needs to be covered. That being said this professor is the most helpful and caring ECE professor that I've had so far. The hw is reasonable and helps you learn. The exams are a very good representation of the subject that is taught. I highly recommend this professor.

gotta say, my favourite professor! A fair and kind professor who actually cares his students! and you feel so bad when you dont study or perform in his class, when he is so helpful and wanted you to understand the materials taught. The course itself is hard but you will never want to lose the chance getting to know this awesome professor!!

A godsend to us ECE students. I hated physics until I got into his class. For an abstract subject, he makes things very clear. It's not too hard if you actually study some. If you study past exams, you will do very well in this class. He is always willing to help you if you don't understand anything. A fair professor.
GREAT professor. the very first prof. i ve met during my 3 yr undergrad so far, who really cares his students. im lucky 2b his student.'nd he gives us extremly clear lectures
The class was great. I especially liked the exams and the helpfulness of the past exams.
Very good teacher but you must attend class to get anything out of it

One of my favorite professors, actually tries to get to know his students which makes getting help from him easier. Will help out with anything that you do not understand. I recommend taking him for 311 and 305
Attend class, its worthwhile and really helps for the exams.
One of the best classes I've taken at Purdue. Exams and HWs were all very fair.
This professor makes the material easy to understand and tests you fairly.
You explain the materials very clearly. I only hope that we have time to discuss the LED and Photovoltaics part of the class. Other than that, it was pretty much perfect.
He really is a good professor. He reviews what he taught at the last lecture, and he gives lots of chances to help students understand and get good grades.

This is a very friendly and helpful professor, whose enthusiasm and passion in electrical engineering attract the attention of the whole class whenever he demonstrates simple experiments illustrating the concept of electromagnetism. However the lecture would be better if he mentions the topic of the day before showing us all the formulas.
I will take ECE305 if Prof. Melloch is teaching it next semester.
excellent professor that actually encourages his students to post feedback on this site.  keep up the good work professor!
one of the most helpful prof. i ve ever met. prof. melloch really paid a looooooot of passion in this class.
The course is hard but the professor is always ready to teach.
Great guy.
Took him for ECE 311 also.  My favorite professor at Purdue.  Very helpful and doesn't make the exams over things he has covered.
One of the best professors, way to underrated. It's sad that the worst professors are the ones with the most reviews.
Extremely well prepared for every class, very clear and concise. Always helpful and willing to answer questions in or out of class.

Professor Melloch is the best! He is very competent, and awesome. He is the area chair for Micro&Nanoelectronics, and also he is the director of graduate admissions. Despite of his busy life, if you go to his office hour, he will still go in great detail to help you. One of the best prof at Purdue!

Great guy..helps a lot whenever you go to him. Surprising esp since hes the dept head of M&Nano. If this guy can spare the time to help undergrads, it makes me wonder why some other profs seem so damn busy.

Bill Gates look-alike?  Anyone?  Anyone?  He's a great teacher--very prepared and will go to any lengths to help you learn.  He is willing to explain any tangent (doesn't get rattled at all) and you can go by his office just about any time.  G
Good professor, looks like he cares about his students.
The exams are were very difficult for the amount that was taught in class. Did not feel like you were ever prepared for the exams.
Over hated, her lectures are good and the course material is standardized so people are getting mad at her for it.
The reason there are so many 1 stars is that the exams are really difficult. She is a decent lecturer and a very nice person, but the exams she makes are ridiculously hard.

Marybeth honestly isn't even that bad of a lecturer she goes over examples and stuff in lecture I think most people give her a bad rating because the course itself is difficult (which it is chem115 is kinda bs sometimes) but honestly Marybeth herself is an alright lecturer. That final was way easier than any of the midterms too.
Just wow, take it at ivy tech or literally anywhere else. Orgo is much better.

Professor Miller is good at describing concepts, and provides good examples during lectures. However, some assignments were difficult with the material not being covered in lecture until the day before it was due and a couple of materials for exams were not very helpful. Overall, a good lecturer but study early for the exams.

She was the course coordinator, and completely changed the course this year. This caused all of the past exam questions and past resources to be unusable and you had to use the textbook problems to actually get a good grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Make sure to study even of you think you know it study it again. Get a tutor if you think you need one. This course is difficult.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: If you think you need a tutor for this course get one. Chem 115 is not for the weak.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Honestly, I didn't hear great things but in my opinion truly this is just a general chem course. You will have to study and you will have to put time and effort in. She is very clear about what is on the exams. They are also very generous with curves and drops. I feel like the course was very fair and matched my AP chem class pretty closely.

Let me leave it at this. I talked to a friend who had a bf in the course and all she had to say was that he hates it and she never gets to see him even on the weekends even though they both go to purdue. Yk why?? Bc thats how much he and everyone else has to study to try to pass this impossible course. I was also in the course.. dont.

This is the worst course I have EVER taken.She was either really nice or just outright rude, no inbetween. Be ready for 25 question long assignments with each question having 4+ parts even during dead week. The tests are so bad, prepare to study for DAYS straight and still get a 60% bc what she gives you to study isnt even accurate to the tests.

The content she goes over in class is useful and her slides and in-class examples are very well organized. That being said, I would've preferred if she went slower in class since it was next to impossible to take notes. Because of this, I just went to lectures for iClicker and learned by reviewing her slides after, which worked well for me.
I loved listening to her lectures! Very engaging and did a great job explaining complex concepts! There were plenty of opportunities for extra credit by answering iClicker questions.

Class is not particularly difficult with exams covering what is outlined in the learning objectives. I came in with very little chemistry knowledge and did not have difficulty grasping material. Exam questions are NOT strictly from practice exams and understanding of material is required. I would consider her lectures the best of the 115 lectures.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Appreciative of teaching style! She implements many ways to visualize/understand concepts in lecture, works out problems step by step, and posts lecture slides, iclicker question answers, and worked-out practice questions online. Clearly cares about everyones learning and understanding of what is happening and is super friendly! Course is tough!
She is the sweetest ever, she's just a tough grader and the courseload is heavy
Impossible class

She is so underrated. You can tell she really cares about chemistry and loves what she teaches. The exams are hard but you just have to lock in and start studying super early. Miller seems scary but she gives every resource to succeed and the people who complain about her just can't handle an actual college-level class. Best lecturer/coordinator!!

Oh my god this class was the worst experience of my life. Left right after class and refused to let anyone ask any questions, was completely disengaged in office hours. 

0 TA accountability with insanely difficult grading standards, extremely low curving, temper. Absolute NIGHTMARE of a professor. My grades got better after not going to class.

She is given a bad rep from freshman who are not used to actual college leveled classes. She gives a LOT of chances to succeed, ICLICKER extra credit, in class practice problems, LOTS of worst score DROPS, and learning objectives to guide you. I am a freshman and definitely struggled in this class but if you put in the effort you'll do good!!

While CHM115 is definitely a hard course, especially for freshmen, Miller is a good professor. She regularly goes over practice problems in class and gives plenty of examples for reference. Although the exams can be rough, she gives you adequate resources to prepare yourself. She is personable and is willing to get to know you as an individual.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: The lectures are long, but she explains everything very well. Also tells you what to study for the tests and if you actually study it you should do very well.

I love marybeth!! She explains the topics thoroughly and doesn't speed through her lectures like other chemistry professors do. She also has a good sense of humor. Yes the class is difficult, but that doesn't make her a bad professor.
Scary Traumatizing Leave
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: basically you're 100% on your own for this one, good luck surviving

She makes the tests the hardest things you've ever done and gives you 0 curve at the end of the semester. The averages for the exams were all below a 60 and the curve for an A went from a 93 only down to a 92. Ridiculous.

Gets a bad rap from those who have been spoon-fed everything their whole lives. She is clear on expectations, from pre-lab to in-lab to post-lab. She can sound rude for chiding people doing things against common sense, but I'll defend her right to call out stuff like that. I felt like I was treated as an adult, and for that she has my full respect.

I feel obligated to clear things up: Marybeth is not an instructor for CHM 11500, nor does she make the exams for CHM 11500. She is the course coordinator, which means she manages the course instructors and all of the head-TAs. She hold office hours and review sessions for students. Her job is to enforce course policies and help course instructors.
Hates children
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: SO mean SOOOO mean, like absolutely horrible

Miller was the reason that I stopped going to in person lectures as she would berate students for making the tiniest of noises. During an exam she yelled at over 2000 students because they weren't quite enough, the only sound was students shuffling into their seats and getting their materials out. She seems very angry at all times, would avoid

A good bit of the reviews on here is misleading. Marybeth gives plenty of opportunities for exams with review sessions so take advantage of those! Also she is not your lecturer she is the course coordinated and creates the exams. Overall a decent class, just must pay attention to lecture and HW, and hope you get a good TA for lab....

Even if you did well in chemistry in high school, you are not prepared for this course. Marybeth creates the HARDEST exams, and you learn nothing from lectures. Know every inch of the textbook before taking the exams. This course needs to be examined by Purdue, something needs to change.

When taking Chem 115 I generally was excited. I had loved the honors course at my high school. However, Marybeth ruined this for me. The exams are made almost impossible. Don't even try looking at BoilerExams either because the person doing it gets the answers wrong too. When trying to drop an exam too, dropping the final does nothing at all.

Not a very great course coordinator.  Some material on the final exam was not taught until 3 days before the exam.  Labs do not help you understand the material in the course and lectures sometimes are behind the homework assignments.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Rude and insensitive.

One of the worst professors at Purdue. The material shouldn't be hard, but she makes it hard. Tests have no reason to be so difficult to understand. Rude, insensitive, and on a power-trip. Labs are so short and not completing them penalizes you in every aspect. No holding TAs accountable for grading. My TA was extremely kind, which is why I passed.

She refuses to release your actual grade after the joke of a "curve". If the dept saw how terrible students did in her class she'd be fired by now. Not to mention she also yells at students and treats them poorly. Test out of chem115 if you can

Terrible course. The labs were often given frustratingly short time limits, a problem compacted by the labs being graded on your response to questions different from the ones asked. The rest of the course was needlessly difficult but reasonably possible. There is a CHM 115 & 116 test-out option. Use it.
PROPAGANDA!!!!! THIS PROFESSOR IS EVIL. STAY AWAY AT ALL COSTS I'M WARNING YOU. She make makes my blood run cold from just the sight of her. Mary stop deleting my posts.

Marybeth Miller is a horrible course coordinator. She makes this class unnecessarily miserable, doesn't hold TAs accountable with grading standards, and the class varies a lot by lecturer. Literally before our first CHM 115 exam she yelled "Shut your mouths" into the microphone in Elliot.

Dr. Miller was the coordinator for this class, and did not lecture. Lectures and book cover the same content, so pick your poison (or both if you struggle). Homework is not a problem. Labs are tough; make a spreadsheet with equations for data entry before getting to lab.
Very passive aggressive and completely rude at times. She did not run the course well compared to other course coordinators.
This course is set up terribly. The test are hard to study for and the averages are never released ON PURPOSE! Beware of this course if you are not good at chem from High School.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: I disliked how she ran this program. She expected that everyone in the course would have a perfect knowledge of all chem from high school. During one of our exams, she yelled at us to be quiet and was very rude throughout other parts of the course. Overall just didn't have a good experience in this class.
This professor has biases she does not hide. Needs a lesson on micro aggressions
Good Gen Chem coordinator. Without her, 115/116 will be a total mess
Good teacher that's all I have to say.

classes are lecture heavy - but you don't really have to pay attention, just answer a question or two in class for participation. It's a bit boring but really easy class, he grades fair and usually you get an A as long as you submit it. Only would take it again bc of how easy it was not bc it was interesting.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Prof. Swanson gave a good, easy introduction to the types of classes you can expected in the honors college curriculum. He assigns easy homework (e.g., watching a 40min documentary) and the class mainly serves as a way to make friends and connections.

I took HONR 19901 in the fall and had the Maps section. Prof. Swanson was a solid professor. The topic of maps and the lectures were a bit boring, but the class itself wasn't hard at all. There is some prep work, but typically never any homework. As long as you show up and participate you'll do well.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Started every class late, and almost always went over time. Had students give presentations for several lectures, but would talk so much after the first one that the second presenters would be rushed or pushed to the next class. Gave no feedback for most assignments. Topic of the class was very niche, but he seemed to care about student learning.
He is just a chill guy. I think that he likes to teach, but he ends up a lot of the time, just yapping. He knows what he is doing and he's fun to chat with.
reviews.rating_tags: Hilarious--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I thought Dr. Swanson's lectures were very dry but he does know his stuff and as others have said, it's an easy A if you do the work. Group work with other honors' students was enjoyable.

His fall semester's topic was about Maps and it proved to be fairly low stakes and easy. The course primarily revolved around group work and making different types of maps. The readings and out-of-class work was minimal. Dr. Swanson himself is relaxed and gives some interesting lectures. A great course to take if you're busy with other classes.

Lots of lectures and the readings are quite high level, but it's okay because a complete understanding of them is not vital. All the projects are group and little solo homework, just do your part and you will be fine.

Dr. O'Brien was the BEST professor that I have had during my time so far at Purdue. Her lectures cover a lot of content in a short period of time, but she makes it clear when concepts mentioned will be on exams, letting you know exactly what to study. She is very knowledgeable and it is clear that she genuinely cares about her students' learning.

Probably the best pre-pharmacy professor here. She teaches immunology with ease and explicitly states when concepts being taught will be mentioned on exams. She's also so enjoyable to listen to, and there are multiple lectures where she has everyone laughing. Loved Dr. O'Brien downnn, wish she taught the entire class and not just half of it :(

She is a very concise lecturer and makes difficult concepts easier to understand. The tests are based directly on the lecture. It's definitely possible to get an A if you memorize the lectures and pay attention in class.

Great lecturer, probably the best in BSPS or pre-pharm. Assignments helpful for studying if you put in effort and don't use AI, and she creates tests that are based entirely off lectures. It is a hard class, but possible to get an A/B if you follow along. Lectures are fine to watch on your own, but don't get behind because it's a lot of content.

I was worried with the bad reviews, but most of the content was not hard to follow. Guessing that most of the reviews are from an earlier period. The exams were difficult, but we were told what to study. This course was more difficult than most but not the hardest I have ever taken.
Disagree with the previous rating. Professor Oh has prior experience and makes reasonable exams
Disagree with the previous rating. Professor Oh no has no prior experience and makes the exams unnecessarily hard.
He knows what he is talking about. Concepts are pretty clear. Although I would say that the class is challenging

Sam O'Connor is not the course coordinator for this class, so I want to give him a bit of grace. I found theclass quite easy, and I think that's because his lectures were pretty solid. The only bad thing is that I found it hard to connect with him, and he speaks very quietly. Sit up front for this class.

Grades are really just the exams and quizzes so was hard to bring my grade up after a tough start. Class is taught at a quick pace and not very in depth or time for in class help. Boiler exam is the most helpful thing to prepare for exams and finals.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I was in this guys class for one week and his lectures were terrible. He talks so light and his hand is blocking the screen the whole time which just leaves you writing so fast and not understanding the whole time. He also has no note outlines, looking back on notes is just hard. His quizzes are significantly harder than the other professors too.

He is the best prof in CHE department imo. The way he translates concepts from his slides is simply amazing. There is no need to attend all his classes. His slides are self-contained. He is very knowledgeable in fluid mechanics. I would like to take his classes again. He gives a lot of HWS but they are very interesting and worth the time.
Get ready to learn a lot.

There is no question Osman Basaran is a great professor, however as a teacher he is terrible. He is passionate and wants you to learn but appears unable to condense the material down to the basics. Fluid Mechanics should be about granting an engineer a basic understanding of fluids, pipping systems, and pumps. I left his class with none of that.

Just do the homework assignments and try to learn (because he really wants everyone to learn), then everything will be more than fine. He loves teaching, he is so much passionate about science, he is a good mentor to everyone, he cares about students and he is super inspirational. He is good that's why he is teaching the toughest classes.

Dr. Basaran is an inspirational professor. His passion toward his subject is infectious and motivates you to pursue research in fluid mechanics! The subject material he covers finds direct use in understanding many aspects of research in diverse fields. He is tough but fair. I wholeheartedly recommend taking any courses he teaches, totally worth it
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Professor Basaran was a great professor who really made the course content interesting and delivered great lectures. However, the homeworks and exams were extremely difficult with very low average scores. Overall great professor who made the class interesting and was always willing to help, but the content of the course was very difficult.

Easily one of the smartest professors in CHE. Do well on the homework and above average on the exams (average is about 5% or lower.) And you will get an A.  Go to class and show you are interested and ask questions. More effort you put better your grade will be without worrying too much about doing well

He's probably the best and the toughest professor I've had in my life! But he's fair... And he really wants one to learn the material more than anything else! He will help more than anything... DO THE HOMEWORK AND YOULL BE FINE WITH A B... But actually though he's awesome!!!! He provides notes cause off which you don't need the text book!

Basaran is a brilliant man, but I feel he has a hard time connecting with the students on what we actually understand. Most of the homeworks and exams are impossible, BUT he just wants to see you try. Put effort into the class and take time out to show him that you're trying. Don't drive yourself mad over the class. Put in effort and you get a B!

Basaran knows what he's talking about, but you never will. He moves quickly through material, gives WAY too much homework, and the exams are impossible. But hang in there, apparently everyone gets a B in the end.

This is the HARDEST class I have ever taken, like ever. There is an immense amount of material, and unfortunately its all rather difficult. He is very helpful, but unfortunately it is almost impossible to get a hold of him. Don't be discouraged if you fail the exams, if everyone fails, no one fails!

Learned more in this class than in all my other ChE courses I took in the last year.  You have to be ready to learn fast and keep on top of your toes otherwise you'll start falling behind before the second week of class.  He assumes you know everything from physics and calculus.  If you're stuck on concepts he'll gladly help you at office hours.
Not that great of a professor.  He likes to think he is the best though.  Pretty arrogant, and easy to trip up.  Just ask him a question to prove one of his statements and watch the hilarity ensue.
Extremely brilliant Prof. Doesn't grade a single homework or assignment but so much to learn from him.

Professor was a very very intelligent and caring man. He just fails to connect well with students at times. He knows what he is talking about, but I don't. Available for help and openly welcomes questions. Hard exams. I got a 13% and was pleased I was above average.
Best professor i have had at purdue. an all around great guy who knows the material very well.

Amazing that time cannot dull his issues. Obviously brilliant man, but no real connection with the students or understanding of what he is (or in most cases, is NOT) clearly conveying. Very obvious from his test averages- in my class 13%, 21%, 24% and a whopping 30% on the final exam.  Its Purdue, you do have a choice in professors.  Chose wisely!

Can you say Neopolean Complex???  This is a little man has major attitude combined with disregard for whether his students are actually learning!!!  He called our whole class stupid when we couldn't get his teaching....  horrible teacher.
No Comments
systematic, and sincere teacher... challenges the students with tough questions.  Extremely hard exams.  sets high standards for himself and for the students...  excellent class notes!
He is a good teacher.  Very smart.  Yet, his exams are hard.. average 13%
Explains clearly. Exams are extremely hard!
Do not be scared Dr. Temi is super caring and the course is light work. Literally no homework whatsoever and it's a free A. Maybe my standards are low but she was nice and the course was easy.

I've never seen such a disorganized professor in my entire life. You will be snowed under with unnecessary and unclear work and spend hours on daily basis for assignments that seem like they are made up just to fill the syllabus. She is extremely tough grader and her feedback is highly ambiguous.

All of the concerns raised in previous reviews are accurate and align with my experience in this course. The class felt consistently disorganized, with unclear structure and expectations throughout the semester. Instructions for assignments were often ambiguous, which made it difficult to understand what was required. Stay away from Dr. Temi.

Professor Olenloa is extremely disorganized and consistently fails to provide clear or coherent instructions for assignments, making expectations difficult to understand. Feedback is vague and unhelpful, offering little to no guidance on what was done correctly or how to improve, which significantly undermines the learning experience.

Overall, not the worst Honors First Year Experience Professor, she was just disorganized and the rubrics were a bit vague. Not hard, but would advise choosing a different professor. She teaches Student Motivation, which may be helpful to some but wasn't to me personally. She was really kind, however, and was passionate about her area of expertise.

Dr. Temi's class tends to be disorganized and often confusing. Content can be hard to find, expectations are unclear. She messed up grades for multiple students in my class. She grades very easily on the assignments only to be harsh on the final. Grades sometimes don't make sense. I felt like I left the class frustrated and learning little.

Dr. Temitope is one of the most disorganized professors I have ever seen. While she has a generally good attitude, she is extremely unclear with instructions, directions, and assignments. Stuff is posted at the last minute or not at all, with very little clarification. Ultimately, the class feels very much like a waste of time and energy. Avoid her

While criticisms of the class are justified (see other reviews here, they are mostly correct), this is rate my *professor*, not rate my class. I am still considering the class in my rating, but Dr. Temi herself was extremely generous and helpful when I had questions or concerns on non-class issues, and clearly cared deeply about student well-being.
She was insufferable. She treated the class like preschoolers. Just stay away from Temitope.
Class was literally pointless and no work, just pissed me off being in her presence.

I don't think I've ever taken a class as disorganized and mismanaged as this class. Dr. Temi seems very lost on what the expectations and ultimate goals of the peer mentor program are. She also insists we do "roleplays" for the class which, frankly, are nothing if not a pointless exercise with poor grading criteria.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment:
Part 4
As you see, I have taken the liberty to write four different parts to this review.
I could write an entire essay about this, but for the purpose of practicality, I will leave this review here.
If you take this class, be in for an utter waste of time, a bad grade, and most importantly accept that you are cooked

~sincerely,
Alex

part 3
and then proceeds to grade by standards that no one could possibly understand or fulfill. The result, very poor overall grades in the class.  
This review may seem brazen and audacious but I don't think I can overstate the atrocity that is this class. It deserves it's own book, perhaps called: "How NOT to mentor"

Part 2.
Not only are the classes, lecture materials, rubrics, group projects, and the syllabus astonishingly disorganized, but the teacher insists on having us do these role plays or skits, based on mentoring, of which she gives a superficial and unclear rubric...

Part 1.
I am currently 2 weeks into this 4 week course about mentoring given over the summer and so far I can confidently say that this teacher, along with the class has been nothing short of catastrophically and appallingly abysmal.

Professor Ott did an amazing job of explaining concepts that were boring or challenging. He offers optional homework problems that align very close with exam material. He also posts practice exams that are super similar to the format of the mid terms. If you go to class, ask questions, and participate you should have no problems succeeding.

The material for this course is difficult and boring; I did not enjoy taking it in the early morning. However, the proffesor is great at explaining each topic, caring and effective at answering questions. I did feel surprised on any exams. Overall, great instructor, but you signed up for accounting, so be prepared to feel eepy.

If you do not have much prior accounting knowledge, this class may be challenging. The course is worth 400 points, with exams making up the majority of the grade, so exam performance is HIGHLY important. Quizzes are straightforward, and staying on top of the material is essential.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Ott teaches exactly what you'd want to learn in this course. The material is overall difficult, but if you go to lectures and pay attention it should be no problem.

Only exams and quizzes are graded. Material is not easy nor hard to understand, you have to go to lectures, pay attention in class, and do the homework to do well. If you do those things, it'll be hard to get anything less than a B, since each exam is curved. Ott is good at what he does, he knows the material and can answer questions well.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Ott is one of the best professors I have had here in my time at Purdue. His lectures are the most useful out of any class I take currently. Exams can be challenging but there is a curve and he provides a coupious amount of practice problems.

Pretty easy class overall. Professor Ott is a very nice guy and gave ample practice material when preparing for exams. There are like 7 at home quizzes that are very similar to in class problems, the 3 part project is very easy if you just follow the syllabus. Exams were very similar to the review material he posts which is very nice.

Awesome guy, impeccable lecture notes. Really caring in office hours. Has a strong accent but still understandable. Manageable homework loads. However, exams are really difficult (maybe complex analysis is just difficult in general). Skippable final which is a big plus. Honestly on the better end of math professors at Purdue.

Some of the best math lectures I have experienced at this school. He explains content at a good pace, simplifies definitions when necessary, takes questions, and gives warm-up and in-class problems. He is also very open during office hours and will take time to explain anything one-on-one.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: One of the best math professors I've had so far at Purdue. His lectures are very well organized and engaging, starting with warm up problems before going to the content. I also found his office hours to be very helpful.
I felt unprepared for exams too.

Probably the best professor I've ever had. He is a lovely, happy, and upbeat guy who is very good at explaining and connecting concepts. In the past I've been more fond of computational math classes but he made me find a passion for theory based math. If you have the chance, take his class.
nice, and helpful

Sasha is one of the best professors I've ever had. I've struggled a lot with Purdue math, and I struggled in MA 265, but he was very good. He's very passionate in a way that made me more interested in the material than I otherwise would have been. He's also very attentive to students in his class and makes sure to involve students in the lecture.

He's extremely caring for his students and explains everything very clearly. He's willing to repeat the same concept however many times it takes to get the point across to the student in office hours. I didn't think I'd like Linear Algebra, especially having taken it before at purdue with another professor, but he changed my mind.
It's so hard to understand him and he does not explain the content well at all. He made this class so hard.
Hard to understand and lecture slides are the same way. If you have him don't even bother trying to look at his slides and just look at the other professors' slides or watch YouTube videos.
Bad experience, you can't understand what he says and doesn't respect content, assumes the student know everything before the class.

Don't be scared by the ratings. It isn't that bad. Yes, he has an accent but it is nowhere near of being incomprehensible. Both of the TAs in the class was fantastic and recitations helped prepare for the exams. Exam has both multiple choice and free response and similar to sample exam provided. Very lenient with partial credit as well.
Can not understand his accent, bad teaching experience with the best TAs I've every met

He has a thick accent, but you get used to it after a couple of weeks. His teaching style is more talking at you and expecting you to know everything else. He doesn't actually fully explain what you asked about. If you have him go to the SI sessions, all the reviews, and watch videos online. He gives a lot of free points in the lectures.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: It wasn't that bad, dont text or talk in class. His accent isn't that difficult to understand and he makes a point to stop if people have questions. He is clearly very knowledgeable, but also don't ask questions. Dont get the textbook, you dont need it. Good luck, pray you have a good TA.
reviews.rating_tags: Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: PVR will not answer questions even though he encourages you to ask them. He cares more about people giving him attention than actually teaching the course, he will constantly halt class to confront students who are disengaged resulting in lectures being incomplete or sped through. Extra credit opportunities will help your grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--EXTRA CREDIT--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: He says at the beggining of the course that he encourages engagement, but then continues to embarrass and make fun of most questions that are asked. He does not articulate his sentences. His lectures are a waste of time- if there weren't pop quizzes every lecture I would just use the time to study on my own.

Professor Ramachandran is very hard to understand due to how quietly he speaks. If he is too hard to understand watch the videos of another lecture. This was my first chemistry course since sophomore year of high school so if you can do some chemistry every day (read textbook, homework, old exams, AI problem sets) you should do fine in this class.

Ramachandran only taught the first half of my chem lectures but he made super simple information so much more complicated. I dont think he is ideal for a intro course. Hes a nice dude but pretty hard to understand. Attendance isnt required so if you hate him you can just go to another section.

This was my first class at Purdue in which I felt that my attendance in lecture was pointless because I could not understand a word the professor said. I ended up using subtitles to watch lectures online. Even when I had subtitles however, PVR was not a good lecturer. His slides were confusing and convoluted. I hope you remember highschool chem.

I'm sorry but for the life of me I could not understand this guy in his lectures. He speaks way too fast and talks in a heavy accent. I know that accents aren't always something that can be controlled, but if you are aware of your heavy accent I imagine you can do things such as speak slower and clearer? Class itself was ok and in my opinion easy.

Lectures are a lot easier to understand if you read or skim the textbook ahead of time. He was very kind and engaged with the students at the front of the classroom as much as he could. Good at speaking, but it's easy to zone out and get lost.

PVR knows his stuff, but connecting with students isn't his strong suit. Exams can be tricky, so use all the resources - practice tests, unstuck ai, and the free textbook. Put in the effort and you can succeed.
God forbid you understand a single word that come out of this guys mouth. Unintelligible rumbling. Save yourself the trouble and do not go to lecture.
Professor made the class as easy as possible considering this class is a review of a two semester but doing well in this class is difficult but extra credit and help is available
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: WORST PROFESSOR EVER. DO NOT TAKE. He continuously made fun of students if they asked questions in the class, harassed anyone who brought up a mistake he may have made, and can't teach at all. Exams were near impossible to get good marks on and unless you're a chemistry whiz he won't care about whether you understand the content or not.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Test heavy

reviews.comment: PVR is a serious but very knowledgeable professor. He cares about his students and has a lot of extra credit on exams. There are in-lecture quizzes, but they are easier than the HW. He makes time to answer any questions and tries his best to speak loudly and clearly, which can be difficult with his accent.

It sounds like he is speaking gibberish all the time. There are random quizzes during lectures that most people get wrong rather than right, yet he sees nothing wrong with his teaching style. He seems to know what he's doing, but if you can understand him or if he answers your questions is another thing.

Professor Ramachandran seems like a genuinely nice guy and is very experienced in the field. He was very approachable and always tried to answer my questions after the lecture. However, I found it extremely hard to understand him. If possible, I recommend sitting at the front of his class and trying to read his lips.

PVR obviously knows what he is talking about, and his tenure at this university speaks well of him. He struggles with communicating exactly what he expects out of us, and uses special cases a lot in exams. He is not nice to his TAs or students. Sometimes professors only teach because they have to contractually, not because they're good professors.

I took organic chemistry with PVR and I do recommend PVR as he is a nice but also serious professor that wants his students to get the best knowledge out of this class. exams are not easy but also not impossible. Some lectures have pop quizzes that can get you attendance points. this is a class where you can succeed if you put in the effort.

PVR is very knowledgeable and passionate about organic chemistry. He provides extra credit opportunities for asking questions in class, even though it may seem intimidating. However, he provides all the information and resources you need to be successful in the class. The subject definitely requires  time to study and learn, but I loved PVR!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: PVR makes the class very doable by providing lots of opportunity for extra credit on exams and in class. The exams are almost identical to the practice exams so if you study those, the exams aren't too challenging. Go to class and study the practice exams and you'll be fine.
Look at it like every exam is cumulative from the last.

You can tell PVR very much loves what he does. He is very knowledgeable in his field. However, his lectures are less than stellar. Many people have said this before, his accent is very difficult to understand. I recommend going over the lecture slides at least once after lecture. If not, you will only understand like 50% of what's going on.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Respected

reviews.comment: This prof was not only difficult to understand, but also rude. He couldn't answer questions and would basically call you dumb if you asked them. Kinda like a little man complex... Not a fun time.
Incomprehensible lectures, and skips over explanations way too quickly.
Not great at all. Worst professor I have ever had, and not helpful to any student in any way.

PVR is genuinely mean. He is rude if you ask questions and once embarrassed a kid in front of the whole class because he checked his email. Not as much about the professor, but the TA did not add my entire recitation section to the recitation bright-space page so we missed out on many helpful powerpoints.

PVR gives so many extra credit opportunities and goes over the important topics multiple times. The lectures have random iClicker quizzes so make sure you go to them all. ALSO, he exempted about the top 10% of the class (or anyone who had a 98+ after exam 3) from the final so keep that in mind, it is very possible because of all of extra credit!

Sometimes very rude in class to students and is often extremely condescending. When he stops using the slides, the information he shares is incomprehensible. The grad TAs are helpful. Generally, the exams are not very hard. He tells students to ask questions and for suggestions. When students do, he shuts them down immediately/gets defensive.

Ramachandran seemed very passionate when teaching the content, but the way he presented the lectures were so unclear to the point where only himself understands what he's teaching. It got very confusing a lot of the times and he speaks unclearly at times which doesn't exactly help. He's also too fast and will just read the powerpoint
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I have never taken a worse class. The content of organic chemistry is hard enough, but PVR made no effort to explain it in a way that made sense to anyone other than himself. He would assign reading before class and then cover material that wasn't in it. Students who asked questions were publicly shamed. Passing this class was a miracle.

Ramachandran's accent is very thick. He talks too fast and he criticizes students for writing notes in the lecture room and asking questions. His office hours are helpful. The only reason I was able to understand chemistry concepts was by meeting with him outside of class. Although, Ramachandran is very passionate in regards to teaching chemistry.

While CHM115 isn't a hard course, Ramachandran is very hard to understand. You can tell how much he loves the topic, but he talks so fast and will rush to try to get through the lecture in time because he always added extra material. Class isn't hard, but he is difficult to understand.

He is simply impossible to understand. I can pick out a couple words in every sentence and that's it. Chem 115 should not be a hard class but because he is impossible to understand, the lectures are useless. I might as well be self-studying.

Not the worst professor I've had but definitely not the best. I think the difficult nature of the class I took with him made it seem like he was a worse professor than he actually is. It's evident that he knows the subject, but he has trouble communicating it to the class.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: PVR is extremely hard to understand and will belittle his students in front of an entire lecture hall for asking questions. His exams aren't hard but the regrading process is extremely trying and when the average isn't what he wants he'll spend class time basically calling the class stupid.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: PVR was personally very hard to understand for me. I had him for the second half of CHM115 and he clearly knows his stuff, but he went a little too quickly through lectures and I usually was confused by his examples. If possible, I'd recommend going to someone else's lecture.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: He truly finds joy in teaching and wants to help his students learn to love organic chem. I didn't mind having him as a professor, but there were times that I did struggle and he make me feel stupid for not understanding, but he was not doing that out of malice. He knows organic chem like the back of his hand, and hopes that his students will too.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: The class was all over the place. PVR seemed confused, the TA's were confused, the students were confused, I'm still confused and the class is over. The labs were pointless and the TA never provided us with feedback. The quizzes were fine and were similar to the homework. I did well because of Youtube videos and khan academy.

This class felt like a complete waste of my time. The entire class was incredibly unorganized and it felt as if the professor had no idea what he was actually supposed to be doing. On top of this, our online lab assignments felt completely pointless and did not contribute to my understanding of the topics whatsoever. Luckily, the TAs were great.

I'll start by saying that I was terrified for this class. PVR was an incredible instructor, and aided by his TAs made orgo one of my favorite classes this semester. It was challenging but super rewarding. There's ample extra credit on exams (earned over 100% on 3/4), comprehensive study, & office hours + SI are readily available for help.

Ramachandran really isn't that bad of a professor. He's very passionate about his work and really wants to get students engaged in chemistry. But, combined with the fact that his accent is thicker than the textbook, he's nearly impossible to understand if you're not in the first three rows. If you get him, you'll be in for a ride.

Ramachandran goes by PVR, just to throw that out there. His lecture content isn't bad, but he can be hard to understand because of a thick accent in combination with not talking into the microphone. Go to lecture, read his slides, answer the iClicker, and watch the lecture video if you need something repeated. You'll probably be fine.

Literally the worst professor I've had at Purdue. If at all possible do not take him for gen chem. Very confusing lectures and skips through several slides. Have to come to class for attendance points. Exams were nothing like the study guides, and were extremely difficult. Felt like he was trying to get most students to fail
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: He cares about his students, but His accent is super heavy, even sitting right next to him can be difficult, it makes homework hellish to do, however, the exams aren't that bad if you just do the practice ones.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: I had heard a lot of bad things about PVR and was scared about how the class was going to go, but it ended up not being that bad once we got passed nuclear chemistry. If you study and pay attention during lecture the exams are easy. This class should be an easy A if you put in the work and ask questions.

He doesnt really teach anything and is hard to understand. He doesnt work out any examples in class which would actually be helpful. I got an A because I did the extra credit and I took AP chem in high school.

P.V.R. is a really great dude. The exams are incredibly easy, literally copy and pasted from previous years. He gives you access to these exams so as long as you know how to do the old exams you will be fine. On the final he gave us 30 POINTS worth of extra credit, he usually makes it know in lecture what it will be/gives hints. Easy A with a curve
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: I liked PVR- he explained concepts well and knows a lot and does really cool research. Tests were not bad at all- he gave practice exams that were very similar and it's not hard to do well if you make an effort to listen in class and do the homework.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: PVR was quite difficult to understand with his accent (also the mic was never loud enough). The only reason I went to lecture was for the iclicker questions. He definitely cares about his students, though, and he wants us to learn the material. His exams were not overly difficult because they closely followed the practice exams that he gave us.

I really liked PVR. Ive always struggled with chem but I actually did alright in orgo and understood/enjoyed the course. Sit up front in class to better hear him. Dont expect to be on your phone, hell call you out on it. He tried to help us succeed as much as possible. Just pay attention and go to class, be respectful; hes a good guy.

Dr. Ramachandran is a very smart man, but has little knowledge on teaching. His accent is very thick, so I suggest sitting near the front so you can understand him better. The topics he covers are difficult and require a lot of attention outside of class. Overall, he is a very good professor. Go to class for the iClicker points and pay attention.

Very difficult to understand. He definitely knows what he is talking about and is super passionate. He thinks pretty highly of himself, too. If you don't sit in the front row, he will automatically think the worst of you. I sat in the fourth row and he scolded me for not paying attention (but I most certainly was) via email.

Seems passionate about the topic, but extremely hard to understand because of his accent. Focuses on the front-row students, and only makes eye contact with the first few rows. Rarely explains answers to his pop-quiz questions, and complains a lot about students getting the answers wrong. Doesn't assign much homework besides reading the textbook.
A bit hard to follow in class (doesn't always go sequentially). Does have powerpoint which are information heavy, and they are posted on BB.

While students who interrupt class are annoying... He will call them out and spend class time complaining about how you can't interrupt him because the material is a lot to go over, while he is interrupting his own lecture? And honestly printing off the lecture notes beforehand doesn't help because he is all over the place. Rent the book. It helps.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY--Tests are tough

reviews.comment: Very condescending and sometimes flat out rude. Chastises students who ask questions in class, and tells them how the content is "so easy" when in reality it isn't. He also embarasses students in front of the whole class if they answer one of his mid-lecture questions wrong. But he is very passionate about Orgo, and this makes the class bearable.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES

reviews.comment: I am usually pretty good at communicating with people with heavy accents but this was not the case. Orgo is already difficult in my opinion and to not be able to understand your professor makes it even worse. Attending the SI sessions really helped and I would recommend going to them right off the bat. All the resources I utilized avoided the prof
The accent is hard to get past. However, he wants to see everyone in his class succeed
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: He's rude, demeaning, and does not seem to care whether or not you learn the material. He makes anyone who asks a question during the lecture feel stupid in front of everyone, and forget about asking him questions during his office hours because he is even more chastising. Don't take this course with this professor if at all possible.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: Organic chem with PVR was by far the most difficult class I've taken at Purdue. Hard to understand lecture material and his accent is pretty thick and he speaks quietly. Often loses the classes attention.
Very hard to understand.

I don't know some people give PVR high rating. Rather you manage to do well enough to have the curve make you pass the class or not, it doesn't change the fact that he's a horrible professor. Very all over the place when teaching with an accent that is difficult to understand.

After gen chem, everybody is scared of orgo but this guy will make your life easier becoz he has a real teaching technique that will help you in his class n in other classes as well.PVR is very passionate abt the subject and he will keep it fun for u...handouts, homeworks, recitation, all very helpful for exam prep.

He is AWFUL. He is so hard to understand, which makes chem even more confusing since many words already sound alike. He only asks students who do well to rate him on here, so that is what I am doing. I did very well in this class but only because I taught myself literally everything. If you ask him a question he makes you feel very stupid.

I was scared of Organic chemistry.  PVR made it enjoyable.  His handouts highlight key concepts and make it easy to learn.  Graded homework helped to prepare for the exams, whcih were very fair.  He gets annoyed when the class is disturbed by talking and texting.

His teaching method and teaching materials he distributed to us, were straightforward and very helpful in preparing midterms. Moreover, graded homeworks and non-graded homeworks were also helpful in preparing midterms.
I enjoyed the class very much.  The handouts were very helpful; could do without the text.  Graded homework package prepared me well for the exams, which were very fair.

He really does love organic chemistry, and does his best to get his students to understand.  A lot of the material is based on his handouts, so try not to miss class.  He does not like people talking or texting in his class and will stop the class to address any interruptions.

Focused more on ways to make the tests so that the average would be higher than normal for organic, not caring how individual students did. all you had to do was memorize the notes and you could get a high grade without  understanding. Some found this easy and others didn't. Constantly lectured about the people who didn't come to class.

In my own opinion I would describe the class material as "very hard".  But that difficulty rating can be brought down significantly depending on how students use him. If you listen during lecture, read the assigned material, and use his tips and tricks throughout the year I am fairly confident that you will receive an A. Excellent and fair prof.

Ramachandran's organic chemistry knowledge can be, sometimes, intimidating.  However, he is very helpful if you go to his office.  He always responded to my requests for additional meetings beyond regular office hours.  Go to class for extra credit quizzes.  Has a heavy accent, but I got used to it soon. Overall, excellent Professor.
This Professor made CHM 256 fun.  Nice course packets and summary sheets.  Emphasis on the reaction mechanisms that we should know helped.  The TAs were also helpful.

I was afraid of this class at the beginning of the semester.  I felt comfortable as the semester progressed and received an A in the course.  The handouts were very helpful.  The Prof. emphasizes on the logic of organic mechanisms.  I enjoyed the class very much

I liked the way Prof. Ramachandran teaches orgo.  He takes teaching seriously and motivates you to think.  Exam questions are from material covered in class.  Gives lots of extra credits.  If you go to class regularly, you can earn good grade.
One of the best Professors in my two years at Purdue.  If you go to the office hours, he is very helpful.  I got an A in this course and learnt lot of stuff.
The prof were much helpful. I thought he try very hard. Thank proffesor for making good class.
This was one of the class where I learned a lot. He is a very good teacher and explains things really well.

BY FAR THE WORST TEACHER ON PURDUE'S CAMPUS. if you ask a question in class he will make you feel stupid. make use of the TAs. This was his first semester back from a 5 year probationary period. He gave us 40 points of extra credit on the first exam so he would have an A average only to make the second exam impossible since grades were too high

Couldn't understand him at first and thought I was going to do really bad in his class; however if you read his notes and try to understand them yourself, it really isn't too bad(well Mass Spec was horrible, but other than that...) However, you should only take his class if you have great memorization skills (for learning mechanisms)
Got an A with this guy. Make use of office hours/recitation and you'll get an A. He offers ample opportunities for extra credit as well, and the TA's are really hot.
If you go to office hours, he is very helpful and helped me understand chemistry better. If you get the course packets and do the homework you will do well in the class.

I don't know what class the other commenters were attending, but I thought his class was great!!!!!  He was very helpful both in and out of class, and had great TAs. He helped us understand by filling in the blanks and actually teaching chemistry. Rather than giving 'memorize only' material, he helped us learn it the right way. Learned so much!!!!!
Mostly ordinary.  Claimed to spend "5 hours" preparing for each lecture (sure...)

Why? Why keep this guy around! Just let him do research, because he is a terrible teacher! His lectures are hard to follow, and when I asked him a question during office hours he told me that he already went over that in lecture ... Ugh! Just give him his Noble Prize so he'll leave innocent students alone ! If you get him -- DROP!
somebody, please, take this guy away from Purdue!
This man made me despise chemistry
By far the worst professor I have ever had.  Very hard to understand and not helpful.  Expects that you know everything already.  Tests were very difficult.  Avoid this professor at all costs!

Not the worst professor I've ever had, but close to it. I had a strong B going into the final, got average, and my grade was dropped to a C. I drove over break to talk to him about it, and he refused to change my grade. Sometimes hard to understand him. He expects students to know as much as he does. Obsessed with the Nobel Prize.
I failed chm256 with a curve when i took his class. the next semester, i retook the class with anothe professor and got an A without a curve... he's just a really bad teacher
Don't risk a good course here

Worst professor ever. I don't know if he is still in rotation for the 255/256 classes... if he is I suggest you drop the class and take with Chmielewski (she is a great teacher) the following semester.

Awful awful awful professor. I didn't have a clue as to what was going on because he clarify one freakin point. If you have this guy for organic chem ever, my advice is to drop it immediatly and take it from Harry Morrison next semester.
Worst professor I've ever had. Got a decent grade both semesters, but it was torture.

He is SO unfriendly and unwilling to answer questions. He is very arrogant and feels like his students should "just know" things without his having to teach them. He makes the averages on his tests about a 40% ON PURPOSE...I think he likes to see people perform badly in his class.
Hard to understand, but does an alright job teaching. Exams are hard but have lots of bonus points. Not a bad professor.
This guy is the worst prof ever hes such a jerk...avoid like the plague
No Comments
No Comments
this professor def does not know how to teach..and his exams are impossible and he's made many people i kno feel very bad during his office hours!! also he grades unfairly!
Wasn't very helpful. Told us that it's in the book and we should know it.

I had him for both organic classes..not only is his class hard but my god is it boring and frankly i never learned anything in his class i had to ask someone else to clarify things..did i mention he has an accent i dont think anyone would enjoy his class
sometimes i wonder if he even thinks about his class before he teaches it
Does this guy get pleasure from this?
Held "help-sessions" that were not announced to students, extremely unclear, unfriendly.
How did this guy get tenure?
Avoid like the plague.  Once said  A monkey could have done better on this test

Thank goodness the class switches professors after 8 weeks or I genuinely am afraid I would fail. I have never gotten this low of test scores. Nothing he teaches is relevant. He is hard to understand and tells you in the beginning that his accent shouldn't affect you. Good luck!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: If you have your lecture with him, go to another time with a different professor. its really hard to understand him for multiple reasons. One, his accent along with the fact he speaks very unclear and you can barely hear him. His slides are unorganized and he doesn't explain the topics well at all. Go to office hours with TA's, they really help.

If you plan on taking this class and want to actually understand what is happening, choose a different professor. He has a heavy accent and doesn't know enough English to properly teach students. This class is hard enough, but he makes it way harder than necessary.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Test heavy

reviews.comment: I tried to give him a chance even through the negative reviews but they're correct. Rambles about irrelevant information, is difficult to understand, and in general does a bad job. I never learned anything from lecture. Most learning was done through homework and studying. The course content isn't hard but he makes it harder than it has to be.
