His accent makes the lectures impossible to understand. Attendance is based on if you get iclickers correct. Everything that you learn will be from the textbook, nothing from him. He does not cover what is on tests.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: If you can get another time with another lecturer DO IT. Everything that you learn will be from the textbook, nothing from him. It is impossible to understand what he's saying, uses incorrect grammar structure, and does not cover what is on tests. I am trying very hard to be respectful, but every single thing I learned was from textbooks.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: His slides jump from topic to topic, with no explanation of anything. Literally said: “what does n [variable in an equation we were seeing] mean? We will learn later”. Then we use that equation for the rest of the lecture, and he still doesn't explain
Class attendance is based on if you get his iclickers correct. Meaning, having issues with content? No attendance for you!
Accent makes classes impossible to understand.

I understand that everyone complains about his accent, but it is because it impedes learning. Don't go to lecture and just watch the lecture videos after with subtitles on to save yourself a headache. Otherwise, his lectures aren't horrible, they're just not good either.

He has an accent, and he doesn't explain new concepts very well, so don't expect to learn too much from the actual lectures. Expect to put more work than you would like to into this class. Also, he's particular about people whispering in lecture (even when you can't hear them) and will just stop lecturing, staring at them until they stop talking.
Get another lecturer, he is too quick and you cannot understand half the words he says

He has years of experience in teaching the course. The lecture pace is quick and he does have an accent, making it difficult for some to follow along. Look at the slides that he sends out beforehand and after class, ace homework and quizzes as much as you can at office hours, and you'll be okay when it comes to exams. Sample exams provided.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Good luck for the rest of the major cuz the profs only get worse from here.
This "professor" assigns data entry as homework in a 400-level class. His classes offer about the same level of insight as an AI-generated LinkedIn chart.

The professor is funny but sometimes comes off as a little snarky if you read him wrong. As for the class, the grading scale is a bit ridiculous, with a 93% being the threshold for an A. The exams for the course have a high impact on the final grade, as well as the group project, which the TAs grade inconsistently with what's taught in the lab.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Whenever he is teaching, it looks like he does not wanna be there. Class is chill and labs are also chill.

Bad professor, hates his job and does not want to be there. Is very snarky and doesn't feel like answering students questions, semester long group project that is graded harshly and the trophy is awarded at random with no rhyme or reason to who gets it, would avoid at all costs.

Prof Thomas is too funny. Some days, he's super mean-spirited and passive-aggressive, while other days, he randomly starts rapping the lesson. Overall, class is not too hard, but the fact that a 93 is a B is insane. Be aware, the group project is graded pretty harshly. The exams are not too bad, just memorize the terms and acronyms in the textbook.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Group projects

reviews.comment: Paul Thomas was not a bad lecturer per se but his passive aggressive tone towards students and abnormal grading scale (94 being an A, and 93 being a B) is simply diabolical. If it weren't for the semester long group project, the class could warrant the grading scale but the project alone could drop you a grade level. No office hours too.

In what class is the cutoff for an A a 94? In what class do you get a fraudulent, passive-aggressive lecturer? Doesn't sound like he's enthusiastic about teaching. Maybe him leaving industry after only 3 years wasn't his decision I don't know! I cheer him on 100% to go back into industry and out of our curriculum. Avoid at all costs!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: I did not have a good semester with Paul Thomas. He was constantly passive aggressive, even to the students that tried their hardest to participate in his boring lectures. As the year went on, I was getting a feeling that he hates his job. For the content taught in this class, a 93 should not be a B. All in all, a horrible professor.
We see the fake ratemyprofessor reviews and profiles that you're making paul.

What did past students do to Paul that makes him so passive aggressive and one of the worst graders I've ever had?? Dude cared more about going viral on insta reels and bad raps than actually teaching the material. 93% is a B, are you kidding me? Grades like this will be the most important class you'll take but teaches like he doesn't even care.

I have never seen a situation where you can get a 93.4 and end up with a B. He is passive-aggressive and acts as if everyone around him is incompetent. He'll gloss over lecture slides, acting as if they are unimportant, but they will be on the exam, which was moved to in-person because someone from an unrelated class cheated.

To get a A is a 94, I'd recommend taking this course over the summer. He has students who suck him off in class to get benefits no one else has. He seems like he hates his job and grades like its an electrical engineering class when its a class I could teach with my eyes closed. I have no clue how he got a PHD degree.
B=93 :) If u get 93 before exam 2, u can skip it cuz 84=93=B
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: It's supposed to be an easy class, and it was before, but I have no idea what he has experienced to make the course so terrible now. Nonsense grading, very tough grader, 93% and B, and you only get attendance 3 times a semester, which adds up to 5%, meaning you lose an A directly after you didn't arrive before class. Second midterm is in-person!!!!

I think I can teach this course, as I just read the slides, do attendance, give class activities, and hold exams. I don't know how he got a Ph.D. degree. I cannot imagine that the grading rubric is given by a normal human-being, as 93.49 is a B.

Comes to class with a pessimistic attitude. Vague instructions on the assignments. They don't foster an environment where I want to be engaged. They do the bare minimum in presenting course materials, and give exams that don't include half of what was presented. A 93 is considered a B, when the course's difficulty does not warrant that.

Not the worst professor I've had when it comes to lecturing, but that's the only thing he's got going for him. Have a question for him? Expect him to roll his eyes at you, ignore your email, or just belittle you. His grading system is pretty stupid, and he just never acts like he wants to be there.

93% = B.
Yeah, nope.

Did not have the best experience with Prof. Thomas. He is super passive aggressive when you talk to him and seems to hate his job. There were two times I got docked a lot of points on coursework for small stuff and his graders aren't helpful. The class is manageable, but make sure to READ THE DIRECTIONS for assignments to avoid dealing with him.

Paul needs to get out of teaching, he clearly hates his job and blames the students for it. This class shouldn't be a required credit in the first place. He has no idea how to manage a large class and will blame students for his crappy system for getting attendance.
Dude just needs to quit his job since all he does is complain. He has a massive ego, lil bro thinks he should be the president of the world and everyone else is incompetent.
Professor Thomas is super passive aggressive  towards students which is why I gave him one star

Professor Thomas as a human being is someone who is passive-aggressive. Once I asked him for help and he spoke to me in a passive-aggressive tone. The class is not too difficult if you put in the work, however, you should try to know what you're doing so you don't have to deal with his passive-aggressive nature. This is for 480 FYI
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I had Paul Thomas for CNIT 480, and Bro had Gooner on his Instagram description; he's passive-aggressive and tends to be in a bad mood a lot, which he then takes out on students.

I had Paul Thomas for CNIT 480, and it was clear he had difficulty separating between his responsibilities as an instructor and outside of this role. He is passive aggressive towards his students and clearly does not respect his students either.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Bro did a pop presentation, like what.... fade pauls classes

Prof. Thomas was a good lecturer and a fair grader. There were a few pop quizzes throughout the semester but they are only graded for participation. There is big group project throughout the semester, it is not very difficult.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Professor Thomas is a good dude and tries to make lectures interesting. The grading criteria is broken into many things so doing bad in one category doesn't weigh you down. If you come to class, pay attention, and do the necessary work to your best ability you'll have no problem getting an A.

Paul Thomas has stopped trying to create an engaging classroom. He often calls out inattentive students mid-lecture, then shifts to a punishing tone for the rest of the class. For example, he'll say, "Since you all seem to know this, we can skip topic, topic, topic," maintaining a degrading tone throughout the lecture.

Overall good lecturer and seems to care about your learning. Occasionally pop quizzes are in class but are done online. Group project teammates are randomly selected. However not flexible with grades at all.

Consistently picks the same 3 students to answer questions during his lectures which inevitably makes the lectures so boring. Had an 89.4 (89.5 gets rounded up to an A) and he wouldn't add 0.1 points to my grade even though he knew me well and I had participated and initiated conversations with him before; he's rarely flexible.

Really funny and makes lectures fun to the best of his ability. You can tell he cares about the content that he's telling you and wants to see you succeed. Also, he does in-class activities so although he doesn't take attendance, you'll want to go to class so a) you learn and b) you don't miss out on in-class activities.
His lectures are good but sometimes boring, the labs are various UML diagrams and a lot of Microsoft Access.
Good instructor, a little strict. Will not give extensions without large amount of advance notice, will not take late work. Pretty funny.

Overall, Dr. Thomas is a good professor. He creates interesting lectures and has an engaging personality. He also answers emails very quickly too. This class definitely has a lot of work (mainly being one semester-long group project) but it is manageable.

The lecture slides are organized terribly. She does not elaborate on the content and simply reads off slides.The few times she actually attempts to go in depth on a concept, her explanation only complicates things more. I'm retaking this class, and she still hasn't incorporated feedback. Stats is general is not hard, but she makes it impossible.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment:
genuinely laughable course.
exam review is 6 questions.
best of purdue btw
Not good at all
Cannot teach I ended up having to teach stat to myself

Professor Porterfield is a genuinely kind and caring person. Unfortunately, her tests are EXTREMELY tough, and her lectures aren't very informative. Excel activities get confusing, and there's no curve for the exams, although the average is well within the 60s. Good luck taking this class!
Her lectures are occasionally confusing and can be very boring. The tests are really only ok because you get cheat sheets. Just do the homework and have a really good cheat sheet for exams.

She's a nice person, but she'll teach you the bare minimum when it comes to what is needed for the exam. She'll give a set of review problems, but there's no guarantee that it'll be on exam. She'll also put the most random concepts that she skips past during lecture that would make you think aren't important. All in all, good luck.
Lectures are unbearably boring and do not offer any support. Tests are also extremely difficult.

Idek where to start with Pam Porterfield's STAT 30301 class. This class made me rethink life. AVOID AT ALL COSTS. Do not be misled by her "niceness"; her exams are unnecessarily difficult. It's like she writes the exams like the practice exam problems, but jumbles up the words so you purposely don't understand what the problems are asking. GOODLUCK
Worse than the Colts Defense. I literally taught myself all of the material. Just watch the review videos and do the practice exam problems. That is your best chance doing well in the course.

I am blessed to be done with this course and Pam Porterfield as a whole and move on with my degree, that was the worst academic semester of my time here.

worst professor I've had in 3 years on campus. Exams are impossible. Excel is encouraged on homework but can't be used on exams. Zero curves or extra credit even though exams average in the 60s. does 1 "practice" problem a week and acts like thats enough to learn the material. avoid at all cost.

She is a good person, and her teaching is kinda understandable. I have no stats background, but her lessons are really easy to understand, given that English is not even my first language. Attendance isn't mandatory, so I didn't come. I watched videos and asked her questions next class. She's kind, but I understand her style doesn't suit everyone.

In all honesty, I don't understand the negativity. I'm not trying to invalidate anyone's opinions (she does call out individuals during exams) but I personally thought Pam was great! The lectures didn't always help me, so I stopped attending regularly, but the ones I did go to, she seemed genuinely invested in her students and cared a lot

She should just write down to not come to lecture and read the textbook on the syllabus. Worst professor I have had by far and this is an intro class. Your guessing skills better be good because the test is nothing like what you learn in class.

reviews.comment: Genuinely worst experience ever.Pam needs 2 read the reviews & take constructive criticism.Tests r nothing like practice test,excels r pointless,class is not made to try and help students actually learn.Ridiculously hard tests 4 a INTRO class & the averages are horrible. She needs to seriously consider changing this class it's insane.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: pam is sweet but boy does this class suck. exams are 64% of your grade in total, and they are nothing like any of the review material she posts beforehand. have never gone into an exam feeling prepared. no clue why recitations are excel based when we don't use it on homework or the exams, practically an hour wasted each week. avoid if you can.

Sweet lady, but a terrible teacher. The lectures drag on and don't make sense, the excel activities seem pointless, and the exams are very hard. Waiting to get my grade back but hoping to receive at least a C-. If you don't have to take this class, don't. Expect to have to study a lot for the exams.

The professor does not believe in curving the class, no matter the average. Most of the people I know have taken this course twice because they did not pass the first time, and she still does not give curve. Do not take this class!! If you take it be ready to study so much before your exams and the exams to be tougher.
i have to retake it

If you have to take this try to take the econ stat class instead with vargas. The grading is hard and very picky. and cant teach. also it will be the end of the semester and they will start changing your grades for excel activities after the already graded them TA's "help" on ea activities then you get a bad grade.

Terrible Professor. She is a nice person, but if your class average for an exam is in the 60s with no curve that tells it is not on the students. No clue how she is still teaching here without a curve. Will ruin you're GPA. DO NOT TAKE!
Do not go to class. Learn nothing.  Getting a B in this class because I taught myself the material.  Not a good teacher.   Kind soul but terrible teacher.
Worst professor on campus at Purdue.

I feel like she taught in just about the most confusing manner. I never went to lecture past the first day and I've preformed well on the 2 exams so far. I watch the lectures from home and take notes. You get a note sheet for each exam. Just cram material on there and take notes and you'll be fine. A or B.

She is the most non-understanding professor I've had, for example, I lost ALL of my notes for all of my classes, and after I told her the situation I was in, in a snarky, fake-friendly, way, she was hardstuck on a no. I don't like to rant, but it seems like she wants you to "slip up". My genuine, best way to describe her as a professor is a Karen.
No care for students learning, just posts lecturers and expects the worst of students
Inhumane towards students
It's wild that she's the only prof teaching this course... would rate a 0/5 if possible. Just like everyone else said run. but fr. do it.
Actually the worst class ever, run please
Class structure is horrible and she makes it so much worse. One problem a week does not help you learn anything.

reviews.comment: Avoid this classs at all costs
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Terrible professor
WHY IS THERE A NEW METHOD FOR FINDING Q3. WHO CHANGES IT.

This class is made to set you up for failure. The only possible way you will do well is if you took AP Stats in high school. Fill-in-the-blank notes for a stem class is a total joke. Her practice quizzes are 138 questions long and somehow dont relate to the test in the slightest. Worst teacher ever who doesnt curve F's btw. Nice person tho.

This is an awful class! I would avoid at all costs. Exams are 60% and there is never enough times to take the exam. The exams are extremely difficult. She has 6 lectures a week for distance and she is very hard to follow.

This is my third stats class. I earned A's in the first two. I spend hours studying before exams and about 3 hours on each homework and Excel assignment to make sure I understand concepts. I watch all lectures for the formulas, even though they aren't helpful. The exams are poorly worded and very different from the practice problems.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: This has genuinely been the worst class I have ever taken at Purdue. Pam's lectures are hard to follow, and it is difficult to keep pace with her and try to take notes. The exams are 64% of your overall grade, and she has absolutely no extra credit or curving for the course. If you can avoid this, then please do- and good luck to all that must.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: avoid avoid avoid avoid avoid (also TAs get personal satisfaction from seeing you fail)

I have never met a more incompetent person in my life. Exam 2 was a mess and no where near enough time to answer the 25, paragraph long, questions in one hour. Her lectures are not only unhelpful but what she manages to get out is completely incorrect. She has got to go. Avoid at all costs
Class is 60% exam. No examples in class lectures so when it is test time, you don't know how to use the 20 different formulas you have to use. She pretty much prays on your downfall...

Just took the second exam today. Genuinely what was that. You could spend hours doing the practice problems and even study in the most efficient way yet still the exam is basically designed for you to not succeed whatsoever. Walked out of this exam today in absolute shock. She is ok as a person but as lecturer, good lord.

She does not set her students up for success at all. Exams are incredibly difficult and she purposely gives harder curveball questions that are worded/presented differently than the practice problems and homework, so when you get to the exam you're clueless. Lectures and slides are also useless and she doesn't curve exams.
It's so so bad.

Oh boy! Don't even get me started on this class. I would AVOID this class and take it online through another school. She expects an hour is enough time to finish an exam with 30 questions. Don't mind the positive reviews, as those are definitely not from students. I hope this review helps in 10+ years because she will definitely still be teaching!
Ended the year w 89.5 and rounded to a B. Good luck.

Idk if pam means well, I failed the class and had to retake it which sucked. I took the online section and I found the book was 1000x better than going to lecture. I don't think she's a good lecturer and the added stress of failed a class really sucked but it worked out cause reading the book actually helped me learn. Binomials shouldn't be hard :(
It is day 2 of lecture and one of her examples she used is simply not correct. The only reason I know this is because I took AP Stats. I don't even know what to do with this

I would say what makes her such a bad professor is her lack of awareness. She thinks that her style of teaching with fill in the blank notes is beneficial. I think she truely believes that she is a good teacher but is actully horrible. The recitation also is useless and you may be stuck with a very poor TA. Read the book and use other reasources
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Prof. Porterfield is a horrendous teacher. I took AP statistics in high school and can say for sure that her explanations and examples are lacking in many ways. Fill in the blank notes are terrible for teaching stats and her example slides essentially require you to have touch screen or a mouse to copy down. Skip lecture, get the slides from video.

This is the worst class I've ever taken. She is a nice person, but cannot teach. Read the textbook and do the homework to study. Excel Activities are pointless. It's hard to get through exams in time. Doesn't curve even when averages are in the 50s. Worst lecturer I've ever had, skip class and watch the videos instead. Has fill in the blank notes

I actually liked Pam. She is good at teaching the concepts, uses examples, and is more than willing to help during office hours (which I utilized several times). The exams are difficult and are in a "new setting" as she would say. As a result, the homework and exam review help reinforce the concepts but are not in the same setting as the exam.

As others have said, the lectures are worthless and so is her teaching skills. Your TA has a good chance of barely speaking English. She will require you to know formula that SHE NEVER TAUGHT and only appears on practice problems. Her study sheets and practice is useless. You're best bet is to try to get a previous students exams.

Pam Porterfield is the worst professor I have had in all 14 years of school so far. While she doesn't seem like a bad person, she should never have been given a teaching position. Her lectures are close to useless and her homework do not at all prepare you for the exams. You are better off learning the material yourself -try to take over the summer

I got an A because I took AP Stats in high school. Homework and Recitation are beyond pointless and achieve nothing in helping you succeed in the class. It would be smart to learn the material on your own outside of lectures and hope that prepares you for the Exams.
How is she still employed? This is genuinely the worst academic experience of my life. No professors average GPA should be below a 2.3... No amount of studying can prepare you
Pam stop writing reviews about yourself
Awful, Just awful.
Worst academic experience of my life bro. Hate ts

Pam, this class made me question every decision I have ever made in my entire life. To call this class unorganized would be generous because of how hard it was to access useful material. A 6 year old boy could teach better then Pam Porterfield. Spend your lecture time doing something useful rather then attending this atrocity.

The online class grading is split into five sections. Homework is worth 20%, lecture questions worth 4%, Excel activities worth 15%, exams worth 60%, and the course eval worth 1%. Homework and Excel activities have virtually nothing to do with the exams, except for the concepts. Don't take the online version if you don't have to. AP Stats helps.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She is the worst stats teacher at the school. Really tough grader and do not expect her to curve anything even when the class averages on tests are super low. Her tests hard and contain lots of stuff not on the reviews.

Pam rambles a lot with her examples which are incomparable to homework. She consistently exceeds the class time. Her exams are NOTHING like the practice exams. EA's are not helpful and tedious. No curve even though the exam averages are around 50%. Recitations are useless. TA's have no idea what is going on and what Pam is doing. AVOID THIS CLASS!!
Easily the worst professor I've had. Exam practice questions are irrelevant, especially on the second exam. Get ready to read to survive.

reviews.comment: Yeah bro she's not great at all, class is terribly run and TAs all grade differently depending on their mood. Would say avoid her like the plague but Purdue has decided that she's the only one capable of teaching this course so until then good luck if you didn't take AP Stats
Pam Porterfield is the sole reason tenure should not exist. I have no words

I took AP Stats in high school and still did not get an A. Lectures are abysmal. At the end of the year, only 30ish people came to lecture because they were so bad. Would recommend watching the lecture videos instead of going in person. Hard to get through tests in time. Study by doing the homework. She is a nice person, but not a good professor.

She is very sweet but this course is so dragged out and so much of the content taught is unnecessary. 60% of the grade is exams and its all math questions that were taught in the most dragged out and laborious way. Just practice a lot of questions and start early if u are taking this class.
Oh man! This class was something. Definition on why tenure shouldn't exist

She doesn't get the concept that her students have lives outside of her class. Gave an exam on Election Day even though the university advised professors not to, so I wasn't able to get to the polls and vote.

Makes the course a lot more challenging then it should be, makes mistakes in lecture and in notes all the time. No curves or extra credit on exams. The EA and homework assignments are free points but you have to lock in on the exams or your pretty much ducked.

this course put me in depression
does not curve her exams, exams are nothing like the practice ones and lectures are so boring AVOID
Worst professor ever do not take this class. It is because like a independent study because she does nothing useful for you to learn.

She is a very bad professor. She does fill in the blank slides which does not help. She will not curve an exam that is 50 percent or lower. She will give reviews that do not help on the exams as well. The homework and recitation are just busy work that do not actually help. I stopped going to recitation because my TA useless.
Don't listen to head, read the book it's your best friend in the course. She can't explain anything so It's always good to just look at the book.
Doesn't curve an exam even if the average is in the 50s. Insanely chinese TA's that I can never understand.

reviews.comment: Took this class three times and still got a C, maybe its a me thing but I know I am not the only one who took three tries.

Gotta be the worst teacher I've ever had. Only person I have ever interacted with that talks in 0.75x speed and has a voice that makes me want to bang my head on a wall. Lectures were brutal, she teaches a stats class with fill in the blank slides. Recitation was useless because it's excel and you don't use excel on the exams. She needs to be fired

i hate this class. her slides are fill-in-the-blank style, and she can't lecture in a timely manner. Half of each class is going over what she was supposed to lecture about last class, and the last half is for the content you were supposed to cover today. Practice exams are useless and don't provide an accurate picture of what the exams look like.
Easy content made difficult by a subpar professor. She's nice, but her slides are fill-in-the-blank and she never finishes her lectures on time. I'm only succeeding because I took AP stats.

Pam is nice but terrible at teaching. Her slides are vague and don't match the exams, which are much more detailed. Also do the hw and excel as it gives grade boosts. Take ONLY if you need!

The first exam might go fine but the second exam is something different. Exam practice sheets are barely helpful. Exam review slideshow is entirely irrelevant to the exams, focus on textbook.
very inconsiderate and unhelpful didnt even really try to give me an extension on the exam when i had mono and said if i did i would get penalized wtf is that
One day I'll be out of this course :(
she hates her job so she hates us

only chance you make it out alive is if you took AP STAT in highschool
Worst prof. Ever
My best advice is skip lecture and just read the book, only way you'll do well in the class
Makes learning statistics more difficult than it should be.
she didnt curve when the exam average was below 70. succeeding in class is genuinely difficult

Pam Porterfield is the reason tenure shouldn't exists. I've never heard anyone say 1 good thing about her class. Lectures: Fill in the blank slides (like that's helpful) Homework: Doesn't relate to lecture material whatsoever. Recitation: God help you the TA won't speak English and it's a waste of time. WORST CLASS EVER. DO NOT TAKE.

Having homework due the night before an exam and learning a new topic the day of is not ideal. Students are trying to study and prepare for a midterm while having to worry about all of this other work at the same time.
I think in all my life I have to say this was by far the worst professor I have ever had in my life.

those leaving positive reviews are ones who got perfect scores in AP Stats, if you have never taken AP Stats you are doomed, an intro to stats class was not enough to do well. find a group in your recitation to work on the excel with, to do well on the homework it needs to be looked up. everything is excel based, while the exam is not.
Horrible, exam on election day, not enough time to complete exams, very low exam averages, refuses to curve, was .001 off on a homework question and she would not even give me half credit.
Has a serious case of Resting Witch Face
Lovely person, but awful teacher
she will NOT curve
Words cannot describe how much I despise her.

reviews.comment: The bane of my existence. Statistics just doesn't have to be as hard as she makes this class. We had problems on the exams that we had literally never seen before and it screwed me over. Never accessible outside of class other than to tell you to "ask the TA's". She gave an exam on Election Day despite the school telling the professors not to.

I am just happy I passed the class. I think she cares about the subject and about teaching but she is not very good at it. Homework takes forever, and the labs have nothing to do with the class. Exams were the worst part, only an hour to do 40 questions each needing their own formula. Even with a study guide, it was nearly impossible for me.

I just genuinely do not think that Porterfield is good at teaching and therefore the class was so much harder than it should have been. Additionally, her grading system is flawed and so even though I did well on assignments, activities, and exams, it was still a STRUGGLE to get the grade that I did. Go to recitation!!!
Awesome professor!
First off, Pam stop writing your own reviews enough is enough. Second off terrible class stay away from it as long as you can

Professor Pam is amazing! She really knows her stuff and always make sure you understand what's going on, especially when it comes to help you preparing for exams. Super approachable and always there to help you succeed.

Blatant slander won't help anyone, so here we are:
If you are sick, in an accident, have interviews, expect to lose points. No help at all. 
What to do?
Gear up to study for yourself. The sample exams are a good indicator for what you will see on the exam, so master every problem. AI is your best TA in this class, the faculty is basically useless.
Fantastic professor!

Easily one of the worst professors I have had in my 3 semesters here. I have not met anyone who liked her, and 99% of people doing well in this class is no thanks to her. who the heck does fill in the blank notes for a stats class. barely managed a B in this class solely by spending around 30 hours studying for the final.

Prof Porterfield was pretty nice as a person, but I never found her lectures helpful; the class itself seemed disorganized, and the homework assignments don't adequately prepare anyone for exams. I had to teach myself the content through Khan Academy and online resources. The material in this class is helpful but it should be taught differently.
I SOMEHOW GOT AN A+ THROUGH THIS CLASS, I SAT THERE STUDYING FOR HOURS ON END! #PAMSUCKS

I was incredible at AP Stats in high school and had an amazing teacher so I feel adequately qualified to say that Pam is not a good stats prof. I had to help multiple friends with the material and our average on exams was consistently very bad. Already having done AP stats was the only reason I did well. If you have to take this class, sorry.
She's writing false reviews to make her rating higher

Anyone who gives porterfield more than 1 star is her herself. Genuinely the worst professor I have ever had, and you can't even escape her. Lectures are fill in the blank notes, for statistics…that just doesn't work. Exams are insanely different from homework, I know more hard working students who just failed out than students that actually passed.

Professor Porterfield is fantastic. Unfortunately, some students who struggled with the course choose to leave negative reviews online, even though it's an easy class for those who put in the effort. Stay clear of those students, as their negative attitude and poor work ethic can be contagious.

professor is very nice, but content is not explained very well. only one homework assignment every week.homework is also very easy and clear, then you take the exam and the questions are 10x harder. there is also recitation once a week which is completely useless. you just work on excel and the exams and homework have no excel use at all,poor setup
Useful class

Porterfield is a nice person who keeps her students updated on Brightspace.The subject is important and the class material reflects this. HW's and ExcelActivities take time and consideration to complete. Personally, I felt there were a few curveballs thrown at me during the Exams that I wasn't totally prepared for.
Nice person, but her exams can be difficult. Useful subject

The class is kinda in a weird spot. There are lots of free points in the excel activities and hw. But, the exams felt overly hard due to the vast amount of content they cover. Additionally, there was no emphasis put on helping students understand why we're doing each test and what it means in context so overall understanding seemed low.

reviews.comment: The tests are indifferent from the material taught in lecture and the material given for homework. This makes the course slightly frustrating, but if that may be how Education is, then so be it.

she might genuinely be the worst teacher I've had in my life, she actually cannot teach, the content is not hard but it's confusing when you have to teach yourself the entire course.she doesn't curve her exams while also teaching nothing in lecture is crazy. can't even avoid her either, she's the only one who teaches this class. HIRE SOMEONE NEW!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: She really just doesn't seem to care about how well the class is understanding the material. Her lectures are very boring and the labs don't help you understand the material on the exams. She doesn't post completed slides and doesn't make it easy to find exam material.
So bad at explaining the concepts. Going to lecture is a waste of time because I don't learn anything.

its a stats class yet all she does is read off the slides and then the recitation is all about excel not actually learning the material. The reviews are nothing like the exams. The lectures slides are not updated from the previous semester. No exam corrections posted so there is no way to learn what you did wrong. GOOD LUCK
This class has no reason to be this hard. Only reads the lecture slides, don't explain the concepts that well. No curves
this is the worst professor at purdue.
the exams are very hard and the the lecture slide does NOT help. Recitation is also a waste of time.

Homeworks make no sense, no curves and the majority of info I learned was outside of class. The class is so hard for no reason, I went to every lecture and most of it was confusing and redundant. She runs class until the very last minute. She is lovely I just think the class is structured horribly.
Shes the only professor for this class. GOOD LUCK. This is the worst taught class ive ever been apart of. NO CURVES

She just reads off lecture slides. I learned more outside of the class on my own than in lectures. Be prepared for many of the short-response questions on the exams. She also doesn't curve the course, even if the class average is really low.
THE WORST!!!!
Porterfield is lovely but the class is boring, overcomplicated and annoying. The TA's try their best but it's hard when the class is taught so weirdly. The homework is irrelevant to the class.
Stay away, she makes class harder than it needs to be.
Who teaches a Statistics class with fill in the blank notes?

Went to the professor after my TA said that all 500+ exams were graded in less than 5 hours, meaning there would be mistakes. I asked if she could walk through the exam with me, after not having a pencil out before we even sat down, she told me that I was only there for the points and nothing else. I just wanted help to prepare for the next exam.

Very lecture heavy, long and extensive, mostly never gets out on time. In addition, recitation is utterly useless, TA doesn't help or always says "I think so," then grades poorly after realizing what they said was wrong. I would not recommend this class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I took this class online, so no clue how to do excel activities. Not allowed to go to in-person recitations because "the roof may fall in." Sentiment towards her is validated by her treatment of students- made one cry. I completed the evaluation weeks before due, submitted it a few hours late and she took 50%. (due finals week) Good luck with her.
The tests were horrible and she made them hard.

Made you use blank lecture slides as notes.  She did not allow anyone to take pictures of the lecture slides because she believed it was a violation of "copyright." She was not engaging at all.  Kept us late during lecture more often than we got out on time.  Recitations were not helpful either.  Good luck.

Reads straight off the slides. She does not prepare you well for exams at all. The exams are extremely difficult and take the hardest things we learned to put on the test. Excel activities were graded extremely harsh. Only two attempts on each homework question so homework grades aren't high to help with the harsh grading.

Professor Porterfield is very helpful, especially in office hours. She is the nicest and most caring professor I've had. She opens the hw weeks in advance and lets everyone know beforehand there are no extensions. This is not an easy course. She provides lecture recordings and daily office hours, and there are tons of resources online to succeed.
Does not remotely prepare you for the exams. All study material is far easier than exam material. Very strict on submission deadlines, even when you are sick or have a valid excuse.

Awful. Avoid this prof like your life depends on it. She can't explain any concept and anything she explains ends up 10 times more confusing. I have never been more lost in class or on a topic. Her exams are unnecessary hard and long. I don't know how she is still employed.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Lecture heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment:
Professor Pam will set you up for success!! I learned so many cool things about statistics in this class. There are a lot of resources if you are willing to put in the effort. Rewatching the recorded lectures is very very useful.

The negative is that recitation feels like just copying down what the TA says which is kind of pointless.

She can't explain concepts at all. If you haven't taken statistics before you will be confused and if you miss a concept and don't ask within the week, she will be no help at all. Excel is used on everything except the tests so it's difficult to know how to do topics without it. Take it with someone else.

She cannot properly explain concepts and instead lectures are simply her stating information off of slides. Notes are taken in the form of slides with blanks that you are to fill in. So if you happen to miss a blank, boo-hoo because she does not give access to the completed lecture slides. Avoid if you can or hope you get a good TA!
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment:
The worst professor one could ask for has no empthy for students. I was 15 mins late for the exam at 7:30 am and she didn't let me take the exam and gave a zero even when she knew I was unwell. 

That says a lot about a person, this is the first time I saw a professor being happy about failing there own students. Its like she wants to fail students

To be honest, I think she is a very nice. It's important to master the content of each lecture. You need to understand well the contents of the PowerPoint slides, both concepts and calculations. The assignments are not very difficult; Exams have a larger weight (60%), but if you master each assignment and LQ, it is very possible to score above 90%.

You just have to go to class, and she covers all the content you need to know for the exams. It is also super helpful to do + understand her exam reviews and come to class with questions. Honestly, if you do that and attempt the homework (multiple attempts) you'll be fine getting a good grade in the class. Do need to put in time outside of class.

Lectures felt like her stating information rather than truly explaining it. Prof. Porterfield is notorious for gatekeeping resources, such as complete lecture slides or answers to review problems. You get to make a cheat sheet for exams though. To (barely) succeed, take time out of class to work on understanding the material.

Have you ever wanted to teach yourself how to do statistics? Yes? Well, then you should take this course! Good luck finding any guidance on homework. An example problem? No way!!! Also, be prepared for 3 exams to be more than 60% of your grade. Avoid Pam's class if you can!

I have had my fair share of bad profs in my life, but she makes it difficult to learn. The lecture slides have blanks which means if you take notes slowly, you will miss critical information. I have taught myself more by watching videos and reading the book than I have learned from lectures and recitation. The exams have way too many questions.
No forgiveness whatsoever in this class. Nice lady, but the class is made way more difficult than it needs to be.

Pam Porterfield is by far the most awful professor I have ever had. She does NOT care about her students. She doesn't know how to teach and her recitations and lecture questions have nothing to do with the exam.  She does not give out past exams to study from and the reviews she does give out do not help at all when taking the test. No EXTRA Credit

She sets you up to fail.  I did fine, but the tests are not a measure of your intelligence, they are a measure of your ability to rapidly regurgitate information on your sheet.  If the professor is somehow reading this, allow more time for test or shorten them, I know students who studied for hours and hours and knew the material who failed.

This class was awful. Test heavy meaning if you don't do well on the majority of the tests, you're basically screwed. Took this class twice; once online and once in person which made no difference. I had someone in AP stats try to help me understand the concepts and they said the way this class is explained makes no sense.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This class is not too good. If you were great at AP stats, things will be great but if not, this class is difficult. The average on our second exam was a 67. She emphasizes the use of Excel but we can't actually use it on exams so it's conflicting. I recommend watching the prerecorded lecture videos instead of boilercast or going to class.
Not a good professor, too much work assigned without any considerations. Not really great at explaining. TA were very helpful and nice. Take this class with someone else if yoy can.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Test heavy

reviews.comment: She's super nice but her lectures themselves are average. I never learned anything from her lectures and her exam review sessions are not helpful at all. I got a higher grade in the supposedly harder continuation of this class. If you can take this with someone else, do it, otherwise be prepared to teach yourself the content.

She barely teaches, and the fill-in-the-blank slides are really not great for learning at all. I ended up skipping lecture because I was extremely sick, and she didn't even give me a Powerpoint! Just a recording of the class so that I could fill out the Powerpoint. You only get one absence and it doesn't matter if it's excused or unexcused.

If you're taking stat 30301, make sure you have a really good TA with your recitation because Pam is not a good professor. The fill in the blank slides are ridiculous and the homework and study guides are nothing like the exams. Majority of the assignments are done with excel, yet you can't use excel on the exams. Show up to lecture and recitation.

This class is the worst class I've ever taken. She uses fill in the blank lecture slides to get you to go to class. She doesn't teach the content and goes over it way too fast. There is no extra credit and there are quizzes every week in recitation. She doesn't explain content well and will make you rely on your TA. Try to get a good TA if you can.

Professor Porterfield is not lenient at all with late work and is a harsh grader. There are lecture quizzes each week in recitation and an activity that is due at midnight that day so try to have an earlier section if you need more time to complete that. Exams were pretty difficult- homework was not like the test.Try to get a good TA!

Professor Porterfield is an extremely nice person, but her class is difficult. Her lectures do not really help with much of the homework or the exams. It seems like there is no good way to study for the exams, and the content of the exams does not really seem to come from anywhere. If you have background in statistics, the class might come easy.
Attendance wasn't required, but Pam uses fill-in-the-blank lectures to force you to go. The material should be pretty easy, just make sure not to fall behind.

She was a very kind professor when I had questions to ask. I took this in the Spring of 22, so halfway through we were forced to switch to online due to her personal problems, but she made it her duty to help as much as she could.  Exams were difficult, Recitations had nothing to do with content, but Homework was incredibly helpful

Pam is a nice person, but she is not a very good professor. The class is pretty tough, so make sure to do lots of practice problems (I used youtube videos and did the problem before they worked through it). Her lectures are boring and she does a bad job explaining the concepts.

Introduction to statistics should not be a difficult class.  My best advice is to make sure you understand everything by studying the material each week. Attending lectures won't help because she does not explain how to do anything. Porterfield is a great person, just not professor. Take this class somewhere else.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Only has online office hours and is very rude if you are confused about a previous concept. The 3 exams take up most of your grade and are short answer tests.

None of the concepts connect, fill in blank lecture slides are not helpful to students in order to understand anything other than basic definitions. Exams are nothing like the review or homework. This does not feel like an introductory class to statistics and does a poor job of effectively teaching students concepts.

I like her course, because she put everything like homework due in the calendar of the brightspace so that I won't forget any homework or quiz like other class. The conception is clear in the PDF she give us, so I always do a good review before the test!

I took the summer 2022 class of this course and i don't think she's that bad of a teacher, maybe because this is a new course and she might've done bad for previously, but the lectures for summer 2022 was organized pretty well, and the exams did not have anything that was out of the lectures as some has commented.
Horribly structured, material in lectures and homework is not similar to exams. Learn everything on your own.
Awful lectures. Most strict proffessor at Purdue.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: She talks about using excel in the exam review when the exam is in-person...
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Lectures are terrible and class is structured very poorly.
The lectures that she gives are completely useless. Be prepared to learn all the material on your own.

She is a very knowledgeable professor. The course content is all over the place. She doesn't explain very well how the concepts connect, so it seems like you're learning a bunch of random content. I personally don't enjoy that the lecture slides are fill in the blank and filled out slides are not uploaded to brightspace.

Professor Porterfield is nice, but doesn't always grasp how much material can fit in a lecture. Granted, it was a new course when I took it, but the material & course itself was fairly disorganized. The full lectures slides are never posted, so if you miss lecture, doing homework is difficult. This class was more effort than it should have been.

She's really nice but it doesn't translate to the lectures that draw on for endless minutes. I wish she used more examples and would refrain from spitting equations at students that will forget them right away. Massive blocks of texts on slides and she doesn't post the finished slides which is a pain if you take handwritten notes. Find someone else
She is a sweet lady, but all material is in a mess. And it feels impossible to focus, you need to fill in the blank while listen, super distracting.
This class is fun if you like stats and really teaches you ways to think about probability in ways you haven't before. Highly recommend.

awful way to learn statistics. Fill in the blank slides with an overload of unnecessary information. never does helpful examples. She sets the class up by the number of lectures given not by chapter so 3 lectures could cover one chapter. She also never gets through the lecture. Talks way too much making it impossible to focus. Homework not helpful

Class was super messy, but it was the first run of the class, so it might be better organized next time. She can explain things alright, but there's so much to cover that she can't take enough time to go over everything thoroughly. I bet she'll get a lot of low ratings, but she's not awful, the content is just difficult and there's a lot to cover.

I would never, ever recommend taking her class for any reason. She has no proper teaching method; you must mostly teach yourself to understand the majority of the material. The exam material is also never clear.

The layout of the course is completely disorganized and confusing. The slides are never imported correctly; thus, leaving out important content. The recitations and homework help absolutely zero with the exams.

Lectures are very disorganized and she only shares parts of the notes on brightspace (leaves blanks for all of the important terms and formulas), the rest she only shows in lecture class. Recitations are not helpful, moreso a pain.

This is the first time this class has been taught (Fall of 2021). Note there is no extra credit given our nor are the exams curved. However, the grading scale is fairly lenient (90-100  A). There are two lectures per week and they are fill-in-the-blank slides. Her lectures were all over the place at the beginning of the semester.

This is the first time Pam has taught this class and it shows. Lectures are long, boring, and rushed through. Homework do not correlate with exams very much and she never will let you know what the exam contents are. Fill in the blank notes are not a way to learn stats. Currently taking the class and it sucks.

In her class right now and honestly Im impressed. W/ COVID, she's made very appropriate changes to the way she runs her class. She has everything planned out and all the materials are super accessible on brightspace. I watch all the lectures online and print out her guided notes. You do have to pay attention, but she is available to help outofclass

Pam is very nice and open, and it's apparent what she does is what she thinks is best for her students. However, her teaching style is very specific and I can see how a majority of her students can get become lost. Powerpoints are fill in the blank; that's the most troubling part. You fill in the blanks then miss ALL the explanations. It's tough.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--Caring

reviews.comment: His lectures are actually very informative and assigns a lot of group work throughout the semester. He does not take attendance, but ensures you are in class by having you complete the group projects in person throughout the sem. The class can really help you to get to know yourself more than you thought you would, as he covers leadership concepts.

Dr. Plaskoff had a very fun and easy class. The textbook is unneeded, as his lectures are very informative. Attendance is not recorded, though, so having flaky group mates will make this class much harder. He's super understanding, though. One of my favorite teachers so far.

Willing to tell the truth about people, but the most caring and entertaining teacher I've ever had. Lots of people have independently said this as well.
Class was fairly easy as long as you pay attention.

Very kind and passionate guy. I'm not sure if I really learned anything since a lot of it seemed like common sense, but it was needed for my major. Attendance isn't mandatory but sometimes there are group projects in class so you shouldn't skip too often. The textbook is lowk irrelevant.

Great guy, you can tell he is passionate about the topic. Lectures can get a bit boring after the first few weeks but you honestly don't have to listen that much. Tests are all online, there is a few in class group assignments that are very easy. You don't need to buy the textbook. Do the assignments and do decent on tests and you will get an A.

Extremely kind, interactive, and engaging professor. Class is straightforward and if you just go to lectures and complete assignments you'll get an A for sure. Course material is interesting and insightful mostly but lectures can get long and tiring though since they're usually nonstop 75 minutes of lecture.

Dr. J always comes to class ready and willing to answer questions and explains things very well. The first few classes are very important to attend as they build up the foundation for EVERYTHING in this class. You have group assignments, but they are easy and quick. Homework is graded very leniently and as long as there's effort you'll get credit.

Literally, go to class, turn everything in on time, listen to a certain extent, and it's a free A. I think the biggest thing is like, don't ask for extensions; he really doesn't give them, and you have to show up when there is a (random) group assignment; otherwise, it isn't necessary. He also talks about his personal life a lot, but it's chill.

Attendance is not recorded but there are group projects that you will not understand or be able to do if you don't go into class. He doesn't tell you when they are until the day of. There are 3 tests, and no final. The tests were easy (imo) and done on Brightspace. He gives everyone chocolate at the end of the semester which is nice.

Dr J. is my favorite professor I've ever had at Purdue- he truly cares so much about his students and what he's teaching and it makes you want to come to class. Harder to be personal when he's in a bigger lecture, but I've had him in smaller classes and he's awesome!

It is a tough lecture to sit through. Content is both easy and mildly interesting, but Professor Plaskoff's attitude toward students makes it very hard to like him. Class itself is easy enough and useful to real life. Attendance is not kept but there are group assignments on random days due in class, so I would recommend going.

Easy class. You are put into small groups that you will work with on assignments and you also have weekly individual assignments called RACO's. Very interesting material that you will take with you into your career. Tip: He HATES when people have phones out and he will call you out in the middle of lecture in front of everyone if you have one out.

Dr. J was an awesome professor. He doesn't like the average class setting, he prefers people to get to know one another as well as himself. He uses groups to make people get closer. Tests are online. If you plan to skip make sure it is not a group project day because the assignments are due in class. Overall, awesome guy! Also, he makes truffles!

she is such a sweetheart and loves what she does but she gives so much work for 255. the quizzes are so odd its like a specific sentence from a specific part of the textbook and the entire quiz is on that, super unfair grading.

She is such an amazing professor. She grades very fairly and makes sure that any questions you have get answered. She genuinely cares and wants to see people succeed. Talking to her is extremely easy and as long as you put in the effort and do the work she will make sure that you get a decent grade. Read the textbook and take notes!
sorry i love you
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: She gave good lectures. She was engaging and connected stories to concepts, which helped deepen understanding. However, her grading practices were unfair, as she often strayed from the rubric, grading based on her subjective assessment rather than what students were doing, and she is difficult to talk to, as she tends to shut individuals down.

Professor Putman ALWAYS brings the energy to class and it makes it a lot of fun even during a 7:30am. She does a great job incorporating stories into her classroom which helps the lecture material soar in engagement and help legal concepts stick.

Cara is, in many ways, an awesome person and professor; she cares for students and does not make you feel left out. She is always willing to meet with students to help them prepare for the weekly quizzes this course requires, but make sure you know your stuff, especially by reading the textbook.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: the most harsh grader ever.

Professor Putman is extremely focused on helping students succeed in MGMT 254! Her quizzes are extremely difficult; however, if you got to office hours she will always go through answers with you and help you prepare for the future! 

Please make sure to go to class!!! If you do the lectures are full of stories that make the concepts jump out!

The quizzes are straight out of the textbook, and you won't do well if you only attend lecture. You have to read and watch all of the videos if you want to pass. Good opportunities for extra credit and easy points through participation.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: attendance is marked, but you can miss a couple of classes. Class is straight forward.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Group projects

reviews.comment: Prof. Putman is an extremely kind and genuine individual who really cares about the work she does. On top of that she provides many good real world examples of the content and helps to really drive home the impact that law has in business. I really enjoyed her class!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Honestly, I think she was one of the best professors I've had at Purdue. I'm looking into TA-ing for her next year because I absolutely love the material she teaches, as well as her overall teaching style. Highly Recommend.

Cara is probably one of the most passionate professors I know. You can tell she puts a lot of effort into her lectures, and makes sure that each student understands the topic and whatever she is covering that day. She has great examples and tries to compare it to the real world as possible. It wasn't a lot of work; weekly quizzes and open note exam
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Respected--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: At first Professor Putman was not my favorite. She's super bubbly and I'm not. I often see bubbly people as fake, but she's extremely genuine. She cares about her students and really tries to help her students retain the material.

Professor Putman is by far one of the best professors at Krannert. She ensures students understand the content and takes the time to connect with her students. I can't wait to take more of her classes in the future.

Prof Putman is a great professor to have. She makes her courses engaging and open to students interest. She has a fun way of teaching classes and does a good job catering to everyone. Would take again

I took 301 under Professor Guo. Solid teacher, makes sure to pause for questions. Quizzes that he had were fair and takes very neat lecture notes in class. Overall would recommend to take 301 under Guo.

One of the best prof I've had at Purdue, and the best choice for 301. Exams are brutally hard but each exam is curved to a 75 average, and the class is curved ~10% on top of it! Beautiful lecture slides, and recorded! As long as you work hard and honestly, you will do well. If you have the option to take it with Guo - run, don't walk!

Very good professor who truly cares about student's learning. Lectures are engaging and he takes time to make sure the class is keeping up with him and to answer questions. Homework take a significant amount of time but exams are very similar to them. Exams are curved to 75%

301 is a tough class, and professor Guo was awesome at both making concepts easy to understand and the class fun and engaging. He's a super genuine person who truly cares about his students. I guess you could say that he's a *Guo*ted professor :^)

Generous curve. I did perfect on homeworks and exactly average on exams and ended with A-. Super friendly and helpful and accessible outside class. Really cares about you. Best prof I've had at Purdue. Very knowledgable and inspiring.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment:
This 3-credit course is actually a 9-credit course in disguise: Probability and Random Processes, Algebraic Graph Theory, and Algorithm Design. Everything is so intertwined that it makes the material much harder to master. 
While the instructor is patient and passionate about teaching, the course pacing is simply indigestible.

Professor Quanrud is a great lecturer, and the class was enjoyable. There were a few hiccups (errors in the textbook, self-grading policy, etc.) but overall the course was well worth it. Would recommend taking a class with him in the future.

He teaches well and breaks down the concepts into smaller chunks, but everything else in this class is poor. He has his own textbook, which is riddled with math errors (good luck studying). Both HWs and Exams use only problems from the textbook. There is no diversity in assessing you. You have to grade your own HW, which the TA evaluates!?!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Test heavy

reviews.comment: Great professor with engaging lectures that always build from basic principles to help build intuition and make clear many complex ideas.
By far the best professor (and class) I have had at Purdue. Kent expects students to put in some effort, but it's definitely worth it!
Best professor I had during my time here

Look at the trend in Kent's reviews, undergrads complain bc his class is hard, and grad students unanimously love him. The reality is he teaches a hard topic, but he actually makes it intuitive. Beyond that, he's one of the most abundantly caring and available CS professors I had at Purdue, he's the kind of person who it is impossible to dislike

The professor has written a book which can be used to understand the concepts better. He also paints an intuitive picture which makes the topics easy to understand. The course is homework heavy and would recommend solving as it helps with the exams.
AMAZING GUY AND DIFFICULT COURSE, but you will love it :)
Hardest class I've ever taken but amazing and super kind professor.
Well organized materials and lectures. A little bit heavy, but you'll overcome it, if you try enough.

Amazing lecturer and very passionate about the topics he teaches. Concepts can be hard but he is always happy to help, and course policies (drops, group work, etc) help blunt the difficulty. There are a lot of topics, and this is probably the hardest class I have taken. Even then, I had a lot of fun taking the course and learning the content.
Kent is smart, creative, organized and knowledgeable. It is a delight to be in his classroom.
Kent is just awesome. Even though there are lot of homework, I enjoyed the course very much.

His teaching skill is amazing. The lecture is very clear, does not any prior knowledge and also a lot of fun. I believe even those who hate this subject would get to love it once they attend his class.

Kent makes algorithms so much more interesting than other professors, in part with the colorful slides and commentary, and just by talking about intuition rather than all theory. I came in expecting to scrape by with barely a B but this wound up being my favorite class this semester. Purdue would be better if every professor had Kent's personality
He's amazing. You just need to be patient to realize his awesome.

Couldn't imagine passing this class without Kent. He's encouraging & motivational beyond any other professor here, & he breaks things down into actual intuition rather than memorizing patterns. Exams are always padded with free giveaway questions, & that's before he even curves. 580 is supposed to be 1 of the hardest classes here, but not with Kent
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I have a love/hate relationship with the actual class content, bc it's pretty hard... But I love Kent. He frames problems in a motivating light and teaches through intuition rather than regurgitating the rigorous proofs found in the textbook. I honestly wouldn't want to take this class from any other professor after seeing how painless he makes it
I really like Kent as a teacher, his lectures are amazing. His approach to algorithms is very good, bit different from traditional algorithms courses. highly recommended to take this course.

I really like Kent as a teacher, but this class is insanely hard. His policies are really weird and you can take advantage of them a little, but that doesn't change the fact that it's still really hard. His lectures are great, really colorful and well-explained, but the nature of this class requires luck and creativity, so studying more won't help.

381 is overall a difficult class by default. But he gives decent practice materials like practice problems for graphs and dp that where similar to exam problems. Overall gives a fair curve. Be ready to put a decent amount of effort if you want a good grade.
The 381 can not undertake his weight and power, change it to 581 or 681 or change the professor
The homework and the exams are actually pretty reasonable if you put in the effort. The course had a rocky start, but now it's actually pretty chill if you manage your time correctly.
reviews.rating_tags: Caring--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: On one of the last homework questions he was kind enough to link to the 17 page research paper which answers the question, although on most questions he is not, and it's common to spend 5-8 hours per problem. His style is extremely dense, which adds unnecessary difficultly, and most students are receiving very poor grades as a result.

This class got some ridiculous policy I've never seen before. Homework is very hard and you can try spending all weekend solving it, which might even result in a lower score. OR you can just don't do it and wait for solution to be posted, and use late day to get free 75%! What on earth is this? Even in exam, this course discourages you from trying.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Lots of homework

reviews.comment: incredibly difficult course, have no idea what we are learning and why we are learning. Algorithm used to be fun for me and now it's just nightmare. He seems want to teach Nobel winner but apperently I am not gonna be the one.......

Kent quanrud...
Every hw is impossible
Next level class
Too hard
Is impossible
Super bs work
A lot of hw
Fml bc of this class.
An impossible class.
Good lord...
Got too much homework.
Omg why is this class required.
Too much work (30+ hours per week).

I was doing one of the homework problems and wasn't sure how to do it, and I did some research. This guy had a 20 page research paper on the topic. I don't get how he expects us to do 80% of the work he assigns. He also said he uses the same problems and text for his graduate class. I don't get how this is fair.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment:
Here is his textbook's link on AWS. 
The order of the class content is just taught in the wrong order. He makes everything more confusing than it should be. His explanation of algos i've learned in 251 are explained in a much worse and more confusing way.

Uses his own textbook which has many mistakes.
Uses confusing notation and doesn't explain it.
Doesn't cover half the proofs in his textbook (asks you to "do it yourself for fun")
Has very vague solutions.
The TAs often times don't know how to do the HW problems optimally.
Homework problems often times unrelated to lectures.

teaches concepts in wrong order
hard homeworks (get like 50% or less if you don't get the optimal algo)
weekly homeworks that take 30+ hours (if you don't have a good group you're screwed)
PSOs have no solutions
doesn't answer half the important questions on piazza
lectures don't make sense b/c first bullet point above

If you don't have a good homework group then be ready to fail, because even with a group you will spend tons of hours trying to solve the homework. Lectures are fast-paced because the professor teaches this course like it is being taught to graduate students, not undergrads. You are better off reading the book to learn.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--So many papers

reviews.comment: Every week I spend 20-30 hours on the homework. This isn't an uncommon phenomenon. Most of my peers say they spend 15 hours minimum and up to 40 hours on the homework. TA's can barely help in office hours because they're not given solutions or guidance either. This isn't the TA's fault. The professor seems unwilling to give the TA's any extra info.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Tough course, graded by 12 quizzes (+1 dropped). Some quizzes rocked, others sucked horrifically. Inconsistent, either breezed through slides or took forever to explain one thing. I would appreciate the addition of programming assignments to lessen the impact of bad quiz grades.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Not recommend professor for PhD and also course, basically disorganized and also it is hard to understand him. The class all gives unuseful staff. Overall, don't recommend his class. Also for his PhD.

Homework is graded with unclear rubric. Attendence is worth 5% and is not extra. He didn't even put down students' names. Scores were given arbitrarily. If you use his starter code in python, you should prepare to debug for him. The system was completely down for a week right before deadline. He barely could answer only a few questions on piazza.

This is the most ridiculous instructor I have ever seen in my life and let me know how awful a professor receive 1 on RMP can be. The ridiculous single-threaded autograder for the project, which was down during most of the time, the slides with the examples that calculated wrong and confused you, and mysterious grading criteria of participation.

5 percent of your grade is from class participation and it's extremely hard to get. Each endorsed answer of piazza gives you 1% and each bug found in project gives 2%, so good luck if you used Java for the compiler where most of the bugs have already been taken care of. Gotta say people are for real upset about this.

Really really bad professor. The baddest professor I have ever met in my life. Especially the way to grade your attendance. This is the only prof who said "Attendance is not based on how often you came to class". If you attend all classes, good luck, you may still get a 0 for your attendance grade. RUN! do not take this class! RNM tui qian.

Actually the worst professor I've had at Purdue. The lectures make no sense and the course is very disorganized. The source code has bugs in it that make your work twice as difficult, and he can't debug his own code (it's another professor's work that he pawns off as his own, he couldn't be bothered to put in effort). Do not take compilers w/ him.

During the lecture, he cannot explain everything clearly, his slides make no sense, and you can't really learn from his lecture. His project is a disaster, you have to debug for him(takes about a day) since his code is pretty buggy and I bet he didn't test his code before he publish it to the class. (Maybe he just can't find or fix his bug^^)
Horrible and unclear grading scheme. Unprepared during office hours and lecture. Students gave up asking questions after a few lectures.

The only prof I have met so far in ECE who seems to think free-response exams should be graded with no partial credit. Ridiculous project step instructions, with basically no support if you do the Python version, then putting the work of fixing their bugs on students. Never take this class unless Kulkarni is teaching it.

His lectures are not very helpful, and he comes off as insensitive and condescending to students. One student asks him for clear instructions on project on Piazza, he replied "we didn't mean to babysit." You should know what you're getting into before taking his 468/595/573, they're basically the same class, but you need to do more in 573.
Very badly designed project. The office hours are extremely useless. The TAs are not even familiar with the codes and are even more confused than you.

The professor attacks you if you ask for an extension even though he didn't even release the assignment until much later. So in order to keep the original due dates, he makes 2 steps due the same week. He is condescending and will constantly remind you that you have poor time management skills even though it's his fault.
The project guidelines are so confusing, and it takes so long to understand anything. Highly recommend not to take this class.

Prof Qin is not good. Lectures were messy, could barely understand her. Many exam questions are ambiguous. Group project had unclear grading; rubric was released 10 min before the due date. Did not receive group project feedback for weeks because Prof Qin did not know how to increase the volume of the computer. Avoid if possible.

Really nothing enjoyable about the class.Vague description of expectations, especially in the group project. Expect typos in basically every homework assignment. If you're taking this online, you'd be more confused about what she expects in the assignments than the material itself. But project was maybe not as bad as other people on here have said.
Expectations for the final project are incredibly unclear. Otherwise, the class was fine. She posts the lecture notes on Brightspace, which is nice.

The other reviews had me worried, but my experience wasn't bad, maybe because I took the class online. Her lecture style is very robotic. But all the information you need for homework is in the notes. She provides example R code. It's just a matter of deciphering her examples. Her notes have lots of typos and sometimes her notation is ambiguous.
group project took ten years off of my life but whatever
The main issue with the class is that the exams are designed to trick the student taking the exam, also the way the project is laid out is extremely bad. Would not recommend taking this class.

Decent lecturer. 2 exams worth 15% each at the middle and end of semester. Exams are doable (pure MCQ with 1 note sheet allowed) and on Respondus Monitor.
