Group project is worth way too much (25%) with unclear directions and unclear expectations. I would rather it be a individual project or a lighter part of the grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Listen to the other reviews. It's as bad as they say. Brutal English, very tough exam (no curve in the class despite averages in the 50s and 60s). Project is also graded harshly unless you put a significant amount of time into it.

Her lectures and exams more focused on statistical concepts understanding and real world application. I think most of her lectures explain the concepts pretty well and are align with her slides. I have to say she is teaching much much better than the other Stats class I took last semester. The format of homework is kind of tricky.

Attending lecture did not help at all, I ended up just reading through the slides on my own time. The homework was tedious but all of the answers were in her slides, just might take some people a long time. The exams were not terrible if you actually understood the material, you get a cheat sheet so I just crammed as much as I could on there.

Low exam averages, tricky questions, lectures are not useful. Project is extremely time consuming, quizzes are also confusing, homework is lengthy and not an adequate amount of office hours. DO NOT take this class. Additionally based on the low exam statistics, everyone was expecting a curve but there wasn't one which was very shocking.

If this class with Qin is not required for your degree, DON'T TAKE IT. She is nice and the content itself is easy but the way she lectures about it and tests you on it is so bad. She has no curve which I was appalled by because many students are worried about passing. Lectures never help and they are long. This was my worst class experience ever.

DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS IF YOU ACTUALLY WANT TO LEARN ANYTHING!!! Exams are difficult and have trick questions, homework and lecture notes are full of typos and inconsistencies. Professor Qin does not know how to teach to save her life. Unnecessarily long group project at the end. Content should be very easy, but made needlessly difficult.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment:
Pros:
- HW takes 5-6 hours top so you can defo procrastinate
- professor knows what she's teaching
- not a bad class for learning stat methods

Cons:
- exams have trick questions, so annoying...
- no leniency on HW, strict guidelines for everything, no late days
- mandatory attendance
- group projects 
- didn't give enough guidelines for project

As others have said, slides and homework are full of typos and inconsistent notation - especially earlier in the content. She is not responsive online. Tests and are full of trick questions that make the content appear much harder than it really is. Homework is extremely tedious. Easy content, hard class.

The class material itself is very difficult, so having a professor that added long and hard homeworks and a huge group project made the class so much harder. She had pretty good slides for the lectures, but her lecturing itself was often confusing and hard to follow. Get ready for a time-consuming class if you take stat512 with her.
She is not good at organizing languages, get ready to learn this course on your own!

I took STAT512 with her last spring. She is horrible at teaching. All of the course materials are poorly written. I sent her emails about the typos and she started giving me attitude during office hours. She would also threaten students over Brightspace. I feel bad for the students that will have to take this course with her in the future.

Not sure able her personality since I'm taking a online class that only uses her recorded lecture and homework wrote by her. Horrible lecturer, refer to everyting as "this" rather than the actual terminology but cares a great deal about you using the correct term and notation in homework, frequent typos and unclear language in slides and homeworks.
Professor Qin is probably the worst professor I have ever had at Purdue. Her class is poorly run and all questions on the HW are confusing. DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS WITH HER YOU WILL REGRET IT.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Group projects

reviews.comment: she sucks. The material isnt hard but she cant teach even if her life depended on it.
I don't know if tiantian knows what proof reading is.

This class would not be that hard but her inability to teach is what makes it hard. The format and organization of her lectures are terrible and there are many typos on the homework that just make it more confusing and longer to do. There are many errors in her slides as well. It seems like she does not know how to use technology.
Worst professor I have ever had, inexplicably bad at teaching. Hard course because of her inability to teach, not the material.

Terrible. She emphasizes the importance of the notation of equations, but there are many errors in her slides. The homework assignments have incredibly unclear directions and are riddled with typos. I spent an hour trying to figure out why lm(count~temperature, BikeProject) was not working. It was because she spelled temperature as tempeture SMH.

She somehow managed to make a class more poorly run than STAT 350. There is no piazza or campuswire, but rather Brightspace discussions where she instructs you to reach out to the TA by email. Everyone spams the TA, but they can only do so much when the professor cannot write clear questions. Avoid this class at all costs.

The homework is hard. The content is not hard, but the way the homework are written makes us so confused. It is like broken English. She expects use to understand what she says in lectures but she talks super fast and mispronounces key terms. The slides are also full of errors that just make the content more confusing. Avoid her at all costs!!!!!!!

The fact she is the only professor for this course is appalling. She cares about the students, but the delivery of course material is horrendous. Gatekeeps corresponding course contents. The class should not be that difficult, but her English is not very good. She interchanges all of the terms and writes poor questions without a discussion page,
Long, badly formatted homework assignments with often outright confusing questions. Class isn't curved not to mention hard to follow lectures.
Dropped her class twice, worst professor

She is caring as a person and tries to help her students, but is utterly disorganised and sometimes makes errors. Her homework is long and tough, while her tests are moderate. The TAs are tough graders.

Not the greatest lecturer, with challenging homeworks and exams. Also for any students prepping for this class prior, just a heads up that Penn State has a good documentation for lecture material. More straight forward and specific than her slides

Prof Qin knows what she's talking about but she isn't a great lecturer. Disorganized slides, mistakes on slides, and lectures too fast. Her homeworks are long and difficult, so start it ASAP. Tests are similar to practice exams but are full of sneaky trick questions. The group project is the saving grace of the course with easy grading on it.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Very harsh graders with no curve in class and super long homeworks
Don't take this course. Go away from her. please, Listen to me, this would be one of the worst courses at Purdue
Homework is easy to get 100 and exam is easy to do. Like this class. Qin is really nice.
She is so nice! I really like STAT512. The exam is really similar to the practice exam and she is always happy to help you! This is the best stat course I've ever taken at Purdue.
Horrible course design, not a lot of online resources available

In STATS 512, Professor Qin was good about showing us how to use the concepts we were learning about, and she did a lot of in-class activities that I thought were helpful in learning how to use the concepts. The only problem was you needed to have learned the concepts during the class in order to participate. Get extra credit for showing up.

Horrible experience. I have went through everything but all in vain! Her lectures are so disorganized! The assignments are way too long and redundant. The group project is fine. However, the questions in the test are super confusing and most cases, language is definitely a barrier for the instructor. Super unhelpful TA.
Be prepared to spend the majority of the semester on homework and group project. Intense course but the professor was very knowledgeable. I enjoyed the knowledge gained at the end of it all.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Group projects

reviews.comment: STAT 512 with professor Qin was the most annoying class I've taken. Her lectures are all over the place and you can barely read her handwriting. You're better off reading the book instead and going to TA office hours to ask questions. She also expects you know R before class so learn it before day 1. Search "R Programming Tutorial" on YouTube.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Prof. Tiantian has a bit of a language barrier and needs a proof-reader for her material (lecture notes, exams, homework assignments). Class was pretty typical in terms of difficulty and time commitment but made unnecessarily tricky by the poor English and tricky questions on exams.

Her recorded lectures were mostly her shuffling papers around as she scribbled something unreadable while not explaining the concept clearly. Too often did I hear "you should know this" and "you should figure this out" when asked a question during live lectures. Sometimes she would get through only 1/5 homework problems during the weekly lecture.

Hard to understand, but provides lecture slides which are pretty helpful. Homeworks are long and grading is pretty harsh, but provides plenty of extra credit opportunities. Had 7 homeworks, 3 quizzes, final project, and final exam, which felt like a lot throughout the semester. Final project criteria is extremely vague, so clarify at office hours

At the beginning of this class it was fine. But at the end, when the staff become more difficult, she skips lots of explanation that makes me so confused. The slides are hard to read since they are not well organized. I would suggest not to take since when materials become difficult she can't explain them clearly.
The professor skip almost all the knowledge needed in the class. I learn nothing from this class, all I remembered is her name.

I genuinely don't understand the critics. She is very organized and starts off from the basics and eventually makes the content challenging. She is very helpful and caring and dresses smartly too. The group project in the class can be challenging due to the nature of the team members. HWs are long but fair as you are given 2 weeks to complete.

My recommendation is that if you do not have strong statistical background, like you are just taking stat as a minor, definitely not taking her class! And I believe the working load is not designed for undergrad cos that is way too much. She gives you the easiest practical sample midterm, but the actual midterm makes me feel failing the course.

The way professor Qin teaches is very confusing. She goes through 20 powerpoint slides a class that are full of examples and R code. If you don't see what she writes before she quickly removes it from the screen, that's too bad. She skips over a lot of "easy" concepts that no one understands making it even more difficult and hard to follow.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Group projects

reviews.comment: It is deifinitely one of the worst professor I have ever seen. The homework is due every two week. The length per homework is unbelievable. You could imagine spending the whole weekend plus Friday to work on this, but still could not finish it. The lecture is so so so confusing. She only tries to cover the topic without any explaining! Don't take!
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things--Hilarious

reviews.comment: Exams are rough as in there arent really much way for you to prepare for them and they account for 60% of your total grade (1 midterm and 1 final). Be prepare with mastery of the prerequisite classes materials as she will assume you to really be good at it. She also go through lectures very fast and her lecture slides are not really helpful.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: She really sucks at teaching. And she never ever ever answers email. Most frustrating professor I have ever had.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: There is a lot of homework. It would be difficult for people who don't know R. The lectures are kinda confusing because there's just so much info every lecture, and my class was the first time she's teaching STAT512. She's not a harsh grader though. There is a group project.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: This course tried to cover too much over the semester. And, you should study most of them for the final and midterm exam. The problem is the lecture notes don't help you prepare for both at all - they are terrible (cuz so many blanks and typos). Furthermore, the lecturer skipped many steps. Don't take this course.

She seems to be motivated, but she makes her students feel desperate. The biggest problem is that she skipped many procedures required for the student to study for review or preview. Another problem is that her midterm is too difficult to solve in time. If solving the questions had been worthwhile, I would have not felt frustrated.

It's extremely hard to study for her exams when her study guides with the solutions have the wrong answers. The exams are also very important as the two for the online class account for 70% of the final grade. I'm have to check myself on everything a few times just to make sure the study guide rounded wrong.
Worst professor at Purdue.

Extremely hard to understand when she talks. Writing is very bad and hard to read. Skips a lot of steps and assumes you know how to do them when doing practice problems, even though it's supposed to be an introductory class. 70% of the final grade was based on the midterm and the final.

Professor Qin is really nice. She gave extra credits just for participation. I skipped almost half of the classes but still recieved some extra credits. But the way she was teaching was confusing. She skipped most of the concepts probably because she believed we have studied them through online video. Just use the homeworks to study yourself.

This professor is knowledgeable in statistics, but could not be worse at teaching the material in a manor that is accessible or understandable. The majority of time in class is spent in confusion. If you want to bond with other students over just how confused you are, this is the class for you!

The class is not bad, but the homework can take awhile. Study the formulas back and forth and you can do alright on the exams. The online lectures are confusing. It is best to show up to class and pay attention during the handouts.

Before coming into this class i had a working knowledge of statistics. After leaving i can honestly say i have no idea what i am doing. Professor Qin does a poor job of wording her thoughts, has no organization in her lecture, and moves insanely fast through difficult subject matter. When asked to clarify she often confuses the entire class more.

Do not take this course.  I spend 10+ hours per week on the homework.  The online book is difficult to navigate and doesn't align well with the class powerpoints.  The instructor moves way too quickly.   Worst course I've taken at Purdue.
Going to class automatically gets you extra credit. This class isn't difficult if you pay attention and take notes. Homework is pretty easy but start early because it can take awhile.

Talks very fast, impossible to keep up.  Does not explain concepts well, or how to use the online software.  Both in class & the recorded online lectures she skips steps, omits calculations, moves too quickly, & does not explain what she is doing so that students can understand. Take 501 with Tadd Colver instead, SAS sucks, but he is way better.
The class is hard, but extremely useful!
Try to find another professor.

She is very nice, but I found lecture completely useless. I've taken a statistics class on the same level and done extremely well. But, her class was easier, and I left the class more confused than I was before I started it. She doesn't explain things well, and bonus points are a joke. Do all the homeworks and use that to study and you'll be fine.

English is pretty decent however speaks VERY FAST.= confusing. Lecture notes are worthless. Suggest getting book. Her calculation questions are easy. Her true/fase. multi choice are hard. Mostly because shes not good at explaining concepts and then words the multi choice to trick you. Homework is easy. quizs hard. Grader is bad. Beg for points back
Not very easy to understand, but she was quite nice. Always willing to help out in office hours.
Hottest prof at Purdue. I wont miss a single class, just to see her.
Not the best at explaining things, but definitely an easy course.  You can use create-your-own formula sheets for exams.
Classes are not very helpful, the textbook is much clearer. Exams and homework are easy, but homework is time-intensive (6-8 hours per week). Grading is lenient.

Had him for a seminar class. Grade was basically entirely attendance (No A if you miss like 2 classes) and completion grades. Felt like a waste of time and you could tell he didn't really want to be there. Also talked quietly without a mic and is kinda hard to understand (OK English).
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. Song was very thorough in his lectures. He was prompt in his responses, reasonable to requests, and fair. I enjoyed his class. Other students may have had a negative experience with the content, but he did a good job making it easier to digest.
He has a good English.

He is without a doubt the most disorganized lecturer I have ever met. He goes way too fast and has trouble communicating the material to grad students. He does not give pragmatic coding examples or clear output explanations. The only saving grace of the class are his homework and exam curve policies. Would not recommend his 526 class.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Qifan is a good professor who means well and has a clear lecturing style. That said, his lectures are a bit disorganized and he talks fast. His exams are extremely challenging, final project isn't specific, and homework's are really long. But they are graded for completion rather than correctness. Overall his course is a good intro regression class
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Only graded on homework, one midterm, and final. The lectures were very dry and hard to pay attention in. He just read off the textbook and gave the same examples without really explaining anything further than the book did. Gave practice exams from other professors but no solutions to them.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: The professor has a very good command of English language. His knowledge of the subject is also pretty good. I was always able to follow his lectures. The course was taught in a way that was easy to understand and build on itself cumulatively. The homework were not difficult and help with learning. Blame the STAT  course content, not the professor!

I signed up for this class and had to drop it. First of all, this professor's ability to explain the material is very limited. It is extremely difficult to follow his lectures. Also, he has a very thick accent. I am not a native speaker as well and I don't judge people easily, but I can easily say that he cannot communicate with the class at all.

He talks to the screen most of the class. Doesn't check if the class has understood the concepts or not.  He needs to improve his English fluency to teach and communicate well.  There's hardly any point taking this class if most of the time you are trying to decipher what he is saying
Easy class
Very interesting topics! Complete the work on time and you'll get an easy A!

This was the easiest course I ever took. There were weekly quizzes, weekly discussion posts, and a final exam. The quizzes were all easy and directly based on the textbook. My only complaints were that the due dates were confusing and I had to buy a $95 textbook.

This was by far the most easiest class I have ever taken. Most of the times I forget I enrolled in this course. Your grades are dependent of quizzes, discussion posts and responding to 2 people. I never used the textbook, however, theres bonus points for buying it. Again, really easy class. Keep in mind, the prof and the TA barely answer your email

The professor and the TA do not care about you. They never answer any questions in the discussion designed for Q/A. This course is also poorly structured with due dates being wrong and confusing. Grading criteria is also blurry. One of the worst class I have taken in my life.

This was one of the easiest classes I have taken so far. It was an online course. Each week consisted of a short essay submitted via blackboard and then feedback to two other classmates. Super easy! There were 8 quizzes. These were a little difficult and I think some questions on the quizzes were never in the reading. Extra credit helps!
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Lots of homework, group discussion is graded based on your English level and your grammar, makes this class like a English course, except his English is too poor to be a English teacher. and the topic he gave us were posted 2 years ago. For circuit he will only give you one day to finish, if you miss one section of circuit, you lose all credits.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Easiest class ever. Only a 8 week only class for 3 credit hours. Take EAPS 120 online if possible. No exams and quizzes are online open book.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Clear grading criteria--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Comes to class, drones to herself, maintains zero eye contact and lectures to herself and leaves. It is almost as if she avoids taking questions. Class attendance has also gone down, ever since she has taken over.

She is ok at lecturing. Nothing great, but doesn't answer student questions well. She is never present in the moment and outsources her responsibilities to the TAs. She doesn't seem involved or available to handle student accommodations. Purdue CS needs to hire better.

Please take 578 with someone else, its been two weeks and she breezes over content like you are the next Einstein. She is unfit for teaching college-level courses and needs to be patient. Like others have experienced, she also has communication issues. Above all, I find her very non-chalant and just uncaring.

She was nice but lacks enthusiasm and could not satisfactorily answer most student questions. This shows her lack of understanding of basic ML.; very detached and uninterested or maybe she is highly unprepared for lectures. She was also very difficult to understand. Most students experienced this because of her garbled way of speaking.

The course is mainly focused on statistical aspect of the ML which means that you will need to have background in linear algebra and probability theory.  She writes all the derivatives/mathematics proofs on the board. It is super clear and I can really understand what's the theory behind each models/algorithms. Easy homeworks. Heavy exams.

Rocommend the course ONLY to Phds studying theoretical mathematics... It is almost no experience as homeworks and exams are all related to long and complex theoretical proofs. If the final grades are also low for most, I will not recommend it to anyone....

I have never been more offended by how a class was such a waste of time. I have learned literally nothing in this class. Way better off taking CS 573 instead of 578. All we do is theoretical math proofs in class and on homework, literally zero coding. And she outsources all her responsibilities to TA office hours, only 2 hours a week. What a waste

Finally found out she copies almost all her lectures from other websites, so you don't even need to show up to class to sleep through her long boring mathematical proofs which she intentionally doesn't put on her powerpoints... Whole class is a waste of time unless your passion is proof-writing, in which case you should get a PhD in Math not CS

she doesnt use a textbook, and intentionally leaves proofs off her slides so you have to show up. class is overly focused on theoretical side of things, so you never actually learn how to use models or compare them. i'm almost convinced that this is the most useless waste of a class in the CS dept, except that compilers also exists, so its close

I've learned all this stuff before, & her way of explaining things isn't helpful. She does a horrible job of deciding which topics to emphasize & which topics to breeze over. We're a week behind her syllabus schedule & I have barely learned anything new all semester. Just a bunch of mathematical proofs for common models, instead of how to use them

She has her PhD in Stats so she focuses way too much on the mathematical proof behind ML models. Maybe that is good so we understand how they work, but it doesn't actually give us experience using it. CS 578 is way too theoretical to be interesting to me, but it's also way easier than CS 573 which assumes we already understand the math theory...

Sit near the front or you won't be able to understand a word she says. Constantly complains about students handwriting when her own is illegible as well. If you attend her office hours bring a respirator you'll thank me later. She also creates an environment of, asking a question is met with either a short degrading speech, or incoherent mumbling.
Lectures were hard to follow despite the simple nature of the material. Professor does not communicate clearly, and there was very little outside resources to help you with in class material.

taken 3 of her classes and will soon take a 4th. Professor Raji initially can seem hard to get along with but if you take the time to get know her she is a very caring person. I think a lot of people don't realize she has a weird sense of humor. If you are a bad student and slack off she will treat you as such. Once she knows you she is very fair.

This is the worst professor I have ever had. Refuses to answer questions and laughs at students for not understanding content. Seeing a decade of reviews that describe my current experience is a huge disappointment; Purdue should have taken care of this. Every comment about her lecturing skills is true;  an awful experience.

Easily one of the worst professors I have ever had. Very bad speaking skills, mumbles every other word, clearly is being forced to teach. She lost a students lab sheet and gave him a zero. When he asked her about it, she said he had to turn it in, even though he couldn't, because he had already. We were also going over new topics 3 days before exam
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Read straight from a book and randomly pulled problems out of thin air

This is the worst class ever, not organized, super arrogant attitude, only gave busy work, not professional at all nothing is structured. I believe she is a good researcher and all that but her quality of teaching put Purdue in shame. This is not Purdue quality class at all...

Professor thinks that an online course need not have recorded lectures or online classes. Hence course is literally bunch of uploaded slides and assignments from 3 years ago. Saying the course is very low in standard and poor quality is an understatement.

By far the worst professor I have had at Purdue. She has made me consider dropping out. She is a very hard grader who focuses more on good wording rather than actually getting the correct answer. I have received typed feedback from her that was unintelligible. It is such a waste of time but it is required by my major.
Quiet speaker with a heavy Indian accent. Gives lots of busywork.

She doesn't seem to have a good grasp on teaching. She mostly spouts out information without actually teaching. She passed out quizzes and homework once a week. There was also a big paper due at the end of the semester that I don't think she actually read. She also seemed to skim most of the lab reports.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: She shouldn't be a teacher. She understands what she is talking about but does not explain it well. If you ask a question about something she'll act like your stupid. Still assumes everyone took 277. I had to come in on other sections in the last 4 labs because she has no idea how to use the HOMER software and I somehow knew it better than her.
Not clear non organized. Very confusing, got an Indian accent.  Hard to deal with.

-Assumes material from ECET 277 is already known. Refuses to explain the material if you tell her that you haven't taken ECET 277. -Rushes through material without stopping to explain. -Only thoroughly explains material if you go to her office after class. (Extra help should NOT be mandatory!) -Assigns busy work and grades arbitrarily.
You can hardly understand her when she lectures. She gave quizzes once a week. The class was very disorganized. She forgot to grade half the lab reports and I never got my final exam grade back.

Labs disorganized, poor spoken and written English skills, nonsensical emails, highly politicizes a technology class, doesn't know much of what she lectures about, incorrectly teaches various calculations, doesn't understand student questions. Pretty much a lab rat that's required to teach so she can do research.
Don't expect to receive anything back anytime soon.

Class was an easy A but sucked. Nobody listens in lecture, and even if you do you wont understand. BUT if you go to her office hours and become her friend, she will help you with everything; homework answers, quiz questions, test questions, and will even change some of your grades for you.
Good professor. Knows the material, willing to work with you, and has great commentary. Made what may have been a difficult subject fun and enjoyable.

Very confusing, course is completely disorganized.  She types her emails in subject line, and does not proof read. Only reason I received a good grade was because the first half of the course material I learned in high school physics.

Knows her stuff well. All tests and quizzes are open ended with partial credit; but very easy to mess up. She makes the class sound easier than it is on the first day and sometimes does not explain concepts well or will just skip to the math. Her math is very good and clear though and she overall is a very good and caring professor.

Professor Ray in an exceptional professor, she is good with explaining concepts and if you have questions she is always willing to help out during office hours. MA 373 is a challenging course but with practice and repetition the content is manageable. Always go to lecture, as some have said, notes are not posted online.

Professor Ray is one of the best professors at Purdue. Not only does she create notes for students to copy during class, she is also easily accessible outside of class if there are any questions. The exams are challenging, but she does her best to prepare us.

Professor Ray is the best professor I have had at Purdue. Her lectures are engaging, and she takes the time to get to know every student. The content is hard, but she explains it in a way to simplify it and make it more clear. Very accessible by email, generally responds that night. Homework heavy, but always there to help.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Amazing professor! She truly cares about the wellbeing/success of each student and knows everyone by name. The material can be difficult, but a strong background in MA373 is helpful and attending lectures is necessary. She want students to understand the material and is always willing to answer questions. I wish I could take more classes with her!

Professor Ray is the best. This class is an absolute grind with all the assignments but that is what you should expect taking a 4 credit math/stat class. The class is difficult but without professor ray teaching, it would be even more difficult. Could not recommend enough.

She always brings a smile to lecture and her classes are light-hearted. Her lecture material is not posted anywhere so get someone's phone number if you are going to miss. The class is decently challenging, but her class website and the platform the homework is on are good at explaining problems. So nice with emails and one on one meetings.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Completely disregard that last comment that's simply untrue. Professor Ray is a wonderful teacher in the Actuarial Science department. I didnt do as well as I wanted to on the 373 midterms, but I did well on the final to get UEC credit. I could not have done it without her help in office hours, where she was more than happy to reexplain topics.

She unfortunately is the only professor for this, but this class was awful. Probably top 5 hardest class at Purdue and she is so test heavy for UEC and she makes the midterm and final as difficult as possible. Do not recommend but you're forced with her anyways. Do not recommend. Most overrated professor at Purdue.

Most overrated and glazed professor; Certainly not a 1/5 but 5/5 don't think so. Had her twice and both times it's been a nightmare as a student who works full-time because she does not grant extensions and isn't reliable whatsoever. It's so frustrating working an 8-hour shift on an 18-credit course load only to be told no when I get home late.

Sally is salt of the Earth. She is truly one of the most caring instructors you will find. She is so approachable and wants to get to know all of us. Very easy to ask questions and responds quickly. She truly cares about our success and loves the material. Very big Purdue fan too! Funny and just plain awesome!

Sally Ray is one of, if not, the best professor at Purdue. She is very caring and is very accessible outside of class. If you are unable to make it to her office hours, she will try and setup someone other time to meet, in-person or on zoom.

The class itself is interesting and the material builds on itself a lot. Get the basics down well.

10/10

Sally is probably the best prof i've had. Notes are not posted online so go to lectures for sure. 2 hw assignments per week, 2 midterms and 1 final, tests are fair. lots of additional help as well with office hours and homework help sessions and when i couldnt make it to either of those sally would have 1 on 1 zooms with me. love her.
I love Sally! She is very caring and makes her lectures fun and engaging! The homeworks were fine as long as you went to the lectures. The final was extremely hard, but there was a big curve.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Genuinely the best professor I have ever had, the material is tough but having someone like Professor Ray helps a ton, goes above and beyond in both helping students understand the material and being an extremely nice person. Also, a very fair grader who gives extra credit opportunities.

This was one of my hardest classes, but she recognized that it was a complicated topic and went out of her way to help us in any way needed. She doesn't take attendance, but you have to go to lectures because they aren't recorded. It's alright, though, because the pace is quick, and the class flies by because it is always a lighthearted environment

Dr. Ray is a gem. Absolutely loved taking STAT 472 with her. She is always well prepared for class, teaches extremely well and creates a great learning environment for students to ask questions. Exams are extremely fair. Never had a better professor in any class before.
Great professor!

Lecture and homework were great ways of practicing the new concepts and I could tell that Professor Ray was trying her best to prepare us for the final and ultimately the actuarial exam. She is easily one of my favorite professors I've ever had since it was clear that she cared about us, both in and out of the classroom. Very approachable.
Her lectures were easy to follow and she made time for her students during office hours.  Would take again!

She is a great teacher and definitely cares. MA 373 is a tough class because of the amount of hw and tests we have but as long as you attend class and listen to the teacher (and go to office hours!!) you will be fine.

absolutely LOVE professor sally. there's several times i send late emails (like 11pm) about homeworks and she replied with in 5-10 mins. she tries her very best to help you with anything in the class, also she remembers everyone's name and something about them which is just so impressive!
One of the kindest professors I've had. She let me make up two quizzes outside of class

My favorite professor at Purdue! Her lectures are very clear, and she is always willing to help clarify anything you don't understand. Homework is manageable and she gives plenty of practice problems for exam prep. The exams/quizzes are very reasonable and she doesn't try to trick you. Very nice and funny professor! Cannot recommend enough:)
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Absolutely amazing prof!  She's extremely transparent with how her class is graded, and she gives out very thorough preparation materials for all of the quizzes and tests.  Very helpful for the FM test.

I love Sally! She's one of the best professors I have had at Purdue, and she always tries to thoroughly explain the material during class. She's super understanding and has no issue helping with homework or answering any questions. She also has a really cool personality and makes a relatively boring class pretty fun and interesting.

Sally is amazing. Explains the material well, and will take time out of class to better explain material that you don't understand if you ask. Exams, quizzes and homeowrk are fair, although there's a good amount of homework. If you're planning on taking FM this class will prepare you well but you probably won't pass FM by only showing up to class.

Prof Ray is very nice and will always try to answer any question you have, if it's about ASC or class. The class itself is tough, makes you work for it, sometimes she's a little sloppy in her lecture/notes but she'll clarify whatever you don't understand. Quizzes and tests do determine your grade.

Absolutely LOVE Sally. Her lectures are concise and clear. Make sure to pay attention in class and not miss lectures because her tests and quizzes are 100% based on the lectures. The stuff she tests you on is very reasonable. She even sends out practice problems so youll know exactly what to expect.

Professor Ray is one of the most nicest professors you'll meet. She is always open to meet with you and cares how her students are doing. Her lectures are concise and easy to understand, and the tests accurately reflect the material taught. I think she has done an excellent job as the new professor for MATH373, and I know she will only get better.

Great professor, nearly gave the answer to all the projects during the lectures. Labs were good and TAs were helpful. He was also nice. I just wish he'd call out the people who talk during lecture more because it makes it hard to follow.

Prof. Gustavo was a great lecturer. Lab grading criteria changed frequently, and grades were occasionally returned late. The online slides were comprehensive, though I noticed some formatting mistakes. I found the exams to be very difficult (avg, med < 50%), but the final was entirely MCQ. Participate in the lectures for bonus points.

252 is a lot of work with the projects. He extended some deadlines but it it is up to you to make good use of it. The last project is nice but it is glossed over. We had a tough midterm and a easy final. Expect to write the projects by hand for these exams. Treat the participation extra credit and the extra credit from the projects as the curve.

Honestly one of the GOATs of Purdue. Super passionate, always willing to answer questions. Make sure to talk to him after class about anything that you find neat or exciting about the class. Class is SUPER project heavy however, so going to labs (and staying) is a must. He cares a lot about his students and will accommodate if asked.

Professor Gustavo gave students enough time to finish lab assignments and make sure most students actually learn from the lab. He also gave fair exams and many opportunities for extra credits. However, you may need to learn more on your own outside of the class if you want to gain a deeper understanding in the field (like OS).

- Class is disorganized
- Content is boring and surface-level
- Exams require memorization of irrelevant trivia and implementation details
- Grading has errors, or grades aren't inputted properly
- Mandatory lecture and lab attendance
- Doesn't hold regular office hours

Pros: One of the few understanding CS profs—gives extensions, shares lab solutions, offers lots of extra credit, and is responsive to feedback. Live coding helps with learning. Chance for curve.
Cons: Course is disorganized with a scattered set of low-level topics. Heavy focus on exams (partly due to LLMs). Project deadlines were messy.

Goatstavo!
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--EXTRA CREDIT--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Extremely test heavy (30% midterm, 30% final) so very difficult for student who are not strong testers. Seem to have some issues with DRC accommodations. His office hours consist of "see me after class" so if you plan to speak to him, try not to have another class directly after his that you will be running off to. Lots of extra credit available!

Gustavo makes the course easier than Turkstra does, but this semester, his exams have been very unforgiving (the midterm more so than the final). There is very little you can do to know which multiple-choice topics will show up on exams, and he does little to help students prepare for them.

Gustavo is a great professor, really helps you understand what's going on. Lectures are engaging. After taking 250 and 252 with him, he is the best CS professor here at Purdue. Exams were difficult and labs are time consuming but your understanding of systems will deepen. Watch out for shell project
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The main thing saving Gustavo is that he clearly cares a lot about the student's success and as such extends deadlines, makes exams easier, and gives extra credit. However, the lectures are abysmal, the projects and slides haven't been updated since 2001, and the TAs have no idea what he wants either. The class is just uninteresting and unorganized
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I love Professor Gustavo! He genuinely cares about his students and the stuff he taught me in the last two semesters will stay with me throughout life.

I dont know if its the recent trend in AI in CS thats made him go mad at his students, but professor gustavo clearly did not like it and the midterm results for this class showed that. I thought CS250 was a fluke with him making bad exams but it seems to be the case in 252 as well now, simply way too test heavy and does not prep you well for them.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--EXTRA CREDIT--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: can't teach to save his life
Incredibly rushed and unorganized class. He expects us to learn things from quiet week on the exam and long functions and structures, which we have no reference or cheat sheet for.

Professor is a great lecturer, both engaging and informative. The projects are time consuming but useful. 

Be careful on exams. Some exam graders make mistakes, grade vaguely, and at times know the content worse than you do. If you go in person to fix grades with the professor, be cautious as professor grades "holistically" and not by the rubric.

Gustavo is liked by students because he outright gives answers to projects and gives extentions. But he doesn't teach well, and most handouts are confusing/don't give enough information to actually complete the projects. TLDR: he fixes problems he creates.
Take GRR if you want to pass, but if you want to actually learn, find other ways.
Goatstavo, just don't procrastinate and it will be an easy class.
He is the GOAT. All these other CS professors don't gaf about students well being at least gustavo does.

Gustavo gave me several personal extensions on labs because I was having a hard time that semester with other classes (this was Spring 2023). He is also kind and caring to his students. However, I felt that the labs were pretty difficult to get started on but that isn't because of Gustavo, its because of the class itself. Also, grading is vague.

He's a good lecturer and a really nice person. If you can take a class with Gustavo instead of Turkstra, then definitely do it. My only complaints are that sometimes the class can be disorganized and that his exams are really unpredictable, especially the final.
It's GOATsavo what's not to like.

Gustavo is an amazing professor who genuinely cares about students' success. Although the course material can be challenging, his engaging lectures, flexible deadlines, and willingness to help make it manageable. unstuckstudy is a great supplementary tool for exam prep.
A little miscommunication with his TAs but generous with extra credit and deadlines as well as lenient grading on exams.

Definitely be sure to work hard and complete the projects. They are what will really help you understand lectures more. As redundant as it sounds, START EARLY. Lectures varied in how easy they were to understand, but I definitely recommend always going.
He is My goat

Prior to taking his class, I had only heard good things about Professor Gustavo. Everyone was right, he really cares about his students' learning, and if you pay attention in lectures and study the material you will have at least a B in the class. Genuinely one of the best professors I've had.

Prof Gustavo is overall a good professor, but not a great lecturer. He focused too much on a Compiler project and rushed the actual CS 250 content in only a week right before the final. Projects are very time consuming, be ready to put in the time.

His lectures are engaging to listen to, but he spends a lot of time explaining and doing the projects in class, so more material could have been covered. Class was sometimes disorganized, like midterm being unavailable or giving everyone a 100 for assignments because not enough time to grade it. People like him as he gives (predictable) extensions.
He's really good but he always makes class end late and it's very annoying.

Had him for 250 and he was about the same in 252. He clearly cares about his students and is overall a pretty cool guy. My only complaint is that the requirements for projects just sometimes aren't accessible, leaving everyone confused. It is not fun to stress about about projects worth literally 15% of your grade because of unclear expectations!

The class was a bit disorganized, and the added content to the shell project did make me lose my mind, but otherwise he's a very caring professor. He extended deadlines as needed, and exam content was pretty well-correlated to labs and lecture content, so there wasn't too much extra self-studying needed.

Best instructor I've had at Purdue, or even ever, period. I've always had retention issues but in his lectures, I actually pay attention and learn. Unlike other profs *cough turk*, he sees students as humans and will understand your situation. Very caring and will HELP if you are struggling. So thankful for Gustavo, he's saving Purdue CS!!!

Gustavo is awesome, CS 252 destroys your perception of HW with Shell, but Gustavo gave deadlines and code and made the class bearable. He answers questions as best as he can. The lectures can be a bit boring, but the slides are posted, and you can study all the theory easily from the slides.
A little disorganized but a great professor and lecturer. He is always willing to help students.
Best professor! He has flexible deadlines and is very knowledgeable. His lectures are engaging and interesting. He's my biggest inspiration!
Gustavo is the love of my life. He's so amazing he made me like systems programming. My only wish in life is to picked for participation. I don't think I'd finish shell if not for him.
Gustavo gives me willpower to continue with my degree

Great:
- Bonus points for answering any question in class
- Incredibly caring and passionate
- Great system for attendance (partake)
- Makes a relatively dry subject (systems) interesting and fun

Not great:
- Course could use more organization
- Question forums could be monitored better at times

Overall, Gustavo is my favorite professor ever!!

Gustavo is a pretty chill professor, and he deserves his 5/5 rating. However, his lectures were good but were mostly reading from his slides, and it was often difficult to catch what information was actually important for the HWs and exams. The class was very interesting and he is clearly passionate about the content. Beware of compiler project!

Prof Gustavo genuinely cares about his students and wants them to learn. He actively listens to his students and extends deadlines when needed to minimize stress. He also gives plenty of hints and tips for projects and homework as well as extra credit opportunities.

He's my favorite professor at Purdue. He's incredibly knowledgeable, very passionate about teaching and course content, and he cares a whole lot about students. The projects we did in this course were meaningful, engaging, and enjoyable. I have literally no complaints, 10/10, GOATstavo

GRR is the best professor that I have encountered at Purdue. I rarely leave reviews for anything, but Gustavo has set himself apart. His lectures are long, but I could listen to him all day. He is an exceptionally nice man and genuinely cares about the students. I learned so much more from GRR than I would have from another professor.
He is really understanding with students. One of the best teachers I have had. Really nice professor and you learn a lot with him.

Gustavo is the GOATstavo. I say the lectures are mandatory because he teaches so well and gives bonus points to those to answer his questions in class. As a result, there's a really nice learning environment. I enjoy his class and his energy. He's funny and so nice. Such a good prof.

Professor Gustavo has to be one of the best professors I've had for a CS class because he really structured the course to set us up to succeed. Though some projects were quite hard, he gave us a lot of guidance along with several extensions that were extremely helpful. The lectures are excellent as he teaches the material very well.

I have heard from pretty much every CS upperclassman that Gustavo is the GOAT, and he truly was. He is without a doubt my favorite professor I have ever had at Purdue. He is extremely caring and flexible with project deadlines. The projects are notoriously for being tough, but there is ample time to complete them.

Some lectures can be a bit dry but he does his best to keep it engaging. Really he's just the most helpful CS professor i've ever had to date, giving plenty of direction on the labs, live coding examples, and psudeo code on the slides. Was scared for the shell project but he extended the due date and made it not a living hell. I respect his style.

Prof Gustavo gives amazing and hilarious lectures, allowing anyone to understand about very dry topics in Systems Programming. In addition, he cares about his students and is willing to give extensions to deadlines if most students are unable to meet them (though this seems to be a yearly occurrence).

Professor Gustavo is a very good lecturer who has the unique ability to make the driest of topics interesting. He is one of the few CS professors who truly cares about his students and is willing to make adjustments to allow students to complete their work without lowering course standards. This course was great, even with the shell project.
Very passionate about the content and the students. An amazing professor to learn from and I always enjoyed his lectures. A bit of an accent but its really not bad

Gustavo is an amazing professor, his class is always fun to participate not to mention that the curve is great as well. Keep in mind that participation does matter since there'll always be a quiz at the end of every lecture. Exams are relatively easy since it's live coding and he'll give you a list of problems that will appear on the exams .

Most of the lecture material is pretty boring, but Gustavo makes jokes and shows to his students that he really cares about our success. He is hard to email or contact outside of class, but is receptive to questions in/directly after class. Overall good guy and you can tell he cares, though his lectures aren't really helpful for the labs/exams.

Good guy, but his lectures (which don't help with labs) are boring despite mandatory attendance.
Class this sem was questionable. Homeworks/exams were graded a whole month after doing them. An entire midterm was somehow lost for many students. Weak structure (ex: no one knew how the 3 practice exams were weighted until the last day of the sem)

Lectures are quite mediocre but are okay. Labs were organized quite well and were fun. I enjoyed the course personally (probably the best course I had this semester) but I know plenty of peers who didn't think so YMMV.

CS240 with Professor Gustavo is a what you make of it. The lecture content is not great for first time C learners. All potential exam questions are given to you a week before the exam. The homework grading is generous and forgiving if you put in time to complete it. He offers extra credit towards the end but it is very difficult. Seek help with TAs

Material in lectures often had little to do with labs. TAs given little material to work with. Professor disorganized, unprepared, and confusing in lectures. Grading extremely late. Lost around 20% of exams. Extremely disappointed with poorly taught and poorly organized course. But hey, C will not get in your way to make your program fast!!
I love him

Professor Gustavo is fantastic at making CS material extremely interesting. The material covered in this course is very important and Gustavo will cover way more than the other CS250 Professors, which sounds like a bad thing, but it's very manageable and puts you ahead in following CS courses.

Gustavo is a phenomenal instructor! He is engaging, passionate about CS, and understands that we're students. Highly recommend going to/watching lecture since he explains important lab concepts. The exams are usually based on lab content, so watching lectures helps with studying! Start labs EARLY; you need all the time that you can get on them!
Professor Gustavo is the best CS professor in Purdue, and he is funny and cute. He really cares about students.
Even though the course material is very difficult, Prof. Gustavo's lecture makes them so much easier to understand. Great professor.
252 with Gustavo is one of those legendary combinations you just can't miss. He is engaging, and he goes over all of the labs in lecture in detail, so you always know what to expect.

Simply the best. Fantastic professor, incredibly passionate about what he's teaching. Funny enough to keep your attention, without getting off-topic in an annoying way. The course is tough, but he does all he can to help students, and willing to take feedback into account. If I could have him as my professor for every Purdue CS class, I would.

Gustavo is seriously one of my favorite professors at the university. Not just in the CS department, but at the school in general. He really cares about his students success and has an amazing way of explaining complicated concepts. But be warned, the projects are EXTREMELY hard. Especially malloc and the shell project.

Gustavo is a great professor. His lectures are clear and easy to understand. He repeats the important stuff multiple times to make sure you definitely remember it. He does give "brownie points" (extra credit), which I did not expect this at all until the final grade was released.

He cares about his class and his students a lot and you can see that for sure. The class is hard but he's very understanding and willing to help you through it. His accent might be something you need to get used to though. Tests worth a lot so make sure you prepare for them

Don't get me wrong cs250 is a challenging course standalone, but Gustavo really knows a lot of things and can teach you things even beyond the scope, and I really appreciate that. He treats student's question with patient, and can often extend the student's questions and teaches us more about fundamental CS. Exam counts as 60%, and it's very hard.

Professor Gustavo is one of the best professors at Purdue CS. He is very encouraging and inspiring. He pushes you to work harder. I would say he is not a strict professor. I learned a lot from the class, the lectures were awesome. He made the class interactive even though we had zoom lectures. I would recommend a class with 100%.

Really nice man and great professor. Good lectures and fairly straightforward exams. The main part of the class is the 5 projects, and while they aren't that easy, if you start early and take your time to work through it, it's very doable.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Respected--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Gustavo is a smart professor and good at communicating and teaching. Class was online so he didn't really have attendance but he had quizzes and tried to make sure people didn't answer on the livestream to encourage you to watch the videos.

Had Professor Gustavo for CS250 and CS252. He was great in both classes. Exams are easy so long as you show up to class and do a little bit of studying. Labs/projects are no issue so long as you start early. Lectures are very entertaining, and will miss them.

I had Gustavo for both CS250 and CS252. In both classes he was an excellent professor that explained the material thoroughly. He is very passionate about his work and that showed in his lectures. Class is very doable as long as you budget your time right for the labs. One of the best professors at Purdue.

I had him for 250 so I was so excited to have him for 252. He only got better. Such a smart, caring and kind person. He actually made me consider pursuing Systems as a track. Very inspirational. Explains labs in class. If you take this class seriously, you will have fun. Start labs ASAP and make sure to get help along the way.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures--Caring

reviews.comment: Amazing professor. The class was not as difficult as expected. START LABS EARLY.
The best prof!

Professor is a very knowledgeable person and explains everything really well. He is also really supportive of asking questions. Overall, if you get him, you are in good hands! Just be genuinely interested in his lectures, take notes and ask questions whenever you have them. Material isn't too hard, just takes a little time to understand.
Now I register for class, I see Gustavo and I pick him right away.

I took 240, 252, and 408 with Gustavo. My absolute favorite professor. He explains material like no other. Exams are hard but fair. Projects relate well to the material. Just remember: C doesn't get in your way to make your program run fast.

By far best CS professor at Purdue. Can't say enough nice things about the guy. Explains concepts very clearly in lectures so that students understand. Provides extensions on assignments when many students are struggling because he believes that it's better for students to do the assignments and learn instead of give up and lose out on learning.
Amazing professor. Would take him again for any other class in a heartbeat.
Gustavo is a rock star.

Gustavo is a great lecturer who is constantly reminding students of the big picture. He does require attendance with iQlicker questions after lecture. 252 is the last painful CS class you'll take, and Gustavo is very reasonable with deadlines and even gave us an extra four days for the shell project. I also had him for 251. Would recommend.

Amazing professor; accent can be a small roadblock, but if you can get past that, he's a great lecturer. Specifically CS252: The "dreaded shell project" is not that big of an issue. You have to write your own version of bash, but lecture+book notes give you amazing code snippets. All 5-6 projects allow for extra credit, class curve was 82% -> A.
Legend!
The best professor at Purdue, end of story.

Always teaches great classes; in my time, his classes for CS240 and CS251 were pretty early so it was hard to want to get up for them, but he's a very effective lecturer. He does have "mandatory attendance," but really it's just an iClicker quiz at the end of class (which he gives you the answer to so you don't lose points). Always take his classes
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Very fair professor. Extended projects when students were struggling. He's a funny guy and makes a lot of jokes during class. He clearly cares a lot about his students' success. On the flip side, he can be a little disorganized, sometimes he'd release solutions or assignments much later than promised. Difficult class but good professor.

Course is tough, but professor is nice. He really cares about his students. Quick response on piazza and emails. Practice exams cover most of real exams, so do do all practice exams Labs are really tough tho but you have whole week to do each.

Very good professor. I took cs240 and cs251 with him. He explains topic pretty well and the exams are very predictable. Going to take cs252 with him again. His accent can be hard to understand at first, but once you got used to it, you will be fine.
Had him for 240 and 251, Amazing professor, knows what he is talking about. Can approach him whenever and is clear on the assignments

He is funny and knowledgable. Also, he is very easy on student, not the bad way. His exams are usually selected form homework or lab problems. He won't throw questions that you never seen before in exam.
CS240 with Gustavo  was amazing! You will need to work hard but he will always be ready to help. He makes lectures interesting and is a funny guy!
Prof Gustavo's CS390-VRA. He showed up for class maybe once or twice. The course was pretty much a self-taught class with TA's to walk around and help you if needed during lab. Easy A

Gustavo is a fantastic lecturer. He explains the topics very clearly and also takes the time to explain why each topic matters and what its real-world applications are. He's also pretty funny. Attendance is 10% of the grade, but it's really not a big deal because the lectures are so great.
Great overall professor who knows what he's doing.

One of the best, if not THE best, professors in the CS department. His lectures are easy to understand and you will learn a lot from him. His projects can be tough, but he will help students to finish them. Exams can be difficult as well, but they are fair and only cover material that was taught in class. He cares about his students' success.
Knowledgeable professor. Completely chill. Classes are easy if you attend his lectures.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: He is a good professor, even though I retake his CS240. Very friendly and open to students.
Gotta work for the grade. Great lecturer, just make sure to pay attention so you can understand his accent.

Wonderful Professor!Very helpful and has great lectures; always provides examples and the accent,despite the natural dryness of the material, makes the lectures interactive and fun!He really wants to help students; if you put work in the labs and projects,you will do fine. Exams are fair but could be lengthy. Definitely recommend taking his classes

Although professor has great accent, but he is very nice. If you have time conflict with the exam, he will push back to another time. If there are a lot of students who can not finish lab on time, he will assist them.
Very heavily project-based class. If you know C you're fine; otherwise, it will require some work on your part. Definitely recommend going to class.
Fantastic professor and very understanding. Really wants to help you succeed.

Excellent professor. He is generous to provide all the slides as long as the semester begins. Since his slides is so clear that you do not need any text book for his course, which is awesome! At first his accent may scare you, but everything's gonna be excellent as long as you get used to it. The best prof I have ever met in CS Department.

PROS:Explains everything clearly, pretty easy project based teaching method, gives clear study material for exams. You literally have to try to fail his class. Go to class, do his exam study, get an A. CONS: Interesting accent, not the worst I've heard. Can feel a little slow if you already understand some of the material since he is very verbose.

HILARIOUS! excellent notes...GO. TO. CLASS. although, he does post some of them and whomever posts the notes gets extra credit. i think he's an ok prof, just go to class and take the notes. he extends basically every project, so not too much worry there, just do them...cuz they are still long and hard
Great professor!! I love him
Wonderful professor.
Had him for 252 and 250. Awesome guy, will do anything to help you out if you ask. Always willing to give extensions if you just ask. Prepares you well for his exams. Highly recommended.

I had Gustavo for Computer Architecture and Data Structures. He has an amusing accent. Exams are difficult. Projects are difficult. Some of his notes are dead wrong. But you have to be in class, or you won't ever know.

Dedicated, friendly, and interesting.  His projects are the best and most educational of nearly everyone in the department.  I and several other students swear by his teaching.  Many of us would take a bullet for him.  He can veer off on wild tangents that somehow end up being surprisingly informative.  I wish I had taken every class he offered.

Gustavo is an amazing professor. His classes are project-based and easy because of his clear explanations. If a certain project is too difficult, ask for an extension and he'll be happy to help. If you have any opportunity to take classes with him, do it. However, don't bother buying the textbook; it's never officially used for class.

This is one of the best professors I have had at Purdue.  The course is easy if you try.  His projects are the most practical and best real-world examples that I have gotten, ranging from a search engine to an internet phone to a Twitter clone.  I definitely recommend him.
AWESOME !!!
CS354

Supplies a LOT of code for projects and explains it well enough for tests to be reasonable.  If you have a complaint about the course or struggle with any material, he'll do everything he can to help you out... no joke.
A tad hard to understand, but interesting lecture and random tangents.  Pretty easy exams and projects.  One of the memorable CS profs.

Gustavo is awesome.  He has great advice on what programmers should know even if it isn't part of the class.  He is more practical than any of the other CS teachers I've had at Purdue and his classes include interesting projects.  I think anybody can get an A in his classes if they try.  He is always available and helpful.
Great professor - lectures are very interesting. Projects are very applied and make learning the material easier.
Awsome prof. Taught well.

Class is way too dumbed down. Lingers on simple things and then practically skips over important stuff. Half of the OS midterm was about linked lists for god's sake. I felt dumber every time I came, so I didn't. Got an A both times.
He's a little hard to understand; but he is also the only professor that I have ever had at Purdue that responds to my emails at that I send at 10:00 pm or 11:00 pm within 5-10 minutes.

Had him for both Operating Systems and Computer Networks....  CS 354 is HIS course.  His course structures are far more practical, informative and fair than most of my CS courses were.  Good Job Gustavo  (and yes, the accent is ..distinctive!)
Awsome professor... What else I can say.
Made the course easy. If taken under Elisha, I would have failed!!!
You learn about ze boffer in leenooks.
eh... guys, it's not difficult...  He had some amusing quirks, but was an amazing professor.  He always went out of his way to help out a student.
There's nobody like Gustavo!
No Comments
Amusing accent and very flexible on assigned due dates
No Comments

Lectures are no-tech/no slides, handwritten note yourself, with limited study material. Reviewing is very difficult. Exams are closed-note and test very detailed points from what he said on class you must memorize exactly. Videos are provided to learn coding html, but you will need to write out the exact code during exam. Do Not Recommend!

Dr. D is an effective lecturer, who, if paid attention to, provides clear conceptualizations through use of personal storytelling. I found that paying attention in class and taking concise notes helped me to succeed. He is old-school, and prohibits technology, but I felt that minimizing distractions helped me to stay focused.

Dr. D is an effective lecturer who explains concepts well. His work sessions prepare you well for HW assignments. Exam is a big portion of the grade, is fully closed‑book/notes, and is quite difficult, with some very specific details tested--I think he takes pride in its difficulty. But he does give partial credit. I did learn a lot in the class.

Mr. Dejoie deeply cares about MGMT 382 and has a lot of time under his belt, however, this class is painful to sit through sometimes. NO TECHNOLOGY in addition to no lecture slides makes you writing in your notebook every thought he spurts out since it's most likely going to be on the exam. If you miss a class you better get the notes from someone.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: RUN! You HAVE to take notes to do well. Practically no resources to learn if you miss class. All short-answer exam: questions require you to remember his specific ramblings/reasoning, not just "correct" answers (despite open-ended questions) - notes from previous sections can be different. No technology allowed - handwritten notes only. Very boring
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Please put all the content in the PowerPoint presentation instead of relying on the professor to lecture, and don't even take notes. Not everyone is a native speaker. I have never seen an exam that is all short answer questions. The worst professor is the one you should choose. If you accidentally choose him, run away!

Absolutely HORRIBLE professor. He would ONLY reach out on Piazza and he would respond to issues with a lot of verbiage but little to no guidance. He loves to gloat about how hard his exam is but that is because it is all closed notes and difficult to prepare for. Mind you this is a Python course that can be for beginners. AVOID.

Group Projects are super easy but only worth a small part of your grade. His lectures are 10x more boring than any other teacher I've ever had. The tests (70% of grade) have questions that make you quote what he said in class which makes studying for them almost impossible. He loves to brag about how hard his class is and wants you to fail.

This professor made the course 10x more difficult than it should be. The material of this course is not bad, the way that it's structured is terrible. It doesn't feel like he wants everyone to succeed, even the peer evaluation is designed in a way where someone has to lose points even if everyone did equal work. Terrible
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Switch to a different professor. There are a total of five assignments including the two exams worth 70% of your grade.  By the end of the semester I had grades for my first exam and first project.  Lectures are confusing as he goes on personal tangents often. The exams aren't too bad as they're purely based on lecture. Your notes are everything

By far one of the worst professors at Purdue. If you miss one of his lectures, you better hope the material covered that day isn't on the exam. His lectures are extremely boring and not engaging at all. If you need help, I suggest asking one of the TAs instead because he will grill you on how you don't understand the material.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Very specific questions on exam. He also doesn't curve the class average to a 3.0.

Dr. D expects you to go to every single lecture, and if you miss class, he won't provide appropriate resources for you to learn the material. The lecture slides cover less than 10% of what you see on exams. His lectures are unbearable to listen to since he goes on tangents on his days at Texas A&M and on personal tales with no relation to class.

No slides or outside resources for topics, and you have to go to class everyday or else you won't make it. Exams are the hardest I've ever taken since it's off of what we talked about in class and it's all open answer with absolutely NO hints. He is very intellectual but lectures are so heavy it's hard to know everything we are expected to know.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: There are no slides at all, the exams are just based on his rambling for the entire class. The exams are worth 70% of your grade and the exam makes you refer straight from what he says in class. I wouldn't take this class if you can't learn from someone just sitting and talking.

All the content for the class is basically him rambling and you just have to try and keep up. Outdated lecture style. Often goes off on tangents and tests were hard. Project criteria makes no sense and you cannot equally grade groupmates. Avoid this professor, other lectures with different teachers have easier tests and a much easier overall class.

Some people don't like Dr D, but I enjoy being in his classes. He is very straight foreward and will tell you how it is and what he expects from you. If you follow along through the progression of the course and genuinely learn the code you will be fine. Don't work with a partner / copy others, just learn it.

Who would set a due date on Sunday at 12 am... Beware!!! The discussion board is due every week on Sunday "12 AM". Why can't he just tell students that it is Saturday 11:59 pm. I was too focused on "Sunday" and didn't realize it's actually due the day before. I don't know why but I feel like this is just tricking students into the wrong deadline.

The exams are challenging but in my section there was a significant curve that helped me out on one of the exams. Three group projects, but none of them take very long to complete. A few warnings: follow all directions on assignments carefully, and take detailed notes. Like, be way way way more detailed than you usually would.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Group projects

reviews.comment: Took this class online and it was very disorganized. There was no clear schedule for him uploading lectures. His lectures were not necessarily related to the material, but him talking about unrelated topics. Exams are said to be based on lectures, but the exam format makes them difficult.

Dr. D is one of the worst professors I've ever had in Krannert. He provides few notes, basically zero slides, no study guides, and no practice exams. You just have to take notes on him rambling the whole class because the exams are based entirely on lecture. He is arrogant and scams you into buying his own textbook (which we never used). Avoid him

Worst professor I have had in Krannert. Doesn't know how to lecture concisely, loses track of lecture, doesn't have any notes, doesn't help students, doesn't care about students, extremely arrogant, extremely rude, not helpful with feedback. His approach to being a professor is as if he's doing us a favor.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Personally I am not a fan of Roy Dejoie, he will not compromise with students and chews your ear off with his stories and nonsense. His method of teaching is outdated and scams students by writing his own textbook so he can pocket more money. I don't recommend him and hope I never have to take another one of his classes.

He is a great professor and answered my questions all of the time. This is a beginning coding class, and it was very challenging. I wish I could have done better but this was my first time ever coding. There are weekly quizzes from the reading and weekly labs. Labs are basically worked out in class though. Overall, a pretty good class!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Easy class as long as you show up to lectures and study your notes. Participation policy is really dumb but other than that great Prof.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Great lecture, need to attend class and participate, exams are based what is said in class! Need to take notes. Few assignment, so everything matter. Treat midterm like finals! Tough exam if do not pay attention in class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Participation matters--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Dr. Dejoie is one of the best professors who teach programming at Purdue (and I have taken many coding classes with the CS department). He gives very clear instructions and uses a lot helpful examples in class that are similar to the homework(aka lab) assignments. If you skip lab days, you will definitely miss out on a LOT of valuable information.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Most hard-grading professor at Krannert
