I am a senior and I graduated with a 3.7 GPA using two and a half years. Let's be honest Purdue sucks and you don't have many options when it comes to selecting Upper division courses. But this guy is the only professor that I hope you guys could avoid at all cost. Boring lectures, exam you could never really prepare for,AVIOD AT ALL COSTS

Dr. D made class fun, engaging, and enjoyable and also has a lot of industry experience. Most of the class is lecture-based, so make sure to pay good attention and take good notes. There are three group projects that, combined, make up 30% of your grade, and then there is one midterm and a final. Participation is important in this class.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: This class has relatively no application outside of MGMT382. If you aren't an amazing note taker good luck. He provides no practice exam or study guides for the exams. Lectures are nearly impossible to follow along and he has almost no slides for notes. You just have to take notes off what you hear.
Great teacher, must pay attention in the lectures and take good notes to do well. No need for a textbook, but everything he says is up for game on the tests.

Dr. D is one of my favorite professors since I've been in Krannert. Immensely helpful, funny, and knowledgeable. The class is difficult, and the tests are hard, but if you pay attention in class, you will be fine.

Professor Dejoie is among the best teachers I have had, ever. The course is not highly technical, but purposely made to give pragmatic knowledge for all majorsminors.  One of the best things about him, aside from always being there and excellent lectures is he has great industry experience. His course and teaching reflect this.
You do not need to buy the textbook, you have to participate during class to get credit, exams are all memorization of useless terms, cram a bunch before the exam and you will be fine

Dr. D keeps class fun and interesting. Didn't need to buy textbook. Be sure to take detailed notes because exams are based off lecture notes. Be sure to brush up on your memorization skills, you'll need them to do well on the exams!
u really have to pay attention in class and take all the notes or else you won't do well on the exam
Great Prof! Yes, goes on tangents (they are slightly related to the subject) if you can make the connections, you will MASTER the subject.
Dr. D rambles incessantly. Constantly going off on a tangent. Probably on 10% of class actually translates into knowledge transfer, the rest is absolute garbage.
No Comments

Dr. D is an awesome teacher.  He does grade a little hard but he make the class a TON of fun.  The tests aren't bad AS LONG AS YOU ATTEND THE REVIEW SESSIONS!  I think he's been my favorite professor at Krannert.
this is the worst class i have ever taken
He made my life so difficult. his lectures are not great. too abstract for no reason and so dense
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: This professor is bad. I dont know if its cause he has nothing to do but he will ensure you also have nothing to do but his work. His class is a 9-5 with himself saying its an "internship". Exams on New Year's Eve instead of being able to spend time with family cause he is miserable. Terribly spaced exams and homework.

I urge you to NOT take this professor: he has ZERO care for his students and wants them to struggle due to his personality. I took his online winter course; he assigned an exam on New Years Eve. I had to pay an additional fee just to ACCESS hw after paying for the course. Just an all around poor expereince led by a "teacher"
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This class was way harder than it should've been. His lecture videos are so confusing I never watched them, I would like give the slides to Chat and have it explain it to me. The homework and computer assignments were annoying, but more annoying is in class worksheets that are not that relevant. You're just going to teach yourself.

lowkey man this was a really hard class i dont know if im the stupid one or not but wow it was hard. i do not recommend he is not a great lecturer i crashed out over this class way too much i really really really did not enjoy it. never knew whether to laugh or cry after exams.

This dude is prolly the most inconvenient person ever. He put his 1st exam the day after fall break, his 2nd exam the Monday of Thanksgiving break, like bro I don't want to see you fr I wanna see the fam yfm. He assigned 3 assignments that take 2-3 hours each all due the same day, like if you wanna give me an F just rip off the band aid already vro

Do NOT TAKE THIS CLASS (Stat 350) IF YOU HAVE A SUMMER INTERNSHIP. Loads of busy work and NOT enjoyable content. Imagine coming home after a long day of work and having to memorize R code semantics and read 15 pages of a dry textbook. Very painful class. The assignments felt like pulling teeth.

This class was taught pretty well and ultimately wasn't too bad. The worst part was long homework assignments and some oddly worded exam questions, however, every single exam had extra credit so it wasn't a big deal. Professor Reese does a good job at keeping Brightspace organized and always keeping the class informed. Overhated to be honest.

His lecture videos and forum answers often go off on entirely irrelevant tangents or around in circles, leaving you as clueless as when you started.

Necessary information is scattered in at least three different places for any given task, meaning you can never answer a question as is and must check for hidden a gotcha point deduction elsewhere.

Prof. Reese responds to questions quickly and is always happy to help. His lecture videos are decent but can be hard to understand sometimes, so make sure to re-watch the hard parts. To succeed in exams, make sure you are VERY comfortable with all the statistics procedures and don't over-rely on cheat sheet. Else, you run out of time.

Stat350 was one of the harder classes that I've taken so far at Purdue. However, the fact that you are forced to truly learn and understand the fundamentals behind statistics is paramount in your future career at Purdue University as well as the real world. I've learned more with him than any other professor so far.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: If you have this prof you're on your own for the semester. He's not an awful person but if you ask him a question he is probably not gonna be able to explain it to you. Some of the exam material comes straight out of left field.
he has a lot of resources but his lectures made no sense to me, i had to learn everything myself and the homework is hard.
Taken Summer 2024-- He was very helpful with questions and the course was very well organized with lectures, homework, and discussions so we could see the questions our classmates had.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Great Professor and seems to care. I found his lectures entertaining because he gives note outlines so you can focus on understanding the material. Tests are few but you have to do good on them
This guy is THE GOAT. Scott is amazing in every way possible. He actually saved my dog from a burning building while simultaneously lecturing about MGMT 201. What a guy.

He can be monotoned, but he is extremely helpful and very understanding. I had a situation during my exam, and he was extremely helpful, making me clam down and still have time to finish my exam. He wants to be an actual professor and knows students have situations so he helps rather than punishes them for being human beings.

Overall Scott is a great professor. He cares about his students a lot and wants them to succeed. Attendance is not mandatory but he gives extra credit if you come. The class if a little dry but its ok. I didn't do so well on the first exam but was able to bounce back by completing his practice exams and ask questions in class. Take him for MGMT 201

Sorensen is a very caring professor who wants his students to succeed.  He is very accessible if you ever have a question and he provides extra credit for attendance.  Accounting is a subject that is hard to make interesting, but I think he does a good job presenting the material in a very straightforward manner.

His lectures are really dry. Spends most of the lectures hunched over the document camera writing problems. Other than that the class itself isn't too hard. The exams and homework are pretty straightforward.

Overall a good professor. Teaches content well and in an easy to follow manor, but lectures aren't very engaging (its accounting). He is very kind and tries to be understanding. Overall a solid pick for someone genuinely trying to teach and help students.
He is a really understanding and nice professor. It was clear he wanted his students to succeed and do well in his class. He is understanding on extensions even after the deadline.

Thank God the class is over ! What a joke. Like others have said, Sorensen was sooo monotone and boring - I could hardly stay awake in class as he just went on and on and on. I understand this is it for him so that's good for all those taking it starting next school year - not soon enough as far as I am concerned. Purdue accounting is a joke.

I appreciated that he recorded lectures because I could not attend class without falling asleep.  The subject itself was fine because he gave us equation sheets for exams, but he was your typical boring accountant who was very monotone.
Nice guy, very monotone and boring class content. Provides a very helpful formula sheet for each exam. Class content is extremely boring and not practical outside of just getting a grade.
He was a very knowledgeable and helpful professor, but the lectures were just very boring.

I really enjoyed this class and Scott as a professor. I think accounting is fun so I didn't have a problem with the lectures being boring. He answered my emails quickly and answered any questions I had after class. He definitely cares about our success. The exams were not too hard and he provides a good formula sheet for each one. I recommend him!
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Sorensen clearly cares about what he teaches and his students' success. I'm repeating others here, but yes, while his lectures do feel boring, that's just due to the content itself. There's not a lot you can do to make accounting exciting. Homework is not bad, and formula sheets on the exams were very helpful.

Professor Sorensen was very nice and clearly explained things, however, he was very monotone which made lectures difficult for me personally. He provided formula sheets for exams which were super helpful, and he posts the lectures online so attendance wasn't required which was nice.

Professor Sorensen is very nice and understanding! There is only a homework assignment per week. All other points for the class are exams. He gives extra credit during random lectures if you are there. If you know the concepts and how to do the problems done in class you will do well on the exams! He responds fast to emails too.

Great professor and super nice guy, but the class itself is pretty boring. 1 point of extra credit randomly for attendance. Class is manageable and professor is very understanding and accommodates to needs of students. Definitely recommend doing homework to pad your grade - HW is significantly easier than mgmt200.
He is willing to help if you reach out to him. He answers any questions that are given out, and provides a formula sheet with the exams.
Professor is intelligent and nice, but lecture can become boring and distracting at times

I was nervous to take the class going into it because of some of the reviews, but the professor does care about your success if you reach out when needed. He mentioned they restructured the class based on previous years reviews. Only bad part was lectures were hard to hear unless you sat up front.
reviews.rating_tags: Caring--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I really liked Professor Sorenson. I accidentally did the wrong homework once and he gave me a 100% for the assignment I missed. He is very understanding, you just have to reach out to him. He gives lots of practice questions and the exams were online with a 24 hr window to take them. Very caring professor and definitely one of my favorites!
Professor Sorensen is pretty nice. He gave us a lots of practice example in the class.
Good communicator, class is 2/3 exams though and they're certainly no joke

Avoid this guy at all costs (take it at Ivy Tech if possible). It is HIM, not the material. I'm not surprised at all that most don't like him, he is a terrible teacher. Yes, accounting is boring but Sorensen makes it 2x as bad. He is NOT helpful - I tried to meet him outside of class but he kept pushing me off to TA. Scott QUIT, you're very bad !
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I don't get the dislike for Prof Sorensen! He is a genuinely great and caring professor and it shows in his class. While his class can be a bit boring, a lot of that comes from the generally uninteresting material in 201, not necessarily him. Provides extra credit for attendance, super helpful formula sheets on exams and has shortened class times.

Prof is monotone and boring however, the slides are helpful in learning the concepts which were pretty easy. Exams are straightforward and he provided a formula sheet with the necessary information to complete them. He is an understanding professor that will try to accommodate students if they need it.

God I'm so glad it's over - this guy is TERRIBLE. He is just up there going through the motions - so boring, monotone, just reading PPTs. I hated having to go listen to this guy. AVOID AT ALL COSTS. Scott, do us all a big favor and quit, you're not a good teacher, stop trying to play one.

Scott is a great professor and makes 505 an easy class. The exams and homework are very straightforward. He cares a lot about his students and is always open to helping students out. I found it easier to read the textbook instead of going to class. However, he sometimes gives out extra credit if you attend classes.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Gives good feedback--Caring

reviews.comment: He's nothing phenomenal, and his lectures surely aren't that exciting. You can get that from others' reviews. The content however is the important thing. Exams 1&2 are fairly easy, so try to learn that content better as the last 2 are more difficult. The class isn't too hard, but some units are tedious which makes things a little more difficult.
Sorenson is a fine professor. He records lectures and seems to care about student learning. Not an amazing lecturer but most accounting professors aren't.

Sorensen is a good guy and cares about the students, but the lectures can be a bit dry if you are bit tired. There are 4 exams, and he gives 1 point EC randomly throughout the semester for attendance. Exams are online with webcam, but the formula sheets are super helpful. Great communicator and HW assignments take less time than MGMT 200.

Professor Sorensen is a great guy and this class was the best accounting class I have taken yet. He talks a little slow, but he posts everything on Brightspace so you can watch the lectures after class on 2x speed and finish the material in about half the time. The slides are hugely beneficial to the exams.

Good god does this man talk slow. Most of the time I end up skipping lecture and watching it later at 1.75x speed. But he gives an unreal amount of info on the formula sheets you can use during you exam so I like him

Sorenson is a really nice guy and pretty funny from time to time. However to no fault of his own, his voice absolutely puts me to sleep. Staying awake and engaged during his lectures is difficult due to how monotone he is. He seems to know what he's talking about and I'm sure I could've done better in this course if I were able to stay awake.

He is very knowledgeable and accessible for help. I recommend reading the textbook because he teaches right out of it. If you read the textbook, lectures are unnecessary. Lectures are monotonous and boring, but he provides extra credit randomly for attendance. He isn't a bad professor by any means, but the content is just not very interesting.
Good teacher who is fair to all students. Lectures are not the greatest, but being someone who is quite monotone themselves, I can definitely feel for him.

Scott is such a nice and helpful person. He really cares about you as a student and is always there to help. I found the course to be very easy (much easier than MGMT200). I never watched the lectures and instead just read the textbook and took notes.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: He does not give more percentage on exam. He just gave us same percentage as the homework. The grading system was the worst thing.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Professor Sorenson is honestly a really great resource. He's made it very clear that he cares about students' success in his class. He's very accessible outside of class and he responds to emails relatively quick. During lectures, he is a little monotone and it can be hard to pay attention, but if you really put the work in, you can do really well.

Professor Sorenson is such a nice professor, he genuinely cares about his students and if you email him about concerns or questions he almost always accommodates to help you out. If you attend class regularly he's more likely to help you out when it comes to earning as many points as possible in the class. He's a dad so he understands kids

Although he teaches what many see as a boring class, Professor Sorensen himself is a good instructor and never blindsides you with any material. You can easily get 100s on all homework if you take notes/go to class. Exam questions are pretty fair and often look like the examples he does in class. If you're a good student, you will be just fine.

The material itself that Professor Sorensen is teaching isn't the best, but he honestly gives a lot of feedback for the complex material. I think he is a solid professor and that he makes the class bearable as well. Also, he always gives updates to us as students on due dates and exams which I like.
Although the professor may be a bit boring, this class is an easy A. The average was a B+/A-. Just do all your Mcgraw-Hill homework.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Its a relatively boring class and like someone else said, he can be a bit dry. He will always be there to answer questions, and he will often end his lectures early. While the subject is boring, the class is what you make of it.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: For everyone saying that he is a "terrible professor", they are upset that they have to put in effort for a class. Yes, he is dry, but he truly does care about his students. He's real down to Earth and will ALWAYS answer any questions you have. His exams aren't too bad if you do the Foundational 15 and Review Problems he gives you as practice. 5/5.

Sorenson is a great professor. He's a little dry, but he's very clear with his lecture materials and due dates. Just take notes on the slides, do the homework on time, and do the practice problems and you'll do great on the exams. These other reviews probably just didn't study. If you do, this is an easy class to get an A in
My guy Scott Sorenson is terrible. Would recommend not taking this course, especially with him. Boring and dry when teaching in lectures.

This is probably the worst professor at Purdue. I'm literally writing this review in his class right now because of how appalled I am he is considered a professor at Purdue. He legit just reads of lecture slides, doesn't look to see if students have questions and does not give proper guidance for the class. Class isn't too hard but WORST PROFESSOR.
Sitting in this guy's class each week listening to his monotone regurgitation of the PPTs was worse than a root canal !
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Absolutely TERRIBLE prof ! He CAN'T teach, he just stands there and reads the slides, so bad. I had to put in so much extra time to do well in the class - I'm going to switch to Finance major. because of this guy - what a joke.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: As long as you watch the videos and do the homework. This class shouldn't be that hard to pass. Make sure you do the practices on Connect before an exam as it helps a lot.

First off. I do not watch his lectures. I've heard horror stories. His slides, which he provides, are sufficient to teach yourself. He is a really nice guy and I've enjoyed being in his course, though the content itself is well.... accounting. Its boring.
BEST ADVICE FOR U! RUN!!!!!!

I can't believe I have to pay for taking this class, this guy is so bad. Where did they get him??? He just reads the PPTs and is so boring. I'd rather have a root canal done than sit through another class with this guy ! Avoid if possible but I see he is back again for summer and fall and only one teaching it - what a nightmare !
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Stopped watching lectures after the second week. Easy class but he really cant teach.
He just slowly reads the PowerPoint to the class every time. You just have to try and teach yourself

Scott Sorenson seems like a lovely person, BUT his teaching method makes for a great lullaby if you want to take a nap in his class. He reads from the slides and has a very monotonous voice and speaks very slowly. He does his examples from slides by reading it to us, rather than showing us. Homeworks aren't terrible because of the unlimited checks.

His section's exam grades are consistently 30 points lower than everyone else. His lectures are pointless and he does not teach well. If you ask him to explain he says to study harder and go to office hours. When everyone fails an exam he tells us it's our fault.

He creates his own exam questions which are much harder than the actual course exam questions. Its also very hard to study for exams using past exams, course problems etc because he makes them himself and does not give you any direction or resources.

He thinks the problems for this course are too easy so he makes his own for the exam, which end up always being too difficult and make it hard to study for the exams because you dont know the type of questions will be on it.

He's okay except for assigning homework over spring break, then making it due the Saturday of the end of spring break and not Sunday which is normally when Pre-Exam Homework's are due. Seems like he wanted students to miss the due date for the homework.

Expects us to study harder for his class all over spring break. Assigns homework due during spring break and also makes an exam the day after spring break is over. Very bad teacher do not take class with him.
He always belittle students when they ask questions, which makes students to not ask questions.

1.watched yeehaw videos in class (on the web)
2. Doesn't teach the correct content
3. Doesn't help

Unhelpful prof with a huge ego who talks down to his students. Online resources and TAs were more useful for learning material than he was. Made the structure of the course drastically different from the main campus (ie, 3 midterms instead of 2, FRQ instead of MC), though the courses are supposed to be the same on both campuses. Avoid this prof.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: He's not the worst, but he complicates the exam too much. Do not recommend but if you get him he's alright I guess.
Bad professor, makes fun of students all the time for not understanding his material. Got caught watching bad videos and has an instagram with shady things

Professors are supposed to encourage you but he makes comments making you feel like you're not worthy to be taught by him. I understand more from the recitation professors and TAs than him. Don't even bother showing up to lecture, you're not going to learn anything. + He got caught watching bad bad videos

Not gonna lie, might be THE WORST possible professor in the history of Purdue, How is West Laf gonna have 10 mcq every exam while we have 5 frqs with 4-8 parts each and our final was 10 questions with 4 parts per question? Tells us just because he can finish the exam in 10 mins so we should be able to do it in 60 like he wasn't the one who wrote it
Hardest FRQs I've ever seen with zero help. Save your GPA and take this with literally anyone else.
He makes the class harder just for the sake of it. No teaching purpose, he just likes seeing the class average drop.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This guy is on a power trip 24/7. Genuinely thinks he's a genius and we're all beneath him.
If you have a class with this guy, just pray to god he gets his ego in check. Got caught with porn too.
Literally the worst professor in the entire ECE department. When you take his exams just pray, the cheat sheets wont help with anything. Cooked me, might have to retake.
One of the worst professors, very mean and has a very big ego. Instead of helping us succeed he cooks us.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: If you have him as a professor just drop the class. One of the worst professors I have ever had.

When you take a class with a professor who cares for the success of their students, and who makes the class a space to enjoy learning, it becomes a safe space to learn. This guy could not be further from that. This guy hates seeing his students happy. His emails about cheat sheet restrictions look like trump tweets. #putthefriesinthebag.

Avoid if you can! He allows cheat sheets, but then restricts them that they become useless. He didn't set rules at the start, but as soon as he saw people putting helpful stuff, he banned content. He also makes the exams harder than West Laf (multi-part FRQs). It feels like he's intentionally trying to make the class harder very frustrating.

Honestly, the worst professor I've had at Purdue. I did well in the class, but Saha is unreasonably hostile, makes fun of struggling students who ask questions often by questioning their intelligence, and has a massive ego. If Saha is meant to represent ECE in Indianapolis, he needs to get in check. Also got caught with porn. Shady person overall.
If you're not religious, get one and start praying.
He does not properly communicate with the classroom when going through lecture material; he merely expects us to know how to do the content before ever being shown how to.

One of the best professors I have had, amazing lectures and in class notes. ECE 2K1 is notorious for being hard but if you watch Professor Saha's lectures and do the homeworks, this class should be a breeze. He can come across as passive aggressive however, I believe that's just the way he is with everyone. Besides this I would 100% recommend him.

If you could do this class without having it with saha do it! My class was a sub section of the 2k class because we weren't electrical base majors but me and motorsports and other engineering majors. Because of this he treated us poorly and with no respect. If it was aloud I could write an entire book of reasons not to have him as a professor.

Good. Cares about students, although sometimes blunt in some respects, this reality check is not a negative thing. His lectures cover everything on the exams and he makes sure to address common misconceptions.

I am a repeat student this summer. Prof. Saha's lectures made so much sense for my understanding. I am doing 10x better this summer. He was always very helpful during his office hours and takes our opinions into consideration when teaching. He may need some editing of his quizzes but he graded very fairly when any error was found.

This TA is extremely rude to students. On the online class form he berated students for asking for past exam solutions to be released. He lead the classwide exam review sessions, which due to his unpreparedness and poor organizational skills went 2-3 hours past the review time. Recitation had the same issues and led to us missing out on content.

This professor is extremely rude and unprofessional, especially toward his students. He also goes out of his way to insult students online in Piazza, gives unhelpful and disrespectful feedback, and has one of the worst egos in the ECE department.
He is absolutely full of himself. He is rude and does not respect student. He has a massive ego that needs to get in check.

Disrespectful and full of himself. Every response he has on piazza is either passive aggressive or straight up rude. Never takes a moment to see things from the other students perspective and always assumes they're in the wrong, tries to force his own study method that works for him onto everyone like it's a one size fits all type of thing.

He responds very disrespectfully to nearly all questions asked, often belittling the student asking and ridiculing them. He knows the content very well and can teach it well, but can not be relied on for any help.

Tridib does alright as a GTA and explaining material, but lacks when it comes to handling other matters, such as final grades. While he does not have much control over the matter, he could approach it by explaining results rather than gloating about his own success.

Tridib is probably the best GTA for 2k1 recitations. He has been teaching for quite a few semesters, so he knows what is important in the class and common mistakes. Also, he is very personable and easy going, and always goes out of his way to help students succeed.

Saha is a caring professor who really wants to see his students succeed. He offers hundreds of practice problems, daily office hours, and lots of extra credit. He is always asking for student feedback and really takes everything into consideration. This class is very difficult but Saha provided the tools needed to excel. Would highly recommend.
Often sets arbitrarily difficult exam questions and a absurd amount of work for what should be a introductory class.

Exams harder than they should be. if you were asked to attend office hours for credit make sure you keep track of the TA's names you attended with. Otherwise, you may lose a point or so and according to some policy that we were only told few days before the final, you either get full credit on that or a zero. Not fun...

Tridib teaches this class as you should already know the material. Even then, his examples are extremely basic and he doesn't explain some things at all. This will leave you not understanding the homework or exams. Essentially he doesn't prepare you from the ground up. If you're coming into this class, you should have studied a lot in advance.
Great dude, teaches very well and often asks for feedback.

Professor Saha knows what he is talking about and explains the content really well. The class in general is tough but the way he explains the material made things more simple and is also willing to set up times outside of class to meet with you and discuss any questions you might have.

He is very accessible outside of the class. Always willing to help students who need them. He is also one of the best lecturer Ive ever had in Purdue. Even though the class is hard, he is very knowledgeable at the class material and open to students feedbacks.

Professor Saha was very knowledgeable with regards to the material he was teaching and taught it well. Although he was very accessible outside of class, if you are not fully understanding the material he will come off as rude and condescending rather than helpful.

Professor Saha is very accessible and helpful outside of class. He is also very knowledgable about what he is teaching and seems to genuinely care about his students and their success in this course. The course is only 2 months long in the summer but professor Saha did well to deliver the relevant contents.

He taught a tough class in two months. He does lectures well, creates charts and notes that are easy to understand and helps during solving problems. He is always very helpful and meets students outside class.
Very unhelpful. When asked questions, answers with sarcasm. Good thing the class is easy.

Dr. Weng is by far the best professor I have ever had at Purdue. His lectures are incredibly engaging, and it is abundantly clear that he truly enjoys his work. I can tell that he is a professor not only focused on research, but also deeply committed to making a meaningful impact on undergraduate students. I would 100% take any class taught by him.

He's nice and funny, and he's a good teacher. However he's not great at making tests. I would go through all the study materials, and half the questions in the exams are phrased so awkwardly and unsimilar to study materials, which made them way harder. Plus, the average on the final was a a fifty and the curve was five percent

Calc 2 is a hard class but Shuyi is the goat. His teaching skill is on the level of the esteemed Chen from Chenflix - he manages to introduce all of the weird calc 2 topics in a way you can understand, which is not an easy task. Also his enthusiasm will make you actually want to show up to lecture even though he doesn't take attendance.

Homework due 3x a week. Calc 2 in general is hard at Purdue since exam grades are determined purely by multiple choice.  I watched every lecture but really your success depends on how much time you put into doing practice problems until you understand calc 2 itself.

All year he was a great professor, however the final was brutal and he only gave a 4% curve for Calc 2. I would say using previous years' final exams is not very helpful since he write his differently than other professors have in the past.
Shuyi's lectures are pretty good. Course relies a lot on tests with three midterms and a final and he doesn't make them easy. Review salons and quizzes for exams.
Don't know much about him, used Chenflix and did well in the class. Fall25 final was a joke tho, 54% class average with a SD of 12

DO NOT USE THE RMP SCORE TO JUDGE HOW HARD THE CLASS IS. This class is unreasonably hard, sometimes making me question if they want to teach the material or your reading skills. Lectures are pretty ok. Recitation barely helps, and quizzes suck. Past exams don't reflect difficulty of exams. Read lecture notes carefully and use hw to study. good luck

This class was the bane of my existence. So much homework, recitation preparation for quizzes, and obviously exams. The exams were extremely difficult and no amount of studying really helped me. Boilerexams helps to an extent.

Shuyi is a great professor. His lectures were amazing and very well organized. The recitations weren't the best and did not help me. The homework takes up a lot of time and there are three a week. Be sure to really study for the third midterm and especially the final.

Shuyi is great at teaching, but sometimes gives dismissive responses. The salons are not helpful, the quizzes are hard, and all tests and final are incredibly hard. The Math Resource Room is underrated, so please go and do not fall behind. There are 3 HW/week, and they took hours for me to finish.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Good professor, but recitations had difficult assignments with Salons and Quizzes that made up a significant portion of your grade. Everything in this class was difficult, HW was not super helpful. You are better off studying for exams with old exams or practice problems. Watching OGT and chenflix carried me for the exams.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: he has incredibly clear, well-organized and methodical lectures that i found wanting to attend despite not being mandatory. focused on the fundamental concepts which improved my understanding. super approachable and gives tons of help to get you through the course. tests are tough as they always are, they're doable if you practice well.

Shuyi is a phenomenal math professor by all standards. However, his approach to teaching Math is more derivation-oriented, so if you're someone who benefits more from practicing exam difficulty questions, Shuyi's classes may seem a tad drawn out. Personally, I love Shuyi's approach since it helps me understand the concepts from the ground up.

For context, I already had a 5 in Calc BC in hs.

For every unit besides series, I feel he taught it extremely well and concisely. He derives everything before giving you the formula to connect the pieces in your head. His notes are very organized, too.

His tests are extremely difficult and concept-heavy, so don't fall behind. Stay consistent!

Shuyi is a very tolerant professor and does his job very well. He does not hold back on exams. Know what you're getting into. Definitely go to lecture and GO TO HIS EXAM REVIEW SESSIONS. Lots of people skip them to review on their own time. He picks specific question types that will be on the exam.
Pretty good professor. Class was difficult but he helped make it better. Some topics aren't gone into enough in lecture due to time constraints.
Go to lectures or watch chenflix and you'll pass, but be careful on those exams, exam 3 was already bad but final came in like a nuke.

Unfortunately, his lectures were outright bad.
Oftentimes his lectures just turn confusing, and would often just make it harder to understand in general. The material itself is presented good, but his explanations did not do it for many of us.
(Watch out for the final exam!)

reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--So many papers--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The class was fair enough that if you kept up with assignments and recitations you would (most likely) pass even with subpar exam scores. He's a good professor, and his exams are usually difficult but not impossible. However, you should watch out for the final. He moves like Von when cooking up the final. Just slaughters the students & their grades
lectures are engaging and you can tell he cares! class is hard just because its a hard class, he doesnt make it harder than it needs to be.

Shuyi truly is very knowledgeable and is quite fun to listen to talk. He always does a lot of problems in class step by step, which is super helpful in understanding. When it comes to sequences and series, I truly believe that no professor can teach that unit perfectly, so I still struggled quite a lot in that regard.
Shuyi was a great professor, his notes were extremely well organized and easy to follow. His lectures were engaging and worth going to. Overall an amazing professor.

Very funny and approachable. Lectures are very clear, but its entirely possible to complete the class by copying the notes later and practicing with the homework. I rarely went to lecture as his notes were written well enough for me to understand without him speaking. 10/10 math professor would take again.

Shuyi is a kind, accessible Calc 2 professor who posts lecture videos and notes. The class is homework-heavy (35 assignments, 3 dropped) with 3 midterms and a cumulative final. Lectures aren't mandatory, but recitations are and include weekly salons or quizzes that equally impact your grade.

Professor Weng was amazing, he has the course set up in an easy and accessible way. He posts his note outline ahead of the lecture, and he also posts his lectures online. Homework and exams are hard, as this is a hard class, but he seemed to be very caring of the students.

Shuyi is an incredibly nice and encouraging human being. That being said, Calc 2 is a hard course, and he doesn't always explain new topics in the best way. He often goes very in-depth with topics, but not always on things that need that much explanation. There is also quite a bit of prior Calc 1 knowledge needed to make sense of certain lectures.
Tough class, but he is a great teacher.
Really awsome professor! Still taking his courses
I love Shuyi.
Great atmosphere and he actually know mathhhh

I really liked Shuyi's easily accessible notes to follow along during and after lecture and his enthusiasm. Homework and quizzes were pretty standard and very difficult, but I especially struggled with the tests. The material on the tests was very difficult and often had a couple questions that did not reflect class topics, with no partial credit.

Probably the best math professor I've had at Purdue. His lectures and notes are clear as day. That said, (may not entirely be his fault), the lecture doesn't always match the depth or difficulty of the homework or exams. If you're willing to put in the time to THROUGHLY understand the homework and ask questions, you'll do well.
Such a great professor with a great personality.
Good lecturer, but came off a bit judgmental when answering questions.  The first exam was difficult but lightened up a lot after that.  Pretty standard IMO
I wish he gave partial credit on tests, but he was a great lecturer, and he records all his lectures. Recitations were helpful

Calc 2 is a challenging course, but Professor Weng makes the material accessible. The class isn't easy by any means, and his exams are quite tough, but he's a professor genuinely concerned with how students learn, where they struggle, and how they can succeed.
The exams are a lot harder than the homework. He tends to go very fast on the complex subjects and slow on the easy stuff. He provides scaffold notes which is great, but the mic didn't always work.

Shuyi is an excellent professor for Calc 2. Admittedly some of the concepts themselves were hard to understand, so for people struggling I would recommend watching Chenflix and then attending Shuyi's lecture as his notes and lectures are far more comprehensive. He's also extremely accessible and helpful during office hours.

Shuyi was a great professor for Calc 2. He explained everything very well, he was very caring, and made sure we did well in the class. If you have a chance to take a class with him, I highly recommend you do. 10/10 professor.

I thought his lectures were easy to follow, and his notes were beautiful, well-organized, and color-coded. He rearranges the order of material, so use boiler exams by topic for midterms rather than by exams. He's kind of sassy in answering questions, so I would lean more on your TAs for that. Class is curved, you'll pass if you do the work.

Shuyi was good at teaching, however I recommend watching a concept video before the class to better understand. For tests, please use your past quizzes and boilerexams-- it'll save you. I have never had a class that required so much work and time to fully understand concepts. He is a bit sassy when you ask questions so don't take it to heart.

Shuyi was my MA 162 professor and he was very helpful. His lectures were very informative so I would recommend you go to as many lectures as you can. He gives outlines for his notes before class and they would help keep you on track as he went through the lecture. I would 100% take his class again if I could.

The curve was generous, but this class was brutal. Afternoon lecture is the worst since he would never finish the material and say to check morning lecture recordings. When responding to questions, he is very blunt. He admitted to making exams too hard and that he was learning how to write better exams. Chenflix is the way to go for this class.
Shuyi is such a goated professor, absolute wizard of curves. Calc II was kind of tough but he explained the concepts pretty well.
Did not answer questions very well. Made exams very difficult and even admitted it himself and didn't change anything about it.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment:
Prof. Shuyi was very well organized. He posts his neat notes for each lecture & reminds students to keep them on track. 

The lecture pacing can feel a bit slow but his explanations were useful to understand the material.
Quite a chill and approachable guy overall. 

Calc 2 material is challenging but he made it simple to absorb.
Do not recommend.

Clear lecturer, easy to follow, and makes notes easily accessible to students. Doesn't answer many questions in class due to the short lecture times, but is very helpful during office hours. Exams are quite difficult, but lots of resources are provided. You'll be expected to know calc 1 thoroughly, so brush up on skills before taking this class.

Great lectures! Shuyi goes through the fundamentals of each topic to help you fully understand and grasp the big idea of the topics learned in class so that you can apply them to the homework.

Notes are also very clean and easy to follow along with.

By far the worst professor Ive had in all my days. the material taught in lecture does not resemble that which is on exams, quizzes, or homeworks. Nor does he provide any materials to aid in being successful in the course. would not recommend ever taking his class.
He is a great professor! He knows how to simplify complex topics and his notes are well organised and easy to study..
He is a great professor, He makes complex topics very easy to understand. His notes are also very illustrative, organised and easy to study.
For such a challenging class, Shuyi helps make the concepts easier to understand. He's a nice guy, who makes time for his students and enjoys answering questions.

Calc 2 is undoubtedly difficult, but Shuyi definitely makes concepts clear and approachable, answering all questions at their appropriate depth whenever they arise. He's a pretty jolly fellow and a nice person, making the content and lectures easier.
Shuyi is a great lecturer very clear and understandable in his explanations.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: This man is an absolute G. Very approachable, and he is really good at teaching. Dude is way too overqualified for this job. Calc 2 is just a hard class here, not the fault of Shuyi.

He is a great lecturer and manages to get the main ideas across in his lectures. However, he is not so great at writing tests, even with some of the quizzes the TA was struggling to solve them. He could definitely tone down the difficulty of the tests and make it more similar to the content learned in lectures.
Best professor in the math department in my opinion.
Very good lecturer. Does a great job of simplifying complex topics and providing real world applications for the math you are learning.

Weng was a great professor! He explained the concepts of Calculus well, and was very helpful and good at answering questions. He's also just a nice guy in general. Taking Calc 2 with him made a difficult class a lot easier.
Took his class for MA162. Did not get through material that we would have homework or quizzes in the hour given. Just watched chenflix after the first three lectures and did fine.

Dr. Weng is amazing.  I had him for calc 2 and even though it is the hardest calc offered, he made it so much better.  He is very easy to understand as long as you pay attention.  He was so funny and cancelled lectures on occasion.

He's an amazing professor! He comes across as someone who really cares about how students are doing in his class. Additionally, he's extremely patient! I went to his office hours and was struggling with a couple of questions, and he was really understanding and made the concepts pretty clear.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Good lecturer, answers questions thoroughly when asked, seems to genuinely care about student success in class.

Professor Weng is amazing. Anyone can understand Calculus with him. He explains even the pieces connecting to Pre-Calculus, talks at a good pace(not too fast, not too slow). His exam reviews are also very helpful and usually have the exact type of problems on the exams. He is also very fun, caring, and great to talk and ask questions to.
Extremely unprepared every class. Students who try to go to his lectures to learn will be out of luck.

Sometimes goes too fast during lecture, but overall he's not a bad teacher considering how difficult the class is. He's also a very nice person.

(Talks kind of like Dr. Doofenshmirtz)
Don't be fooled by how sweet and kind he comes off. Overall, will not be a good experience because he just doesn't teach well. You are in for a tough semester.
Tests are extremely difficult. Lectures are also unbearably boring and do not help at all in understanding material.

I had asked around and the averages for our section was ~20% lower than all other sections. This points to an underlying issue with the professor and it shows in how disengaged he is with students in his lectures. Very poor instruction overall and the worst teacher I have come across
Best advice I can give incoming students: Take with mummert if you can.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Absolute waste of my semester going to his lectures. All he does is write in silence and keep his head glued to his notes. Nothing else! No point in trying to go to his class, super unhelpful professor. Very bad look on Purdue hiring this guy... DO NOT TAKE AT ALL COSTS!!!

Really disappointed that this is the standard for Purdue Math Professors. I've had bad math teachers in high school, but I never realized that university could have even worse. I very much liked my other professors for chemistry and ENGR. Hopefully Purdue takes this into account.

I really do not think this professor is a bad guy at all. Genuinely seems very nice. Unfortunately he does not prepare any of us well for the exams. His communication skills are very hard to follow, he clearly does not put any planning into his lectures, and it just looks like he's in another world.
Big GPA Ruiner. Had to work really hard to just get a minimum mark for passing. Never wanna see him again.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Teaching style is very bad and clearly disregards the fact that people are suffering because of his poor lectures.
Should be fired. Actually makes me mad how bad of a teacher he was.
I don't know a single one of my friends who actually found his class useful. Now I will have to retake the class. I'm going to self study this time around because no way will I be going back.
So happy that I passed. My gpa standards lowered because of him.
I already retook the class so I ended up doing well this time around, but the professor was way worse than my previous one last semester.
Ended up learning more from the mylabmath examples than this guy. I suggest doing that instead.
The transition from Calc 1 and 2 was already hard enough. This guy made it impossible to learn anything.
Lectures = him quietly copying notes super fast while mumbling. Zero eye contact, zero interaction. Questions? Good luck. Self-teach from textbook if you take him.
Coming from other profs, he was a shock. Just scripts notes on iPad without looking up or elaborating.

This guy is a walking disaster for anyone trying to pass Calc 3. Head buried in his notes the entire time, writing furiously without a single explanation beyond a barely audible mutter. Questions? Forget it, he won't even glance up. Lectures are just him copying stuff super fast with zero context.
I genuinely learned more Calc 3 from YouTube comments than from these lectures.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Imagine watching someone solve a puzzle while refusing to explain the rules. That is this lecture. Head down, mumbling, no engagement. By week four like 90% the room had stopped coming. ChenFlix carried this class harder than anything else.

I kept thinking, “maybe today's lecture will be better.” It never was. He writes extremely fast with messy handwriting and mumbles the entire time. Examples skip importnat steps that magically reappear on exams. I survived solely through external resources. Would not take again.
I did well in MA 261, but NOT because of him. If you are in this class you either go to a different lecturer or just watch ChenFlix. DON'T WATSE YOUR TIME!!!!
Very nice man, very bad teacher.

Prof Yassemi is a good person. But he does not know how to teach MA261. He spends the entire lecture rewriting hard-to-read notes and textbook definitions from pre-written notes that he has on the side; he speeds through the math examples and skips a lot of steps, and the examples are rarely helpful in recitations or exams. SWITCH LECTURES ASAP

Yassemi is one of those guys who's really smart but can't teach to save his life. His handwriting looks like a foreign language, speeds through the slides, and doesn't look up while he quietly mumbles to himself, making some people raise their hands for 10 minutes before he notices. For a class as notoriously difficult as Calc 3, take someone else.

Dr. Yassemi: the Gordan Ramsay of nothing burger lectures. He seems nice, but his lectures were beyond unhelpful. Mostly, he just read definitions from the textbook and sped through examples with little to no explanation, all while mumbling and writing very messily. If you can take MA261 with another prof, do so: your GPA will thank you.

He literally just keeps his head down, doesn't say anything, and writes notes extremely fast the whole lecture. Zero explanations and clearly doesn't care.  Amazed how he hasn't been fired yet. Also doesn't bother to answer questions when students raise their hands because he's looking down all the time.

All he does is copy lecture notes on an ipad and quietly mumbles what he is writing without looking up at all. This extremely hard to learn from especially compared to Matsuki's teaching style in Calc 1 and 2, which was far more engaging. He also does not offer any study guides for exams.
Had this professor first semester. He is very personable and interesting to talk to in office hours

He has conducted wonderful lectures, but he also made some mistakes in the lectures. The grading of homework is fair. However, the setup of the exams is not persuasive since we had only multiple choices for final without partial credits. For a course of mathematics, this isn't representing the mathematical capability of students well.
Very good lectures
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Really good at explaining concepts, made the class easy to understand and follow. Low ratings seem so unfair because he definitely made the class quite easy to learn and get a good grade

As others have said, he is an amazing lecturer who explains concepts pretty well. The tests were quite brutal, but he later curved the class after significant, maybe even excessive pushback. All in all, a very excellent educator and very highly recognized also

I'm writing this review for SUM'24, Prof Yassemi has been my hands down MOST favorite Math Teacher ever, nevermind that I got a C-, I had an unfortunate circumstance and ultimately I'm no math genius but he is AND is an amazing teacher to boot! Every single concept in Linear Algebra he walked through was demonstrated clearly and with great care.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: He gave C as an average in a Grad Course!!! Half of the class will have to retake it. He set the final exam as a total multiple-choice exam with no partial marking. He didn't curve at all. Review classes were useless as the final exam had so little from it and was way more complicated.  The whole course was highly mismanaged. Avoid this professor.

I never had any professor as passionate and as caring about subject that they taught as much as Prof. Yassemi. I'm not really good at math but the way he explained things were very clear and very approachable. He's also very kind and very understanding. I wish all the math classes can be taught by him. Thank you so much for an awesome summer class.

The tests were a mess. The final was all multiple choice. In a Linear Algebra the smallest mistake will ruin your whole answer. The exam opened for 24hr. Questions were not randomized -> some students potentially cheated by giving their peers the answer key. The class average is C in a grad-level class, which means half of the class will repeat it.

Prof. Yassemi is a wonderful professor. His lectures are such an amazing joy to attend. He is also very, understanding, patient and kind. The homework is very fair and reasonable. The tests however, can be very time-constrained, hence the averages were quite low, I'm assuming he will curve the class though. Otherwise it was an excellent class.

Rating is for MA511 Summer Semester - Prof Yassemi is awesome. Super clear lectures, great quality videos, very easy to follow in terms of class format. HW is reasonable and follows course content and exams. If you put in the time for this class, you will definitely succeed. Prof also is very active on Piazza and answers all student questions.
Best math professor I have ever had in my life. Very caring about students and amazing lecturer.
Not a good person

Just go to class!! Attendance is very important but it definitely pays off bc she makes it very clear what you need to know for the exams. If you pay attention and put the time into studying with the resources she provides, you'll be perfectly fine!

This class was pretty easy but not very lax on any missing attendance. Attendance is required and there are NO drops or points that you can earn back for missing class. Zuelly is very funny, and I really enjoyed her class, but you really cannot miss much before your grade starts going down.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Fun and chill, but unorganized with unclear or last-minute group project requirements. But, she is an easy grader and lets the class be fun. Not the best for in depth learning but a laid back professor.

Dr. Zuelly is an amazing professor, and I highly recommend taking her classes. Attendance is mandatory, but if you don't have any absences, you are able to opt out of the final exam! At the end of each lecture and lab there is a mini quiz, and if you paid attention during class, you will be fine!

Professor Zuelly's class had clear expectations and was not difficult. It was nice to not have final exams with 100% attendance throughout the semester as well. I was not a fan of her as a person. She has a lot of grace for herself and her own mistakes (which happen a lot) but is very strict on her students. Class is easy, but she's not kind.
attendance mandatory

You don't have to attend class, but she does an iClicker for attendance as a grade. If you have perfect attendance you don't have to take the final. Exams are heavy on attendance quiz questions and lectures.
Super respectful of your beliefs/morals. Lectures were great and she explained everything very clearly.

Absolutely amazing! She explains concepts very well and breaks it down so even if you aren't good at ochem or bio, you will understand what she is teaching. Very chill and if you do all of the hw you get a chance to waive the final.

I loved having Dr. Zuelly in ANSC 255. She outlines her expectation of the class in the very first lecture, so there are no surprises in the class. Make sure to take good notes over the lectures for exams. Labs are interesting and relevant to the lecture material we cover in class. Overall, an amazing professor.

I highly recommend taking a course with Dr. Zuelly, the material is never too difficult and is very easy to understand if you pay attention to the lecture quizzes and lecture material. The course is made up of online discussions, lecture quizzes, group lab assignments, and individual quizzes. The exams are easy but you must study for them.

Such a fun class - the labs are the best part! I learned a lot and Dr. Zuelly is a wonderful professor. If you had perfect attendance you didn't have to take the final at the end of the semester, which is a plus.

Dr Zuelly is an amazing professor! she does a great job lecturing, and posts recordings of her lectures so you could go back and rewatch the lecture if needed. She also has great labs and we were doing something different every week. My only negative is her grading scale, Her grading scale is changed so a C+ is an 83.9%
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Difficult class with lots of information for one semester. Dr. Zuelly is an alright professor but can easily be annoyed by small things. She will get upset if her attendance rates drop. Very strict phone rules, lose 25 points if she sees you on a phone. Labs are interesting, make sure to have old clothing you don't mind getting a little blood on.
Dr. Zuelley's class is tough. She gives lectures and lab quizzes so be prepared to know a lot of material. In-person lectures aren't required since she records everything.

I loved having Dr. Zuelly in ANSC 255! She's such a good teacher and is very thorough in explaining. The exams will cover everything she teaches per section, but as long as you take the notes and study (quizlet is the best for this) then you will do just fine. If you have any confusion or questions, she will definitely answer them well!

I'm so glad I took this class with Dr. Zuelly! She is very knowledgeable and fun to be around. You will learn a lot and feel confident about the skills you learn. Exams aren't hard if you study, and she has EC on them. There are quizzes every lecture and lab, but they are easy. The final is optional if you have attended every class and lab.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: He is one of the best instructors I have had at Purdue ECE - great lectures and useful homeworks, exams are tough but graded generously.
Focus on the exams and keep up with him in lecture and you will be fine

Goes quick in lecture does an okay job explaining concepts. 302 is just not horribly interesting. Nice guy but beware of his curve the actual % you earn in the class is irrelevant. It is graded on a normal curve completely relative to your classmates.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Lectures were some of the best I have had in ECE, exams were on the harder side. He clearly cares about the students' learning and explains complicated concepts very well.

Good lecturer. Uploads notes containing the weeks info rather than slides, which could be a good or bad thing (not a lot of useful examples in those notes). 302 is a hard class, but I've been pretty happy with Tripathi. He doesn't require attendance, but the class is constantly building on itself, so it's best to go.
Nothing special; kind of boring lectures and has an attendance grade. Claims readings are required but def not. Pretty easy exams and easy course. Hw harder than exams.
Very knowledgeable and explains concepts clearly.
Overall, the class served as a huge benefit to my education as well as my work ethic. Tara clearly cares about her students' comprehension and opinions, so feel free to speak up.
Interesting lectures, cancels class pretty often, and grading is pretty chill.

Maienbrook is a great in two ways---
1. Structured: makes it clear what you need to do by when, what to expect during exams, and slides are pretty much all you need to know (I would suggest reading textbook to master understanding/if you're struggling).
2. Fun: actually makes accounting sound interesting, lectures are engaging, and so on.
She really explains the topics very well in class and is overall an amazing teacher and gives extra credit on all exams as well.

Professor Maienbrook is AMAZING, she gave so much extra credit bumpimg my 90% final exam to above a 100%. She knew so much about accounting but broke it down into simple terms and was very understanding. Also the exams are multiple choice making them very doable.

Maienbrook is clearly knowledgeable about what she teaches. The class is well-structured, and there are plenty of extra-credit opportunities. She is also very approachable and offers flexible office hours. If you show up to class and actually do the homework, you're bound for success.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Maienbrook was the most competent professor I had this semester. Her course is well-organized, and her exams are straightforward. Watch her highlight videos for a good pre-class gloss. There are only 2 weekly assignments - both of which prepare you adequately for the exams. She genuinely cares. S/O to her TAs this semester (Nathan and Joseph) too!

Best MGMT200 professor. She is incredibly knowledgable in her field and her lectures make that obvious. Conveys the information of the class very clearly, keeps lectures engaging, and is always open to taking questions. Provides opportunities for easy extra points, just show up to class. Exams are incredibly fair, final is not cumulative.

Amazing professor, teaches well and is very knowledgable about the subject. Has worked out a great system for lectures, homework, testing, and participation. The course feels very well organized and the content isn't hard to learn as long as you do the homework.

She is a great professor and her class, though test heavy, is fairly easy. Her lectures are engaging, and lowest homework & review are dropped. There are three tests that make up 60% of the grade in total (15, 20, 25) but the last is the final exam, studying should get you at least a B on each. Extra credit can be obtained and applied to each test.

She is the goat. She explains topics very well and helps 1 on 1 very well too, whether after class or office hours. She is very good at explaining topics and if you are confused she answers questions and breaks it down well.

Amazing professor who really cares about the students. Class is somewhat difficult, especially if you fall behind, so be sure to stay on top of everything. Pay attention in class and make sure you actually know how to do the homework, and you'll do pretty okay.
Maienbrook is an amazing professor. She makes a somewhat difficult course way more approachable. Great professor!!!

Terra was an amazing lecturer and would prepare us well for exams. I left the semester with a very good understanding of basic accounting principles as well as how they relate to more complex topics. She is super approachable and definitely made time for students if they were struggling. I miss having her as a professor!!!
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: She was overall a great prof! Very approachable. MGMT200 is also quite easy content-wise, but she is very adjustable. A decent amount of credit is given for attendance, so please make sure to show up to class.

Maienbrook is a great accounting professor and seems very knowledgeable in her field. She is super approachable and nice, and her lectures are never too boring. There is tons of extra credit in this class, so just showing up will give you an easy A.
In class lectures are good and get out early! IClicker questions very similar to exam. She's a wonderful person as well. Lots of extra credit opportunities.
Incredible, incredible, incredible , cares about her students so much and is the sweetest person you'll ever meet. Do everything you can to take her class
Very easy class if you read her lecture slides and go to class. She's very good at explaining things as well

she was a pretty mid prof tbh. seemed nice enough, read powerpoint slides, but was helpful to like go over basically exactly what was on the homework. extremely structured class, 2 online homeworks a week, iclicker points for attendance. i didnt rly go to class and it was pretty ok. boring af tho. midterm 2 is randomly hard so be ready.

Well-organized lectures, attendance is mandatory in the sense that it is iClicker and you answer questions. Felt that all assignments assisted in my learning and understanding. Overall, super nice, cares a lot, and wants to see you succeed.

Terra clearly cares for her students, and it shows with the absolutely insane amount of curving and extra credit she gives. She uses McGraw so those can take a long time. Definitely recommend making friends with people in class to figure out the homework. Exams are hard, but the curve has saved me.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Very good overall, but exams are confusing and/or include stuff we never learned.
Great professor she really cares about her students doing well.

Make sure to show up, she takes attendance with iClicker. Even if you get question wrong you get full pts for attendance. She doesn't excuse any hw without dean of students excuse, so try to do all hw it's pretty easy just look at the piazza
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: Makes accounting enjoyable and understandable, a very cool and nice person. She cares a lot to make her course better each year. Exams are like the content.
Easy course, homework can be completed as many times as you want until due date. The tests are easy and each bumped to a B average. There is extra credit for course ratings.

She's a nice professor. Definitely knows what she's talking about and tries to make lectures fun and engaging. Accounting can be a difficult subject to follow, so she takes time to explain concepts well.

great professor! she brings in a lot of industry knowledge and uses real examples to relate the content. her lectures are informative, and attendance is taken with iClicker cloud. exams are very similar to iClicker questions and practice exams -- no surprises.

Good lecturer, has industry experience, friendly both in and out of class, and genuinely cares about her students. Exams do require studying, but it's not too bad as long as you do the homework and go to lectures.

Lectures are easy to follow and all you need to do well on homework and exams. Prof is super available and wants to help you do well. Attendance questions and practice exams are exactly like the exam questions.

Professor Maienbrook is very helpful and makes learning the hard content a little easier with her acronyms and examples. She is very flexible with her hours if you need help and gives plenty of extra credit for the exams.

Prof Maienbrook is a great professor. Her lecture's make accounting very easy to understand and apply. As a STEM major I found accounting to be very easy because her lecture's where fantastic in covering the difficult concepts and explaining things. Her tests are pretty easy just make sure to do the practice ones. Lots of extra credit for exams.

Overall, Professor Maienbrook is a phenomenal professor, and she does a very great job of explaining the course that she teaches in a digestible manner. Attendance is required (iClicker) but there is a lot of leeway regarding how much you can miss. The homeworks are pretty long and take a lot of time to complete, but it prepares you well for exams.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Attendance required. Homeworks are a little too much work but class is very well organized, she teaches very good, gives extra credit opportunities and curves the exams when needed. Asks what she says she's gonna ask in the exams. Loved this class.
She actually seems to care about how well the class is doing. She offers plenty of extra credit for every exam. Lectures can be a little boring but she often ends class early.

Homework takes a while to do so be ready for that. Study for the exams by self studying the slides AND the textbook, its a lot easier that paying attention in the lecture especially if you are in the 7:30 section. Attendance is mandatory but she gives about 3-5 days of absence with iClicker. Expect a curve on exam 2 and 3, not exam 1.

Good lecturer, does iClicker (online) every lecture but if you go to ~75% of lectures you'll get full points. Homeworks help a lot with the more technical material and lecture slides are posted online for studying concepts. Overall will work with you if you need help and gives lots of EC.

She is a very nice and understanding professor. The homeworks are pretty easy. The tests require you to understand the concepts, but they are multiple choice. She gives extra credit for attendance occasionally using hotseat questions.

Passionate about accounting and takes the time to make sure everyone understands the concepts. Lots of opportunities for additional help if needed. Guaranteed 100s on all assignments as long as you complete them. Exams are fairly easy as well. Be prepared to answer iclicker questions.
Tons of extra credit and big curves if you're not a great test taker, actually cares about your grade.

Super great, very good at explaining things. All lecture slides posted on Brightspace, homework is very cyclical and easy to keep track of. Lots of study opportunities and review for tests. Lots of extra credit and possible test curving.

She is a well qualified instructor and handles thousands of students taking MGMT200 quite well. It is respectable how she fits all of her materials in one class session. However if your major is not related to accounting, good luck, it is challenging.

Lots of extra credit- scored in the 70's on all three exams but ended course with a 90%. Very easy-to-read lecture slides and sample exams are identical to the real exams. Homework takes a while to solve, around 1-2 hours. Hard to get a perfect score without multiple submissions but professor is great. Attendance is 5% of grade.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: She's great! The material is super easy and you wouldn't even need to go to class if it wasn't mandatory because all the notes are posted. She does go through examples in class, though, which is helpful.

She is one of my favorite professors I have had at Purdue! I averaged around low 60's on the exams but with the amount of extra credit she gives on each exam I was able to finish with a pretty solid B in the class! Her office hours are also great if you need extra help!

Professor Maienbrook is a very caring and respectful professor, she accepts questions after class ends and tries her best to make accounting as simple as possible. The tests are fairly difficult if you don't do the read the textbook and don't study right.

Great lecturer, she does her best to show real-life examples and does a good job making content easy to understand. Offers extra credit, study guides, and overall a really good simple class experience.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Terra is a great teacher. Utilizes HotSeat (typically once per week) so missing classes is not the end of the world. Homework is straightforward to get 100s on if you put in the work. She often gives extra credit during review weeks so make sure to show up for those.  Would take a class with her again in a heartbeat.
Do all the homework and you'll get an A
I really enjoyed this class! I knew nothing about accounting going in, and I had no issues with the assignments and concepts. She makes things very easy to understand and is very willing to help.

While the content can be a bit dull at times, Professor Maienbrook kept thing as interesting as possible. She was a fun person who answered everyone's questions to the best of her ability and the class was fun.

Maienbrook is a great lecturer and covers all the necessary material on easy-to-understand slides. She answers all questions in a respectful and thoughtful way. I felt that the exams were an accurate reflection of the content we covered in class and in the assignments.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: The content was not very interesting to me personally as an engineering major.

Professor Maienbrook was a great teacher to have for my very first accounting class here at Purdue! As long as you stay on track with completing the reviews and homeworks, and study for the exams, you will be good to go. Maienbrook can sometimes be on the boring side, just reading off of slides, but she is available for extra help.

Maienbrook read from slides which I found dull, but the content was interesting. The homework goes into way more detail than the exams ever do, but it makes the exams much easier. She also gives generous EC for the exams, I did the bare minimum for this class and easily got an A.

Nicest professor. Lectures are straightforward and easy to follow. The homework can be quite hard and annoying but using piazza and office hours will help a lot. Class can be somewhat difficult to students new to accounting like myself. Its just different rather than hard.

Terra is great. She makes accounting simple for everyone. Tests are fair, homework is a little long but never awful (piazza helped me a lot). She is a super nice person and is always willing to help. Usually does hotseat on Friday. She is very generous with extra credit on exams so go to the in class review. Overall highly recommend.

Maienbrook is one of my favorite professors I've had. She offers extra credit and curves exams. Exams are weighted pretty heavily, but she provides resources like past exams, which are similar to the actual exams. The class is very structured, 1 assignment due every Wednesday and Sunday. Hotseat on Friday for participation. Definitely recommend!

Overall pretty good, her assignments are due at the same time every week. However she uses Hotseat randomly so you have to be in class all the time and if you get an 8:30 its going to be challenging. Tests are fairly easy if you study. The lectures are pretty much all you need to get through the class and the textbook is pretty much optional.

Professor Maienbrook cares about her students doing well and will make reasonable accommodations when it comes to exams. The hw and reviews were time consuming but have unlimited attempts so are basically guaranteed A's. The exams are like the exam review problems she links in Brightspace. She generously curves exams and has extra credit in class.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Professor Maienbrook is a good professor overall. The class itself isnt super interesting, she doesnt make it super fun. Gets through the material kind of quick. She uses hot seat every Friday mostly and sometimes other days for participation points. She also gives extra credit during review day before the exam which is very nice! Lots of busy hw
