Professor Maienbrook knows her stuff. She's able to break down the large topics into comprehensive pieces. Lots of outside resources for extra help. Only complaint about this course is that the lecture recordings are not uploaded in a timely fashion, they often are uploaded much later than 1-2 days after lecture, which is what I would expect.

She is an amazing professor. Homework's can be tough but get easier, plus she uses piazza. Exams are difficult and definitely need to be studied for. Usually does hot seat on friday's so those are really the only days you NEED to come, but still go. Overall the class is very well laid out and I never had to question when anything was due.
Very understanding and easy to understand. Accounting lectures aren't always the most exciting but the way she explained and taught stuff made it very easy to understand.

Fantastic class, Professor Mainebrook is a great lecturer. Explains concepts well and has no problem answering questions outside of class. This class structure is set up in a way that it is very easy to get a good grade in if you do the bare minimum amount of work and understanding of the material. The exams are also curved VERY well.
She's a good professor!

Had a 7:30 am lecture so I never went, which made the hot seat annoying AF. I did go to an afternoon lecture tho. The class wasn't hard and tests aren't that bad, I would recommend others pay more attention than I. She is a pretty alright prof tho, pretty accessible and is a good lecturer.

Professor Maienbrook has clear grading criteria. Students can easily earn full points on homework and smartbook reviews. There are a few extra credit opportunities for exams via Hotseat. However, most Hotseat Questions are for participation credit, which is worth 5% of your grade.

you don't need to go to class, but you do need credit for being there (there are online check ins that count for participation grade)

The homework are challenging but become easier with time and practice.

exams will be the difference from a B and an A if you put in minimal effort
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She is pretty fair, but sometimes she can run through things too fast. I also asked questions on piazza and never received a reply back. The reason why I chose test heavy is because, although we don't take exams a lot, the exams are weighed a lot more than the homework and review. Good luck!
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: All in all, chill teacher, but homework will take a long time and exams are based heavily on lecture content. If you slack the first 3 chapters, you won't understand the rest of the content because it's all tied in together. Attendance isn't mandatory, but will have HotSeat questions/points to make sure you are in class.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Professor Maienbrook is a fair instructor. She tends to give extra credit which is very nice. She also uses a platform called Piazza which you should take advantage of. It is very helpful. Personally, I just don't like accounting, but Professor Maienbrook is a good instructor who seems like she really cares about her students.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Accessible outside class--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Very easy course. Lectures mostly uninteresting but required by taking hotseat quizzes for points most on every friday. You can succeed solely by reading the highlighted parts in the book. Very kinda prof too.

Best part about this class is that if you read the textbook and do the homework, you can get an A+ without attending any of the lectures on Monday or Wednesday, just the one on Friday for the attendance credit. Plenty of opportunities of extra credit + a pretty generous curve on all exams (at least for my semester)!
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Clear grading criteria--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Very good lecturer. Offers extra credit for the exams in the class. Uses hotseat which is helpful to study for each exam.

Maienbrook is an excellent instructor and has been teaching this material for a long time. She does an excellent job of formatting her class. You should give 100% on all homework as there are unlimited attempts, and the rest of the class (60%) is 3 multiple choice exams. She also gives out a fair amount of extra credit before exams. Excellent class

Prof. Maienbrook is a solid instructor. The lectures could get a little boring, but if you show up to class, and do the homework this class is an easy A. The tests are very easy and to go along with this there are heavily weighted homework's that are free 100's.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I really enjoyed this class. Attendance is only mandatory for Hot Seat questions on Fridays. I would definitely be prepared to put some time into this class at least at the beginning of the semester. There are two homework assignments due each week(one on Wednesdays and one on Sundays). They're time consuming but rewarding. Piazza saved me.
She cares about the subject! I believe she did a great job, even with a lecture setting. Just be sure to catch up on what you don't understand in the textbook.

Honestly a great professor. Prof Maienbrook is a lovely professor and she seems to genuinely care about her students. There are 2 assignments a week which weigh heavily on your grade, but it is very easy to get 100% on these assignments. This means its okay if you do poorly on tests, you will still do well in the class! 100% would recommend.

I like her homework setup but is not very accommodating to her students. I worked while taking her class and was scheduled a work shift on a night we had an exam. I reached out to her a week before the exam and she denied me another test time. I find this extremely unaccommodating considering a lot of students have to work while attending school.
Terra was a great professor that had clear-cut lectures and was forward about what was expected in her course. She was available and ready for assistance when needed. Great choice.

Prof. Maienbrook is a really solid lecturer. I will say, accounting in general is hard to understand. Theres so many ins and outs, its all very complicated. She explains it in a way that is more simplistic, and a lot can be said about her lectures. Overall, she is very accessible and answers many questions through Piazza.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Clear grading criteria--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Prof. Maienbrook is a kind, well-organized instructor who gave clear lectures and is always accessible outside of class. This class is achievable, but it required me to work outside of class to complete weekly reviews and homework assignments. The exams are not cumulative, but are still pretty detailed on the chapters that they cover.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Test heavy--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Amazing accounting class! Professor Maienbrook gives good lectures and is approachable outside of class. We use McGraw hill for all of our assignments, and its very easy to navigate. Homework consists of reviews, and appliance questions.
An awesome professor but it is accounting, therefore, it is not fun at all and is rather confusing at times. Lots of homework but isn't to hard, tests are where they get you.

Mrs. Maienbrook is amazing. She teaches her class really well and although the homework can sometimes seem like too much, it helps you prepare for your exams. Exams are weighted heavily, so do not screw up on exams. She does have a sense of humor sometimes which I love. She knows what she is talking about and is always open to questions.

She knows what she's talking about and teaches it clearly. There is a lot of homework, but it can be achieved in a few hours. She's an accountant, so she understands the material outside of the book. Always answered questions in and out of class and tried her best to help us understand topics that non-MGMT majors felt welcomed
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Clear grading criteria--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Terra is knowledgeable in what she teaches. She is an accountant after all! However, she doesn't make her own slides, understandably once you see the content, but it's quite a lot. So much information packed into each slide. Hard to keep up as it has to be "go go go." The whole class was based on debits and credits. KNOW IT, OR YOU'LL GET AN F!

Prof Maienbrook is a great professor. Her syllabus and online content is very clear. She gives extra credit for tests AND curves all tests if the average is below 75. Homework is easy as long as you take the time to learn the material. Very nice to students and responds to emails/piazza very quickly.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: A lot of students complain about how long HW takes but you can very easily speed through the smartbook assignments and submit blank HW's to get answers the first time - I usually spent an hour doing work outside class per week when other students spent 3-5. Stupid easy class, but Maienbrook is still helpful if needed. Nice engineering elective.

Love Professor Mainebrook! She explains things really clearly in her lectures so you dont really need to read the textbook. She is always available for help when I needed it. Also gave out extra credit for the exams which were super helpful. Easy to work with so take MGMT 200 with her if you can.

If you're not a Krannert student this class is ridiculously easy. The amount of work required to recieve a good grade is nowhere near that of engineering, math, and stats classes. Take this course if you want an easy A or are interested in accounting. If you're an acturial science major, you can pass this class without ever watching a lecture :)

The financial accounting you learn in this class is mostly just memorization. The homework really isn't hard, but can take some time (3-4 hours most weeks). She's fairly available on Piazza and definitely does her best to help students learn. Solid choice for 200.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: This class sucks, no questions asked. As a professor she does her best and is very nice to talk to. I think the work required for this class is a lot more than a 3 credit course. You have reading assignments due every wednesday and homework due on Sunday. This may seem nothing but as the semester picks up, this is a lot. Nice Prof, Terrible Class.

Took class as first sent sophomore and Professors first year at Purdue. Theres a bit of hwk that takes a long time to do but if you just submit the hwk and get the answers and then take it again itll cut down many hours for you. Shell normally do hotseats on a certain day. Tests are MC and shell give EC sometimes. Memorize smart books 4 tests.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Terrible monotone professor. I hated having to go to class to get clicker points. She just stands up front and reads the slides. So boring I lost interest after just a few class sessions. I tried to get help from her outside of class but she was "out of town". Not going to take any more accounting classes - avoid this prof at all costs !
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: very good lecturer. posts many resources, helps with homework, posts all powerpoint slides WITH notes (aka everything she says), so attendance wasn't necessary except for hotseat days (usually friday). Homeworks were pretty time consuming but doable. Easy class and straight forward exams.

Prof. Maienbrook is generally a very good professor. She makes the course content clear and answers questions very understandably. This course contains a lot of information, so don't simply trust the title "intro." But Maienbrook does make things well organized. She also gives somewhat generous curves for the exams and even cutoffs.

I thought Professor Maienbrook's class was pretty straightforward and reasonable. If you do the homework, you should be able to score highly on the exams. She also answers all questions and it is not hard to get help or feedback. MGMT 200's content can be hard, but I don't think the class could have been taught much better.

This class is supposed to be easy, this professor made us feel like we should be professionals in accounting before taking her class. There are hotseats every Friday, do not skip class. She does not provide any study guides for exams.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: This is Professor Maienbrook's first year at Purdue. Overall, she's a great professor, I felt I learned a lot in this class. Prepare to set aside a couple of hours for HW, they do take a while but are helpful when it comes to exam time. While attendance is not required, go to class! There is a lot of material that needs to be memorized and used.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: His homework are very hard and sometimes impossible to solve!

Condori hate is way overblown. The course structure is extremely forgiving with exam, quiz, HW drops, up to 5-6% extra credit too. Go to his office hours or the TA office hours for homework/quiz help. Exams were very fair and graded generously. Lectures aren't great, but just watch Jeff Hanson on the side and you'll be set for an A. Statics is fun!
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Really not much else to say as the other comments sum up the professor very well. He told the entire class day one that he was in trouble with the university due to performance from previous semesters. Out of 3 exams 90% of the class was not able to finish any of them in the allotted time. Exam averages were so bad they will not tell the class.

Lectures are behind by a week. We are learning material right now which was literally on the exam study guide. Constantly lost in class. Can't tell when he's teaching material or asking a question.  Doing examples does not equal teaching. Why is that why we answer it like that? So many questions.

I have had him for three classes, the content is hard but he is good at lecturing. A lot of people don't like him and I didn't used to either. However, his exams are incredibly fair and I've had no issues with wondering if I will pass or not. There are way worse professors in the program and he does not deserve all the hate.

I didn't think he was a bad lecturer but the course is not well organized. Felt like I was gonna fail the whole time and then get huge bumps at the end. Difficult class imo and hw is exhausting, expects high quality work. high time requirement and exam prep is miserable. ~8 hours per week on hw

Prof. Condori's classes are by and large quite disorganized and his lectures are somewhat lacking. He is hard to reach outside of class and changes the course schedule and grading policies last minute. He provides a lot of homework and quiz drops, but the classes are generally not enjoyable.

He gets lots of bad reviews because the content of the class is difficult for many people. He clearly cares about what he is teaching and does lots of examples in class that I found make homework easier. I found him approachable after classes. Handwriting can be sloppy at times. Exams had some questions that were way too hard.
Too heavy homeworks

Honestly many people told me when I got to upper CE courses that I would miss Condori. I realized this is because he gives a lot of extra credit and lets students use cheat sheets for the exams. He is genuinely a decent professor when you see what else CE has in store. For this class I'd recommend reverse image search and Jeff Hanson. Good luck!

I have had this guy 3 times in a row now, CE 270, 298, and now 371. Worst lecturer I've ever had. Does not give useful examples that are not comparable to homework questions. Can hardly read the class notes which aren't even useful anyway. Gives quizzes that are nothing like the homework. Extremely unreliable and hard to reach when you need him.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Super nit picky and had unclear grading criteria on homework and exams. Cared too much about neatness/presentation. Expected students to dedicate lots of time- even suggesting that homework should be solved seperately and then rewritten entirely to have cleaner presentation. The lectures were ok but I mainly went for the attendance extra credit.

This professor is potentially the worst professor I have ever had at my years at Purdue. His lectures make no sense and often go off on tangents about advanced theory that you will never need, not be tested on. The exams and homework and unlike any problems from the lectures or each other, so good luck trying to study. Worst Prof experience in CE

Professor Condori's classes are theory-focused and challenging, but homework is supportive with points for attempts (Part 1) and corrections (Part 2). Exams are difficult, though often curved or adjusted late in the semester. He's deeply committed to student success and approachable. You get the grade you earn in his class for sure.

He hasn't showed up for class in weeks. The entire last month of the course has been taught by TA's. We were told his email was "deactivated". Was he fired? Who knows. We should NOT have to take this final if that is the case. Awful experience in his class. All good reviews are fake.

This class was fairly difficult, but it isn't all that bad if you attend all of the lectures. There is a good amount of homework, but it really helps you understand and apply the content. Overall, I think he does a great job teaching the content and helping you understand. He is also helpful during his office hours.
Very traditionalist professor, has no mercy for you if you aren't sacrificing your entire life to learn from him.

He is not a good lecturer. You are better off showing up solely for the attendance extra credit points. His explanations are subpar at best and is not able to answer student questions when asked. He spends far too much time on theory and not enough on actually solving the problems or teaching the steps that should be taken. Positive ratings are cap

For statics I felt like he was a good lecturer and made sure to connect concepts we were learning in class to civil applications. Exams test your fundamental understanding of concepts and are easier than homework and quizzes. Homework is designed to ensure understanding the mistakes made. It's a good basis for civil engineering fundamentals.
he is an awful lecturer; you are better off using the internet compared to how he teaches. There is also no clear criteria for how grading is done, and it is entirely bipolar based on the TA's

Literally horrible, he should never teach statics ever again. :( 
RIP my gpa

Had to do a full grade appeal in order to get a C- because he wouldn't admit that grading mistakes were made on practically all of my labs. Took me over 5 months after the semester ended. Lecture attendance is for extra credit, exam 1 wasn't bad but as time went on the class just got more ridiculous. Labs could take hours on end, TAs saved me.
Exam 1 virtually completely unrelated to lecture material.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Condori was a very good lecturer. I enjoyed learning about things theoretically in his lectures but the class requires that you do work outside of class. Reading the textbook and asking questions is like 50% of the class because there's too many examples to go over in lecture. He was always available for office hours and i went every week

Lectures could've used more practice examples before delving into heavy theory. Office hour heavy class. Lots of study material from past exams and set up exam practice sessions. Overall, a good person intent on growing from mistakes but hasn't quite landed the execution. Curving due to giving too much extra credit was a very questionable decision.

One of if not the worst professor in the civil department. Does not know how to effectively lecture students on complex material. Homework problems do not mirror lecture, test problems do not mirror homework. Failed 35% of the class Fall 2023. Awful at his job

This dude is a few fries short of a Happy Meal. Spends entire lectures mumbling about theory when exams and homeworks do not reflect his lecture. Proceeded to curve the class DOWN without zero warning after failing 40% of the class the semester before. If you get stuck with him just go ahead and start going to church again, cause you'll need it.

He is undoubtedly the worst professor in civil. Second time taking this class with him again after he failed 35% last semester. He legit curved the class down because he thought he made it too easy this semester. Lectures are useless and mumbles on about theories instead of teaching how to work out a problem. Avoid this man.

My friend scored a 99.5 and got a A-. He himself sent us an email that 100%+ will be given an A and A+. Another friend scored 95.2 and still got a B+. All he does is mumble in his lectures and his TAs are left with the actual teaching bit.

3 lectures a week and they are just 50 minutes of him rambling about theory based stuff, and then the exams are a super specific problem you have never seen before, and his TAs are incredibly tough graders. Was at a 69.5% and he would not round up for me to pass. Just an amazing dude.

For my class specifically he gave an excess of extra credit but then implemented a reverse curve. This reverse curve caused me to drop from a B+ to a B-. I am just disappointed, there is certainly room for improvement. My TA was a lot better at explaining the concepts in office hours. If you are struggling I suggest attending those.

Grade shouldve been an A-. He gave us 6% extra credit, but for final grades he changed the grading scheme to be out of 106 instead of 100. This change was made without notice, my TA didnt even know about it. No mention of a curve all semester so everyone thought the class was uncurved. Finally emailed why and decision to curve down was last minute.

This man goes on a random tangent for 2 weeks about how you need to read multiple 500 page books in their entirety to pass his class and be an engineer, but if you think thats the worst oh brother. This man randomly decided to hit the most devious move since Bill Clinton and fleece every student by illegally curving DOWN two letter grades smh.

Awful class. failed 35% last fall and looks like now will be no different. Lectures are mumbled nonsense, and dropping lower exam is pointless when the averages are all in the 60s TOPS. 270 is arguably one of the hardest yet most important core classes yet its "taught" by a man I wouldn't trust to pet sit for an evening. CE dept should be ashamed!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Test heavy

reviews.comment: The worst part of his classes were his disjointed lectures. Uribe would consistently finish class in the middle of a lecture leaving the confusing chapter info incomplete until next class. Beyond this it was near impossible to apply Uribe's longwinded theoretical examples to actual problems encountered in the homework and on exams. good luck.

Worst professor I have ever had throughout my 4 years at school. Very hard to understand lectures and he doesnt even update his lecture notes properly on Brightspace so they are pointless to use when preparing for the exams. He changed his mind about the way he will grade exams several times. He is little to no help when asked to explain things.

Professor Condori speaks softly and mumbles a lot in lecture. He focuses on theory/deriving equations rather than doing examples and teaching how to solve problems. Exams are fair if you prepare properly and do the past exams. Highest Midterm Score replaced lowest midterm score. Weekly quizzes which are difficult, but you get unlimited attempts.

Professor Condori speaks very softly and mumbles a lot in lecture. He focuses a lot on theory and deriving equations rather than doing examples and teaching how to solve problems. Exams are very difficult and do not resemble practice exams. However lowest mid-term was dropped and you get extra credit for attending class.

Professor Uribe speaks very softly and focuses too much on the theory versus actual applications. Most of his class time is spent deriving equations, and when he does examples, he does not finish them or use numbers. Really nice guy. Not the best professor. Used highest exam score as both exams though, and gave extra credit as well.

This professor was AWFUL, lectures were hard to understand and the TA's did most of the teaching. It was his first time teaching, and it was painfully clear. Changed how he was going to grade the class multiple times and did not have one passing average between two classes and four midterms. Exams are nothing like past exams.

That Condori is a professor is an insult to Purdue's reputation as a "world class" university. Mumbles broken english through every class, very vague on expectations and exams are nothing like past exams. No offense to him but you'd honestly be better off if the TAs taught the class, they at least seem competent and in touch with students' needs.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: This man has no clue how to teach. Dynamics is a difficult enough course without having to contend with subpar instruction. Highly recommend attending office hours and the textbook is your best friend. However, the best alternative is to switch out of the class entirely, New students beware!

Mumbles during lectures, hard to stay attentive when so monotone. Practice exams given prior to exams are on completely different difficulty scales than actual exams. CE 270 TAs were more helpful than him. Would have hoped for a better experience this semester.

Painfully obvious its his first time teaching CE 298 & CE 270, mumbles through vague lectures loosely related to homework. Overcomplicates basics, TAs do all the teaching. Office hours a necessity, Structures and Dynamics are hard enough without this BS. Study from the book, lectures are a disorganized time-waster, don't know how he got this job.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Very nice professor, impeccable lecture notes. However (maybe because it's a grad class) he doesn't post lecture notes which forces into an attendance mandatory class. Exams are pretty tough. Otherwise, he is one of the better profs out there, definitely recommend him for other classes.
Very caring. For HWs, he basically put emphasis on computation, which is not very helpful for understanding.

Lectures are directly from lecture notes posted on Brightspace. Exams were mostly quite easy but with some tricky multiple-choice questions. HW is overwhelmingly easy but could be a bit more reflective of the exam content.

Not generous with partial credit, so small mistakes on exams can cost you big-time in this class. The final exam was worth 200/600 points in the class, and it was difficult. Homeworks were to be turned in in-person in paper form, which was inconvenient. Lectures were just him writing out definitions and theorems and proofs- not super helpful.

Very fair exams and reasonable amount of homework. His lecture style is easy to follow. MA35301 is the opposite of the "multiply matrices pointlessly" sort of linear algebra class. It's focused on proofs and actually understanding what's going on logically, which Professor Ulrich accomplishes well.

Amazing professor. Material is structured based on the standard text for this course but he makes his own revisions which are quite helpful (clearer proofs, more precise notation). Explains everything thoroughly in lecture and tries to actually develop intuition for the theorems. Exams consist of few questions but aren't unreasonably difficult.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: lecture notes are impeccable
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: One of the best Professors I've ever had. Very fair but also understanding & kind. He doesn't use a textbook for 503 & doesn't write everything he says on the board. Tip: Listen carefully to what he says and write it down if it isn't obvious to you. He is very helpful during office hours. 11/10
Incredibly consistent and competent. The material in this course is inherently difficult and complex, and the grading curve isn't particularly friendly, but he did a great job teaching.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Ulrich is a very precise, organised lecturer. This class was hard, and the homeworks were weekly and difficult, but I learnt a lot of useful pure algebra knowledge.  He's not an exciting lecturer, but is very helpful and friendly in office hours. You will definitely understand the material if you put the work in. Great professor!
He explains all problems in-depth and is a great recitation professor. He does make time to help you after class if you have questions.

Prof. Upadhyaya is an excellent 2k1 recitation professor. His recitations have just the right mix of lecture review and example problems. He's an excellent explainer, breaks down difficult examples/concepts well, and takes time to answer questions thoroughly. Highly recommend his section for 2k1.

Fantastic lectures, seems to genuinely care that students do well. Listens to feedback, holds review sessions and extra problem solving sessions when needed. Attendance is not mandatory but I would highly recommend attending his lectures.

While this class is notorious for having exam averages in the 40s, Prof Upadhyaya really did his best in explaining the material. He recorded his lectures,broke down concepts slowly, worked out examples, and even did supplementary recordings when students had questions. His explanations really made things click, even if the test material was hard

teaches a notoriously hard class, but very clearly cares about you succeeding. a lot of people asked questions during lecture, and he always tried to make sure you understood. maybe not always the clearest on explaining topics or ideas, but will work with you to help understand.
He's a great guy who takes hours out of his schedule to run exam review sessions that basically reteach the whole class
Pramey is a solid professor. He lectures well and was very easy to interact with in office hours when I needed help. Would recommend.

Dr. Upudhyaya's lectures are some of the best lectures I've ever attended. His lectures are paced very well and he is very good at recognizing when students are struggling with new concepts. He is extremely accessible outside of class, and hosts 3-4 hour review sessions before exams.

He is by far one of the nicest professors I have ever had. He is extremely accessible outside the class and is very approachable. His teaching pace is also perfect and you will understand everything clearly if you pay attention. He doesnt mind slowing down and repeating concepts should people need him to. Get this professor, you wont regret it!

Very nice person and very good teacher. He makes sure everyone in the classroom understands each topic before moving on. Dedicates extra time to students when they express a lack of understanding in the class.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Such a gem of a prof. Lectures aren't mandatory, but lecture + book  much better understanding. Won't hesitate to re-explain material. Make sure to read the book. Exams are fair, but tough for sure. Most of my respect for him comes from his exam review lectures, which he'll go for as long as needed to go over all the material. Love him

Amazing professor! Explains the simplest of doubts cleary with a proper explanation. Breaks down complicated numerical for better understandability. Devoted extra time for students before the exam day to go over important concepts and numerical which was really helpful. Not strict at all, very chill and enthusiastic about his work.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Dr. Umbeck is one of the best professors at Purdue. Even during a 7:30am class, he still brought energy and engagement to each lecture that made the content easy to follow and understand. The course material can get a little confusing at times, but Dr. Umbeck goes over everything in great detail and answers questions perfectly.
One of the nicest professors at Purdue and teaches really well because he genuinely cares about the subject.

I really enjoyed his class. He does not require attendance yet most of the class still shows up every day because he makes the subject so interesting. I wasn't sure about econ until I took this class.
One of the best professor in Purdue. Always patient to students, answer questions on time.
Hard grader
Professor Ryan is a good teacher but I found his tests to really difficult and hard to prepare for.

Making an entry-level statistical inference course interesting is a very difficult task, but Professor Umbeck has a bright and encouraging energy that makes you want to pay attention simply out of respect for the effort he is putting into making the material understandable. Fantastic person, gives great feedback on issues with Stata HW (use him!).
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Gives good feedback--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Ryan brings an energy to lectures that is unmatched. He is super knowledgeable in his field and is eager to share with everybody and help however he can. He's always available and answers stuff really effectively. One of the best experiences I've had at Purdue, even though my head struggled to wrap itself around the content sometimes. 10/10

Professor Umbeck is absolutely incredible. He is the ONLY professor that kept me afloat this semester. He made me feel like an actual human and I felt like he actually heard my questions and concerns throughout the course. Would totally recommend taking any of his courses.

If you're taking MGMT 310, take it with another professor. Umbeck's class had very little structure, and it took him weeks to get things graded. He does weekly quizzes and his there is very little info given on what tests will cover.
reviews.rating_tags: Test heavy--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Hands down best professor at Purdue

Really really nice professor, he is very willing to help. If you go to lecture, you won't even need the book at all. Pretty fair exams and quizzes. Not a terribly difficult class. I expect to get a B and I didn't put very much effort into studying; however I did pay attention in class and take detailed notes.
Very nice teacher as well as a person. His lecture is super expressive and easy to understand. You won't regret taking his class

Decent professor. Exams are straightforward and a mix between free response and multiple choice. I relied heavily on the textbook but others just used their notes in class. Sometimes the homework was a bit time consuming, but nothing awful.

Awesome professor. Lectures are really informative, and he is very clear in how he explains things. He'll stop lecture and re-cover one small item if a student asks a question about it and also throws multiple different scenarios about how questions can be asked. Exams were kinda difficult, mainly because he only gave 50 minutes to complete them.

This was an online class and I'm assuming he may be a little better in person but he did almost nothing for the online section during the summer. He would only answer a few emails and would posts things late giving you less time to read and write the discussion answers. I did however ask for a deadline extension and he gave us that.
Very good prof! Knowing his stuff and explain it crystal clear! You don't even have to read the book. Very useful and exams are very related to his lectures.
Really great professor. I would highly recommend to take him if you can. Lectures are short so we he lets you off like 15 minutes early and still manages to teach a very wide array of material.

He explains complex topics well and respects students. Much more down to earth and approachable than some of the other professors here. He is pretty funny and there is no iclicker or mandatory attendance, yet the class is pretty full which means students actually like hearing him talk.

Dr. Umbeck has been at Purdue since 1975. That said this class was one of the most difficult I've ever taken, given the circumstances etc. Those that did exceptionally well in this class came with strong, even VERY strong background in Finance and Accounting. If you are not strong in Stats, Finance, Accounting you'll find this class VERY difficult.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Professor Umbeck is one of the WORST professors you will meet. On the very first day of class, he will tell the class that he doesn't care if he FLUNKs you. He gives out only a Mid-Term and a Finals Test. It only matters that you obtain his old exams. Memorize the answers on his old exams and you will obtain an 'A'. Otherwise, who knows?

Going into senior year, hardest class I've ever taken without a doubt. I have no chance at pulling my grade up and my GPA is going to take such a hit. Unless you have a great understanding of law and a half decent econ background it's going to be pretty difficult. The class is made up of 3 exams and that's all.
reviews.rating_tags: TEST HEAVY--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Don't ever take this class... Three multiple choice exams. Don't take it in summer or spring. Just don't take it. There is curve but it is fixed curve so it does not depend on the average to the class.

I do not recommend this class. Law and Economics course is just horrible. There are three exams and those are impossible. If you want to get a bad grade, please take it. There is no help in this class. Just don't take this course. Horrible... just horrible.

I respect Professor Umbeck. I felt he was very fair. He's a bit long-winded and his tests are hard, but if you do the practice tests and homework you should not be surprised by anything on the midterm or final. But be prepared to work. It's a hard class and a lot of work.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Teamed up with professor Fisher to team-teach econ and law. This was possibly the worst-run class I have experienced in my time as a student. There was no student support, no specific class schedulecalendar, no exam help, and possibly the worst part of all: full class lectures AND hours upon hours of required deathly boring videos.

Umbeck is clearly a master in his field and makes lectures interesting. I thoroughly enjoyed a lot of the examples that he conveyed to explain different topics of law and economics. The exams are tough, but would still recommend.
Has him 20 years ago and I still recall some of his lectures!  Don't fool yourself though.. He's tough!
awesome
the best prof I've come across
great classes!
He made economics a pleasure to learn
best of the best
One of the best lecturers I have ever had.  He will influence your perspective about the world around you.
Dr. Umbeck is obviously a master of his field. He knows the material as if it were his own vital stats. He conveys it clearly, even though it is a tough field to learn.
Makes economics seem interesting and fun. That's a difficult thing to do when studying economics
I was uninterested in microeconomics till I sat in his class and decided that the world runs on his subject!
best professor i have had at purdue's business school so far.

A Lecture hall of 150 students (2 classes of 150 each) with no attendance requirement was always jam packed. the man talks  very softly, yet the class is so quiet, you can hear him all the way at the back
The way this guy teaches is great and unique. He makes this boring course ECON accessible to anyone and gives a broad understanding to it. Tests and Exams are very challenging.
Awesome teacher. Econ professors should learn Econ from him so that they can teach better in class. Really was an eye-opening class for me. This is the way Econ should be taught folks.
good teacher, explains well, but tests are much harder than they need to be.
No Comments
Awesome teacher--great sense of humor, explains well--difficult tests!
he's cool and funny looking
Professor Uyeda's notes are easy to follow and templates are posted before class. Attendance is mandatory and iClicker is used. I would take a class with Uyeda again.

Dr. Uyeda has got to be one of the best profs at Purdue! His explanations are incredibly clear, and you almost never need to reference the textbook to excel in this class. He's kind, compassionate, caring, and always open to answering students' questions, no matter how "silly" they may seem. 100% recommend registering for his class over Dr. C's.

Probably best chem professor. Has note packets that prepare you very well for the exams. Exams are easy if you take time to practice and study. Show up to lectures because he'll sometimes give out extra points during lecture. Great lecturer. He is also super sweet.

Literally the best chemistry professor ever!!! I am terrible at chemistry but he made the class super understandable and gave a lot of resources to study for exams. The exams, the ones he wrote, were carbon copies to the practice ones. He's so so amazing!
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: The best chem prof! Prepped us well for exams, very approachable in office hours, gave extra credit for iClicker when 3/4 ditched class at 9:30 AM. I scored over 100% on all exams and felt confident before each one (way better than chem129). He is kind + patient when people ask questions and doesn't insult other orgo chem classes (unlike Dr. C).

Chill guy, explanations are aight. Easier to learn on your own, textbook, or online IMO. Started skipping lectures and had 10x easier job getting As. Beware, some participation polls are for extra credit. I guess he's upset that some people don't like his lectures? Weird, but cry I guess? So yeah, be careful of that.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: awesome lecturer, made the concepts super clear, and office hours are SUPER helpful

Uyeda was very good at lecturing. He explained concepts slowly and thoroughly and provided printed notes for us to use. His tests were very fair, and the notes, PSO worksheets, and practice exams were enough for studying without the textbook. The tests were also graded within a few days. The TAs were very helpful and good at explaining concepts.

The textbook didn't really matter in this course (I used it like one time). Make sure to go to PSO because the example questions are similar to the exam. The TAs are always there to help during their scheduled hours (trust me I went almost every time) & open to emails outside of hours. Take advantage of the resources, and you will do well in class.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Uyeda is a good teacher and will go over everything that appears on the exam, sometimes it is a smaller detail but you have always seen it. The exams are difficult and it is important to get as many points as possible on the first exam because they get harder. He does good practice questions during class just doesn't always give enough time.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Overall good professor but lectures are boring.

Uyeda is a really amazing professor who really cares about student success. His exams are hard but fair and using the previous exams provided will help you do well. Make sure not to fall behind in this class as it goes fast and content builds. An A is very possible with hard work.
Overall a very nice professor. Knows a lot about what he is talking about. Would recommend anyone interested at taking chm255
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Uyeda is a pretty good professor. He definitely understands what he is teaching and he does a good job communicating. Lectures can be very boring, 50min feels like 5 hours in there. Overall, he's everything that you would need and want in a professor -- just not super funny or anything.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Final grade weighed heavily on exams. No homework or extra credit. Heavy content class. In lectures, he barely goes over them before introducing a new concept. He fills in notes with you but goes too fast to follow along. You are left very unprepared. First class I actually wished for homework/quizzes. Harsh grading. Thinks C averages are good.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: He is an excellent professor, and I really enjoyed the class. You have to study to do well, but he gives you practice exams that help.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Such a wonderful professor. He is truly a great guy. I really appreciated how he ran his lectures too. Prof was great, but CHM 255 wasn't. The class was nearly completely based on exams. The lab was also separate from the lecture class and only a single credit.

He is a great professor and makes everything very clear. He makes learning the material actually enjoyable. But take notes and watch the lectures for sure! He actually cares if students are struggling. This class is very test heavy, but after a bad class average, he allowed us an opportunity to get points back!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Caring

reviews.comment: Prof. Uyeda is the only professor at Purdue who has made me enjoy AND understand chemistry. He is truly is an AMAZING lecturer and professor and you can tell he cares about what he's doing.
Grádeme was heavily based on tests. Material is complex
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Such a sweet man. I loved sitting in his class and his voice was fun to listen to as it was animated and always upbeat. Fills out his notes by hand with you so you can see all his examples and mechanisms.

Dr. Uyeda is super smart and an amazing lecturer. His lectures are very clear and are on Boilercast. Majority of the grade for the class is exams, but they are pretty easy as the content is straight from the notes. Plus there's always extra credit on exams. Recitation worksheets are graded for completion, providing an easy 100 points.

This man is amazing! Attend the lectures or else you are screwed. Also the recitations are super helpful. The class is 80% test and 20% worksheet completion. There is extra credit on EVERY test. I am horrific at chemistry I really suck at it but this professor somehow taught in a way that worked for me.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: One of the greatest professors that I have ever had. Although he is a genius and everything is extremely easy for him, he treats you as a beginner and helps you understand concepts with ease. He clearly is passionate about organic chemistry, and it shows during his amazing lectures.

i was 3 points close to getting an A in the class. I talked to him and the TAs and I was able to get 4 points back. He is a very great professor and I do not recommend cramming everything before the exam. You will need to understand the chemical mechanisms.

An extremely good professor that obviously knows the content very well and knows how to teach it to students. Exams can be pretty difficult even though he gives you practice exams. My biggest thing is that there are barely any (500) points in the course, so if you fail an exam and do mediocre on the others, there's a good chance you can still fail.

Very very knowledgeable on the course material and explains things in a super clear way. Getting a good grade is very possible in his classes, as he gives you all you need to learn. You just need to put in the effort to learn it.

Great professor, he explains things very well in lecture. I found CHM 255 to be pretty easy just by going to lecture and doing the practice problems before exams. Recitation is optional and graded only for completion.

So helpful and is amazing at explaining the material, he is such a smart man and a wonderful professor. He made this class so easy and I was nervous about taking organic chemistry after a rough first semester. My favorite professor I have had at Purdue so far.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: so nice and helpful. I wish he could teach every chemistry class I take!
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: Uyeda is a WONDERFUL professor.  Easily my favorite so far at Purdue.  For someone who absolutely hates chemistry and did sub-par in chem 115/116, I thoroughly enjoyed orgo and did well,  mainly because of this professor.  He goes through each topic and explains the topics so that they are easily understandable, and he cares about his students.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Amazing professor! Makes hard topics easier. Also takes student questions seriously and reviews material before exams.
reviews.rating_tags: ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Dr. Uyeda is an incredible lecturer. He explains things very well and speaks in a clear and precise manner. If you're ever confused he'll make time to sit down with you one on one and he'll take his time with explaining and making sure that you understand. Full of information! He really loves what he teaches and you it shows in his teaching style.

THIS MAN IS JUST WONDERFUL!! First prof ever at Purdue that I have had that actually cares! Always super nice and actually knows how to teach. Gave lecture notes and explain everything clearly. I was shocked how great his class was because of his young age.
The book problems and practice exams help a ton!!!  I loved having him.  You don't get an A without putting in effort but getting a good grade is possible.

Really good professor!  I was scared to take orgo, but he explains everything in a straightforward manner so it's really easy to understand.  He also spends the first couple of minutes reviewing material from the previous class which doesn't seem important but it's a really nice touch!

Honestly one of the worst professors I've had at Purdue. Her attendance policy for 413 was points-based all or nothing (so technically required if you want 10% of your grade). She is hard to hear, she writes on the board but often in places hard to read, and her exams are difficult. The group projects are a mess, too. Avoid if possible!

She honestly shouldn't have a teaching job. The worst instructor I've ever experienced. We asked her to release study material to the class that SHE told us we would need to study for the exam and she said no???
Took 413 with her. This was the worst class of my life.

I can't say I have enjoyed her class at all. The exam was incredibly hard compared to the practice problems and her ability to communicate is nonexistent. I wouldn't recommend in the slightest. Take class with someone else.

Please do not take this class if there are other options available. Grade is basically fully decided by final exam. She cannot solve and gets tripped up on her own in class practice questions. Expects you to be able to complete question perfectly after seeing one example that she struggles to solve. Struggles to answer in class questions as well.

Homework + Attendance = 30%, Midterm = 35%, Final Exam = 35%.  Extremely hard exams.  I felt like I could've just read the slides and gotten the same info instead of having to show up to class.  My raw grade for the overall class was a 53.33%, so the class curve was around 25%.  She can be funny sometimes, but overall usually boring.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: If you have any other options, absolutely do not take her class. The practice problems she gives in class are almost unhelpful for the exams, and she can't even solve the problems she presents on her own slides. And you are not allowed to ask a question.

Not a bad person but her course is extremely rigorous. Make sure that you get a 70% overall on your iclicker grade to get a 100 in participation, if not you will receive a 0% even if you get a 68% on iclicker. The exams are hard. The 3.0 curve will round everyone up tremendously because of how low everyone's grade is.

Solely taught by reading off the slides, she seemed to believe that if she showed something/did a problem once, you immediately would understand the concepts and how to do them. Final grade determined by 3 exams and homework, leading to many students to drop after failing the first exam. If this class is required for you, good luck.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She's nice but the class is hard. I think most of us walking out of the second/final exam felt bad. I think the main issue is sometimes with how fast she goes. Be prepared to spend a lot of time studying if you have mgmt 310 with her.

Not good at all. She is super hard to understand and mumbles a lot. Her examples in class are unhelpful and she makes things more complicated and confuses more than helps. It's hard to get a good grade in this class since each exam is 35% and there are only 2 the whole semester so if you have a bad day then your whole grade is screwed.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She is well meaning but very difficult to work with if you have a question. She won't tell you the average score on the exam unless you go to class? Never seen that before...

Even if she were to slow down and speak clearly, her slides don't give clear direction or computations. She does not take the time to truly teach concepts. For our last exam, homework for chapters 12-14 were non-credit, as she didn't leave time to teach them in class, but they will be on the exam. You will be teaching yourself.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She obviously cares about students and seems like a nice person, but the lectures are very hard to follow with her accent and the material is quite boring. No point in going to lecture. 75% of your grade is going to be exams but if you read the textbook and practice they are fairly easy.
i wouldn't check the streets when crossing when exam season came around!

I would recommend another professor. She reads off of slides with an accent that is hard to understand. I can tell she cares for students but explains concepts too fast and can make things overly complicated. Exams are the majority of your grade and are pretty tough. iclicker quizzes are used every class. I have to teach myself everything.

If you can avoid her do so. her lectures are confusing and 70% of your grade is from two exam. the slides make no sense whatsoever and she teaches too fast in class for people to keep up. this is not clear enough for a prereq class

You cannot understand anything she is saying even with a microphone. She reads directly from her very unorganized slides, hardly teaching anything. Really wish I knew that I would be teaching myself the entire course material... there's a reason why no one shows up to class!! Exams at 25% each, setting you up for failure with no assignments.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She does not do a good job at explain and breaking down problems and concepts in the class. She does not do many example problems and rarely explains how to do a problem.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She's a good person, unfortunately her accent makes lectures difficult to understand. Slides are also more often confusing than not. Lectures are only somewhat related to topic, ended up just reading the textbook instead.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Moves way too fast and says to “understand, not memorize,” but doesn't make the material clear or digestible. Lectures are dry, slides are overloaded, and it's hard to know what matters. Test averages were much lower than other sections. I did better just reading the textbook than paying attention in class. Recommend another prof if possible.

310 with Yatsenko is easy if you pay attention and study. Her tests involve plugging numbers into formulas from your cheatsheet. She teaches well, providing examples and iClickers are like exam questions, so there are no surprises. Her reviews shouldn't be this bad.

Surprised by her low rating—likely because 310 is tough. Lots of content, hard exams, but practice materials help. She's knowledgeable, keeps class on track, and prepares you well, even if she's newer to teaching.

She cares a lot about her students and how much effort they put into the course. She has an accent that can be difficult to understand, but you get used to it after a few lectures. The exams are close to the practice exams, but are proven to be a bit tricky.

Professor Yatsenko is a very nice woman. She makes a lot of jokes and overall the classroom environment is positive. However she moves very very very fast and its difficult to even attempt to understand a concept before moving forward to the next.

She shows no interest in teaching the material. She is very monotone, almost like a constant whisper. Often times hard to understand and overall, just a lazy professor. She made fun of the class for doing bad on exams rather than trying to help people understand. Would not recommend.

MGMT310 wasn't an option for her to review—makes sense given the course's lack of structure. She relies on poorly made slides that don't connect well with students. After a tough exam, she used memes instead of real feedback. She's a kind person, but her teaching style makes it hard for students to succeed. Would not recommend.
Terrible professor. Does not put in the effort to teach. Mostly just reads off the slides and is not helpful. Changed exam format on short notice. Would avoid at all costs
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment:
Very passionate about the material, explains in very strain-forward way. 
Loved her class a lot!
Thick accent makes it difficult to understand. Content makes sense to her but she cannot communicate effectively. Would not take again.
terrible and changes syllabus depending on how she feels

Bad professor, mainly due to her inability to communicate. Lectures are not very helpful and are not worth going to unless it is for participation points. She is so bad to the point she offers candy to people who participate, and even then, that is meaningless, as very few people take up that offer.
He is very hard. His class is impossible.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--So many papers

reviews.comment: This was a good class, valuable information taught, very reasonable expectation with homeworks/exams, and learned a lot. His teaching is mediocre sometimes, as he likes to brush through certain topics fast with minimal explanations leaving it up to you to understand it to the depth required to do the homeworks. But it's not impossible and doable.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Vikas is a pretty basic but good professor. Lectures are sometimes tough to understand but are handwritten, so the pace is easy to follow. Exams are straightforward, with few tricks. He's a pretty chill guy too and keeps things real with the class. Sometimes he will joke during lectures and catch you off guard. Just don't bug him about crib sheets.

He is a good professor but admittedly took some getting used to.  I learned a lot, this class was hard.  The good thing is it counts as a math class.  I would say he is not very good at answering questions in class, either repeating what he already has stated or answering a question not asked.  Apart from that he was a good prof.
Good Professor, really wants to make sure the students learn. Responds quickly to questions.
